# Janson Coffee — Product Roadmap (Phases 1–4)

> **Provenance:** synthesized from a ~100-agent `nw-discuss` investigation
> (`janson-coffee-INTEGRATION-INVESTIGATION.md`, kept in the build owner's home dir).
> Vaulted into the repo so no future session reinvents it.
>
> **Status (2026-06-21):** **Phase 1 is built** — the backend spine, slices S0–S9
> (test substrate → plot geometry/map → event-log spine + lot graph → derived
> metrics → green inventory/ATP → genealogy → activity-based COGS → EUDR → nav +
> command palette), plus the cost-entry and EUDR-declare write UIs. The one Phase-1
> item NOT yet shipped: the **live per-plot Open-Meteo weather feed** (the topbar
> weather strip is still static) — carry it into Phase 2 or a Phase-1 follow-up.
>
> Phases 2–4 below are the planned, not-yet-built scope. The full investigation
> (eight pillars, gap analysis, integrations, product strategy, differentiators)
> is the appendix beneath the roadmap.

---

## Build principles (NON-NEGOTIABLE — apply to every slice of every phase)

1. **World-class UI from the jump — never an afterthought.** The UI is *as important
   as the features working*. Every slice ships Apple-grade, 60fps, buttery liquid-glass,
   reduced-motion-aware, WCAG-AA-on-(animated)-glass, mobile-first UI **inside the slice**
   — not as a later "polish pass". A feature that works but looks mediocre is NOT done.
   Always design and build with a world-class UI in mind from the first line.
2. **Bug-free, fully working end-to-end.** Test-first (red→green) on every PR; a per-slice
   adversarial multi-agent review that fixes every confirmed flaw test-first; an
   end-to-end browser smoke test of every surface before it lands. No "ship now, fix later".
3. **Most robust, most in-depth coffee-farm software in the world** is the target — when a
   slice could go deeper (more correctness guards, more domain fidelity, a richer UI), it does.
4. **$0 forever** — free tiers only (Supabase / Vercel / Neon / Open-Meteo / OpenFreeMap …).

---

# Janson Coffee — World-Class Agribusiness Integration Investigation

> DISCUSS-wave (Jobs-to-be-Done) investigation of the full feature/integration surface for the Janson Coffee farm-operations system. Produced by a ~99-agent fan-out (87/88 capability investigations + 8 pillar synthesizers + chief-architect roadmap + product strategy + completeness critic).

- **Domains investigated:** 87/88  ·  **Pillars synthesized:** 8/8
- Date: 2026-06-20

---

## 1. The One-System Vision

Janson Coffee becomes a single living digital twin of the finca-plus-reserve, where one canonical record — the JC lot code, anchored to a GPS plot polygon — threads grow → harvest → process → store → sell → host → comply, and every screen, email, bag QR, auction sheet, payroll run, and audit pack is a trustworthy PROJECTION of that one record, never re-keyed marketing fiction or a hand-typed aggregate. The system the family runs today (plots, harvests, workers, processing, tasks — mostly read views, mock data) is exactly the right spine: it already threads lotCode from Harvest → ProcessingBatch and carries per-plot altitude/shade/variety/area. The vision is to (1) make that spine real and geometric (PostGIS polygons, an append-only event ledger, derived metrics instead of hand-authored season_summary tables), (2) extend the pipeline past its current dead-end at `green` into inventory, sale, shipment, and the roasted bag, and (3) close every workflow into an action loop — observe/forecast → alert → spawn a task on the existing board → log the action → feed the outcome back so models and recipes improve season over season. The unifying truth across all eight pillars is identical: for a single-estate Geisha house where a microlot can clear five figures per kilo at Best of Panama, provenance is not compliance overhead — it IS the product premium, and the same lot data that proves deforestation-free EUDR, computes true cost-per-kg-green, and prices a fixation is the same data that tells a Tokyo roaster "Tizingal Alto, Block A, Geisha, 1,650 masl, 24 Brix, inside the 200-ha quetzal reserve."

The bar is Hacienda La Esmeralda's commercial instinct + Cropster's specialty depth + a modern DTC retention playbook, made explicit, repeatable, and provable — but pointed origin-out for one great family farm that owns the whole chain and captures the full premium instead of a broker's commodity price. AI runs quietly across all of it (drafts offer copy and lot stories from real harvest data, advises fixation windows with the margin math done, diagnoses roya from a field photo offline, triages inbound buyer email into structured offers) under one hard invariant inherited from Andres's operating model: AI advises and drafts, a human signs every financial move and irreversible commitment, and untrusted public/customer text can never autonomously drive an action. And it all lives inside the $0-forever envelope — free Copernicus/Sentinel, Open-Meteo, Global Forest Watch, MapLibre+PostGIS on Supabase free tier, Listmonk/Postiz/Plausible, BirdNET — with spend only ever one-time field hardware (a Tempest station, a multispectral drone, a LoRaWAN gateway), never a recurring SaaS bill.

## 2. World-Class Integration Architecture

_The cross-cutting platform pillars that make it one integrated system rather than a bundle of tools._

- Unified data model / single source of truth: canonical IDs for every real-world thing (farm, plot, tree-block, lot, batch, worker, device, party), the bare lotCode promoted to a queryable farm-to-bag lineage GRAPH (split/merge/blend with mass carried on every edge), a UCUM unit + yield ledger, an append-only hash-chained event log, and a derived-metrics semantic layer that replaces today's hand-authored season_summary/daily_cherries/weekly_harvest aggregates — so no number ever disagrees between two screens. Built first; everything else is a thin projection. Single schema author, never forked (the src/lib/** contract already enforces this discipline).
- PostGIS geometry spine + spatial home screen: one canonical plot polygon per plot (extending today's flat altitude/area scalars) plus elevation/slope/aspect from a free DEM, with the 200-ha quetzal reserve as a first-class protected geometry. MapLibre + OpenStreetMap + Turf.js renders the farm as the primary index — every harvest, task, scouting pin, lot, and sensor is one click from its boundary. This single layer simultaneously unblocks NDVI monitoring, EUDR export, per-plot weather, IPM maps, irrigation zones, and the buyer-facing provenance map.
- Offline-first capture + on-farm edge gateway: a local-first PWA (service worker + IndexedDB/Dexie outbox + CRDT/last-writer-wins reconciliation) so a sub-3-second picker weigh-in, a roya pin, or a ferment pH reading is captured at 1,700 masl under canopy with zero signal, then syncs conflict-free. A ruggedized on-farm gateway (Raspberry Pi + ChirpStack/Mosquitto) ingests LoRaWAN sensors and fires frost/over-ferment alerts locally in seconds even during a cloud/power outage. Co-foundational with the data model, not polish — the highest-value data is born where there is no signal.
- Unified telemetry & device ingestion pipeline: ONE measurements store fed by IoT soil/microclimate sensors (LoRaWAN/MQTT/SenML), Bluetooth scales and moisture/Brix meters (Web Bluetooth), and voice capture — every reading device-traceable and calibration-stamped, a dead/drifting probe raising an incident instead of silently corrupting a season. Manual fast-entry is the $0 day-one fallback; the schema is designed for live probe streams later.
- Grounded AI/intelligence layer over the SSOT: a single Spanish-first brain doing per-plot yield forecasts (P10/P50/P90 by variety AND grade), offline on-phone leaf-rust/broca image diagnosis auto-mapped to the right plot, sell-now-vs-hold against the live ICE 'C' curve plus Janson's realized Geisha premium, offer-copy and lot-story drafting from real ops rows, and a copilot that cites Janson's own data (never generic web advice). Model-tiered (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus) with prompt caching and hard per-feature cost caps to stay viable on $0. Agentic-but-safe: typed/audited/human-confirmed writes; untrusted channel text never drives an action.
- Cross-cutting platform services built ONCE and consumed everywhere: the hash-chained, externally-time-anchored (OpenTimestamps) audit ledger that doubles as the activity feed, certification evidence trail, and payroll-dispute resolver; a rules/automation engine over the event bus (forecast→task→log→feedback loop); a WhatsApp-first multi-channel alerting fabric (SMS/voice escalation — Latam reality); identity/RBAC-over-RLS with seasonal worker auto-expiry; es-PA-first localization with SI/UCUM units; and security/backup/DR. Re-inventing these per module is how a platform becomes spaghetti.
- Integration hub as one signed, idempotent event bus: fan-in (Shopify, QuickBooks Online, Open-Meteo, ICE 'C' feed, Cropster, bank/Wise) and fan-out (Cropster, QR Digital-Link passport, certifier/EUDR TRACES-NT portals, carriers) with dead-letter retry and a one-screen health board — so 'YTD revenue' and 'kg of lot X remaining' are one live truth reconciled across green-export invoices and roasted-bag DTC sales.
- Compliance-as-byproduct, promises enforced at the data layer: thresholds and promises (Bird Friendly 40% shade, pH 5.0–5.5, broca 5%, frost <10°C, PHI/REI withholding windows, certification-prohibited agrochemicals, minimum-wage floor) live in ONE canonical server-enforced store and fire on every write path — never an honor system in the UI. EUDR DDS, ICO certificate of origin, phytosanitary, audit packs, and the carbon/biodiversity evidence graph all generate from the same events, turning an audit from a fire drill into a one-click export.

## 3. Phased Roadmap

### Phase 1 — Make the spine real & geometric — turn read-views into a trustworthy single source of truth (build on the current app)
- Promote the bare lotCode string into a canonical farm-to-bag lineage GRAPH (split/merge/blend, mass carried on every edge); auto-mint lot codes on cherry intake
- Add a PostGIS plot polygon per plot (plus the 200-ha reserve as a first-class geometry) and replace the static dashboard with a MapLibre spatial home screen — every plot/harvest/task one click from its boundary
- Replace hand-authored season_summary/daily_cherries/weekly_harvest aggregates with a derived-metrics semantic layer (numbers computed, never typed)
- Swap the static hand-typed weather strip for a live per-plot Open-Meteo forecast feed keyed to plot coordinates/altitude — the single highest-leverage cheap swap
- Append-only event log + activity feed as the audit substrate everything else writes into
- Extend the pipeline past its dead-end at `green`: a GreenLot inventory entity (grade/screen/moisture/location/available-to-promise) so the sellable asset stops vanishing from the system
- EUDR geolocation export: plot polygon + establishedYear + deforestation-free check vs the 2020 Global Forest Watch/JRC baseline → one-click TRACES-NT Due Diligence Statement (table-stakes EU market access, nearly free on this data model)
- Per-lot/per-plot cost-of-production rollup (activity-based COGS on green weight) — the number the whole business turns on, sharing the cost ledger with future accounting sync

### Phase 2 — Close the make-quality loop & the people system — protect the cup and pay the crew with dignity
- Offline-first per-picker weigh capture (badged picker + geofenced plot + lot + kg + ripeness in <3s, BLE scale, queue-and-sync) — the genesis event feeding traceability, pay, attendance, mill-intake all at once
- Morning crew dispatch + ripeness-aware routing delivered as a WhatsApp card; promote the free-text crew string into real Crew/CrewMembership entities
- Ripeness-driven harvest planning & pasada scheduler (per-plot GDD/NDVI maturation model staggering the 1,360–1,700 masl harvest, re-planning around rain fronts)
- Fermentation & wet-mill tracker: versioned altitude-tuned recipe library, live pH/temp/Brix curves (manual taps now, BLE later), predicted cut-point + alert before the window closes; eco-mill water-per-kg log
- Drying management with the reposo GATE — a lot physically cannot advance to milling until moisture stability (10.5–11.5%) AND minimum rest days are met; weather-coupled cover/move alerts; patio string promoted to capacity-tracked stations
- QC & cupping (SCA CVA + legacy 100-pt), cupper-drift calibration, defect grading, QC-hold quarantine — every score bound back through lotCode to its ferment time, drying curve and plot (the cup-to-cause loop)
- Unified worker system of record: one identity + append-only attendance ledger + digital por-obra contracts + certification ledger; one-tap seasonal rehire treating the Ngäbe-Buglé crew as named returning partners
- Blended piece-rate + hourly payroll with the minimum-wage make-whole guard enforced at the data layer; CSS/Seguro Educativo/décimo withholding; Yappy/Nequi/ACH/signed-cash disbursement with bilingual QR payslip
- Satellite NDVI/NDRE + Sentinel-1 SAR fusion with an honest data-confidence badge (survives Volcán cloud cover); IPM scouting with economic-threshold engine + PHI/REI-safe spray log; phenology GDD bloom→cherry prediction — all firing tasks onto the existing board

### Phase 3 — Monetize the chain — commerce, provenance-as-premium, and the books reconcile themselves
- Dual-regime pricing engine as one shared service: hedge/fixation against the live ICE 'C' curve for commodity volume; decoupled cup-score/scarcity/auction-comp pricing for reserve Geisha — never anchor a $13k/lb lot to the commodity index
- B2B green backbone: offer list → standards-based contract (GCA/ECF) with Incoterms 2020 → sample tracking → fixation cockpit → one-click export document pack (commercial invoice, ICO certificate of origin, MIDA phytosanitary, packing list, B/L) → contract-to-cash
- Specialty auction & marketplace channel (Best of Panama / Cup of Excellence / Algrano) — the single highest price-multiplier capability for this farm
- DTC roasted-bag storefront on the $0 headless stack (Next.js + Supabase + Stripe), lot-linked SKUs, and the public per-lot QR provenance microsite (GS1 Digital Link) — the trust mechanic that closes the specialty premium
- Single-estate Reserve Club subscriptions (pause/skip/swap, scarcity allocation, dunning via Stripe Smart Retries) + offline-first DGI-compliant farm-store/café POS — closing cherry-to-cash at the gate
- Dry milling (huller→polisher→screen grader→gravity table→optical sorter) with closed outturn mass balance; roasting with versioned golden profiles + Artisan .alog curve import + roast-to-SKU traceability
- QBO/Xero bi-directional accounting sync + Panama DGI Factura Electrónica via a certified PAC; multi-currency export AR/invoicing/collections with FX gain/loss attribution; cash-flow runway & pre-harvest finance planner
- Direct-trade CRM + lifecycle marketing automation (lot-launch broadcast the moment a batch hits `green`, DTC replenishment, B2B sample follow-up) on the same contact backbone; reputation ledger tying every cup score/award/cert to a lotCode
- Storage/controlled-environment monitoring (LoRaWAN temp/RH/aw vs target bands) protecting stored green from Volcán humidity — a documented storage certificate per lot

### Phase 4 — Estate intelligence & the conservation moat — the least-copyable differentiators
- Grounded ML/copilot crown: explainable P10/P50/P90 yield forecast by variety AND grade, offline image pest diagnosis, sell-vs-hold advisor, Spanish-first 'ask your farm' NL query — all citing Janson's own rows, human-confirmed writes only
- The 200-ha reserve as a first-class engine: AudioMoth + BirdNET bioacoustic quetzal monitoring, Bird Friendly thresholds encoded (40% shade, native-majority species), reconciling satellite + acoustic + human evidence streams
- Sustainability evidence graph (one substrate, four views): per-lot CO2e via Cool Farm Tool / Verra VM0042 agroforestry insets, biodiversity log, mill-water L/kg, certification audit dossiers — conservation becomes a quantified, monetizable, brand-defining asset
- Prescriptive agronomy: renovate-vs-stump-vs-hold by tree-age + yield-decline + cup-trend, varietal-to-terroir fit (Geisha to the highest mistiest parcels), rock-and-roll stumping, 12–18-month nursery seedling-demand back-planning
- IoT soil/microclimate sensor nervous system (moisture/NPK/pH/leaf-wetness/temp) feeding the rust-risk and frost-nowcast engines their validated predictors; irrigation/regulated-deficit bloom-trigger scheduling (FAO-56 ET) against the ANATI/MiAMBIENTE water concession
- Per-tree census & nursery propagation provenance (seed-lot → germination → transplant → field tree) — flagship Geisha trees earn per-tree QR/RFID provenance no commodity crop would
- Multi-tenant isolation at the database layer so the proven platform can be licensed to neighboring Volcán estates
- Agro-tourism self-service booking bridging the Lagunas Adventures tour to the literal micro-lot a guest tastes and then buys via QR — converting an experience into a traceable sale

## 4. Top Differentiators (unique to Janson)

- The JC lot code as the single hyperlink unifying operations, marketing, sales, and compliance: a buyer scans a bag and in under a second sees the exact GPS plot at 1,650 masl, the named picker crew and Brix, the ferment and full drying curve, the SCA cup score, the EUDR attestation, and the quetzal-reserve conservation claim — every fact generated from one append-only ledger, nothing re-keyed. No farm software fuses ops, provenance, and commerce on one lotCode like this.
- The 200-ha private quetzal nature reserve treated as a first-class mapped, bioacoustically-monitored (AudioMoth + BirdNET), carbon-quantified asset — Janson's single least-copyable moat. It sits exactly where Verra VM0042 agroforestry carbon, Smithsonian Bird Friendly (born in Panama at STRI), and EUDR deforestation-free all converge, turning conservation from a tagline into monetizable carbon insets, audit evidence, a tourism draw, and a direct-trade premium that compound each other.
- Decoupled reserve-Geisha pricing routed to Best of Panama / Cup of Excellence (where 2025 washed Geisha set a $30,204/kg world record) — never anchored to the commodity ICE 'C' index — sitting in the exact Volcán terroir that produces those lots. The gap between selling a competition microlot as commodity green vs on the auction block is 100–1000x, and Janson owns the whole chain (grow→mill→green→sell) so its chain of custody is complete and uncontested in a way a co-op buying from many smallholders can never be.
- SAR-fused satellite monitoring with an honest data-confidence badge tuned for Volcán's near-daily cloud cover — a naive optical-only NDVI tool is useless half the year here; Sentinel-1 radar fallback plus a freshness badge is the genuine edge over any generic ag-monitoring product, and the same free Earth-observation layers double as the EUDR compliance engine.
- Dignity-first, offline-first labor system for the ~90% Ngäbe-Buglé seasonal migrant workforce: sub-3-second badged weigh-ins at 1,700 masl with no signal, a minimum-wage make-whole guard enforced at the data layer, bilingual (Spanish/Ngäbere) QR payslips, certification-gated hazard work, and one-tap seasonal rehire — turning documented decent-work evidence into the exact protection the Best-of-Panama premium needs under EU-CSDDD buyer due diligence.
- Vertical integration made provable end-to-end: 'from our farm to your cup since 1990' is only true if the farm controls grow → wet/dry mill → green → roast → sell, and the system captures every custody hop, so the roasted bag's QR resolves to its own tree block — a claim no reseller can fake and most origins structurally cannot make.
- The entire world-class stack inside a $0-forever envelope (free Copernicus/Sentinel, Open-Meteo, GFW/JRC, MapLibre+PostGIS on Supabase free tier, Listmonk/Postiz/Plausible, BirdNET, OpenTimestamps), with spend only ever one-time field hardware — proving the rigor of a Cropster + La Esmeralda + Klaviyo playbook without a recurring SaaS bill.

## 5. Quick Wins (extend the current app)

- Swap the static hand-typed weather strip for a live per-plot Open-Meteo forecast feed keyed to each plot's coordinates and altitude band — free, no key, and immediately makes the dashboard real instead of mock.
- Generate a public per-lot provenance page (/lot/JC-564) and a QR resolver straight from the existing Harvest + ProcessingBatch + Plot data the app already holds — plot, altitude, variety, Brix, process, drying — turning provenance into the price story with zero new data capture.
- Add a GreenLot entity so the processing pipeline stops dead-ending at `green`: promote a finished batch into a tracked, graded, located asset — the single linchpin between processing and revenue that currently has no system at all.
- Replace the hand-authored season_summary / daily_cherries / weekly_harvest aggregate tables with derived metrics computed from the harvest/processing rows, killing the #1 trust defect (numbers disagreeing across screens).
- Compute and surface true cost-per-kg-green per lot from the workers/tasks/processing data already captured — the single most decision-relevant number for a specialty farm, buildable as a derived view today.
- Add a PostGIS polygon column to plots and render a MapLibre map of the finca + reserve as a second home view — the keystone geometry layer that later unblocks EUDR, NDVI, IPM maps, and irrigation, started cheaply now.
- Promote the free-text `crew` string into a real Crew entity and the free-text `patio` string into capacity-tracked drying stations — small schema cleanups that unblock dispatch and drying-curve features downstream.
- One-click EUDR draft export (plot polygon GeoJSON + deforestation-free declaration) from the plot geometry + establishedYear — a near-free compliance win that protects EU market access ahead of the 2026 SME deadline.
- Make the dashboard event-driven: add an append-only event log behind the existing activity feed so every harvest/processing/task write becomes the audit substrate later phases build on, with no UI change required.

## 6. Product Strategy — single-farm vs SaaS, build-vs-buy, competitive moat

I'm the Product Strategist. Here's my opinionated read across the four questions. No fluff.

## 1. Single-farm internal tool vs multi-tenant SaaS

**Recommendation: Build single-tenant first, but architect multi-tenant from line one. Do NOT sell it as SaaS for ~2-3 seasons.**

The Platform pillar already names the right move: "multi-tenant-isolated at the database layer so Janson can later license it to neighboring estates." That's correct — but the *productize-and-sell* decision is a trap if rushed, for concrete reasons:

- **The buyer pool is tiny and the depth bar is brutal.** The features that make this world-class (EUDR polygon export, SAR-fused NDVI under perpetual cloud, GDD bloom→cherry per varietal, the reserve bioacoustic engine, dual-regime pricing that decouples Geisha from the C) are *Volcán-Geisha-specific*. The total addressable market for "high-altitude specialty single-estate with a private reserve that wants to self-host" is maybe a few hundred farms worldwide, most of whom already pay Cropster. SaaS economics need either many customers or high ACV; you'd have neither early.
- **The $0-forever constraint is incompatible with operating SaaS.** Free Copernicus/Open-Meteo/BirdNET quotas and self-hosted Listmonk/ChirpStack are fine for *one* farm. The moment you onboard tenant #2, you're operating someone else's compliance-critical infrastructure (EUDR DDS, payroll, DGI factura) — that's an SLA, a support burden, a security liability, and a recurring bill. The constraint that makes the single-farm build elegant is the same one that makes SaaS untenable.
- **But the data model decisions are nearly free to get right now and ruinously expensive to retrofit.** Tenant-scoped RLS, a `farm_id`/`org_id` on every row, no hardcoded "Janson" anywhere, reference data (varieties, thresholds, certifications) as data not constants. The MEMORY notes already show the pain of multi-author schema drift and tenant-clamp bugs — design the isolation in, leave the go-to-market dormant.

**The honest framing:** this is a single-estate digital twin that *could* become a niche vertical SaaS for the ~Best-of-Panama tier of farms (Esmeralda, Elida, Sophia, Carmen, Lamastus, Ninety Plus) — a "Cropster for estates that own their whole chain and a reserve." That's a real but small wedge. Decide to *sell* it only after Janson's own data proves the moat (see #4). Until then, multi-tenant is an *architecture* decision, not a *business* decision.

## 2. Build vs buy, per major area

The governing principle: **build the spine and the farm-specific intelligence; integrate the commodity rails; never rebuild a system of record that buyers already standardize on.**

| Area | Verdict | Why |
|---|---|---|
| **PostGIS geometry / lot-genealogy SSOT** | **BUILD** | This is the moat and the keystone. No vendor models a 200-ha reserve as a first-class protected polygon tied to lot codes. Owning this is the whole point. |
| **Cupping / QC / green inventory / roast curves** | **INTEGRATE Cropster (with local mirror)** | Cropster is the gravitational center of every pillar's integration list — it's what roasters and BoP already run on. Do NOT rebuild cupping or roast logging; you'd lose interop and gain nothing. BUT: keep a local lot-event mirror so you're not hostage to their API, and design Cropster-compatible export schemas day one. Cropster killed its standalone Origin capture app — that capture gap (offline weigh, field scouting) is exactly where you *build*. |
| **Accounting / payroll / tax** | **BUY/INTEGRATE (QBO/Xero + a Panama PAC)** | Never build double-entry bookkeeping or factura-electrónica clearing. Integrate QBO/Xero and a DGI-registered PAC. BUILD only the *coffee-native* layer on top: per-lot activity-based COGS and the CSS/décimo payroll *calculation* (the make-whole minimum-wage guard is farm-specific logic worth owning). |
| **Satellite / remote sensing / weather** | **INTEGRATE free data, BUILD the fusion + alert loop** | Copernicus/Sentinel + Open-Meteo are free pipes. The *value* is SAR-optical fusion with an honest confidence badge under Volcán cloud and the forecast→alert→auto-task loop. Build that; never pay a managed NDVI SaaS at this scale. |
| **DTC commerce / payments / subscriptions** | **BUY rails, BUILD the storefront** | Stripe (Checkout/Billing/Terminal/Tax), Yappy, Wise. Headless storefront on the existing Next.js stack — *not* Shopify (rent + the lot-story integration is custom anyway). The lot-linked SKU + QR passport is the build. |
| **Logistics / customs / shipping** | **INTEGRATE (EasyPost/Shippo, EUDR TRACES, ICO), BUILD the doc generator** | The carrier and TRACES-NT seams are integrations. The export document pack that auto-fills from lot data with zero re-keying is the build — and it's the same Shipment object three pillars duplicated. |
| **Marketing / email / social / booking** | **BUY/free-tier the channels, BUILD the lot→story engine** | Listmonk/Resend/Postiz/Bokun on free tiers. The unfakeable asset is the per-lot Story Builder auto-drafted from ops data. Channels are commodities; the content engine is the moat. |
| **AI / copilot** | **INTEGRATE Claude API, BUILD the grounding** | Don't build models. Build the RAG grounding over the SSOT, cost governance, and the hard injection invariant (untrusted text never drives a write). |

**The one rule that ties it together:** anything that is the *provenance graph* or *farm-specific intelligence* you build and own; anything that is a *ledger of record buyers already trust* (Cropster, QBO) you integrate and mirror; anything that is a *commodity rail* (Stripe, weather, carriers) you wire and stub until volume justifies the paid tier.

## 3. Competitive landscape & where Janson differentiates

**The map:**
- **Cropster / Tastify** — own the *quality/QC/trade* layer (cupping, green inventory, roast, sample/offer). Deep, trusted, sell-side. Weak/absent on agronomy, GIS, labor, payroll, conservation. *They are a partner, not a competitor* — interop, don't fight.
- **Granular/Corteva, Agrian, Conservis** — row-crop/broadacre North American ag (corn/soy/wheat). Field ops, inputs, agronomy-as-input-sales. Zero coffee process model, zero specialty quality, zero estate/reserve concept. Wrong crop, wrong geography, wrong economics.
- **FarmOS** — open-source, generic, flexible, but a blank framework — no coffee model, no GIS depth, no compliance packs out of the box. A reference for the open ethos, not a competitor.
- **Cropin, Agriwebb, generic ag-monitoring NDVI tools** — broad, shallow; built for cloud-free optical and commodity yield, useless half the year in Volcán and blind to cup quality.

**Nobody sits in the middle.** There is no product that unifies grow → process → cup → sell → host → comply *on one lot-code spine for a single estate that owns its whole chain and a biodiversity reserve.* The market is bifurcated: ag-tech that knows fields but not cups, and coffee-tech that knows cups but not fields. Janson's system is the seam.

**Where it differentiates, concretely:**
1. **The closed loop, not the dashboard.** Every pillar's stated "world-class bar" is the same: forecast → alert → auto-spawn task → log → retrain. Competitors show you a map; this *acts*. That's the product thesis.
2. **One lot-code thread from tree polygon to the bag QR to the EUDR DDS to the cup score to the invoice.** Cropster has the cup half; nobody has the unbroken thread including agronomy, GIS, labor cost, and conservation.
3. **The reserve as a first-class asset.** No competitor models a quetzal reserve as a mapped, bioacoustically-monitored, carbon-quantified entity. This is genuinely uncopyable.
4. **Volcán-cloud-honest remote sensing** (SAR fusion + confidence badge). Generic NDVI tools can't operate here; this is a real technical wedge.
5. **Dual-regime pricing** that refuses to anchor a $13k/kg Geisha to the ICE C — a commodity tool can't, a farm tool won't.

## 4. The defensible moat

Ranked by durability:

1. **Origin trust / unbroken provenance (strongest, durable).** Because Janson controls *every* custody event in-house (grow→mill→green→roast→sell), its chain of custody is *complete and uncontested* end-to-end — a co-op buying cherry from many smallholders structurally cannot match this. The lot-code-threaded, tamper-evident, one-query passport (optionally OpenTimestamps-anchored) is the moat that directly converts to the $40–300/lb premium. **This is the real moat** — not the software, the *verifiable truth* the software produces.

2. **The conservation/terroir asset (uncopyable, but it's the farm's, not the software's).** The 200-ha reserve + shade-grown Geisha at 1,350–1,700 masl sitting exactly where EUDR + Verra VM0042 + Smithsonian Bird-Friendly converge. The software *quantifies and monetizes* it (carbon insets, Bird-Friendly evidence, biodiversity log) but doesn't create it. Durable for Janson; not transferable as a SaaS moat.

3. **Compliance-as-moat (real, time-boxed advantage).** EUDR delists farms that can't produce a polygon + DDS. Janson's reserve makes it the easy-pass case. This is a *competitive* moat over un-traceable origins *right now* (2026/27 deadlines), but it decays toward table-stakes as everyone is forced to comply. Capture the premium window; don't bet the long game on it.

4. **Data network effects (weakest here — be honest).** The classic SaaS moat (more tenants → better models) **barely applies** at single-farm scale. The per-plot GDD, yield, and pest models improve from *Janson's own* seasons (a within-farm compounding loop — real and valuable), but there's no cross-tenant network effect until you have many tenants, which #1 argues against rushing. **Don't oversell "data network effects"** — the within-farm season-over-season model improvement is genuine; a multi-tenant flywheel is hypothetical and small.

**The synthesis:** The moat is *origin trust made provable and the conservation story made quantifiable*, both expressed through one lot-code spine. The software is the *mechanism* of the moat, not the moat itself — which is exactly why building single-tenant-excellent beats rushing to SaaS. Win by making Janson's coffee provably worth more, then *maybe* license the mechanism to the handful of peer estates who'd value it — as a deliberate, funded business decision, not a default.

**One thing I'd flag as out-of-scope-but-important:** the biggest strategic risk isn't competitive, it's *operational* — a 1-3 person family team cannot run a system this broad. The roadmap must be ruthlessly sequenced (SSOT + GIS + offline capture + EUDR first; everything else earns its place) or it becomes shelfware. The ambition is correct; the discipline to ship the spine before the crown is what determines whether any of this is real.

## 7. Gap Analysis (Completeness Critic)

I have enough grounding. The codebase confirms the syntheses' premises: flat `Plot` scalars (no geometry/lat-lon), `lotCode` as a bare string spine, pipeline dead-ending at `green`, hand-authored aggregates, mock-data with no backend. The seven pillars are strong but I can now identify what's genuinely missing across the whole-system view.

Here is my completeness critique.

---

# COMPLETENESS CRITIC — Gaps & Highest-Value Additions

The seven pillars are individually excellent and the cross-pillar convergence (one `lotCode` spine, one PostGIS geometry layer, one event ledger, offline-first capture) is correct. But reviewed as a *complete* world-class coffee-agribusiness system, there are material gaps — whole capability areas nobody owns, risky assumptions, integration collisions, and sequencing traps. Sharpest findings first.

## A. CAPABILITY AREAS NO PILLAR OWNS (true white space)

1. **Equipment, machinery & asset maintenance — ORPHANED, and it's a harvest-stopper.** Every pillar assumes the wet mill, Guardiola dryers, pulpers, optical sorter, the diesel genset, vehicles, and the drone exist and run — but no pillar owns their maintenance, runtime hours, breakdown logging, spare-parts inventory, or preventive-maintenance scheduling. On a vertically-integrated estate, a pulper failure at peak harvest or a genset dying mid-dry is a cup-quality catastrophe. Finance touches fuel/diesel and Grow mentions the drone as "one-time hardware," but nobody tracks asset health. **Add: an Asset & Maintenance register** (asset → runtime meter → PM schedule → work-order → parts-draw from the input inventory ledger). Wire it to the IoT pipeline (genset/dryer telemetry) and the alerting fabric. High value, fully $0.

2. **Disaster / catastrophic-risk & business continuity — UNOWNED.** Volcán sits on Barú, an active volcano; the region has frost events, landslides on steep slopes, and Panama's rainy-season extremes. Finance mentions parametric weather insurance (ISA/Etherisc) almost in passing, but no pillar owns a **risk register, catastrophe response runbook, or crop/asset insurance claims workflow with evidence capture**. A frost or ashfall event needs: instant geotagged damage documentation (feeds the insurance claim AND the EUDR/audit trail), a continuity plan for the mill, and a financial-impact reforecast. This is a genuine gap for a real farm, not a nicety.

3. **Internal knowledge / SOP authoring & succession — UNOWNED.** Process & Quality has a "recipe library" and "golden roast profiles," but the broader estate has decades of tacit knowledge (which plot floods, how Las Lagunas frost behaves, the rock-and-roll stumping cadence) held by the family. No pillar owns a **living SOP/playbook system** so that knowledge survives a key person leaving and trains seasonal supervisors. This is the soft-but-real moat for a 1990-established family farm.

4. **Multi-entity / household & personal-vs-business separation — UNOWNED.** This is a *family* farm with a guest house, café, tours, AND a private nature reserve. Finance assumes one clean P&L, but real family farms blur personal and business spend, run the reserve as a quasi-separate entity, and may have multiple legal entities (farm S.A., tourism operation). No pillar addresses **cost-center / entity segmentation** — which matters for true margin, tax, and any future financing or sale. Flag as a design assumption to resolve, not necessarily build now.

## B. RISKY ASSUMPTIONS SHARED ACROSS PILLARS

5. **The "$0-forever + no-database" constraint vs. the world-class bar is an unresolved contradiction, and the syntheses paper over it.** Every pillar's `world_class_bar` assumes PostGIS, append-only event ledgers, Supabase Realtime, RLS, hash-chained audit, LoRaWAN ingestion — i.e. a **live backend**. But the project CLAUDE.md says *"no database… in-repo mock data, no backend"* and the current state is mock-data files. The Platform pillar quietly assumes Supabase is the backbone; the others inherit that. **This is the single biggest sequencing risk in the whole program.** Recommendation: make the mock-data → Supabase-free-tier transition an *explicit, gated, foundational decision* (it's a prerequisite for ~90% of every pillar's differentiators), with an honest free-tier ceiling analysis (Supabase free tier: 500MB DB, 1GB storage, pauses after 7 days inactivity — time-series telemetry and media will blow past this fast). Nobody has costed the free-tier envelope against the data volume the IoT/satellite/media features generate. **This must be resolved before any pillar fan-out.**

6. **"AI advises, human signs" is stated everywhere but never specified as a shared enforcement primitive.** Commerce, Brand, Platform, and the dispatcher all repeat the injection invariant and human-in-the-loop rule — but each describes it locally. It must be **one shared approval/guard service** (typed action proposals → human confirm → audited write; untrusted inbound text can never reach a write path), built once in Platform and consumed by all. As written, it's at risk of five divergent implementations — exactly the spaghetti the Platform pillar warns against. Elevate it to a named Platform capability.

7. **EUDR timeline is stated inconsistently across pillars — a factual drift that will mislead sequencing.** Grow says "large operators 30 Dec 2025, SMEs/micro 30 Jun 2026." Finance says "Dec-2026/Jun-2027 deadlines." Supply Chain says "2025+ shipments." These cannot all be true. The team must **pin one authoritative EUDR effective-date table** (in the canonical promise/threshold store) and have every pillar read it — otherwise the highest-stakes compliance feature is built against three different deadlines. Flag as a must-reconcile-before-build.

8. **Cherry:green mass-loss ratio is asserted as a constant (~5.5:1) but is process- AND variety-dependent.** Process & Quality and Platform both bake in a mass-balance anomaly check. Naturals, honeys, and washed have materially different ratios, and Geisha differs from Caturra. A single hardcoded ratio will throw false anomalies on every natural lot and mask real loss on washed. **The ratio must be a per-method, per-variety calibrated curve in the canonical store**, not a constant — and it's exactly a "promise" that belongs in the server-enforced threshold store.

## C. INTEGRATION & ARCHITECTURE GAPS

9. **Cropster appears as a "must-integration" in all 7 pillars — but its actual data model isn't validated, and a key assumption is already stale.** Harvest & People itself notes "Cropster discontinued its standalone Origin app." Building Cropster-compatible export schemas "from day one" against an unverified/changing API across 7 pillars is a concentrated risk. **Recommendation: one spike to confirm current Cropster API surface, auth, and lot/cupping/green-inventory schemas BEFORE any pillar designs to it** — and a fallback (CSV interchange) if API access isn't obtainable at $0. Right now every pillar assumes Cropster works; none has verified it.

10. **No pillar owns the canonical UNIT and CURRENCY conversion service end-to-end, yet money and weight cross every boundary.** Platform mentions UCUM and ISO 4217, and Finance mentions FX, but coffee has brutal unit traps: *lata* (the picking can — and its kg varies by farm), *quintal*, 60kg/69kg/70kg bag equivalents, lb-vs-kg auction pricing, parchment-vs-green weight, JPY/KRW/EUR settlement. A mis-converted lata-to-kg or bag-equivalent silently corrupts payroll, yield, inventory, and margin simultaneously. **This needs to be one hardened, tested conversion service** (the Harvest pillar's lata, the Finance pillar's bag-equivalents, the Commerce pillar's lb-pricing all route through it), not scattered.

11. **Sample inventory is decremented in BOTH Process & Quality and Commerce — collision risk.** Process & Quality's "Sample & offer management" and Commerce's B2B backbone both draw down green inventory for samples. If these are separate writers, a scarce Geisha micro-lot gets double-committed (the exact failure both pillars promise to prevent). **One sample-draw ledger, one allocation/available-to-promise authority** — resolve which pillar owns it (it should be the green-inventory/SSOT layer, with both surfaces as consumers).

12. **The `plot.status` (healthy/watch/at-risk) multi-writer problem is correctly flagged in Grow but the fusion logic owner is undefined.** Grow names five candidate signal-writers and says "a single status-resolution layer fuses inputs." But that resolution layer is a Platform/SSOT concern (it's a derived metric), not a Grow concern — and Platform's "derived-metrics semantic layer" should own it. **Assign the status-fusion rule to the semantic layer explicitly**, or Grow and Platform will each build half of it.

## D. SEQUENCING MISTAKES

13. **Geometry/PostGIS is claimed as "the keystone, build first" by BOTH Grow and Platform — and they describe DIFFERENT scopes.** Grow's keystone = plot polygons + DEM + reserve zones for agronomy. Platform's = the same geometry as "the first link of geographic traceability" + spatial home screen. Supply Chain and Finance and Commerce and Brand ALL also depend on plot polygons (EUDR, QR maps, lot-story maps). **This is one foundational deliverable with six consumers — it must be a single, Platform-owned, cross-pillar Phase-0 item with one schema author**, not a Grow feature that the others happen to reuse. As currently written, Grow "owns" it, which buries a cross-cutting keystone inside one pillar.

14. **Offline-first capture is co-foundational (Platform + Harvest both say so) but the mock-data current state means there's nothing to sync to yet.** The genuine Phase-0 critical path is: **(a) decide the backend (gap #5) → (b) build the SSOT + geometry + event ledger + offline sync substrate → (c) build the conversion/threshold/approval shared services → THEN fan out pillars.** Several pillars say "build my spine first," but there's a *program-level* spine beneath all of them that no single pillar is accountable for sequencing. This needs an explicit Phase-0 program gate before the 50-agent fan-out.

15. **DTC/POS/subscriptions (Commerce) and the public website (Brand) both claim the storefront/customer/QR-microsite layer — overlapping ownership.** Commerce owns "DTC storefront + QR microsite + customers/orders." Brand owns "public website + per-lot Lot Passport pages + QR resolver." These are the *same surface* (the public site, the lot page, the QR resolver, the customer record). **Resolve: one public-site + lot-passport + customer-identity layer**, with Commerce owning transactions and Brand owning content/SEO/story — or they'll build two QR resolvers and two customer tables.

## E. COFFEE-SPECIFIC NEEDS UNDER-EXPLORED

16. **Water/effluent compliance is mentioned but under-weighted as a shutdown risk.** Finance notes COPANIT 35-2000 effluent norms; Grow notes eco-mill water. But wet-mill wastewater (aguas mieles) is a *regulated discharge* in Panama — a violation can halt milling. This deserves first-class standing in Supply Chain or Finance, not a passing mention: an effluent-monitoring + discharge-permit-compliance workflow tied to the mill's processing events.

17. **Seed-to-nursery genetic provenance & varietal authenticity verification is thin.** Grow's per-tree census and WCR Variety Catalog are noted, but Geisha specifically has a rampant **authenticity/mislabeling problem** in the market (Geisha vs "Gesha" vs Catuaí-passed-as-Geisha). For a farm whose premium IS the variety, a documented genetic-provenance chain (seed lot → mother tree → field block → lot) is a real differentiator and an anti-fraud asset — under-explored.

18. **Harvest-labor housing, food, and camp logistics for ~80 migrant Ngäbe-Buglé workers is unaddressed operationally.** Harvest & People tracks worker identity, pay, and contracts, and mentions "housing/camp assignment" as a field — but the *operational* job (feeding 80 people, bunk allocation, potable water, the company-store/fiado ledger that nets against pay) is a real recurring farm job that's only half-modeled. The fiado/advance netting is in payroll, but the store inventory and camp logistics aren't owned anywhere.

19. **No pillar owns weather/climate LONG-RANGE adaptation & planting strategy.** Grow has hyperlocal forecasting and phenology (tactical, days-to-weeks). But climate change is shifting the viable altitude band for arabica upward — a multi-decade strategic question for a farm deciding what to plant in 2026 to harvest in 2030+. The reserve and altitude spread are assets here. This is a "future-bet" strategic-planning capability nobody holds.

## HIGHEST-VALUE ADDITIONS (ranked)

1. **A formal Phase-0 program gate** that resolves the backend decision (#5), pins EUDR dates (#7), and builds the *cross-pillar* spine (SSOT + geometry + event ledger + offline sync + shared services: units/currency, thresholds/promises, approval-guard) BEFORE any pillar fan-out. This is the single most important correction — most pillars assume a backend and a spine that nobody is accountable for delivering first.
2. **Equipment & Maintenance pillar** (#1) + **Disaster/continuity & insurance-claims** capability (#2) — the two biggest genuinely-orphaned operational areas for a real vertically-integrated farm.
3. **One shared unit/currency conversion service** (#10) and **one calibrated mass-loss curve** (#8) — both are silent-corruption sources that touch payroll, yield, inventory, and margin simultaneously.
4. **De-collide the overlapping ownership**: sample-draw ledger (#11), public-site/QR/customer layer (#15), geometry keystone ownership (#13), status-fusion (#12) — assign single owners now, before fan-out, or you ship duplicate writers of the exact resources the syntheses promise never to double-write.
5. **Validate Cropster reality with one spike** (#9) before 7 pillars design to an unverified, partially-discontinued API.
6. **Wet-mill effluent compliance** (#16) and **Geisha genetic-authenticity provenance** (#17) — the two most under-weighted *coffee-specific* needs, one a shutdown risk, one a premium-protection moat.

Grounding note: the current repo (`/Users/andres/coffee-farm-operations/src/lib/types.ts`, `src/lib/data/*`) confirms the syntheses' premises are accurate — flat Plot scalars with no geometry, bare-string `lotCode`, pipeline ending at `green`, hand-authored aggregates, mock data with no backend — which is exactly why gap #5 (the backend/$0 contradiction) and #14 (the missing program-level Phase-0 spine) are the load-bearing risks the individual pillars don't resolve because none of them can see the whole board.

---

## 8. The Eight Pillars (synthesized)

### Grow — Agronomy & Production

The Grow pillar is where Janson's brand is actually made: 1,350–1,700 masl Geisha and reserve micro-lots whose price (10–100x commodity, $800+/lb at auction) is set by terroir, cup score, and a credible origin/conservation story. Across all thirteen capability investigations, the same structural truth emerges: today's `Plot` is a flat row of scalars (`altitudeMasl`, `shadePct`, `trees`, `status`, `expectedYieldKg`, `harvestedKg`) with NO spatial geometry, NO lat/lon, NO time series, and NO sub-plot resolution. Eleven of the thirteen capabilities independently name "the plot has no polygon / no coordinates" as their blocking gap. That makes the verdict unambiguous: a PostGIS plot-and-reserve geometry layer (boundaries, elevation/slope/aspect from a DEM, per-plot lat/lon, the 200-ha reserve as a first-class protected polygon) is the one foundational keystone the entire pillar inherits. Build it first; everything else is an overlay on it. The existing `LOT_CODES` chain (JC-541…) is the second backbone already in place — every capability wants to stamp its signal (terroir, soil, microclimate, pest history, water regime) onto the lot so it survives into the cup and the buyer-facing story.

Two forcing functions order the roadmap above pure ambition. First, EUDR is now live for coffee (large operators 30 Dec 2025, SMEs/micro-operators 30 Jun 2026): every kilo of green coffee into the EU needs a GPS plot polygon plus a deforestation-free declaration against the 31 Dec 2020 cutoff. For a green-coffee exporter, this is not a differentiator — it is table-stakes market access, and it is satisfied by the exact same geometry layer the agronomy features need. Second, Volcán's defining technical constraint is persistent cloud cover, which makes a naive optical-only NDVI tool useless half the year; Sentinel-1 SAR fusion plus an explicit data-confidence/freshness badge is non-negotiable and is the genuine differentiator over a generic ag-monitoring product. The $0-forever constraint is not a limitation here — it is a design discipline that the Copernicus free quota, MapLibre+PostGIS, Open-Meteo, BirdNET/AudioMoth, offline Pix4Dfields, and a one-time WeatherFlow Tempest station all satisfy without a recurring SaaS bill. The paid drone is one-time farm equipment, not subscription.

The decisive sequencing is a three-tier build on one shared spine. Tier 1 (foundation, build now): the GIS/geometry layer + EUDR/deforestation evidence export — this unblocks ten other capabilities and protects export revenue, so it is the single highest-leverage move in the whole product. Tier 2 (the closed-loop differentiators that ride on the geometry): satellite NDVI/NDRE+SAR monitoring, the per-plot hyperlocal weather + threat-alert engine, the IPM scouting/threshold engine (roya + broca), and phenology/GDD bloom-to-cherry prediction. These four share a common pattern that defines world-class for this pillar — forecast/observe → alert → auto-spawn a task into the existing Tasks board → log the action → feed the outcome back to tune the model. A passive map is the failure mode; an action loop is the bar. Tier 3 (the long-horizon estate intelligence and the conservation moat): ML yield forecasting by variety AND grade, prescriptive agronomy/renovation (rock-and-roll stumping, varietal-to-terroir, nursery demand back-planning), soil fertility on volcanic Andisols, IoT sensor nervous system, irrigation/deficit bloom-trigger, per-tree census, and the reserve biodiversity/carbon engine. The reserve capability is Janson's sharpest, least-copyable moat — almost no farm software treats a 200-ha quetzal reserve as a first-class mapped, bioacoustically-monitored, carbon-quantified asset, and it directly compounds the EUDR, Bird Friendly, and direct-trade-premium stories.

The tensions to resolve up front: (1) Many capabilities each propose their own `plot_geometry`/`plot_boundaries`/`plot_geometries` table — these MUST collapse into ONE canonical PostGIS geometry on the plot (plus `reserve_zones`), authored by a single owner, never forked (the project's `src/lib/**` contract already enforces single-source-of-truth discipline). (2) Soil tables, weather tables, NDVI/canopy tables, and phenology GDD tables are claimed by multiple capabilities; they must be designed once as shared time-series tables keyed by `plot_id`, not re-invented per feature. (3) Several capabilities want to drive `plot.status` (the healthy/watch/at-risk badge) from their signal — satellite vigor, IPM thresholds, soil scorecard, climate. The resolution is a single status-resolution layer that fuses inputs, not five writers racing each other. (4) Thresholds and agronomic targets (Bird Friendly 40% shade, coffee pH 5.0–5.5, broca 5%, frost <10°C) are "promises" that must live in one canonical, server-enforced store per the global promise-to-enforcement rule — not hardcoded in UI. Resolve these as contract decisions before fan-out, and the pillar composes cleanly.

**Top capabilities:**

| Capability | Priority | Opp. | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| Plot/parcel & land GIS + PostGIS geometry layer (the keystone) | differentiator | 10 | Eleven of thirteen capabilities are blocked on the same gap: today's Plot is flat scalars with no geometry or lat/lon. One canonical PostGIS polygon + elevation/slope/aspect DEM + the reserve as a first-class protected geometry is the single spine the entire pillar inherits — NDVI, weather pull, scouting maps, yield, soil, irrigation, per-tree census all clip to it. Highest leverage move in the product; build first and never fork it. |
| EUDR / deforestation-free origin geolocation export | table-stakes | 9 | Table-stakes for market access, not a nicety: coffee is live under EUDR (large operators Dec 2025, SMEs Jun 2026) — every kilo of green into the EU needs a GPS plot polygon + deforestation-free declaration vs the 31 Dec 2020 cutoff. It rides the exact same geometry layer as the GIS keystone, so it is nearly free once that exists, and it directly protects Janson's export revenue. Ship it bundled with Tier 1. |
| Satellite crop monitoring — NDVI/NDRE + Sentinel-1 SAR fusion | differentiator | 8 | Turns the hand-set healthy/watch/at-risk badge into a measured, sub-plot vigor read. The genuine differentiator is SAR fusion + an explicit data-confidence/freshness badge to survive Volcán's perpetual cloud cover — a naive optical-only tool is useless half the year here. NDRE specifically reads N/chlorophyll in dense shade canopy where plain NDVI saturates. Runs entirely on free Copernicus quota at this scale. Closes the loop: anomaly → GPS pin → auto-scouting task. |
| Weather stations & per-plot hyperlocal forecasting + threat-alert engine | differentiator | 9 | Replaces one farm-wide forecast strip with ten actionable per-plot reads tuned by a one-time Tempest station + free Open-Meteo/IMHPA. Frost/cold-stress on the highest Geisha (Las Lagunas 1,700m), afternoon rain on the drying patios, and leaf-wetness for roya are the real under-served threats — and they arrive on the crew lead's phone with hours of lead time, firing protect-tasks. The forecast feed is also a shared dependency for phenology, irrigation, and IPM. $0-recurring. |
| Pest & disease IPM — roya + broca scouting, thresholds, PHI/REI safety | differentiator | 9 | Roya and broca are the existential threats to high-altitude arabica, and a single bored/diseased cherry drags a $200+/lb Geisha micro-lot down a grade. Offline-first phone scouting + economic-threshold engine + PHI/REI-safe spray log (blocks harvest inside a withholding window) + certification-ready IPM history. Closes ground-truth → threshold → auto-drafted PHI-safe task. The biological/cultural-control bias fits the reserve brand and the Rainforest Alliance/organic premium. |
| Phenology & flowering→cherry tracking (GDD bloom prediction) | differentiator | 9 | Knows each plot's harvest date months ahead with a confidence band, re-forecasting as weather unfolds. Janson's altitude/cultivar spread means high Geisha matures weeks after low Caturra — a per-plot GDD model turns 'everything peaks at once' into a staggered, mill-capacity-aware pipeline and lets the commercial team commit flagship Geisha volumes to Best of Panama / direct buyers credibly. The flowering_event becomes the new T0 anchor the existing harvests/lot codes count forward from. |
| ML yield estimation & forecasting (green-kg by variety AND grade) | differentiator | 9 | Buyer-grade explainable P10/P50/P90 forecast that updates daily from flowering rainfall, satellite vigor, and realized harvests, and a Kalman-style in-season nowcast off the existing daily cherriesKg log. Must output green-kg by grade because Janson pre-sells specialty Geisha on committed volumes — and a few hundred kg of mis-forecast Geisha outweighs tons of commodity Caturra. SHAP 'why' panel earns trust; beats a naive last-year baseline measurably. |
| Agronomy advisory & crop planning — varietal/density/shade/renovation | differentiator | 9 | The prescriptive estate brain: renovate vs stump vs hold by tree-age + yield-decline + cup trend, varietal-to-terroir fit (Geisha to the highest mistiest parcels), rock-and-roll stumping so volume never craters, and a nursery seedling-demand forecast 12–18 months ahead. This is the La-Esmeralda-tier instinct made explicit and repeatable — directly tied to the cup quality and price that pay the bills. |
| Soil health & fertility — testing, amendment & fertilization plans | differentiator | 8 | Volcán's acidic, P-fixing volcanic Andisols make pH (target 5.0–5.5) and P availability THE soil lever for coffee here. LLM-parse IDIAP/CATIE lab PDFs into a 4R-aligned, variety-specific, rainfall-timed, costed plan; log every kg applied against plot/batch/applicator for organic/RA audit. Diagnoses at-risk plots like Paso Ancho with data instead of guesses. Over-fertilizing flattens the delicate Geisha cup, so per-varietal tuning matters. |
| Shade, agroforestry & biodiversity — the 200-ha reserve engine | differentiator | 9 | Janson's single least-copyable moat: almost no farm software treats a private quetzal reserve as a first-class mapped, bioacoustically-monitored (AudioMoth+BirdNET), carbon-quantified asset. Encodes Bird Friendly thresholds (40% shade, 10 species/ha, native majority), tags quetzal-food Ocotea/aguacatillo, and reconciles three evidence streams (satellite + acoustic + human). Canopy is also a direct Geisha cup-quality lever. Compounds EUDR, Bird Friendly, carbon-credit, and direct-trade premiums. |
| IoT soil & microclimate sensors — moisture/NPK/pH/leaf-wetness/temp | differentiator | 7 | A per-block agronomic nervous system over LoRaWAN (free Things Stack / pay-per-packet Helium) feeding the rust-risk engine its exact validated predictors (leaf-wetness duration + cardinal temp + RH), frost nowcasting, and irrigation tension thresholds. A dead node is itself an alert. Binds each lot's microclimate signature to its code for provenance. Powerful but a hardware/maintenance step beyond the software-only Tier 2 wins — sequence after the satellite+weather loop proves out. |
| Irrigation scheduling & water management/rights + deficit bloom-trigger | differentiator | 7 | Supplemental, strategic water on Barú's dry-season Pacific slope. The killer coffee-specific lever is regulated-deficit stress-and-flush to trigger a single uniform bloom (concentrates harvest, lifts Geisha cup). FAO-56 ET + soil tension drives per-zone prescriptions; metered abstraction reconciles against the ANATI/MiAMBIENTE concession for compliance and the reserve-watershed story. High value but depends on sensors + geometry + weather already being live. |
| Per-tree census & lifecycle + nursery propagation pipeline | future-bet | 7 | Promotes trees to first-class geotagged entities (replacing the round '14,800') with lifecycle/pruning history, gap maps, and a nursery seed-lot→germination→transplant→field-tree provenance chain. Flagship Geisha trees genuinely merit per-tree QR/RFID provenance the way no commodity crop would, and it underpins EUDR/RA audits and the rock-and-roll renovation rhythm. A future-leaning depth play once the estate-intelligence tier is delivering — highest data-capture cost per unit value. |

**Must-have integrations:** Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem — Sentinel-2 L2A (NDVI/NDRE) + Sentinel-1 SAR (cloud-penetrating radar fallback) + Statistical/Process API; the $0-native primary satellite source, PostGIS on the existing Supabase/Postgres — single canonical geometry store (plot polygons, reserve zones, GPS points, spatial queries); free, native, MapLibre GL + OpenStreetMap + Turf.js — open-source $0 vector basemap, glass map renderer, and client-side zonal stats, Copernicus DEM / NASA SRTM — 30m elevation for auto-derived slope/aspect/elevation-zone model (the terroir layer), EU TRACES / EUDR Information System + Global Forest Watch / JRC forest-baseline — regulatory GeoJSON geolocation + deforestation-free due-diligence target (live: coffee Dec 2025, SMEs Jun 2026), Open-Meteo (free, ERA5 historical + 16-day forecast, elevation param) + IMHPA/ETESA Panama hydromet — per-plot weather, GDD accumulation, ET0, and the blossom-trigger first-rain feed, WeatherFlow Tempest weather station — one-time hardware, no subscription; on-farm ground truth to calibrate the hyperlocal microclimate model (honors $0-recurring), LoRaWAN stack — The Things Stack (open-source/self-host) or Helium (no gateway capex) for soil/microclimate node ingestion via MQTT/webhook; Sensoterra/RAK/Sentek probes, Cropster (Origin/Cultivate) — pushes per-lot agronomy, soil, microclimate, NDVI, and predicted-vs-actual phenology into the cupping/QC/sales record the specialty trade runs on, keyed to the existing JC-xxx lot codes, World Coffee Research Variety Catalog + Verified Seed — cultivar agronomic/rust-resistance reference and seed-lot provenance for the varietal advisor and nursery registry, Smithsonian Bird Friendly + Rainforest Alliance Regenerative + USDA/EU Organic standards — encoded as compliance rule sets and audit-export schemas (the reserve/certification premium), AudioMoth + BirdNET (Cornell) / eBird / iNaturalist / GBIF — passive bioacoustic + observation pipeline for reserve biodiversity and quetzal-detection evidence, Pix4Dfields (offline laptop processing, $0/no-cloud) + DJI Mavic 3 Multispectral — one-time drone for orthomosaics, NDVI/NDRE, AI stand counts and high-res gap detection, Panama ANATI / MiAMBIENTE — cadastral finca tenure layer and water-concession (concesión de uso de agua) limits for ownership and abstraction compliance, Regional soil labs — IDIAP (Panama national) + CATIE — soil & leaf-tissue lab report ingestion (LLM PDF/CSV parse) against plot + depth for the fertility time series

**World-class bar:** A living digital twin of the finca-plus-reserve on ONE shared PostGIS geometry spine, where the operator works primarily on the map and tables are secondary. Every plot is a versioned polygon carrying its real elevation/slope/aspect, canopy structure, tree census, soil-carbon and microclimate time series; the 200-ha quetzal reserve is a first-class protected geometry, not a tagline. The healthy/watch/at-risk badge is COMPUTED — fused from satellite NDRE trend (SAR-gap-filled with an honest data-confidence badge so a cloud-blocked pixel is never passed off as healthy), IPM threshold breaches, soil scorecard, and per-plot climate — never hand-typed. The pillar runs as a closed loop, not a passive map: a frost warning, a roya spray-window, a declining sub-zone, or a slipping ripening date each auto-drops a GPS pin and spawns an assigned task into the existing Tasks board, the action is logged, and the outcome retrains the model so trust is earned season over season. Forecasts are buyer-grade and explainable — P10/P50/P90 green-kg by variety AND grade and a confidence-banded harvest date the commercial team can sign a Best-of-Panama or direct-trade contract against. Compliance is a byproduct of good agronomy, not a fire drill: one click emits an EUDR-grade polygon + deforestation-free statement, a Smithsonian Bird Friendly / Rainforest Alliance evidence pack, and a buyer-facing terroir-and-conservation story, every claim tracing to a timestamped, geotagged event. Thresholds and agronomic promises (40% shade, pH 5.0–5.5, 5% broca, frost <10°C) live in one canonical server-enforced store. And the whole thing runs inside the $0-forever envelope — free Copernicus/Open-Meteo/GFW/BirdNET, MapLibre+PostGIS, offline Pix4Dfields — with the only spend being one-time field hardware (a Tempest station, a multispectral drone), never a recurring SaaS bill. The benchmark is the instinct of a Hacienda La Esmeralda-tier estate made explicit, repeatable, and provable.

### Harvest & People

Harvest & People is the operational heart of Janson Coffee and the single pillar where cup quality, labor ethics, and margin are won or lost on the same mountain on the same morning. Seven capability investigations converge on one truth: the farm's entire premium rests on selective, ripe-only picking of staggered Geisha lots from 1,360–1,700 masl by a ~90% Ngäbe-Buglé seasonal migrant workforce paid by the lata — and today that whole chain runs on paper, verbal handoffs, and a thin read-only data model (a single `workers` table with one `attendance` enum, a free-text `crew` string, and `today_kg`; a `harvests` view that already carries picker/plot/ripeness/Brix/lotCode). The opportunity is to make the daily transaction — a picker, a plot, a weight, a ripeness — the genesis event that simultaneously creates traceability, pay, attendance, and compliance evidence, then lets everything upstream (mill intake, payroll, audit packs, cost-per-lot) derive from it without re-keying.

The pillar has a clear spine and a clear sequencing logic. The non-negotiable foundation is offline-first per-picker weigh capture: under-canopy at 1,690 masl with no signal, tap a badged picker, read a Bluetooth scale, attribute to plot and lot in under three seconds, queue locally, sync on reconnect. That single `weigh_event` is the atom three other capabilities consume. Built directly on it is crew dispatch (the 5am board that says who picks which plot) and harvest planning (the ripeness/GDD model that decides which plots are ready). These three — capture, dispatch, planning — form a tight feedback loop and should be treated as one integrated build, not three apps: the planner's ripeness board feeds the dispatch board, dispatch assigns crews to plots, capture grounds the maturation curve in real Brix and ripeness samples, and the cup score closes the loop on whether the cadence was right.

The second layer is the people-and-money system that the weigh events flow into: blended piece-rate + hourly payroll with the minimum-wage make-whole guard, statutory deductions (CSS/SIPE, Seguro Educativo, décimo), and multi-rail disbursement (Yappy/Nequi for the unbanked majority, ACH for staff, signed cash manifests). Riding alongside payroll are the three compliance capabilities — labor contracts, time & attendance, and worker training/safety — which share the same extended worker identity record and the same export-an-audit-pack endgame. These three overlap heavily and must be deduped into ONE worker system of record rather than three parallel modules: there is one worker identity, one attendance ledger, one certification ledger, and the contract/payroll/Rainforest-Alliance/GRASP exports are all views over it. The strategic insight is that compliance here is not overhead — it is a price lever: documented decent-work and fair-pay evidence is exactly what protects the Best-of-Panama Geisha premium under EU-CSDDD-style buyer due diligence.

Tensions resolved: (1) The dossier proposes piece-rate capture in three places (planning, crew mgmt, dedicated capture) and payroll in two — these are not five features, they are ONE capture surface feeding ONE payroll engine; the dedicated capture investigation is the canonical spec, the others consume it. (2) Compliance is split across contracts/attendance/training/payroll — collapse to one worker identity + one append-only attendance/event ledger with pluggable export schemas. (3) The $0 / no-database / read-only constraint of the current practice app means v1 ships as offline-first PWA capture with local IndexedDB and free-tier rails (Open-Meteo, WhatsApp Cloud API free tier, Web Bluetooth, Sentinel-2 free) — the hardware (FairPick/2nd Sight, NTEP scales, ZKTeco biometrics) is the production-grade target, prototyped against generic BLE scales and QR/NFC badges that survive an undocumented workforce. Decisive sequencing: build the weigh-event capture + worker-identity spine FIRST (it is load-bearing for everything), then dispatch + ripeness planning as the quality loop, then payroll, then layer the three compliance views — but design the worker identity and the append-only event ledger up front so the later layers don't fork the schema.

**Top capabilities:**

| Capability | Priority | Opp. | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| Offline-first per-picker weigh capture (the genesis event) | differentiator | 10 | This is the load-bearing atom of the entire pillar — a sub-3-second, signal-free weigh-in (badged picker + plot + lot + kg + ripeness) is simultaneously the first traceability link, the genesis pay event, and the input weight for mill intake. Three other capabilities (dispatch, payroll, planning calibration) are derived views over this single append-only weigh_event. Without it everything upstream re-keys paper. The current schema already carries picker/plot/ripeness/Brix/lotCode in the harvests view, so this turns a read-only daily aggregate into the live transactional source it should roll up from. The FairPick bar (offline sync, legal-for-trade weight, instant receipt, one-click payroll export) is the proven world-class reference. |
| Ripeness-driven harvest planning & pick-round (pasada) scheduler | differentiator | 9 | THE defining workflow for Janson's value proposition: the premium rests on Geisha picked at uniform 95–97% ripeness, and 10 plots across 340m of altitude ripen on staggered 8–10 day cycles. A per-plot maturation model (days-since-bloom + accumulated GDD + NDVI + trailing Brix/ripeness, all of which the existing data already signals via altitude/variety/shade) turns a daily walk-every-plot guess into one optimized pick sequence with a 10-day weather-aware re-plan, plus distinct rebusca/repela closing-pass tracking that resets flowering and suppresses berry borer. It feeds the dispatch board and closes the loop against cup scores so cadence compounds in quality season over season. |
| Morning crew dispatch & ripeness-aware routing | differentiator | 9 | At 5am the supervisor must put the right crew on the right plot before cherry over-ripens or rain hits — picking is the highest-cost, highest-quality-leverage step on a Panamanian farm. An AI-proposed dispatch plan keyed to overnight ripeness, each picker's historic kg/hr, weather, and inter-block travel, delivered as a phone-friendly card over WhatsApp, lifts kg/picker-day and guarantees best pickers hit peak Geisha first. It is the operational bridge between the planning model (which plots are ready) and the capture surface (who picked what), and it promotes today's free-text crew string into a real Crew/CrewMembership entity with field zones finer than a plot. |
| Blended piece-rate + hourly payroll with minimum-wage make-whole guard | table-stakes | 9 | One Sunday-night 'Lock & Pay' that turns the week's weigh-events and attendance into exact per-worker pay across three bases (per-kg cherry, daily, hourly), hard-enforces the Chiriquí agricultural minimum-wage top-up at the data layer (the single biggest labor-rights risk and the line every Fairtrade/RA audit checks), withholds CSS/Seguro Educativo/ISR/décimo, nets advances/fiado, locks an FX rate, and disburses via Yappy/Nequi/ACH/signed cash with a bilingual QR payslip. Because every weigh-event carries plotId and lotCode, labor cost attributes to cost-per-kg-of-green by lot — answering whether the low-yield high-value Geisha blocks actually pay for their pickers. Table-stakes legally, but world-class in dignity and cost intelligence. |
| Unified worker system of record — identity, contracts, attendance & compliance | table-stakes | 9 | Dedupes four overlapping investigations (labor contracts, time & attendance, seasonal/migrant contracts, and the worker half of training) into ONE canonical worker record that extends today's thin workers table: cédula/carné/no-ID, indigenous group, DOB (drives the minor-age gate), dependents, preferred language (Spanish/Ngäbere), housing/camp assignment, an append-only hash-chained attendance/punch ledger (geo + biometric-optional, offline-first), and digital por-obra contracts. Every downstream artifact — SIPE planilla, finiquito, Rainforest Alliance/GRASP audit pack — is a requirement-mapped export over the same data. One-tap seasonal rehire treats the Ngäbe-Buglé workforce as named returning partners, not anonymous day labor. Legally mandatory and the protector of the certification premium. |
| Worker training, safety & certification-gated work | differentiator | 8 | Differentiator because it physically gates hazard work: the task assigner refuses to send anyone to spray whose pesticide cert, annual medical, or cholinesterase test isn't green, offering the nearest qualified substitute — hard enforcement, not an honor system. Pictographic microlearning pushed to phones in Spanish/Ngäbere with logged acknowledgement inducts an 80-person crew in days; an expiry radar auto-schedules refreshers and clinic visits; one incident entry auto-populates the CSS R.A.T.E.P. form plus a corrective toolbox talk. Turns the steep-slope, machete/snakebite/agrochemical reality of Volcán and the variable-literacy migrant workforce into documented evidence that becomes a marketable brand proof point alongside lot-to-cup traceability. |

**Must-have integrations:** WhatsApp Business Cloud API (free tier) — the channel this Ngäbe-Buglé/Spanish workforce and crew leads actually use: dispatch cards, bilingual QR payslips, pictographic safety induction, grievance intake, attendance/payslip notifications, Yappy (Banco General) + Nequi mobile-money rails, plus Banco General/Nacional ACH batch files — same-day digital disbursement to a largely unbanked workforce, with a signed cash manifest fallback, Web Bluetooth API + generic BLE / NTEP-certified scales (FairPick / 2nd Sight / Dinamica Generale as the production hardware target) — auto-tared, dispute-proof weight at hillside collection points; QR/NFC (MIFARE/NTAG) picker badges for tap-to-identify a no-cédula workforce, Caja de Seguro Social (CSS) SIPE planilla electrónica + MITRADEL Código de Trabajo contract templates + Executive Decree No.13/2025 Chiriquí agricultural minimum-wage tables (machine-readable) + DGI ISR/Seguro Educativo — the statutory spine for payroll, contracts, and the wage-floor make-whole guard, Open-Meteo / Visual Crossing weather API (free tier) + on-farm station (Davis WeatherLink / Arable) for per-plot GDD, and Sentinel-2 via Sentinel Hub (free) for NDVI/vigor — the inputs to the per-plot maturation/ripeness-window model, Cropster (Origin / C-sar) — push verified picker/field/cherry deliveries and pull cupping scores to close the round-timing→cup-quality loop and carry labor cost into the green-coffee cost basis; fills the capture-layer gap left by Cropster discontinuing its standalone Origin app, Rainforest Alliance 2020/2026 Standard (Social Annex, HRDD/child-labor toolkit) + GLOBALG.A.P. GRASP + Fairtrade living-wage (Anker, rural Chiriquí) — the requirement schemas the one-click audit/evidence-pack export maps to, and the premium-price justification, Offline-first stack: PWA service worker + IndexedDB/Dexie (or Capacitor SQLite) with conflict-free / last-write-wins per-event sync, syncing to the existing free-tier Supabase Postgres at the beneficio's connectivity point — honors the $0 constraint, Brix/biometric capture hardware: Atago / DiFluid R2 Bluetooth refractometer for direct sugar sampling per round; ZKTeco/Suprema or on-device ML Kit/Apple Vision face match for offline, privacy-preserving identity of illiterate/undocumented workers, Thermal ESC/POS / Bluetooth Zebra receipt printers + Twilio SMS fallback — instant Spanish picker receipts where data coverage dies

**World-class bar:** Zero-paper, offline-first, dignity-first: at 5am an AI-proposed dispatch plan — keyed to which plots hit peak ripeness overnight from a per-plot GDD/NDVI maturation model that auto-re-plans around a rain front — is on every supervisor's $120 Android with no bars of signal. Every can is weighed in under 3 seconds against a badged picker, a geofenced plot, and a lot code, becoming one immutable append-only event that is at once the first link in the cup's traceability story, the genesis pay line, the attendance proof, and the mill-intake input weight — synced conflict-free the moment a bar appears. Pickers see their running kilos and earnings live in Spanish/Ngäbere and trust the number. Payroll is a single 'Lock & Pay' that computes blended piece-rate/hourly pay, hard-tops-up anyone below the Chiriquí agricultural minimum (itemized for audit), withholds CSS/Seguro Educativo/ISR/décimo, locks FX, nets advances, and disburses same-day via Yappy/Nequi/ACH/signed cash with a bilingual QR payslip. Every seasonal worker is registered once in under two minutes, signs a contract they understand, is inducted by a 4-minute pictographic video before touching a plot, and can never be assigned spray work without a green cert/medical/cholinesterase. Any sampled worker file or whole-farm evidence pack — contracts, attendance, wage-floor proof, training, remediation cases — is one click and passes a MITRADEL inspection and a Rainforest Alliance/GRASP audit on the same data. Labor cost attributes back to cost-per-kg-of-green per lot, the rebusca is tracked to closure on every plot, and each round's timing is validated against its cup score so cadence compounds in quality season over season. It feels like Cropster Harvest fused with a vineyard's GDD pick-date model and FairPick's weigh-station, tuned for Volcán microclimates and a $22/day reality — and it treats the Ngäbe-Buglé workforce as named, returning partners with portable reputation, not anonymous day labor.

### Process & Quality

Process & Quality is the pillar where Janson's money is actually made or lost. Their economics are not commodity volume — they are reserve Geisha, Pacamara and Catuaí micro-lots grown at 1,350–1,700 masl that price on cup score, where Best of Panama lots have cleared from ~$800/lb to a 2025 record above $13,000/lb and a 1–2 point swing is the line between a record auction lot and commercial green. Every capability in this dossier (wet milling/fermentation, drying, lot genealogy, QC/cupping, green inventory, dry milling, roasting, sample/offer management) converges on a single thesis: turn the farm's tacit, notebook-bound craft into a versioned, sensor-grounded, certification-ready record that both PROTECTS the cup (don't lose a $100+/lb lot to a missed ferment cut point or a rain-soaked patio) and PROVES it (an unbroken, tamper-evident passport from tree block to the bag a roaster scans). The current app embodies the exact problem this pillar fixes: one opaque `ProcessingBatch` with a flat `cherry→...→green` stage enum, a single scalar `moisturePct`, a free-text `patio` string, and a pipeline that literally dead-ends at `green` — the moment the coffee becomes a sellable asset, it vanishes from the system.
