The unifying spine across all eight capabilities is the lot genealogy graph: an immutable cherry→parchment→green DAG where every split, merge, ferment, dry, mill, cup, store, sample and roast event hangs off the existing `lotCode` (JC-541, JC-564, JC-602…) with mass carried forward on every edge. This is the non-negotiable foundation — without it, each downstream module re-invents traceability and they drift apart. On top of the spine sit two enforcement gates that turn quality promises into data-layer guarantees rather than honor systems: the fermentation cut-point engine (recipe-driven predicted wash time + alerts so a dusk-landed Geisha is never over-fermented) and the drying reposo gate (a lot physically cannot advance to milling until both moisture stability in the 10.5–11.5% band AND minimum rest days are met). These two gates, plus the QC quarantine hold, are where Janson's premium is operationally defended.

The decisive sequencing insight is that this is a deliberately serialized lane, not a fan-out. Drying is the specialty maxim's "where the most quality is lost," fermentation is "where the premium is decided," and green inventory is the literal revenue asset that today has no system at all — these are the differentiators. But genealogy must land FIRST as the contract every other module binds to (and it requires extending the frozen `src/lib/types.ts` and the processing pipeline model — strictly one author, per the schema-author rule). Within a $0/no-DB practice repo, "sensor-grounded" begins as fast manual-entry time-series (pH/temp/Brix readings, moisture meter taps) plotted against target curves — the data model is designed for BLE/Modbus/LoRaWAN probe streams later, but manual capture delivers 90% of the value immediately and costs nothing. Cropster is the gravitational center of every integration list (it is what the roasters and auctions Janson sells into already standardize on), so Cropster-compatible export schemas should shape the data model from day one even before any live API is wired.

The tension to resolve decisively: roasting and sample/offer management are genuinely high-value (roasting is the literal payoff of "from our farm to your cup since 1990," and samples ARE the entire sales engine for the high-value tier) — but they live downstream of green inventory and belong to the commercial/sell-side story more than the make-quality story. Sequence them after the make-and-prove chain is solid: fermentation and drying protect the cup, genealogy + QC + green inventory prove and price it, then dry milling finishes it to grade, and only then do roasting and sample-shipping monetize it. Building roasting before green inventory exists would once again leave the asset un-tracked between stages. The world-class bar for the whole pillar is a closed loop: cup scores feed back to ferment recipes and plot agronomy so the protocol library measurably improves every harvest, and every bag carries a QR passport good enough for an importer audit, an EUDR/Rainforest/organic certification, and a Best of Panama entry — all generated, never hand-typed.

**Top capabilities:**

| Capability | Priority | Opp. | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| Lot & batch genealogy (cherry→parchment→green DAG with mass-carry) | table-stakes | 9 | The non-negotiable spine every other Process & Quality capability binds to. The current app has a single opaque ProcessingBatch keyed loosely on lotCode; without an explicit merge/split graph with kg carried on each edge, ferment, drying, milling, cupping, inventory, roasting and sample modules each re-invent traceability and drift apart. Lands first as the contract (extends the frozen src/lib/types.ts — single schema author). Delivers auto lot-code minting on cherry intake, mass-balance reconciliation (~5.5:1 cherry:green with anomaly flags), and a buyer/auditor passport (QR + EUDR geolocation) that the whole pillar reuses. |
| Fermentation & wet-milling batch tracker with recipe library + cut-point alerts | differentiator | 9 | Where the premium is decided. A dusk-landed high-Brix Geisha over-ferments and a $100+/lb lot is vaporized; cool Volcán nights make generic clock-timing wrong, so recipes must be tuned to altitude/ambient temp. Replaces the single 'fermentation' stage dot with per-vessel records, a versioned SOP library (e.g. 'Geisha 72h anaerobic, cut at pH 3.8'), live pH/temp/Brix curves (manual taps now, BLE/MQTT probes later), a predicted wash time, and alerts before the window closes. The eco-mill water log (~0.2 L/kg vs ~20 L/kg) turns sustainability into a sellable, certifiable claim. This is a top differentiator. |
| Drying management with reposo gate (curves, stations, weather-coupled alerts) | differentiator | 9 | The specialty maxim: drying is where the most quality is lost. At Volcán's cool, humid, rain-prone 1,350–1,700 masl, drying runs 15–30+ days and is constantly threatened by reabsorption. Turns the scalar moisturePct into an auditable curve against a method-specific target band (10.5–11.5%), replaces the free-text patio string with first-class capacity-tracked stations (raised beds/patios/parabolic/Guardiola), pushes weather-driven cover/move alerts before a rain cell, and enforces the reposo gate — a lot CANNOT advance to milling until moisture stability AND minimum rest days are met (a promise enforced at the data layer, not on trust). Differentiator that protects reserve cup scores. |
| QC & cupping — SCA CVA + legacy forms, calibration, defect grading, quarantine | differentiator | 9 | This is the pricing engine for Janson's entire economic model — it decides which lots go to the Best of Panama auction vs commercial channels, and a 1–2 point swing is dollars per pound. Delivers the current SCA Coffee Value Assessment (CVA-103/104) plus the legacy 100-point form (market is mid-transition, buyers ask for both), blind/randomized session management, a cupper-drift calibration engine (mirrors the Q-grader exam), green-grading defect counts on a 350g sample with auto SCA grade, a QC-hold quarantine that blocks a tainted lot from sale, and — critically — binds every score back through lotCode to its ferment time, drying curve and plot, closing the cup-to-cause loop that improves agronomy and recipes. Differentiator. |
| Green coffee inventory & warehousing (lot/grade/screen/moisture, available-to-promise) | table-stakes | 9 | The linchpin between processing and revenue, and today the single stage with NO system — the pipeline literally ends at the green batch and the asset vanishes. Janson sells specialty green; the bodega IS the inventory of the business. Auto-promotes a batch to a tracked, graded, located, valued GreenLot the moment it rests stable; answers 'how many kg of Geisha Washed screen 17+ at 86+ are available and where?' in seconds; watches moisture/aw drift and freshness windows; allocates available-to-promise against orders so a scarce micro-lot is never double-sold; tracks every gram (moves, blends, samples, shrink). Table-stakes because nothing downstream (sampling, sales, roasting) can be honest without it. |
| Dry milling — hulling, screen/density/optical grading with outturn mass balance | differentiator | 8 | Where Janson's grade and final money are decided: as an SHB/EP estate, screen-size and defect-prep command the premium and the right to enter Best of Panama, and a 4-point outturn swing on Geisha at 5–50x commodity is enormous money. Explodes the single opaque 'milled' stage into a machine-pass chain (huller→polisher→screen grader→gravity table→optical sorter) each logging in/out/reject kg for a closed mass balance, a pre-mill readiness gate (won't hull out-of-spec parchment), an SCA/SCAP grade classifier, peaberry/caracol separation, byproduct (cascara/pasilla) ledger, and clean lot splits (export 17/18 + pasilla local). Strong differentiator, sequenced after green inventory exists to receive its output. |
| Roasting — versioned golden roast profiles, live curve logging, yield economics | differentiator | 8 | The literal payoff of vertical integration and the brand promise — 'from our farm to your cup' is only true if the farm controls the roast. Today the pipeline dead-ends at green; roasting is the missing link that turns green into the product Janson actually sells on jansoncoffee.com and wholesale. Delivers a versioned golden-curve library, live BT/ET/RoR capture (free Artisan/.alog import is the $0 path; Modbus/PID later), profile-vs-target deviation alerts that catch an off-spec roast at the roaster not in the cup, roast-loss/shrinkage and cost-per-roasted-kg economics for real pricing, and roast-to-SKU traceability for the per-bag QR. Differentiator, but downstream of green inventory — build after the make-and-prove chain. |
| Sample & offer management — buyer sample tracking, customs, offer portal, deal pipeline | future-bet | 8 | Samples ARE the entire sales engine for the high-value tier: Geisha/competition micro-lots are tiny (<30 bags) and priced by the score a buyer assigns, so a single misplaced sample is a lost five-figure deal. Delivers a sample registry with the industry sample-type taxonomy (Type/Offer/Stocklot/PSS/Arrival), a draw-down ledger that decrements real green inventory (never double-offer a scarce lot), carrier + customs-doc generation with delivery tracking, a deal/offer Kanban (offered→sent→cupping→approved→contracted), a buyer CRM, and a branded self-serve offer portal with per-lot QR stories. Future-bet for sequencing: it depends entirely on green inventory + QC scores being live first, so it lands last in the pillar. |

**Must-have integrations:** Cropster (Origin / Cup / Roast / Hub) — the specialty-coffee standard the roasters and auctions Janson sells into already run; bidirectional lot, process-log, cupping-session, green-inventory and roast-profile sync. Design Cropster-compatible export schemas from day one even before a live API key., SCA standards suite — Coffee Value Assessment (SCA-102/103/104, 2023) + legacy 100-point form, Arabica Green Coffee Grading / Defect Handbook (defect-count rule engine), and ISO 6673/11294 moisture/water-activity methods., CQI Q-grader licensing & calibration framework (now an SCA program) — cupper identity and drift-calibration logic., Best of Panama / SCAP auction lot-entry + scoresheet format — Janson's flagship premium Geisha sales channel., Lab & field metering — Atago/Hanna/Sinar/DICKEY-john/Aqua-Boy refractometers, pH meters, moisture meters and AquaLab/Novasina water-activity meters, captured via Bluetooth/serial (manual entry is the $0 fallback that ships first)., Open-Meteo / OpenWeather / Tomorrow.io plus Panama ETESA-IMHPA — hyperlocal Volcán forecasts for fermentation-rate context and drying cover/move alerts (Open-Meteo is the free tier)., QR / GS1 Digital Link + DataMatrix lot coding (free `qrcode` lib + Zebra/Brother label printers) — the per-lot traceability passport on every bag., EU EUDR geolocation export, Rainforest Alliance, USDA/EU Organic, Bird Friendly and Alliance for Water Stewardship — certification/audit evidence fields baked into the passport (the quetzal-reserve + eco-mill water story made verifiable)., Artisan (open-source, .alog import) for $0 roast-curve capture, with Modbus TCP/RTU + PID/thermocouple DAQ as the later hardware lane., Shopify / WooCommerce (jansoncoffee.com) for roast-dated SKU push + finished-goods decrement, and DHL/FedEx (via EasyPost/Shippo) + US FDA Prior Notice/APHIS phytosanitary doc generation for sample shipping — sell-side, wired after green inventory is live.

**World-class bar:** A reserve Geisha is never a black box and never lost: it ferments against a versioned, altitude-tuned recipe with a model-predicted cut point and an alert before the window closes; it dries on the right station along a continuous moisture/water-activity curve, weather-shielded, and is physically blocked from milling by a reposo gate until moisture stability and minimum rest days are met; it is cupped on the current SCA CVA by a drift-calibrated panel with every score bound back to its ferment time, drying curve and plot (the cup-to-cause loop that makes quality engineered, not discovered); it becomes a gram-accurate, graded, located, valued green lot the instant it rests stable, answerable to a buyer in seconds and never double-sold; it is hulled and sorted to an SHB/EP screen-17/18 grade with a closed mass balance; and — when the farm chooses — roasted to a locked golden profile and shipped as a tracked sample with customs papers. Through every hop, an immutable cherry-to-green genealogy graph carries mass forward, and the finished bag bears a QR passport — tree block, altitude, harvest day and Brix, ferment recipe and pH/temp curve, water-per-kg, drying curve, hull date, screen split, defect count, SCA grade, cup score and operator — good enough for an importer audit, an EUDR/Rainforest/organic certificate, and a Best of Panama entry, all generated automatically and flowing straight into Cropster. World-class is repeatable, sensor-grounded, certification-ready process that turns the 200-hectare quetzal reserve's terroir from an unverified marketing claim into a documented, defensible, sellable price.

### Supply Chain & Logistics

Supply Chain & Logistics is the downstream half of Janson's seed-to-cup spine, and the existing schema makes the strategy unambiguous: the `lots` table already threads a single traceability code (JC-541, JC-564...) from `harvests` through `processing_batches`, the pipeline visibly dead-ends at the `green` stage, and `shipment` is already an `activity_kind` enum value with no backing tables. The system was architected to close this loop — this pillar finishes it. The unifying thesis across all seven capability investigations is the same: provenance is not a compliance chore, it is the product's premium. Janson is a single-origin Panama estate growing Geisha — the one variety whose entire market is built on origin trust, where Best of Panama lots fetch $40-300/lb and a single 24-bag container can be worth six figures. Because Janson controls every custody event in-house (grow → wet/dry mill → green → sell), its chain of custody can be complete and uncontested end to end, unlike a co-op buying cherry from many smallholders. The 200-hectare quetzal nature reserve is a genuine, ready-made deforestation-free + biodiversity asset that is simultaneously gold for EUDR compliance and for premium marketing.

The seven investigations cluster into three layers that must be sequenced, not built parallel-equal. Layer 1 is the LEDGER: a canonical, append-only `lot_events` chain of custody that extends the existing lot backbone forward (rest → mill → grade → bag → ship) plus the `lot_lineage` split/merge/blend graph with mass-balance. Everything else hangs off this — there is no point generating an EUDR DDS, an export document pack, a QR passport, or a storage certificate if they each re-key from a different source. The dossier is emphatic and consistent that the world-class bar is ZERO double-entry: the mill operator's stage update IS the provenance record, and the auction lot sheet, the EUDR statement, the customs binder, the QR page, and the storage certificate ALL generate from the same events. Layer 2 is COMPLIANCE & DOCUMENTS, which is genuinely existential rather than nice-to-have — EUDR (EU market access, delisting risk for 2025+ shipments), Panama MIDA/DECA phytosanitary, ICO certificate of origin, and US FDA Prior Notice/ISF. A single lapsed phytosanitary cert or missing ICO cachet can strand a container worth more than a month of payroll. Layer 3 is the PHYSICAL FLOW (input inventory feeding cost-of-production, storage/environment monitoring protecting cup score, and freight/3PL/DTC fulfillment moving the coffee), which delivers margin truth and operational resilience.

I am resolving two tensions decisively. First, export-document generation appears in THREE investigations (logistics freight, dedicated export-documentation, distribution 3PL). These are ONE capability — a single Shipment/ExportLot object with a document-pack generator and a per-destination compliance checklist — not three. I consolidate them into "Shipment & export-document engine," with DTC/3PL last-mile as a distinct lighter capability layered on top once the export core exists. Second, input inventory and procurement & supplier management overlap heavily (input SKU master, stock ledger, reorder points, the agrochemical compliance guard, input-to-lot cost roll-up). I treat these as ONE capability ("Input inventory, procurement & per-lot cost") — the procurement workflow (requisition → PO → goods-receipt → 3-way match) is the front of the same pipe whose back end is the perpetual-inventory ledger and the cost-per-kg-green roll-up. The single most important cross-cutting principle the dossier surfaces repeatedly: the agrochemical compliance guard (expiry, REI/PHI, certification-prohibited, MRL) must be enforced at the DATA LAYER on the issue/purchase write path, never an honor system in the UI — a banned active ingredient on a Geisha block disqualifies the lot and wipes the premium that funds the farm.

On the $0/no-CI/no-paid-service constraint that governs this repo: most of this pillar is achievable on free tiers. The Lot Passport, lineage graph, QR generation, EUDR geolocation pack, and export documents are pure Postgres + PostGIS + app logic. Storage monitoring runs on on-farm LoRaWAN (The Things Network free community tier + a one-time gateway, no per-sensor recurring cost). Optional DLT tamper-evidence uses OpenTimestamps (free, anchors a daily Merkle root to Bitcoin — anchoring only, no per-record gas). The genuinely paid/hardware-dependent pieces (live carrier rate-shopping via EasyPost, freight-forwarder booking APIs, reefer telemetry loggers, IoT tank sensors) belong to later phases and should be modeled as integration seams now but stubbed with mock data until real volume justifies them — consistent with the app's current mock-data, read-view posture.

**Top capabilities:**

| Capability | Priority | Opp. | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| Lot Passport — append-only chain-of-custody ledger + lineage graph | differentiator | 10 | The spine the entire pillar hangs off. Promotes the existing lots/harvests/processing_batches backbone into a canonical, timestamped, tamper-evident lot_events timeline (pick → pulp → ferment → dry → rest → mill → grade → bag → ship) plus a lot_lineage parent/child graph with mass-balance so a roasted bag always resolves to its true green parent(s) and 'more sold than produced' is impossible. Every downstream artifact (EUDR DDS, export docs, QR page, storage cert, cost roll-up) generates from THESE events — without it, every other capability re-keys from a different source and the zero-double-entry promise collapses. Pure Postgres, fully $0-compatible. |
| Public provenance microsite + dynamic QR / serialized GS1 Digital Link | differentiator | 9 | Turns the ledger into the price premium itself. A buyer scans the QR on a 30kg GrainPro/jute bag (or a serialized retail bag) and gets, in under a second, a verifiable liquid-glass origin story: GPS-mapped plot at 1,550 masl, varietal, process, full drying curve, Q-grader 90+ cupping score, EUDR attestation, certifications, and the quetzal-reserve conservation claim. This is the literal mechanism that justifies $40-300/lb and drives re-orders. Serialized GS1 Digital Link codes add per-unit anti-counterfeit protection for high-value Geisha. Pure app + PostGIS/Mapbox, $0-compatible; scan_events also feed fraud/engagement analytics. |
| EUDR deforestation due-diligence + compliance status board | differentiator | 9 | Existential and time-sensitive: EU buyers require deforestation-free compliance on 2025+ shipments or Janson gets delisted from European market access. Bundles geolocated plot polygons (GeoJSON), a deforestation-free-since-2020 declaration, satellite NDVI baseline evidence, and a TRACES-NT Due Diligence Statement reference tied to the exact lot. Janson's 200-ha reserve makes this a one-click win rather than a scramble. Pairs with a per-destination compliance checklist + deadline alerts (phyto cert validity, FDA Prior Notice, ISF 48h cutoff, ICO 4-year retention) so a lapsed stamp never strands a six-figure container. |
| Shipment & export-document engine (Sales Order → Shipment → doc pack) | differentiator | 9 | Consolidates THREE overlapping investigations (freight, export-documentation, distribution) into one capability. Converts a confirmed sale into a Shipment/ExportLot object (which lots, bag count/type, net+gross kg, Incoterm, buyer, price) and one-click generates the full validated dossier — commercial invoice, packing list, ICO certificate of origin (ICC-102-9), certificate of origin (Form A/EUR.1), MIDA phytosanitary request, bill of lading — pre-filled from lot data with zero re-keying. Activates the dormant 'shipment' activity_kind. The Incoterms landed-cost waterfall makes margin-per-lot a button. Core doc generation is $0; live carrier/forwarder booking APIs are a later integration seam. |
| Input inventory, procurement & per-lot cost (with compliance guard) | differentiator | 8 | Consolidates input-inventory and procurement&supplier into one pipe: requisition → price-checked PO → goods-receipt (batch/expiry) → 3-way match on the front, perpetual multi-location stock ledger (bodega/mill/fuel tanks) with offline mobile vale-digital drawdown on the back, every issue allocated to plot/task/lot so input cost rolls into true cost-per-kg-green. The non-negotiable core is the agrochemical compliance guard enforced at the DATA LAYER — block issuing/ordering any product that is expired, past REI/PHI, or prohibited under the lot's certification/MRL regime. A banned active ingredient on a Geisha block disqualifies the lot and destroys the premium. Fuel tracking keeps the diesel-dependent mill/dryers running through peak harvest. |
| Storage conditions & controlled-environment monitoring | differentiator | 7 | In Volcán's cloud-forest microclimate, the same humidity that grows world-class Geisha is the single biggest threat to stored green. Models storage locations (closing the gap where the model dead-ends at drying), streams per-zone temp/RH/aw against target bands (0.45-0.55 aw, 50-60% RH), fires phone/WhatsApp alerts on drift or dehumidifier/genset loss, logs per-lot aw trajectory, and runs FEFO rotation. A single humid week can drop a lot two cup points = four-to-five-figure loss. The tamper-evident storage certificate per lot_code becomes a documented, monetizable part of the provenance story. Fully $0-achievable on on-farm LoRaWAN (The Things Network free tier + one gateway). |
| DTC & 3PL last-mile fulfillment (roast-date FEFO, multi-carrier, DDP) | future-bet | 7 | The roasted-side commercial channel layered on top of the export core: jansoncoffee.com DTC orders auto-allocate the freshest FEFO bag inside the roast cure window, multi-carrier rate-shop (EasyPost/Shippo) the cheapest service that still arrives fresh, print labels, charge DDP landed cost at checkout (Zonos/Avalara) so parcels clear customs, and stream live tracking to a branded page with one-click claims. Protects the freshness promise that justifies specialty pricing and removes the customs friction that kills international DTC. Lower sequencing priority — depends on real roasted-sales volume and paid carrier/3PL APIs, so model the seams now and stub until volume justifies. |

**Must-have integrations:** Cropster (Origin + Cup + Roast) — the specialty-coffee industry standard; import/export lot, drying-curve and cupping data via API/CSV so Janson plugs into roasters' existing workflows, EU TRACES-NT — Due Diligence Statement submission returning a reusable DDS reference, plus the EUDR geolocation GeoJSON standard for plot polygons, GS1 standards — GTIN/SSCC + GS1 Digital Link (a 2D barcode that is both scannable and a resolvable URL) for serialized consumer units and pallet/parcel traceability, ICO (International Coffee Organization) — exporter ICO code + ICC-102-9 certificate of origin e-issuance, Panama MIDA / DECA / AUPSA — phytosanitary (fitosanitario) certificate issuance, AUPSA pesticide registry for legal-product/restricted-use validation, and the SIGA/VUCE single window; HS 0901 classification, PostGIS + Mapbox/Leaflet — plot polygons for the public provenance page and the EUDR geolocation file (in-repo, $0), Satellite NDVI / deforestation baseline providers (Sentinel-2 via Sentinel Hub, or Global Forest Watch) — evidence plot polygons predate the 2020 EUDR cutoff, QuickBooks Online (or Xero) — purchase orders, bills, AP/3-way-match, COGS sync so on-hand inventory and per-lot input cost reconcile to the farm's books, LoRaWAN temp/RH/aw sensor stack (Milesight/Aranet/Monnit) over The Things Network free community tier + one on-farm gateway — $0 recurring storage-environment telemetry; AquaLab/Sinar benchtop meters for per-lot water-activity readings, WhatsApp Business API + Twilio — reorder, low-stock, storage-excursion, and shipment-exception alerts to phones (the de-facto channel with rural Panama suppliers, caretakers, and crew), Zebra/Brother thermal label printing (ZPL) — QR + serialized 2D bag labels and hangtags, Multi-carrier shipping + freight-forwarder booking (EasyPost/Shippo for DHL/FedEx/UPS/USPS/Correos de Panamá; Flexport or a forwarder/NVOCC + container-visibility API like Terminal49/project44 for ocean export out of Balboa/Manzanillo) — later-phase, paid; model the seam now, Zonos / Avalara AvaTax Cross-Border — DDP landed-cost, duties/VAT, and HS-code classification for international DTC, OpenTimestamps (Bitcoin) — optional, $0 daily Merkle-root anchoring for tamper-evident provenance (anchoring only, no per-record gas), Open-Meteo / OpenWeather — ambient weather to time fertilization/spray windows and correlate outdoor humidity spikes with indoor storage drift

**World-class bar:** A green buyer or roaster scans the QR on a Janson bag and, in under one second, sees a verifiable, tamper-evident, beautifully rendered story generated entirely from a single append-only event ledger: the exact GPS-mapped plot at 1,550 masl, the trees' establishment year, the pick date and Brix, the picker, the ferment and full drying curve, the unbroken post-drying temp/RH/aw chart with every excursion explained, the Q-grader's 90+ cupping score, the EUDR deforestation-free attestation, the certifications, and the quetzal-reserve conservation claim — every fact anchored to a record they can independently verify (and, for the skeptical, a public-ledger timestamp proving nothing was edited after the sale). Internally it is ZERO double-entry: the mill operator's stage update and the bodeguero's mobile drawdown ARE the provenance and cost records; the auction lot sheet, the EUDR DDS, the customs binder, the QR passport, the storage certificate, and the per-kg-green cost breakdown all generate from the same events. The moment a lot is sold, the system knows the destination market, generates a complete validated document pack, files the electronic submissions, tracks every deadline, and books the cheapest compliant sailing — the export coordinator reviews and approves instead of chasing paper. Compliance is enforced at the data layer (expiry, REI/PHI, certification/MRL prohibitions block the issue or the PO; a lapsed cert blocks the shipment), so an audit is a one-click export. Every gram of fertilizer and liter of diesel rolls into a defensible landed cost-per-kg-green per micro-lot. Provenance stops being a compliance chore and becomes the product's premium — the reason a roaster pays $300/lb, trusts the score will arrive intact, and re-orders.

### Commerce & Customers

Commerce & Customers is the pillar that monetizes the entire farm — and for Janson it is not a back-office add-on, it is the literal business model. Janson grows, harvests, runs its own wet/dry mill, and sells both specialty green and roasted coffee; the existing app stops at the mill door, hinting at sales only through a lone hand-keyed `season_summary.ytd_revenue_usd` and a `'shipment'` activity kind. The decisive architectural insight, which all six capability investigations independently converged on, is that the existing `lots(code)` spine — already shared by `harvests` and `processing_batches`, with a `batch_stage` enum that terminates at `'green'` — is the natural commercial backbone. Every capability here is the same one-link extension: promote a finished green-stage `processing_batch` into a sellable `green_lot`, then thread that lot through offer lists, quotes, contracts, samples, fixation, shipment docs, and contract-to-cash. The result is a single tree-to-cash thread where a roaster in Tokyo can trace a contracted bag back to "Tizingal Alto, Block A, Geisha, 1,650 masl, 24 Brix" inside the quetzal reserve. This shared `green_lot` table is the keystone — it must be built first and owned by a single schema author, because all seven commerce capabilities and the AI layer depend on it.

Janson straddles two pricing regimes that this pillar must serve simultaneously, and conflating them is the single biggest strategic trap. The commercial-grade Caturra/Catuaí/Typica volume is C-correlated, exposed to ICE Coffee 'C' (KC) swings, and wants hedging/fixation against a live futures curve and differential math. The crown jewels — high-altitude Geisha and Pacamara — are sold at Best of Panama / Cup of Excellence auctions and direct-trade reserve prices that are fully decoupled from the commodity index (2025 BOP washed Geisha set a world record at $30,204/kg; Janson sits in the exact Volcán terroir). A generic commodity tool would wrongly anchor the Geisha to the C and leave one-to-three orders of magnitude on the table; a generic farm tool ignores hedging entirely. The pricing engine therefore must do both: hedge the commodity tail against ICE, and price the reserve lots on cup score, scarcity, and auction comps. This dual-regime pricing engine is the connective tissue beneath wholesale contracts, the auction module, quotes, and even DTC scarcity pricing — it should be modeled as one shared service, not re-implemented per surface.

Three distinct sales motions stack on this spine, and they are deliberately sequenced. The B2B green-coffee thread (offer list to contract to fixation to shipment docs to payment) is where the farm makes most of its money today and is the highest-leverage, build-first lane — and the auction/marketplace module is its single highest-multiplier sibling, because the gap between selling a competition-grade Geisha as commodity green versus on the Best of Panama block is literally a 100-1000x price difference. The DTC roasted-bag storefront plus subscriptions is the second motion: it captures full retail margin, turns the farm's traceability data into the marketing asset (QR-to-lot-story), and converts the customer relationship into a first-party owned asset and predictable recurring revenue — all buildable on a true $0 headless stack (Next.js + Supabase + Stripe). The on-site POS / agro-tourism café is the third: it closes cherry-to-cash at the gate, monetizes the reserve experience, and is the top of the funnel for the higher-margin online channel via QR-to-reorder. CRM and quotes/e-signature are not separate products but the connective relationship-and-paper layer running underneath all three motions.

The deduplication is decisive. Six investigations independently described a buyer record, an offer-list builder, a contract lifecycle, a sample-tracking workflow, a fixation cockpit, and a shipment/document pack — these are not six features, they are one B2B commerce backbone with shared tables (`buyers`/`accounts`, `green_lots`, `offers`, `samples`, `contracts`, `fixations`, `shipments`, `shipment_documents`, `payments`). Likewise the DTC storefront, subscriptions, and POS all share `customers`, `products` (lot-linked SKUs), `orders`, `payments`, and the QR lot-story microsite — they are one consumer commerce engine with three front doors (web, recurring, in-person). The pillar's job is to build these two shared backbones once and present them through the right surfaces, with a single reconciled price book and a single revenue ledger feeding the real number behind `ytd_revenue_usd`. AI runs quietly across all of it — drafting offer-sheet and lot-story copy from real harvest/processing data, advising fixation windows with the margin math done, triaging inbound buyer emails into structured offers, validating EUDR/export document packs before a container ships, and scoring churn/allocation — but with one hard invariant inherited from the broader operating model: AI advises and drafts, a human signs every financial move and every irreversible commitment (fixation, contract, auction allocation). Compliance is a load-bearing differentiator, not overhead: EUDR plot-polygon traceability is a burden for most origins but a selling point for a named single-estate farm whose plots are already GPS-defined, and Panama DGI factura-electrónica is a non-negotiable local reality for any POS sale.

**Top capabilities:**

| Capability | Priority | Opp. | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| Green-lot spine + B2B contract backbone (offer list → contract → fixation → shipment docs → contract-to-cash) | differentiator | 9 | This is how the farm actually makes money today and the keystone every other commerce capability depends on. It promotes a finished green-stage processing_batch into a sellable green_lot, then threads it through a branded offer list, a standards-based contract (GCA/ECF/ESCC) with correct Incoterms, fixed-or-differential pricing, sample approval, the full export document pack, and deposit/balance/settlement — giving Daniela a live book of committed-vs-open and locked-vs-market-exposed revenue, with every bag traceable to its plot polygon. Build this first and let a single schema author own the green_lots/contracts/samples/shipments tables; the storefront, auction module, quotes, CRM and pricing engine all bind to them. |
| Specialty auction & marketplace channel (Best of Panama / Cup of Excellence / Algrano) | differentiator | 9 | Janson's single highest-multiplier capability. It sits in the exact Volcán terroir whose washed Geisha set the 2025 BOP world record ($30,204/kg). The difference between selling a competition-grade microlot as commodity green and getting it onto the BOP/CoE auction block — with a credible CVA quality profile and plot-tied provenance — is one-to-three orders of magnitude per kilo. Auto-matches finished lots to eligible channels and deadlines, tracks the live bid board, and converts a winning bid into a fail-closed export order. Reuses the green_lot spine and the decoupled-pricing branch of the shared price engine. |
| Dual-regime pricing engine: ICE 'C' differentials/fixation + decoupled reserve pricing | differentiator | 9 | The connective tissue under wholesale, auction, quotes, and DTC scarcity. Streams the live ICE Coffee 'C' (KC) curve, resolves any 'C + X c/lb' to net USD/kg after FX and cost-of-production, tracks a hedge/fixation coverage gauge (% of crop fixed vs floating), and refuses quotes below a true per-lot break-even rolled up from workers/tasks/processing. Critically, it cleanly separates C-correlated commodity volume (hedge it) from decoupled reserve Geisha (price it on cup score, scarcity, auction comps) — solving the trap of anchoring a $13k/lb Geisha to a commodity index. Model once as a shared service; never re-implement per surface. A human signs every fixation. |
| DTC roasted-bag storefront + lot-linked SKUs + QR traceability microsite | differentiator | 9 | Monetizes everything upstream at full retail margin on a true $0 headless stack (Next.js + Supabase + Stripe Checkout/Payment Links — no Shopify rent, no PCI burden). Each SKU links by lot_code to the real plot/harvest/processing record, so a bag of 'Tizingal Alto Geisha, Washed, 1,650 masl' carries provenance no reseller can fake, and the bag's QR resolves to that same lot story — the trust mechanic that closes a $30-100+/bag specialty premium. Gives Janson a first-party customer list it owns outright and every sale flows back into the unified revenue ledger. |
| Coffee subscriptions / single-estate Reserve Club (pause·skip·swap, allocation, dunning) | differentiator | 8 | Converts one-time buyers and farm-tour visitors into predictable, high-margin MRR (a $780-1,200/yr LTV) and gives a forward demand signal that drives roast-to-order batching and green-kg reservation. The Reserve Geisha Quarterly tier monetizes scarcity wine-club style — fair allocation across tiers with a waitlist for the rarest microlots — and each box ships a QR traceability passport drawn from the farm's own data. Self-serve portal plus Stripe Billing Smart Retries kill involuntary churn (skip beats cancel). Shares the customers/orders/payments tables with the storefront. |
| Direct-trade CRM — buyers, importers, cafés, relationship & sample/cupping history | differentiator | 8 | The relationship spine under every B2B motion. Today the team lives in WhatsApp, email, a spreadsheet, and memory. One account record holds every interaction, sample sent with its cupping score, lot ever bought with price and margin, and a value/loyalty rank — so scarce Geisha is allocated deliberately by relationship value (not first-come), lapsed buyers surface before they churn, and the harvest-season farm visit (including the quetzal reserve tour) becomes a first-class sales motion that converts visiting cuppers into multi-year contracts. Ties accounts to the SAME traceability lot codes used everywhere else. |
| On-site POS — farm store & agro-tourism café (offline-first, lot-linked, DGI-compliant) | table-stakes | 7 | Closes cherry-to-cash at the gate and is the top of the funnel for the higher-margin online channel. Offline-first PWA survives Volcán connectivity drops; retail bag SKUs decrement against the real roasted lot; tap-to-pay handles international tourist cards and local Yappy QR; tour/cupping ticketing is a first-class revenue line monetizing the reserve experience; and every receipt's QR opens the lot story with a one-tap ship-home reorder. Z-reports reconcile the till into the same revenue figure the dashboard shows. Requires Panama DGI factura-electrónica (non-negotiable). Shares customers/products/orders with the storefront. |
| Quotes & contracts with cross-border e-signature | differentiator | 8 | The paper layer that lets a deal close the same day a buyer asks. Lot-aware quote builder pulls real sellable green lots, proposes a defensible price (fixed or 'C' differential), renders a Janson-branded offer, and on acceptance auto-assembles the correct standard template (GCA/ECF/direct) with Incoterms 2020 and a sample-approval (PSS/SAS) clause — sent for legally binding eIDAS/ESIGN e-signature with a tamper-evident audit trail, countersigned across continents in hours. Inventory commitment stays truthful (no double-sold microlots). This is largely a thin orchestration layer over the B2B backbone + pricing engine + CRM, not a separate product — sequence it alongside, not before, the spine. |

**Must-have integrations:** Stripe — Checkout + Payment Links (DTC, zero monthly platform fee, zero PCI burden), Billing + Customer Portal + Smart Retries (subscriptions/dunning), Stripe Tax (cross-border), and Stripe Terminal/tap-to-pay (POS), ICE Coffee 'C' (KC) futures price feed by contract month, via a market-data vendor (Barchart OnDemand, Nasdaq Data Link/Quandl, or a free delayed feed) — for differential pricing, fixation, and the coverage gauge, Best of Panama (SCAP) + Alliance for Coffee Excellence / Cup of Excellence (V-Auction/Vollers) + Algrano — specialty auction calendars, registration, live bids, results, and direct-trade marketplace listings, EUDR / EU TRACES due-diligence submission — plot-polygon GeoJSON + deforestation-free evidence (couples to satellite/NDVI providers and Meridia/Agriplace-style EUDR tooling); a selling point for a named single-estate, not just overhead, DocuSign eSignature API (or free-tier-friendly Dropbox Sign / SignWell / PandaDoc) — eIDAS/ESIGN-compliant cross-border contract signing with audit certificate, Cropster (Origin / Commerce / Hub) — green inventory, QR-coded sample labels, ICO marks, and SCA/CVA cupping-score sync so lots carry their real quality scores into offers, contracts, and the auction module, Standard green-coffee contract templates + Incoterms 2020 — GCA (US/Canada, NY arbitration), ECF/ESCC 2018 (Europe), SCA FOB/CIF framework, and ICO certificate-of-origin marks for the export document pack, Panama DGI Factura Electrónica (CAFE) via a PAC such as The Factory HKA — legally required electronic tax receipts for every POS/retail sale; plus AUPSA/MIDA phytosanitary + certificate-of-origin for exports, Shippo or EasyPost (multi-carrier labels + live rates) over DHL Express / FedEx International / Correos de Panamá — landed-cost at DTC checkout, sample tracking, and export shipment tracking, Local LATAM payment rails — Yappy (Banco General) and ACH Xpress QR for domestic POS/DTC, plus Wise/Airwallex for smaller cross-border direct-trade roaster settlements and PayPal as fallback, WhatsApp Business Platform (Cloud API) + a lifecycle email engine (Resend/Postmark transactional; Klaviyo/Brevo marketing) — WhatsApp is the dominant buyer channel in Panama/LATAM; thread into the CRM timeline and drive renewal/dunning/win-back flows, QuickBooks Online / Xero — push contract invoices, settlements, deferred-revenue on prepaid subscriptions, and daily POS Z-report journals so CRM/commerce value reconciles to real cash and the season P&L, Supabase Auth + Postgres + Storage — customer accounts, order/contract tables, product imagery, and the public lot-story/QR microsite, all on the existing free stack; GS1 Digital Link standard for globally resolvable, audit-grade bag QR codes

**World-class bar:** A finished green lot flows in one click from the wet mill into a sellable asset carrying its full plot-tied provenance and CVA quality profile, and the system tells the owner exactly which premium channels it qualifies for and by when. Commercial-grade volume is hedged/fixed against a live ICE 'C' curve with a one-glance coverage gauge; reserve Geisha is priced and routed to the Best of Panama / Cup of Excellence block where a single microlot can clear five figures per kilo — never anchored to the commodity index. A roaster in Oslo emails 'what's available?'; AI parses it, matches live uncommitted lots, and drafts a branded offer list and a defensible price before a competitor responds; on acceptance it auto-assembles the correct standard contract (GCA/ECF/direct) with Incoterms 2020 and a sample-approval clause, sent for legally binding e-signature and countersigned across continents in hours, with inventory commitment always truthful — no double-sold microlots — and an EUDR-ready traceability pack attached. The same lot's roasted bags sell direct on a beautiful $0 storefront where a buyer anywhere sees the exact plot at 1,650 masl inside the quetzal reserve, the picker crew and Brix, picks whole-bean 340g light roast, sees a true DHL landed cost, checks out in under 60 seconds, and the bag's GS1 QR resolves to that same lot story plus the real roast date; they subscribe and manage pause/skip/swap themselves, and a farm-store visitor rings the same bag offline on a $200 tablet that keeps working through a mountain Wi-Fi blip, pays by tap or Yappy, gets a DGI electronic receipt, and scans to reorder online. Every dollar — wholesale, auction, DTC, subscription, POS — reconciles automatically into one revenue ledger that is the real number behind the dashboard's ytd_revenue_usd, so cherry-to-cash is one unbroken, lot-traceable thread. The bar is the commercial machine of Hacienda La Esmeralda or a Sucafina/Volcafe trade desk plus Cropster Commerce plus a modern DTC brand — but pointed origin-out for a single great family farm that owns the whole chain, captures the full premium instead of a broker's commodity price, proves provenance instantly, owns its customer list outright, pays no marketplace rent, and never loses a deal to a missing document or a stale spreadsheet. AI drafts, advises, and validates everywhere; a human signs every fixation, contract, and irreversible commitment.

### Brand & Marketing

The Brand & Marketing pillar has one unifying insight that runs through all seven investigations: Janson's operational data IS its marketing engine, and the existing `lotCode` spine (JC-564 already threads Harvest -> ProcessingBatch in src/lib/types.ts) is the single primitive that makes everything else possible. Every capability — campaigns, storytelling, social, the public site, loyalty, agro-tourism, and reputation — converges on the same move: a finished lot, with its real variety/process/Brix/cupping-score/plot/altitude, becomes the atomic unit of a sales story that fires the same day it hits the 'green' stage. No generic CPG marketing tool has origin data baked in; Janson does, for free, because the ops app already captures it. This is the pillar's unfair advantage and its organizing principle — the website, the email, the Instagram post, the loyalty portal, and the QR on the bag are all PRESENTATION LAYERS over one lot record, never re-keyed marketing fiction. That single decision (one lot, many surfaces) resolves nearly every overlap across the seven dossiers.

The pillar serves a 1-3 person family team that punches like a 20-person DTC growth org by leaning on automation and AI drafting, and it serves two distinct economies simultaneously: the B2B green channel (roasters, importers, Best-of-Panama auction bidders — where Coffee Review/SCA scores and verifiable certifications set the price, and a record $30,204/kg Geisha ceiling makes timely segmented launches worth real money per send) and the DTC roasted/agro-tourism channel (where lifecycle email, shoppable UGC, loyalty-as-access, and self-service tour booking compound near-zero-CAC revenue). A unified contact/CRM record is the shared backbone both economies write into — green_buyer, dtc_customer, reserve_lead, tour_guest, roaster — so the same person tracked across a sample dispatch, a bag purchase, and a farm tour resolves to one profile with lifetime value and variety affinity.

Sequencing is decisive and follows three tiers. FOUNDATION FIRST (table-stakes, must ship before anything else has leverage): the data-driven public brand website + per-lot Lot Passport pages + QR resolver, because Janson's current jansoncoffee.com is actively leaking demand at the exact moment its category breaks world records (broken Instagram feed, dead 'No Products Selected!' quote form, English-only, weak SEO). A working site with verifiable lot pages is the destination every other channel points to; without it, email/social/loyalty have nowhere to convert. Layered alongside it: the per-lot Story Builder (auto-drafts narrative from ops data) and a working wholesale-RFQ / lot-reservation funnel — the two things that turn traffic into money. SECOND TIER (the differentiators that compound): the unified contact CRM + lifecycle marketing automation (lot-launch broadcasts, DTC replenishment, B2B sample follow-up, abandoned-cart, tour pre-arrival), the reputation ledger tying every score/award/cert to a lotCode, and agro-tourism self-service booking (a REAL existing revenue line — 'Lagunas Adventures' is already on Viator/TripAdvisor and loses bookings to phone tag). THIRD TIER (future bets that monetize scarcity and conservation): loyalty-as-access (Bean/Reserve/Founder tiers, Adopt-a-Tree, reserve-lot waitlists) and full social-media management with offline-first field capture and UGC rights flows.

Two hard tensions to resolve. (1) The $0/no-paid-services constraint (CLAUDE.md: 'No paid services, ever') eliminates the dossiers' default best-in-class SaaS picks (Klaviyo, Ayrshare, Bokun paid tiers, LoyaltyLion) in favor of self-hosted/free-tier equivalents (Listmonk, Postiz, Plausible/Umami, Resend free tier, Decap CMS, Bokun/FareHarbor free starter). For a practice/portfolio app this is correct — but it must be an explicit, documented downgrade, because the world-class bar assumes the paid playbook. (2) The injection invariant from the house rules is NON-NEGOTIABLE and applies everywhere AI touches inbound text: untrusted public comment/DM/review/guest text can NEVER autonomously trigger an action (a payment, a refund, a send, a published reply). AI drafts; a human approves and sends. The brand voice acts as the OWNER, never as a puppet of commenters. This is a hard co-requirement on every AI feature in the pillar — the social inbox, the WhatsApp tour concierge, the review auto-reply, and the loyalty concierge all draft-only.

**Top capabilities:**

| Capability | Priority | Opp. | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| Data-driven brand website + per-lot Lot Passport pages + QR resolver | table-stakes | 9 | The destination every other channel converts into, and the single highest-leverage fix for THIS farm right now: jansoncoffee.com is leaking demand (broken Instagram feed, dead quote form, English-only, weak SEO) exactly as Panama Geisha breaks $30k/kg auction records. A public /lot/JC-564 page auto-generated from real ops data (plot, altitude, varietal, Brix, process, cup score, map, farm-to-cup timeline) — resolved by a QR on bags and packing lists — turns provenance from a compliance chore into the price justification. Server-rendered JSON-LD (Product/Offer/LocalBusiness/FAQ), bilingual ES/EN with hreflang, and sub-1s Core Web Vitals win 'Panama Geisha farm Volcán' search and AI-overview citations that today leak to Hacienda La Esmeralda. Built on the existing Next.js 15 app on Vercel free tier with $0 tooling (Decap/Tina git-CMS, Plausible self-host). |
| Per-lot Story Builder + provenance/EUDR dossier (one record sells AND complies) | differentiator | 9 | Auto-drafts a structured origin story for any lotCode by pulling already-linked ops truth (plot/altitude/variety, picking crew + Brix + ripeness, process method, days-on-bed, green moisture, cup score) into an editable narrative + spec/offer sheet — collapsing weeks of fact-chasing into an afternoon and guaranteeing the story matches reality. The same record one-click-exports the EUDR due-diligence statement (geolocation polygon, no-deforestation attestation) and a GS1 Digital-Link Digital Product Passport — one source of truth serves both selling and the 30-June-2026 SME compliance deadline rather than two systems. Requires extending the lots spine with cup_score, process_protocol_notes, story_slug, plus new cupping_scores, lot_provenance, certifications, and media_assets tables. The story-completeness AI detector flags lots missing geolocation/cupping/photos and warns when narrative claims drift from the operational record. |
| Unified contact CRM + lifecycle marketing automation (email/SMS/WhatsApp) | differentiator | 8 | One contact record per buyer/consumer/lead (type, country, LTV, variety_affinity, per-channel consent) feeding trigger-based journeys: lot-launch broadcast the moment a batch hits 'green' to a matched roaster segment; DTC replenishment timed to bag depletion; post-sample B2B follow-up; abandoned-cart recovery; tour pre-arrival + post-visit review request; harvest-season win-back. This is recurring revenue and lead conversion on autopilot for a two-person team. WhatsApp is the dominant B2B/LatAm channel and must be first-class. Deliverability is engineered (authenticated domain SPF/DKIM/DMARC, double opt-in, country-aware consent for GDPR/CAN-SPAM/Panama, suppression hygiene, RFC 8058 one-click unsubscribe). The CRM is the shared backbone the website funnel, loyalty, tourism, and reputation all write into. |
| Reputation ledger — scores, awards, certifications, reviews tied to lotCode | differentiator | 8 | One timeline aggregating third-party cupping scores (Coffee Review, SCA 100-pt), competition/auction results (Best of Panama, Cup of Excellence), press, and verified buyer/consumer reviews — every row anchored to a specific lotCode + harvest year. Turns scattered proof into one credibility asset that directly moves the next auction reserve and wholesale rate. Scan-to-review via the same per-lot QR captures reviews at peak delight (5-10x email volume) and feeds a moderated social-proof wall with Google rich-snippet schema. Tamper-evident, click-to-verify badges for Smithsonian Bird Friendly and Rainforest Alliance convert the 200-ha quetzal reserve into a measurable sustainability premium an honor-system claim never could. Serves both reputation economies (B2B score-as-currency, DTC reviews-as-conversion) on the one lot spine. |
| Agro-tourism self-service booking + farm-to-cup tour bridge | differentiator | 8 | A REAL existing revenue line ('Lagunas Adventures on The Janson Coffee Farm' — Geisha tastings, guest house, 9-5 cafe, bike/kayak/horseback, quetzal birding) that today takes reservations by phone and admits it 'books up all the time,' so captured-lost-bookings is pure upside. One canonical experience catalog + live-availability calendar kills double-booking across Viator/TripAdvisor/GetYourGuide/Airbnb/Booking.com/walk-in/WhatsApp via a two-way channel manager; self-service prepay in USD (Panama is USD, no FX) catches the 11pm traveler. The killer differentiator no generic tour platform can replicate: the booking lives in the SAME app as plots/harvests/lots, so a tour ties to the literal micro-lot a guest walks past, tastes, and then buys traceable via the existing QR — converting an experience into a specialty green/roasted sale. AI WhatsApp concierge drafts itineraries under the strict no-autonomous-payment guardrail. |
| Loyalty & referral — reward in access/scarcity, not discount | future-bet | 7 | A closed loop where loyalty is paid in ACCESS, not margin: tiered membership (Bean -> Reserve -> Founder) unlocks early harvest access, reserve-lot first-refusal, and origin-tour credit — monetizing desire for the farm's scarcest asset rather than discounting a $30k/kg-ceiling Geisha. Double-sided referral links (give-50%-off / get-credit, fraud-guarded) acquire vetted higher-LTV customers at near-zero CAC. Adopt-a-Tree/Adopt-a-Lot plugs straight into the reserve conservation story (each adoption funds a second tree, returns a traced annual harvest box) — emotionally sticky recurring revenue unique to a real farm with a quetzal reserve. A B2B mirror (refer-a-roaster + cumulative-volume allocation tiers) strengthens the direct-trade green channel, the farm's biggest money. The member portal shows the literal lot story (plot/picker/Brix/process) behind the bag in someone's hand. Depends on the CRM and lot spine being live first. |
| Social media management + offline-first field capture + UGC rights flow | future-bet | 7 | The bridge between a $30k/kg-tier origin and the buyer — green and reserve-lot sales start as relationships that now begin on Instagram/WhatsApp. Offline-first field capture lets anyone on the farm at 1,600 masl with patchy signal snap a cherry-picking/fermentation moment, tag the plot/lot/varietal, and queue it; it syncs to a Drafts inbox when signal returns. A unified scheduler publishes once to IG/FB/TikTok/YouTube; UGC discovery auto-listens for @mentions and runs one-tap rights-approval into a legally clean shoppable wall. Campaigns fire off real farm events (first pick, Best of Panama lot reveal, quetzal sighting). AI drafts on-brand captions a human approves; the injection invariant means untrusted comment/DM text never triggers an autonomous action. Highest ongoing-effort capability and most dependent on the website/CRM foundation, so it sequences last — but the content is genuinely scarce and unfakeable, which is the whole moat. |

**Must-have integrations:** Next.js 15 on Vercel (free tier) — ISR-rendered SEO/Lot-Passport pages, edge image optimization, Vercel OG/Satori for social cards; the app already runs this stack, Decap CMS or TinaCMS (git-backed, $0) for editable brand-story / heritage / reserve content blocks — the $0 alternative to Sanity/Contentful, Listmonk (open-source, self-hostable) for email/SMS campaigns + segmentation + flows — the $0 alternative to Klaviyo; Resend or Amazon SES free tier for transactional email with React Email templating, WhatsApp Business Cloud API (free tier) — the dominant B2B + Latin-American buyer and tour-booking channel; Twilio as SMS/email fallback, Cropster Origin — pull authoritative cupping scores and lot/green-grading data so lot pages and launch emails carry real cup notes, not adjectives, Cloudflare R2 or Supabase Storage (free tier) — the tagged media-asset library (photos/video/drone/quetzal footage) linked to plot_id/lot_code/harvest_id, Plausible or Umami (privacy-first, cookieless, self-host for $0) + Google Search Console API + Bing Webmaster Tools — analytics and rank surfaced inside Farm Operations, no cookie banner, Schema.org JSON-LD (Organization, Product, Offer, LocalBusiness, Article, FAQPage, BreadcrumbList) + sitemap/robots/IndexNow + hreflang ES/EN — technical SEO and AI-overview citation, EUDR due-diligence statement format + GS1 Digital Link Digital Product Passport (ESPR) + WGS84 geolocation polygon — the QR-to-passport resolver doubles as the 30-June-2026 SME compliance export, SCA cupping protocol / Coffee Value Assessment (CVA) flavor-wheel taxonomy; Best of Panama / SCAP auction lot format; Coffee Review + Alliance for Coffee Excellence (Cup of Excellence) for the reputation ledger, Certification registries: Smithsonian Bird Friendly + Rainforest Alliance (the quetzal-reserve premium) — verifiable click-to-verify badges, Meta Graph API (Instagram + Facebook) + TikTok Content Posting API + YouTube Data API v3, fronted by Postiz (open-source, self-hostable, $0) to avoid per-SDK maintenance — the $0 alternative to Ayrshare, Bokun or FareHarbor (free starter tiers) as the agro-tourism booking engine + Viator/TripAdvisor/GetYourGuide/Airbnb Experiences channel manager; Booking.com/Hostaway for the guest house, Stripe (+ Stripe Terminal for on-site cafe/retail tap-to-pay) — DTC store, tour deposits/prepay, reserve-lot holds, tree-adoption billing, and the order/subscription events that feed LTV and replenishment timing; Panama DGI factura electrónica for on-site receipts, Anthropic Claude API — on-brand bilingual (EN/ES) lot-story and caption drafting, subject-line/send-time optimization, predictive segmentation and churn scoring, vision-model photo auto-tagging, review-theme summarization, and story-completeness/provenance-drift detection — all DRAFT-ONLY behind the human-approval / injection invariant

**World-class bar:** World-class is when the JC lot code is the single hyperlink that unifies operations, marketing, sales, and compliance — and nothing is ever re-keyed. A buyer in Tokyo Googles 'Volcán Panama Geisha reserve' or scans the QR on a bag of JC-564, lands on a sub-1-second, cinematic, bilingual page that ranks #1 and is cited in AI Overviews, and sees the page IS the live operations data: the exact plot on a map at 1,520 masl, the Pacamara trees, the named crew that picked it at 19.4 Brix, the 14-day natural drying curve, an 88.5 SCA score with its flavor wheel, drone footage of the 200-ha quetzal reserve, click-to-verify Bird Friendly and Rainforest Alliance badges, the Best of Panama placing and price, and a moderated wall of real buyer/consumer reviews — every element anchored to that one lotCode and harvest year. The same record one-click-exports the EUDR geolocation polygon and Digital Product Passport, so provenance is a sales weapon AND clears compliance. The moment that lot hit 'green,' a lot-launch email/WhatsApp fired to perfectly-matched roasters; DTC replenishment and win-back flows quietly compound; B2B sample follow-ups never slip; loyalty is paid in access (reserve-lot first-refusal, adopted trees, tour credits) not eroded margin; an 11pm traveler self-books a Geisha tasting + guest-house night + sunrise quetzal walk in one prepaid USD checkout that lands on an overbooking-proof single calendar; and every touch enrolls and attributes into ONE customer record. AI drafts on-brand bilingual copy from the farm's own data and a human approves in seconds — never autonomously acting on untrusted public text — so a 1-3 person family team operates like a 20-person growth org. It matches or beats Cropster Origin + a bespoke agency site + the Klaviyo/Shopify retention playbook, built on $0 free-tier tooling, with provenance as the product and the operations system itself as the content engine. The measurable bar: >99% inbox placement, sub-1s LCP, #1 ranking and AI-overview citation for 'Panama Geisha farm,' every lot launch firing same-day to a matched segment, and a single dashboard inside Farm Operations tying every search query, QR scan, and campaign to wholesale RFQs, lot reservations, and revenue — the full grow -> process -> sell -> host -> retain loop, closed.

### Finance, Compliance & Sustainability

This pillar is unusually coherent because every one of its 13 capabilities hangs off the same spine that already exists in the codebase: the `lotCode` that threads `Harvest → ProcessingBatch`, plus the per-plot `shadePct`, `altitudeMasl`, `establishedYear`, and `areaHa` fields on `Plot`. That is the synthesis's central insight — Finance, Compliance & Sustainability is not 13 separate modules but one traceable graph viewed through three lenses. The same per-lot cost rollup that tells Miguel whether his trophy Geisha is funding the farm or quietly losing money is the cost floor the sales lead quotes a Best-of-Panama buyer; the same plot GPS polygon that proves deforestation-free for EUDR is the carbon-sink boundary, the Bird-Friendly shade record, and the quetzal-habitat conservation claim. World-class here means a single number — net margin per lot, with its compliance and sustainability provenance attached — that is true the moment a buyer commits and defensible the moment an auditor or the DGI asks. Compliance and sustainability stop being cost centers and become the moat: a Volcán estate that can produce a one-click EUDR Due Diligence Statement, a per-lot carbon balance, and a reconciled DGI Factura Electrónica keeps the premium EU/Asia buyers that drop un-traceable suppliers, while the conservation it already does (the 200-ha quetzal reserve, dense shade) becomes monetizable carbon insets and audit evidence rather than marketing copy.

The decisive sequencing tension is between table-stakes plumbing and Janson-specific differentiators. Three capabilities are table-stakes — you cannot run the business legally or get paid without them: Accounting/bookkeeping (QBO/Xero sync), Invoicing/AR-AP/collections, and Audit-readiness/document management. These must ship first because everything else either posts into them (cost ledger → QBO) or reads from them (audit packs → document vault). But the per-lot Cost-of-Production engine (opp 9) is the keystone differentiator that turns generic bookkeeping into a coffee-native superpower, and it should ship in the same wave as the accounting sync, not after — they share the `LedgerEntry`/`cost_entry` table and the lot-costing rollup is what makes the QBO journal entries meaningful. EUDR compliance (surfaced redundantly across five capabilities) is existential, not optional, given the Dec-2026/Jun-2027 deadlines and Janson's EU buyers — and it is nearly free to build because the plot polygon + `establishedYear` + 2020-baseline check is a thin extension of data already captured. I rank it among the highest opportunities precisely because it is high-stakes and low-cost-to-build on this data model.

I deduped heavy overlaps. EUDR Due Diligence appears in six dossiers (Tax/regulatory, Audit-readiness, Certifications, Carbon, ESG, Government/ICO) — I consolidate it into ONE shared "Geolocation & EUDR engine" (plot polygons, 2020 forest-cover baseline via Global Forest Watch/JRC, TRACES-NT DDS export) that every consumer reads from. Per-lot cost-of-production appears in three (Accounting, Cost-accounting, Budgeting) — one `LotCost` rollup engine, period. The Panama tax/payroll realities (DGI SFEP Factura Electrónica via a certified PAC, CSS social-security, décimo-tercer-mes, USD-pegged Balboa removing domestic FX) recur across Accounting, Tax, Invoicing, and Banking — these are shared services, not per-capability features. The carbon ledger, biodiversity monitor, certifications evidence vault, and ESG impact report are four views of the same conservation evidence graph; they should share a single `carbon_ledger_entry` + `plot_geometry` + `certification` substrate and differ only in their output artifact (a credit vintage, a quetzal sighting log, an audit dossier, a bag QR). The real tension I resolve decisively: do NOT build certifications, carbon credits, biodiversity monitoring, and water stewardship as standalone subsystems first — build the shared evidence vault + plot-geometry layer once (it's table-stakes for EUDR anyway), then layer each sustainability view on top as a future bet that compounds the brand.

The Panama-and-coffee specificity is what makes this pillar a differentiator rather than a SaaS commodity. USD legal tender removes domestic FX gymnastics but the money risk is inbound — a single Geisha microlot can sell for thousands to a Tokyo/Seoul roaster paying JPY/KRW, losing 3-5% to unmeasured bank spreads, so per-lot invoice-to-cash with rate-lock matters far more here than for a commodity farm. The brutal seasonality (12 months of standing labor/input cost against one harvest-sale window) makes the cash-flow runway projection and pre-harvest finance desk a solvency tool, not a nicety. And the 200-ha quetzal reserve plus shade-grown Geisha at 1,350-1,700 masl is an authentic, hard-to-replicate carbon/biodiversity asset sitting exactly where Verra VM0042 agroforestry methods, Smithsonian Bird-Friendly (born in Panama, run from STRI), and EUDR all converge — the operational story and the financial story are literally the same story, told through the same lot data.

**Top capabilities:**

| Capability | Priority | Opp. | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| Per-lot/per-plot cost-of-production engine (activity-based COGS) | differentiator | 9 | The keystone differentiator and the number the whole business turns on. A Geisha microlot can sell for 20-100x a commercial Caturra but is far more labor- and process-intensive (selective picking, anaerobic/honey fermentation, slow bed-drying). Without lot-level COGS the farm cannot tell whether its trophy lots fund the rest or quietly lose money. It reuses the existing plot→harvest→lotCode→ProcessingBatch spine and the encoded cherry-to-green mass-loss curve directly, so cost/kg restates correctly on green weight per method. The same number that proves provenance to a Best-of-Panama buyer proves profitability to the owner and sets the negotiation floor. |
| QBO/Xero bi-directional accounting sync + Panama payroll (CSS, décimo) | table-stakes | 8 | Table-stakes plumbing: every operational event (picker pay, fertilizer, diesel, lot sale) should code itself once and flow as a tax-aware journal entry into the contador's QBO/Xero, with picker payroll rolling daily attendance and per-kg picking into a payroll run that computes CSS contributions and the décimo-tercer-mes accrual. Eliminates the midnight shoebox-of-receipts double-keying and is the ledger every other capability (cost engine, AR/AP, tax pack) posts into or reads from. USD-pegged Balboa keeps the GL effectively single-currency. |
| Multi-currency export AR / invoicing / collections (invoice-to-cash per lot) | table-stakes | 9 | You don't run the business if you don't get paid, and Janson's money risk is inbound: high-value, low-volume Geisha lots sold per-lot to named foreign roasters in JPY/KRW/EUR, on Net-30/60/90 or Letter-of-Credit terms, where one aging $40k+ receivable or one mistyped HS-code/Incoterm stalls a container. The export invoice must auto-pull the lot's weight, grade, variety, process, HS code (0901.11) and Incoterm from processing records, age receivables on a live board with auto-dunning, and self-match Wise/Stripe/wire deposits with FX gain/loss attribution. Domestic sales clear DGI SFEP/CUFE in the same gesture. |
| Geolocation + EUDR Due Diligence engine (shared compliance keystone) | table-stakes | 9 | Existential, not optional, and nearly free to build on this data model. EU buyers will demand farm-level GPS polygons and a TRACES-NT Due Diligence Statement past the Dec-2026/Jun-2027 deadlines or drop Janson as un-traceable. The build is a thin extension of data already captured — plot polygon + establishedYear + a deforestation-free check against the 2020 Global Forest Watch/JRC baseline — and the 200-ha reserve makes Janson the easy-pass case rather than the at-risk one. I consolidate the six redundant per-capability EUDR features into this ONE shared engine that the tax, audit, certification, carbon, and government-reporting views all read from. |
| Audit-readiness vault + lot-linked document management (CAPA, share portal) | table-stakes | 9 | Table-stakes connective tissue: every certificate, contract, lab result, agrochemical record and inspection report stored once, tagged to the lotCode/plot spine, so any auditor or buyer DDS request is answered by exporting one timestamped, tamper-evident folder in minutes instead of a week of binder-digging. A lifecycle tracker with tiered renewal reminders kills the 'we forgot to renew and lost the premium' failure mode; a time-boxed watermarked share portal lets auditors/importers self-serve scoped evidence. This vault is the substrate the certifications, carbon, water, and biodiversity views all write into. |
| Budgeting, cash-flow runway & financing-gap planner | differentiator | 8 | Coffee's seasonality is a solvency problem, not a reporting nicety: fixed costs run all year against one concentrated harvest-and-sale inflow. A yield-driven bottom-up season budget that reforecasts daily from actual harvest intake, plus a 12-month cash-flow projection with low-balance early warning, tells the owner exactly when to buy inputs, make payroll, and draw a working-capital line — and surfaces eligible green-clause LC / pre-shipment advances against signed Geisha contracts so the harvest is financed by design, not by selling lots cheap. Closes the loop by pulling QBO/Cropster/bank actuals back so next year's plan starts from this year's truth. |
| Sustainability evidence graph — carbon ledger, certifications, biodiversity, water (one substrate, four views) | future-bet | 8 | Janson's single most authentic, hardest-to-replicate edge: a real 200-ha quetzal-habitat reserve plus shade-grown Geisha at altitude is a genuine carbon sink and biodiversity asset exactly where Verra VM0042 agroforestry, Smithsonian Bird-Friendly, and EUDR converge. Built as ONE shared substrate (carbon_ledger_entry + plot_geometry + certification + shade/species records on top of the vault and EUDR engine), it yields a per-lot CO2e balance, a Bird-Friendly/RA/Organic audit dossier, a quetzal sighting log, mill-water L/kg-green, and a bag-QR sustainability passport — turning conservation into a sustainability premium, EU market access, and monetizable carbon insets sold to the very roasters buying the coffee. A future bet that compounds the brand once the table-stakes ledger and compliance keystone exist. |

**Must-have integrations:** QuickBooks Online Accounting API (OAuth2) and Xero Accounting API (OAuth2) — books of record the external contador lives in, Panama DGI SFEP / Factura Electrónica via a DGI-registered PAC (e.g. EDICOM, P4 Software) for CUFE + Firma Electrónica Avanzada; DGI Facturador Gratuito below the B/.36,000 / 100-doc threshold, Caja de Seguro Social (CSS) contribution tables + Panama Código de Trabajo décimo-tercer-mes rules for true loaded labor cost (USD/Balboa pegged 1:1 removes domestic FX), Wise Business API (multi-currency receiving + batch payouts at mid-market FX) and Stripe (card/ACH + roasted-retail D2C); Yappy / Banco General API for local crew + supplier payments, Plaid or QBO/Xero native bank feeds for transaction reconciliation; SWIFT MT103 inbound-wire parsing for export-deposit matching, EU EUDR Information System / TRACES NT for Due Diligence Statement submission (GeoJSON, WGS-84/EPSG-4326), Global Forest Watch / JRC Global Forest Cover 2020 + Sentinel-2 NDVI (Sentinel Hub) for the deforestation-free baseline check, ICO Certificate-of-Origin regime (ICC-102-9 Rev.5, 60kg-bag equivalents) + Panama MIDA/APA phytosanitary & food-safety certificates and MICI VUCE Chiriquí export window, Cropster / Cropster Origin — the specialty-coffee lot-traceability, green-inventory and cupping-score system buyers already trust; sync lotCode, green kg, cup score, Cool Farm Tool API + Verra VM0042 / Gold Standard / Plan Vivo registries for per-kg GHG footprint and agroforestry carbon/biodiversity credits, Certification bodies: Rainforest Alliance (RACP), FLOCERT/Fairtrade, Smithsonian Bird-Friendly (via NaturaCert/QCS), USDA NOP + EU Organic (CERES/Kiwa BCS/Control Union), ICE Coffee 'C' futures + Best-of-Panama / SCAP auction clearing prices as the revenue/benchmark side of margin and differential-priced contracts, OCR/Document AI (AWS Textract / Google Document AI / Claude vision) for receipt and lab-report extraction into coded ledger/vault entries, Pre-harvest / trade finance: Sucafina Farm-to-Roaster, green-clause LC desks, Starbucks Global Farmer Fund; ISA (Instituto de Seguro Agropecuario, David office) + parametric weather insurers (Etherisc/OKO/Pula); Rabobank ACORN for carbon-backed sustainability-linked rates, MiAmbiente / DGNTI-COPANIT 35-2000 effluent norm (and 24-99 reuse) for mill wastewater compliance; LoRaWAN water-quality + flow sensors for per-lot water ledger

**World-class bar:** Accounting, compliance, and sustainability are a by-product of running the farm, never a second night job. A picking crew is paid, a pallet of fertilizer is bought, and a Geisha lot ships — and the owner never opens QuickBooks: each event is captured at its source, coded once, and flows as a tax-aware journal entry into QBO/Xero with the bank feed auto-reconciling and the DGI Factura Electrónica issued in the same gesture as the sale. The owner opens any lot and sees a fully-loaded cost/kg-green (labor/inputs/processing/overhead), a live margin against the actual sale in the buyer's currency netted of FX and lifting fees, and a defensible break-even — accurate enough to put in front of a Best-of-Panama buyer and a DGI auditor on the same screen. The moment a shipment is booked, its full export dossier — ICO Certificate of Origin, MIDA/APA phytosanitary, VUCE declaration, and a TRACES-NT EUDR Due Diligence Statement with plot GPS polygons proving deforestation-free since 2020 — assembles itself from data already captured at harvest, reviewed not retyped. Every certified bag carries a QR resolving to its exact plots, shade profile, processing trail, net CO2e balance, liters-of-water-per-kg, this season's quetzal count, and the premium paid to named workers — every number traceable to a timestamped record and a recognized methodology (Cool Farm, EUDR baseline, Bird-Friendly, COPANIT). Cash runway is projected weeks out so the harvest is financed by design via a green-clause LC against signed contracts, not by discounting lots; insurance pays out parametrically within days of a weather event. The auditor's annual visit becomes a confirmation, not a discovery; an importer's MRL or OTA test never surprises the farm because the risk was flagged at spray time. Compliance stops being overhead and becomes the moat — small estates that cannot produce this packet lose EU access while Janson's is automatic — and the 200-ha reserve and shade canopy become a quantified, monetized, brand-defining carbon and biodiversity asset rather than a cost center. The benchmark to beat is what Cropster + a competent farm accountant + Avalara/EDICOM + Descartes each do in isolation, unified on the same lot data, in Spanish, tuned to Panamanian law and specialty-coffee reality, runnable by a 14-person family farm.

### Platform & Intelligence (cross-cutting)

Platform & Intelligence is not a feature area — it is the spine the entire Janson system stands on, and the dossier makes the dependency order unambiguous. One capability sits above all others: the Unified Data Model / single source of truth (opportunity 10, table-stakes). Every other pillar — agronomy, processing, sales, labor, finance, sustainability — and every capability inside THIS pillar (telemetry, AI, audit, alerting, automation, BI, maps, identity) reuses its canonical entities, units, events, and audit trail rather than inventing its own. The keystone insight repeated across nine separate investigations is that today's hand-authored aggregate tables (season_summary, daily_cherries, weekly_harvest, variety_shares) and bare-string lot_code are the #1 trust defect: numbers disagree across screens, and provenance — which IS the product for a single-estate Geisha house — cannot be proven. Fix that first (canonical IDs, a lot-genealogy graph, a UCUM unit+yield ledger, a derived-metrics semantic layer, append-only event log) and every downstream capability becomes a thin, trustworthy projection. Build anything else first and you compound the drift.

The second foundational truth is physical: Janson farms on volcanic slopes from 1,350–1,700 masl beside a 200-ha cloud-forest reserve where cellular coverage is patchy at the farmhouse and dead under canopy — exactly where the highest-value moments happen (weighing a picker's Geisha at a remote station, pinning a roya outbreak, logging an anaerobic ferment pH). This makes offline-first capture (opportunity 10, table-stakes) and an on-farm edge gateway (opportunity 8) co-foundational with the data model, not optional polish. The dossier converges hard here: a local-first PWA + durable outbox + CRDT/last-writer-wins reconciliation, backed by a ruggedized gateway (ChirpStack/Mosquitto/Postgres mirror) that ingests LoRaWAN sensors and fires frost/ferment alerts locally in seconds even during a cloud outage. Voice capture, Bluetooth scales/moisture meters, and the entire IoT/hardware estate all feed THIS substrate — they are not separate platforms but one telemetry+capture pipeline that normalizes (SenML, MQTT, BLE, voice) into the SSOT's measurements table, every reading device-traceable and calibration-stamped.

The third truth is that a swarm of capabilities the dossier lists separately are actually shared cross-cutting platform services that must be built ONCE and consumed everywhere: the append-only hash-chained audit ledger (which doubles as the activity feed, the certification evidence trail, and the payroll-dispute resolver), the rules/automation engine over the event bus, the multi-channel alerting fabric (WhatsApp-first, SMS/voice escalation, Latam reality), identity/RBAC-over-RLS with seasonal auto-expiry, es-PA-first localization with SI units, and the security/backup/DR posture. Building these as one service each — rather than re-inventing per module — is the difference between a coherent platform and spaghetti. Finally, the intelligence layer (yield/disease/demand/anomaly ML + a grounded conversational copilot) is the differentiating crown, but it is strictly downstream: it is only as good as the SSOT it reasons over and the telemetry it consumes.
