Remote-sensing (satellite NDVI + EUDR verification, opportunity 9) is the one AI-adjacent capability that earns near-top priority on its own merits because EUDR compliance is a hard legal gate on selling green coffee into the EU by 2026/2027 — it doubles as both the agronomy-intelligence feed and the export-compliance engine, and it runs on free public Earth-observation data.

**Top capabilities:**

| Capability | Priority | Opp. | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| Unified data model & single source of truth (canonical IDs, lot-genealogy graph, UCUM unit+yield ledger, append-only event log, derived-metrics semantic layer) | table-stakes | 10 | The non-negotiable spine. Every other capability in every pillar bolts onto its entities, units, events, and audit. Replacing hand-authored aggregates with derived metrics and turning bare lot_code into a queryable farm-to-bag genealogy kills the #1 trust defect (numbers disagreeing across screens) and makes provenance — the literal premium driver for single-estate Geisha — provable in one query. Build this first or compound drift forever. |
| Offline-first field capture + on-farm edge gateway (local-first PWA, durable outbox, CRDT reconciliation, LoRaWAN/MQTT ingestion, local alerting) | table-stakes | 10 | Volcán's terrain (1,350–1,700 masl, dead zones under canopy) means the highest-value data is born exactly where there is no signal. Without conflict-safe offline capture and an on-farm gateway that survives cloud/power outages and fires frost/ferment alerts locally in seconds, the SSOT is fed by paper and the traceability/premium story breaks at the first link. Co-foundational with the data model. |
| Unified telemetry & device ingestion pipeline (IoT sensors + Bluetooth scales/moisture meters + voice capture, normalized to one measurements store) | differentiator | 9 | Four separate dossier capabilities (IoT, hardware ecosystem, edge, voice) all write the same thing: device-traceable, calibration-stamped readings tied to a plot/bed/tank/lot. Converging them onto ONE pipeline (SenML/MQTT/BLE/voice → measurements table) turns typed-in claims into measured ground truth — auto-captured weigh-points give an audited cherry-to-green mass balance, live drying curves protect the $40/lb Geisha drying window, and a dead/drifting probe raises an incident instead of silently corrupting a season. |
| Cross-cutting platform services as single shared instances: audit ledger, automation/rules engine, alerting fabric, identity/RBAC-over-RLS, localization, security/backup-DR | table-stakes | 9 | The dossier lists these as separate capabilities, but each appears as a sub-feature of nearly every other — they MUST be built once and consumed everywhere. One hash-chained audit ledger powers the activity feed, certification evidence, and payroll disputes. One WhatsApp-first alerting fabric serves frost, ferment, task, and buyer events. One es-PA-first localization+SI-unit layer prevents weight/currency corruption everywhere downstream. Building per-module reinventions is how a platform becomes spaghetti. |
| Remote-sensing pipeline + EUDR deforestation-free verification (Sentinel-2/1 NDVI/NDRE per plot polygon, GFW/JRC forest-loss layers, signed DDS dossier) | differentiator | 9 | A rare capability that is simultaneously agronomy intelligence AND a hard legal export gate: every Geisha/Pacamara lot literally cannot enter the EU by 2026/2027 without a geolocated polygon and deforestation-free due-diligence statement. The same free Earth-observation layers that prove EUDR compliance feed per-plot vigor trends, roya/frost early-warning, and timestamp the 200-ha quetzal reserve's conservation story. Optical+radar fusion is required (not optional) under Barú's near-daily cloud. |
| Grounded intelligence layer: ML (yield / disease-image / demand-price / anomaly) + conversational copilot over the SSOT | differentiator | 9 | The differentiating crown — but strictly downstream of the data model and telemetry it reasons over. A single grounded brain: per-plot yield forecasts with confidence bands, offline on-phone leaf-rust/broca detection auto-mapped to the right plot, sell-now-vs-hold against ICE Coffee C plus Janson's realized Geisha premium, mid-process anomaly catches before the cupping table, and a Spanish-first copilot that cites Janson's own rows (never generic web advice), is agentic-but-safe (typed/audited/human-confirmed writes, untrusted channel text can never drive an action), and cost-governed (model tiering, prompt caching, hard caps) to stay viable on the $0 posture. |
| Maps/GIS engine & spatial home screen (MapLibre + PostGIS plot polygons, status choropleth, drop-a-pin scouting, reserve/buffer layers, audit-grade map export) | differentiator | 9 | Geometry is the keystone the SSOT lacks today (altitude/area scalars, no polygons) and is the first link of geographic traceability. Spatial becomes the primary index of the whole system: every harvest, task, scouting pin, and lot is one click from its boundary; terrain/elevation overlays turn Volcán's 60m elevation deltas into a Geisha-placement and quality tool; and the drawn reserve + buffers are exactly the cartographic evidence certifiers and EUDR demand. |
| Self-serve BI / semantic layer + real-time role-aware dashboards & alerting | differentiator | 9 | Replaces the static 4-KPI page and hand-authored aggregates with one governed metrics registry feeding both live operational dashboards (role-tuned, Supabase Realtime) and a no-SQL self-serve report builder. The decision-relevant number for a specialty farm — true cost-per-kg-green by lot (activity-based costing netted against the lot's sale) — lives here, plus audit-grade certification/buyer report packs and a natural-language 'ask your farm' query. Same metric definitions everywhere means no number ever disagrees between two screens. |
| Integration hub — API, webhooks, connectors (QBO, Shopify, weather, Cropster, carriers) as one signed/idempotent event bus | differentiator | 8 | Janson runs the full chain and sells both green (importer invoices) and roasted (jansoncoffee.com), so the same lot must reconcile across QuickBooks, a Shopify SKU, and a buyer/auditor traceability record. A bus-based hub (fan-in Shopify/QBO/weather, fan-out Cropster/QR-passport/certifier portals) with dead-letter retry and a one-screen health board makes 'YTD revenue' and 'kg of lot X remaining' one live truth. The highest-leverage single swap is replacing the static hand-typed weather table with a live plot-coordinate forecast feed. |
| Document & media management + buyer-facing lot digital passport | differentiator | 8 | Provenance only converts to price if it can be proven and handed over. A polymorphic attach-to-anything media store (geotagged field photos auto-attached to the right plot polygon, OCR-classified certs/invoices), a certification vault with expiry nags that can fail-close a shipment dossier built on a lapsed cert, and one-click signed lot passports (origin photos, altitude, varietal, cupping score, process, harvest window) turn Janson's Geisha + quetzal-reserve story into a measurable premium and collapse audit prep from weeks to a day. |

**Must-have integrations:** Cropster (Origin/Cup) — de-facto specialty-coffee traceability + cupping + processing standard; bidirectional lot/cupping/process sync so Janson interoperates with the buyers and roasters who already use it, EUDR compliance stack — geolocated plot polygons + Due Diligence Statement (TRACES NT) format, backed by Global Forest Watch (GLAD/RADD), Hansen tree-cover-loss, JRC Tropical Moist Forest, and FAO Open Foris Whisp; a hard legal gate on selling green into the EU, Copernicus Data Space / Sentinel Hub — free Sentinel-2 (NDVI/NDRE/EVI) + Sentinel-1 SAR (cloud-penetrating, required under Barú's cloud) per plot polygon, with Copernicus DEM + ESA WorldCover for terrain/canopy, WhatsApp Business Cloud API (Meta) — the primary, Latam-first notification + two-way task-update channel for a Spanish-monolingual, app-averse workforce; with Twilio SMS + TTS-voice escalation as the never-miss-a-frost fallback, Supabase stack (Postgres + PostGIS + Auth/RLS + Realtime + Edge Functions + Storage + pg_cron) — the $0-tier backbone for SSOT geometry, tenant/role isolation at the data layer, change-stream propagation, scheduled jobs, and object storage, LoRaWAN via The Things Stack + ChirpStack/Mosquitto (MQTT) + an on-farm edge gateway (Raspberry Pi/industrial mini-PC) — long-range low-power sensing across the 200-ha estate, with SenML (RFC 8428) / Sparkplug B payload normalization and offline store-and-forward, Anthropic Claude API — grounded RAG copilot, vision pest/disease diagnosis, anomaly narration, and NL-to-query; model-tiered (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus) with prompt caching and per-feature cost caps via a gateway, QuickBooks Online + Shopify Admin/Storefront APIs — accounting and DTC commerce reconciliation so green-export invoices and roasted-bag sales both decrement the same lot inventory, Weather APIs — Open-Meteo (free, no key) primary, with Tomorrow.io / NASA POWER for hyperlocal frost/rain/GDD modeling pinned to plot coordinates and altitude bands, Standards layer — GS1 (GTIN/GLN, 2D Data Matrix/QR Link for the bag passport), UCUM (units), ISO 8601 / ISO 4217 (PAB/USD), OGC GeoJSON/STAC/COG (geometry), ICO + HS 0901 (export docs), and certification schemas (Rainforest Alliance, USDA/EU Organic, Smithsonian Bird Friendly, Fair Trade) as reference-data + audit-export targets, Offline/local-first sync layer — PowerSync or ElectricSQL (Postgres↔client replication) + Workbox/Serwist PWA + IndexedDB/Dexie outbox with CRDT/vector-clock reconciliation, OpenTimestamps / RFC-3161 TSA — free external time-anchoring of the hash-chained audit ledger for tamper-evident, court/auditor-grade provenance

**World-class bar:** World-class for this pillar is an invisible, trustworthy spine where every number on every screen is derived (never hand-typed) from one canonical record, every real-world thing (farm, plot, tree-block, lot, batch, worker, device, party) has exactly one stable ID, and any finished bag of Geisha resolves in a single query to its GPS polygon, picker, fermentation curve, drying moisture trace, cupping score, and certifications — with a hash-chained, externally-time-anchored, who-changed-what audit trail behind it. The farm runs identically online or offline: capture is instant and local on a $120 Android phone in the rain at 1,700 masl, an on-farm gateway fires a frost or over-ferment alert in seconds during a total cloud outage, and everything reconciles conflict-free with zero data loss when signal returns. Telemetry is single-sourced — agronomy, processing, BI, certification, and the AI all read the same device readings, each calibration-stamped, with a dead probe raising an incident rather than showing stale-green. Intelligence is grounded and proactive: the copilot opens each morning in Spanish with the one or two decisions that matter, cites Janson's own rows, diagnoses leaf rust from a field photo offline, forecasts yield with tightening confidence bands, and can take typed/audited/human-confirmed actions — but untrusted inbound text can never silently drive a write. EUDR compliance is one button because every lot already carries a deforestation-checked polygon. The whole thing is es-PA-first with SI-canonical units, multi-tenant-isolated at the database layer so Janson can later license it to neighboring estates, and it runs on a near-$0 free-tier posture using open infrastructure — proving rigor and auditability without enterprise weight. The bar is Cropster-grade specialty depth fused with Temporal/Inngest-grade durable reliability, Stripe-clean API ergonomics, and PagerDuty-grade alerting — all tuned for one connectivity-poor, premium-Geisha highland estate.

---

## 9. Appendix — All Capability Investigations

### Grow — Agronomy & Production

#### Plot/parcel & land GIS — boundaries, blocks, ownership, elevation zones
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Farm Manager / Agronomist-Owner (the Janson family operator who walks the blocks) — supported by the field supervisor who needs to find a parcel by name, and the export/compliance lead who must produce plot-level proof for buyers and regulators. Today this person knows the farm "in their head"; the GIS layer makes that knowledge a shared, queryable, defensible asset.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I'm planning the season or onboarding a new supervisor, I want every parcel drawn as a real boundary on a map with its block, varietal, elevation band and tree count, so I can stop relying on memory and hand everyone the same authoritative picture of the farm.
- When a European buyer or our exporter asks for EUDR proof, I want each plot to carry a regulator-grade polygon (or GPS point) tied to its harvest lots, so I can generate a deforestation-free due-diligence package in minutes instead of scrambling with paper titles and a GPS handheld.
- When I'm deciding where to plant the next Geisha block or which lot to push as a reserve micro-lot, I want to slice the farm by elevation zone, slope, aspect and shade against the cadastral title boundaries, so I can match varietal-to-terroir and not plant Geisha below 1,500 masl by mistake.
- When a parcel underperforms or a worker reports a problem, I want to tap the block on a map and see its agronomy, ownership, lease status and history, so I can act on the exact piece of land instead of a spreadsheet row.

**Features:**
- **Interactive farm map (boundary draw + import)** — A pan/zoom basemap (satellite + topo) where every plot is a real polygon — drawn in-app, imported from GeoJSON/Shapefile/KML/GPX, or walked with a phone GPS — replacing the flat plot list with a spatial source of truth everyone reads the same way.
- **Block / sub-block / row hierarchy** — Nest parcels into the existing Block A–E structure plus sub-blocks and planting rows, so a 6.5 ha Caturra block can be split into management units for targeted pruning, renovation cohorts, and yield attribution down to the row.
- **Elevation, slope & aspect zones (auto-derived)** — Pull a DEM over each polygon and auto-compute min/mean/max masl, slope %, and aspect — turning the single altitudeMasl number into a terroir zone map that powers varietal-fit and frost/erosion risk, the core of Volcán's 1,350–1,700 masl story.
- **Cadastral / ownership & tenure layer** — Attach the legal finca number, owner/lessor, lease term, and the official ANATI cadastral boundary to each operational plot — so the family can see where managed blocks sit inside (or stray outside) titled land, and resolve the common gap between 'where coffee grows' and 'what's on the title.'
- **EUDR / origin geolocation export** — One-click export of plot polygons (>4 ha) or GPS points (<4 ha) in the exact GeoJSON schema EU due-diligence systems consume, bundled with lot linkage — making Janson a turnkey, deforestation-free supplier the moment a roaster asks.
- **Map-linked drill-down & spatial search** — Tap any parcel to open its agronomy, harvest, processing and task history in context, and query the farm spatially ('all Geisha above 1,600 masl on north-facing slopes due for renovation') — so land decisions are made on the map, not in a table.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Sentinel Hub / Copernicus Data Space (Sentinel-2 basemap + Process & Batch Statistical API for per-parcel NDVI), Planet Labs (PlanetScope high-res imagery + Field Boundaries Planetary Variable for boundary detection), Copernicus DEM / NASA SRTM (30m elevation for slope/aspect/elevation-zone derivation), Mapbox or MapLibre + OpenStreetMap (vector basemap rendering, free tier / open-source), Esri ArcGIS Online / QGIS (Shapefile + GeoJSON + KML import-export interop for surveyors and consultants), PostGIS on the Supabase/Postgres backend (free, native geometry storage + spatial queries), Panama ANATI cadastral registry & finca numbers (catastro / Mensura Catastral as the legal tenure source), EUDR / EU TRACES due-diligence GeoJSON geolocation standard (regulatory export target, live for coffee Dec 2025 / micro-operators Jun 2026), Mobile GPS / GNSS field capture (phone or handheld GPX/RTK for walking boundaries and dropping tree-block points)

**Data model additions:** Parcel (geometry POLYGON/MULTIPOLYGON in PostGIS, replacing the flat Plot.areaHa with computed area; centroid, perimeter), ParcelHierarchy / Block / SubBlock / Row (self-referential parent_id tree over the existing Block A–E labels), ElevationZone (per-parcel min/mean/max masl, slope %, aspect, derived from DEM; supersedes the single altitudeMasl scalar), CadastralTitle (finca_number, owner, tenure type owned|leased, lease_term, ANATI boundary geometry, title_doc_url), MapLayer (named overlay: satellite, NDVI, soil, reserve boundary, hydrology — toggle state + source), BoundaryRevision (versioned geometry history: who/when/why an edge changed, for an auditable land record), GeoPoint / TreeBlockMarker (point features for sample trees, mill, drying patios, water sources, signage), ReserveArea (the 200-ha quetzal-habitat conservation polygon as a first-class protected geometry), EudrDeclaration (plot polygon snapshot + lot linkage + deforestation-free cutoff date, export-ready)

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-detect field boundaries from satellite imagery (Planet Field Boundaries / segmentation) so the operator confirms polygons instead of tracing every block by hand.
- Varietal-to-terroir fit recommender: cross elevation/slope/aspect zones with varietal performance to flag 'this north-facing 1,400 masl block is mismatched for Geisha' and suggest replant cohorts.
- Deforestation / land-cover change detection on each parcel boundary (compare imagery against the Dec-2020 EUDR cutoff) to auto-flag any parcel at compliance risk before a buyer asks.
- Natural-language spatial search ('show Geisha above 1,600 masl due for renovation') that compiles to a PostGIS query, so non-technical family members can interrogate the map by typing.
- Plot-area & tree-count reconciliation: detect drift between the legally titled area, the drawn operational boundary, and the recorded tree count, and surface the discrepancy.

**World-class:** A living digital twin of the finca: every square meter of Janson's land — managed blocks, the 200-ha private nature reserve, titled vs. leased parcels — is a versioned geometry in PostGIS, layered with satellite imagery, an elevation/slope/aspect model, and cadastral title. The operator works primarily on the map; tables are a secondary view. The system knows that Tizingal Alto is a 4.2 ha Geisha block on a south slope at 1,650–1,690 masl inside finca #X, and can in one action emit a regulator-grade EUDR polygon, a buyer-facing terroir story, or a renovation plan by elevation cohort. Boundary edits are history-tracked (so the farm's land record is auditable over decades), spatial queries answer 'where' questions instantly, and the reserve boundary is first-class — making conservation a provable, mappable asset, not a tagline.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Volcán terroir is the whole brand — Janson sells 1,350–1,700 masl Geisha and reserve lots, and altitude/slope/aspect is what makes a competition-grade micro-lot. A real elevation-zone GIS turns 'high-altitude Panama Geisha' from marketing copy into queryable, per-parcel proof, and lets the family deliberately match varietal to band. The 200-ha private nature reserve (quetzal habitat) becomes a first-class mapped, protected geometry — a verifiable conservation and shade-grown story for premium buyers. Critically, coffee is now live under EUDR (Dec 2025; micro-operators Jun 2026): every kilo of green coffee into the EU needs the GPS polygon of its origin plot plus a deforestation-free declaration tied to the Dec-2020 cutoff. Plot-level geometry tied to Janson's existing lot codes (JC-541…) makes them an EUDR-ready origin and a defensible direct-trade partner, not a farm scrambling at the border.


#### Satellite crop monitoring — NDVI/NDRE vegetation health & change detection
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm Agronomist / production manager at Janson (the person who today walks plots and hand-sets each plot's status to healthy/watch/at-risk). Secondary: the farm owner (portfolio-level vigor and yield-risk view) and the field supervisors who get routed to the exact GPS sub-zone flagged as declining.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I manage 200+ hectares across plots at 1,350-1,700 masl that I can't physically walk every week, I want each plot's health status to be set by measured vegetation data instead of my last eyeball inspection, so I can trust the dashboard's healthy/watch/at-risk flag and stop relying on memory and luck.
- When a leaf-rust (roya) outbreak or water stress starts in one corner of a plot, I want a satellite to flag the declining sub-zone within days and drop a GPS pin, so I can send a scout to that exact spot before the stress spreads across the block and costs me Geisha cherries.
- When clouds cover Volcan for two straight weeks (as they routinely do in the highlands), I want the system to fall back to radar (SAR) and gap-fill so my vigor map doesn't just go blank, so I get a usable health read every week regardless of cloud cover.
- When I plan fertilization and pruning for next month, I want to compare this season's NDVI/NDRE curve against the same plot's prior seasons and against expected phenology, so I can target inputs to the blocks that are actually underperforming instead of spreading nitrogen evenly.

**Features:**
- **Plot vigor map (NDVI/NDRE choropleth over plot boundaries)** — Turns each plot's hand-set status into a measured, color-graded canopy-health layer; the agronomist sees within-plot variability (the declining NW corner) instead of one flat status pill.
- **Vegetation time-series per plot with phenology baseline** — A per-plot NDVI/NDRE chart across the season vs. prior years and an expected curve, so dips that precede yield loss are visible weeks early and fertilization/pruning is targeted to underperforming blocks.
- **Change-detection & anomaly alerts** — Auto-flags statistically significant week-over-week vigor drops in a sub-zone, drops a GPS pin, and auto-promotes the plot's status to watch/at-risk + spawns a scouting task assigned to the nearest crew — catching rust/water-stress before it spreads.
- **Cloud-aware acquisition engine (Sentinel-2 optical + Sentinel-1 SAR fusion + cloud-mask gap-fill)** — Guarantees a usable weekly health read in a perpetually cloudy highland by masking clouds (s2cloudless/SCL), falling back to radar backscatter when optical is unusable, and showing a clear data-freshness/confidence badge so nobody trusts a stale cloud-blocked pixel.
- **NDRE nitrogen / chlorophyll stress layer** — Red-edge index reads canopy chlorophyll and N status more sensitively than NDVI in dense shade-grown coffee, so the agronomist can distinguish nutrient deficiency from water stress and prescribe the right intervention.
- **Reserve & shade-canopy monitoring layer** — Extends the same engine to the 200-ha private reserve and shade trees — detects illegal clearing, canopy loss, or shade-pct drift (which the model already tracks per plot), directly supporting the quetzal-habitat conservation story.
- **Yield-forecast assist from cumulative vigor** — Correlates seasonal integrated NDVI with the plot's expected_yield_kg/harvested_kg already in the model to refine the season target per plot — sharpening labor and buyer-commitment planning.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem (free quota) — Sentinel-2 L2A optical for NDVI/NDRE; Statistical API for per-plot aggregates and Process API/openEO for tiles; the $0-native primary source, Sentinel-1 SAR (C-band backscatter, also via Copernicus) — cloud-penetrating radar fallback for the perpetually clouded highland, Sentinel Hub (EO Browser free; paid Processing Units if scale grows) as a hosted alternative to self-hosting the imagery pipeline, AWS Open Data Registry Sentinel-2 COGs + the s2cloudless / Sen2Cor SCL scene-classification cloud masks, Planet Labs PlanetScope (3 m, near-daily) and Planet Crop Biomass — future-bet paid tier for high-resolution gap-filling when free 10 m isn't enough, Microsoft Planetary Computer / STAC catalogs for analysis-ready imagery discovery, Mapping/GIS layer: MapLibre GL or Leaflet + Turf.js for plot-boundary GeoJSON polygons and zonal stats (all open-source, $0), Standards: GeoJSON / OGC, STAC for catalog, COG (Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF), and Cropster (export NDVI-derived vigor context into the roast/QC traceability record)

**Data model additions:** satellite_observations — one row per plot per acquisition: plot_id FK, captured_at, source (sentinel-2|sentinel-1|planetscope), cloud_pct, scene_id/stac_item, valid (post-mask) boolean, plot_vegetation_indices — plot_id FK, observation_id FK, index_kind (ndvi|ndre|evi|savi), mean, min, max, stddev, p10/p90 percentile (zonal stats over the plot polygon), plot_boundaries — plot_id FK, geometry (GeoJSON Polygon / PostGIS geography), centroid, source/digitized_by (the current model has no spatial geometry at all), vegetation_anomalies — plot_id FK, sub_zone geometry/centroid (GPS pin), detected_at, delta_vs_baseline, severity, status (open|scouting|confirmed|dismissed), linked_task_id FK to tasks, phenology_baselines — plot_id FK + variety, day-of-season, expected_ndvi band (computed from prior seasons) for the time-series comparison, satellite_tiles — cached COG/PNG raster refs (object-storage URL + bbox + index_kind) so the vigor map renders without re-fetching imagery

**AI / automation:**
- Anomaly detection on the per-plot NDVI/NDRE time series (seasonal-decomposition / STL + z-score, or a small per-plot Prophet-style model) to separate true vigor decline from normal phenology and cloud-noise — fires the change-detection alert.
- Cloud/shadow gap-filling that learns each plot's Sentinel-2-to-Sentinel-1 relationship so radar can stand in for optical with a calibrated NDVI proxy during cloud-locked weeks.
- Image-to-cause classifier: given the NDRE/NDVI signature, spectral shape, and season, suggest the likely driver (leaf rust / roya, water stress, nutrient deficiency, shade overgrowth) to prime the scouting task — an LLM-assisted explanation grounded in the indices, not a guess.
- Cumulative-vigor-to-yield regression feeding the existing expected_yield_kg per plot, retrained each season against actual harvested_kg.
- Natural-language plot briefing ('Tizingal Alto NDRE down 12% in the NW corner over 2 weeks, likely N stress; prior 3 seasons stayed flat here') auto-drafted for the agronomist's weekly review.

**World-class:** A world-class implementation guarantees a fresh, trustworthy vigor read for every plot every week despite tropical cloud cover — by fusing Sentinel-2 optical with Sentinel-1 radar and showing an explicit data-confidence/freshness badge so a cloud-blocked pixel is never silently passed off as healthy. It detects change at the sub-plot (sub-hectare) level, not just the plot level, drops a GPS pin a supervisor can navigate to, and closes the loop by auto-promoting plot status and spawning a scouting task — turning a passive map into an action. It runs entirely on free Copernicus quota at Janson's scale, caches derived statistics (not raw rasters) so it never re-downloads, and reconciles satellite signal with ground truth (the agronomist confirms or rejects each alert, training the anomaly threshold over time).

**Coffee-specific angle:** Volcan sits in cloud forest at 1,350-1,700 masl, so persistent cloud cover is THE technical problem — which is exactly why Sentinel-1 SAR fusion (cloud-penetrating radar) is non-negotiable here and is the differentiator over a naive optical-only NDVI tool. NDRE specifically matters for shade-grown specialty coffee because dense canopy and shade trees saturate plain NDVI, while red-edge still reads chlorophyll/N status. The signal directly powers the value story: catching roya (leaf rust) and water stress early protects high-value Geisha and reserve/competition lots whose price per pound dwarfs the cost of acting late. The same engine doubles as a conservation monitor for the 200-ha private quetzal reserve and the shade canopy (the model already tracks shade_pct per plot), giving Janson a verifiable, satellite-backed sustainability and origin-story narrative for buyers — and it all runs on free Copernicus quota at this farm's scale, fitting the $0 constraint.


#### Drone mapping & multispectral aerial imagery — stand counts, stress maps
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm agronomist / production manager at Janson (the person who today sets each plot's `status` of healthy/watch/at-risk by walking the rows and stamping `last_inspected`). Secondary stakeholders who consume the outputs: the owner (capital + planting decisions, EUDR sign-off), block supervisors (where to send pickers/sprayers tomorrow), and the green-coffee sales/compliance lead (who must hand buyers a plot polygon + deforestation-free statement).

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I suspect a plot is sliding from healthy to at-risk but I can only walk a fraction of 200+ ha of steep, shaded terrain each week, I want a recent farm-wide stress map that flags the exact rows declining, so I can ground-truth and treat the problem before roya or drought spreads across the block.
- When the season's planting/replanting budget is finite, I want an objective tree-by-tree stand count and gap/missing-tree map per plot (not the round number 14,800 someone typed in 2014), so I can re-seed only the gaps and trust my yield forecast and density math.
- When a European green buyer or our certifier demands EUDR-grade proof, I want an accurate GPS boundary polygon for every plot plus dated imagery showing no forest was cleared after 31 Dec 2020, so I can ship into the EU and protect our specialty premiums and our 200-ha quetzal reserve story.
- When I plan the day's crews, I want yesterday's drone-derived hotspots overlaid on the same plot map the rest of the app uses, so a 'spray Paso Ancho rows 12-18' task is created automatically and assigned, instead of me re-describing where on a radio.

**Features:**
- **Plot boundary & GIS layer (the missing geometry)** — Adds true polygon/area to every plot record — replaces the hand-typed area_ha with a surveyed boundary, auto-computes hectares, and becomes the spatial backbone the whole agronomy pillar (irrigation, scouting, yield-per-ha) inherits. Directly satisfies the EUDR polygon requirement.
- **Drone flight & survey log** — Track each mission (date, drone, pilot, plots covered, weather, GSD, % overlap, sun-sensor calibration) so every imagery product is traceable and repeatable — turns ad-hoc flights into a comparable time series.
- **Orthomosaic & vegetation-index map viewer (NDVI / NDRE / GNDVI)** — A buttery glass map tile viewer with a date slider: flip between RGB orthomosaic and false-color NDVI/NDRE/GNDVI per plot. The agronomist sees stress zones before the human eye can — Red-Edge (NDRE) catches sub-clinical canopy decline in dense shade where plain NDVI saturates.
- **Automated stand count & gap/missing-tree map** — Per-plot tree count and a gap map from the imagery — replaces the static `trees` integer with a measured number and a re-seeding worklist, sharpening yield forecasts and planting spend.
- **Stress hotspot detection -> auto-scouting task** — Cluster declining pixels into ranked hotspots, drop a geotagged pin, and auto-create a Pest Control / Soil scouting task (existing task entity) assigned to a crew — closes the loop from 'an image flagged it' to 'someone is standing there tomorrow.'
- **Plot health time-series & change detection** — Mean NDVI/NDRE per plot over flights, charted next to the existing healthy/watch/at-risk status, so the status badge becomes data-driven and a sudden drop raises an alert instead of waiting for the next foot inspection.
- **Shade-canopy coverage measurement** — Derive actual canopy/shade % from the imagery to replace the typed shade_pct — material for a shade-grown / agroforestry claim and for managing the quetzal-habitat reserve buffer.
- **EUDR / certification evidence pack export** — One-click GeoJSON polygon set + dated baseline imagery + due-diligence statement per lot, exportable for buyers and certifiers — turns a compliance scramble into a button.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** DJI Mavic 3M (Multispectral) — RGB + G/R/Red-Edge/NIR bands with onboard sunlight/irradiance sensor; primary capture hardware (200 ha on one battery suits Janson's footprint), MicaSense RedEdge-P / Parrot Sequoia+ as alternative multispectral payloads (Pix4Dfields-compatible), Pix4Dfields — offline laptop processing of RGB+multispectral into orthomosaics, NDVI/NDRE index maps, AI stand counts and boundary digitizing (no cloud cost; fits the $0/offline constraint), DroneDeploy — cloud alternative with plant-health/crop-stress algorithms and tiled map serving, DJI Terra — capture-to-orthomosaic for DJI ecosystem, Sentinel-2 (ESA Copernicus, free 10 m, 5-day revisit) and Sentinel Hub / Google Earth Engine — free satellite NDVI between drone flights for cheap whole-farm trend baselines, Planet / Soar / NICFI tropical basemaps — historical imagery for the EUDR 31-Dec-2020 deforestation baseline, Global Forest Watch / JRC Global Forest Cover deforestation-alert layers for EUDR overlay, GeoJSON / Shapefile / GeoTIFF / Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF + OGC WMTS/XYZ tiles as the interchange standards, MapLibre GL (open-source, $0) for the in-app glass map renderer; PostGIS for plot geometry in the existing Supabase/Postgres, EU TRACES / EUDR Information System for due-diligence statement submission, Certifier bodies — Rainforest Alliance, Fair Trade, SCA — as consumers of the geolocation + canopy evidence

**Data model additions:** plot_geometry — adds a PostGIS polygon (and optional centroid) to each existing plot; surveyed area_ha derived from it (extends the current plots anchor rather than replacing the text PK), drone_surveys — one row per flight: date, drone_model, pilot (-> workers), plots covered, weather, ground sample distance, overlap %, irradiance-calibrated flag, processing platform, imagery_products — per survey+plot artifact: kind (rgb_ortho | ndvi | ndre | gndvi | dsm | canopy), tile_url/storage_ref, capture_date, GSD, plot_health_observations — per survey+plot metrics: mean/median NDVI, mean NDRE, stressed_area_ha, derived_status; the data source behind the healthy/watch/at-risk badge, stress_hotspots — geotagged point/polygon clusters with severity, suspected_cause (rust | drought | nutrient | gap), area, and a link to the scouting task it spawned, stand_counts — per survey+plot measured tree_count, gaps_detected, density_per_ha (supersedes the static plots.trees), canopy_measurements — measured shade/canopy coverage % per plot per survey (supersedes static shade_pct), eudr_evidence — per plot/lot: polygon GeoJSON ref, baseline imagery ref, deforestation-alert status, due-diligence statement id (ties into the existing lots traceability codes)

**AI / automation:**
- CNN/decision-tree coffee-leaf-rust (roya) classifier on NDVI/NDRE/GNDVI tiles — published models hit 80-91.5% accuracy on arabica; flags rust onset days before it's visible from the row
- Object-detection stand counting — auto-count individual coffee trees and detect canopy gaps / dead trees from the orthomosaic (Pix4Dfields AI + a custom shade-coffee-tuned model, since dense shade confuses generic counters)
- Anomaly/change detection across flights — alert when a plot's mean NDRE drops beyond a threshold vs its own trend, auto-flipping status to 'watch' and pinging the agronomist
- Auto-prescription / variable-rate zones — segment a plot into management zones and propose targeted fertilizer/spray maps, generating draft tasks instead of blanket treatment
- Cause inference — fuse hotspot shape, weather, and altitude to suggest rust vs drought vs nutrient deficiency vs simple tree-gap, pre-filling the scouting task's category
- Natural-language brief — an LLM summary per flight ('Paso Ancho: NDRE down 12% on the SE slope, ~340 trees stressed, pattern consistent with rust; recommend scouting rows 12-18') attached to the activity feed

**World-class:** "World-class is a living digital twin of the farm where every plot is a true surveyed polygon, re-flown on a regular cadence (drone for resolution + free Sentinel-2 for cheap continuity), and the healthy/watch/at-risk status is computed from NDRE trend and rust-model output rather than typed in after a foot inspection. The agronomist opens the map each morning to ranked, geotagged hotspots that already became assigned scouting tasks, with an LLM brief explaining the likely cause. Stand counts and canopy % are measured, not remembered, so yield forecasts and planting budgets are defensible. And the same geometry layer doubles as the EUDR/certification evidence engine — one click produces polygon GeoJSON plus dated baseline imagery — so compliance is a byproduct of good agronomy, not a separate fire drill. Crucially it stays inside the $0 envelope: offline Pix4Dfields processing, MapLibre + PostGIS for rendering, and free Copernicus/GFW layers, with the paid drone being a one-time piece of farm equipment, not a recurring SaaS bill."
<parameter name="coffee_specific">"This is uniquely high-leverage for Janson specifically. (1) Geisha is the crown jewel — its premium justifies tree-level vigilance, and NDRE catches stress in Janson's high-shade canopy (48-60% shade on the Geisha plots) where ordinary NDVI saturates. (2) Roya (coffee leaf rust) is the existential disease for arabica at 1,350-1,700 masl; drone multispectral gives days of early warning across steep terrain a scout can't fully cover. (3) The 200-ha private nature reserve is both a marketing asset and an EUDR liability — canopy mapping documents the quetzal-habitat shade and proves no forest was cleared after 2020, turning the reserve into verifiable provenance rather than an unverified claim. (4) Janson sells green coffee, so EUDR (live for large operators 30 Dec 2025, SMEs 30 Jun 2026) makes plot polygons a hard condition of EU market access — this capability is the single feature that protects export revenue. (5) Altitude and slope vary plot to plot (Las Lagunas at 1,700 m vs Palmira at 1,390 m); per-plot geometry lets agronomy decisions respect that instead of treating the farm as one block."


#### IoT soil & microclimate sensors — moisture, NPK, pH, leaf-wetness, temp
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm Agronomist (Janson's resident agrónomo who walks the lots and decides irrigation, fertilization, and fungicide timing). Secondary stakeholders consuming the same data: the Plot/Block Supervisor (acts on per-block alerts), the Owner/Production Manager (sees aggregate plot-health and cost-of-water), and the Mill side downstream (microclimate context for harvest-window decisions). The agronomist is the one who installs/maintains nodes and interprets readings, so the system is built to make her field-decision job faster and evidence-backed rather than gut-feel.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When the dry-season rains are inconsistent and my younger Geisha blocks are at 1,650 masl, I want to see real per-block soil-moisture and root-zone tension at multiple depths, so I can irrigate only the blocks that actually need it and stop guessing from a shovel test.
- When warm humid nights follow rain and leaf surfaces stay wet, I want a per-microclimate-zone coffee-leaf-rust (roya) risk score from on-leaf wetness + temp + RH, so I can spray copper/fungicide on exactly the at-risk blocks at the right window instead of calendar-spraying the whole farm.
- When a cold front threatens the high lots overnight, I want a frost/low-temp early warning pushed to my phone, so I can run protective measures before bud and cherry damage happens.
- When I plan the next fertilization pass, I want trended soil pH, EC and NPK by block against agronomic targets, so I can apply lime and nutrients site-specifically and prove to the owner the spend was justified by the soil data.

**Features:**
- **Sensor node & gateway registry** — One canonical place to register every LoRaWAN node, its gateway, depth/orientation config, battery and last-seen heartbeat — so a silent or dead sensor is itself an alert, not a blind spot, and field techs know exactly which node to service.
- **Per-block live soil & microclimate dashboard (glass tiles + sparklines)** — Map/block view showing current moisture (multi-depth), root-zone tension, soil temp, pH, EC, NPK, air temp/RH, and leaf wetness with 7/30-day trends — turns the read-only plot card into a live agronomic instrument.
- **Coffee-leaf-rust (roya) risk engine** — Per-zone daily rust-risk score from leaf-wetness duration + cardinal-temperature window + RH + altitude + shade%, with a spray-window recommendation — the highest-value coffee-specific output; converts validated rust models into a fungicide-timing decision that saves both yield and chemical cost.
- **Irrigation-need & water-budget advisor** — Combines soil tension thresholds with ET/forecast to flag exactly which blocks need water and how much — drives the documented 'cut water ~50% while holding yield' outcome and feeds a cost-of-water line for the owner.
- **Threshold-based alert center (frost / drought-stress / pH drift / sensor-offline)** — Configurable per-block thresholds that push notifications (frost overnight, soil too dry, pH out of range) so action happens before damage, replacing reactive after-the-fact inspection.
- **Soil-target & amendment ledger** — Per-block agronomic targets (pH 5.0–5.5 for coffee, target EC/NPK) with trended readings and an applied-amendment log, so lime/fertilizer decisions are site-specific and auditable against the soil data that justified them.
- **Sensor-to-lot context binding** — Stamps the microclimate/soil conditions of the source block onto each harvest lot and processing batch — so a buyer-facing lot story (and the cupping team) can see the exact terroir conditions the cherries grew under.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** LoRaWAN network servers: The Things Stack (open-source, self-host or community TTN), Helium (pay-per-packet, no gateway capex), LORIOT, Senet — webhook/MQTT uplink ingestion, Sensoterra soil-moisture probes + Sensoterra Cloud API (soil calibration library, irrigation thresholds, best-in-class REST API), RAK Wireless WisNode soil sensors (RK500-series soil pH / moisture / EC / NPK, LoRaWAN + NB-IoT), Sentek Drill & Drop multi-depth capacitance probes (root-zone moisture/temp/salinity by depth), Davis Instruments weather stations + Barani Design MeteoHelix LoRaWAN stations + Decagon/METER Group dielectric leaf-wetness sensors (LWS) for the rust model, Seeed SenseCAP S2120 8-in-1 LoRaWAN weather sensor as a low-cost microclimate node, Weather/ET context APIs: Open-Meteo (free) and OpenWeather One Call for forecast + evapotranspiration, cross-referenced against on-farm readings, Satellite NDVI/canopy providers (Sentinel-2 via Sentinel Hub / Planet) to corroborate ground sensors at block scale, MQTT broker + JSON payload-codec standards (CayenneLPP / TTN payload formatters) and webhook HMAC verification for secure ingestion, Push delivery to the agronomist's phone (web push / Twilio SMS / WhatsApp Business API for rural low-data field use)

**Data model additions:** sensor_gateways (id, name, network_server enum[the-things-stack|helium|loriot|senet], eui, plot_id?, last_seen, status), sensor_nodes (id, gateway_id, plot_id, dev_eui, vendor enum[sensoterra|rak|sentek|davis|barani|sensecap], kind enum[soil|microclimate|leaf-wetness|weather], depth_cm?, orientation?, battery_pct, last_seen, status enum[online|stale|offline]), sensor_channels (id, node_id, metric enum[moisture|tension|soil_temp|ph|ec|npk_n|npk_p|npk_k|air_temp|rh|leaf_wetness|rainfall], unit, target_min?, target_max?), sensor_readings (id, channel_id, observed_at timestamptz, value numeric) — append-only time-series, the one high-volume table; partition/rollup by day, block_microclimate_zones (id, plot_id, name, altitude_band, shade_pct) — groups nodes for zone-level rust/frost scoring, rust_risk_scores (id, zone_id, date, leaf_wetness_hrs, mean_temp, mean_rh, score numeric, band enum[low|moderate|high|severe], spray_recommended bool), sensor_alerts (id, node_id?, zone_id?, kind enum[frost|drought-stress|ph-drift|rust-risk|sensor-offline|battery-low], severity priority, opened_at, acknowledged_by worker_id?, resolved_at), soil_amendments (id, plot_id, date, amendment, rate, applied_by worker_id, reading_basis_id?) — links a fertilizer/lime application to the reading that justified it, alert_thresholds (id, channel_id|zone_id, kind, comparator, value) — the canonical 'promise' store enforced server-side, not in the UI

**AI / automation:**
- Per-zone coffee-leaf-rust risk model (start with the validated leaf-wetness-duration + cardinal-temperature + RH regression / ExpeRoya-style qualitative model, evolve to an ML classifier trained on Janson's own outbreak history) producing a spray-window recommendation
- Frost/low-temp nowcasting that fuses on-farm air-temp nodes with forecast APIs to fire an overnight warning hours ahead
- Irrigation optimization that learns each block's soil-tension dry-down curve to recommend volume + timing and quantify water saved
- Anomaly detection on the readings stream to catch a drifting/biased sensor (flatlined, out-of-physical-range, divergent from neighbors) before it pollutes decisions
- Plant-health gap-fill: fuse sparse ground sensors with satellite NDVI to interpolate a continuous stress map across blocks that lack a node
- Natural-language field assistant: 'which blocks should I irrigate this week and why' answered from the live readings, targets, and forecast

**World-class:** World-class is a farm that has effectively replaced the shovel-and-gut-feel inspection loop with a live, per-block agronomic nervous system: every microclimate zone reports soil moisture at multiple depths, root-zone tension, pH/EC/NPK, air temp/RH, leaf wetness and rainfall over LoRaWAN with multi-day battery autonomy and self-reporting health, so a dead node is an alert not a silent gap. Decisions are pushed, not pulled — the agronomist gets a frost warning hours before dawn, a roya spray-window flagged on the exact at-risk blocks days before symptoms, and an irrigation list scoped to the blocks that genuinely dropped below tension threshold. Thresholds and agronomic targets live in one canonical, server-enforced place (not hardcoded in the UI), every amendment is logged against the reading that justified it, and the terroir conditions of each block flow downstream onto the harvest lot story. The result is the documented industry outcome — roughly half the water for equal-or-better yield, targeted rather than calendar fungicide, and a fully auditable agronomic record — delivered through a buttery, low-data field UI that works on a phone in a wet ravine at 1,650 masl.

**Coffee-specific angle:** This is where IoT pays off uniquely for Janson rather than for generic row-crop ag. (1) Roya/coffee-leaf-rust is the existential disease of Central American Arabica; the validated predictors are precisely leaf-wetness duration + cardinal temperature + RH — exactly what these microclimate nodes measure — so Janson can run a real per-block rust risk engine and spray surgically. (2) Janson's 1,350–1,700 masl altitude spread is both a frost-risk axis (high Geisha lots) AND a rust-suppression axis (cooler = slower rust), and the existing plots table already carries altitude_masl and shade_pct, which feed the rust/frost models directly. (3) Geisha is the farm's premium, terroir-defined cup; binding each lot's microclimate signature (the soil and weather the cherries actually grew under) to the lot code turns sensor data into a buyer-facing provenance story and a cupping-correlation dataset — high-value differentiation for a reserve specialty producer selling green and roasted. (4) The 200-hectare quetzal reserve and shade canopy mean shade% is a live agronomic lever, not a fixed setting — sensors let Janson tune shade against rust and microclimate empirically.


#### Irrigation scheduling & water management/rights
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **7/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the Farm Agronomist / Production Manager (at Janson, often the owner-operator or head agrónomo) who owns when, where, and how much to irrigate across the 10 plots spanning 1,360-1,700 masl. Secondary actors: the irrigation/field technician who opens valves, services pumps and checks emitters; the finca administrator who tracks the water-concession (ANATI/MiAMBIENTE) compliance, pump energy cost, and water budget; and downstream, the QC/processing lead who cares because pre-harvest water stress and post-bloom irrigation timing directly shape cherry size, Brix and Geisha cup quality.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When the dry-season (Dec-Apr) Pacific slope of Barú dries out and my soil-moisture sensors and a satellite ET model say plot X is approaching its stress threshold, I want the system to compute a per-plot irrigation prescription (mm to apply, run-time per valve zone) from real evapotranspiration, rainfall, soil water-holding capacity and canopy shade, so I can apply exactly enough water to protect the trees without wasting water, pump diesel/power, or my limited concession allocation.
- When the rains break at the end of the dry season and I want a single uniform bloom (so harvest is concentrated and pickers aren't sent out for tiny scattered passes), I want to deliberately impose and then release a controlled water deficit (regulated deficit / stress-and-flush irrigation) with a guided schedule, so I can trigger synchronized flowering across a plot and lift both yield concentration and Geisha cup quality.
- When I hold a water concession with a permitted annual/seasonal volume and MiAMBIENTE or ANATI can audit it, I want every liter pumped from the quebrada, spring or well metered, logged per source and reconciled against my permit limits and water-balance, so I can stay compliant, renew the concession, and prove sustainable water use for my certifications and direct-trade buyers.
- When a pump trips, a mainline bursts, or a zone valve fails to close overnight, I want an immediate alert with the affected plot, the water/energy already lost, and the likely cause, so I can dispatch a technician before a plot goes dry or I blow my water budget on a leak.

**Features:**
- **Per-plot irrigation scheduler with soil-water-balance engine** — Turns weather + ET + soil + sensor data into a concrete daily/weekly prescription per plot and per valve zone (mm to apply, minutes to run), replacing guesswork — saves water, pump energy and labor while preventing under/over-watering.
- **Irrigation zones & infrastructure registry (sources, pumps, mainlines, valves, emitters, sensors)** — A live map/inventory of the physical water system tied to existing plots, so every prescription, run, alert and meter reading has a real asset to attach to and maintenance can be scheduled against it.
- **Soil-moisture & flow telemetry dashboard** — Streams sensor depths (e.g. 20/40/60 cm), flow meters and tank/reservoir levels into one screen with field-capacity and wilting-point bands, so the agronomist sees stress before the canopy shows it.
- **Regulated-deficit / bloom-trigger playbook** — A guided, plot-by-plot stress-then-flush workflow that times the controlled dry-down and the flush irrigation to synchronize flowering — the coffee-specific lever that concentrates harvest and sharpens Geisha quality.
- **Irrigation run log & water-balance ledger (metered actuals vs prescribed)** — Records what was actually applied per zone/source/event versus the plan, computing liters and cost per plot and per kg of cherry, so the farm learns its true water productivity (kg cherry / m^3 water).
- **Water-rights & concession compliance tracker** — Models each permitted source, its allocation, seasonal limits and renewal dates; reconciles metered abstraction against the permit and warns before overdraw — keeps the farm legal with ANATI/MiAMBIENTE and audit-ready for buyers.
- **Fault & leak alerting + irrigation maintenance scheduler** — Detects abnormal flow / pressure / unexpected overnight runs and a pump that won't prime, raises an alert with estimated water + energy lost, and opens a maintenance task — stops slow leaks and dry plots.
- **Fertigation planner (irrigation x nutrition)** — Couples water events with injected fertilizer dosing so nutrient delivery rides the irrigation schedule, improving uptake efficiency and tying water spend to the existing Fertilizing task category.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Open-Meteo and/or OpenWeather One Call API for hourly Volcán forecast + historical rainfall (free tiers; replaces the static 5-day mock), FAO-56 Penman-Monteith reference evapotranspiration (ET0) computed from weather inputs (open standard; the agronomic core of the scheduler) with crop coefficient Kc for coffee, OpenET / satellite ET providers and Sentinel-2 NDVI (Copernicus/Sentinel Hub, or Planet) for plot-level actual-ET and canopy vigor without ground sensors, NASA POWER + CHIRPS rainfall datasets for long-run dry-season water-balance baselines in Chiriqui, Soil-moisture & weather hardware: Davis Instruments, Sentek/Drill-&-Drop, METER Group TEROS, or low-cost LoRaWAN probes via The Things Network; flow meters and tank-level sensors over MQTT, Irrigation controllers: Netafim NetBeat / GrowSphere, Rivulis Manna, Hunter/Rain Bird/Galcon valve controllers, and Lindsay/Valley FieldNET where pivots/pumps are networked, ANATI / MiAMBIENTE Panama water-concession (concesion de uso de agua) records and permit limits for compliance tracking, Rainforest Alliance / Smithsonian Bird Friendly / organic certification water-use and watershed criteria as compliance rule sets, Pump energy: utility (Naturgy/ENSA Panama) tariff or fuel cost per kWh / liter of diesel to price each irrigation event, Cropster / processing system link so pre-harvest water-stress and bloom timing carry forward to lot QC records

**Data model additions:** irrigation_zones (id, plot_id -> plots, name, area_ha, emitter_type drip|micro-sprinkler|none, flow_rate_lph, design_application_rate_mm_h, controller_id, status), water_sources (id, name, type spring|quebrada|well|reservoir|rainwater, concession_id, max_seasonal_m3, lat/lon, status), water_concessions / permits (id, authority ANATI|MiAMBIENTE, permit_no, source_id, annual_limit_m3, season_limit_m3, valid_from, valid_to, renewal_due, status), irrigation_equipment (id, kind pump|mainline|valve|filter|injector, zone_id/source_id, model, install_date, last_serviced, status), soil_moisture_readings (id, zone_id/plot_id, sensor_id, depth_cm, vwc_pct, captured_at) plus sensor registry with field_capacity/wilting_point/readily_available_water, weather_observations & et0_daily (date, ref_et0_mm, rainfall_mm, temp, rh, wind, source) - upgrades the static weather table to real time series, irrigation_prescriptions (id, plot_id, zone_id, for_date, soil_water_deficit_mm, recommended_mm, run_minutes, mode normal|regulated_deficit|bloom_flush, rationale, status proposed|approved|skipped), irrigation_events / runs (id, zone_id, source_id, started_at, ended_at, volume_m3_metered, mm_applied, energy_kwh, cost_usd, fertigation_batch_id?, prescription_id?), water_balance_daily (plot_id, date, et_mm, rain_mm, irrigation_mm, soil_water_storage_mm, deficit_mm), irrigation_alerts (id, zone_id/equipment_id, type leak|no-flow|overrun|pump-fault|permit-overdraw, severity, raised_at, est_water_lost_m3, est_cost_usd, resolved_at), fertigation_recipes & injections (id, event_id, product, dose, n_p_k) linking to the Fertilizing task domain

**AI / automation:**
- ET- and ML-driven irrigation recommendations: blend FAO-56 ET0, satellite actual-ET, soil sensors, rain forecast and per-plot soil water-holding capacity into a next-7-day prescription, learning each plot's true crop coefficient from realized soil-moisture response
- Bloom-trigger optimizer: an agent that, given the dry-down state and the incoming rain forecast, recommends the exact stress duration and flush date/volume per plot to synchronize flowering for concentrated harvest and Geisha quality
- Anomaly detection on flow/pressure/energy telemetry to flag leaks, stuck valves and failing pumps in near-real-time, with an estimated water and dollar loss and a probable-cause hint
- Forecast-aware skip/defer: automatically propose skipping a scheduled run when high-probability rain is incoming (saves water and avoids waterlogging on the wet Barú slope)
- Natural-language ops assistant: 'Did Paso Ancho get its water this week, how close am I to my concession limit, and what's my cost per m^3?' answered from the water-balance ledger
- Water-productivity analytics: correlate irrigation regime with downstream yield (harvests), Brix and cup score to surface which water strategy actually pays per plot/variety

**World-class:** World-class is a closed sensor-to-valve-to-ledger loop: every plot has a continuously updated soil-water balance fed by real Volcan weather, FAO-56/satellite ET, and ground sensors; the system issues a per-zone prescription each morning that a networked controller (Netafim/Rivulis/Rain Bird) can execute or a technician confirms; every liter is metered per source and auto-reconciled against the ANATI/MiAMBIENTE concession with overdraw warnings; deficit-irrigation bloom strategies are planned and measured against realized flowering and cup quality; leaks and pump faults page someone within minutes with quantified water/energy loss; and the farm can prove, to a Rainforest Alliance auditor or a direct-trade buyer, its exact water use, water productivity (kg cherry and cup-points per m^3), and watershed stewardship for the 200-ha reserve. It is precision-ag rigor applied to a specialty highland farm, not a sprinkler timer.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Volcan sits on Baru's Pacific slope with a sharp dry season (Dec-Apr) on top of otherwise high rainfall, so irrigation here is supplemental and strategic, not constant. The killer coffee-specific lever is regulated-deficit / stress-and-flush irrigation to trigger a single uniform bloom: deliberately drying the trees post-harvest then flushing water (or anticipating the first rains) concentrates flowering, which concentrates the harvest window, reduces scattered low-yield picking passes, and is widely associated with better, more even Geisha cup quality. For Janson, water management is also a brand and certification story: a 200-ha private nature reserve and quetzal habitat means watershed stewardship and metered, permit-compliant abstraction from springs/quebradas are part of the sustainability narrative that premium green and direct-trade Geisha buyers pay for. Tying water-stress and bloom timing to specific traceability lot codes (JC-xxx) links the irrigation regime directly to the cup in the bag.


#### Weather stations & hyperlocal forecasting — frost/heat/rain alerts
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Don Miguel Janson — the farm owner/head agronomist who carries the weather risk on his shoulders (with the field supervisor / crew leads, e.g. the "Crew Norte" lead, as the people who act on the alerts). He stands to lose a whole Geisha micro-lot to one bad cold night or a mistimed rain on the drying patios, and today he reads the sky off the Barú volcano and a single farm-wide phone forecast.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a clear, still night is forecast over my highest Geisha plots (Las Lagunas 1,700 m, Tizingal Alto 1,690 m), I want a per-plot overnight low + radiative-frost/cold-stress alert hours in advance, so I can fire up irrigation/wind machines or send crew to protect the trees before the cold settles in the hollows — not discover scorched leaves at dawn.
- When I have green coffee and parchment laid out on the drying patios/raised beds, I want a hyperlocal next-6-hour rain probability and humidity alert for the mill yard specifically, so I can decide whether to rake-and-cover or leave the beds open and protect moisture targets (13.5%) and cup quality.
- When I'm planning the week's fieldwork (pruning, fertilizing, spraying for broca/roya), I want a plot-by-plot forecast plus a spray/leaf-wetness window, so I can schedule crews on the days each microclimate block will actually be workable and not waste a paid crew-day to an afternoon downpour.
- When the season ends, I want the actual logged temperature, rainfall, GDD and chill/heat-stress history per plot, so I can explain a low-yield or off-flavor lot, defend my certification/climate claims, and plan next year's planting and flowering windows with evidence instead of memory.

**Features:**
- **Plot-resolved hyperlocal forecast (microclimate engine)** — Replaces the single farm-wide 5-day strip with a forecast per plot/elevation band (1,360–1,700 m, Blocks A–E), elevation- and shade-adjusted, so the owner sees that Las Lagunas at 1,700 m will hit 9°C tonight while Palmira at 1,390 m stays at 14°C — turning one generic number into ten actionable ones.
- **Threat alert engine (frost/cold-stress, heat, excess-rain, leaf-wetness, wind)** — Configurable per-plot thresholds (e.g. overnight low < 10°C on Geisha, >35mm/6h rain on the drying yard, prolonged leaf-wetness for roya risk) that push SMS/WhatsApp/web-push alerts with enough lead time to act, so a protectable loss is actually prevented instead of merely recorded.
- **On-farm station ingestion + ground-truth dashboard** — Ingests live readings from one owner-purchased weather station (temp, humidity, rain, wind, pressure, solar) and shows actual vs. forecast, calibrating the hyperlocal model to the farm's real microclimate and giving the trusted ground truth that off-the-shelf forecasts for 'Volcán' can never match in mountain terrain.
- **Drying-yard / processing weather guard** — A mill-yard-specific rain/humidity/dew-point widget wired into the processing batches view, flagging 'cover the beds' or 'safe to leave open' for each active drying lot — directly protecting moisture targets and cup quality on parchment worth thousands per bag.
- **Agronomy weather windows + spray/workability calendar** — Overlays workable-day and spray-window forecasts onto the tasks calendar so pruning, fertilizing and pest-control are scheduled into the days each block will actually be dry/workable, protecting paid crew-days and chemical efficacy.
- **Climate history & GDD/chill-stress ledger per plot** — A permanent, queryable record of temperature, rainfall, growing-degree-days and cold/heat-stress events per plot per season — the evidence base for explaining yield/quality variance, supporting flowering-date prediction, and backing certification and climate-resilience claims.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** WeatherFlow Tempest weather station (one-time hardware purchase, NO subscription) + Tempest REST/UDP API for live on-farm readings — fits the $0-recurring constraint, Davis Instruments WeatherLink (alternative pro-grade station + WeatherLink API) if the farm already owns Davis hardware, Open-Meteo free forecast API (no key, free for non-commercial/low volume) with elevation parameter — the zero-cost hyperlocal forecast backbone, queried per plot lat/lon/altitude, IMHPA (Instituto de Meteorología e Hidrología de Panamá) public station/forecast data + ETESA hydromet network for regional Chiriquí context, The Weather Company / OpenWeatherMap / Tomorrow.io agricultural weather APIs (paid hyperlocal + frost/severe-weather alerts) as an upgrade path beyond free tiers, Pessl Instruments METOS / FieldClimate and Sencrop — full agronomic station ecosystems (disease models, frost alerts) named as the commercial gold-standard upgrade if budget ever opens, Twilio / WhatsApp Business API or Telegram Bot API for push alert delivery to crew leads' phones (free Telegram path honors the $0 constraint), Windy / Ambient Weather PWS network for crowd-sourced nearby-station cross-checks, NASA POWER + ECMWF/ERA5 reanalysis for historical climate backfill and GDD baselines, WMO observation standards and FAO/Penman-Monteith ET0 reference for evapotranspiration/irrigation math

**Data model additions:** weather_stations — physical/virtual stations (id, name, vendor, lat, lon, altitude_masl, plot_id or block, api_source, last_seen), weather_observations — time-series live readings (station_id, observed_at, temp_c, humidity_pct, rain_mm, wind_kmh, gust_kmh, pressure_hpa, solar_wm2, leaf_wetness, dew_point_c), weather_forecasts — per-plot/per-station forecast snapshots (plot_id, source, issued_at, valid_at, temp_hi, temp_lo, rain_pct, rain_mm, humidity_pct, wind_kmh, icon) — supersedes the current flat farm-wide weather table, alert_rules — per-plot thresholds (plot_id, metric, operator, threshold, lead_time, channel, active) e.g. overnight_low < 10 on Geisha plots, weather_alerts — fired alert events (rule_id, plot_id, triggered_at, severity, predicted_value, message, acknowledged_by, acted_on, linked_task_id), plot_climate_daily — derived daily rollups per plot (plot_id, date, min_c, max_c, rain_mm, gdd, chill_hours, heat_stress_minutes, leaf_wetness_hours) feeding the seasonal ledger, Extends plots with lat/lon (today plots only have altitude_masl) so each plot can be geolocated for the forecast engine

**AI / automation:**
- Localized downscaling model that blends the on-farm station's ground truth with the regional Open-Meteo/IMHPA forecast to correct for elevation, slope/aspect and cold-air-pooling in the hollows — learning each plot's offset over time (e.g. Las Lagunas always runs 3°C cooler at night than the valley forecast).
- Frost/cold-stress nowcasting: predict overnight minimum per plot from current dew point, cloud cover, wind and clear-sky radiative cooling, and rank tonight's protect-or-not decision per plot with a confidence band.
- Natural-language morning briefing ('Cold risk on Las Lagunas and Tizingal tonight — protect by 9pm; mill yard dry through noon, good for raking JC-552; spray window closes Tuesday') generated from the forecast + active drying lots + open tasks.
- Pest/disease pressure modeling driven by leaf-wetness and temperature history — broca (coffee berry borer) and roya (leaf rust) risk indices per plot that auto-create scouting tasks when thresholds trip.
- Auto-scheduling: AI proposes the optimal day for each open agronomy task by matching its weather requirements (dry, low wind, no rain 24h post-spray) against the per-plot forecast, and re-flags tasks when the forecast shifts.
- Anomaly detection on station readings (sensor drift, blocked rain gauge, stuck temp) so a dead guard doesn't silently produce false 'all clear' nights.

**World-class:** "World-class is the farm operating on its own calibrated microclimate model, not a generic 'Volcán' forecast: every plot has a forecast tuned by an on-site station and historical offsets, frost/cold and excess-rain alerts arrive on the crew lead's phone with hours of actionable lead time and a one-tap 'protect' task that dispatches the right crew to the right plot, the drying yard has a dedicated rain/dew guard wired to active processing lots, and every degree-hour and millimeter is permanently logged into a per-plot climate ledger that explains yield/quality variance and feeds flowering prediction. The owner trusts it enough to stop reading the sky off the volcano — the system tells him which of his ten plots is at risk tonight, and proves it was right the next morning. Closed loop: forecast -> alert -> task -> action -> logged outcome -> model improvement."

**Coffee-specific angle:** "Geisha is Janson's crown jewel and grows on their two highest plots (Las Lagunas 1,700 m, Tizingal Alto 1,690 m) — exactly the elevation band most exposed to radiative cold nights and cold-air pooling in the highland hollows, and exactly the lots where a single bad night destroys the most per-kg value. Panama highland coffee rarely sees true freezing frost, so the real, under-served threats are sub-10°C cold-stress on flowering/cherry development, mistimed afternoon green-season rain hitting open drying beds (moisture-target and cup-quality killer), and humidity/leaf-wetness driving roya and broca — none of which a generic forecast surfaces per plot. The 200-hectare quetzal reserve and the brand's specialty/altitude story make a documented per-plot climate ledger a marketing and certification asset, not just an ops tool. And the $0-forever constraint shapes the build: a one-time Tempest station plus free Open-Meteo + IMHPA + Telegram alerts delivers the differentiated outcome without any recurring SaaS bill."


#### Pest & disease — coffee borer (broca) & leaf rust (roya) IPM, scouting logs
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Janette, the farm agronomist (worker w-02), and the field scouting supervisors/crew leads who walk the rows. Janette owns crop-health decisions across all plots; she is the one logging scouting counts, deciding when broca (Hypothenemus hampei) infestation or roya (Hemileia vastatrix) incidence crosses an economic threshold, and prescribing the IPM response. Secondary users: pickers/scouts who capture field observations on a phone, and the farm owner who needs to see disease pressure as a portfolio risk before it eats yield and cup quality.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I walk a plot during the rainy season and see leaves spotting or bored cherries on the ground, I want to log a geotagged scouting observation with a standardized incidence/severity count in under 30 seconds on my phone offline, so I can build a real infestation map instead of relying on memory and a paper notebook.
- When broca counts or rust incidence in a block cross the published economic threshold (e.g. >5% bored cherries, or rust incidence climbing past ~10% of leaves), I want the system to flag that block and tell me exactly which trees and what action to take, so I can intervene before the threshold becomes a yield-and-grade loss instead of after.
- When I see 6+ consecutive days of warm nights, leaf wetness and high humidity on a high block, I want an early-warning rust/broca pressure forecast tied to that specific plot and varietal, so I can pre-position traps, sprays and labor before an outbreak rather than chase it.
- When I apply a control (Beauveria bassiana, copper/triazole spray, trap servicing, sanitation/pepena), I want to record the product, dose, applicator, target pest, pre-harvest interval and re-entry interval against the plot, so I can prove the IPM history for organic/Rainforest Alliance certification and never harvest inside a withholding window.

**Features:**
- **Mobile field-scouting capture (offline-first PWA)** — A scout drops a geotagged observation per scouting point — pest/disease type, count of bored/diseased units out of N sampled, severity class, photo — in seconds, even with no signal on a high block; auto-syncs on reconnect. Turns paper notebooks into a living infestation dataset.
- **Threshold engine + economic-threshold alerts** — Per-pest, per-varietal action thresholds (broca % infestation, roya incidence/severity index) computed from scouting counts; a block flips plot.status to watch/at-risk and raises a prioritized alert with the recommended IPM action and labor estimate — intervene at the threshold, not after the loss.
- **Plot-level infestation & disease heatmap** — A block/tree-row choropleth of broca pressure and rust incidence over time, overlaid on the existing plots map, so the owner and agronomist see hotspots, spread vectors and which Geisha lots are at risk to cup quality at a glance.
- **IPM intervention & spray log (with PHI/REI safety)** — Every control event (biological, cultural, chemical) recorded with product, active ingredient, dose, area, applicator, target pest, and auto-computed pre-harvest interval and re-entry interval — blocks harvest scheduling inside a withholding window and produces the audited treatment history certifiers demand.
- **Broca trap network + sanitation tracker** — Register alcohol/methanol-ethanol trap locations, log weekly catch counts and re-baiting, and track pepena/repela sanitation passes — quantifies trap pressure and proves the cultural-control backbone of broca IPM that no spray can replace.
- **Pressure forecast & scout-route planner** — Weather-driven rust/broca risk score per plot (degree-days, leaf-wetness, humidity windows) that recommends which blocks to scout next and pre-stages traps/sprays/labor — shifts the program from reactive firefighting to forecast-led prevention.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Cropster (Cropster Cultivate / farm-management) for shared lot + crop-health data exchange, Open-Meteo and Visual Crossing weather APIs for leaf-wetness, humidity and degree-day inputs to the rust/broca pressure models, CABI PlantwisePlus & coffee rust epidemiology models (PROMECAFE / CATIE / WCR roya-resistance varietal data) as the agronomic knowledge base, Plantix / iNaturalist Seek + a fine-tuned vision model (Claude vision or TF Lite on-device) for photo-based pest/disease ID, World Coffee Research variety catalog for varietal rust-susceptibility ratings (Geisha and Caturra are highly susceptible; Catimor/Marsellesa resistant), Rainforest Alliance / USDA Organic / Global GAP certification schemas for IPM and pesticide-record reporting, plus Panama's MIDA / AILA pesticide registry for legal product + PHI lookups, EPA/PAN pesticide active-ingredient + PHI/REI reference data; Beauveria bassiana (BroccaPlus / Mycotrol) biopesticide product catalogs, Sentinel-2 / Planet NDVI + red-edge satellite imagery to corroborate canopy stress hotspots against ground scouting

**Data model additions:** scouting_sessions — a scout's walk of a plot on a date (worker_id, plot_id, date, weather snapshot, gps track, points_sampled), scouting_observations — one record per scouting point/tree: pest_disease_type, sampled_n, affected_n, computed incidence_pct, severity_class, lat/lng, photo_url, notes, pest_disease_catalog — reference of pests/diseases (broca, roya, ojo de gallo, nematodes, antracnosis) with action thresholds, lifecycle, host varietals and susceptibility ratings, pest_thresholds — per pest × varietal economic/action threshold values and the recommended IPM response template, ipm_interventions — control events: plot_id, target pest, method (biological/cultural/chemical), product, active_ingredient, dose, area_ha, applicator worker_id, applied_at, phi_days, rei_hours, traps — broca trap registry (plot_id, lat/lng, type, installed_date) and trap_catches (trap_id, date, count, rebaited bool), pest_alerts — threshold-breach + forecast alerts (plot_id, pest, severity, recommended_action, status) that drive plot.status and generated tasks, plot_risk_scores — daily weather-driven rust/broca pressure score per plot feeding the forecast and scout-route planner

**AI / automation:**
- Photo-to-diagnosis: a scout snaps a leaf/cherry, an on-device + Claude-vision pipeline classifies broca vs roya vs ojo de gallo vs nutrient deficiency and pre-fills the observation with a confidence score
- Forecast-led outbreak prediction: a model fusing scouting history, plot weather (leaf wetness, warm nights), varietal susceptibility and altitude to predict rust/broca pressure 7–14 days out per plot
- Auto-prioritized scout-route planner that tells the agronomist which blocks to walk today and the minimum sampling points for statistical confidence
- Threshold-to-action automation: when an observation crosses threshold, auto-draft the IPM intervention task (product, dose, labor, target rows) into the existing Tasks board for one-tap approval
- Natural-language IPM copilot: 'where is broca worst and what do I spray on the Geisha in Block A given we harvest in 9 days?' answered with PHI-safe, certification-aware recommendations
- Certification-report generator that compiles the full season's scouting + intervention log into a Rainforest Alliance / Organic IPM audit packet

**World-class:** World-class is a closed prevention loop, not a logbook: forecast-led pressure scoring per plot pre-positions traps, biocontrol and labor before an outbreak; offline-first phone scouting makes capturing a statistically valid sample frictionless for crews who do not type; threshold breaches auto-flip plot status and auto-draft a PHI-safe, certification-aware intervention into the task board; vision AI removes the misidentification that wastes spray; and every control is traceable down to the lot so cup quality and certification are provably protected. The benchmark is intervening at 5% broca / first rust pustules — measured, mapped and acted on within a day — versus the typical reactive farm that notices only after defoliation and graded-down lots. It connects ground truth (scouting), sky (NDVI/weather) and action (interventions) in one auditable system tied to the very lots that get cupped.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Roya and broca are the two existential threats to a Panamanian high-altitude specialty farm, and Janson's economics make the stakes unusually high: its flagship Geisha and Caturra are highly rust-susceptible varietals, and a single bored or defective cherry drags a $200+/lb auction-grade Geisha micro-lot down a grade. Volcán's 1,350–1,700 masl bands and bimodal rains create exactly the warm-night, high-humidity windows that triggered the 2012–13 Central American roya epidemic — so per-plot, altitude-aware forecasting matters more here than on a low commodity farm. The 200-hectare quetzal reserve and Janson's specialty/reserve positioning push the program toward biological and cultural control (Beauveria, shade management, trap networks, pepena sanitation) over heavy chemistry, and the IPM record directly feeds the Rainforest Alliance / organic story that the brand sells on. Tying pest pressure to specific lot codes connects this pillar straight to the processing and green-coffee quality the farm is famous for.


#### Soil health & fertility — testing, amendment & fertilization plans
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: Janette, the resident Agronomist (worker role "Agronomist", w-02) who owns plot health decisions. Secondary: the Farm Owner/Manager (Janson family) who approves the annual fertilizer budget and signs off on organic/certification compliance, and the Block Supervisor (Miguel, w-01) who schedules and verifies the actual amendment applications by field crews.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a plot's vigor or yield drops (e.g. Paso Ancho is 'at-risk' at 4,100 kg vs 12,600 kg target), I want to see that plot's latest soil-test results, leaf-tissue analysis, and nutrient-deficiency history in one place, so I can diagnose whether it's a soil/nutrition problem before I waste a season guessing.
