- Deforestation-free anomaly watch: scheduled NDVI/forest-loss diff on each plot polygon that flags any canopy loss against the EUDR baseline before it becomes a compliance or auditor problem.
- Practice recommender: given a plot's SOC trend, shade %, altitude and variety, suggest the next-best regenerative intervention and quantify expected SOC gain and likely carbon-credit value.
- Auto-draft the auditor/buyer narrative: assemble the MRV dossier and write the plain-language 'what we did and the measured outcome' summary from the structured event log, citing each claim back to a logged event.

**World-class:** "World-class is a closed loop where every regenerative action is logged once at the row, automatically reconciled against a continuously-modeled soil-carbon and canopy time series (sparse lab samples fused with free Sentinel-2 imagery and cheap IoT probes), and rendered on demand as a verifier-ready Verra VM0042 / Rainforest Alliance dossier and an EUDR-compliant deforestation-free statement — with zero double-entry. The farm stops treating sustainability as a paperwork cost and starts treating soil carbon and biodiversity as quantified, sellable assets. Crucially it never lies: every premium claim ('regeneratively grown, +X tC/ha since 2024, quetzal-habitat shade') traces to a timestamped, geotagged, ideally third-party-measured event. That is the difference between greenwashing and a credible specialty story buyers pay double for."

**Coffee-specific angle:** "This is Janson's deepest moat, not a bolt-on. Janson is 1,350–1,700 masl shade-grown Geisha inside a 200-ha private quetzal reserve — the regenerative/biodiversity story IS the brand and the price premium. The system can valorize their own waste streams: coffee husk and pulp from the wet/dry mill become logged biochar and vermicompost inputs (a closed nutrient loop unique to a farm that runs the full grow-to-green chain). Multi-strata Inga/native shade that protects quetzal habitat becomes a measured canopy asset feeding both carbon and biodiversity claims. And because Volcán's high-altitude microclimate and the reserve are non-replicable, quantified soil-carbon + biodiversity outcomes let Janson command Geisha-tier prices and access EU buyers post-EUDR while neighboring farms get locked out."


### Harvest & People

#### Harvest planning & ripeness-based pick rounds by plot
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Farm manager / harvest planner (at Janson this is Janette Janson, the agronomist, working with the field supervisor Miguel Janson and the four crew leads). She owns the season calendar: she decides which plots are ready, when the next pasada (pick round) happens on each plot, and which crew goes where each morning. Secondary daily users are the crew leads (Crew Norte, Crew Tizingal) who receive the per-plot pick assignment, and the wet-mill lead who needs advance warning of incoming cherry volume and ripeness so fermentation tanks and patios are staged.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When peak harvest hits across 10 plots spanning 1,360-1,700 masl and trees ripen on staggered 8-10 day cycles, I want to see at a glance which plots are at picking ripeness TODAY and which are 2-5 days out, so I can sequence pick rounds by altitude/microclimate and never strip a plot underripe or let a Geisha lot tip overripe.
- When I plan tomorrow's work, I want the system to recommend a per-plot pick round (round number, target ripeness window, crew size, and expected cherry kg) and auto-route my crews so high-value Geisha at peak Brix is always picked first by my best pickers, so I protect the premium lots and keep every crew productive.
- When a 3-day rain front or a heat spike is forecast, I want my ripeness/maturation curve to re-forecast which plots will reach peak before or after the weather window, so I can pull a round forward or hold it and avoid picking wet, splitting cherry, or losing fruit to over-maturation and berry borer.
- When the season winds down on a plot, I want to schedule and track the rebusca/repela (final strip pass) distinctly from selective rounds, so I clean the tree to reset flowering and suppress coffee berry borer, and I can prove to my crew leads the plot is fully closed for the season.

**Features:**
- **Plot ripeness board (today / soon / not-yet)** — A kanban-style board ranking all 10 plots by readiness — driven by a per-plot maturation curve (days-since-bloom + accumulated GDD + last-round ripenessPct/Brix trend). Manager instantly sees Las Lagunas + Tizingal Alto (Geisha, 1,690-1,700m) at 96-98% ripe = pick now, vs a plot 4 days out = hold. Eliminates the daily walk-every-plot guesswork.
- **Pick-round scheduler (pasada calendar by plot)** — Each plot gets a recurring round cadence (default 8-10 days, tightened/loosened by the maturation model). The scheduler lays rounds on a season calendar, tracks round number per plot (Round 3 of ~7), flags overdue rounds in red, and books the rebusca/repela as a distinct closing round. Turns ad-hoc picking into a planned, auditable season.
- **Crew assignment & route optimizer** — Given which plots are ready and each crew's location/skill, it proposes the morning assignment — best Geisha pickers to peak Geisha plots first, crew sized to expected kg, ordered to minimize truck movement between blocks. Crew leads get a phone-friendly assignment card. Lifts kg/picker-day and protects premium lots.
- **Ripeness-window forecast & weather-aware re-plan** — A 10-day per-plot maturation forecast (peak-ripeness window with a confidence band) that re-computes when the weather feed changes — surfacing 'pull Barú Vista forward 2 days, rain front Thu' or 'hold Talamanca, +2 days to peak Brix'. Converts weather risk into concrete schedule moves before fruit is lost.
- **Field ripeness sampling (mobile, offline-first)** — Crew leads log a quick pre-round ripeness sample per plot — phone-camera cherry photo + a Brix reading + tap-count of ripe/green/overripe — which calibrates the maturation curve against reality. Works offline in low-signal field zones and syncs later. Grounds the forecast in ground truth, not just GDD math.
- **Round-to-lot handoff to mill** — When a round is scheduled/completed it auto-creates the day's expected lot codes and pushes a heads-up to the wet mill (expected kg, variety, avg ripeness/Brix, process intent) so fermentation tanks and raised beds are staged before cherry arrives. Closes the planning-to-processing gap that today is verbal.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** On-farm weather station + API — Davis Instruments WeatherLink / Arable Mark 2 (or free tier: Open-Meteo / Visual Crossing) for hourly temp to compute Growing Degree Days per plot microclimate, Satellite/aerial NDVI & canopy ripeness — Sentinel-2 via Sentinel Hub / EOS Crop Monitoring / Planet Labs for plot-level vigor and maturation proxies, Cropster (Cropster Origin / C-sar) — the de-facto specialty-coffee origin platform, for lot traceability and cupping-score feedback that closes the loop on round timing vs cup quality, Atago / DiFluid R2 digital Brix refractometer (Bluetooth) for direct sugar-content sampling logged per round, WhatsApp Business Cloud API — how Panamanian crew leads actually receive the daily pick assignment and confirm completion, ICO / SCA harvest-window and ripeness-grade standards; Panama SCAP (Specialty Coffee Association of Panama) Best of Panama lot conventions for premium Geisha, Twilio / SMS fallback for assignment delivery in low-data field zones, Open-Meteo or NOAA/IMHPA Panama precipitation forecast feed for the weather-aware re-plan (free tier honors the $0 constraint)

**Data model additions:** PickRound — { id, plotId, roundNumber, type: 'selective' | 'rebusca', status: 'planned'|'ready'|'in-progress'|'done'|'skipped', plannedDate, targetRipenessPct, expectedCherriesKg, assignedCrew, completedDate, actualCherriesKg, lotCode }, RipenessForecast / MaturationCurve — { plotId, asOf, bloomDate, accumulatedGdd, peakWindowStart, peakWindowEnd, confidencePct, currentRipenessPct, projectedRipenessPctByDay[] }, RipenessSample — { id, plotId, sampledBy, sampledAt, ripeCount, greenCount, overripeCount, brix, photoUrl, syncedAt } (field ground-truth, offline-first), CrewAssignment — { id, date, crewId, plotId, pickRoundId, plannedPickers, sequenceOrder, status }, Plot extensions — bloomDates[] (multiple per season), roundCadenceDays, microclimateZone, lastRoundDate, nextRoundDue, harvestWindowStart/End, BloomEvent — { id, plotId, observedDate, intensity, triggeredByRainEvent } (anchors the GDD-to-maturation clock for each fruiting wave)

**AI / automation:**
- Per-plot maturation/ripeness-window prediction model fusing accumulated GDD (weather station), days-since-bloom, NDVI trend, altitude, shade%, and the trailing ripenessPct/Brix from prior rounds — outputs the peak-pick window with a confidence band
- Computer-vision cherry-ripeness grading from the crew-lead phone photo (classify red/green/overripe %, estimate plot-level readiness) — calibrates the forecast against field reality and removes subjective eyeballing
- Weather-triggered schedule re-optimization agent — watches the precip/heat forecast and proactively proposes round pull-forwards/holds with the kg-at-risk and quality rationale, surfaced as a 'needs your OK' suggestion to the manager
- Crew-routing optimizer — assigns crews to ready plots to maximize premium-lot-at-peak-Brix coverage and kg/picker-day while minimizing inter-block travel, learning each picker's variety-specific throughput from harvest history
- End-of-season retrospective: correlate round timing/ripeness against Cropster cupping scores to recommend next season's cadence per plot ('Tizingal Alto cupped highest when picked at 95-97% ripe, 8-day cadence — tighten Barú Vista to match')

**World-class:** World-class is a living, weather-aware harvest plan that turns 10 staggered plots into a single optimized pick sequence: the manager opens one board each morning and sees exactly which plots are at peak ripeness, which crew goes where, expected kg, and a 10-day forecast that auto-re-plans when rain or heat shifts the maturation curve — with every premium Geisha lot reliably picked at uniform 95-97% ripeness and peak Brix, zero plots stripped underripe or lost to over-maturation/berry-borer, the rebusca tracked to closure on every plot, and each round's timing later validated against its cup score so the cadence compounds in quality season over season. It feels like Cropster Harvest fused with a vineyard's GDD-driven pick-date model, tuned for Volcán microclimates and delivered to crew leads over WhatsApp on a $5/day-picker reality.

**Coffee-specific angle:** This is THE defining workflow for Janson's value proposition. Their entire premium rests on Geisha picked selectively at uniform peak ripeness — and their plots span 1,360-1,700 masl, so higher Geisha lots (Las Lagunas 1,700m, Tizingal Alto 1,690m) mature noticeably later than lower Caturra/Catuaí/Typica blocks, forcing a deliberately sequenced multi-week pick calendar with 8-10 day pasadas per plot. The existing data already encodes this signal: Geisha lots run Brix 22.6-23.9 and ripeness 93-98%, while lower-altitude Caturra/Catuaí sit at Brix ~19-20 and ripeness 85-91% — the model can lean on altitude + variety + shade% to forecast each plot's window. The rebusca/repela closing pass is genuinely coffee-specific (resets flowering, suppresses coffee berry borer). And because picking is the most labor-intensive, highest-cost step on a Panamanian farm ($22/day pickers), getting the right crew to the right plot on the right day is where margin and Best-of-Panama-grade quality are simultaneously won or lost.


#### Picker/crew management & daily field dispatch
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the Jefe de Cuadrilla / Harvest Supervisor (Janson's "Field Ops" lead, e.g. Miguel Janson) who at 5am decides which crews pick which lots today, tracks who showed up, weighs in each picker's cans through the day, and answers to the owner for cherry quality and labor cost. Secondary personas it serves: the Ngobe-Bugle crew lead (capataz) who rosters and vouches for their own family/village group; the picker who wants their kilos counted fairly and to be paid in full, on time; the owner/agronomist (Janette Janson) who needs labor-cost-per-kg, attendance, and compliance records; and the camp/comisariato manager handling on-farm housing, meals, and store-credit advances for migrant families.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When it's 5am and I know which lots are at peak ripeness, I want to assign each crew and picker to the right field zone with a target volume, so I can clear the ripe lots before the cherries over-ripen or rain hits, without scribbling assignments on paper nobody else can see.
- When a picker brings a full can (lata/cajuela) to be weighed at a collection point on a steep hillside with no cell signal, I want to capture their kilos against their ID and the correct lot in three seconds offline, so I pay them fairly to the gram and keep cherry traceable to its block from the very first weigh-in.
- When the harvest week ends and I have 60+ seasonal Ngobe-Bugle pickers paid by volume plus day-rate crew, I want piece-rate pay, attendance, and any commissary/store-credit advances to roll up automatically into payslips and one payout run, so everyone is paid correctly the same day and I have a clean labor-cost-per-kg and compliance record.
- When a crew lead recruits their village group for the season, I want to onboard the whole family unit (who's housed in camp, dietary needs, emergency contacts) and track day-by-day attendance and productivity, so I can plan housing and meals, catch a no-show crew early, and re-invite my best pickers next season.

**Features:**
- **Morning Dispatch Board (drag-to-assign)** — Supervisor assigns crews/pickers to plots and field zones with a per-lot target (kg or rows) on a glass board; ripeness-driven so today's ripe Geisha lots get the experienced hands. Replaces the paper roster and prevents two crews colliding on one block or a ripe lot being missed.
- **Offline mobile weigh-in (Bluetooth scale + picker badge)** — Tap a picker (RFID/QR badge or photo tile), drop their can on a Bluetooth scale, and the kg auto-posts to that picker, lot, and field zone in 3s; tare-by-container. Works fully offline at a hillside collection point and syncs when signal returns. Each weigh-in is the genesis traceability event and the genesis pay event in one tap.
- **Live crew & picker productivity (kg/hr, today vs target)** — Real-time leaderboard and per-crew progress bars so the supervisor sees who's behind target, which lot will finish today, and which picker is a future crew lead, instead of guessing until the evening weigh-out.
- **Seasonal roster & family-unit onboarding** — Onboard the Ngobe-Bugle workforce as crews tied to a crew lead, capturing family units, cedula/ID or none, housing assignment, dietary needs, emergency contact, and rehire history. Turns verbal, every-season-from-scratch recruitment into a one-tap rehire and an accurate housing/meal headcount.
- **Piece-rate payroll run + advances/commissary ledger** — At week end it sums each picker's kg x lot rate (with minimum-wage top-up where volume falls short), nets out cash advances and comisariato store credit, and produces a payslip per worker and one payout batch. Pays correctly same-day and exposes true labor-cost-per-kg by lot.
- **Attendance / clock-in & no-show alerts** — Geofenced or badge check-in at the field marks present/absent/rest-day automatically and flags an under-strength crew at 6am, so the supervisor can pull pickers from another lot before half a day is lost.
- **Quality-at-pick gate (ripeness/float deductions)** — At weigh-in the supervisor can log a ripeness grade or float-test reject %, which protects the lot's cup quality and feeds a fairness-preserving quality bonus/penalty into the picker's pay line.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** 2nd Sight FairPick / QuickPick / FairTrak harvest scales and RFID worker badges (de-facto piece-rate hardware standard), Generic Bluetooth LE weighing scales via the Web Bluetooth API for low-cost weigh-in, PickApp / Croptracker / AgriERP piece-rate engines as reference models for rate + minimum-wage top-up logic, Cropster C-sar / Cropster Origin for handoff of lot + first-mile traceability into processing/QC, QR / NFC / RFID badge encoding (low-cost picker IDs that survive no-cedula migrant workers), Yappy and Nequi (Panama mobile-money / ACH rails) plus Banco General / Banco Nacional batch payout files for same-day cash-alternative pay, Panama MITRADEL salario minimo agricola tables + CSS (Caja de Seguro Social) reporting for seasonal-labor compliance, WhatsApp Business API for crew-lead dispatch messages and payslip delivery (the channel this workforce actually uses), Offline-first sync (PWA + IndexedDB / service worker) for dead-zone hillsides; Starlink/satellite uplink at the beneficio as the sync point

**Data model additions:** Crew (id, name, crew_lead worker id, season, default_zone) - promotes today's free-text Worker.crew string into a real entity, CrewMembership (worker_id, crew_id, season, role: lead | picker | day-rate, rehire_of_prior_season), FieldZone / pick block (plot_id, zone polygon/geofence, rows, current ripeness window) - finer than a Plot, the unit you actually dispatch to, DispatchAssignment (date, crew_id or worker_id, field_zone_id, target_kg, status: planned | active | done), WeighIn (id, worker_id, lot_code, field_zone_id, gross_kg, tare_kg, net_kg, ripeness_grade, float_reject_pct, captured_at, captured_offline, device_id) - genesis pay + traceability event, Attendance (worker_id, date, status, check_in_at, check_out_at, source: badge | geofence | manual), PieceRate (lot_code or variety, season, rate_per_kg, min_wage_floor) and PayrollRun (period, status) + PaySlip (worker_id, kg_total, piece_pay, topup, quality_adjust, advances_deducted, net_pay), AdvanceLedger / Comisariato entry (worker_id, type: cash advance | store credit, amount, date), CampAssignment (worker_id/family_unit, housing_unit, meals_plan, dietary_notes, emergency_contact) - extends Worker for migrant on-farm living

**AI / automation:**
- Ripeness-aware auto-dispatch: suggest the morning crew->lot plan from plot ripeness windows, weather, distance, and each picker's historic kg/hr, so the board opens pre-filled and the supervisor only adjusts
- Live yield-vs-target forecasting: predict by 9am whether each lot will finish today and recommend pulling pickers between zones
- Anomaly/fairness guardrails on weigh-ins: flag implausible kg, duplicate badge taps, or a picker whose rate suddenly doubles (theft/error), and flag pay lines that fall below the minimum-wage floor before payout
- Picker-quality scoring: correlate each picker's lots to downstream Brix/ripeness and cup scores to identify who consistently picks cleaner cherry, feeding both bonuses and next-season rehire ranking
- Spanish/Ngabere voice + photo capture: speak or photograph a can count when hands are full and gloves are wet; OCR legacy paper tally sheets into WeighIns
- Natural-language end-of-day brief: auto-generate the owner's evening summary (kg by lot, labor cost/kg, attendance, lots needing a return pass)

**World-class:** World-class is a zero-paper harvest where, at 5am, an AI-proposed dispatch plan is on every supervisor's phone keyed to which lots hit peak ripeness overnight; every can is weighed offline in 3 seconds against a badged picker and a geofenced block, so the first traceability link in the cup's story is the picker's own hands; pickers and crew leads see their running kilos and earnings live in Spanish/Ngabere on their own phones and trust the number; payroll is a one-tap run that respects Panama's agricultural minimum wage, nets commissary advances, and pays out same-day via Yappy or a bank batch with a WhatsApp payslip; and the owner gets a true labor-cost-per-kg per lot plus a rehire-ranked roster for next season. It treats the Ngobe-Bugle workforce as named, returning partners with portable reputation, not anonymous day labor, while staying $0 to run on free tiers and offline-first hardware the farm already owns.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson's harvest is hand-picked on steep 1,350-1,700 masl terrain by a ~90% Ngobe-Bugle indigenous seasonal migrant workforce paid by volume (the lata/cajuela can), where a strong picker does ~300 lb/day. That makes selective, ripe-only picking the single biggest lever on cup quality for their Geisha and reserve lots, and makes fair, same-day, fraud-free piece-rate pay both an ethics and a retention issue (good pickers are scarce and return to crew leads they trust). Tying the weigh-in to the lot from the first can preserves the micro-lot traceability that Panama Geisha buyers pay record auction prices for, and onboarding whole housed family units fits Janson's on-farm camp/comisariato reality that generic ag-labor tools ignore.


#### Piece-rate harvest capture — per-picker weight by plot, mobile/offline
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the harvest weigh-station recorder / crew supervisor (e.g. Miguel Janson, "Field Ops" supervisor) who logs every picker's cherry weight at the end of each picking round. Secondary personas served by the same data: the individual picker (mostly seasonal Ngäbe-Buglé migrant labor who want a fair, transparent record of what they earned per lata/kg), the farm owner/manager (Janette Janson) who needs accurate daily labor cost and yield-per-plot, and the bookkeeper who runs harvest payroll.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a picker comes to the weigh station mid-row with a half-full sack and there is no cell signal under the canopy at 1,690 masl, I want to capture their weight, plot, and ripeness against their identity in under 10 seconds and have it sync later, so I can keep the line moving and never lose a record.
- When the harvest day ends and I have logged 14 pickers across 8 plots, I want each picker's total kg auto-converted to pay at the current piece-rate (with quality bonuses for high-ripeness Geisha lots), so I can pay correct wages that same evening without a paper ledger or end-of-day math errors.
- When a seasonal picker who cannot read fluently disputes their tally, I want to hand them an instant printed/SMS receipt showing every weigh-in with time, plot, and running total, so trust is preserved and I avoid the daily arguments that drive good pickers to competing farms.
- When I am the owner reviewing the season, I want every kilo of cherry tied to a picker, plot, lot code, and timestamp, so I can compute true cost-per-kg by plot and varietal and feed verified cherry volumes straight into the wet-mill intake and lot traceability.

**Features:**
- **Offline-first mobile weigh-station (PWA)** — Records a weigh-in (picker + plot + kg + ripeness + timestamp) with zero connectivity under the canopy; queues locally in IndexedDB and auto-syncs when signal returns, so no record is ever lost and the line never stops.
- **Picker identity by tap — RFID/NFC card or QR badge scan** — One tap resolves the picker (vs. spelling names for migrant workers with similar names), eliminating mis-attributed weights and the illegible-paper-ticket bottleneck at the scale.
- **Bluetooth digital scale capture (legal-for-trade)** — Auto-reads weight from a paired NTEP-certified scale (deduct tare for the lata/sack), removing manual keying errors and giving disputes a defensible, certified number.
- **Piece-rate engine with quality + plot multipliers** — Versioned rate table (base $/kg or $/lata) plus bonuses for ripeness% and premium Geisha lots auto-computes each picker's daily pay, turning raw weights into payroll with no spreadsheet.
- **Instant picker receipt — thermal print + WhatsApp/SMS** — Every picker leaves with a per-pick and running-total receipt in Spanish, building trust with a low-literacy seasonal workforce and cutting end-of-day wage disputes.
- **Live harvest-day dashboard & crew leaderboard** — Real-time kg-by-plot, kg-by-picker, and crew totals let the supervisor rebalance pickers toward ripe rows and surface the top pickers (replacing today's static top-pickers card with live data).
- **Cherry-intake handoff to the wet mill** — Summed verified cherry kg per lot code flows directly into a processing batch's input weight, closing the gap where harvest totals are re-keyed at mill intake and breaking traceability.
- **Daily harvest payroll run + disbursement export** — One action rolls the day's weigh-ins into a payroll sheet and exports to Panama payment rails (Yappy / ACH banca móvil) or cash-envelope lists, replacing manual paydays and creating an auditable labor-cost record.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** FairPick / innov8.ag (2nd Sight Bio) — NTEP-certified offline harvest-scale + RFID picker cards as the reference standard and a potential white-label hardware path, Dinamica Generale FIELD scale — harvest weighing & tracking hardware with API for field weigh stations, Bluetooth LE digital scales (Decent Scale open API / Acaia for prototyping; industrial bench scales via BLE weight characteristic) for auto-capture, NTEP / OIML R76 legal-for-trade scale certification standard for dispute-proof weights, RFID/NFC picker cards (MIFARE / NTAG) and QR badge printing for tap-to-identify, Cropster Origin — push verified picker/field/cherry deliveries (especially relevant since Cropster discontinued its standalone Origin App, leaving a capture-layer gap to fill), Panama payment rails: Yappy (Banco General) and ACH banca móvil / Tigo Money for digital wage disbursement, WhatsApp Business API + Twilio SMS for Spanish-language picker receipts and tally confirmations, Payroll/accounting export to QuickBooks Online (and CSV for local Panamanian accountant) for labor-cost posting, Thermal receipt printers (ESC/POS / Bluetooth Zebra) for on-the-spot picker tickets, GS1 / lot-traceability standards so each weigh event's lot code carries to green-coffee export docs

**Data model additions:** weigh_event — immutable append-only pick record: id, picker_id, plot_id, lot_code, kg_gross, kg_tare, kg_net, ripeness_pct, captured_at, captured_by (supervisor), gps, container_id, device_id, sync_status, source (scale_ble | manual), picker_credential — picker_id, rfid_uid / nfc_tag / qr_code, issued_at, active (tap-to-identify), piece_rate — versioned rate table: id, effective_from, base_rate_usd_per_kg (or per_lata), unit (kg|lata), varietal/plot multipliers, ripeness_bonus_rules, currency, harvest_payroll_line — picker_id, work_date, total_kg, gross_pay_usd, bonus_usd, deductions, rate_version_id, status (computed|paid), disbursement_ref, disbursement — payroll_run_id, method (yappy|ach|cash), rail_ref, paid_at, amount, scale_device — device_id, model, ntep_cert, last_calibrated_at, pairing_key, container / lata — container_id, type, tare_kg (for accurate net weight), sync_log — device_id, queued_count, last_synced_at, conflicts_resolved (offline integrity)

**AI / automation:**
- Ripeness/defect vision assist — phone-camera or scale-cam model scores % ripe cherry vs. green/overripe/floaters per weigh-in, validating the manually-entered ripenessPct and auto-applying the quality bonus without a refractometer at every station.
- Anomaly & fraud detection — flag implausible weigh-ins (a picker logging 3x their rolling average, duplicate taps, weights that don't reconcile to mill intake) for supervisor review, protecting payroll integrity.
- Daily picker-allocation optimizer — combine live weigh-in pace, plot ripeness windows, and weather to recommend which crew moves to which plot next so ripe cherry is picked at peak Brix.
- Yield & labor-cost forecasting — project remaining-season cherry per plot and labor spend from capture velocity vs. expectedYieldKg, warning when a plot is being over- or under-picked.
- Conversational capture & receipts — Spanish/Ngäbe voice or chat entry for hands-busy supervisors, and auto-generated plain-language picker receipts/season summaries.
- Smart tare & weight-confidence — learn each container's tare and flag scale drift, prompting recalibration before disputes arise.

**World-class:** World-class is a weigh-station interaction that takes under 10 seconds end to end and works with the phone in airplane mode: picker taps an RFID card, the paired NTEP-certified scale auto-fills tared weight, the supervisor taps the active plot/lot, ripeness is captured (manually or by a vision assist), and the picker walks away with a printed/WhatsApp receipt — all queued offline and conflict-free-synced (CRDT/last-write-wins per event) the moment a bar of signal appears. Every weigh-in is an immutable, append-only event with picker, plot, lot, GPS, time, container, and certified weight — a single digital identity that flows untouched into payroll, mill intake, and export traceability. Pay is computed from a versioned rate table the moment weight lands, disbursed digitally that evening, and the whole season reconciles to the kilo: zero paper tickets, zero re-keying, zero unexplained variance between field-captured cherry and mill-received cherry. This is precisely the FairPick bar (offline sync, legal-for-trade accuracy, instant receipts, one-click payroll export) tuned for specialty coffee's per-lot quality economics.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson's economics are quality-stratified in a way commodity harvest tracking ignores: a kilo of 96%-ripe Geisha cherry from Tizingal Alto (1,690 masl, ~23.4 Brix) is worth a multiple of a kilo of Caturra from lower Talamanca — so the piece-rate engine must reward selective ripe-only picking with per-lot/per-varietal bonus rates, directly protecting the cup score and the reserve-Geisha premium that defines the brand. Volcán/Renacimiento harvest runs on ~10,000 mostly Indigenous Ngäbe-Buglé seasonal migrants paid traditionally by the lata (volume can), often cash and on paper — so tap-to-identify (no literacy needed), instant Spanish receipts, and digital disbursement are not nice-to-haves but the difference between retaining skilled pickers and losing them to neighboring farms. The existing Harvest rows already carry plotName, picker, cherriesKg, ripenessPct, brixAvg, and a JC- lot code; piece-rate capture turns that read-only daily aggregate into the live transactional source those rows should roll up from, and the verified cherry total becomes the trustworthy input weight for Janson's own wet/dry mill batches — closing the field-to-mill traceability loop on the farm's 200-hectare reserve operation.


#### Labor compliance & seasonal/migrant worker contracts
*Priority:* **table-stakes**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm administrator / RR.HH. encargado (in Janson's case Janette/Miguel Janson, who hire and pay crews) — the person legally on the hook to MITRADEL, the CSS, and the certification auditor. Secondary: the cuadrilla foreman (jefe de cuadrilla) who registers each Ngäbe-Buglé picker family on arrival and logs daily attendance in the field, and the seasonal picker themselves (often unbanked, sometimes illiterate, Ngäbere-first-language) who needs to see they were credited and paid correctly.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When the harvest cuadrillas (mostly Ngäbe-Buglé families migrating in from the comarca) arrive at the finca, I want to register each worker once — capturing cédula or carné, nationality/indigenous status, dependents, and bank/wallet details — and instantly generate a compliant fixed-term/por-obra contract in Spanish (and Ngäbere summary), so I can prove a written labor relationship to MITRADEL and stop chasing paper at the gate.
- When I run the monthly planilla, I want every picker's piece-rate earnings (per lata/kg) automatically reconciled against the legal agricultural minimum wage and the CSS/SIPE deductions computed, so I can pay everyone correctly, file before the 15th without multas, and never under-pay below the Decreto salario mínimo floor.
- When a child appears in the field or a worker is housed in sub-standard conditions, I want the system to flag it, open a remediation case, and document the corrective action, so I pass the Rainforest Alliance / Fairtrade assess-and-address audit instead of losing certification (and the green-coffee premium that depends on it).
- When the season ends, I want to compute and pay each worker's pro-rated décimo tercer mes, vacation, and final liquidación, generate the finiquito, and offboard them in CSS, so departing migrant crews leave fully and provably paid and I carry zero open labor liability into the next cycle.

**Features:**
- **Worker registry with identity & vulnerability profile** — One canonical worker record extends the thin Worker type with cédula/carné/passport, nationality + indigenous group (Ngäbe-Buglé), date of birth (drives the minor-age guard), dependents, preferred language (Spanish/Ngäbere), housing assignment, and emergency contact — eliminates duplicate gate paperwork and is the single source of truth every other module (harvest, payroll, certification) joins to.
- **Digital contract generator (por-obra / tiempo definido)** — Generates a Código de Trabajo-compliant fixed-term or por-obra-determinada contract pre-filled from the worker record, in Spanish with a plain-language Ngäbere summary, captured with an e-signature or thumbprint — gives Janson a written contract for every seasonal hire (which MITRADEL requires for definido/por-obra) and a one-click bundle for a labor inspection.
- **Piece-rate ledger reconciled to legal minimum wage** — Daily picking (kg/lata already captured in Harvests) rolls into a per-worker earnings ledger; the system compares piece-rate output against the Decreto No.13 agricultural hourly floor (Region 2, small vs large employer) and flags any day a picker fell below minimum so Janson can top them up — converts an opaque cash-per-lata practice into auditable, legally-floored pay.
- **CSS / SIPE planilla export + décimo & liquidación engine** — Computes employer/employee CSS contributions, ISR, the three décimo tercer mes installments, vacation accrual, and end-of-season finiquito; exports the monthly planilla in SIPE-ready format — lets Janson file before the 15th without penalties and produces a defensible final-settlement document per departing worker.
- **Child-labor & decent-work compliance module (assess-and-address)** — Implements Rainforest Alliance's five-step due-diligence loop (commit > assess > mitigate > remediate > monitor): age-gate on registration, on-site presence-of-minors logging, housing/PPE checklists, grievance intake, and a remediation case tracker — turns the single biggest audit-failure risk on a Panama coffee farm into documented, exportable evidence.
- **Field attendance & crew check-in (offline-first, biometric-optional)** — Foreman checks each worker in/out by photo-roster or fingerprint on a phone that works with no signal and syncs later — accurate attendance for illiterate/unbanked workers without paper sign-in sheets, feeding both payroll and the no-show/overtime view.
- **Worker pay statement & disbursement (wallet/cash)** — Generates a per-worker, per-pay-period statement (kg picked, days, gross, deductions, net) viewable as a printed slip or via a simple phone link, and records disbursement via Yappy/Nequi wallet or logged cash — gives unbanked Ngäbe-Buglé pickers transparent proof of correct pay and Janson an auditable payment trail.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** CSS (Caja de Seguro Social) — SIPE planilla electrónica format for monthly social-security filing and employer/worker registration, MITRADEL — Código de Trabajo contract templates and contract-registration requirements (fixed-term, por-obra, agricultural exemptions), Panamá Tributario / DGI — ISR (income tax) withholding tables, Executive Decree No. 13 (Dec 31, 2025) minimum-wage schedule — agricultural rates by Region 1/2 and employer size, machine-readable for the wage-floor check, Rainforest Alliance 2026 Regenerative Agriculture Standard — Social Annex, HRDD Toolkit, and Child/Forced-Labor Sector Risk Maps for the assess-and-address loop, Fairtrade International standards — child-labour prohibition + Minimum Price/Premium model for certified lots, Yappy (Banco General) and Nequi payment rails — wallet disbursement to unbanked workers; ACH Xpress / Banco Nacional for banked staff, Tribunal Electoral cédula verification + SNM (Servicio Nacional de Migración) carné checks for foreign/indigenous-border workers, Fingerprint/biometric capture hardware (e.g. low-cost USB/phone fingerprint readers) for attendance of illiterate workers, Cropster / lot-traceability systems — to tie certified-lot labor evidence to the green-coffee batch that earns the premium

**Data model additions:** LaborContract (workerId, type: indefinido|definido|por_obra, startDate, endDate/obra, agreedRate, rateUnit: day|hour|kg|lata, region, signedAt, signatureMethod, docUrl, mitradelRegistered), WorkerIdentity (extends Worker: nationalId/cédula, idType, nationality, indigenousGroup, dateOfBirth, dependents, preferredLanguage, housingUnitId, cssAffiliated), PieceRateEntry (workerId, date, plotId/lotCode, quantity, unit, ratePerUnit, grossEarned, minWageFloorThatDay, belowFloorFlag, topUpAmount), PayPeriod / Planilla (periodStart, periodEnd, status, sipeExportedAt) and PayrollLine (workerId, daysWorked, kgPicked, gross, cssEmployee, cssEmployer, isr, decimoAccrued, vacationAccrued, net, disbursementMethod, disbursedAt, txRef), ComplianceCase (workerId/crewId, type: child_labor|forced_labor|housing|ppe|grievance|wage_below_floor, severity, status: open|remediating|closed, assessStep, evidenceUrls, correctiveAction, closedAt), AttendanceRecord (workerId, date, checkIn, checkOut, method: photo|biometric|manual, crewId, syncedAt), EndOfSeasonSettlement (workerId, decimoPaid, vacationPaid, finiquitoUrl, cssOffboardedAt, fullyPaidConfirmed)

**AI / automation:**
- Wage-floor watchdog: nightly job recomputes every picker's piece-rate day against the live Decreto minimum-wage floor and auto-drafts the top-up adjustment plus a CSS impact note — turns the promise 'no one is paid below minimum' into hard, automated enforcement.
- Minor-risk detector: flags registrations with DOB under legal working age, and (future) lightweight on-device vision to surface possible children in field check-in photos, opening an assess-and-address case automatically.
- Contract & finiquito drafting: LLM fills the MITRADEL-shaped contract and end-of-season liquidación from structured worker data, with a Ngäbere/Spanish plain-language summary for low-literacy workers.
- Audit-pack assembler: on demand, compiles the Rainforest Alliance/Fairtrade evidence bundle (contracts, attendance, wage-floor proof, remediation cases, housing checklists) into one export, mapped to each standard requirement.
- Crew-demand forecaster: predicts pickers needed per week from ripening/Brix and weather signals, so Janson recruits the right number of Ngäbe-Buglé families and avoids both labor shortfall and over-hiring.

**World-class:** Every seasonal worker is registered once in under two minutes at the gate, walks away with a signed contract they understand in their own language, and is provably paid at or above the legal floor — with CSS, décimo, and vacation all computed automatically and filed in SIPE before the 15th with zero manual re-keying. Child labor and sub-standard housing are caught at intake, not by an auditor; a remediation case opens itself and closes with documented corrective action. At any moment the administrator can produce a one-click, requirement-mapped evidence pack that passes a MITRADEL inspection and a Rainforest Alliance audit on the same data — and unbanked pickers get transparent, traceable pay to a wallet or logged cash, ending the opaque cash-per-lata norm. Labor compliance stops being a shoebox of paper and becomes a live, defensible system of record that also protects the certification premium the farm's green coffee depends on.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson's harvest in Volcán literally runs on Ngäbe-Buglé families who migrate seasonally from the comarca — the most labor-compliance-exposed workforce in Panama (extreme poverty, low literacy, unbanked, children in the field, rustic farm housing). For a farm selling specialty and reserve Geisha into export markets that increasingly demand Rainforest Alliance/Fairtrade and EU-CSDDD-style human-rights due diligence, documented decent-work compliance is not paperwork — it is what protects the premium price the Geisha commands and the brand's reputation. This module also fits the piece-rate reality of coffee (paid per lata/kg of cherry) and the December–March harvest pulse, where dozens of workers arrive and leave in a compressed window, making one-tap registration, offline field attendance, and end-of-season liquidación uniquely high-value here versus a generic farm.


#### Payroll — blended piece-rate + hourly, multi-currency
*Priority:* **table-stakes**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Doña Janette Janson (owner-operator / farm administrator) is the one who runs the Sunday-night payroll close, with the crew leaders ("cabos" — e.g. the Crew Norte / Crew Tizingal supervisors) as the upstream data-entry persona who tally each picker's latas/kg in the field. The third stakeholder is the Ngäbe-Buglé migrant picker who needs to trust the number and get paid (often in cash or via mobile remittance, frequently unbanked). World-class = serving all three: the administrator who must be statutorily compliant, the cabo who captures piecework at the tree, and the picker who must be paid fairly and transparently.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When the harvest week ends on Sunday and I have 14+ workers paid three different ways (per-kg cherry for pickers, daily rate for supervisors/drivers, hourly for mill operators), I want one screen that turns the week's harvest and attendance records into exact pay per person, so I can pay everyone correctly Monday morning without a spreadsheet and a calculator at midnight.
- When a picker had a slow week on a low-yield Geisha block and their piece-rate earnings fall below the Chiriquí agricultural minimum wage, I want the system to automatically top them up to the legal minimum (the 'make-whole' rule) and flag it, so I never underpay a worker and never fail a Fairtrade/Rainforest Alliance or Panama MITRADEL labor audit.
- When I close the period, I want CSS (Caja de Seguro Social) employer + employee contributions, décimo (13th-month), and any advances/loans automatically withheld and itemized on each payslip, so my statutory filing and the picker's net cash match to the cent and I can hand over a clear receipt they understand.
- When a Ngäbe-Buglé picker is unbanked and another supervisor is paid into a Panama bank in USD while a cross-border seasonal worker thinks in colones, I want to pay each person through the right rail and currency at a locked FX rate, so the books reconcile and nobody loses money to a bad conversion.

**Features:**
- **Blended pay-rule engine (per-kg / per-lata + daily + hourly per worker)** — One worker can carry multiple active rate cards (e.g. $0.55/kg cherry as a picker plus an $8/hr mill shift); the engine picks the right basis per logged activity and sums them, ending the manual three-method reconciliation that currently lives in nobody's head.
- **Auto harvest-to-pay rollup from existing harvest + attendance records** — Every harvests.cherriesKg row and attendance day already captured flows straight into gross pay with zero re-keying — the payroll period is a derived view of operational data, not a separate ledger that drifts.
- **Minimum-wage make-whole guard (Decreto Ejecutivo No.13/2025, Chiriquí agri band)** — Hard data-layer enforcement that piece-rate gross is topped up to the statutory hourly/daily floor for hours worked, with the top-up itemized — turns the single biggest specialty-coffee labor risk and audit failure into an automatic, provable line item.
- **Statutory deductions: CSS, Seguro Educativo, ISR, décimo accrual** — Employer + employee Caja de Seguro Social contributions (post Law 462/2025), the 1.25% Seguro Educativo, income-tax thresholds, and 13th-month (décimo) accrual computed automatically, so the filing to CSS and MITRADEL is a one-click export instead of a quarterly scramble.
- **Multi-currency payroll with locked FX (PAB/USD, CRC, EUR)** — Pay each worker in their settlement currency at a rate locked at period close, with the books carried in USD; export bonuses tied to a EUR/USD green-coffee sale convert cleanly, and cross-border Ngäbe seasonal pay is unambiguous.
- **Pay rails + cash run sheet (bank file, mobile money, cash envelope manifest)** — Generates a Banco General / ACH payment file for banked staff, a Yappy/Nequí mobile-money batch, and a printable signed cash-disbursement manifest for the unbanked majority — every payment method covered, every disbursement signed for.
- **Advances, loans & store-account ledger ('fiado')** — Tracks the cash advances and farm-store credit that are universal on coffee fincas, auto-deducting installments at period close with a running balance, so the informal 'I'll take it off your next pay' is now a transparent, auditable line.
- **Per-worker bilingual payslip (Spanish + Ngäbere/visual) with QR receipt** — A clear, dignity-preserving payslip showing kg picked, rate, top-up, deductions, and net — readable by a low-literacy workforce and signable on a phone, which is exactly the transparency Fairtrade living-wage standards demand between picker, cabo, and owner.
- **Cost-of-labor attribution to plot / lot / process method** — Because every harvest row carries plotId and lotCode, labor cost rolls up to cost-per-kg of cherry by plot and cost-per-kg of green by lot — feeding true gross-margin-per-lot so the owner finally knows whether the Geisha block actually pays for its pickers.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Caja de Seguro Social (CSS) — SIPE planilla / e-filing format for monthly social-security contributions (post Law 462 of 2025), MITRADEL (Ministerio de Trabajo) — labor-registry and minimum-wage compliance per Decreto Ejecutivo No. 13 of 2025 (Chiriquí agricultural wage band), DGI / Tributos (Dirección General de Ingresos) — income-tax (ISR) withholding tables and Seguro Educativo 1.25%, Banco General / Banistmo ACH batch payment files (Panama domestic bank transfers in PAB/USD), Yappy (Banco General) and Nequí mobile-money disbursement APIs for unbanked / mobile-first workers, Wise / Remitly / Western Union remittance rails for cross-border Ngäbe-Buglé and Costa Rica-linked seasonal pay (CRC/USD), Open Exchange Rates or ECB reference rates API for period-close FX locking, Croptracker / FairTrak (2nd Sight) / Agri-Trak piecework time-and-attendance feed (barcode picker badges, weigh-station tares) as an upstream field-capture source, Cropster — links lot-level labor cost into the green-coffee cost basis already tracked there, QuickBooks Online / Xero export for the GL journal of payroll expense, accrued décimo, and CSS liability, Fairtrade International / Rainforest Alliance living-wage benchmark data (Anker Living Wage for rural Panama) for the make-whole / fair-pay dashboard

**Data model additions:** pay_rates — worker_id, basis (per_kg | per_lata | daily | hourly), amount, currency, activity_scope (picking | mill | driving | agronomy), effective_from/to (rate cards are versioned, not overwritten), pay_periods — id, start_date, end_date, status (open | calculating | review | locked | paid), fx_rate_snapshot, locked_by, locked_at, pay_lines — period_id, worker_id, basis, quantity (kg/hours/days), source_ref (harvest_id or attendance_day), gross_amount, currency, payroll_runs / payslips — period_id, worker_id, gross, minwage_topup, css_employee, css_employer, seguro_educativo, isr, decimo_accrual, advances_deducted, net_pay, settlement_currency, settlement_rail, signed_at, deductions_and_advances — worker_id, kind (advance | loan | store_credit | uniform), principal, installment, balance, period_applied, statutory_config — jurisdiction, css_employee_pct, css_employer_pct, seguro_educativo_pct, min_wage_table (region × sector × company_size), decimo_rule, effective_from (single source of truth for every legal rate), fx_rates — base_currency, quote_currency, rate, as_of, source, labor_cost_allocations — pay_line_id, plot_id, lot_code, allocated_cost_usd (the bridge from payroll to cost-per-lot)

**AI / automation:**
- Anomaly detection on period close — flags a picker whose kg/day deviates sharply from their own trailing average or crew median (data-entry error, scale fault, or a worker who needs help), before the run is locked
- Natural-language payroll close — 'Close last week and top up anyone under minimum' executed by an LLM agent against deterministic pay rules, with the human approving the locked summary (never letting free-text drive an irreversible disbursement)
- Predictive labor-cost forecast — projects next week's payroll from the harvest-curve forecast and current rate cards, so the owner knows the cash needed before Sunday
- Make-whole 'why' explanations — auto-drafts the plain-language reason a top-up fired, in Spanish and simplified terms, for the payslip and the audit trail
- OCR/voice capture of field tally sheets — a cabo photographs the paper lata-count sheet or dictates counts; the model parses them into pay_lines for review, bridging farms still on paper
- Living-wage gap insight — continuously compares net pay against the Anker living-wage benchmark for rural Chiriquí and surfaces the gap as a fair-pay KPI, not just the legal minimum

**World-class:** World-class is a Sunday-night close that takes minutes, not hours: the owner opens one period view, every picker's kg and every supervisor's days/hours are already there from operations, the engine has computed gross across all three pay bases, auto-topped-up anyone below the Chiriquí agricultural minimum, withheld CSS / Seguro Educativo / ISR / décimo correctly, deducted advances, locked an FX rate, and produced a bilingual signable payslip plus a bank file, a Yappy batch, and a signed cash manifest — all from a single 'Lock & Pay' action. It is provably compliant (one-click CSS/MITRADEL export, every top-up itemized for a Fairtrade or Rainforest Alliance audit), dignified for a low-literacy migrant workforce (clear visual payslips, QR receipts, no opaque math), and financially intelligent (labor cost attributes back to plot and lot, feeding true cost-per-kg-of-green). Nothing is re-keyed, nothing drifts from operations, and no worker is ever silently underpaid.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson's payroll is defined by the realities most generic farm-payroll tools miss: a heavily Ngäbe-Buglé indigenous migrant picking workforce paid per lata/kg of cherry, blended with year-round daily-rate supervisors/drivers and hourly mill operators — and a hard, audit-relevant legal floor (Panama's Chiriquí agricultural minimum wage and CSS contributions under the 2025 reforms). The make-whole top-up is not a nicety here: it is the single biggest labor-rights risk in specialty coffee and the line every Fairtrade / Rainforest Alliance / SCA-buyer audit looks for, and Janson's premium-export and reserve positioning depends on being able to prove fair pay. Because every harvest record already carries plotId, lotCode, and brix, payroll can attribute true labor cost down to the Geisha lot — answering the owner's real question of whether the high-altitude Geisha blocks, which yield less but sell for far more, actually pay for the hands that pick them. The multi-currency layer mirrors a farm that pays workers in PAB/USD cash but sells green coffee in USD/EUR to international buyers, so a quality/export bonus can flow from the sale back to the crew that picked the lot.


#### Time & attendance — geo/biometric clock-in, offline-capable
*Priority:* **table-stakes**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the Cuadrilla Supervisor / Mayordomo (field crew boss like "Miguel Janson, Supervisor") who is on the mountain with the pickers each morning and is accountable for who showed up, who is on which plot, and who earned what. Secondary personas: the Farm Office Administrator (does payroll + CSS/Seguro Social reporting and needs auditable hours), the seasonal Picker themselves (wants their attendance + day-rate/contract-kg recorded honestly), and the Owner/GM (wants real-time labor cost + presence visibility without driving up the mountain).

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I gather my crew at the trailhead at 5:45am before we spread out across Tizingal Alto and the Norte blocks (often with no cell signal), I want to record exactly who is present, on which plot, in seconds and without paper, so I can prove attendance later and start paying people fairly from minute one.
- When a seasonal migrant picker (often without a Panama cédula, sometimes Ngäbe-Buglé comarca labor) shows up for the first time mid-harvest, I want to enroll and identify them in under a minute without forcing literacy or a smartphone, so nobody is paid to a name I can't verify and our certification/labor audit holds up.
- When the harvest day ends and connectivity finally returns in the valley, I want every clock-in/out and crew movement captured offline on the mountain to sync automatically and reconcile against the day's cherry weigh-ins per worker, so payroll, CSS contributions, and per-picker productivity are computed without me re-keying anything.
- When a coffee buyer, Rainforest Alliance auditor, or the Panama labor ministry (MITRADEL) asks me to prove no child/forced labor and that we paid at least the agricultural minimum wage, I want a tamper-evident time-and-attendance record tied to each worker and lot, so I can export a clean compliance report on demand.

**Features:**
- **Offline-first crew clock (PWA + native shell)** — Supervisor opens an installable app on a cheap Android phone; clock-in/out works fully offline at altitude with no signal, queues events in IndexedDB/SQLite, and syncs on reconnect with conflict-free merge — captures the 90% of the workday that happens out of coverage, which any cloud-only system silently loses.
- **Geo-fenced + GPS-stamped punches** — Each punch is stamped with GPS lat/lng and matched to the plot polygon (per the existing plots model) so attendance is auto-attributed to the right lot and 'ghost' punches from off-farm are flagged — turns attendance into per-plot labor cost without manual entry.
- **Multi-modal identity: PIN + photo + optional fingerprint/face** — Enroll any worker in under a minute with a 4-digit PIN and a face photo; optional fingerprint (USB/Bluetooth reader) or on-device face match for higher-trust sites. Works for non-literate, non-smartphone, undocumented seasonal labor — eliminates buddy-punching without excluding the actual workforce a Volcán farm hires.
- **Crew-batch & kiosk modes** — Supervisor checks in a whole cuadrilla from one device in seconds (tap each avatar present), or runs a fixed tablet kiosk at the wet-mill gate for self check-in — matches how Janson actually muscles people onto the mountain at dawn vs. how mill operators arrive.
- **Day-rate vs. contract (por lata/por libra) timesheet engine** — Each worker is on either a daily wage (jornal, like the $22/day pickers) or piece-rate; the system reconciles hours-present against cherry-kg weighed that day and computes the higher-of/agreed pay rule automatically, surfacing under-minimum-wage days before payroll runs.
- **Live presence + labor-cost dashboard** — Owner/office see real-time present/absent/rest-day per crew and per plot (evolving the current static AttendanceCard/CrewBoard into live event-driven tiles) plus accruing labor $ for the day — answers 'who's on the mountain and what is today costing me' without a phone call.
- **Tamper-evident attendance ledger + compliance export** — Append-only, hash-chained punch log with edit-audit trail; one-click export of hours, ages-verified roster, and minimum-wage attestation for Rainforest Alliance / SCA / MITRADEL / CSS — converts a recurring audit scramble into a report button.
- **Leave, rest-day & overtime rules engine (Panama labor law)** — Encodes Panama Código de Trabajo: día de descanso, recargo for the 7th day, overtime multipliers, and feriados — so the existing 'rest-day' status becomes a paid/unpaid rule rather than a label, keeping the farm out of labor disputes.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Cropster (cropster.com) — link per-worker harvest weigh-ins/lots to attendance so picking productivity ties to the green-coffee traceability already used by specialty mills, Panama CSS (Caja de Seguro Social) — planilla / employer contribution reporting from accumulated hours; MITRADEL minimum-agricultural-wage tables as the wage-floor data source, Payroll/payout rails: Banco General & Banco Nacional de Panamá ACH/batch files, plus Yappy (Banco General's wallet, ubiquitous in Panama) and Nequi for paying unbanked seasonal pickers to a phone, Biometric/identity hardware: ZKTeco fingerprint terminals & SDK, Suprema BioStar, on-device face match via Apple Vision/Google ML Kit; FIDO2/WebAuthn for supervisor device auth, Geospatial: device GPS + Mapbox / Turf.js point-in-polygon against plot boundaries; optional what3words for trailhead muster points with no addresses, Offline/sync stack: PWA Service Worker + IndexedDB/Dexie or SQLite (Capacitor) with CRDT/last-write-wins merge; Supabase Realtime + Postgres on reconnect (free tier, matches repo's existing Supabase setup), Certification bodies' audit schemas: Rainforest Alliance 2020 Standard labor criteria, Fair Trade USA, SCA — export formats for labor/age/wage evidence, Twilio / WhatsApp Business API for daily attendance + payslip notifications to workers and supervisors (SMS works where data doesn't)

**Data model additions:** work_sessions / time_punches — append-only event log: worker_id, crew, plot_id (geo-resolved), punch_type (in|out|break), ts (device + server), lat/lng, accuracy_m, device_id, identity_method (pin|face|fingerprint), offline_captured_at, sync_status, hash_prev (chain), attendance_days — derived daily rollup per worker: status (present|absent|rest-day|leave), hours_worked, first_in, last_out, plot_ids[], reconciled_kg — replaces today's single static attendance enum on workers, worker_identity / enrollments — pin_hash, face_template_ref, fingerprint_template_ref, cedula_or_doc (nullable for undocumented seasonal), age_verified_at, photo_ref, pay_agreements — worker_id, pay_basis (daily|piece|hourly), daily_rate_usd (today's field, promoted here), piece_rate_per_lata, effective_from/to, contract_ref, devices — registered supervisor phones/kiosks: device_id, assigned_supervisor, last_sync_at, public_key, leave_records & holiday_calendar — leave_type, paid?, Panama feriados; powers the rest-day/overtime rules engine, labor_cost_daily (view) — joins attendance_days + pay_agreements + harvests for per-plot, per-crew, per-lot labor cost feeding the dashboard and processing cost-basis

**AI / automation:**
- Buddy-punch / anomaly detection — flag punches whose face-match confidence is low, whose GPS is off-farm, or whose pattern (always-clusters, identical timestamps across a crew) suggests one person clocking many — catches payroll fraud no enum field ever could.
- On-device face verification (ML Kit / Vision) so identity works offline with no biometric data leaving the phone — privacy-preserving and connectivity-independent.
- Productivity insight — correlate hours-present vs. cherry-kg per picker per plot to surface top/struggling pickers (extends the existing top-pickers card) and recommend crew rebalancing across plots for the next day.
- Predictive labor planning — combine harvest-readiness (ripeness %, weather API, plot maturity) with historical attendance to forecast how many pickers each plot needs tomorrow and auto-draft the muster roster.
- Natural-language attendance + payroll assistant — supervisor asks in Spanish 'cuántos faltaron hoy en Tizingal' or 'cierra la planilla de la semana' and the system answers/acts against the ledger.
- Wage-floor guardrail — automatically recompute each worker's day against the live MITRADEL agricultural minimum and Panama overtime rules, blocking a payroll close that would pay below the floor.

**World-class:** "World-class time & attendance for a mountain coffee farm means a supervisor enrolls and clocks an entire dawn cuadrilla in under 60 seconds on a $120 Android phone with zero bars of signal; every punch is GPS-stamped, identity-verified, and tamper-evident; the data syncs and self-reconciles against the day's cherry weights the moment the truck rolls back into valley coverage; payroll, CSS contributions, and a Rainforest Alliance/MITRADEL labor-compliance pack generate themselves with no re-keying; and the owner watches live presence and accruing labor cost per plot from anywhere. It is invisible to the worker, effortless for the supervisor, and audit-bulletproof for the buyer — the opposite of paper roll-call books and cloud-only apps that die above the cloud line."

**Coffee-specific angle:** "Coffee harvest is brutally seasonal and altitude-bound: Janson picks across plots from 1,350–1,700 masl in Volcán during a few intense months, fielding a surge of seasonal/migrant pickers (frequently Ngäbe-Buglé comarca labor, often undocumented and non-literate) who are paid por lata/por día and disperse across remote, no-signal lots. Generic SaaS time clocks assume connectivity, smartphones, and documented W-2 staff — all false here. The differentiator is binding attendance to the existing plot polygons and lot codes so labor cost flows straight into per-lot green-coffee cost-of-goods and traceability (the thing specialty Geisha buyers and Best of Panama auction provenance actually care about), and turning the labor/age/minimum-wage record into the certification evidence (Rainforest Alliance, the Panama Specialty Coffee scene) that protects the Janson brand and reserve story."


#### Worker training, safety & certifications
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the Farm Safety & Compliance Lead (at Janson, typically the agronomist or HR/finca administrator who also owns the Rainforest Alliance certification file). Secondary users: crew supervisors (jefes de cuadrilla) who deliver toolbox talks and verify PPE at shift start; individual workers/pickers (many Ngäbe-Buglé, Ngäbere-first-language, variable Spanish literacy) who consume training and acknowledge it; and the external certification auditor who reads the worker file once a year.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a new seasonal picking crew arrives at the start of the Volcán harvest (Nov-Mar) and I have 80 people to onboard in a week, I want to push a short pictographic safety induction to each worker's phone in Spanish or Ngäbere and capture a verifiable acknowledgement, so I can prove every picker was inducted before they ever entered a plot without burning a full day on paper sign-in sheets.
- When I am about to send a worker to apply or handle an agrochemical, I want the system to block the task assignment unless that worker has a current pesticide-handling certificate, a valid annual medical exam, and (for organophosphates/carbamates) a current cholinesterase test on file, so I can guarantee Rainforest Alliance compliance and never expose an untrained or medically-at-risk person to a hazard.
- When a worker is injured on the finca, I want to record the incident, attach photos, classify it, and auto-generate the Caja de Seguro Social R.A.T.E.P. accident report within the legal reporting window, so I stay compliant with MITRADEL/CSS and turn the incident into a corrective action and a refreshed toolbox talk instead of a repeat injury.
- When my Rainforest Alliance / GLOBALG.A.P. GRASP audit is scheduled, I want to generate a complete, sampled-ready worker file (trainings, certificates, medical exams, PPE issuance, grievances) for any worker on demand, so I can pass the audit in hours instead of reconstructing scattered paper records over weeks.

**Features:**
- **Worker competency & certification ledger** — A per-worker record of skills and certifications (pesticide handling, IPM, first aid, machinery, food-safety/hygiene for mill staff), each with issue date, expiry, issuing body, and a scanned/photo credential. Drives a live 'compliance status' chip on every worker, so the farm always knows who is cleared to do what.
- **Multilingual pictographic microlearning + acknowledgement capture** — Short video/pictogram training modules (FAO pesticide pictograms, PPE, ladder/quebrada slip hazards, snakebite/heat) delivered to worker phones via WhatsApp/SMS in Spanish and Ngäbere, with a tap/voice acknowledgement logged. Turns a low-literacy, low-connectivity seasonal workforce from a compliance liability into a documented, trained one.
- **Certification-gated task assignment** — The existing task assigner refuses to assign agrochemical, machinery, or height work to a worker whose required cert/medical/cholinesterase is missing or expired, and surfaces the nearest qualified worker instead. Hard data-layer enforcement of the Rainforest Alliance training requirement — not an honor system.
- **Expiry radar & auto-scheduled refreshers** — A dashboard and push alerts for certs/medical exams/PPE expiring in the next 30/60/90 days, auto-creating refresher-training tasks and medical-exam bookings. Nothing lapses silently the week before an audit or a spray window.
- **PPE issuance & inventory register** — Track which respirator, gloves, boots, and apron each agrochemical handler was issued, sizes, condition, and replacement cadence, tied to the spray-day checklist. Proves PPE was supplied (a direct MITRADEL/CSS employer obligation and an RA control point) and flags worn-out gear.
- **Incident, near-miss & R.A.T.E.P. reporting** — Log injuries and near-misses with geotag (which plot), photos, body-part/severity, and witness; auto-populate the CSS R.A.T.E.P. accident form and route a corrective action plus a refreshed toolbox talk to the relevant crew. Closes the loop from incident to prevention.
- **Audit-ready worker file & evidence pack export** — One-click generation of a sampled worker file (or whole-farm summary) mapped to Rainforest Alliance 2020 and GRASP control points, exportable as PDF for the auditor or buyer. Compresses audit prep from weeks of paper to minutes.
- **Anonymous worker grievance channel** — A simple SMS/WhatsApp grievance line (in-language) with case tracking and resolution log, satisfying the GRASP/SA8000 grievance-mechanism requirement and giving migrant pickers a real, safe voice.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Rainforest Alliance 2020 Sustainable Agriculture Standard / Farming Annex (control points for agrochemical-handler training, annual medical exams, cholinesterase monitoring, IPM, PPE) — the compliance ruleset the ledger maps to, GLOBALG.A.P. GRASP (Risk Assessment on Social Practice) add-on and SA8000 — social-practice / grievance / health-and-safety control points, Caja de Seguro Social (CSS) R.A.T.E.P. occupational accident & illness report plus MITRADEL labor-safety obligations (Panama regulatory layer), WhatsApp Business Cloud API and an SMS gateway (e.g., Twilio) for in-field, low-connectivity training delivery and grievance intake — the WhatsApp-video approach is RCT-validated for pesticide-label/pictogram comprehension, FAO pesticide hazard pictograms and OSHA / Susan Harwood low-literacy ag-safety training assets as the source content library, SCORM / xAPI (Tin Can) microlearning content standard so modules and completions are portable and auditable, Local occupational-health clinics/labs in David, Chiriquí for annual medical exams and cholinesterase (acetylcholinesterase) blood testing, with results attached to the medical record, Cropster / farm-management exports so training and certification data ride alongside the existing lot/processing traceability the farm already keeps

**Data model additions:** worker_skills (worker_id, skill, proficiency, verified_by, verified_on) — extends the existing thin workers table from operational-only to competency-aware, certifications (id, worker_id, type [pesticide_handling | ipm | first_aid | machinery | food_hygiene], issuing_body, issued_on, expires_on, credential_url, status), training_modules (id, title, topic, language [es | ngabere], format [video | pictogram | toolbox_talk], standard_ref, scorm_url) and training_completions (worker_id, module_id, completed_on, acknowledgement_method [tap | voice | signature], crew, delivered_via), medical_exams (id, worker_id, exam_type [annual | cholinesterase_baseline | cholinesterase_periodic], clinic, taken_on, next_due, result_status, notes) — RA-required for agrochemical handlers, ppe_issuance (id, worker_id, item_type, size, issued_on, condition, replace_by) plus a small ppe_inventory table, safety_incidents (id, worker_id, plot_id, occurred_at, category, severity, body_part, photos, witness, ratep_filed_on, corrective_action_id), corrective_actions (id, incident_id, action, owner_worker_id, due, status, linked_training_module_id) — reuses the existing tasks pattern, grievances (id, worker_id_nullable, channel, submitted_at, category, status, resolution, resolved_at) — anonymous-capable for GRASP/SA8000, certification_requirements (task_category -> required_cert_type) — the lookup that powers gated task assignment off the existing task_category enum

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-translate and auto-narrate Spanish safety scripts into Ngäbere audio so a tiny finca can produce in-language pictographic training without a translation budget or studio
- Vision model to read a photo of a paper certificate or clinic medical-exam slip and auto-extract issuing body, dates, and result into the certification/medical record (OCR + extraction) — kills manual data entry for migrant workers' existing paper credentials
- Predictive expiry/risk scoring: flag which workers will hit a cert/medical lapse before a known spray window or before the audit, and pre-build the refresher schedule
- Incident-photo triage: classify injury severity and likely root cause from a field photo plus a short voice note, draft the R.A.T.E.P. narrative and a matching corrective-action toolbox talk
- Anomaly detection across attendance + incidents + grievances to surface a crew or plot with elevated safety/heat-stress risk before it becomes a serious accident
- A WhatsApp-based conversational training quiz that adapts difficulty and re-teaches the specific pictogram a worker got wrong, in their language

**World-class:** A worker's qualification is the single source of truth that physically gates work: you cannot assign a spray task to anyone whose pesticide cert, annual medical, or cholinesterase test isn't green — the system simply won't let you, and offers the nearest qualified substitute. Every seasonal hire is inducted on day one via a 4-minute pictographic video in their own language on their own phone, with a logged acknowledgement, before they touch a plot. Certs, medicals, and PPE never lapse silently — the expiry radar schedules the refresher and the clinic visit automatically. An injury is recorded once in the field with photos and geotag, and the CSS R.A.T.E.P. form, the corrective action, and the refreshed crew toolbox talk all flow from that single entry. When the Rainforest Alliance or GRASP auditor arrives, any sampled worker file is one click and the farm passes in hours. The whole system is bilingual Spanish/Ngäbere, works offline-first on cheap Android phones over patchy mountain connectivity, and treats the migrant Ngäbe-Buglé picking workforce as first-class users with a real grievance voice — turning compliance from a once-a-year paper scramble into a daily operating advantage.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson's harvest runs on migrant Ngäbe-Buglé pickers from the comarca — a workforce that is Ngäbere-first-language with variable Spanish literacy and high seasonal churn, exactly the population that text-and-Spanish-only safety training structurally fails. The terrain is the steep, wet 1,350-1,700 masl slopes of Volcán bordering a 200-hectare quetzal reserve, so the real hazards are slips on quebradas, machete cuts, snakebite, and heat — not factory-floor risks — and the agrochemical-handler controls (pesticide cert + annual medical + cholinesterase testing + PPE) are precisely what a Rainforest Alliance / GLOBALG.A.P.-aspiring specialty farm must evidence to protect both its workers and its certification, which is itself a premium-price lever for Geisha and reserve lots. Done well, this capability also becomes part of Janson's brand story: a specialty farm that can show buyers a fully documented, trained, fairly-treated harvest workforce alongside the existing lot-to-cup traceability — turning worker safety from a cost center into a marketable proof point for high-value green and roasted sales.


### Process & Quality

#### Wet milling / fermentation / washing — washed/honey/natural methods
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Mill Operator / Beneficio lead (Janson's wet-mill foreman who receives cherry at the despulpadora, runs the fermentation tanks and the Penagos ecological demucilager, and executes washed/honey/natural/anaerobic per lot). Secondary persona: the Q-grader / processing agronomist who authors the fermentation recipes (SOPs) and signs off on cut points.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a Geisha lot finishes picking at high Brix and lands at the despulpadora at dusk, I want to start its fermentation against a saved recipe and have the system tell me exactly when to wash, so I can hit the target flavor instead of guessing by feel and losing a premium lot to over-fermentation.
- When fermentation tanks are filling up during peak harvest, I want to see every active tank's temperature, pH, elapsed time and predicted cut-point on one board, so I can wash the right tanks first and never let one tip past its window while I attend another.
- When a buyer or certifier asks how a specific micro-lot was processed, I want one tap to produce the full fermentation/washing record (vessel, method, hours, pH and temperature curve, water used, who washed it), so I can prove provenance and command a specialty price.
- When we run anaerobic or honey experiments on small Geisha lots, I want to log each protocol variant and tie it back to the final cup score, so I can repeat the recipes that win and kill the ones that don't.

**Features:**
- **Fermentation batch tracker (vessel-level)** — Each lot becomes a tracked ferment with method, named vessel, start/stop timestamps, target hours and a live elapsed clock — replaces the single 'fermentation' kanban dot with a per-vessel record so no tank is forgotten during peak intake.
- **Fermentation recipe / SOP library with cut-point alerts** — Saved protocols per method+variety (e.g. 'Geisha 72h anaerobic, sealed CO2, cut at pH 3.8') drive a predicted wash time and push an alert as a tank nears its window — turns expert intuition into a repeatable standard and stops over-fermentation, the #1 cause of lost premium lots (spoilage up to 20% without control).
- **Live fermentation telemetry timeline (pH / temp / Brix curves)** — Per-vessel charts of pH (the canonical ~5.1 to 3.2 descent), temperature and degrees-Brix over the ferment, sourced from probes or quick manual readings — gives the operator the actual chemistry, not just a clock, to call the cut point objectively.
- **Wash + demucilage step log with water accounting** — Records the wash/demucilage event, water volume used and channels-vs-Penagos-eco method, computing litres-of-water-per-kg-cherry — proves the eco-mill story (0.2 L/kg vs ~20 L/kg traditional) and feeds sustainability/certification reporting.
- **Process-method selector with yield + conversion ratios** — Captures washed/honey/natural/anaerobic per lot and tracks the mass conversion (cherry to wet parchment to dry parchment to green, ~5:1 cherry:green) so the operator sees true outturn per method and per variety, informing pricing and which methods to scale.
- **Vessel / tank inventory and occupancy board** — A registry of fermentation vessels (open tanks, sealed Grainpro/PET barrels for anaerobic, eco-mill demucilager) with capacity and live occupancy — answers 'where do I put this incoming lot' during a peak-harvest crunch and prevents cross-lot contamination.
- **Ferment-to-cup linkage** — Joins each fermentation record to its eventual SCA cupping score and sensory notes, surfacing which recipe + variety + altitude combinations score highest — turns experimentation into a compounding library of winning protocols.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Cropster Origin / Cropster Hub — processing-step + cupping system used by specialty origins; bidirectional lot and process-log sync, Penagos ecological wet-mill hardware (UCBE-7500 / ECOLINE series, vertical pulper + demucilager) — water-use and throughput data capture, Pinhalense (P&A) wet-processing equipment — mechanical siphon / demucilage telemetry as an alternate hardware lane, Atago / Hanna Instruments / Sensorex digital refractometers, pH meters and probes (Brix, pH, temperature) — manual or BLE-streamed fermentation readings, Apax Lab / fermentation IoT probes and DIY ESP32-based pH+temp loggers for live tank telemetry over MQTT, SCA cupping protocol + Q grading scoresheet standard for the ferment-to-cup linkage, Open weather API (OpenWeather / Tomorrow.io) for ambient temperature + humidity context affecting fermentation rate, Certification bodies / standards: Rainforest Alliance, Specialty Coffee Association, and water-stewardship (Alliance for Water Stewardship) for the eco-mill and traceability reporting, QR / GS1 Digital Link for the per-lot provenance record exposed to buyers

**Data model additions:** fermentation_batch (process_batch_id FK, method, vessel_id, recipe_id, started_at, target_hours, predicted_cut_at, actual_cut_at, status), fermentation_recipe (id, name, method, variety, target_hours, target_ph, target_temp_c, sealed_anaerobic bool, steps_jsonb, author_worker_id, version), fermentation_reading (id, fermentation_batch_id FK, recorded_at, ph, temp_c, brix, source enum[manual|probe], probe_id), wash_event (id, fermentation_batch_id FK, washed_at, method enum[channel|eco_demucilage|mechanical], water_litres, equipment_id, operator_worker_id, output_parchment_kg), vessel (id, name, type enum[open_tank|sealed_barrel|eco_demucilager|grainpro], capacity_kg, location, active), processing_equipment (id, vendor enum[Penagos|Pinhalense|other], model, type, capacity, water_per_kg_spec), process_method_yield (process_batch_id FK, method, cherry_kg, wet_parchment_kg, dry_parchment_kg, green_kg, conversion_ratio), cup_link (process_batch_id FK, cupping_score, sensory_notes, q_grader_worker_id, scored_at) — joins ferment record to outcome

**AI / automation:**
- Cut-point prediction model: fit the pH/temperature/Brix descent curve per vessel and forecast the optimal wash time, accounting for ambient temperature and altitude — the core 'don't lose the lot' assist.
- Anomaly detection on live telemetry: flag a stalled or runaway ferment (pH plateau, temperature spike signaling unwanted microbial activity) before it becomes spoilage.
