- Recipe recommender: given variety, Brix at intake and target flavor, suggest the historical protocol that scored highest in the ferment-to-cup library.
- Natural-language voice/photo logging in Spanish for the operator: 'tanque 3, pH tres punto nueve' or a snapped refractometer photo auto-parsed into a fermentation_reading — keeps hands free on the wet-mill floor.
- Auto-generated processing passport: assembles the full traceability narrative + QR for each lot from the structured records, ready for buyers and certifiers with no manual write-up.

**World-class:** A mill operator at Janson never loses a premium Geisha to a missed cut point: every fermentation runs against a versioned recipe, a single board shows all active vessels with live pH/temperature/elapsed and a model-predicted wash time, alerts fire before the window closes, and the wash + demucilage step is logged with exact water-per-kg. Each finished lot carries a tamper-evident processing passport (method, vessel, hours, full pH/temp curve, water, operator) that flows straight into Cropster and a public QR provenance page, and every protocol is automatically tied back to its cup score so the recipe library gets measurably better each harvest. World-class is repeatable, sensor-grounded, certification-ready fermentation — not intuition and a notebook.

**Coffee-specific angle:** This is where Janson's premium is actually made or lost. Their flagship is Geisha plus Pacamara/Catuaí grown at 1,350–1,700 masl, and the differentiating money is in processing: anaerobic and honey micro-lots on small, high-value Geisha batches where a missed cut point at dusk can vaporize thousands of dollars per lot. The cool Volcán highland nights slow fermentation, so generic clock-based timing is wrong for them — they need recipes tuned to their altitude and ambient temperature. Their 200-hectare reserve and quetzal-habitat brand also make the water story strategic: logging the Penagos ecological mill's ~0.2 L/kg against the ~20 L/kg of traditional washing turns sustainability into a verifiable, sellable certification claim, not just marketing copy.


#### Drying management — patios, raised beds, mechanical, moisture curves
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Drying-yard / patio manager (the "secador" or mill drying lead) — owns every lot from the moment it leaves fermentation/washing until it hits target moisture and goes to rest in pergamino. Reports to the head of processing; supervises the bed-turning crew. Secondary users: head of processing/QC (sets target curves, signs lots off before milling) and the owner/agronomist (traceability, certification, defect post-mortems). On a 1,350–1,700 masl farm running Geisha/Pacamara reserve micro-lots, this is the single highest-leverage role for cup quality.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a freshly-washed or de-pulped lot comes off fermentation and I must decide where to lay it and how thick, I want to assign it to a specific bed/patio/mechanical dryer with a target drying curve matched to its method and value tier, so I can dry it slowly and evenly without mold, case-hardening, or over-drying — protecting the cup score of a $100+/lb Geisha lot.
- When I am walking the drying yard with a moisture meter and a rain cell is moving in over Volcán, I want to log each lot's moisture reading, layer depth and turning in seconds and be told which lots to cover or move first, so I can hold the controlled curve toward the 10.5–11.5% finish band and never let a near-finished lot reabsorb water or stall in the mold danger zone.
- When a lot reaches target moisture, I want the system to flag it for a mandatory rest/reposo period in pergamino and block it from milling until both moisture stability and minimum rest days are met, so I can stop premature hulling that flattens the cup and guarantee every export lot is dried to spec.
- When a buyer or an SCA/certification auditor asks how a specific lot was dried, or when a lot cups with a defect, I want a complete per-lot drying record — every moisture reading, turn, weather exposure, handler, total drying days and degree-hours — so I can prove the process for traceability and run a root-cause post-mortem that fixes the next batch.

**Features:**
- **Drying station registry (beds / patios / mechanical dryers)** — Replaces the free-text patio string with first-class, capacity-tracked drying assets (African raised beds, concrete/tiled patios, parabolic solar dryers, Guardiola/silo mechanical dryers) so the manager sees live occupancy, avoids over-loading a bed, and routes a delicate Geisha away from a hot patio.
- **Per-lot moisture-curve tracker with target bands** — Turns the single scalar moisturePct into an auditable time-series plotted against a method-specific target curve and the 10.5–11.5% finish band, catching a too-fast drop (case-hardening) or a stall in the 18–25% mold danger zone before it ruins the lot.
- **Mobile offline field-logging PWA for readings & turns** — Scan a lot QR/RFID tag and capture moisture %, water-activity, layer depth (kg/m²), and turn/cover events in seconds with no connectivity, instead of a clipboard that never reaches a computer.
- **Drying protocol templates per method & value tier** — Reusable recipes (Washed / Natural / Honey / Anaerobic × commercial vs reserve-Geisha) specifying max layer depth, turn frequency, target days and finish band, making the reserve lots' slow-dry discipline an enforceable SOP rather than tribal knowledge.
- **Weather-coupled drying advisor & cover alerts** — Pulls the local Volcán forecast and pushes prioritized cover/move/uncover actions before rain, high humidity, or harsh midday sun, protecting near-finished and natural-process lots most vulnerable to reabsorption and uneven drying.
- **Rest/reposo gate before milling** — Once a lot hits target it is locked into a mandatory reposo countdown in pergamino and cannot advance to milled until both minimum rest days and moisture stability are satisfied — enforcing the quality promise at the data layer, not on trust.
- **Drying-yard occupancy & throughput board** — A live map/board of every station, its lot, days-in, current moisture and ETA-to-target, so during peak harvest the manager plans space, predicts when beds free up, and avoids the cherry pile-up that forces shortcuts.
- **Degree-hours & shrinkage / outturn analytics** — Computes cumulative drying degree-hours, drying rate, and wet→dry shrinkage (outturn) per lot and per station, feeding green-yield forecasts and revealing which beds dry fastest or most unevenly so the farm can tune its layout.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Cropster Harvest / Processing — specialty-coffee standard for logging fermentation and drying curves, moisture readings, and lot traceability; two-way sync of lot and drying-event data, Open Food Chain / Farmer Connect / Beyco-style traceability with GS1 lot identifiers for export documentation, Sinar / DICKEY-john / Aqua-Boy / PCE coffee moisture meters and AquaLab/Pawkit water-activity meters (Bluetooth/serial reading capture), LoRaWAN ambient temp/RH and bed-level moisture probes via The Things Network, OpenWeatherMap / Open-Meteo / Tomorrow.io plus Panama ETESA-IMHPA met service for hyperlocal Volcán forecasts and rain alerts, Mechanical-dryer telemetry (Pinhalense / Penagos Guardiola dryers; Modbus / 4-20mA temperature and airflow controllers), QR/RFID lot tagging (Zebra/Brother label printers, NFC tags) for in-yard scanning, SCA green-grading and CQI Q-grading moisture/water-activity standards (ISO 6673 / 11294), Rainforest Alliance & Fair Trade record-keeping, and EU EUDR / USDA Organic traceability evidence

**Data model additions:** drying_stations (id, type: raised_bed|patio|parabolic_dryer|mechanical_guardiola, name, capacity_kg, max_layer_cm, zone, covered, status), drying_lots (links processing_batch to its drying run: station_id, protocol_id, layer_depth_cm, kg_per_m2, started_at, target_band, target_rest_days, status), moisture_readings (drying_lot_id, taken_at, moisture_pct, water_activity_aw, grain_temp_c, ambient_rh_pct, reader_worker_id, device_id) — the curve time-series, turn_events / handling_log (drying_lot_id, at, action: turn|rake|cover|uncover|move, station_from/to, worker_id), drying_protocols (id, method, value_tier, max_layer_cm, turn_frequency_hrs, target_days, finish_band_low/high), rest_periods (drying_lot_id, rest_started_at, min_days, moisture_at_rest_start, cleared_for_milling, cleared_by), drying_weather_exposures (drying_lot_id or station_id, date, max_temp_c, rain_mm, rh_pct, hours_in_danger_zone), drying_outturn (drying_lot_id, wet_kg_in, dry_kg_out, shrinkage_pct, total_drying_days, cumulative_degree_hours)

**AI / automation:**
- Curve-anomaly detection: flag lots drying too fast (case-hardening), too slow, or stalled in the 18–25% mold danger zone, and predict ETA-to-target from the live curve
- Weather-aware action prioritizer: rank which lots to cover/move/uncover before an incoming Volcán rain cell or harsh midday sun, weighting proximity-to-target and process sensitivity
- Optimal-load advisor: recommend layer depth, station type, and turn cadence for an incoming lot given method, value tier, yard occupancy, and 5-day forecast
- Defect root-cause assistant: when a lot cups with a baggy/fermented/uneven defect, correlate its drying curve, weather exposure, turn history, and station against the lot library to suggest the likely cause
- Green-outturn / shrinkage forecasting from wet weight plus live curve to plan export volumes and pricing
- Photo-based bed QA: vision check of layer evenness and foreign-matter / mold spotting from a phone snapshot of the bed

**World-class:** A lot is never a black box: every micro-lot has a continuous, meter-and-sensor-fed moisture + water-activity curve plotted against a method-specific target, slow-dried on the right station at a controlled layer depth, turned on a logged cadence, shielded from rain and harsh sun by predictive weather alerts, and held in a mandatory reposo gate that physically blocks milling until moisture stability and minimum rest days are met. The yard manager runs a live occupancy board, hits the 10.5–11.5% finish band on a smooth curve every time, and can hand any buyer or auditor a complete, tamper-evident drying dossier (readings, turns, weather, handlers, degree-hours, shrinkage). It is the operational embodiment of the specialty maxim that drying is where the most quality is lost — done right, it is where Janson's reserve cup scores are protected and proven.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson's reserve lots — washed and natural Geisha, plus Pacamara and Catuaí — are the economic core, and at $80–$300+/lb green a single mishandled dry can erase a micro-lot's entire premium. At 1,350–1,700 masl in Volcán the climate is cool, humid, and rain-prone, so drying is slow (often 15–30+ days on raised beds) and constantly threatened by reabsorption — exactly the regime where a controlled curve, weather-coupled cover alerts, and a reposo gate pay off most. Tying each drying run to the existing lot codes (JC-564, JC-602…) and the per-plot/varietal harvest data makes the dry the connective tissue of origin-to-export traceability across the 200-hectare farm and reserve, and the documented curve becomes a selling point for direct buyers and certifiers (EUDR, Rainforest Alliance, organic) — turning meticulous drying from a cost into a marketable provenance story.


#### Lot & batch genealogy — cherry→parchment→green traceability
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Mill / processing manager (beneficio supervisor) running intake, mill and green release.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When an auditor or buyer asks me to prove a green bag origin, I want an unbroken cherry-to-green chain in seconds.
- When I receive cherry, I want an auto lot code that every split, merge and stage inherits, so no kilo goes untraced.
- When I merge or split batches, I want it modeled as a graph with mass carried forward, so genealogy stays honest.
- When a lot finishes, I want cherry-kg reconciled to green-kg with anomalies flagged, and cup scores fed back to plots.

**Features:**
- **Lot genealogy graph (merge/split, mass-carry)** — DAG cherry-to-green; edges carry kg; traceable both ways.
- **Cherry intake + auto lot-code** — Weigh, attach plot/picker/Brix, mint a traceable code.
- **Mass-balance reconciliation** — Yield ~5.5:1 with out-of-band anomaly flags.
- **Green-lot passport + cup-score loop** — Buyer QR/PDF (genealogy, certs, EUDR); SCA scores back to plots.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Cropster Origin/C-sar, SCA + CQI Q-grader, GS1 + DataMatrix QR, EUDR geolocation export, Rainforest Alliance / Organic / Bird Friendly, Connected scales + fermentation loggers

**World-class:** Unbroken immutable cherry-to-green graph traceable both ways, auto mass-balance, sensor profiles, cup scores looping to plots, a bag QR with Q-score and EUDR proof. Cropster-grade, offline.


#### Quality control & cupping — SCA scoring, defect logging, calibration
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Head of Quality / Q-grader lab lead (Janson's licensed cupper, supported by the agronomist and mill manager who own the upstream signals, and the green-buyer/export sales lead who consumes the scores). On a Volcán farm this is often a family member or a CQI-licensed Q-grader who runs the on-farm sensory lab and is the gatekeeper for which lots go to the Best of Panama auction vs. commercial channels.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a processing batch reaches the green stage, I want to cup it on a structured SCA protocol and have the score auto-attach to its lot code, so I can decide whether it earns the auction/reserve channel or goes to commercial sale — without scores living in a notebook that never reaches the sales team.
- When I run a calibration session before a buyer visit or auction prep, I want the system to score each cupper against the panel mean and flag who is drifting (high/low/erratic), so I can keep my lab speaking one sensory language and trust a single farm score the way a Q-grader exam calibration does.
- When I grade a 350g green sample, I want to log primary/secondary defects by category and have full-defect equivalents and the SCA grade (Specialty / Premium / Exchange) computed automatically, so the physical grade and the cup score travel together on the lot's quality certificate.
- When I taste a standout micro-lot, I want to capture descriptive intensities and flavor descriptors (CVA-103) plus an affective 0–100 quality impression (CVA-104), so I can build a defensible flavor sheet and price story for green buyers instead of a single naked number.
- When this season's Geisha cups against last three seasons and against this plot's history, I want score trends segmented by varietal, plot, altitude band, process method and fermentation profile, so I can prove which agronomy and processing choices actually move the cup — and feed that back to the agronomist.

**Features:**
- **CVA + legacy cupping forms (SCA-102/103/104 and 2004 100-point)** — Score every green lot on the current Coffee Value Assessment (Descriptive CVA-103 intensities/descriptors + Affective CVA-104 quality impression) AND the legacy 100-point form, because the market is mid-transition and buyers/auctions still ask for both — one tap toggles the active protocol.
- **Cupping session manager with blind/randomized flights** — Set up a session, auto-assign blinded sample codes across the table, collect each cupper's sheet on a tablet, and roll up the panel mean per attribute — turns a paper table into a synced, auditable scoring session even offline at the mill.
- **Calibration & cupper-reliability engine** — Per-cupper deviation from the panel mean, drift-over-time charts, and triangulation-style odd-cup tests; flags cuppers running consistently high/low/erratic so the lab score stays trustworthy — directly mirrors the Q-grader calibration discipline.
- **Green-grading / physical defect logger** — Tap-count primary (Category 1) and secondary (Category 2) defects on a 350g sample; the app computes full-defect equivalents and the SCA grade automatically, plus moisture, density, screen-size distribution and water activity — produces the physical half of the quality certificate.
- **Lot quality certificate & flavor sheet (PDF/share link)** — One branded, traceable document per lot code: cup score, CVA flavor profile, defect grade, process, plot/altitude provenance and origin story — the artifact a green buyer or auction actually needs, generated instead of hand-typed.
- **Cup-to-cause traceability linker** — Every cupping result binds to its lotCode → processing batch → harvest → plot, so a low score or a taint (over-fermentation, phenol, mold) links back to the exact ferment time, drying curve, picking ripeness and plot — closing the loop between the cup and the field.
- **Defect/taint taxonomy & QC hold workflow** — Standardized taint vocabulary (ferment, phenolic, mold, potato/Antestia, baggy) with a QC-hold flag that can quarantine a lot from sale until re-cupped — stops a defective lot reaching a buyer and protects the brand.
- **Quality-vs-agronomy analytics dashboard** — Score trends sliced by varietal, plot, altitude band, process and fermentation profile against historical baselines, so the team sees which choices move the cup and the agronomist gets a feedback signal — not just a per-cup number.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** SCA Coffee Value Assessment standards — SCA-102 (sample prep & cupping mechanics), SCA-103 Descriptive Assessment, SCA-104 Affective Assessment (2023, supersede the 2004 protocol); plus the legacy SCA 100-point form and CoE scoresheet, Cropster Cup / Cropster (cupping + green-inventory) — import/export sensory sessions and lot data so Janson's lab interoperates with roasters who already standardize on Cropster, CQI Q-grader licensing & calibration framework (now an SCA program as of 2025) — calibration logic and licensed-cupper identity, SCA Arabica Green Coffee Defect Handbook / Green Arabica Classification System — the canonical primary/secondary defect taxonomy and full-defect-equivalent math, Best of Panama / SCAP (Specialty Coffee Association of Panama) auction scoresheet + lot-entry format — Janson's premium Geisha sales channel, Lab hardware: moisture/water-activity meters (e.g. Sinar / Aqualab/Novasina), green-bean color analyzers, sample roaster log (Cropster Roast / Ikawa sample roaster) so the roast that feeds the cup is recorded, Surveyor/SCAA cupping form data exchange (CSV/JSON) and a printable QR-coded lot certificate for buyer hand-off

**Data model additions:** CuppingSession — id, date, purpose (calibration | pre-auction | QC | buyer-visit), protocol (CVA | SCA-2004 | CoE), location, leadCupperId, sampleRoastRef, CuppingScore — id, sessionId, lotCode, cupperId, protocol, affectiveScore (CVA-104 0–100 or 2004 total), attribute breakdown (fragrance/aroma, flavor, aftertaste, acidity, sweetness, mouthfeel, overall), cupsEvaluated, isBlind, notes, CvaDescriptive — scoreId, attribute intensities (aroma/flavor/aftertaste/acidity/sweetness/mouthfeel high–low) + structured flavor descriptors (CATA tags), DefectGrade — id, lotCode, sampleGrams (350), primaryDefectCount, secondaryDefectCount, fullDefectEquivalents (computed), scaGrade (Specialty | Premium | Exchange | Below), moisturePct, waterActivity, density, screenSizeDistribution, gradedBy, Cupper — id, name, cqiLicenseId, role, calibrationStatus, rollingDeviationFromPanel (drift metric), DefectTaxonomy — code, name, category (1|2), sensory (taint) vs physical, fullDefectWeight, QualityCertificate — id, lotCode, cupScore, cvaProfileRef, defectGradeRef, processMethod, plotProvenance, status (draft | issued | revoked), shareToken, QcHold — id, lotCode, reason (taint/defect), raisedBy, status (held | re-cupped | released | rejected), Lot (extends the existing lotCode link) — adds qualityScore, scaGrade, channel (auction | reserve | commercial) so the cup outcome routes the sale

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-route a green-stage batch into the cupping queue and pre-fill a draft sheet from its upstream signals (varietal, process, ferment time, drying curve, brix, ripeness), so a lot is never sold uncupped.
- Cupper-drift detection: statistical flag when a cupper's scores diverge from the calibrated panel mean beyond a threshold, with a 'who needs recalibration' nudge before high-stakes sessions.
- Predicted cup-score band from upstream agronomy + processing data (model trained on Janson's own cup history), so the team can triage which lots to cup first and forecast auction-grade volume early in the season.
- NL flavor-note normalization: free-text tasting notes mapped to the SCA Coffee Taster's Flavor Wheel / CATA descriptor set, so notes become structured, filterable data instead of prose.
- Root-cause assist: when a lot scores low or shows a taint, surface the most-correlated upstream variable (e.g. 'ferment ran 6h longer than this plot's high-scoring lots') as a hypothesis for the agronomist/mill.
- Auto-draft the buyer-facing flavor sheet and price-story copy from the CVA profile and provenance, kept in Janson's brand voice.

**World-class:** "A closed sensory loop: every green lot is cupped on the current SCA Coffee Value Assessment (and legacy form on demand) by a calibrated, drift-monitored panel, with each score bound to its full traceability chain (cup → batch → harvest → plot → ferment/drying curve). The lab self-calibrates with measurable per-cupper reliability, physical green grading and cup scoring produce one auditable quality certificate, and analytics feed agronomy and processing decisions so quality is engineered, not discovered. It interoperates with the tools roasters and auctions actually use (Cropster, CoE, Best of Panama), generates the buyer-ready flavor sheet automatically, and can quarantine a defective lot before it ever ships — matching the rigor of a CQI Q-grader exam calibration applied to the farm's own daily QC."

**Coffee-specific angle:** "Janson grows Geisha at 1,350–1,700 masl in Volcán — Panama Geisha is the single highest-value coffee on earth, where Best of Panama auction lots clear hundreds to thousands of dollars per pound and a 1–2 point swing in cup score is the difference between a record auction lot and commercial green. For Janson, cupping is not back-office QC — it is the pricing engine for the farm's whole economic model and the proof behind the 'from our farm to your cup since 1990' brand. A world-class QC/cupping module lets them defend Geisha micro-lot scores to international buyers, enter Best of Panama with auction-format sheets, and tie a celebrated cup back to the exact plot, altitude band and anaerobic/washed/natural ferment that produced it — turning the 200-hectare reserve's terroir into a documented, sellable quality story rather than an unverified claim."


#### Green coffee inventory & warehousing — by lot/grade/screen/moisture
*Priority:* **table-stakes**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Mill & warehouse manager (Janson's bodega/almacén lead) — the person who takes a processing batch the moment it hits the "green" stage and owns it as a stored, sellable asset until it ships. Secondary users: the green-coffee sales lead (allocates inventory against orders/PO contracts), the Q-grader/cupper (assigns grade + cup score that re-prices a lot), and the owner (sees true sellable inventory value and aging risk). Today this role lives entirely in a spreadsheet + a notebook taped to the bodega wall.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a processing batch finishes drying and rests to a stable ~10.5–11.5% moisture, I want it to automatically become a tracked green-coffee inventory lot with its weight, moisture, screen size, grade and physical location, so I can stop losing track of what's actually in the bodega the moment it leaves the patio.
- When a buyer or our sales lead asks 'how many kilos of Geisha Washed screen 17+ at 86+ cup score do we have available right now and where are the bags?', I want an instant, accurate answer by lot/grade/screen/moisture, so I can quote and reserve confidently instead of counting sacks by hand.
- When green coffee sits in the warehouse, I want the system to watch moisture creep, water activity and age against each lot's freshness window and flag any lot drifting toward defect or out-of-spec, so I can sell or re-cup the at-risk lots first and never ship coffee that's gone past crop or rehydrated.
- When I move, blend, repackage, sample, ship, or write down a lot, I want every gram tracked through a full chain of custody back to the harvest plot, picker and processing method, so a Q-graded micro-lot's provenance is airtight for an importer audit, a certification body, or a competition entry.

**Features:**
- **Green lot ledger (lot → grade → screen → moisture)** — A first-class GreenLot record auto-created when a ProcessingBatch hits the 'green' stage, carrying weight, moisture %, water activity, screen-size distribution, density, defect count (full + secondary defects per SCA), grade and physical bin/bay location — turns the terminal 'green' batch into a managed, queryable, sellable asset instead of dropping off the board.
- **Warehouse bin / location map (GrainPro-aware)** — Visual rack/bay/pallet map of the bodega showing exactly which sacks (jute, GrainPro/Ecotact hermetic liner, vacuum) sit where, with capacity and current fill — so anyone can physically find a lot in seconds and the manager can plan space before the next harvest wave lands.
- **Quality grade & cup-score engine (SCA / CQI)** — Records green grade (Specialty Grade / Premium per SCA Green Arabica standard: defect count from a 350 g sample) and the cup score from the lab, and re-prices the lot the moment an 88-point Geisha is logged — connecting physical inventory to its true market value and unlocking reserve/competition pricing.
- **Moisture / water-activity & freshness-window watcher** — Per-lot moisture and aw thresholds with age-since-milled tracking and a 'sell-by / re-cup-by' window; flags lots drifting out of the 10–12% safe band or aging past peak so the manager works at-risk lots first and never ships flat or moldy coffee — directly protects Janson's specialty reputation.
- **Chain-of-custody & traceability passport** — Every lot links back through processing method, drying patio, harvest date, plot, altitude and picker, and forward through every movement (split, blend, repack, sample-pull, ship); generates a one-click traceability sheet / QR passport for importer audits, Rainforest Alliance / organic certification, and competition (Best of Panama) entries.
- **Inventory movements & physical-count reconciliation** — Logs every receipt, internal move, blend, repackage, sample withdrawal, shrink (further moisture loss in storage), and dispatch as immutable transactions; supports cycle counts that reconcile system kg vs scanned-sack kg so book inventory always matches the bodega floor.
- **Available-to-promise allocation against orders** — Soft-reserve and hard-allocate green kg against sales orders / export contracts so the same Geisha micro-lot is never double-sold; shows on-hand vs reserved vs available-to-promise by lot/grade, feeding directly into quoting and the export/shipping pillar.
- **Sampling & offer-sample management** — Tracks pre-shipment and offer sample pulls (weight deducted from the parent lot, sample ID, recipient roaster/importer, send date), so samples are never untracked shrink and every roaster sample is tied to the exact lot it represents.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Cropster Origin / Cropster Cup — the specialty-coffee standard for green lot, cupping-score and inventory management; sync GreenLot + cup scores both ways, Sucafina IMA / similar green inventory & contract platforms for export-side lot/contract reconciliation, SCA Green Coffee Grading standard (defect classification from a 350 g sample) + CQI Q-grade cupping protocol as the grading rule engine, Sinar / Aqualab (Addium) water-activity meters and Dickey-john / Sinar moisture meters — Bluetooth/USB capture of moisture % and aw straight into the lot record, GrainPro / Ecotact hermetic bag & SuperGrainBag metadata for packaging type and the storage-stability model, Zebra / Brother label printers + QR/Code-128 + GS1 lot coding for sack labels and the traceability passport, Bench scale / platform-scale serial/Bluetooth capture (e.g. Mettler Toledo, Ohaus) for receipt and cycle-count weights, Best of Panama / SCAP competition lot registry and ICO/exporter lot-code conventions for Panama green export, IoT warehouse environment sensors (Govee / SensorPush temperature + humidity) to log bodega conditions affecting stored lots, QuickBooks / Xero for inventory-asset valuation and COGS once a lot is dispatched

**Data model additions:** green_lots — id, source_batch_id, lot_code, variety, method, weight_kg, moisture_pct, water_activity, density_g_l, screen_avg, grade, cup_score, status (resting | available | reserved | shipped | held), milled_date, sell_by_date, location_id, green_lot_screen_sizes — green_lot_id, screen (10–20), pct (screen-size distribution per lot), green_lot_defects — green_lot_id, defect_type, category (primary/secondary per SCA), count, cup_scores — id, green_lot_id, cupper (worker_id), cupped_date, total_score, fragrance, flavor, acidity, body, balance, notes, protocol (SCA/CQI), warehouse_locations — id, type (rack | bay | pallet | bin), label, capacity_kg, current_kg, env_sensor_id, inventory_movements — id, green_lot_id, kind (receipt | move | blend | repack | sample | shrink | dispatch | adjust), qty_kg, from_location_id, to_location_id, at, worker_id, ref (order/sample/blend id), packaging_units — id, green_lot_id, location_id, bag_type (jute | grainpro | vacuum), net_kg, qr_code, tare_kg, green_lot_blends — blend_lot_id, component_lot_id, qty_kg (parent/child lineage for blended lots), green_samples — id, green_lot_id, weight_g, kind (offer | pre-shipment | lab), recipient, sent_date, sample_code, allocations — id, green_lot_id, order_id, qty_kg, status (soft | hard | fulfilled)

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-promote batch→lot: when a ProcessingBatch crosses the 'green' moisture/age threshold, auto-generate the GreenLot with carried-over provenance and a draft grade, surfaced for one-tap confirm — removes the manual re-keying that loses lots today
- Defect / screen detection from a sample photo: vision model estimates screen-size distribution and flags visible defects (blacks, sours, broken, insect-damage) from a phone photo of a green sample tray, pre-filling the SCA grade for the grader to confirm
- Moisture/aw drift forecasting: time-series model predicts which lots will breach the safe band given bodega temp/humidity trends, ranking a 'work-these-first' queue before any lot actually goes out of spec
- Demand-aware sell-down advisor: recommends which lots to allocate/sell next by crossing freshness window, cup score, holding cost and open orders — so peak-quality micro-lots ship at peak and tail lots don't age into shrink
- Natural-language inventory Q&A: 'show available Geisha screen 17+ 86+ resting under 90 days' answered conversationally over the lot ledger, with the bin locations to pull from
- Cup-note + lot-story generator: drafts the marketing/traceability narrative (plot, altitude, picker crew, process, cup notes) for each micro-lot's offer sheet and QR passport from the linked provenance data

**World-class:** World-class is a living, gram-accurate green inventory where book stock always equals the bodega floor, and every lot is queryable across the four axes that actually price coffee — lot, grade, screen, moisture/aw — in real time. The instant a batch finishes drying it becomes a graded, located, valued, sellable asset with a freshness clock; a buyer's question ('200 kg Geisha Washed, screen 17+, 87 cup, resting <60 days — what's available and where?') is answered in seconds with bin locations and an instant reserve. Quality data (SCA defect grade + Q cup score) re-prices inventory automatically and flows to/from Cropster; moisture/aw is captured straight off the meter, not retyped; every sack carries a QR that resolves to a full farm-to-bodega traceability passport good enough for an importer audit, an organic/Rainforest cert, or a Best of Panama entry. Nothing ages silently into defect, nothing is double-sold, and no sample leaves untracked.

**Coffee-specific angle:** This is the linchpin between Janson's processing pillar and its actual revenue — Janson sells specialty GREEN (and roasted) coffee, so the green bodega IS the inventory of the business, yet today it's the one stage with no system (the pipeline literally ends at the 'green' batch stage and the asset vanishes from the board). At Volcán's 1,350–1,700 masl, dense high-grown Geisha and Pacamara command reserve/auction prices where a single cup-score point swings dollars per pound, so screen size (Geisha micro-lots often spec screen 17/18+), moisture stability (the band that keeps a competition lot from going flat or rehydrating in humid Chiriquí), and airtight provenance back to a named plot and picker crew are exactly what importers and Best of Panama judges pay for. Hermetic GrainPro storage to protect those high-value lots, per-lot cup scores from the farm's own cupping lab, and a QR traceability passport tying each sack to a Tizingal Alto / Las Lagunas Geisha block are what turn Janson's quality into price.


#### Dry milling — hulling, grading, density/screen sorting
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Dry mill operator / mill manager (the "beneficio seco" lead). At Janson this is the person who takes dried parchment ("pergamino") out of storage and runs it through hulling, screen/density/color sorting to produce export-grade green coffee — accountable for outturn (green yield from parchment), defect counts, screen-size distribution, and hitting the SCA grade promised to buyers. Secondary users: the QC/cupping lead (who signs off grade and links physical grade to cup score), the green-coffee inventory/export manager (who needs final green weights and grades to allocate to contracts), and the farm owner/GM (who watches outturn % and milling cost per quintal as a margin lever).

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When dried parchment comes out of rest/storage and is ready to mill, I want to confirm it has equilibrated to the right moisture (10.5-11.5%) and water activity (<0.60 aw) before I hull it, so I can avoid milling brittle or mushy beans that shatter into broken/chipped defects and tank my green outturn.
- When I run a lot through the huller, polisher, screen grader, densimetric (gravity) table and optical sorter, I want every machine pass to record input weight, output weight, screen-size distribution, density-table cuts and defects-removed in real time, so I can compute exact parchment-to-green outturn and a full mass balance instead of guessing where the weight went.
- When I finish milling a lot, I want the system to auto-classify it against SCA green-grading and Panama/SCAP specialty rules (screen size, defect count per 300-350g, moisture, density) and tie that to its cup score, so I can confidently label it 'SHB EP, screen 17/18, Specialty Grade, 88+ Geisha' for export contracts and the Best of Panama auction.
- When milling is done, I want byproducts (cascara/parchment husk, broken/triage 'pasilla', defective beans) weighed and routed — husk to compost/biomass, pasilla to local-market lots — so nothing leaves the mill untracked and I capture revenue and sustainability data from what used to be waste.

**Features:**
- **Pre-mill readiness gate (moisture + water-activity check)** — Blocks hulling a lot until moisture (10.5-11.5%) and water activity (<0.60 aw) are in spec, recording the reading and meter used; prevents the single biggest outturn-killer (milling under/over-dried parchment) and protects the green from mold/quaker risk in storage.
- **Milling run / machine-pass tracker (huller → polisher → screen grader → gravity table → optical sorter)** — One run = an ordered chain of machine passes, each logging input kg, output kg, settings (screen sizes, table tilt/air, sorter sensitivity) and reject kg. Gives a complete mass balance and the per-machine loss breakdown, so a 78% vs 82% outturn lot can be explained, not shrugged at.
- **Green outturn & yield dashboard** — Auto-computes parchment→green outturn % per lot, per varietal, per process and per season, with milling cost per kg/quintal. Turns the mill from a black box into a margin lever — Geisha at 5x the price makes every lost point of outturn materially expensive.
- **Screen-size & density distribution profile** — Captures the screen grader's size split (screen 14-20, peaberry/caracol fraction) and gravity-table density cuts per lot, producing the size histogram buyers ask for and the basis for premium screen-17/18 separation.
- **SCA / SCAP green-grade classifier + defect-count workbench** — A 350g sample workflow that tallies Category 1 (full defects) and Category 2 (secondary defects) against SCA green-grading rules, computes Specialty/Premium/Exchange grade, and pulls in screen + moisture + density to stamp a defensible grade certificate — the document the export contract and Best of Panama entry stand on.
- **Grade ↔ cup-score linkage** — Binds each milled green lot to its QC cupping score and tasting notes so 'physical grade' and 'cup grade' live on one record — essential for a Geisha estate selling on the 90+ score, not just on screen size.
- **Byproduct & pasilla ledger** — Weighs and routes husk/cascara (compost/biomass), triage beans and pasilla (local-market lots), giving a closed mass balance and turning waste streams into tracked, sometimes saleable, inventory + ESG data.
- **Green lot creation & lot-code split/merge to inventory** — On mill completion, generates the final green-coffee inventory lot(s) — including clean splits when one parchment lot yields multiple grades (export 17/18 vs pasilla) — with full lineage back through drying, processing, harvest, plot and tree-block; feeds the green inventory / export module directly.
- **Milling equipment log & maintenance/calibration tracker** — Tracks huller blade wear, screen cleaning, gravity-table calibration and optical-sorter recipes per machine, with hours-run and maintenance-due alerts — worn hulling settings are a stealth source of broken-bean defects, so calibration state is a quality input, not just upkeep.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Cropster Roast/Cropster Origin (specialty-coffee lot, grading and traceability standard) — green lot, grade and outturn sync, Aeris / Sucafina IMA or Pinhalense / Penagos dry-mill equipment data where PLC/serial outputs exist (huller, catador/gravity table, screen grader), Optical color sorter telemetry — Bühler SORTEX or Satake (reject counts, throughput) via their data exports, Lab moisture & water-activity meters — Sinar AP/Sinar 6070, DICKEY-john GAC, or AquaLab/Rotronic for aw — Bluetooth/serial capture so readings aren't hand-typed, SCA Arabica Green Coffee Grading + Coffee Defect Handbook (the defect-count rule engine) and SCA cupping protocol, SCAP (Specialty Coffee Association of Panama) / Best of Panama auction lot-entry format and grade categories, ICO / ICE Arabica 'C' contract reference and Panama export documentation (ICAFE-equivalent phytosanitary + certificate-of-origin fields), Calibrated platform/floor scales (Mettler Toledo, Avery) and screen-grader sieve sets (Tyler/SCA screen gauge 14-20) for trusted weights and sizing, Certification bodies for chain-of-custody at the mill — Rainforest Alliance, USDA/EU Organic, Fair Trade — green-lot certificate fields, QR/RFID lot tagging (GrainPro / Ecotact liner + jute bag labels) for the physical green bags that leave the mill

**Data model additions:** milling_runs — one milling event for a parchment lot: links source processing_batch/lot_code + green lot output, operator, start/end, input parchment kg, total green kg out, computed outturn_pct, milling_cost, machine_passes — ordered passes within a run (huller, polisher, screen_grader, gravity_table, optical_sorter): pass_type, machine_id, input_kg, output_kg, reject_kg, settings (jsonb: screen sizes, tilt/air, sorter recipe), green_lots — final export inventory unit produced by a run: green_lot_code, parent lot lineage, variety, process, grade, screen_distribution, density_class, moisture_pct, water_activity, weight_kg, cup_score_ref, certifications[], green_grades — grading record per green lot: sca_grade (Specialty/Premium/Exchange), cat1_defects, cat2_defects, sample_weight_g, screen_primary, peaberry_pct, graded_by, graded_at, grade_certificate_ref, byproduct_outputs — husk/cascara, pasilla, triage per run: byproduct_type, weight_kg, destination (compost/biomass/local-market/sale), revenue_usd nullable, milling_equipment — huller/polisher/screen grader/gravity table/optical sorter registry: type, vendor/model, hours_run, last_calibrated, calibration_due, status, equipment_maintenance_log — calibration/maintenance events per machine: event_type, performed_by, notes, next_due, green_inventory_movements — green lot weight changes (mill-in, allocated-to-contract, shipped, sample-out) for live exportable-stock balance, Extend batch_stage / pipeline so the single 'milled' stage explodes into hulling → grading → sorting → green sub-states (or a dedicated milling pipeline keyed off the existing lot_code)

**AI / automation:**
- Outturn anomaly detection — flag lots whose parchment→green yield deviates from the varietal/process/drying-curve baseline and surface the most likely machine pass (e.g. over-aggressive huller or gravity-table air) responsible for the loss.
- Computer-vision green-grading assist — phone/lab-camera image of a 350g sample auto-counts and classifies Category 1/2 defects (blacks, sours, broken, insect-damage, quakers) against the SCA Defect Handbook, pre-filling the grade workbench for human confirmation.
- Vision-based screen-size & peaberry estimation from a sample photo as a cross-check on the mechanical screen grader.
- Optimal-mill-day predictor — combines moisture/aw readings and rest time to recommend exactly when a rested parchment lot is at its sweet spot to hull, minimizing broken-bean loss.
- Optical-sorter recipe recommender — suggests color/defect sensitivity settings per varietal/process from the history of which recipes produced the cleanest grade at the best outturn.
- Grade-to-price / contract-fit advisor — given the computed grade, screen split and cup score, recommends which export contract, auction (Best of Panama) or local-market channel each green lot and its pasilla fraction should go to.
- Auto-drafted SCA/SCAP grade + traceability certificate generated in natural language from the run's mass balance, grading record and full lineage.

**World-class:** "A buyer scans the QR on a 60kg GrainPro-lined bag and sees the bean's whole life: tree block and altitude, harvest day and Brix, ferment recipe, drying curve, the exact day it was hulled, its screen-17/18 split, gravity-table density cut, defect count per 350g, final moisture/aw, SCA grade, cup score and the operator who ran it. Outturn is computed to the gram with a closed mass balance — every kg of parchment is accounted for as green, pasilla, husk or moisture loss — so the owner sees milling cost per quintal and yield per varietal as a live margin lever. The mill won't let an out-of-spec lot be hulled, auto-grades against SCA/SCAP rules, and emits an export-ready grade + traceability certificate in one click. Calibration state of every machine is a tracked quality input, and byproducts are weighed and monetized, not dumped. This is the level a Geisha estate selling into Best of Panama must operate at — where milling is a precision finishing step, not a weight-loss mystery."

**Coffee-specific angle:** "Dry milling is where Janson's money is decided. As a 1,350-1,700 masl estate, its lots are SHB/EP ('Strictly Hard Bean, European Prep') and screen-size + defect-prep is exactly what commands the SHB premium and the right to enter Best of Panama. For Geisha selling at 5-50x commodity, a 4-point outturn swing or a handful of missed Category-1 defects is enormous money — so a precise hulling/grading/density-sorting record is not bookkeeping, it's the difference between an 'EP Specialty Geisha 90' lot and a downgrade. The system must natively model the parchment→green outturn that washed/honey/natural and anaerobic lots each hit differently, separate the prized peaberry (caracol) fraction, and split a single dried lot into an export-grade 17/18 green lot plus a pasilla local lot — closing the traceability chain from a specific tree block and the 200-ha quetzal reserve all the way to the bag a roaster buys. The 'milled' stage in today's model is a single opaque hop; world-class means exploding it into the auditable, certificate-producing finishing line that justifies Janson's price."


#### Roasting — roast profiles & logs (vertical integration)
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Head Roaster / Roast-and-QC Lead at Janson (the family member or trusted operator who runs the sample roaster + production drum roaster after the dry mill hands off green coffee). Secondary users: the QC/cupping lead who scores roasts on the SCA form, and the owner who needs roast yield, shrinkage, and cost-per-roasted-kg to price the jansoncoffee.com retail bags and reserve Geisha lots.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a new green-coffee lot finishes resting after the dry mill (e.g. washed Geisha JC-596), I want to develop and lock a repeatable roast profile (charge temp, turning point, first-crack time, development-time ratio, drop temp) on a sample roaster, so I can hit the same cup on every production batch instead of relying on one person's memory.
- When I run a production roast on the drum roaster, I want the bean-temperature/ET curve, rate-of-rise, gas/airflow events, and end weight captured automatically and tied to the exact green lot it consumed, so I have a defensible record of what I did and full bean-to-bag traceability for that JC lot.
- When a roast finishes, I want green-in vs roasted-out weight logged so roast loss/shrinkage and cost-per-roasted-kg compute automatically, so the owner can price retail bags and reserve lots on real numbers instead of guesses.
- When green inventory of a prized lot (200-hectare reserve Geisha) is running low, I want to see remaining kg, roasts scheduled against it, and reorder/allocation alerts, so I never over-promise a sold-out micro-lot to a wholesale buyer or the webshop.

**Features:**
- **Roast Profile Library (golden curves)** — Versioned, approved roast curves per green lot + varietal + intended use (filter/espresso/sample). Locking a 'golden' profile turns tribal knowledge into a repeatable standard and lets any roaster reproduce a medal-winning Geisha roast.
- **Live Roast Logger + curve capture** — Records the bean-temp (BT) / environmental-temp (ET) curve, rate-of-rise (RoR), charge/turning-point/first-crack/drop milestones and gas/airflow events for every batch — auto via Artisan/Modbus where hardware allows, or a fast manual mode (tap milestones, enter temps) when it doesn't. Gives a defensible per-batch record and the data to debug stalls/crashes/scorching.
- **Green Inventory & lot allocation** — Tracks green coffee on hand by JC lot/variety/process (fed straight from the processing pipeline's 'green' stage), decrements on each roast charge, and warns before a reserve lot is over-committed. Closes the gap between what's sold and what's actually in the warehouse.
- **Roast-loss & yield economics** — Auto-computes shrinkage % (green-in vs roasted-out), cost-per-roasted-kg (green cost + labor + energy), and margin per retail bag / wholesale lot. Turns roasting from a craft cost-center into a priced, defensible product line.
- **Profile-vs-target deviation alerts** — Overlays the live/finished curve against the locked golden profile and flags drift (RoR crash, baked/under-developed, drop temp off target) so off-spec batches are caught at the roaster, not in the cup a week later.
- **Cupping & QC scorecards tied to the roast** — SCA-form cupping scores, defect notes, and tasting tags attached to the exact roast batch + green lot, so the cup result feeds back to refine the next profile and substantiates the jansoncoffee.com lot descriptions and competition entries (Best of Panama).
- **Roast-to-product & traceability label** — Links a roast batch to finished retail SKUs / wholesale lots and emits a per-bag traceability story (plot -> harvest date -> process -> roast profile -> cup score) for QR labels — the literal embodiment of Janson's 'from our farm to your cup' promise.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Artisan (artisan-scope.org) — free/open-source roast logger: import/export .alog JSON roast profiles and ingest live BT/ET/RoR; the $0-friendly capture path, Modbus TCP/RTU + PID controllers (Fuji, Delta, Arduino-TC4) — read bean/environment thermocouples and gas/airflow off the production drum roaster, Cropster Roast / Cropster Cup API — industry-standard interop for roast profiles, green inventory and SCA cupping data (import existing curves; export for buyers who run Cropster), Roastime / Aillio Bullet cloud — sample-roaster profile import for the development bench, SCA cupping protocol + Coffee Value Assessment (CVA) descriptor lexicon — the scoring standard for the QC scorecards, Phidgets / generic K-type thermocouple USB DAQ — low-cost probe input where there is no PID, Shopify / WooCommerce (jansoncoffee.com) — push roast-dated, traceable SKUs and decrement finished-goods stock on sale, Energy/utility metering (gas flow meter, smart-plug kWh like a Shelly/Emporia) — feed energy cost into cost-per-roasted-kg and a roastery carbon line, QR / GS1 Digital Link label generation for the bean-to-bag traceability story on retail bags

**Data model additions:** roasters (machine registry: id, name, type sample|drum, capacity_kg, fuel gas|electric, probe/Modbus config), green_inventory (lot_code FK -> lots, variety, process, kg_on_hand, warehouse_bin, cost_per_kg, received_date) — fed from processing_batches at stage='green', roast_profiles (id, name, green lot/variety target, version, status draft|golden|retired, charge_temp, turning_point_s, first_crack_s, drop_temp, dev_time_ratio_pct, target_roast_level, notes) — the golden-curve library, roast_batches (id, profile_id FK, roaster_id FK, lot_code FK, green_in_kg, roasted_out_kg, shrinkage_pct generated, charge_at, drop_at, roast_level, operator worker_id, energy_kwh, cost_usd), roast_curve_points (batch_id FK, t_seconds, bean_temp, env_temp, ror) — the time-series curve, plus roast_events (batch_id, t_seconds, kind charge|TP|FC|drop|gas|airflow, value), cupping_sessions + cupping_scores (batch_id FK, lot_code, cupper worker_id, sca_total, fragrance/flavor/acidity/body/aftertaste subscores, descriptors[], defects), finished_products / roast_sku_links (sku, roast_batch_id FK, bag_size_g, retail_price, channel retail|wholesale|webshop) — closes roast -> product traceability, New enum values: roast_level enum (light|medium-light|medium|medium-dark|dark), roaster_type, and a new activity_kind = 'roast'

**AI / automation:**
- Roast-curve anomaly detection: flag RoR crashes, baking, scorching or stalls in real time by comparing live BT/RoR against the locked golden profile, and suggest the corrective action (cut gas, increase airflow).
- Profile recommendation: given a green lot's density, moisture, screen size, varietal and process, suggest a starting roast profile from the history of similar lots that cupped highest.
- Cup-score predictor / closed loop: learn which curve shapes (DTR, FC timing, drop temp) correlate with the highest SCA scores for Geisha vs Caturra and propose next-batch tweaks.
- Auto-generated tasting copy: turn cupping descriptors + lot provenance into on-brand jansoncoffee.com product descriptions and QR-label stories.
- Demand-aware roast scheduling: forecast retail/wholesale demand and green-inventory burn-down to auto-draft the week's roast plan and reorder/allocation alerts.
- Roast-log assistant: parse a photographed paper roast log or voice notes into structured curve milestones for the manual capture path.

**World-class:** "World-class roasting means every bag Janson sells is traceable to a specific roast batch, green lot, plot and harvest date, roasted to a locked, version-controlled golden profile, with the actual curve captured automatically and scored on the SCA form — and the system catches an off-spec roast before it ever reaches a customer. It is Cropster-grade roast profiling + cupping + green-inventory + cost economics, but unified with the upstream farm/processing data so a reserve-Geisha micro-lot flows seamlessly from cherry on the tree to a QR-labeled bag, with yield, margin and cup quality all measured. The roaster reproduces a competition-winning roast on demand; the owner prices on real cost-per-roasted-kg; nothing is sold that isn't actually in the warehouse."

**Coffee-specific angle:** "Roasting is the literal payoff of Janson's vertical integration and brand promise — 'from our farm to your cup since 1990' is only true if the farm controls the roast, not just the green. Today the app's pipeline dead-ends at the 'green' stage; roasting is the missing link that turns green coffee into the roasted product Janson actually sells on jansoncoffee.com and to wholesale. It matters most for the high-value Geisha and reserve micro-lots (1,350-1,700 masl, the 200-ha quetzal-habitat reserve) and Best of Panama competition entries, where a single locked golden profile on a celebrated lot is worth a large premium and a botched roast destroys an irreplaceable, scarce harvest. It also closes the traceability loop the specialty market and certifications demand: plot -> harvest -> process -> roast -> cup, all on one record."


#### Sample & offer management — buyer sample tracking & shipping
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Green coffee sales lead / export manager at the farm — at Janson this is a family member or trusted estate manager who owns buyer relationships, decides which micro-lots to put on offer, ships physical samples to roasters and importers worldwide, and shepherds each deal from first cupping through PSS approval to contract. Secondary users: the QC/cupping lab tech who roasts and scores samples, and the importer/roaster on the receiving end (a portal guest).

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a micro-lot finishes drying and I have a finished green lot to sell, I want to draw representative samples, label them with full traceability, and log how much green I have left to send, so I can put the lot on offer to the right buyers without losing track of which of my limited Geisha kilos are already promised.
- When a roaster in Tokyo or Oslo requests a sample, I want to package it, generate the customs paperwork and a courier label, and track the shipment to their door, so I can prove it arrived, follow up at the right moment, and never let a $600/lb-potential lead go cold.
- When a buyer cups my sample and sends back a score and notes, I want every sample's status (offered → sample sent → cupped → approved/rejected/countered) and the agreed price differential captured in one place, so I can see my live pipeline, reconcile their cupping score against my own, and convert approvals into PSS and contracts.
- When I prepare a seasonal offer sheet or enter a Best of Panama lot, I want to assemble lots with their scores, processing, altitude, varietal, and remaining availability into a shareable, branded offer, so buyers self-serve the catalog and I stop rebuilding the same PDF for every email.

**Features:**
- **Sample registry with type taxonomy (Type / Offer / Stocklot / PSS / Arrival)** — Every physical sample is a tracked entity linked to its source lot_code, with the industry-standard sample type so a buyer's offer sample and the later pre-shipment sample of the same coffee chain together — no more guessing which jar on the shelf is which.
- **Sample inventory & draw-down ledger (grams sent vs. green kg remaining)** — Drawing a 100–300g sample decrements the lot's available green; the offer board shows true remaining availability so the same scarce Geisha micro-lot is never double-offered or oversold.
- **Shipment tracker with carrier + customs document pack** — One click generates the courier label (DHL/FedEx), commercial invoice, certificate of origin, and Prior Notice / phytosanitary checklist, and tracks status to delivery — turning a manual, error-prone export chore into a logged, traceable shipment with proof of arrival.
- **Offer & deal pipeline (Kanban: offered → sent → cupping → approved/rejected/countered → contracted)** — A live board of every buyer conversation with the negotiated price differential and quantity, so the sales lead always knows what is in flight and what to follow up on, and approvals flow straight into a PSS request and contract.
- **Buyer / contact CRM with relationship history** — Each importer and roaster has a profile (region, certifications they need, past lots bought, scores they gave) so offers are targeted to the buyers most likely to pay — and the family's decades of relationships outlive any one person's memory.
- **Branded offer sheet builder + buyer self-serve portal with QR** — Assemble lots (score, varietal, process, altitude, harvest window, remaining kg, price) into a shareable forest-green Janson catalog; each lot gets a QR that opens its full traceability story, so buyers self-serve and Janson looks world-class instead of emailing a spreadsheet.
- **Cupping-score capture & reconciliation (SCA CVA / CoE form)** — Record the lab's own score and the buyer's returned score side by side on the standard form, flagging gaps — protecting price on high-value lots where a one-point swing is real money.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Cropster Hub (publish offer lots to the global specialty marketplace) and Cropster Origin/Cup for sample, cupping-sheet and inventory sync, IKAWA Pro sample roaster app sync (roast logs/curves for each sample roast), SCA cupping standards: Coffee Value Assessment (CVA 2024) form and the legacy 100-point SCA form; Cup of Excellence (CoE) form for competition lots, DHL Express and FedEx APIs for sample courier labels and tracking; EasyPost/Shippo as a multi-carrier abstraction, US FDA Prior Notice and APHIS/phytosanitary documentation, certificate-of-origin and commercial-invoice generation, Best of Panama / SCAP auction platform export format for competition-lot entries, QR code generation (qrcode) + the app's own lot traceability pages for buyer scanning, Stripe / Wise for sample-fee or deposit collection and FX on international offers, Email/calendar (Gmail/Resend) for offer send-outs, cupping reminders, and follow-up nudges

**Data model additions:** buyers — id, name, company, type (roaster/importer/auction/direct), region, contact, certifications_needed, relationship_since, notes, samples — id, lot_code (FK lots), sample_type (type/offer/stocklot/pss/arrival), grams, drawn_date, status, lab_score, prepared_by, parent_sample_id (links offer→PSS chain), sample_shipments — id, sample_id (FK), buyer_id (FK), carrier, tracking_number, ship_date, delivered_date, customs_docs[], cost_usd, status, offers — id, lot_code (FK lots), buyer_id (FK), offer_date, price_per_lb_usd, differential_basis, quantity_kg, stage (offered/sent/cupping/approved/rejected/countered/contracted), expiry, buyer_score, notes, offer_sheets — id, season, title, published_at, public_slug, lot_codes[] (the catalog of lots on offer), cupping_scores — id, sample_id (FK), scorer (lab/buyer), form (CVA/SCA/CoE), total_score, attribute breakdown JSON, tasting_notes, cupped_date, lots additions — green_kg_available, status (available/reserved/sold) so sample draws and offers decrement real inventory

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-draft offer emails and a tailored lot shortlist per buyer from their past scores, certifications, and price history ('these 3 lots match what Saza bought last season')
- Cupping-note generation and reconciliation — summarize the buyer's returned CVA notes vs. the lab's, and flag score gaps >1.5 points that put price at risk
- Follow-up nudge engine — predict the right moment to chase a sent-but-silent sample based on transit time + delivery + typical cupping turnaround, and surface the deals most likely to close
- Demand/price intelligence — suggest a price differential for a new lot from its score, varietal, process, altitude and recent comparable Best-of-Panama / Cropster Hub clears
- Customs-paperwork autofill — populate Prior Notice, commercial invoice, and certificate-of-origin fields from the sample + buyer destination, flagging country-specific phytosanitary requirements
- OCR/parse inbound buyer cupping sheets (PDF/photo) into structured scores against the right sample automatically

**World-class:** "World-class is a closed traceability and sales loop: a finished green lot is one click from sampled, labeled, shipped-with-papers, and published — to targeted buyers and to a branded self-serve portal with per-lot QR stories. Every physical gram is accounted for against real remaining inventory, so a scarce Geisha micro-lot is never double-sold. The sales lead opens a single pipeline board and sees every deal's stage, score, and differential live; approvals convert in one tap from offer-sample to PSS to contract with the SAS-NANS/SAPS/SAPDS approval term attached. Cupping scores flow in from Cropster/IKAWA and inbound buyer sheets, reconciled automatically. It matches what Cropster Hub + Origin do for the largest exporters, but tuned to one estate's high-value, low-volume reality where a single sample decision gates a five-figure-per-bag lot."


**Coffee-specific angle:** "For Janson, samples are the entire sales engine for the high-value tier. Geisha and competition micro-lots are tiny (often <30 bags) and priced by the cupping score a buyer assigns — at Best of Panama, Panama Geisha has cleared from $803/lb up to a 2025 record of $13,705/lb. At those numbers, the physical sample IS the product demo and a single misplaced or mistracked sample is a lost five-figure deal. The system must respect Janson's lot/lotCode traceability (already the spine of harvests→processing→green), the named micro-lots from specific plots/altitudes in the 1,350–1,700 masl band, the varietal+process matrix (washed/natural/honey/anaerobic Geisha, Pacamara, Caturra, Catuaí), the Best-of-Panama/SCAP auction channel, and the reserve-and-quetzal origin story that buyers will scan via QR. Direct-relationship and reserve-lot sales — not commodity volume — are where the value is, and that is exactly the muscle this capability builds."


### Supply Chain & Logistics

#### Seed-to-cup traceability & provenance — QR, optional blockchain/DLT
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Janson's owner / export manager (the family member who sells green + roasted lots and represents the farm's provenance at the Best of Panama auction). Accountable for the provenance story and the price premium. Supported by the wet/dry-mill operator who generates the chain-of-custody events at each stage, and consumed by the downstream green buyer / specialty roaster / end consumer who scans the result.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I close out a finished green lot (e.g. JC-552 Geisha Washed) for sale or for the Best of Panama auction, I want every event from the exact plot and pick date through fermentation, drying curve, milling and bagging stitched into one tamper-evident lot record, so I can prove 'this 1,500 masl Geisha was picked at 23 Brix on these trees' without assembling a PDF by hand the night before the sale.
- When a specialty roaster in Tokyo or a green buyer scans the QR on a 30 kg GrainPro/jute bag, I want them to see a live, verifiable origin story (plot, altitude, varietal, process, drying days, cupping score, the quetzal reserve), so I can justify a $40-$300/lb auction price and turn provenance into the premium itself.
- When an EU importer asks for EUDR deforestation-free compliance on a 2026 shipment, I want to hand over geolocated plot polygons, harvest dates and an immutable due-diligence statement tied to the lot, so I can keep European market access instead of being delisted.
- When a buyer suspects the roasted bag on their shelf isn't really our Geisha, I want each consumer unit to carry a unique, hard-to-clone serialized code that resolves to exactly one parent green lot, so I can detect counterfeits and defend the Janson name.

**Features:**
- **Lot Passport — append-only chain-of-custody event ledger** — Promotes the existing lots/harvests/processing_batches backbone into a canonical, timestamped, append-only event timeline (pick > pulp > ferment > dry > rest > mill > grade > bag > ship). One auditable record per lot replaces the manual night-before-auction PDF and becomes the single source of provenance truth.
- **Public provenance microsite + dynamic QR generator** — Auto-generates a beautiful per-lot QR (on the bag label / hangtag) that resolves to a liquid-glass public page showing plot map, altitude, varietal, process, drying curve, cupping notes and reserve story. Converts traceability into a marketing and price-premium asset buyers can scan at the shelf.
- **Lot split / merge / blend lineage graph** — Models the messy reality that one green lot fans out to many roasters and that some SKUs are blends. Tracks parent-child mass balance so a roasted bag always resolves to its true green parent(s) and quantities reconcile (no 'more sold than produced').
- **Serialized consumer-unit codes (GS1 Digital Link / serialized 2D barcode)** — Each retail bag gets a unique serialized code, not just a lot-level QR, enabling anti-counterfeit verification, first-scan / re-scan detection and a per-unit warranty of authenticity that protects a high-value Geisha brand.
- **EUDR / deforestation due-diligence pack** — Bundles geolocated plot polygons, harvest dates and a signed due-diligence statement into an exportable Geolocation file + DDS reference, keeping EU market access and pre-empting the 2025+ regulation that delists non-compliant origins.
- **Cupping & quality record attached to the lot** — Captures SCA cupping score, sensory descriptors and Q-grader identity against the lot, so the score that drives auction price is part of the verifiable provenance rather than a loose spreadsheet.
- **Tamper-evidence / optional DLT anchoring** — Hashes each event and (optionally) anchors a daily Merkle root to a public ledger so a buyer or auditor can prove the record wasn't back-edited after the sale — credibility without running a private blockchain.
- **Export shipment & document binder** — Links lots to the outbound shipment (container, bags, ICO marks, phytosanitary cert, bill of lading, certificate of origin), closing the seed-to-cup loop the existing 'shipment' activity kind already anticipates.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** GS1 standards: GTIN/SSCC + GS1 Digital Link (2D barcode that is both scannable and a resolvable URL) for serialized consumer units, Cropster (Origin + Cup + Roast) — the specialty-coffee industry standard; import/export lot, drying and cupping data via its API/CSV so Janson plugs into roasters' existing workflows, EUDR / EU TRACES-NT Information System — geolocation file + Due Diligence Statement reference for deforestation-free compliance, Satellite NDVI / deforestation baseline providers (Sentinel-2 via Sentinel Hub, Planet, or Global Forest Watch) to evidence plot polygons predate the 2020 EUDR cutoff, ICO (International Coffee Organization) ICE-style export marks + Panama MIDA / phytosanitary and certificate-of-origin documents, Best of Panama / SCAP auction platform export format (lot sheets, green-buyer lot catalog) and Q-grader / SCA cupping protocol, Certification bodies & registries: Rainforest Alliance, Fair Trade, organic, plus the farm's own quetzal/200-ha private-reserve conservation claim, QR + serialized 2D label printing (Zebra / Brother thermal printers via ZPL, or a print-and-apply service) for bag labels and hangtags, Optional DLT anchoring via a low/zero-cost public ledger (e.g. OpenTimestamps on Bitcoin, or Polygon) for tamper-evident hashes — anchoring only, no per-record gas, Logistics / freight + cold-chain visibility (e.g. Flexport or a forwarder API) and bill-of-lading / container tracking for the export leg, Mapping/geospatial: PostGIS + Mapbox/Leaflet for plot polygons on the public provenance page

**Data model additions:** lot_events — append-only chain-of-custody ledger (id, lot_code FK, event_type, occurred_at, actor worker_id, location, payload jsonb, prev_hash, event_hash) — the spine of traceability, lot_lineage — parent/child edges for split/merge/blend with mass-balance (parent_lot_code, child_lot_code, kg_transferred, relation), lots (extend existing) — add status, parent_plot polygons reference, current_owner, gtin, public_slug, qr_token, consumer_units — serialized retail-unit records (serial, lot_code FK, gtin/sscc, first_scan_at, scan_count, weight_g), cuppings — SCA score, descriptors jsonb, q_grader, sample_date, lot_code FK, certifications — body, scheme, cert_id, valid_from/to, lot_or_farm scope, document_url, shipments — container_no, ico_marks, incoterm, buyer, destination_country, bill_of_lading, phyto_cert_url; line items link bags/lots, plot_geometry — PostGIS polygon + EUDR pre-2020 baseline evidence reference, joined to existing plots, due_diligence_statements — eudr_dds_ref, geolocation_file_url, generated_at, lot/shipment scope, anchor_proofs — daily Merkle root, ledger, tx/timestamp, covered_event_range (for optional DLT tamper-evidence), scan_events — public-page/QR scan log (lot_code or serial, scanned_at, geo, user_agent) for engagement + counterfeit analytics

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-draft the consumer-facing provenance narrative per lot (plot, altitude, process, drying days, cupping notes, reserve story) from structured events, in the farm's voice and multiple buyer languages (EN/ES/JA) — one-click marketing copy from the ledger.
- Anomaly / fraud detection on scan_events: cluster of scans from one geography far from the buyer, or re-scans of an already-activated serial, flags possible counterfeiting of a high-value Geisha.
- Mass-balance integrity check: an LLM/rules agent that watches lineage and flags 'more kg sold than produced' or impossible split chains before a lot ships.
- EUDR risk pre-screen: run the plot polygon against satellite deforestation layers and surface a green/amber/red readiness signal with the evidence, so the owner isn't surprised at customs.
- Predict the achievable price band for a lot from its cupping score + altitude + varietal + recent Best-of-Panama comparables, helping the owner set auction reserves.
- Drying-curve QA: learn the normal moisture trajectory per process/patio and alert when a batch deviates (over-fermented / case-hardened risk) before it becomes a defect baked into the lot.

**World-class:** World-class is a buyer scanning a Janson bag and getting, in under a second, a verifiable, tamper-evident, beautifully rendered story: the exact GPS-mapped plot at 1,550 masl, the trees' establishment year, the pick date and Brix, the fermentation and full drying curve, the Q-grader's 90+ cupping score, the EUDR-compliant deforestation-free attestation, the certifications, and the quetzal-reserve conservation claim — every fact anchored to an append-only ledger they can independently verify (and, for the skeptical, a public-ledger timestamp proving nothing was edited after the sale). Internally it is zero double-entry: the mill operator's stage updates ARE the provenance record; the auction lot sheet, the EUDR DDS, the customs binder and the public page all generate from the same events. Provenance stops being a compliance chore and becomes the product's premium — the reason a roaster pays $300/lb and re-orders.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson is almost uniquely positioned to monetize this: it is a single-origin Panama estate growing Geisha — the variety whose entire market is built on provenance and where Best of Panama auction lots fetch record per-pound prices precisely because buyers trust the origin story. The existing schema already carries lot codes (JC-541, JC-564...) from harvest through every processing stage and even reserves a 'shipment' activity kind — the seed-to-cup spine is half-built; this pillar finishes it downstream of green. Two assets compound the value: (1) the 200-hectare private nature reserve / quetzal habitat is a ready-made, genuine deforestation-free + biodiversity story that is gold for both EUDR and premium marketing; (2) running the full chain in-house (grow > wet/dry mill > sell green and roasted) means Janson controls every custody event, so unlike a co-op buying cherry from many smallholders, its provenance can be complete and uncontested end to end.


#### Input inventory — fertilizer, fuel, agrochemicals, supplies
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Farm Operations Manager / Mayordomo at Janson Coffee — the owner's right hand who buys and controls inputs. Backed by two secondary users: the bodeguero (store-keeper) who physically issues stock from the locked finca bodega in Volcán each morning, and the agronomist who specs which products go on which plots. On a Panama finca this person buys the fertilizer, agrochemicals, and fuel, holds the bodega keys, and signs off on every crew drawdown.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I'm planning the rainy-season fertilization round across 40+ plots at 1,350–1,700 masl, I want to see exactly how much urea, dolomitic lime, and foliar I have on hand versus what the agronomist's per-plot program calls for, so I can place one consolidated purchase order before prices spike and never strand a crew mid-application.
- When a crew leader draws agrochemical or diesel from the bodega at 6am, I want to record the issue against the specific plot/task and have stock decrement in real time, so I can prove cost-per-hectare per lot and catch shrinkage or theft before the season's books close.
- When I'm about to apply a fungicide for roya (coffee leaf rust) or a restricted agrochemical, I want the system to block me if the product is expired, past its re-entry/pre-harvest interval, or not permitted under our Rainforest Alliance / organic-transition rules, so I never contaminate a Geisha lot or fail a certification audit.
- When diesel for the wet mill, pulpers, mechanical dryers, and the 4x4s is running low during peak harvest, I want a reorder alert showing current tank level and the nearest Volcán supplier's price, so the mill never stalls and I'm not paying a premium for an emergency top-up.
- When the auditor or my accountant asks at year-end where $38k of agrochemicals went, I want a full issue-and-receipt trail per product, plot, and worker, so I can reconcile spend to QuickBooks and to actual hectares treated without rebuilding it from paper vales.

**Features:**
- **Input catalog & SKU master** — One canonical record per product (fertilizer, agrochemical, fuel, supply) carrying active ingredient, concentration, unit-of-measure, pack size, hazard class, and certification status — ends the 'urea in five spreadsheets' problem and gives every other module one truth to reference.
- **Multi-location stock ledger (bodega / mill / fuel tanks)** — Real-time on-hand by storage location with an append-only movement log (receipt, issue, transfer, adjustment, waste). Manager sees true coverage; shrinkage shows up as an unexplained adjustment instead of disappearing.
- **Mobile issue / drawdown (vale digital)** — Bodeguero or crew leader logs an issue against a plot + task + worker from a phone, decrementing stock instantly even offline — replaces the paper vale and makes cost-per-hectare per lot computable.
- **Reorder points & consolidated purchase orders** — Per-SKU min/reorder levels with lead-time-aware alerts that roll into a single PO per supplier — prevents both stockouts mid-application and emergency premium buys in a remote highland town.
- **Agrochemical compliance & application interval guard** — Hard block on issuing/applying a product that is expired, past re-entry (REI) or pre-harvest interval (PHI), or prohibited under the lot's certification regime — protects Geisha lots and the certification audit at the data layer, not the honor system.
- **Fuel & tank management** — Diesel/gasoline tank levels with per-asset consumption (mill, dryers, vehicles, generators) and burn-rate forecasting — keeps peak-harvest processing running and exposes fuel theft.
- **Input cost roll-up to lot & plot P&L** — Every issue carries a cost basis (FIFO/weighted-average) that rolls into cost-per-hectare and cost-per-kg-green by lot — turns inventory into the input side of true unit economics for each Geisha micro-lot.
- **Batch/lot expiry & MSDS/SDS vault** — Tracks supplier batch numbers, expiry dates, and attached safety data sheets per product — drives the expiry guard and satisfies GAP/Rainforest Alliance documentation requirements.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** QuickBooks Online API (purchase orders, bills, COGS sync to the farm's books), Cropster (input/application records tied to lots for traceability and cupping context), agroPanama / local Volcán agro-supplier price lists and the agrochemical importer catalogs (e.g. Melo, Agrocentro) for SKU + pricing reference, Panama AUPSA / MIDA pesticide registry for legal-product and restricted-use validation, Rainforest Alliance 2020 Sustainable Agriculture Standard prohibited-pesticide list (and EU/US MRL references) for the compliance guard, GS1 barcodes + mobile camera scanning (and optional Zebra/Honeywell handheld scanners) for receipt and issue, IoT tank-level sensors (e.g. Banner/Dwyer ultrasonic, or a low-cost ESP32 + ultrasonic rig) for diesel/water tank telemetry, WhatsApp Business API + Twilio for reorder and low-stock alerts to the manager's phone (the de-facto channel in rural Panama), Open-Meteo / weather API to time fertilization and spray windows against rainfall, Stripe/ACH or local bank rails are out of scope here; PO approval hands off to the procurement/payments pillar

**Data model additions:** input_products (SKU master: name, type fertilizer|agrochemical|fuel|supply, active_ingredient, concentration, uom, pack_size, hazard_class, rei_hours, phi_days, restricted bool, cert_status), storage_locations (bodega, mill store, fuel tank A/B, vehicle tanks — with capacity and type), input_stock (current on-hand per product per location, computed from movements), input_movements (append-only ledger: receipt|issue|transfer|adjustment|waste, qty, unit_cost, location, plot_id, task_id, worker_id, at), input_batches (supplier batch no, expiry_date, received_qty, sds_url, product_id), suppliers (name, contact, lead_time_days, price_list ref), purchase_orders + purchase_order_lines (status draft|sent|received, supplier_id, expected_date), reorder_rules (product_id, location_id, min_level, reorder_level, reorder_qty), input_applications (links an issue to a plot/lot + dose-per-ha for agronomy + compliance record)
