**AI / automation:**
- Demand forecasting that learns from historical issues + the agronomist's per-plot program + weather + crop stage to predict next month's fertilizer/fuel needs and pre-stage the PO.
- Anomaly detection on the movement ledger — flags an issue that is out of pattern (a plot drawing 3x its normal dose, fuel burn spiking with no harvest) as possible theft, mis-entry, or a leak.
- Photo-to-receipt: snap the supplier invoice/delivery note, an LLM extracts line items and reconciles them against the open PO and the SKU catalog.
- Natural-language compliance copilot: 'Can I spray this on the JC-602 Geisha lot today?' returns a yes/no with REI/PHI, expiry, and certification reasoning cited from the rules vault.
- Optimal-buy timing: model local price seasonality and lead times to recommend when to lock in a bulk urea/lime purchase before the rainy-season spike.

**World-class:** A single perpetual-inventory system of record where every gram of fertilizer, liter of diesel, and bottle of fungicide is tracked from PO to receipt to the exact plot/lot/worker it was issued against — never a spreadsheet, never a paper vale. On-hand is always real-time and reconcilable to the penny against QuickBooks. Reorders are predicted, not reacted to: the system stages a consolidated PO before anyone notices a low bin. Compliance is enforced at the data layer (expiry, REI/PHI, certification prohibitions block the issue), so an audit is a one-click export. Inputs flow straight into per-lot cost-per-kg-green economics, closing the loop from a sack of urea to the margin on a Geisha micro-lot. Works offline-first on a phone in a highland bodega with spotty signal, and alerts reach the manager on WhatsApp.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson's edge is high-value, separable lots — Geisha and Pacamara micro-lots that fetch multiples of commodity prices — so input tracking isn't bookkeeping, it's provenance and margin. Tying every agrochemical/fertilizer issue to a specific lot lets Janson (a) prove the cost-per-kg-green of each Geisha lot to justify its auction/reserve pricing, (b) document inputs for Rainforest Alliance and any organic-transition or single-origin export claim, and (c) protect the 200-hectare quetzal reserve by hard-blocking prohibited agrochemicals near buffer zones. The fuel dimension is acute during peak harvest: the wet mill, mechanical dryers, and 4x4s on steep 1,350–1,700 masl terrain all run on diesel that must be trucked up to Volcán — a stockout stalls processing of perishable cherry and degrades cup quality. The compliance guard directly protects against roya-treatment mistakes contaminating premium lots.


#### Procurement & supplier management
*Priority:* **table-stakes**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the Farm Operations Manager / Owner-buyer (at Janson, this is Janette Janson, agronomist, who decides what inputs to buy and when, and Miguel Janson, who signs off on spend). Secondary users: the Agronomist (raises requisitions for fertilizer/agrochemical programs tied to plot needs), the Mill Operator / Production lead (orders processing consumables and export packaging — GrainPro liners, jute bags, hessian, parchment storage), the Storekeeper/Bodeguero (receives goods, logs deliveries against POs, manages the input bodega), and the Bookkeeper (matches supplier invoices to receipts for cost-of-production accounting).

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I plan next month's fertilizer and foliar-spray program for a specific block (e.g. the Geisha at Tizingal Alto), I want to turn that agronomic plan into a requisition and a purchase order to the right Chiriquí supplier at a known price, so I can secure stock before the application window and never have a crew show up to spray with an empty bodega.
- When the storekeeper receives a delivery of urea, lime, or GrainPro liners, I want to log exactly what arrived against the open PO (quantity, lot/batch number, expiry, condition), so I have a true on-hand inventory and can three-way-match the supplier invoice before we pay a centavo.
- When I'm about to commit to a supplier for a season's worth of agrochemicals or export packaging, I want to compare quotes, past on-time-delivery, price history, and certification/MRL compliance across vendors, so I pick the supplier that protects both my margin and my organic/specialty certifications.
- When I close out a processing lot for export (e.g. JC-602 Geisha Anaerobic), I want every input it consumed — fertilizer per hectare, fungicide, fermentation additives, drying labor, GrainPro+jute packaging — rolled into a true landed cost per kilo of green, so I can price the lot at auction/contract and know my real margin before I ship.

**Features:**
- **Supplier master & scorecard** — One canonical vendor record per supplier (Chiriquí agrochemical houses in David, GrainPro/jute importers, nursery/seedling suppliers, fuel, lab/cupping services, labor contractors) with contacts, payment terms, RUC/tax ID, certifications held, and a live scorecard (on-time-delivery %, price variance vs quote, defect/return rate, lead-time). Turns 'who do we usually call' tribal knowledge into a ranked, auditable shortlist.
- **Catalog & input master with price history** — Canonical input items (urea, lime, specific fungicides by active ingredient + EPA/Panama AUPSA registration, foliar nutrients, fermentation cultures, GrainPro SuperGrainbag liners, jute/hessian bags, drying-bed mesh, fuel) each with unit-of-measure, current price per supplier, and full price-history sparkline — so spend planning uses real numbers, not guesses.
- **Requisition → PO → Goods Receipt → 3-way match workflow** — Agronomist/mill raises a requisition (optionally pre-filled from a task or a per-hectare application plan), it converts to a PO with approval thresholds, the storekeeper books a goods-receipt (partial deliveries supported, with batch/expiry capture), and the bookkeeper three-way-matches PO↔receipt↔invoice before payment — eliminating overpayment and ghost deliveries.
- **Input inventory & bodega stock control** — Real-time on-hand by item and storage location, with min/reorder points, expiry tracking (critical for agrochemicals and biological cultures), and consumption posted automatically when a Task logs an application or a processing batch consumes packaging — so the bodega never silently runs dry mid-application or holds expired, unusable chemical.
- **Input-to-lot cost allocation** — Each input issue is allocated to a plot, task, or processing batch/lot, so consumed inputs roll up into a true cost-of-production per hectare and a landed cost-per-kg-green per lot — the number that lets Janson price a Geisha lot at the Best of Panama auction with confidence instead of a gut markup.
- **Compliance & MRL / certification guard** — Every agrochemical purchase is checked against the farm's certification scheme (organic/Rainforest Alliance/specialty buyer spec) and EU/US maximum-residue-limit (MRL) rules, blocking or flagging a banned active ingredient before it's even ordered — protecting export eligibility and the premium that pays the farm's bills.
- **Contract & seasonal blanket orders** — Lock seasonal volumes and prices with key suppliers (e.g. a pre-harvest blanket PO for fertilizer or a season's GrainPro liners) and release call-offs against them, hedging price spikes ahead of the May–February Panama harvest cycle.
- **Spend dashboard & budget vs actual** — Input spend by category, plot, supplier, and month against a season budget, with variance alerts — gives the owner a single screen answering 'where is the money going and are we on plan' that today lives in a notebook.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Cropster API (sync green-coffee purchasing, lot inventory and purchase-volume planning so procurement of packaging/processing inputs reconciles against the same lots the roast/quality side uses; Cropster API documented to sync with NetSuite/Odoo/SAP), Panama AUPSA / MIDA (Ministerio de Desarrollo Agropecuario) pesticide registration data — validate that an agrochemical's active ingredient is legally registered/permitted in Panama before purchase, EU MRL database (EU Pesticides Database) and US EPA tolerances — screen purchased actives against destination-market maximum residue limits to protect export contracts, Rainforest Alliance / SCA / organic certifier input lists (allowed/prohibited substance lists) for compliance guards, GrainPro / SuperGrainbag and jute-bag importers/distributors (Panarese Group, regional GrainPro distributors) as catalog suppliers for export packaging, Local Chiriquí agro-input suppliers in David & Tierras Altas (e.g. Abonos del Pacífico, Servicios Agropecuarios El Llano, Wb Agro) onboarded as vendor records — surfaced via Dun & Bradstreet business directory enrichment, QuickBooks Online or Xero (or local Panama accounting) for AP / invoice matching and posting input costs to cost-of-goods, Yappy / ACH-Banco General payment rail (Panama's dominant local bank-to-bank rail) plus Wise/SWIFT for paying imported-input suppliers, Email/WhatsApp Business API for sending POs to suppliers and receiving order confirmations (WhatsApp is the de-facto B2B channel with Panama suppliers), Currency/FX feed (e.g. exchangerate API) for imported inputs priced in USD vs other currencies — Panama uses USD (balboa-pegged) so mostly a check, but relevant for non-US imports, OCR / document AI (e.g. AWS Textract free tier or a local LLM pass) to parse supplier invoices and delivery notes into structured goods-receipt lines

**Data model additions:** suppliers (id, name, ruc_tax_id, category[agrochemical|fertilizer|packaging|nursery|fuel|lab|labor_contractor|equipment], contacts jsonb, payment_terms, lead_time_days, certifications text[], scorecard fields: on_time_pct, price_variance_pct, defect_rate, active), input_items (id, name, category, active_ingredient, unit_of_measure, aupsa_reg_no, mrl_flag, certification_status, reorder_point, current_on_hand) — the input/SKU master, supplier_prices (supplier_id, input_item_id, unit_price_usd, valid_from, valid_to) — price history per supplier, purchase_requisitions (id, requested_by worker_id, plot_id?, task_id?, processing_batch_id?, status[draft|approved|rejected], needed_by date), requisition_lines (requisition_id, input_item_id, qty, unit_of_measure), purchase_orders (id, supplier_id, requisition_id?, status[open|partial|received|closed|cancelled], order_date, expected_date, total_usd, contract_id?), po_lines (po_id, input_item_id, qty_ordered, unit_price_usd, qty_received), goods_receipts (id, po_id, received_date, received_by worker_id, condition, batch_no, expiry_date), goods_receipt_lines (goods_receipt_id, po_line_id, qty_received), supplier_invoices (id, supplier_id, po_id, invoice_no, amount_usd, status[unmatched|matched|paid], match_exceptions jsonb), input_inventory_txns (id, input_item_id, qty_delta, location, txn_type[receipt|issue|adjustment|expiry_writeoff], plot_id?|task_id?|processing_batch_id? for allocation, at) — the consumption/allocation ledger that ties into existing tasks, plots and processing_batches, supplier_contracts (id, supplier_id, season, committed_qty, locked_price_usd, input_item_id) — blanket/seasonal orders, input_cost_allocations (lot_code, input_item_id, allocated_usd) — view/rollup linking input spend to existing lots for landed-cost-per-kg-green

**AI / automation:**
- Demand forecasting that reads the agronomic calendar + plot areas + historical consumption to auto-draft requisitions ('Tizingal Alto Geisha needs its pre-flowering fertilizer in 3 weeks — order 1.2t urea now, lead time 6 days') and suggest reorder timing before stockouts
- Invoice/delivery-note OCR + auto three-way-match: parse a scanned supplier invoice or WhatsApp PDF into structured lines, match to the open PO and goods receipt, and surface only the exceptions (price drift, short-ship) for human review
- Supplier recommendation engine: rank vendors for a given input by blended score (price history, on-time-delivery, defect rate, compliance, lead time) and flag a cheaper-but-riskier alternative with the trade-off explained
- Price-anomaly and margin-leak alerts: detect when a quoted unit price drifts above the rolling median or a season budget category is trending over, and notify the owner before the PO is approved
- Compliance copilot: before any agrochemical PO, an LLM cross-checks the active ingredient against the farm's certifications and the destination-market MRL list and blocks/flags non-compliant purchases in plain Spanish
- Auto cost-of-production narration: at lot close, generate a plain-language landed-cost breakdown ('this Geisha lot cost $X/kg green; 38% packaging, 22% fertilizer, 18% picking labor') to inform auction/contract pricing

**World-class:** World-class procurement here is invisible plumbing that makes input spend a first-class, traceable cost driver rather than a shoebox of receipts. Every gram of fertilizer or fungicide is requisitioned from an agronomic plan, ordered against a price-checked PO, received with batch and expiry capture, automatically consumed off inventory when the field task or processing batch records it, and allocated to a plot and a lot — so the system can state the true landed cost per kilo of green for any lot on demand, broken down by input category. Suppliers are managed on a live scorecard, not relationships and memory; agrochemical purchases are guarded against the farm's certifications and destination-market MRLs before money moves; seasonal blanket contracts hedge price spikes; and AI drafts the next requisition before anyone notices the bodega is low. It closes the loop with the rest of the unified system: procurement feeds Tasks (the application that consumes the input), Processing (packaging + mill consumables per batch), Plots (per-hectare input cost), and ultimately the revenue/lot economics — making cost-of-production per lot a button, not a quarterly accounting exercise.

**Coffee-specific angle:** For a specialty Panama farm whose entire economic model rests on premium Geisha and reserve lots, two procurement angles are existential rather than nice-to-have. First, certification & MRL compliance: a single banned or wrong-MRL active ingredient sprayed on a Geisha block can disqualify the lot from organic/Rainforest Alliance status and from EU/US buyers — wiping out the very premium that funds the farm — so the compliance guard on agrochemical purchases directly protects revenue. Second, export packaging is a coffee-specific procurement category most generic systems miss entirely: GrainPro/SuperGrainbag liners inside jute/hessian bags are what keep green-coffee moisture stable in transit (the existing processing model already tracks moisture_pct down to ~10.8% at green), and the right packaging spend per lot is part of landed cost. Janson also runs the full chain on a 200-hectare reserve — meaning nursery/shade-tree seedling procurement (for the quetzal-habitat shade canopy) and biological/fermentation inputs (for the Anaerobic and Natural processes already in the data) are real, recurring purchase categories. Tying input cost to specific lots (JC-541…JC-611) lets Janson price each micro-lot at Best of Panama / private-auction with a defensible cost floor instead of guesswork.


#### Logistics & export freight — containers, Incoterms, booking
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Export & Logistics Coordinator at Janson Coffee (in practice the owner-operator / family member wearing the export hat). Secondary users: the green-coffee buyer/importer abroad and the freight forwarder. This person owns everything after a green-coffee lot is sealed in GrainPro/jute: booking the container, choosing Incoterms, generating export paperwork, tracking the vessel, and protecting the cold/dry chain on $40–200/lb Geisha until it lands in the buyer's warehouse.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a buyer confirms a purchase of a specific Geisha lot, I want to instantly turn that sale into a bookable shipment with the right Incoterms (FOB Balboa vs CIF/DAP buyer's port) and a documented packing list of exactly which named lots and bag counts are loaded, so I can quote a landed price the buyer trusts and never ship the wrong lot.
- When export season hits and ocean rates and reefer space tighten, I want one screen that books space with a forwarder/NVOCC, locks the sailing from Panama (Balboa/Manzanillo) and warns me about cut-off and rollover risk, so my auction-winning lot makes the contracted delivery window instead of sitting on a hot dock for three weeks.
- When a container is in transit, I want live vessel/container tracking plus reefer temperature and humidity telemetry tied to the specific lot codes inside it, so I can prove the cold/dry chain held and intervene (or file a claim) before a six-figure Geisha shipment arrives stale or moldy.
- When a shipment is ready to leave Panama, I want every export document — commercial invoice, packing list, phytosanitary certificate, certificate of origin (Form A / EUR.1), ICO certificate of origin, and bill of lading — generated from the same lot/sale data with no re-keying, so customs clears it cleanly and I get paid without a documentary discrepancy on the letter of credit.

**Features:**
- **Sales Order to Shipment pipeline** — Converts a confirmed green-coffee sale into a shipment record (which lots, bag count/type, net + gross weight, contract price, Incoterm, currency), linking buyer, lots, and price into one auditable object — eliminates wrong-lot/wrong-weight errors on high-value coffee.
- **Incoterms & landed-cost calculator** — Pick the Incoterm (EXW, FOB Balboa, CFR, CIF, DAP, DDP) and the system auto-builds the cost waterfall (inland trucking Volcán->port, terminal handling, ocean freight, insurance, duties) so the owner quotes a defensible landed price and knows the exact margin per lot.
- **Container & booking manager** — Books FCL/LCL or reefer space against a sailing, tracks cut-off dates, container/seal numbers, and stuffing — surfaces rollover and cut-off risk so the contracted delivery window is actually met.
- **Export document generator** — One click produces the commercial invoice, packing list, certificate of origin (ICO + Form A/EUR.1), phytosanitary application, and shipping instructions pre-filled from lot + sale data — kills re-keying and documentary-discrepancy delays on L/C payments.
- **Live shipment & cold-chain tracker** — Shows vessel position, ETA, milestones (gate-in, loaded, departed, arrived, delivered) and, for reefer lots, the temperature/humidity log tied to lot codes — proves chain integrity and triggers exception alerts.
- **Traceability handoff & QR passport** — Carries the existing lot_code lineage (plot -> harvest -> process -> green) into the export bag-level packing list and a buyer/consumer-facing QR 'lot passport' — turns Janson's farm-to-bag story into a sales asset and satisfies EUDR due-diligence.
- **Buyer & contract ledger** — Tracks each buyer, their contracts, sampling status, payment terms (L/C, T/T, deposit + balance), and shipment history — so the family knows who bought which microlot and what is still owed.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Freight visibility & tracking: project44, FourKites, or Terminal49 / Vizion API for container milestone + vessel ETA tracking, Ocean carrier / NVOCC booking: Maersk Spot + Maersk Booking API, CMA CGM, MSC eBusiness, or aggregator Flexport API (Panama sailings via Balboa & Manzanillo), Specialty-coffee platform: Cropster (Cropster Origin) and Sucafina/algrano green-trading marketplaces for offer lists, sample tracking, and lot data exchange, Reefer cold-chain telemetry: Controlant, Sensitech, Tive, or Globe Tracker IoT loggers for in-container temperature/humidity, Customs & export docs: Panama's SIGA / Ventanilla Unica de Comercio Exterior (VUCE) and Aduanas; phytosanitary via MIDA / Panama AUPSA, Certifications & origin: International Coffee Organization (ICO) certificate of origin, SCA scoring/Q-grade, plus Fair Trade / Rainforest Alliance / organic chain-of-custody and EU EUDR geolocation due-diligence, Trade finance & payment rails: letter-of-credit handling, Wise/Mercury for USD T/T settlement (Panama is USD-denominated), and Stripe/PayPal for sample and small-lot sales, Standards/data: Incoterms 2020 rules, GS1/SSCC pallet labels, HS code 0901 classification, and EDI/EPCIS for lot-level event sharing

**Data model additions:** sales_orders (id, buyer_id, contract_ref, incoterm, currency, price_per_lb_usd, total_amount_usd, payment_terms, status), shipments (id, sales_order_id, forwarder, vessel_name, voyage, container_no, seal_no, booking_ref, bill_of_lading_no, origin_port, dest_port, etd, eta, status, is_reefer), shipment_lots (shipment_id, lot_code -> lots.code, bag_count, bag_type, net_kg, gross_kg) — line-level join carrying existing lot lineage into the container, buyers (id, name, country, importer_of_record, contact, default_incoterm, payment_terms), export_documents (id, shipment_id, doc_type[commercial_invoice|packing_list|phyto|cert_origin|ico|bill_of_lading|eur1], doc_no, issued_date, file_ref, status), incoterm enum + ports reference, plus landed_cost_lines (shipment_id, cost_type, amount_usd, paid_by) for the Incoterms cost waterfall, coldchain_readings (shipment_id, recorded_at, temp_c, humidity_pct, source_device) and shipment_milestones (shipment_id, event, location, occurred_at) for live tracking, Extends the existing 'shipment' activity_kind (already in the enum) so export events flow into the unified activity feed

**AI / automation:**
- Sailing optimizer: rank available sailings out of Balboa/Manzanillo by landed cost, transit time, and historical rollover risk for the contracted delivery window
- Document auto-fill + discrepancy checker: an LLM drafts the commercial invoice/packing list from lot+sale data and pre-validates against the letter-of-credit terms to flag discrepancies before submission
- Cold-chain anomaly detection: watch the reefer temp/humidity stream, predict spoilage risk for the specific varietal/process, and auto-draft a carrier claim with the evidence attached
- Landed-cost + FX forecasting: estimate ocean-rate and FX movement to advise FOB vs CIF/DDP and the best moment to book
- Buyer-facing shipment copilot: answer 'where is my coffee / will it hold its score' from the live tracking + telemetry, in the buyer's language
- HS-code & cert classifier: auto-assign HS 0901 subheadings and flag which certs (EUDR geolocation, phyto, ICO, Form A/EUR.1) each destination requires

**World-class:** A buyer wins a Janson Geisha microlot at auction; the coordinator clicks 'Create shipment' and the system already knows the lot's full lineage from the existing plot->harvest->process chain. It proposes the cheapest compliant sailing out of Balboa, returns live ocean rates and a complete Incoterms landed-cost breakdown, books reefer space against the carrier API, and auto-generates every export document pre-validated against the L/C terms. From booking to delivery, the buyer and the family watch the same live map with container milestones and a continuous reefer temp/humidity graph bound to the lot codes; an anomaly (door open, temp excursion, vessel rollover) pings both parties instantly with a pre-drafted claim. The bag the roaster opens has a QR passport linking back to the exact tree block, picker, ferment, and the unbroken cold chain. Zero re-keying, zero wrong-lot shipments, every dollar of landed cost and margin attributed per lot — world-class is the farm's $200/lb coffee arriving exactly as scored, with documentary proof, and getting paid on time.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson's economics are dominated by ultra-high-value lots: Geisha and Pacamara that sell as named microlots and can fetch $40-200+/lb, where a single 24-bag container can be worth six figures and the farm sells both green and roasted directly to international buyers. At those values the export-freight layer is not commodity logistics — it is margin and reputation protection. The cold/dry chain matters acutely: green specialty coffee degrades with moisture and heat, so reefer telemetry on a Geisha shipment is the difference between a coffee that arrives at its cupped score and one that loses points (and value). Panama is USD-denominated and ships through Balboa (Pacific) and Manzanillo/Colon (Atlantic), so port choice and Incoterm directly shape landed cost to US vs EU vs Asia buyers. The capability also lets Janson extend its existing lot_code traceability (plot -> harvest -> process -> green) all the way to a bag-level QR passport — turning the farm's 200-hectare quetzal reserve and altitude story into a documented, EUDR-ready selling point that commodity exporters cannot match, and replacing today's broker-dependent, paper-based export process with direct-trade control.


#### Export documentation & customs — ICO cert, phytosanitary, certificate of origin
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Export & Compliance Coordinator (at Janson, this is the owner/general manager wearing the export hat — the person who books containers with the freight forwarder, gets the phytosanitary cert from MIDA, files the ICO certificate of origin, and answers the buyer's importing broker). Secondary users: the dry-mill/warehouse lead who weighs and seals the export lot, and the green buyer's import broker / customs agent in the destination country (Tokyo, Oslo, NYC) who consumes Janson's documents.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a green-coffee lot is sold and a sailing is booked, I want every required export document (commercial invoice, packing list, ICO certificate of origin, MIDA/DECA phytosanitary certificate, certificate of origin) auto-assembled from the lot's existing traceability data, so I can clear Panamanian customs and hand the buyer's broker a complete, error-free dossier without re-keying weights and lot codes five times.
- When my EU buyer (or any roaster importing into the EU) asks for EUDR compliance, I want to hand over plot-level GPS polygons, the deforestation-free declaration, and a TRACES Due Diligence Statement reference tied to this exact lot, so the shipment isn't held at a European port and I stay on the buyer's approved-supplier list.
- When a shipment is in transit or a document is about to expire (phytosanitary certs are time-boxed, the ICO code dossier must be retained 4 years), I want a single status board with deadline alerts and a complete audit trail, so a missing stamp or a lapsed cert never strands a $40/lb Geisha container at port.
- When a buyer or auditor later questions a lot, I want to pull the full chain — plot to picker to ferment tank to drying bed to milled green weight to the container seal number and bill of lading — in one click, so I can prove provenance and protect Janson's Best-of-Panama reputation.

**Features:**
- **Shipment & Export Lot builder** — Compose an export lot from one or more green ProcessingBatches (by lotCode), capture buyer/consignee, Incoterm, container/seal number, net+gross weight in bags, and sailing details — the single object every document and every status alert hangs off.
- **Document-pack auto-generator** — One click renders the full dossier — commercial invoice, packing list, ICO certificate of origin (ICC-102-9 layout), certificate of origin, and the data-request packet for MIDA/DECA's phytosanitary cert — pre-filled from lot traceability, killing the re-keying errors (mismatched bill/container numbers) that get coffee held at port.
- **EUDR due-diligence module** — Bundles each plot's GPS polygon (GeoJSON), the deforestation-free-since-2020 declaration, and risk assessment into a TRACES-ready Due Diligence Statement, stores the returned DDS reference number against the lot, and surfaces it to the EU buyer — turning a deal-breaker into a one-click export and keeping Janson on approved-supplier lists.
- **Compliance status board + deadline alerts** — A per-shipment checklist (ICO cert issued? phyto cert valid? FDA Prior Notice filed? ISF 48h cutoff? bill of lading received?) with countdown alerts on expiring/missing docs, so a lapsed cert or a missed filing window never strands a container.
- **Traceability passport (plot-to-port)** — A shareable, immutable provenance record linking plot → picker → ferment/dry batch → milled green weight → container seal → BoL; answers buyer/auditor provenance questions instantly and becomes a marketing asset (QR on the bag) for a single-origin Geisha story.
- **Document vault with retention clock** — Versioned storage of every issued cert and signed PDF with the ICO-mandated 4-year retention timer and the 'for internal use only' copy flagged, so audits and re-prints never depend on someone's email inbox.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** ICO (International Coffee Organization) — exporter ICO code + ICC-102-9 Certificate of Origin form/SOIC e-issuance rules, MIDA / DECA (Dirección Ejecutiva de Cuarentena Agropecuaria) — Panama phytosanitary (fitosanitario) certificate issuance, APA / AUPSA SIT (Sistema Integrado de Trámites) — Panama single-window for export sanitary/phytosanitary procedures, ePhyto Hub (IPPC) — electronic phytosanitary certificate exchange standard, EU TRACES NT — Due Diligence Statement submission, returns reusable DDS reference number (EUDR), EUDR geolocation standard — plot-level GeoJSON polygons + deforestation-free declaration, US FDA Prior Notice System Interface (PNSI) — pre-arrival food import filing, US CBP Importer Security Filing (ISF 10+2) — vessel cargo, 48h-before-lading cutoff, Freight forwarder / NVOCC booking APIs (e.g. Flexport, Maersk) — container booking, bill of lading, sailing schedule, Cropster / cup-score & green-inventory platforms — pull green-lot data and grading the buyer expects, ICC eUCP / certificate-of-origin chambers and DUCA-form customs declaration (Central America), PDF/UBL e-invoice generation (commercial invoice + packing list) and QR provenance label printing

**Data model additions:** Shipment (id, buyer/consignee, Incoterm, port of loading/discharge, vessel, sailing date, container no., seal no., status, freight forwarder ref, bill-of-lading no.), ExportLot (id, shipment_id, composed-from green batch lotCodes[], net/gross kg, bag count + bag weight, grade/cup score, FOB price/lb), ExportDocument (id, shipment_id, type ['commercial_invoice'|'packing_list'|'ico_certificate_of_origin'|'phytosanitary'|'certificate_of_origin'|'fda_prior_notice'|'isf'|'bill_of_lading'|'eudr_dds'], status, issued_date, expiry_date, file_ref, version, issuing_authority), ComplianceRequirement (id, destination_country/market, doc_type, mandatory bool, lead_time_days) — drives the per-destination checklist, EUDRDeclaration (id, lot_id, plot_geojson_ref, deforestation_free_since, risk_level, traces_dds_reference, submitted_at), ICOCertificate (id, ico_exporter_code, certificate_serial, customs_cachet, certifying_officer, retain_until [4yr clock]), Buyer/Consignee (id, name, country, broker, eudr_operator bool, ico_buyer_code), DocumentAuditEvent (id, document_id, actor, action, at) — immutable provenance/audit trail

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-fill + cross-check: AI pre-populates the commercial invoice / ICO form from lot data and flags mismatches (container vs. BoL number, net vs. gross weight, lot code drift) before submission — the exact errors that get coffee held at port.
- Destination rule engine: given buyer country + market (EU/US/JP), AI assembles the correct required-document checklist and lead times, and warns when a market's rules change (e.g. EUDR enforcement date shifts).
- Document OCR + validation: ingest a scanned phytosanitary cert or signed BoL, extract fields, and reconcile against the shipment record; alert if a stamp/signature/cachet is missing.
- Deadline & expiry prediction: AI watches sailing dates against FDA Prior Notice / ISF 48h cutoffs and phyto-cert validity windows, and proactively nudges before a window closes.
- EUDR risk-assessment drafting: auto-generate the deforestation-free narrative + risk classification from plot GPS, satellite NDVI history, and establishment year for the TRACES DDS.
- Buyer-broker Q&A assistant: a grounded chatbot that answers the importing broker's provenance/spec questions from the lot's traceability passport, in the buyer's language.

**World-class:** "World-class is zero re-keying and zero stranded containers: the moment a green lot is sold, the system knows the destination market, generates a complete, validated document pack from data already captured upstream (plot GPS, picker, ferment, drying, milled weight, seal number), files the electronic submissions (FDA Prior Notice, ISF, TRACES DDS) via API, tracks every deadline, stores every signed PDF with its retention clock, and exposes a one-click provenance passport to the buyer's broker. The export coordinator's job shifts from frantic document chasing to reviewing and approving. EUDR readiness is a non-event because plot polygons and deforestation-free proof are already part of every lot. A buyer in Oslo scans the bag's QR and sees Tizingal Alto, 1,690 masl, the picker, the ferment profile, and the container it sailed in."

**Coffee-specific angle:** "This is the make-or-break link for Janson specifically: they sell high-value single-origin Geisha and Pacamara (Best-of-Panama tier, often $20-60+/lb green) to discerning importers in the EU, US, Japan and Scandinavia — exactly the markets with the strictest paperwork (EUDR for the EU, FDA Prior Notice + ISF for the US). A single missing ICO cachet or a lapsed MIDA phytosanitary cert can strand a container worth more than a month of farm payroll. Janson's existing lot-traceability backbone (lotCode JC-541 → plot Tizingal Alto → variety/altitude → ferment method → milled green weight) is already the precise provenance EUDR and premium buyers demand — so this module is less about new data capture and more about projecting that backbone into the exact regulatory forms each destination wants. It also turns compliance into brand: the 200-ha quetzal reserve and deforestation-free plots become a documented, EUDR-verified selling point, not just a story on jansoncoffee.com."


#### Storage conditions & controlled environment monitoring
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Mill and Warehouse Manager (the bodega/dry-mill lead at Janson), accountable for green coffee from the moment a batch leaves the drying beds (the existing green stage) until it ships. Secondary users: the QC/cupping lead, the owner/export manager, and the night/weekend caretaker who responds to alerts.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a Geisha lot finishes drying and rests as parchment in the bodega before milling, I want to keep its water activity locked in the 0.45-0.55 aw band and the room at 50-60% RH, so I can protect six-plus months of cup-score stability and not lose a high-value microlot to mold, baggy taint, or fade.
- When the afternoon cloud-forest humidity spikes or a power flicker kills the dehumidifier overnight, I want an instant phone alert naming the exact rack or zone affected, so I can act within minutes instead of discovering ruined coffee at the next cupping.
- When a green-coffee buyer or the Best of Panama auction asks me to prove how a lot was stored, I want a continuous, tamper-evident temperature/humidity/aw record tied to that lot code, so I can win the sale and defend the price with data instead of promises.
- When I rotate stock for an export order, I want the system to flag which bagged lots are aging out of their freshness window and exactly where they sit, so I can ship FEFO and never let value-bearing green sit past its prime.

**Features:**
- **Storage Locations and bin map** — Models warehouses, conditioning rooms, parchment rest bins, and green-bag racks as first-class places with a visual map; every batch in the existing pipeline finally gets a real physical home after the green stage, closing the gap where the model currently dead-ends at drying.
- **Live environment dashboard (temp / RH / dew point / VPD per zone)** — One glanceable liquid-glass view of each storage zone's current and trending conditions against its target band, so the manager sees a problem in 2 seconds instead of walking the bodega with a handheld.
- **Threshold alerts and escalation (push/SMS/WhatsApp)** — Per-zone min/max rules with debounce and escalation chains fire to phones the moment RH, temp, or aw drifts or a dehumidifier/genset drops, turning an overnight disaster into a 5-minute fix.
- **Per-lot water-activity and moisture log** — Records every handheld aw/moisture reading (AquaLab/Sinar) against the lot code and storage entry, building the freshness curve that predicts when a lot exits the 0.45-0.55 band.
- **Hermetic packaging and bag tracking** — Tracks GrainPro/Ecotact liners, oxygen absorbers, and bag fill dates so each export bag's barrier history is known, the difference between 6-month and 12-month shelf stability.
- **Freshness window and FEFO rotation engine** — Computes a best-ship-by window per lot from its aw trajectory and storage exposure and surfaces a rotation queue, so the highest-value Geisha ships at peak and nothing fades on the rack.
- **Storage-integrity certificate (per-lot export report)** — One-click PDF with the full T/RH/aw chart for a lot's storage life, packaging, and any excursions, a sales asset for direct-trade buyers and competition submissions.
- **Excursion / incident timeline** — Auto-logs every threshold breach with duration, peak, and cause note, giving QC a forensic record to explain or rule out a cupping defect.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** LoRaWAN temp/RH sensors (Milesight EM300-TH, TEKTELIC, Disruptive Technologies, Monnit ALTA, Aranet) for in-zone monitoring with multi-year battery life, AquaLab / METER Group water-activity meters and Sinar / Dickey-john moisture meters for benchtop aw and moisture readings per lot, The Things Network / Helium or a Milesight LoRaWAN gateway as the on-farm ingestion path (no per-sensor cellular cost, fits the $0/free-tier constraint), Cropster (specialty-coffee QC/inventory standard) for two-way green-lot and cup-score sync, Sensaphone Sentinel / cellular fallback for power-loss and offline-bodega alerting, Twilio / WhatsApp Business API for SMS/WhatsApp alerts to caretakers who don't use the app, OpenWeather / Tomorrow.io ambient humidity feed to correlate outdoor cloud-forest spikes with indoor drift, GrainPro / Ecotact as the referenced hermetic-packaging standard for the bag-tracking module, SCA green-coffee grading and ICO/ISO 6673 moisture standards as the rule-band reference

**Data model additions:** storage_locations (id, name, kind: warehouse|conditioning_room|parchment_bin|green_rack, parent_id, target_temp_min/max, target_rh_min/max, target_aw_min/max, capacity_kg), sensors (id, location_id, vendor, model, kind: temp|rh|aw|power, lorawan_dev_eui, battery_pct, last_seen), environment_readings (id, sensor_id, location_id, at, temp_c, rh_pct, dew_point_c, vpd, source: sensor|manual), aw_readings (id, lot_code, location_id, at, water_activity, moisture_pct, instrument, operator_id), inventory_lots (id, lot_code, batch_id, location_id, kind: parchment|green, weight_kg, bag_count, packaging_type: jute|grainpro|ecotact, o2_absorber, packed_date, freshness_due), storage_alerts (id, location_id, sensor_id, metric, threshold, observed, severity, opened_at, resolved_at, cause_note), storage_certificates (id, lot_code, generated_at, period_start, period_end, excursion_count, pdf_url)

**AI / automation:**
- Predictive humidity breach: forecast RH/aw drift hours ahead from indoor trend plus OpenWeather ambient feed and pre-trigger dehumidification before the band is breached.
- Freshness/aw trajectory model: project each lot's water-activity curve to estimate its best-ship-by date and rank the FEFO rotation queue.
- Anomaly detection on sensor streams: distinguish a real environmental excursion from a flapping/dying sensor (battery, drift) so guards never go silently dead.
- Excursion-to-defect correlation: cross-reference storage excursions with later cupping defects to learn which conditions actually move Janson's cup scores.
- Natural-language storage briefing: a morning summary generated by Claude for the manager and owner, e.g. conditioning room held band overnight, rack B3 Geisha aw rose to 0.58, pull for milling this week.

**World-class:** A buyer scans a QR code on a Janson export bag and sees the lot's entire post-drying life: a continuous temp/RH/aw chart, every excursion explained, the hermetic packaging used, and the predicted freshness window, proving the cup in the bag today is the cup that was scored at harvest. Internally, sensors stream into per-zone target bands, AI forecasts an RH breach hours before it happens and pre-runs the dehumidifier, and the FEFO engine tells the manager each morning exactly which bags to pull. Zero lots are ever lost to silent humidity creep, and storage integrity becomes a documented premium Janson charges for.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson sits at 1,350-1,700 masl in Volcan's cloud-forest microclimate, where the same persistent humidity that grows world-class Geisha is the single biggest threat to green-coffee storage, so controlled-environment monitoring is existential for the high-value lots. Premium Geisha and reserve microlots command very high prices, so a single humid week that pushes a lot out of the 0.45-0.55 aw band and drops it two cup points is a four-to-five-figure loss. Tying a tamper-evident storage record to each traceability lot code (the system already carries lot codes from harvest through processing) lets Janson defend Best of Panama and direct-trade pricing with proof, turning store-it-right into a documented, monetizable part of the brand story, fully achievable on free-tier infra via on-farm LoRaWAN gateways with no recurring per-sensor cost.


#### Distribution & 3PL / DTC last-mile fulfillment
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the Fulfillment & Logistics Coordinator at Janson Coffee — the person who turns a finished green lot or a roasted batch into a tracked parcel/pallet/container on a buyer's doorstep, across three very different channels: (1) high-value green export to importers and Best-of-Panama auction winners (containers, pallets, sample boxes), (2) wholesale roasted to cafes/distributors, (3) DTC roasted-bag last-mile to consumers off jansoncoffee.com. Secondary users: the farm owner/commercial lead (margin, landed cost, claims), the roastery lead (roast-to-ship cure window + FEFO), and the end customer/importer (self-serve tracking + customs docs).

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a 98-point Best-of-Panama Geisha lot or a 60kg-bag green order is ready for an importer in Dubai/Japan/the US, I want to rate-shop ocean vs. air, auto-generate the full export document set (commercial invoice, packing list, ICO certificate of origin, phytosanitary cert, Bill of Lading, HS 0901.11), and hand off to a freight forwarder in one flow, so I can move six-figure coffee with zero paperwork errors and a clean chain of custody.
- When a DTC customer orders a bag of fresh-roasted Geisha on jansoncoffee.com, I want the order to drop into a fulfillment queue that picks the freshest FEFO bag within the roast-date cure window, rate-shops the cheapest carrier that still arrives fresh, and prints the label automatically, so I ship same-day without overpaying or sending stale coffee.
- When I quote international DTC and wholesale buyers, I want landed cost (duties, VAT/GST, carrier handling) calculated and collected at checkout as DDP, so the customer is never surprised by a customs bill and parcels don't get abandoned at the border.
- When a parcel is delayed, damaged, or lost in transit, I want proactive tracking alerts, a one-click claims/reship flow, and a self-serve tracking page for the buyer, so I cut 'where is my coffee' emails and protect both margin and the Janson brand.

**Features:**
- **Unified Outbound Order & Shipment workspace** — One queue for every outbound channel (green export, wholesale roasted, DTC) so the coordinator stops juggling email, spreadsheets, and carrier portals; each shipment inherits its lot/batch traceability code from the existing harvest-to-processing chain straight onto the label and customs docs.
- **Multi-carrier rate-shopping + label/manifest engine** — EasyPost/Shippo integration compares DHL, FedEx, UPS, USPS, and Correos de Panama live, auto-picks the cheapest service that still meets the roast-date cure window, and batch-prints labels + end-of-day SCAN manifests; kills per-parcel portal data entry and shipping overspend.
- **Green-export & freight-forwarder module** — Container / pallet / sample-box loads with auto-generated commercial invoice, packing list, ICO certificate of origin, phytosanitary certificate, and Bill of Lading; booking handoff to a forwarder (Flexport/forwarder API); turns the highest-value, highest-risk shipments into a guided, error-proof workflow.
- **DTC fulfillment + FEFO roast-date allocation** — Orders from the jansoncoffee.com Shopify/WooCommerce store sync in and auto-allocate the freshest first-expired-first-out bag inside the cure window, blocking any pick that would ship stale coffee; protects the freshness promise that justifies specialty pricing.
- **Subscription & recurring-shipment scheduler** — Recharge/Skio/Smartrr-style coffee subscriptions schedule recurring fulfillment cycles tied to roast days, so repeat DTC revenue runs on autopilot and ships fresh on a predictable cadence.
- **Landed-cost / DDP duty & tax calculator** — Zonos/Avalara cross-border calc shows duties, VAT/GST, and carrier handling at checkout and collects DDP, so international buyers see one all-in price and parcels clear customs instead of stalling; directly lifts international DTC conversion.
- **Live tracking, exception alerts & branded tracking page** — Webhook-driven status from every carrier surfaces delays/damage early, fires proactive customer notifications, and powers a Janson-branded self-serve tracking page; slashes WISMO support load and turns delivery into a brand touchpoint.
- **Claims, returns & reship workflow** — One-click file-a-claim (carrier API + optional shipment insurance) and reship with margin/loss capture, so damaged or lost high-value lots are recovered without ad-hoc email chains.
- **3PL / warehouse handoff & inventory sync** — Optional connection to a roast-date-aware 3PL (e.g. ShipBob, 3PLGuys) and a finished-goods inventory ledger, so Janson can outsource US/EU last-mile while keeping one source of truth for stock, roast dates, and channel demand.
- **Cartonization & shipping-cost optimizer** — Box/pallet packing logic picks optimal carton/pallet config by weight and dimensional weight, trimming dim-weight surcharges on every parcel and consolidating wholesale orders.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** EasyPost (multi-carrier rate shop, labels, tracking, address verification, shipment insurance), Shippo (alternative multi-carrier shipping API), DHL Express, FedEx, UPS, USPS, and Correos de Panama / national post carrier accounts, Flexport or a freight-forwarder/NVOCC API for ocean FCL/LCL green-coffee export bookings + Bill of Lading, Shopify + Shopify Fulfillment / WooCommerce as the DTC storefront and order source, Recharge / Skio / Smartrr (coffee subscription management), Zonos and Avalara AvaTax Cross-Border (landed cost, DDP duties/VAT, HS-code classification), ShipBob / 3PLGuys (roast-date-aware 3PL outsourced last-mile for US/EU), ICO (International Coffee Organization) certificate-of-origin + Panama MIDA/AUPSA phytosanitary export certification standards, GS1 standards: SSCC pallet labels, GTIN, and barcode/QR for parcel and lot traceability, Cropster / SCA Best of Panama auction lot-allocation data feed (ties an auction-won lot to its export shipment), Stripe / PayPal for DTC order payment and DDP duty collection at checkout

**Data model additions:** Shipment (id, channel: green-export|wholesale|dtc, status, carrier, service, trackingNumber, originPlot/mill, destination, incoterm DDP/DAP, costUsd, insuredValueUsd, labelUrl), ShipmentLine (shipmentId, lotCode/batchId ref to ProcessingBatch, productType: green|roasted, weightKg, bagCount, roastDate), Order (id, channel, customerId, placedAt, channelRef e.g. Shopify order #, totalUsd, dutyUsd, status), Customer / Buyer (id, type: importer|cafe|consumer|auction-winner, name, country, addresses, taxId/EORI), CarrierAccount (id, carrier, credentials ref, serviceLevels, negotiatedRates), CustomsDocument (id, shipmentId, type: commercial-invoice|packing-list|ICO-cert|phyto-cert|bill-of-lading, hsCode, fileUrl), TrackingEvent (shipmentId, timestamp, status, location, carrierCode, exceptionFlag), Subscription (id, customerId, productSku, cadence, nextShipDate, status), InventoryItem / FinishedGoods (sku, lotCode, roastDate, onHandKg, location/3PL, FEFO expiry), ShippingRate (shipmentId, carrier, service, amountUsd, estDeliveryDays, chosen flag), Claim (shipmentId, type: damage|loss|delay, status, amountUsd, resolution), FreightBooking (shipmentId, forwarderRef, container/BL number, vessel/sailing, ETA)

**AI / automation:**
- Carrier/service recommendation: ML predicts on-time delivery and damage risk per carrier+lane, then ranks rate-shop results by expected freshness-on-arrival, not just price.
- FEFO allocation optimizer: auto-pick which roast-date bags fill which orders to minimize stale-coffee risk and waste across DTC + subscriptions.
- Auto HS-code classification and customs-doc drafting from product description (Avalara-style); coffee defaults to 0901.11/0901.21 but the model handles roasted/decaf/blends and flags mismatches.
- Delivery-exception prediction: flag shipments likely to be delayed/lost from early tracking signals and pre-empt with reship or customer alert before the buyer complains.
- WISMO support agent: an LLM assistant that answers 'where is my coffee' from live tracking data on the branded page and drafts proactive delay emails.
- Demand-aware fulfillment routing: forecast channel demand by region to decide whether to ship from the farm, hold at a 3PL, or consolidate into a forwarder container.
- Cartonization/packing optimizer: AI chooses box/pallet config to minimize dimensional-weight surcharges per shipment.

**World-class:** World-class is a single outbound nerve center where a finished green lot or roasted batch flows from the existing harvest-to-processing traceability chain into a fully documented, rate-optimized, tracked shipment across all three channels with near-zero manual data entry. Green export auto-produces a compliant document pack (commercial invoice, packing list, ICO origin, phyto cert, BL) and books a forwarder; DTC orders pick FEFO-fresh bags inside the cure window, rate-shop the cheapest carrier that arrives fresh, print labels, and charge DDP landed cost at checkout so nothing stalls at customs; every parcel/pallet/container streams live tracking to a branded self-serve page with proactive exception alerts, one-click claims, and shipment insurance on high-value lots. The whole thing is unit-economics-aware (true landed cost and margin per shipment) and can flex between farm-direct fulfillment and a roast-date-aware 3PL as volume grows; the buyer of a $30k/kg Geisha gets Apple-grade tracking, and the coordinator never touches a carrier portal.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson sells across the full value spectrum in one operation: green coffee export (containers, pallets, and competition lots that can fetch tens of thousands per kg at Best of Panama) AND roasted DTC/wholesale, which is exactly why a one-size shipping tool fails them. The differentiator is binding the existing lot/batch traceability (plot to harvest lotCode to ProcessingBatch) directly onto export documents and parcel labels, so a buyer can scan a QR and trace a bag back to the specific Volcan plot, altitude, varietal, and process. Roast-date FEFO discipline protects the freshness premium that specialty pricing depends on, and DDP landed-cost at checkout removes the customs friction that kills international Geisha DTC sales. The 'shipment' activity kind already exists in the unified model, signaling this pillar was always meant to close the farm-to-cup loop.


### Commerce & Customers

#### Wholesale / B2B green-coffee contracts — importers, roasters
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Daniela — Janson's commercial/export manager (owner-operator wearing the green-coffee sales hat). She negotiates and tracks every green-coffee sale to overseas importers and roasters, owns the relationship from sample to shipment to final payment, and is the person who decides when to fix price against the C market. Secondary users: the QC/cupping lead (builds offer lists from approved lots) and the family owner (cash-flow + margin oversight).

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a roaster in Oslo who cupped our Geisha at a competition emails asking 'what's available and at what price,' I want to send them a live, branded offer list of exactly the green lots I have uncommitted — with score, process, screen size, available bags, and price per lb — so I can convert interest into a signed contract before a competitor's offer list does.
- When I agree a 50-bag Geisha sale on a differential against ICE 'C' (e.g. +120 vs Dec contract), I want the system to track the contract through fixation, partial shipments, and final invoicing so I always know my locked-in revenue, what's still price-open to market swings, and what's actually been paid.
- When an EU importer requires EUDR compliance before they'll sign, I want to attach each contracted lot to the GPS polygons of the exact plots its cherries came from plus the deforestation-free due-diligence evidence, so I can hand them a complete, audit-ready traceability pack and not lose the sale to a farm that can't prove origin.
- When a buyer requests a pre-shipment sample before approving a contract lot, I want to log the sample shipment, the buyer's cupping verdict, and the approval, so a yes-from-the-cupping-table flows straight into a confirmed contract and a no triggers a re-offer of that lot to someone else.

**Features:**
- **Green Lot Inventory & Offer-List Builder** — Turns finished 'green'-stage processing batches into sellable inventory (bags available, GrainPro/jute, screen size, cup score, lot story) and generates branded, shareable offer lists (PDF + live link) per buyer — so Janson sells from a single source of truth instead of stale spreadsheets, and never double-sells the same bags.
- **B2B Contract Manager (fixed / differential / forward)** — Models the real green-coffee contract lifecycle — buyer, Incoterm (FOB Puerto Armuelles, CIF Hamburg), lot(s), bags/weight, quality spec, fixed price OR differential vs an ICE 'C' contract month, fixation status, deposit/balance terms — giving Daniela a live book of committed vs. open business and locked vs. market-exposed revenue.
- **Sample Tracking & Approval Workflow** — Logs offer/pre-shipment/type samples sent to each buyer with courier tracking, links the buyer's cupping verdict and approval back to the lot, and auto-promotes an approved sample into a confirmable contract — closing the gap where deals stall waiting on 'did they like the sample?'
- **Price-Fixation & C-Market Cockpit** — Pulls the live ICE Coffee 'C' price by contract month, computes the implied price per lb for every open differential contract, and alerts when a fix window is favorable — so Janson captures upside on differential deals instead of fixing blind or letting margin erode.
- **Shipment & Documents Workspace** — Tracks each contract's shipment (bags, container/booking, ICO marks, weights) and assembles the export document pack (commercial invoice, packing list, certificate of origin, ICO certificate of origin, phytosanitary, EUDR due-diligence statement, weight/quality cert) so a shipment goes out complete and payment isn't held up by a missing doc.
- **Buyer CRM & Repeat-Order Pipeline** — A relationship record per importer/roaster (cupping history, past contracts, preferred lots, payment behavior, EUDR/cert requirements) so Janson nurtures the high-value roasters who pay premiums and forecasts likely repeat volume for next season's planting/processing plan.
- **Contract-to-Cash & Margin Ledger** — Ties each contract to its deposit, balance, and final settlement (vs. estimated cost per lb from the farm/processing side) to show realized margin per lot and feed the real number behind the dashboard's ytd_revenue_usd — turning the farm app into a P&L on every lot.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** ICE / Intercontinental Exchange — Coffee 'C' (KC) futures price feed by contract month (via a market-data vendor such as Barchart, Nasdaq Data Link/Quandl, or StoneX) for differential pricing and fixation, ECF European Standard Contract for Coffee (ESCC) and GCA (Green Coffee Association) contract terms & arbitration rules — the legal templates for green-coffee sales, EUDR / EU TRACES & Information System — due-diligence statement submission, plot geolocation polygons (GeoJSON), deforestation-free evidence (couples to satellite/NDVI providers and Meridia/agriplace-style EUDR tooling), ICO (International Coffee Organization) certificate of origin and ICO marks for export documentation, Cropster Commerce / cropster.coffee — interop for roasters who already run Cropster ordering portals (offer-list and lot-spec exchange), Incoterms 2020 (ICC) — FOB/CIF/FCA/DDP delivery-term standard baked into the contract model, Trade-finance & payment rails — letter of credit (L/C) and documentary collection via the family's bank; cross-border settlement (Wise/Airwallex) for smaller direct-trade roaster payments; optional pre-harvest financing partners, Freight forwarder / NVOCC booking APIs (e.g. Maersk, container line) and shipment tracking for export bookings out of Panamanian ports, Panama AUPSA/MIDA phytosanitary certificate and certificate-of-origin issuance for exports, Certification bodies — SCA cupping protocol/score, plus Rainforest Alliance / Fair Trade / organic certificate references attached to lots, QuickBooks Online / Xero — sync contract invoices and settlements into the family's accounting, DocuSign / e-signature for counter-signing sales contracts; courier APIs (DHL/FedEx) for sample-shipment tracking

**Data model additions:** buyers — importer/roaster CRM record (company, country, contact, EUDR operator status, cert requirements, payment terms, preferred Incoterm), green_lots — sellable green-coffee inventory derived from a completed processing batch (lot_code FK, variety, process, screen size, cup_score, bags_total, bags_available, packaging, harvest_year, warehouse location), offers / offer_list_items — a buyer-specific offer list and its line items (green_lot, price_per_lb, currency, valid_until, sample_status), samples — sample shipments (green_lot FK, buyer FK, type: offer/pre-shipment/type, courier, tracking_no, sent_date, cupping_score, verdict: approved/rejected/pending), contracts — the sales agreement (buyer FK, contract_no, template: ESCC/GCA, incoterm, total_bags, total_kg, quality_spec, price_type: fixed|differential, fixed_price_per_lb, ice_contract_month, differential_cents, fixation_status, deposit_pct, payment_terms, status: draft|confirmed|shipping|shipped|paid|closed), contract_lots — junction tying contracts to one or more green_lots with allocated bags (handles split/blended lots), fixations — price-fixing events against a differential contract (contract FK, fixed_date, ice_price, contract_month, bags_fixed, resulting_price_per_lb), shipments — physical export against a contract (contract FK, container_no, booking_ref, ico_marks, gross/net/tare weights, ship_date, forwarder, status), shipment_documents — document pack per shipment (type: commercial_invoice/packing_list/cert_origin/ico_cert/phyto/eudr_dds/weight_cert, file_ref, issued_date, status), eudr_declarations — due-diligence statement per contract/lot (plot polygons GeoJSON, dds_reference_number, deforestation_free_evidence_ref, risk_assessment, submitted_date), payments — deposits, balances and final settlements per contract (amount, currency, method: lc/wire/wise, received_date, fx_rate), price_feed — cached ICE Coffee 'C' quotes by contract month (for the fixation cockpit)

**AI / automation:**
- Offer-list copywriter — auto-drafts a buyer-ready lot description and tasting-note narrative from the lot's variety, process, altitude (plot data), Brix/cup score, and harvest story, so every offer reads like the marketing-grade sheets the best origins send.
- Fixation advisor — watches the live ICE 'C' feed against each open differential contract and surfaces a plain-language recommendation ('Dec contract is up 14c this week; fixing your 50-bag Geisha now locks $X/lb — above your floor') with the margin math done, flagging windows Daniela would otherwise miss.
- Demand & repeat-order forecaster — learns from buyer CRM + past contracts which roasters reorder, at what volume and premium, to forecast next season's likely committed demand and feed the planting/processing plan (closing the loop back to plots and harvests).
- Document-pack autofill & validator — extracts and cross-checks the export document set, auto-populates the EUDR due-diligence statement from the lot's linked plot polygons, and flags any missing/inconsistent doc before the container ships (weights don't match, cert expired, polygon missing).
- Lead triage from inbound buyer emails — parses 'what's available?' inquiries from importer emails, matches the ask (variety/score/volume/price band) against live green-lot inventory, and drafts the offer-list reply for Daniela to review and send.
- Margin & FX sentinel — recomputes realized margin per lot as costs and FX move, alerting when a differential contract's implied price or a currency swing pushes a deal below the target margin.

**World-class:** A producer-side commercial OS where a lot flows seamlessly from the wet mill into sellable green inventory, onto a beautiful branded offer list, into a standards-based (ESCC/GCA) contract with correct Incoterms and either a fixed price or a live-tracked differential against ICE 'C', with samples, fixation, shipment, the full export document pack, EUDR due-diligence, and contract-to-cash payment all in one continuous thread — every contracted bag traceable back to the exact plot polygons it grew on, every dollar of locked vs. market-open revenue visible at a glance, and AI quietly drafting offers, advising fixation windows, and validating documents. It rivals what Cropster Commerce + a trade-finance desk give a large importer, but pointed origin-out for a single great farm: Janson sells direct, captures the premium, proves provenance instantly, and never loses a deal to a missing document or a stale spreadsheet.

**Coffee-specific angle:** This is the literal business model of jansoncoffee.com — they grow, process, AND sell specialty green (and roasted) coffee, so B2B green-coffee contracts are how the farm actually makes money; today's app stops at the green-coffee mill door and only hints at sales via a lone ytd_revenue_usd field and a 'shipment' activity kind. Janson's edge is exactly what commands premiums and de-risks EUDR: a named single-estate origin in Volcán at 1,350–1,700 masl with prized Geisha/Pacamara/Caturra and a 200-hectare quetzal-habitat private reserve — a story that sells offer lists to roasters and whose plot-level GPS polygons (already implied by the plots table) make EUDR deforestation-free proof a selling point, not a burden. The existing lot_code traceability that already chains harvests → processing_batches is the natural spine: extend it one link further into green_lots → contracts so a roaster in Tokyo can trace a contracted bag all the way back to 'Tizingal Alto, Block A, Geisha, picked at 24 Brix' inside the reserve.


#### DTC e-commerce — roasted-bag storefront
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the Janson family owner-operator / brand lead who runs DTC sales (sets prices, decides which micro-lots get roasted-and-bagged vs sold green, writes the origin story). Secondary: the mill/roast-fulfillment operator who batches roasts and packs orders, and the end customer/home brewer or cafe buyer who buys a bag of Janson Geisha online and wants it fresh, traceable, and re-orderable.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a micro-lot of our Geisha finishes drying and milling, I want to publish it as a roasted-bag product with its own origin story and price in minutes, so I can sell it direct at a specialty premium instead of dumping it to a broker at commodity rates.
- When a customer loves a coffee they got from us, I want them to subscribe to recurring shipments they can pause, skip, or swap by varietal/roast, so I turn a one-time buyer into predictable monthly revenue and plan my roast batches against real demand.
- When I roast and pack orders each week, I want roast-to-order batching, a printed pack/ship list, and carrier labels generated automatically, so coffee leaves the farm within days of roasting and arrives fresh with accurate international shipping cost.
- When a buyer scans the QR code on a Janson bag, I want them to see the exact plot, altitude, picker crew, process method, roast date and cupping notes for that lot, so the farm's traceability data becomes the proof that justifies the price and earns repeat orders.

**Features:**
- **Roasted-bag product catalog (lot-linked SKUs)** — Each product SKU is tied to a real lot_code, so a bag inherits its plot, varietal, altitude, process and harvest data automatically — listing a new micro-lot becomes a 2-minute publish, not a re-keying exercise, and inventory is reconciled against milled green kg upstream.
- **Roast-profile + grind/size/freshness variants** — Customers pick whole-bean vs grind, 250g/340g/1kg, and roast level; each variant carries a roast_date and a freshness ('rest 7-12 days, best within 4 weeks') indicator so the product page sells freshness honestly and reduces stale-coffee complaints.
- **Cart + Stripe Checkout (headless, $0 infra)** — Stripe-hosted Checkout + Payment Links wired into the existing Next.js/Supabase app means real card payment with zero PCI burden and no monthly platform fee — a true DTC store on the free tier, no Shopify rent.
- **Coffee subscriptions (pause / skip / swap)** — Recurring weekly/bi-weekly/monthly plans via Stripe Billing with a self-serve customer portal to pause, skip, swap varietal or change cadence — converts one-time buyers into $780-1,200/yr LTV and gives a forward demand signal to plan roast batches.
- **Roast-to-order fulfillment queue + pack/ship sheet** — Orders aggregate into a weekly roast batch list (kg by varietal/roast), a pick-pack sheet, and one-click carrier labels — coffee ships within days of roasting and the operator never hand-builds a spreadsheet.
- **Carrier-rate shipping + customs/landed cost** — Live international rates and customs paperwork (origin Panama) shown at checkout so the customer sees true landed cost and the farm isn't eating cross-border shipping margin.
- **Bag QR traceability microsite** — Each bag's QR resolves to a public lot-story page (plot, altitude, quetzal-reserve shade, picker crew, brix, process, drying days, roast date, cupping notes) — turns farm-ops data into the marketing asset and a reorder funnel.
- **Customer accounts, order history & reorder** — Buyers track orders, manage subscriptions, and one-click reorder a past lot — drives repeat revenue and gives the farm a first-party customer list (email/marketing) instead of renting it from a marketplace.
- **Discounts, gift sets, limited-drop & waitlist** — Coupon codes, curated gift/sampler bundles, and a 'notify me' waitlist for scarce auction-grade micro-lots — captures demand for tiny Geisha lots that sell out in hours and maximizes price on scarcity.
- **Tax & invoice / receipt generation** — Automated receipts and country-aware tax handling so every DTC sale is clean for the books and ties back into the farm's revenue ledger (season_summary.ytd_revenue_usd).

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Stripe (Checkout, Payment Links, Billing for subscriptions, Customer Portal, Tax) — works for USD-denominated Panama-origin sales and carries zero monthly platform fee, PayPal / PayU Latam / Páguelo Fácil as local Panama payment fallbacks for buyers who lack international cards, Shippo or EasyPost (multi-carrier label + live rate API) for DHL Express, FedEx International, UPS and Correos de Panamá international parcels, DHL Express / FedEx Cross Border APIs for door-to-door international green-and-roasted shipments with customs docs, Resend or Postmark (transactional email: order confirm, roast/ship notice, subscription reminders) on free tier, Cropster / roast-software export to pull roast curves and roast_date into product variants, GS1 Digital Link standard for the bag QR codes (globally resolvable, audit-grade lot identity), Klaviyo or Loops (first-party email/SMS marketing to the customer list) — optional, free starter tiers, Supabase Auth + Postgres + Storage (customer accounts, order tables, product imagery) staying on the existing free stack, Avalara/Stripe Tax or TaxJar for destination tax on cross-border DTC, Best of Panama / Specialty Coffee Association lot-grade and cupping-score references surfaced on the lot-story page

**Data model additions:** products (lot_code FK to lots, title, story, origin metadata, status: draft/live/sold-out), product_variants (product_id, grind, weight_g, roast_level, roast_date, price_usd, freshness_window), inventory (variant_id, on_hand_units, reserved_units; reconciles against milled green kg upstream), customers (id, email, name, country, marketing_opt_in, stripe_customer_id), addresses (customer_id, shipping/billing, country, postal), orders (id, customer_id, status, subtotal, shipping, tax, total, currency, placed_at), order_items (order_id, variant_id, lot_code, qty, unit_price), subscriptions (customer_id, variant_id or plan, cadence, status: active/paused/cancelled, next_ship_date, stripe_subscription_id), shipments (order_id, carrier, service, tracking_no, label_url, ship_date, customs_value), payments (order_id, stripe_payment_intent, amount, status), discounts (code, type, value, scope, expires_at), lot_stories (lot_code FK, narrative, cupping_score, cupping_notes, qr_slug, roast_date) — public traceability microsite source, waitlist_signups (variant_id/product_id, email, created_at) for scarce-lot drops

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-draft lot/product copy and cupping-note prose from the harvest + processing data (plot, varietal, altitude, brix, process, drying days) so a new micro-lot listing writes itself for owner review
- Demand forecasting: predict subscription churn and next-month roast volume by varietal/roast from order + subscription history, so roast batches and green-kg allocation are planned, not guessed
- Personalized recommendations / 'coffees like the one you loved' on the storefront and in re-engagement emails to lift repeat purchase
- Smart subscription save-flows: detect a pause/cancel intent and auto-offer a swap, skip, or discount to retain LTV
- AI support assistant (brew guidance, order/shipping status, 'when does my coffee ship') over the order + freshness data to deflect routine customer-service tickets
- Dynamic scarcity/pricing suggestions for auction-grade micro-lots based on cupping score, lot size, and waitlist demand
- Auto-generate the bag QR lot-story page from the unified lot record, including localized translation for international buyers

**World-class:** A buyer anywhere in the world lands on a Janson lot page, sees a Geisha micro-lot with its Best-of-Panama-style cupping score, the exact plot at 1,650 masl inside the quetzal reserve, the picker crew and brix, picks whole-bean + 340g + light roast, sees a true DHL landed cost to their country, and checks out in under 60 seconds. The coffee is roasted-to-order that week, ships within 48h of roast, and the bag's GS1 QR resolves to that same lot story plus the actual roast date. They subscribe, manage everything (pause/skip/swap) from a self-serve portal, and get re-engaged when next season's lot of 'their' coffee drops. The farm runs the whole thing on a $0 headless stack (Next.js + Supabase + Stripe), owns its customer list outright, pays no marketplace rent, and every sale flows straight back into the unified ops ledger so revenue, green-kg inventory, and demand forecasting are one system — not five disconnected apps.

**Coffee-specific angle:** This is the pillar that monetizes everything upstream and is uniquely powerful for Janson because they own the WHOLE chain (grow → harvest → wet/dry mill → roast → sell). The storefront's SKUs link by lot_code straight to the existing plots/harvests/processing_batches model, so a bag of 'Tizingal Alto Geisha, Washed, 1,650 masl' carries verifiable provenance no reseller can fake. The quetzal nature-reserve and shade-grown story, the Geisha varietal, and Best-of-Panama-grade micro-lots are exactly the scarcity + narrative that command $30-100+/bag specialty pricing — and selling roasted DTC captures the full retail margin instead of the commodity green price a broker pays. Roast-to-order freshness and the QR-to-origin story are the trust mechanics that close that premium sale.


#### Subscriptions & coffee-club management
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the D2C Commerce / Coffee Club Manager (a Janson family member or hired e-commerce lead who owns recurring revenue, the club roster, and customer relationships). Secondary: the Roaster/Fulfillment lead (who must know how many bags of which roast to roast and ship each cycle) and the end Subscriber/Club Member themselves (the self-service persona managing their own plan).

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a tourist or cafe buyer falls in love with a Geisha cup during a farm visit, I want to convert them into a recurring monthly/quarterly subscriber on the spot, so I can turn one-time delight into predictable, high-margin recurring revenue instead of a single bag sale.
- When a new micro-lot finishes drying and is graded (e.g. an 88+ anaerobic Geisha), I want the system to forecast exactly how many subscriber bags I'll need next cycle and reserve green inventory against it, so I can roast-to-order without overcommitting scarce reserve lots or stocking out my best customers.
- When a member's card fails or their renewal is coming up, I want dunning, pause, skip-a-shipment, and gifting handled automatically, so I can keep churn low and avoid manually chasing payments or losing a subscriber over a $0 expired-card problem.
- When I plan a quarterly 'Reserve Club' allocation of our rarest Geisha, I want to fairly allocate limited bags across tiers and run a waitlist, so I can reward loyal members and create scarcity-driven demand without overselling what the farm actually produced.

**Features:**
- **Subscription Plans & Tiers builder** — Define recurring products (Estate Monthly 250g/500g/1kg, Reserve Geisha Quarterly, whole-bean vs ground, washed/natural/honey rotation) with cadence, price, currency, and roast-on-demand windows -- turns the existing lot catalog into recurring revenue SKUs.
- **Member roster & lifecycle (active / paused / skipped / churned)** — A single CRM-grade view of every subscriber with lifetime value, tenure, next-ship date, and at-risk flags -- gives the family one place to manage relationships and spot churn before it happens.
- **Self-service member portal (pause, skip, swap roast, gift, change address)** — Members manage their own plan without emailing the farm, cutting support load to near-zero and reducing involuntary churn (skip beats cancel).
- **Recurring billing + smart dunning** — Auto-charges each cycle, retries failed cards on an optimized schedule, sends update-your-card flows, and handles proration -- recovers revenue that would otherwise silently leak.
- **Roast-batch & fulfillment forecasting** — Aggregates upcoming renewals into a single roast/pack/ship work order per cycle and decrements reserved green inventory -- lets the roaster roast-to-order for peak freshness and gives the mill a real demand signal tied to processing batches.
- **Reserve Club allocation & waitlist** — Fairly distributes scarce micro-lots (limited Geisha) across tiers with overflow waitlists and next-harvest notifications -- monetizes scarcity and rewards loyalty, a hallmark of world-class single-estate clubs.
- **Gifting, prepaid terms (3/6/12 mo), and corporate/cafe wholesale subscriptions** — Captures gift recipients and bulk B2B recurring orders (cafes on standing weekly green orders) -- expands beyond individual D2C into prepaid cash-up-front and wholesale recurring revenue.
- **Lot-traceability story card per shipment** — Each box ships with (and the portal shows) the exact plot, altitude, varietal, process, and harvest date pulled from the existing Plot/Harvest/ProcessingBatch records -- converts Janson's real provenance data into a differentiating brand experience subscribers cannot get elsewhere.
- **Recurring-revenue analytics (MRR, ARR, churn %, LTV, cohort retention)** — A recurring-revenue dashboard that makes the health of the club legible to the owner the same way the harvest dashboard does for the field.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Stripe Billing / Subscriptions (recurring charges, proration, customer portal, Smart Retries dunning) -- primary global rail, Yappy and ACH/Panama local rails (Banco General) plus PayPal for Panama-resident and LATAM customers who lack international cards, Shopify Subscriptions (Recharge or Shopify native subscription APIs) if Janson runs storefront on Shopify -- sync products, customers, and recurring orders, EasyPost / Shippo for multi-carrier label buying, plus Panama Correos/Cargo and DHL/FedEx International for export shipments and tracking webhooks, Klaviyo (or Resend plus a flow engine) for lifecycle email/SMS -- renewal reminders, dunning, win-back, shipment story cards, Twilio / WhatsApp Business API for shipment and renewal notifications (WhatsApp is the dominant channel in Panama/LATAM), Cropster / Cropster Roast to pull roast profiles and SCA cupping scores that feed the roast-batch forecast and the per-lot story card, Avalara / Stripe Tax for VAT/ITBMS (Panama 7%) and US sales-tax on cross-border shipments, QuickBooks Online for deferred-revenue recognition on prepaid subscriptions and reconciliation, SCA cupping score standard plus a QR-coded digital traceability passport (aligns with EU Deforestation Regulation / EUDR geolocation expectations for green exports)
