**Data model additions:** Customer (id, name, email, phone, country, channel -- farm-visit/web/wholesale, lifetimeValueUsd, createdAt), SubscriptionPlan (id, name, tier -- Estate/Reserve, cadence -- monthly/quarterly, grindType, sizeG, roastWindow, priceUsd, currency, isReserve), Subscription (id, customerId, planId, status -- active/paused/skipped/churned, startDate, nextShipDate, prepaidCyclesRemaining, giftRecipientId|null), ShipmentOrder (id, subscriptionId, cycleDate, roastBatchId, lotCode -- ties to ProcessingBatch/Harvest, status -- queued/roasting/packed/shipped/delivered, carrier, tracking, addressId), ClubAllocation (id, lotCode, planTier, bagsReserved, bagsAllocated, waitlistCount) -- for scarce Reserve lots, Payment (id, subscriptionId, amountUsd, currency, status -- paid/failed/retrying/refunded, rail -- stripe/yappy/paypal, attemptedAt, dunningStage), Address (id, customerId, line1, city, country, postal, isDefault), RecurringRevenueSnapshot (date, mrrUsd, activeSubscribers, churnPct, newSubscribers, reactivations) -- feeds the analytics dashboard

**AI / automation:**
- Churn-risk scoring: rank subscribers by likelihood-to-cancel (signals: recent skips, support contacts, declining engagement, failed payments) and auto-trigger a retention offer or a personal note from the family.
- Demand & roast forecasting: predict next-cycle bag counts per roast/varietal from the renewal pipeline plus historical skip rates, so the roaster never over- or under-roasts scarce Geisha.
- Smart dunning sequencing: optimize retry timing and channel (card retry vs WhatsApp update-card nudge) per customer to maximize involuntary-churn recovery.
- Personalized roast/varietal recommendation: suggest the next lot a member will love from cupping-preference and past-shipment history (nudge a washed-Caturra subscriber toward the new natural Pacamara).
- Auto-generated, lot-specific shipment story cards: LLM drafts the tasting-note and provenance narrative per box from real Plot/Harvest/ProcessingBatch plus Cropster cupping data, on-brand, in EN/ES.
- Win-back campaign generation: draft personalized re-subscribe offers for churned members timed to the next Reserve harvest drop.

**World-class:** A world-class single-estate coffee club feels like a wine futures program: predictable MRR/ARR the owner sees at a glance; near-zero involuntary churn because dunning, skip, pause, and gifting are frictionless and self-service; roast-to-order freshness driven by a demand forecast that reserves real green inventory against renewals (no overselling scarce Geisha); a scarcity-driven Reserve tier with fair allocation and a waitlist that turns the reserve's rarest micro-lots into a coveted membership; and every box carrying a verifiable, QR-linked traceability passport drawn straight from the farm's own plot/harvest/processing data. Benchmarks: Trade Coffee, Atlas Coffee Club, Blue Bottle and Onyx subscriptions, plus wine-club mechanics (allocation, waitlist, members-only releases) -- but uniquely backed by Janson actually owning the land, the lots, and the story end-to-end.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson is a rare full-chain single estate (grow, harvest, wet/dry mill, green plus roasted) with a genuinely premium asset base: Geisha and Pacamara at 1,350-1,700 masl and a 200-hectare quetzal nature reserve. That provenance is the entire moat of a coffee club. A Reserve Geisha Quarterly tier monetizes the scarcest, highest-cup-score micro-lots the way a winery sells allocated bottles; the existing lotCode/Plot/Harvest/ProcessingBatch chain becomes a literal per-shipment traceability passport (plot, altitude, varietal, process, harvest date, cupping score) that mass roasters cannot replicate. It also closes the loop on farm tourism -- visitors who taste at the finca convert to subscribers on the spot -- and aligns with EUDR geolocation traceability expectations Janson already needs for green exports.


#### Pricing, differentials & C-price/futures hedging
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the Owner / Commercial lead at Janson (the family principal who signs export contracts and decides when to sell). Secondary: an export/sales coordinator who books and reconciles green-coffee contracts, and the finance/admin person who tracks landed revenue per lot. On a farm this size all three may be 1-2 people wearing many hats, so the tool must collapse what a Sucafina trader does on a Bloomberg terminal into a few honest screens.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a roaster offers to buy my Caturra/Catuaí volume at "C + 40 c/lb" for September shipment, I want to instantly see what that nets me in USD/kg landed at today's KC and after FX/processing/cert costs, so I can accept or counter on the spot instead of doing it on a napkin and underpricing.
- When the ICE Coffee C rallies hard during harvest while I still hold unsold parchment, I want to lock (hedge/fix) a price for the C-correlated portion of my crop, so I can protect a known margin instead of gambling that the market holds until I ship.
- When I prepare my Geisha for the Best of Panama auction and direct-trade reserve lots, I want to price them on cup score, scarcity and prior-year auction comps — fully decoupled from the C — so I capture the true premium of a 90+ Geisha rather than anchoring to a commodity number.
- When a buyer asks 'what's your price?' across green spot, pre-ship contracts, and roasted retail at the same time, I want one price book that reconciles all three channels against my real cost-of-production per lot, so I never sell below cost or quote two buyers inconsistent numbers.

**Features:**
- **Live C-market ticker & curve board** — Streams ICE Coffee C (KC) front-month + forward months and intraday change into the app so every pricing decision starts from the real market, not a stale guess.
- **Differential pricing calculator (the napkin, killed)** — Enter 'C + X c/lb' or an outright USD/lb, pick a shipment month, and it resolves to net USD/kg and total contract value after FX (USD/PAB is 1:1 but EUR/JPY buyers aren't), export/processing/cert cost-per-kg, and weight conversions (cherry->parchment->green), so quotes are correct in seconds.
- **Cost-of-production floor per lot** — Rolls up labor (workers table), agronomy tasks, and mill costs into a true $/kg-green break-even per lot and per varietal, then refuses to let a quote drop below floor without an explicit override — enforces the promise 'never sell below cost'.
- **Hedge / fixation ledger & coverage gauge** — Tracks how many bags are price-fixed vs still floating (against KC), shows % of expected harvest hedged, and models P&L of a price-to-be-fixed (PTBF) contract as the C moves — turns an abstract market risk into a single 'you are 60% covered' number.
- **Multi-channel price book (green spot / forward contract / roasted)** — One reconciled source of truth for every price Janson quotes across channels, versioned, so the family never double-quotes a buyer or sells the reserve Geisha at commodity logic.
- **Geisha / reserve auction pricing module** — Prices top micro-lots on cup score, lot size, process and auction comparables (Best of Panama / private-auction history) fully decoupled from the C — captures the real premium of Panama Geisha instead of anchoring to a commodity index.
- **Contract & fixation lifecycle tracker** — From offer -> signed sales contract -> fixation window -> shipment -> payment, with the differential and any to-be-fixed legs attached, so nothing falls through and finance can reconcile landed revenue to season_summary.ytd_revenue_usd by lot.
- **Margin & exposure dashboard** — Per-lot and whole-farm view of locked margin, unsold/unhedged exposure in USD, and downside if the C drops to a stress level — the board-level answer to 'how much money is at risk right now'.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** ICE / Intercontinental Exchange — Coffee C (KC) futures + options market data (delayed/EOD via a vendor; the contract standard itself: 37,500 lb arabica, origin/port differentials e.g. Colombia +1000, Brazil -600), Barchart OnDemand / Nasdaq Data Link (Quandl) / TradingView or Investing.com feeds for KC front-month and forward-curve quotes on the free/low tiers, A coffee-specific data source such as Cropster Hub / Sucafina market reports / ICO composite indicator for specialty differential benchmarks and origin context, An FX rate API (exchangerate.host, ECB, or OpenExchangeRates free tier) for EUR/JPY/GBP buyer pricing — Panama uses USD/PAB pegged 1:1 so it's the buyer currency that varies, A brokerage / hedge execution counterparty for the C-correlated volume — in practice the exporter (Sucafina, Volcafe, Caravela) or an FCM, since a single farm rarely clears a full 37,500 lb contract directly; the app models the fixation, the broker executes, Best of Panama (SCAP) auction results + private-auction platforms for Geisha comparable pricing, Stripe / payment + invoicing rail for the roasted/retail channel and direct-trade green deposits; QuickBooks/accounting export for landed-revenue reconciliation, Standards: ICE Coffee C contract spec & differentials, SCA cupping protocol/score, Incoterms (FOB/FCA/CIF) on contracts, ICO country/quality codes

**Data model additions:** c_market_quote (symbol KC, contract_month, price_usd_lb, change, as_of, source) — time-series of the futures curve, fx_rate (base, quote, rate, as_of) — buyer-currency conversion, sales_contract (id, buyer_id, channel[green-spot|forward|roasted|auction], incoterm, shipment_month, qty_bags/qty_kg, pricing_type[differential|outright|PTBF|auction], differential_c_per_lb, outright_usd_lb, currency, status[offer|signed|fixing|shipped|paid]), price_fixation (id, contract_id, qty_kg_fixed, fixed_c_price, fixed_at, remaining_to_fix_kg) — the hedge/fixation ledger, lot_cost (lot_code, labor_usd, agronomy_usd, mill_usd, cert_usd, total_green_kg, breakeven_usd_kg) — cost-of-production floor, rolled up from workers/tasks/processing, price_book_entry (id, lot_code/variety, channel, list_price, currency, valid_from, valid_to, version) — versioned multi-channel prices, buyer (id, name, country, channel, currency, terms) — roaster/importer counterparties, auction_comp (year, farm, variety, process, score, lot_size_lb, clearing_price_usd_lb) — Geisha/reserve comps, hedge_position / exposure_snapshot (as_of, total_kg, kg_fixed, kg_floating, locked_margin_usd, at_risk_usd) — derived board metric

**AI / automation:**
- Sell-timing copilot: given current KC, forward curve, harvest calendar and unsold inventory, suggest 'fix X bags now vs wait' with a plain-language rationale and a confidence band — decision support, never auto-execution of a financial trade (human signs every fixation).
- Quote-from-message extraction: a roaster emails 'can you do 10 bags Caturra C+45 Oct shipment?' — AI parses it into a structured offer in the differential calculator, ready to accept/counter.
- Auction price recommender: predicts a defensible reserve/ask price for a Geisha micro-lot from cup score, process, lot size and auction-comp history.
- Margin-erosion alerts: watch the C and FX, fire a notification when a signed PTBF contract's implied margin crosses below the cost-of-production floor so the owner fixes before it goes underwater.
- Natural-language exposure Q&A: 'how much of my Geisha is still unsold and what's my downside if the C drops to 280?' answered against the exposure snapshot.

**World-class:** World-class is a single-farm version of what a Sucafina/Volcafe trade desk has: a live KC curve, every contract tagged as differential / outright / price-to-be-fixed, a real-time coverage gauge (% of crop hedged), and a per-lot margin that always knows true cost of production — so the owner can answer 'should I sell or wait, and what's at risk' in one glance. It cleanly separates the two pricing worlds Janson lives in: C-correlated commercial volume that gets hedged/fixed against ICE, and decoupled reserve Geisha priced on cup score and auction scarcity. Quotes are correct to the cent in seconds across green-spot, forward, and roasted channels, never below cost, never inconsistent between two buyers; AI advises on timing but a human signs every financial move; and landed revenue reconciles automatically back to the season P&L.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson uniquely straddles two pricing regimes at once, which is the whole point of this module. Its commercial-grade Caturra/Catuaí/Typica volume is C-correlated and exposed to ICE Coffee C swings — and the 2026 forecast Brazil surplus pushing the C down is precisely the downside a Volcán grower wants to hedge/fix before shipment. But its crown jewel — high-altitude (1,350-1,700 masl) Panama Geisha and Pacamara — sells at Best of Panama auction and direct-trade reserve prices that are fully decoupled from the commodity index (Panama Geisha has cleared into the hundreds of dollars per pound, orders of magnitude above the C). A generic commodity tool would wrongly anchor the Geisha to the C and leave money on the table; a generic farm tool ignores hedging entirely. This module does both: hedge the commodity tail, price the reserve lots on scarcity and score. It also ties straight into the existing model — cost-of-production rolls up from the workers/tasks/processing tables and the cherry->parchment->green weight conversions already implied by ProcessingBatch, and landed revenue closes the loop on season_summary.ytd_revenue_usd, which today is a hardcoded figure with no pricing engine behind it.


#### CRM — buyers, importers, cafés, relationship history
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm's Commercial / Sales lead (at Janson this is typically a family principal or export manager who owns green-coffee and roasted-coffee relationships). Secondary daily users: the owner/GM (reviews pipeline and key accounts), an admin/export coordinator (logs samples, contracts, shipments), and — at origin farms like Janson — the host who manages visiting buyers and cuppers during harvest. The CRM must serve a small, non-technical team that today lives in WhatsApp, email, a spreadsheet of buyers, and memory.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a new importer or roaster emails or DMs us after Best of Panama or a referral, I want every contact, their volume/varietal interest, and our whole prior history in one place, so I can reply within hours with the right lots and not lose the lead in my WhatsApp.
- When harvest is ramping and I have limited Geisha and Pacamara volume, I want to see which accounts buy what, at what price, and how loyal/profitable they are, so I can allocate scarce reserve lots to the relationships that matter most instead of first-come-first-served.
- When a roaster who bought 5 bags of our washed Geisha last season goes quiet, I want the system to flag the lapsed relationship and remind me with their last order and cupping notes, so I can re-engage before they source elsewhere.
- When a buyer visits the farm or requests samples during the season, I want to log the visit, the lots they cupped, their scores and feedback, and the samples shipped, so the next conversation and contract picks up exactly where we left off — across years, not just this trip.

**Features:**
- **Unified Account & Contact directory (buyers, importers, roasters, cafés, distributors, direct consumers)** — One source of truth for every commercial relationship — type, country, channel, segment, decision-makers and roles — killing the WhatsApp/spreadsheet sprawl and making every reply fast and informed.
- **Relationship timeline / interaction log** — A chronological history of every email, call, WhatsApp, farm visit, sample, quote and order per account, so anyone on the family team can pick up a relationship cold and never repeat or contradict prior conversations.
- **Deal / opportunity pipeline (inquiry to signed contract) with lot allocation** — Visual Kanban of live deals tied to specific traceability lots and available green inventory, so scarce Geisha/Pacamara is allocated deliberately and forecastable revenue is visible weeks ahead of harvest.
- **Sample & cupping-feedback tracker** — Logs every sample request, courier tracking, the lot/grade sent, and the buyer's cupping score + tasting notes — turning sample feedback into a structured asset that drives the next sale and informs processing decisions.
- **Account scoring & segmentation (RFM-style: value, loyalty, recency, lifetime volume & margin)** — Ranks accounts by lifetime kg, revenue, margin and recency so reserve lots and the owner's attention flow to the highest-value, most-loyal relationships, and lapsed accounts surface automatically.
- **Buyer-facing relationship portal / offer sheet** — A branded, shareable current-offer list (available lots, varietal, process, score, price, harvest year, traceability story) that buyers can browse and request from — making Janson feel like a modern direct-trade partner, not a phone-and-email farm.
- **Origin-visit & harvest-trip CRM** — Schedules and records buyer farm visits during the season — who's coming, which lots to cup, lodging, the reserve/quetzal nature tour — so harvest hosting turns visiting cuppers into committed contracts.
- **Tasks, reminders & follow-up cadences tied to accounts** — Reuses the existing tasks engine to nudge follow-ups (sample due, contract follow-up, lapsed-account re-engage) so relationships never go cold for lack of a reminder.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Email & calendar sync — Gmail / Google Workspace API and Microsoft Graph (Outlook) for logging correspondence and scheduling visits to/from the account timeline, WhatsApp Business Platform (Cloud API) — the dominant buyer channel in Latin America; thread messages into the relationship log, HubSpot / Pipedrive / Zoho CRM — optional two-way sync or import for teams already on a generic CRM, plus a clean migration path off spreadsheets, Cropster (Origin / Cropster Hub) — link cupping scores, lot data and sample shipments so CRM feedback ties to the same lot IDs used in processing/QC, Mailchimp / Brevo (Sendinblue) — offer-sheet and harvest-update broadcasts to segmented buyer lists, Stripe + Wise / PayPal — payment-intent and invoice links for direct/online green & roasted sales and deposits on contracts, Calendly / Cal.com — buyer-bookable farm-visit and cupping-session scheduling during harvest, Best of Panama / Specialty Coffee Association (SCA) and Alliance for Coffee Excellence auction platforms — import auction-winner buyer contacts and realized lot prices into accounts, Shipping/export carriers & freight forwarders (DHL/FedEx for samples; Maersk/Flexport tracking for green container exports) to attach logistics status to a deal, Twilio (SMS/voice) for follow-up reminders and visit confirmations, QuickBooks Online / Xero — push won deals and contracts to invoicing so CRM value reflects real cash

**Data model additions:** accounts — id, name, type (importer | roaster | cafe | distributor | direct_consumer | broker), country, region, channel (direct_trade | auction | wholesale | online_retail), segment, website, currency, lifetime_kg, lifetime_revenue_usd, value_tier, status (prospect | active | lapsed | dormant), source (best_of_panama | sca_auction | referral | inbound | trade_show), owner_worker_id, established_year, notes, contacts — id, account_id (FK accounts), name, role (owner | head_roaster | green_buyer | qc | logistics), email, phone, whatsapp, preferred_channel, timezone, is_primary, interactions — id, account_id, contact_id, kind (email | call | whatsapp | visit | sample | meeting | note), at (timestamp), direction (inbound | outbound), subject, body, worker_id (logged_by), related_deal_id, related_lot_code, deals — id, account_id, stage (inquiry | sampling | negotiating | contracted | won | lost), variety, process_method, lot_code (FK lots), volume_kg, price_per_kg_usd, currency, incoterm (FOB | FCA | EXW), expected_close, harvest_year, probability_pct, owner_worker_id, samples — id, account_id, deal_id, lot_code (FK lots), grade (green | roasted), weight_g, sent_date, carrier, tracking_no, cupping_score, tasting_notes, status (requested | shipped | received | scored), contracts — id, deal_id, account_id, signed_date, volume_kg, price_per_kg_usd, incoterm, certification (FK certifications where modeled), delivery_window, deposit_usd, status (active | fulfilled | cancelled), account_visits — id, account_id, scheduled_date, attendees, lots_cupped (lot_code[]), reserve_tour (bool), outcome, follow_up_task_id, crm_tasks (or reuse tasks with account_id/deal_id FKs added) — follow-up cadences linked to accounts/deals so reminders live in the existing task engine

**AI / automation:**
- Inbound-lead triage: parse incoming emails/WhatsApp messages, auto-match or create the account, draft a context-aware reply suggesting the right available lots based on the buyer's stated varietal/volume interest
- Lapsed-account & churn-risk detection: flag accounts whose buying cadence has slipped versus their own history and surface a one-click re-engage draft with their last order and cupping notes
- Lot-allocation recommender: given scarce reserve volume and the open pipeline, rank which deals/accounts should receive limited Geisha/Pacamara by value, loyalty and win-probability
- Interaction summarization: auto-summarize long email/WhatsApp threads and farm-visit notes into a tidy timeline entry and extract action items into tasks
- Smart offer-sheet generation: assemble a personalized offer list per buyer from available inventory, weighted to their past preferences and price band, with auto-written lot story copy
- Cupping-feedback synthesis: cluster buyers' tasting notes per lot/process to advise the processing team which methods and varieties the market is rewarding (closes the loop to the processing pillar)
- Price-band guidance: suggest a price range for a deal using realized Best of Panama / auction comps and the account's history
- Next-best-action nudges per account based on stage, recency and prior outcomes

**World-class:** World-class is a single relationship spine where a salesperson opens an account and instantly sees: every interaction across email/WhatsApp/visit/auction, every sample sent with its cupping score, every lot ever bought with price and margin, the account's loyalty/value rank, and the live deals in flight — all tied to the SAME traceability lot IDs that flow from plot to harvest to processing to inventory. Reserve Geisha is allocated deliberately by relationship value, not chaos; lapsed buyers surface before they churn; offer sheets are generated and shared in one click; harvest-season farm visits convert cuppers into multi-year contracts; and forecasted revenue from the pipeline informs how the farm processes and grades the next crop. It feels like a direct-trade command center, not a Rolodex — closing the loop from the cup back to the tree.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson is an origin farm selling specialty GREEN (and some roasted) coffee — its CRM is fundamentally direct-trade and origin-centric, not retail. The crown jewels are scarce, high-value lots: micro-lot Geisha, Pacamara, and reserve auction lots that command Best of Panama / SCA-auction prices many multiples above commodity. The buyers are international importers, specialty roasters and cafés whose relationships are built on cupping scores, traceability and trust over years. The CRM must (1) tie every account to the SAME traceability lot codes (JC-541, JC-564…) that already flow through harvests and processing, so a buyer's history is lot-true; (2) treat the harvest-season farm visit — including the 200-hectare quetzal nature reserve tour, a genuine Janson differentiator — as a first-class sales motion that converts visiting cuppers into multi-year contracts; (3) capture cupping scores and tasting notes as the currency of the relationship; and (4) respect that WhatsApp and farm hospitality, not cold web forms, are how specialty-origin deals actually close in Panama. Done right it turns the family's heritage (Carl Axel Janson, 1926; founded 1990) and reserve story into a relationship asset, not just folklore on the website.


#### Specialty green-coffee marketplaces & auction platforms (e.g. Cup of Excellence)
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Janson's owner / commercial lead (the family member who decides which microlots to enter into competitions and direct-sale channels) — supported by the head of quality (Q-grader / cupping lead) who preps samples and scores, and an export/logistics coordinator who handles ICO marks, contracts, payment, and shipping once a lot sells.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When my best Geisha microlot finishes drying and I see it could compete, I want to instantly know which auctions and marketplaces (Best of Panama / SCAP, Cup of Excellence, Algrano, private auctions) it qualifies for and their deadlines, so I can enter the right channel before the submission window closes instead of missing a year of premium upside.
- When a buyer or jury cups one of my lots, I want every cupping score (CVA descriptive + affective, legacy 100-pt) and the lot's full provenance (plot, altitude, variety, process, lot code, harvest date) bound to that single lot record, so I can publish a credible, defensible quality story and justify a four-figure-per-kilo price.
- When the auction closes and I have a winning bid, I want the platform to turn that bid into a sales order with the buyer, agreed price, ICO marks, payment terms and shipping milestones tracked to completion, so I get paid in full and the green coffee actually ships without anything falling through email threads.
- When I'm deciding what to plant, pick separately, and process as a microlot next season, I want to see which of my lots historically earned the highest per-kilo auction premiums by plot/variety/process, so I steer my farm toward the coffees the world pays the most for.

**Features:**
- **Lot-to-Market catalog & channel matcher** — Promotes a finished green ProcessingBatch into a sellable GreenLot, then auto-matches it to eligible channels (Best of Panama/SCAP, Cup of Excellence Panama, Algrano listing, private/online auction) with each channel's open dates, min cup-score, lot-size and sample requirements surfaced — so no premium window is ever missed.
- **Submission & sample-tracking workspace** — One screen per competition entry: deadline countdown, required fields (altitude, variety, process, traceability), reserve-sample inventory, and shipped sample status (pre-shipment / spot / evaluation) with QR-coded sample labels — eliminating the spreadsheet scramble before every BOP/CoE deadline.
- **Cupping & CVA score ledger** — Captures SCA Coffee Value Assessment descriptive (intensity 1-15 + CATA descriptors) and affective scores plus legacy 100-pt and jury results, all bound to the GreenLot — turning each lot into a quality-defensible, contract-ready asset rather than a number in someone's notebook.
- **Live auction tracker & bid board** — Tracks each lot's auction in real time (current bid, rank, lot # like the BOP washed-Geisha block), records the winning bid and buyer, and pushes alerts — so the owner watches Janson's lots climb against the field instead of refreshing a third-party site.
- **Won-lot → sales order & export pipeline** — Converts a winning bid into a GreenSalesOrder with buyer, agreed USD/kg, ICO marks, Incoterm, payment terms and shipping milestones, with a fail-closed checklist (contract signed → payment received → export docs → shipped) so money lands and the coffee actually leaves the mill.
- **Premium analytics & next-season steering** — Ranks realized per-kilo premiums by plot, variety, process and channel across seasons, and projects revenue of a candidate microlot — so planting, separate-picking and processing decisions are driven by what the market actually rewards (e.g. natural vs washed Geisha spread).
- **Public lot offer page & buyer outreach** — Generates a branded, shareable offer sheet per lot (provenance, CVA profile, photos, reserve sample request button) and logs roaster/importer inquiries — giving Janson an Algrano-style direct-trade front door without ceding the relationship to a marketplace.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Specialty Coffee Association of Panama (SCAP) — Best of Panama competition & auction calendar/registration, Alliance for Coffee Excellence / Cup of Excellence — Panama program submission & national jury results, V-Auction (Vollers) — current CoE online auction platform; lot listing, live bids, results export, M-Cultivo — prior CoE auction platform / producer back-end lot management (legacy), Algrano — direct-trade green-coffee marketplace listings, roaster messaging, logistics, SCA Coffee Value Assessment (CVA) standards — Descriptive + Affective forms, CVA score calculator, Cropster Origin — green inventory, sample labels with QR codes, ICO/container/tracking-number sample management, lot-sale records, ICO (International Coffee Organization) marks / country-of-origin export codes, International payment & escrow rails — bank wire / SWIFT, Wise or Stripe for sample-fee and deposit collection, letters of credit for large lots, Vollers / green-coffee forwarders & freight (B/L, phytosanitary cert, certificate of origin), Trazabilidad / blockchain provenance options (e.g. SourceTrace, FoodTraze, TraceX) for tamper-proof lot history

**Data model additions:** GreenLot — a sellable finished lot promoted from ProcessingBatch: id, lotCode, plotId, variety, process, greenKg, harvestSeason, screenSize, moisturePct, defectCount, reserveSampleKg, status (available/entered/sold/shipped), SalesChannel — a marketplace/auction: id, name, kind (competition|auction|marketplace|direct), org (SCAP/ACE/Algrano/Vollers), openDate, closeDate, minCupScore, minLotKg, sampleRequirementG, feeStructure, ChannelEntry / Submission — a GreenLot entered into a SalesChannel: entry status, deadline, jury rank, qualification result, sample-shipment status + tracking #, CuppingScore — id, greenLotId, protocol (CVA-descriptive|CVA-affective|legacy-100|jury), cupper/Q-grader, date, score/intensities + CATA descriptors, notes, AuctionResult — greenLotId, channelEntryId, lotNumber, winningBidUsdPerKg, totalUsd, buyer, auctionDate, Buyer — id, name, country, type (roaster|importer|trader), contact, ICO importer ref, GreenSalesOrder — id, greenLotId, buyerId, agreedUsdPerKg, kg, incoterm, icoMarks, paymentTerms, paymentStatus, contractUrl, shipmentMilestones, OfferSheet / Listing — public lot offer doc: shareSlug, provenance snapshot, CVA profile, photos, inquiry log

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-draft the marketing offer sheet / tasting-note narrative from CVA descriptors + provenance (CATA flavors → buyer-facing copy), generated in EN/ES.
- Channel-fit recommender: rank a finished lot's best channel and project a price band from its cup score, variety, process and historical comparable auction results (e.g. Boquete/Volcan washed-Geisha curve).
- Submission deadline + sample-prep autopilot: watch every channel calendar, alert when a qualifying lot exists before a window closes, and generate the pre-filled entry packet.
- Bid intelligence: monitor live auction position and notify on rank changes / sniping windows; post-auction, summarize how Janson's lots performed vs the field.
- Quality anomaly + reserve-sample guard: flag lots whose moisture/defect/screen drift from their cup score, and warn when reserve-sample inventory is too low to fulfill buyer requests.
- Price-realization analytics narrative: explain which farm decisions (separate Geisha picking, anaerobic vs washed) drove the highest realized premiums.

**World-class:** A finished green lot at the mill flows in one click into a sellable asset with a complete, tamper-evident provenance and CVA quality profile; the system tells the owner exactly which of the world's premium channels it qualifies for and by when, files the entry and ships the reserve sample with QR-tracked labels, then tracks the live auction bid-by-bid (like watching a Janson lot climb the Best of Panama block). When it sells, the winning bid becomes a fully-tracked export order — buyer, ICO marks, payment escrow/wire, freight and phytosanitary docs — that fails closed until the producer is paid and the coffee ships. Over seasons it becomes a price-discovery engine that steers the farm toward the coffees the market pays the most for. World-class here means a small Volcán family farm operates the same caliber commercial machine as Hacienda La Esmeralda or the Lamastus estates — without a broker taking the relationship or the margin.

**Coffee-specific angle:** This is Janson's single highest-leverage commercial capability. Janson sits in Volcán, Chiriquí — the exact terroir whose washed Geisha just set the 2025 Best of Panama world record at $30,204/kg (Hacienda La Esmeralda) with an average winning price of $2,861/kg; the Lamastus estates' Elida Geisha did $10,013/kg in 2024. SCAP is even moving to trademark 'Panama Geisha.' Janson already grows Geisha, Caturra, Catuaí and Pacamara and runs its own wet/dry mill, so it can produce competition-grade washed/natural/anaerobic microlots today. The difference between selling those as commodity green and getting them onto the Best of Panama or Cup of Excellence Panama auction block — with a credible CVA profile and provenance tied to a named plot and its 200-ha quetzal reserve story — is literally one to three orders of magnitude in price per kilo. The marketplace/auction module is the bridge from 'farm that grows good coffee' to 'estate that captures specialty-auction economics.'


#### POS — farm store & agro-tourism café
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm-store / café manager ("anfitrión de finca") who runs the on-site tienda + agro-tourism café at the Janson estate — rings up retail green/roasted bags, brewed drinks, tour tickets, and merch; supervises 1-3 seasonal baristas/cashiers. Secondary personas it serves: the owner (wants the day's till to roll up into the same revenue figure the dashboard shows, instead of a hand-keyed number), the barista/cashier (fast checkout on a phone/tablet, even with spotty Volcán connectivity), and the tour guide (sells/scans tour + cupping-experience tickets).

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a tourist finishes the finca tour and wants to take coffee home, I want to ring up a 250g bag of our Geisha Natural by scanning it and tap-to-pay in USD or a foreign card, so I can capture the sale in under 30 seconds and hand them a receipt with the lot's QR traceability — turning a visit into revenue and a repeat online customer.
- When the internet drops mid-service (common at 1,500 masl in Volcán), I want the café POS to keep taking orders, printing tickets and queuing card auth offline, so I never lose a sale and everything syncs to the farm dashboard the moment connectivity returns.
- When I sell the last retail bag of a roast batch, I want inventory to decrement against the actual roasted lot it came from and warn me when a SKU drops below par, so the store never oversells and the owner sees true sellable stock tied back to the green-coffee lot it was roasted from.
- When the season ends and the owner asks 'how much did the store and café actually make, and which SKUs/varietals move?', I want one report that reconciles every till, channel and tax line into the same YTD revenue figure the operations dashboard shows, so cherry-to-cash is one continuous number instead of a guess.

**Features:**
- **Touch POS register (tablet/phone, PWA, offline-first)** — Baristas/cashiers ring sales in seconds on cheap hardware; works through Volcán connectivity drops via an offline IndexedDB queue that syncs on reconnect — zero lost sales, zero new hardware spend.
- **Product & SKU catalog with modifiers** — Retail green/roasted bags (by size/grind/roast), brewed drinks with modifiers (milk, +shot, pour-over vs espresso), pastries, merch, and gift sets — one priced catalog so every item rings consistently and reports cleanly.
- **Lot-linked retail inventory (roast-batch → bag → sale)** — Each retail bag SKU is backed by the roasted lot it came from, so a sale decrements real stock, par-level alerts prevent overselling, and the customer's bag carries the same JC-### lot code shown on the farm's traceability spine.
- **Tour, cupping & experience ticketing** — Sell and scan finca tours, Geisha cupping sessions, and reserve walks at the till or via a QR link; capacity + time-slot control so the agro-tourism side books, checks in, and reports alongside retail.
- **Tap-to-pay + multi-tender (card, cash USD, QR wallet)** — Accept international tap/chip cards, cash (Panama uses USD), and local mobile wallets on the same device — no separate card terminal, foreign tourists pay how they expect.
- **QR traceability receipt & 'scan to reorder online' link** — Every receipt prints/emails a QR that opens the lot's story (plot, altitude, varietal, process, cup score) and a one-tap reorder/ship-home link — converts an on-farm visitor into a direct e-commerce customer.
- **Cash-drawer, shift & Z-report reconciliation** — Open/close shifts, blind cash counts, tip handling, and end-of-day Z-reports that roll the till into the operations dashboard's revenue figure — the owner stops hand-keying YTD revenue.
- **Café recipe → green-inventory consumption (bar-to-bean)** — A brewed-drink recipe consumes grams of a specific roasted lot, so espresso/pour-over sales draw down the same inventory as bag sales — true cost-of-goods and 'how much of our own coffee did the café drink' visibility.
- **Loyalty / visitor CRM capture at checkout** — Capture email/phone at the till (opt-in), tag visitors by tour vs walk-in, and seed a direct-sales list — the farm store becomes a top-of-funnel for the higher-margin online channel.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Stripe Terminal (tap-to-pay on Android/iOS + WisePad/BBPOS readers; supports USD and international cards) — or Square for Retail / Square Terminal as the turnkey alternative, SumUp or Clip (clip.mx) — card readers widely used in Latin America/Panama for low-friction mobile card acceptance, Yappy (Banco General) and/or ACH Xpress QR — Panama's dominant local mobile-payment QR rails for domestic visitors, Panama DGI electronic invoicing (Factura Electrónica / CAFE via a PAC such as HKA or The Factory HKA) — legal tax-receipt compliance for retail sales in Panama, Cropster Roast / roast-batch data — to source the roasted-lot identity, roast date and cup score behind each retail SKU, Shopify or WooCommerce Storefront API — shared product catalog + the 'ship home / reorder' direct-to-consumer channel the QR receipt links to, QuickBooks Online or Xero — push daily Z-report journals (sales, tax, tips, COGS) into the books, Star Micronics / Epson TM (ESC/POS, Bluetooth/LAN) thermal receipt printers and a USB barcode/2D scanner for bag SKUs and ticket scan-in, Mailchimp or Klaviyo — sync the checkout-captured visitor CRM list for direct-sales marketing, GS1 barcodes / QR + a verifiable digital-product-passport style link for the per-bag traceability page

**Data model additions:** sales_channels — enum-like anchor (farm_store, cafe, tour_desk, online) so every transaction is attributable to a channel, products — SKU catalog: id, name, type (retail_bag | brewed | pastry | merch | ticket), variety (nullable, reuses coffee_variety), price_usd, unit, taxable, active, product_variants — size/grind/roast options on a product (250g/340g/whole-bean/ground) with their own price + barcode, retail_inventory — on-hand qty + par level per variant, linked back to the source roasted lot (roast_batch_id / lot_code) to extend the existing lots spine to sellable stock, roast_batches — roasted output keyed to a green processing_batch lot_code (roast date, roast level, cup score) so a retail bag maps to a real lot, drink_recipes — brewed-product → grams-of-roasted-lot consumption, for bar-to-bean COGS, orders (sales) — id, channel, opened_at, closed_at, cashier (worker_id), subtotal, tax, tip, total_usd, tender_type, sync_state (for offline), order_line_items — order_id, product_variant_id, qty, unit_price, lot_code (for the receipt's traceability), experiences — tour/cupping/walk products: capacity, duration, time_slots, experience_bookings — experience_id, slot, party_size, status (booked|checked_in|no_show), customer_id, shifts / cash_drawer_sessions — open/close, opening float, counted_cash, over_short, Z-report rollup feeding season_summary.ytd_revenue_usd, customers (visitor CRM) — opt-in email/phone, tags (tour|walk_in|online), consent flags, link to loyalty + online reorders, tax_lines / dgi_receipts — per-order tax breakdown and the electronic-invoice (CAFE) reference for Panama compliance

**AI / automation:**
- Demand forecasting per SKU and per day-of-week tied to the tour-booking calendar and seasonal tourist flow — tells the manager how many bags to roast/stock before a busy weekend so reserve lots don't sell out (or sit).
- Dynamic bundling / next-best-offer at checkout: 'visitors who bought the Geisha Natural also took a cupping kit' — lifts average ticket on the highest-margin items.
- Vision-assisted self-serve / fast catalog: snap a photo of a bag to auto-select the SKU, and OCR a foreign passport/ID only when needed for tax-exempt export sales — speeds the line.
- Post-visit re-engagement: an LLM drafts a personalized follow-up email referencing the exact lot story the visitor bought, with a reorder link — converts one-time tourists into direct online repeat buyers.
- Anomaly detection on shift reconciliation — flags unusual voids, discounts, or cash over/short patterns for the owner without manual audit.
- Multilingual barista copilot — instant ES/EN (and more) menu, brew-method explanations, and tasting-note descriptions for international tourists at the till.

**World-class:** A visitor finishes the reserve tour, the guide checks them in by scanning their ticket QR, and at the café the barista rings a flat white (which silently draws 18g off a named roasted Geisha lot) plus a 250g retail bag — on a $200 tablet that keeps working when the mountain Wi-Fi blips. Tap-to-pay clears an international card and the receipt's QR opens that bag's full story (Tizingal Alto plot, 1,650 masl, Natural process, 90.5 cup score) with a one-tap 'ship another to my door.' By night's close the manager hits one button: a blind cash count reconciles the drawer, an electronic DGI tax receipt is on file, COGS posts to QuickBooks, and the day's revenue lands in the exact same YTD figure the owner sees on the operations dashboard — so 'cherry to cash' is one unbroken, lot-traceable number. The store is not a side till; it's the bottom of the same funnel that started in the field and the top of the funnel for the higher-margin online channel.

**Coffee-specific angle:** This is where Janson's product narrative monetizes face-to-face. As a single-estate farm running its own wet/dry mill, Janson can sell coffee whose retail bag is provably the same JC-### lot the visitor just watched on the drying beds — closing the existing `lots → harvests → processing_batches` traceability spine all the way to a paid receipt. The crown jewel is reserve/Geisha: scarce, high-priced micro-lots (Panama Geisha commands top auction prices) sold at the gate as an experience — cupping flights, named-lot bags, allocation lists for the famous lots. The 200-hectare quetzal reserve makes the agro-tourism café a real destination, so tour/cupping ticketing is a first-class revenue line, not an afterthought. Pricing in USD (Panama's currency) plus DGI factura-electrónica compliance are non-negotiable local realities. The QR-to-reorder loop turns a once-a-year Boquete/Volcán tourist into a recurring direct buyer of the world's most sought-after coffee.


#### Quotes, contracts & e-signature
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm's commercial lead / sales owner — at Janson this is a family principal (the owner or a "comercial" sibling) who fields buyer inquiries, builds the annual green-coffee offer list, negotiates price (often as an ICE Arabica "C" differential or a fixed FOB/lb), and closes deals with importers, roasters, and direct accounts. Secondary users: the buyer on the other side (importer green trader / roaster owner — the counter-signer who must approve a sample and sign), and the farm bookkeeper/admin who turns a signed contract into a shipment + invoice and watches the unsigned/unfulfilled pipeline.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a roaster emails asking what Geisha I have this harvest and at what price, I want to spin up a branded, lot-specific quote in minutes — pulling the real lot (JC-564), variety, process, cupping score, screen size, available bags, and my FOB Volcán→port price — so I can respond same-day and look like a world-class exporter instead of typing a one-off email.
- When a buyer accepts an offer, I want to convert that quote into a proper green-coffee sales contract (GCA for US/Canada, ECF/ESCC for Europe, or my own direct-trade terms) with the correct Incoterm, lot quantities, payment terms, and a sample-approval quality clause auto-filled, so I never recreate legal boilerplate by hand or ship under a vague email.
- When the contract is ready, I want to send it for legally binding e-signature to a counterparty in another country and get it back countersigned with a tamper-evident audit trail, so a 2,000 kg Geisha deal closes in a day instead of weeks of scanned-PDF ping-pong across time zones.
- When I've sold against a sample, I want the contract conditional on the buyer's PSS/approval-against-sample sign-off and to always know which lots are committed vs still available, so I never double-sell the same 132 kg of micro-lot Geisha or ship coffee a buyer can later reject.
- When I'm allocating against a private or Best of Panama auction result, I want to issue the winning bidder a contract at the hammer price with the exact lot and bag count locked, so the record-setting Geisha sale is papered correctly the moment the auction closes.

**Features:**
- **Lot-aware Quote Builder (green-coffee offer sheet)** — Compose a branded quote in minutes by picking real sellable lots straight from the green/milled inventory (JC-541…JC-611 with variety, process, altitude, cupping score, screen, available bags); price per lb/kg as a fixed FOB or an ICE 'C' differential; quote auto-totals and renders as a Janson-branded PDF — same-day buyer response instead of ad-hoc emails.
- **Offer List / availability catalog with live commitment math** — A seasonal offer list that shows each lot's bags-available net of what's already on open quotes and signed contracts, so the Geisha micro-lots are never double-sold and the catalog is always truthful.
- **Quote → Contract conversion with standard templates** — One click turns an accepted quote into a sales contract pre-filled from GCA, ECF/ESCC, or a Janson direct-trade template — Incoterm (FOB Puerto Armuelles/Balboa, FCA, CIF), payment terms, quality/arbitration clause, lots and quantities all carried over, eliminating manual boilerplate and contract-type errors.
- **E-signature workflow with multi-party routing + audit trail** — Send the contract for legally binding signature, route to buyer then countersign, capture a tamper-evident certificate (signer identity, IP, timestamp) — cross-border deals close in a day with an enforceable record.
- **Sample-approval (PSS / approval-against-sample) gate** — Track the pre-shipment-sample / SAS condition as a first-class contract state; the contract only becomes shippable once the buyer signs off the sample, so coffee is never shipped into a rejectable deal.
- **Pricing engine: differential ↔ fixed with live ICE 'C'** — Quote and lock price as 'C ± X cents' or fix it against today's ICE Arabica futures; the system shows the implied $/lb on both sides so Janson and the buyer negotiate from the same number and fix-the-price events are logged.
- **Commerce pipeline board (quote → sent → accepted → signed → fulfilled)** — A single board of every deal with status, value, expiry, and which lots are committed — the commercial lead and bookkeeper see the whole book and chase the right deal at the right moment, with hand-off to shipment + invoice.
- **Auction-allocation contracts** — Generate a winning-bidder contract at the hammer price for Best of Panama or private-auction lots, locking the exact lot + bag count the instant the auction closes.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** DocuSign eSignature API (or Dropbox Sign / PandaDoc / SignWell — free-tier-friendly alternatives) for legally binding cross-border e-signature with audit certificate; eIDAS / ESIGN-Act compliant, GCA (Green Coffee Association) standard contract terms — used for 95%+ of US/Canada green imports, NY arbitration — as a contract template source, ECF / European Standard Contract for Coffee (ESCC 2018) — template source for European buyers, SCA Green Coffee FOB/CFR/CIF contract framework as template basis, Incoterms 2020 (ICC) as the structured shipping-terms vocabulary (FOB, FCA, CIF, CFR, DAP), ICE Futures U.S. Coffee 'C' (KC) price feed for differential/fixed pricing (Barchart / ICE market-data or a free delayed quote source), Cropster / Cropster Hub offer-list + cupping-score sync (specialty trade lingua franca) so lots carry their real Q/cupping scores into the quote, Best of Panama auction platform (bestofpanama.auction) / private-auction results import for hammer-price contracts, Currency/FX reference rate (e.g. exchangerate API) for USD-denominated FOB plus buyer-local display, PDF generation (branded Janson offer sheet + contract render) and email delivery (Resend/Postmark free tier) for sending quotes

**Data model additions:** customers / accounts (buyer org: importer, roaster, direct account; country, default Incoterm, payment terms, currency), contacts (per-customer signer/buyer people with email + signing authority), quotes (header: customer, currency, valid_until, status, pricing_basis = fixed|differential), quote_lines (links a sellable lot to bags/kg, $/lb or differential, process, score, screen) — bridges to the existing lots / processing_batches (green-stage), contracts (converted from quote: template_type = GCA|ECF|direct, incoterm, port_of_loading, payment_terms, arbitration_clause, total_value, status = draft|sent|signed|fulfilled), contract_lines (committed lot allocations — the source of 'bags committed' that nets against availability), signatures / signature_events (envelope_id, signer, role, status, signed_at, ip, audit_certificate_url), sample_approvals (PSS/SAS state per contract or line: sample_sent, approved|rejected, decided_at), price_fixings (for differential deals: ICE 'C' reference, differential_cents, fixed_price, fixed_at), lot_availability (derived view: bags_total − committed_on_open_quotes − committed_on_signed_contracts)

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-draft a quote from an inbound buyer email — parse 'do you have washed Geisha around 88+ for fall shipment', match it to available lots, and pre-fill a draft offer for the commercial lead to approve.
- Suggested pricing: recommend a $/lb or 'C' differential per lot from the lot's cupping score, variety, altitude, recent comparable Janson sales, and Best-of-Panama/market comps — with a confidence band.
- Contract clause assistant: pick the right template (GCA vs ECF vs direct) from the buyer's country and flag clauses that deviate from Janson's standard terms before signing.
- Pipeline nudges: flag quotes nearing expiry, contracts sent-but-unsigned past N days, and signed-but-unfulfilled deals — and draft the follow-up message.
- Risk/availability guard: warn before sending a quote that would over-commit a lot beyond available bags, and detect double-sold micro-lots across open quotes.
- Demand forecast: from historical signed contracts, predict which varieties/processes will sell out and recommend what to put on the offer list first.

**World-class:** World-class here is a deal closing the same day a buyer asks: the commercial lead picks real lots from live green inventory, the system proposes a defensible price (fixed or ICE-'C' differential), renders a Janson-branded offer, and on acceptance auto-assembles the correct standard contract (GCA / ECF / direct-trade) with Incoterms 2020 terms and a sample-approval clause — sent for legally binding, eIDAS/ESIGN e-signature with a tamper-evident audit trail, countersigned across continents in hours. Inventory commitment is always truthful (no double-sold micro-lots), pricing is transparent to both sides, and the signed contract flows straight into shipment + invoicing with full lot-level traceability back to the plot and picker. It is the operating layer of a $13,000/lb-caliber Geisha business, not a folder of scanned PDFs.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson sells the highest-stakes coffee in the world: Panama Geisha that clears five figures per pound at the Best of Panama auction, where a single 132 kg micro-lot can be the most valuable inventory on the farm. That makes contracts and e-signature mission-critical, not back-office: buyers are importers and roasters across the US, Europe, and Asia, so deals are cross-border, sample-conditional (PSS/approval-against-sample), and priced either as a fixed FOB or as an ICE 'C' differential. The sellable unit maps cleanly onto the system's existing lot codes (JC-541…JC-611) and processing batches at the green/milled stage — a quote line IS a green lot — so commerce inherits Janson's tree-to-cup traceability (plot, altitude, picker, process) as a selling point. The 'Panama Geisha' trademark and reserve/quetzal story become contract-attached provenance that justifies the premium and protects the brand.


### Brand & Marketing

#### Marketing automation & email/SMS campaigns
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the Janson commercial / brand lead (a family member or hired sales-and-marketing manager) who owns relationships with green-coffee buyers, the direct-to-consumer roasted shop, and reserve/agritourism inquiries. Secondary users: the green-sales rep (B2B roaster/importer outreach), and the farm storyteller/agronomist who supplies the origin content (harvest photos, cupping scores, lot stories) that the automations send.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a new microlot finishes processing and gets its cupping score, I want every roaster and importer who has ever bought or sampled that variety to automatically get a personalized offer with the lot's traceability story, so I can sell out high-value Geisha lots fast before they age — without me hand-writing 60 emails.
- When a DTC customer's last bag of roasted coffee is about to run out (based on their order date and bag size), I want an automated replenishment email/SMS to nudge a reorder or subscription, so I can turn one-time buyers into repeat revenue at near-zero marginal cost.
- When someone requests a green-coffee sample, books a reserve tour, or abandons a cart on the shop, I want them dropped into the right nurture sequence automatically (sample-follow-up, pre-arrival tour info, win-back), so no warm lead goes cold while we're busy in harvest season.
- When I plan a campaign, I want to segment contacts by buyer type, variety affinity, lifetime value, country, and certification interest, so I can send the right story (a Geisha auction lot vs. an everyday Caturra blend vs. a quetzal-reserve eco-stay) to the right audience and prove which campaigns drove sales.

**Features:**
- **Unified contact CRM + segmentation engine** — One contact record per buyer/consumer/lead with tags (buyer_type, variety affinity, country, LTV, certifications) and saved dynamic segments — so every send targets the right audience instead of one undifferentiated blast
- **Lot-launch broadcast composer** — Pick a finished processing lot (auto-pulls variety, process, cupping score, harvest date, traceability lot code, photos) and fire a templated 'new microlot available' email/SMS to a matched segment — turns the moment a lot hits 'green' into a same-day sales push
- **Lifecycle / replenishment flows (visual automation builder)** — Trigger-based journeys: DTC reorder reminder timed to bag depletion, post-sample B2B follow-up, abandoned-cart recovery, tour pre-arrival + post-visit review request, harvest-season win-back — recurring revenue and lead conversion running on autopilot
- **Campaign calendar + A/B testing + UTM attribution** — Schedule sends around the harvest/auction calendar, A/B subject lines, and tie opens/clicks/orders back to revenue per campaign and per lot — so marketing spend and effort is measured, not guessed
- **Double opt-in, consent & suppression manager** — Per-channel consent capture, country-aware opt-in (GDPR/CAN-SPAM/Panama), unsubscribe + hard-bounce suppression, and quiet-hours for SMS — keeps sender reputation and deliverability high and the brand legally clean across international buyers
- **Transactional + order-event hooks** — Order confirmations, shipment/tracking notices, sample-dispatch alerts, and subscription receipts sent on data events — one messaging layer covers both marketing and operational comms

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Klaviyo (email + SMS, segmentation, flows — the specialty-coffee DTC standard; Shopify-native) OR open-source Listmonk on a free tier as the $0 alternative, Resend or Amazon SES (transactional email API with generous/free tiers; React Email for templating), Twilio / Twilio SendGrid (SMS + WhatsApp Business API — WhatsApp is the dominant B2B channel in Latin America), Shopify Storefront + Webhooks / Recharge (DTC roasted-coffee store, subscriptions, order + abandoned-cart events feeding flows), Stripe (payment + subscription events for receipts and LTV calculation), Cropster (pull cupping scores and lot/quality data so lot-launch emails carry real cupping notes), Meta Conversions API + Google Ads Customer Match (push segments to paid retargeting/lookalike audiences), Anthropic Claude API (AI copy + subject-line generation, lot-story drafting, send-time and segment suggestions), DNS/email auth standards: SPF, DKIM, DMARC, and one-click List-Unsubscribe (RFC 8058) for deliverability

**Data model additions:** contacts (id, type: green_buyer | dtc_customer | reserve_lead | press, name, email, phone, whatsapp, country, company, lifetime_value_usd, variety_affinity[], created_at), contact_tags / segments (saved dynamic + static audiences with filter rules), consents (contact_id, channel: email|sms|whatsapp, status, opted_in_at, source, ip, locale) — per-channel opt-in/suppression ledger, campaigns (id, name, channel, subject, body_template, segment_id, scheduled_at, status, ab_variant), flows / automations (id, trigger_type: lot_green|order_placed|sample_sent|cart_abandoned|tour_booked, steps[], delays, conditions), messages / sends (id, campaign_id|flow_id, contact_id, channel, status: queued|sent|delivered|opened|clicked|bounced|unsub, provider_message_id, sent_at), lot_offers (lot_code → contacts/segment mapping linking a processing_batches lot to the campaign that sold it, for revenue attribution), subscriptions (contact_id, product, cadence, next_ship_date, status) for DTC replenishment

**AI / automation:**
- Generate on-brand lot-launch copy and SMS/WhatsApp variants automatically from a finished batch's data (variety, process, cupping score, traceability code, harvest date, plot story)
- AI subject-line and send-time optimization per segment to lift open and click rates
- Predictive segmentation: surface 'roasters most likely to buy this Natural Geisha' and 'DTC customers due to churn' from order + sample history
- Auto-classify and route inbound replies/leads (sample request vs. tour inquiry vs. wholesale pricing) into the right flow
- Replenishment timing model that estimates when each DTC customer's bag runs out (bag size + brew habits) and times the reorder nudge
- Multilingual generation (EN/ES) so the same campaign ships to international roasters and Latin-American WhatsApp buyers without manual translation

**World-class:** World-class is the Klaviyo/Shopify retention playbook adapted to a single-origin farm: every contact has a living profile fed by real order, sample, and tour data; lots launch to perfectly-matched segments the same day they finish processing; replenishment and win-back flows quietly compound DTC revenue; B2B sample follow-ups never slip; and the whole thing is measured campaign-by-campaign and lot-by-lot with revenue attribution. Deliverability is engineered (authenticated domain, double opt-in, suppression hygiene, >99% inbox placement), consent is country-aware, and AI drafts on-brand copy from the farm's own origin data so a two-person family team punches like a 20-person DTC growth org.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson is a famous origin name resold by dozens of roasters worldwide (Klatch, Bean & Bean, Roastmasters) — yet today the marketing motion is third-party and email-by-hand. The killer move is selling the *story plus the lot* directly: the system already tracks each lot from plot → harvest (variety, Brix, ripeness) → processing (Washed/Natural/Honey/Anaerobic, cupping score) with a traceability lot code, so a 'new Geisha microlot, 89.5 cupping, Los Alpes, Natural' email can fire to matched buyers the moment that batch hits the 'green' stage — no other generic marketing tool has the origin data baked in. The reserve adds a second, premium channel: quetzal-habitat eco-stays and farm tours marketed to the same coffee-lover audience, and a 'reserve a Geisha auction lot' VIP flow for top buyers. Geisha's auction-grade scarcity makes timely, segmented launches worth real money per send.


#### Origin storytelling & content — farm-to-cup narrative, per-lot stories
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the Janson family brand owner / direct-trade sales lead (a son running the brand side) who sells green + roasted coffee and must convince specialty buyers, roasters, and end consumers that a JC lot is worth a premium. Secondary contributors who feed the story: the agronomist/farm manager (plot + cultivation truth), the mill/processing lead (fermentation + drying detail), and the harvest supervisor (picking crew + brix). Tertiary consumers of the output: the specialty green buyer doing due diligence, the roaster building a menu card, and the end consumer scanning a bag.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a specialty buyer or roaster is evaluating a JC micro-lot against a dozen other Panama offerings, I want to hand them one credible, data-backed origin story per lot (plot, altitude, variety, picking date, brix, exact process, days on the bed, cup score), so I can justify a premium price instead of competing on commodity terms.
- When we ship a green lot or bag a roast, I want every JC lot code to resolve to a public farm-to-cup page reachable by QR on the bag and packing list, so the buyer's customers can verify provenance themselves and the story travels all the way to the cup without me re-telling it.
- When the EUDR/Digital-Product-Passport deadline hits and a buyer demands proof of farm geolocation and a no-deforestation, no-child-labor narrative, I want the same lot record that powers our marketing story to also emit the compliance dossier, so one source of truth serves both selling and regulation rather than maintaining two.
- When I'm preparing a harvest-season campaign or a competition submission (e.g., Best of Panama / a Geisha auction), I want to assemble a rich lot dossier — photos, video, terroir, processing protocol, sensory notes, the 200-ha quetzal reserve angle — from data already captured in operations, so I produce world-class content in an afternoon, not weeks of chasing facts.

**Features:**
- **Per-lot Story Builder (lot dossier composer)** — Auto-drafts a structured origin story for any JC lot code by pulling the already-linked operational truth — plot/altitude/variety/established year, picking crew + brix + ripeness, process method, drying patio + days-on-bed + green moisture — into an editable narrative + fact-sheet, turning weeks of fact-chasing into an afternoon and guaranteeing the story matches reality.
- **Public farm-to-cup lot page + QR** — Every lot code resolves to a beautiful public /lot/JC-564 page (terroir, process timeline, crew, cup score, photos/video, reserve story) addressable by a printable QR for bags and packing lists; the story travels to the end consumer and buyers verify provenance themselves, supporting premium pricing.
- **Media library tied to lot + plot + harvest** — A tagged asset store (photos, video, drone/canopy shots, mill clips, quetzal/reserve footage) linked to plots, harvest days, and process batches so the right imagery is one click from any story — no hunting through camera rolls; every page is visual and on-brand.
- **Provenance & sustainability dossier (EUDR / DPP export)** — One-click export of a lot's geolocation polygon, no-deforestation attestation, certifications, and chain-of-custody as an EUDR due-diligence statement / Digital Product Passport, so the same record that sells the lot also clears 30-June-2026 SME compliance — one source of truth, not two.
- **Sensory / cupping record per lot** — Captures SCA cupping scores, descriptors, and Q-grader notes against the lot so the story carries a credible 85+ score and flavor wheel that buyers trust, not marketing adjectives.
- **Buyer-facing offer sheet & spec sheet generator** — Renders a branded, printable/email-ready lot offer sheet (available kg, price, screen size, moisture, process, story snippet, QR) so the sales lead sends a roaster-grade spec in seconds and closes direct-trade deals faster.
- **Brand story CMS (heritage + reserve narrative blocks)** — Reusable, curated narrative blocks (1926 Carl Axel Janson arrival, 1990 founding, 200-ha quetzal reserve, terroir) that drop into any lot page, keeping the heritage and conservation story consistent and on-brand across every lot without copy-paste drift.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Cropster Origin (lot traceability, green grading, cupping data import/export), EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) due-diligence statement format + EU TRACES geolocation polygon submission, Digital Product Passport under ESPR (serialized QR / GS1 Digital Link unique product identifier), GS1 Digital Link standard for the QR-to-page resolver, OpenSC / blockchain provenance attestation (optional verifiable chain-of-custody), SCA cupping protocol + Coffee Value Assessment (CVA) descriptors / flavor wheel taxonomy, Best of Panama / Specialty Coffee Association of Panama (SCAP) auction lot submission format, Certification bodies: Rainforest Alliance, Fair Trade, Bird Friendly (Smithsonian) — relevant to the quetzal reserve angle, Cloudflare R2 / Supabase Storage (free-tier object storage for the media library), Instagram Graph API / Meta + a Shopify or WooCommerce storefront for syndicating lot stories to social and e-commerce, QR generation (qrcode library, client-side, $0) + open-meteo for terroir/weather context on the story page, What3words or plain WGS84 lat/long for human-readable plot geolocation on the public map

**Data model additions:** lot_stories — one narrative record per lot_code (headline, terroir paragraph, process narrative, status: draft/published, published_at, slug); FK to lots(code), story_media (media_assets) — id, lot_code/plot_id/harvest_id refs, type (photo/video/drone), url, caption, credit, sort_order; the tagged asset library, cupping_scores — id, lot_code, cupper, score, protocol (SCA/CVA), descriptors[], aroma/acidity/body/aftertaste sub-scores, cupped_at, lot_provenance — lot_code, geo_polygon (GeoJSON), gps_lat/lng, deforestation_attestation, harvest_window, eudr_statement_ref, dpp_id, certifications — id, body (Rainforest Alliance/Fair Trade/Bird Friendly), cert_number, valid_from/to; linked to plots and/or lots, offer_sheets — id, lot_code, available_kg, price_per_lb, screen_size, packaging, generated_at (derived/printable artifact record), brand_story_blocks — id, key (heritage-1926/founding-1990/reserve-200ha/terroir), title, body, locale — reusable CMS content, lots table extension — add cup_score, process_protocol_notes, story_slug to the existing lots spine so the story layer hangs off current traceability without forking it

**AI / automation:**
- Draft lot narrative from structured ops data — given plot/harvest/process rows, generate an on-brand origin-story draft and tasting copy (owner edits, never auto-publishes), collapsing content production time
- Auto-translate every lot page (EN/ES + buyer-market languages) so the story reaches Japanese, Korean, and EU specialty buyers without manual translation
- Tasting-note generator from cupping descriptors — turn raw Q-grader sub-scores into consumer-readable flavor copy and a suggested flavor-wheel graphic
- Photo auto-tagging + best-shot selection — vision model tags media by subject (cherry, drying bed, quetzal, crew) and surfaces the strongest hero image per lot
- Story-completeness / provenance gap detector — flags lots missing geolocation, cupping, or photos before publish, and warns when narrative claims (altitude, variety, process) drift from the operational record
- Campaign + social copy spinner — recompose a published lot story into Instagram captions, an offer-sheet blurb, and a competition submission abstract from one source

**World-class:** World-class is when the JC lot code is the single hyperlink that unifies operations, marketing, and compliance: a buyer in Tokyo scans the QR on a bag of JC-564, lands on a fast, cinematic, multilingual page showing the exact plot on a map at 1,520 masl, the Pacamara trees, the crew that picked it on a named date at 19.4 brix, the 14-day natural drying curve, an 88.5 SCA score with its flavor wheel, drone footage of the 200-ha quetzal reserve above the lot — and the same record one-click-exports the EUDR geolocation polygon and Digital Product Passport. Nothing is re-keyed; the story is a presentation of operational truth, so it is always accurate, never marketing fiction. It matches or beats what Cropster Origin + a bespoke agency site deliver, built on free tooling, and it makes provenance a sales weapon rather than a compliance chore.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson is unusually story-rich and that story is currently undersold: a 1926 Swedish-immigrant heritage (Carl Axel Janson), a 1990 family founding, a 200-hectare private quetzal-habitat reserve, and trophy varieties (Geisha, Pacamara) at 1,350–1,700 masl — exactly the terroir + conservation + heritage triad that wins Best of Panama auctions and commands Geisha-tier prices. The killer angle is that the existing lot-code spine (JC-541, JC-552, JC-564...) already threads plot → harvest → process, so a per-lot story needs almost no new data capture — it is a presentation layer over operations. And the EUDR moment (SME deadline 30 June 2026, days away) plus Digital Product Passports turn the bird-friendly reserve and farm-plot geolocation from a feel-good narrative into a hard compliance asset — Janson can lead Panama on verifiable, bird-friendly, deforestation-free provenance while every page doubles as a quetzal-conservation marketing story.


#### Social media management & user-generated content
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Brand & Content Lead at Janson Coffee — a member of the founding family (or a hired marketing coordinator reporting to them) who runs @jansoncoffee across Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, and YouTube, fields DMs from roasters and consumers, and is frequently on the farm with a phone during harvest. Secondary users: the agronomist/mill operator capturing raw field moments (without being a "marketer"), and the family owner who approves brand-sensitive posts and reviews monthly reach/sales attribution.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When peak harvest is happening and I'm physically on the farm at 1,600 masl with patchy signal, I want to capture a cherry-picking or fermentation moment and queue it to post later, so I can build an authentic origin story without breaking my workflow or losing the moment.
- When a roaster or home customer tags @jansoncoffee in a brew shot or latte-art reel of our Geisha, I want to discover it, request rights in one tap, and republish it into a shoppable gallery, so I can turn genuine customer love into proof-of-quality content without legal risk.
- When I'm planning content around a real farm event (Best of Panama lot reveal, first-pick of the season, quetzal sighting in the reserve, a new processing experiment), I want to schedule a coordinated multi-platform campaign tied to that event, so I can drive demand and tell our terroir story consistently across channels.
- When the owner asks 'is the social media actually selling coffee?', I want one dashboard linking posts to reach, engagement, link-in-bio clicks, and store/auction inquiries, so I can prove which stories move green-coffee and roasted-bag sales and double down on what works.

**Features:**
- **Field Capture & Draft Inbox (offline-first)** — Anyone on the farm snaps a photo/clip, tags the plot/lot/varietal, and queues it offline; it auto-syncs into a Drafts inbox when signal returns — so authentic origin content is never lost to bad connectivity.
- **Unified Multi-Platform Scheduler & Calendar** — Compose once and publish/schedule to Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, and YouTube via a single API, with per-platform aspect-ratio previews — eliminating manual cross-posting and keeping a consistent cadence between harvest seasons.
- **UGC Discovery, Rights-Request & Shoppable Gallery** — Auto-listens for @mentions, tags, and brand hashtags, sends a one-tap rights-approval request, stores the consent record, and surfaces approved customer content in a curated shoppable wall embeddable on jansoncoffee.com — turning roaster/consumer love into legally clean social proof that drives sales.
- **Event-Linked Campaign Planner** — Ties content plans to real farm-ops events (first pick, Best of Panama lot, processing experiment, reserve wildlife) pulled from the unified operations data, so marketing fires in lockstep with what is actually happening on the farm — turning a lot code into a launch.
- **Lot-to-Story Linking** — Connects each post/asset to a specific plot, lot code, or processing batch so a buyer scanning the feed (or a QR on a bag) can trace the exact story behind their coffee — making traceability a marketing asset, not just a compliance file.
- **Unified Social Inbox** — One inbox for comments and DMs across platforms with templated replies for common roaster/buyer questions (availability, cupping scores, MOQ, shipping) so inbound interest converts instead of going cold.
- **Marketing Performance & Sales-Attribution Dashboard** — Liquid-glass dashboard tying posts to reach, engagement, link clicks, and downstream store/auction inquiries — answering the owner's 'is this selling coffee?' with per-story ROI evidence.
- **Brand Asset Library & Approval Workflow** — Central tagged media library (by varietal, plot, season, person) plus an owner approval gate for brand-sensitive posts — protecting the premium Geisha/heritage brand while letting field staff contribute raw material.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Meta Graph API (Instagram + Facebook publishing, mentions, comment/DM webhooks) — free, native to the two primary channels, TikTok Content Posting API + Display API for publishing and engagement, YouTube Data API v3 for Shorts and long-form farm films, Unified publishing API to avoid maintaining each SDK: Ayrshare, Postiz (open-source, self-hostable for $0), or Postproxy/Late per the $0 constraint, UGC rights & shoppable-feed tooling: TINT or Bazaarvoice (enterprise) — modeled at small scale via Meta's mentions API plus a self-built consent record; Foursixty/Pixlee patterns for the shoppable gallery, Link-in-bio + click attribution: self-hosted /go redirect with UTM tagging (Shlink, open-source) or Linktree API, Canva Connect API for on-brand template generation from raw field assets, Cloudflare R2 or Supabase Storage free tier for the media asset library, WhatsApp Business Cloud API (free tier) — the real inbound channel for Latin American buyers and roasters, Best of Panama / SCAP auction (bestofpanama.auction) as an event/data source to time lot-reveal campaigns

**Data model additions:** social_account (platform, handle, oauth token ref, status), content_asset (media url, type, captured_by_worker_id, plot_id, lot_code, batch_id, geo, captured_at, tags[], approval_status), social_post (asset_ids[], caption, platforms[], scheduled_at, published_at, status, campaign_id, linked lot_code/plot_id), campaign (name, linked_event, start/end, goal, theme e.g. 'Best of Panama 2026 Geisha'), ugc_item (source_platform, author_handle, source_url, media, brand_mention, rights_status, consent_record_url, approved_for_shop), social_engagement (post_id or thread_id, type comment|dm|mention, author, text, replied, sentiment), social_metric (post_id, platform, reach, impressions, likes, comments, saves, link_clicks, captured_at), attribution_event (link click / inquiry -> optional order or auction-lot reference)

**AI / automation:**
- AI caption & hashtag generation in Janson's brand voice from a photo plus its lot/varietal/plot metadata (Geisha, Washed, 1,600 masl) — drafts written, human approves
- Auto-tagging of uploaded media (varietal, scene type: cherries/fermentation/drying/reserve/people) via a vision model so the asset library self-organizes
- Best-time-to-post and cadence recommendations from historical engagement and audience geography (US/EU/Asia roaster time zones)
- Sentiment + intent triage on incoming DMs/comments to flag genuine buyer leads vs. fan comments and suggest templated replies
- UGC quality/brand-fit scoring to auto-surface the strongest customer content worth requesting rights for
- Auto-generate a per-lot 'story card' (capture highlights + cupping notes + processing) as a Reel or QR landing-page draft
- CRITICAL injection invariant (carried from house rules): untrusted public comment/DM text can never trigger an autonomous action — AI drafts replies, a human sends; the brand voice acts as the owner, never as a puppet of commenters

**World-class:** World-class here is the origin story told end-to-end and made shoppable: every public post is traceable to a real plot, lot, and person on the farm; field staff contribute raw moments offline-first without being marketers; customer brew shots flow through one-tap rights approval into a legally clean shoppable wall; campaigns fire automatically off real farm events (first pick, a Best of Panama lot reveal, a quetzal sighting in the reserve); AI drafts on-brand captions a human approves in seconds; and one dashboard proves which stories actually sold green and roasted coffee. It feels like Hacienda La Esmeralda / Ninety Plus-tier brand polish, but run by a 3-person family team for effectively $0, with the operations system itself as the content engine.
