**Coffee-specific angle:** For Janson the social feed is the bridge between a $30k/kg-tier Geisha origin and the buyer — most green-coffee and reserve-lot sales start as a relationship, and that relationship now begins on Instagram/WhatsApp. The unique unlock is that Janson already owns the full chain (grow -> harvest -> wet/dry mill -> green/roasted sale) AND a 200-hectare quetzal reserve, so the content is genuinely scarce and unfakeable: anaerobic fermentation experiments, cherry brix at picking, the Swedish 1926->1990 family heritage, terroir at 1,350-1,700 masl, and conservation/biodiversity storytelling that doubles as an ESG narrative. Tying posts to lot codes turns the marketing feed into a traceability and provenance asset, and timing campaigns to the Best of Panama auction (where Panama Geisha sets world records) converts a sourcing event into a demand event. No generic CPG brand can replicate this; it is the farm's own operational reality, published.


#### Brand website, SEO & web analytics
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Brand & marketing lead / farm owner (the Janson family member who tells the story and sells the lots) — supported by an agronomy/QC liaison who feeds real lot data into public content. On a small specialty farm this is often one or two people wearing the "voice of the brand" hat, not a marketing department.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a Specialty buyer or auction bidder is comparing Panama Geisha lots before Best of Panama, I want to point them to a credible, beautiful page that proves our terroir, altitude, varietal and processing with real farm data, so I can command a premium price instead of competing on a generic brochure.
- When a roaster or curious consumer Googles 'Panama Geisha farm Volcán' or scans the QR on a bag, I want our site to rank, load fast, and open a verifiable lot story, so I can capture demand that today leaks to Hacienda La Esmeralda and other better-marketed estates.
- When I publish a new harvest, reserve lot, or auction announcement, I want to update the public site in minutes from the same operations data I already track, so I can keep the brand current without a web developer or a broken Instagram embed.
- When I report to the family on what marketing actually drives inquiries and sales, I want one dashboard tying web traffic and SEO rank to wholesale quote requests and lot reservations, so I can invest in what converts instead of guessing.

**Features:**
- **Lot Passport pages (data-driven, public)** — Auto-generates a public, shareable page per lot from the SAME operations data (plot altitude/varietal/shade, harvest pick dates + Brix + ripeness, processing method/stage, cup score) — turns provenance into a sales asset and replaces guesswork brochure copy with verifiable proof that justifies Geisha-tier pricing.
- **QR lot-traceability landing (bag-to-story)** — Each lotCode resolves to a scannable QR on bags/auction sheets that opens the Lot Passport with map, photos and a 'farm to cup' timeline — gives roasters and consumers instant provenance, the #1 trust driver in specialty, and pulls scan traffic into analytics.
- **Headless CMS + brand story modules** — Editable heritage story (Carl Axel Janson 1926 → 1990), reserve, awards, and farm-visit/agritourism pages without a developer — kills the stale-site problem (broken Instagram feed) and lets the family ship content in minutes.
- **Technical-SEO + structured-data engine** — Server-rendered JSON-LD (Organization, Product/Offer for lots, LocalBusiness for the farm cafe, Article for blog, FAQ), sitemap, canonical + hreflang, fast Core Web Vitals — wins 'Panama Geisha farm' / 'Volcán coffee' search and AI-overview citations that today go to better-optimized estates.
- **Bilingual ES/EN content (hreflang)** — First-class Spanish and English with proper hreflang pairing — serves both Panama clients ('Clientes en Panamá') and the global buyer market the current English-mostly site half-ignores.
- **Unified web analytics dashboard** — Privacy-friendly analytics (no cookie banner) plus Search Console rank data surfaced INSIDE Farm Operations and tied to inquiry/lot-reservation events — one place to see what marketing drives revenue, replacing zero current measurement.
- **Wholesale-inquiry & lot-reservation funnel** — Replaces the broken 'No Products Selected!' quote form with a working RFQ + reserve-this-lot flow that writes a lead into the unified model and notifies the owner — converts the traffic SEO brings instead of dropping it.
- **Auction / harvest announcement campaigns** — Newsletter + social-card generator tied to lot events (new harvest, Best of Panama entry, reserve release) — turns the operational calendar into timely marketing pushes the farm cannot send today (no newsletter, broken social).

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Headless/Git CMS (Sanity, Contentful, or Decap/TinaCMS for a $0 git-backed option), Next.js on Vercel (free tier) for ISR-rendered SEO pages + edge image optimization, Google Search Console API (rank, impressions, CTR, indexing) + Bing Webmaster Tools, Plausible or Umami (privacy-first, cookieless analytics — self-host for $0) or GA4, Schema.org JSON-LD: Organization, Product, Offer, LocalBusiness, Article, FAQPage, BreadcrumbList, Google Business Profile (the farm cafe with volcano views — local + Maps discovery), Resend / Mailchimp / Buttondown for newsletter and auction announcements, Meta Graph API + Instagram Basic Display (fix the broken feed) and social-card OG image generation (Vercel OG / Satori), QR generation (qrcode lib) resolving lotCode → public passport URL, Stripe Payment Links / Shopify Lite or BigCommerce B2B for paid micro-lots / sample packs (free-tier-friendly start), Cloudinary or Vercel image CDN for farm photography; sitemap + robots + IndexNow ping, Best of Panama / SCAP lot-listing and the trademarked 'Panama Geisha' provenance standard (link out + structured claims)

**Data model additions:** MarketingPage (slug, locale, title, meta_description, og_image, status, content_blocks, schema_type), LotPassport (lotCode FK → Harvest/ProcessingBatch, public_slug, hero_media[], cup_score, processing_notes, published, qr_url), SeoMetadata (entity_type, entity_id, canonical_url, hreflang_map, json_ld payload, indexed_at), WebAnalyticsDaily (date, page_path, locale, source/medium, visits, scans_from_qr, avg_engagement, conversions), SearchRanking (query, page, country, position, impressions, clicks, ctr, captured_at), MarketingLead (kind: wholesale-rfq | lot-reservation | newsletter, name, country, lot_interest FK, message, status, source_page), NewsletterSubscriber (email, locale, consent_at, segments[], source), CampaignEvent (type: harvest | auction-entry | reserve-release, lotCode, channels[], sent_at, opens, clicks), MediaAsset (url, alt_text, locale_alt_map, plot_id?, lot_id?, license, credit)

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-draft a Lot Passport narrative from structured ops data (altitude, varietal, shade %, Brix, ripeness, process, cup score) — human-edited, never auto-published, in EN + ES.
- AI SEO assistant: generate meta titles/descriptions, alt text, and JSON-LD from page content; flag missing/duplicate metadata and thin pages.
- Machine translation (EN↔ES) with human review for hreflang content parity.
- AI image alt-text + auto-tagging of farm photography for accessibility and image SEO.
- Insight summaries over analytics + Search Console: 'which queries are rising', 'which lot pages convert', 'what to publish next' weekly digest to the owner.
- Generate social/OG cards and newsletter copy from a lot/harvest event with on-brand tone and palette.
- Answer-engine optimization: structure FAQ/lot data so AI Overviews and assistants cite Janson for 'Panama Geisha farm' queries.

**World-class:** "A buyer Googles 'Volcán Panama Geisha reserve', lands on a sub-1s, bilingual page that ranks #1 and is cited in AI Overviews; the page IS the live operations data — this exact lot's plot, altitude, pick dates, Brix, process and cup score, with a map and a farm-to-cup timeline — not marketing prose. They scan the QR on the bag and get the same verifiable passport. The family updates it from the ops app in minutes; an analytics dashboard inside Farm Operations ties every search query and QR scan to wholesale RFQs and lot reservations, closing the loop from terroir to revenue. Provenance is the product, and the website is its proof — the same league as Hacienda La Esmeralda's storytelling, but generated from real data rather than hand-built."

**Coffee-specific angle:** "Panama Geisha is the most expensive coffee on earth — Best of Panama 2025 hit a record $30,204/kg for a washed Geisha, and SCAP is trademarking 'Panama Geisha' as a luxury provenance mark. At that tier the website is not a brochure, it is the price justification: terroir (1,350–1,700 masl, Volcán/Chiriquí), the 200-hectare quetzal-habitat reserve as a biodiversity/shade-grown story, the 1926 Swedish-immigrant heritage, and per-lot processing/cup data are exactly what auction buyers and reserve roasters pay for. Janson's current site leaks all of this (broken Instagram, dead quote form, no blog, English-only, weak SEO) precisely when its category is breaking world records — making the brand-website pillar unusually high-leverage for this specific farm."


#### Loyalty & referral programs
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the DTC growth / brand owner at Janson Coffee (likely a family member who runs the jansoncoffee.com store and the green-coffee buyer relationships). Secondary personas the module serves: (1) the repeat retail customer / coffee subscriber who loves Janson Geisha and wants to bring friends in; (2) the wholesale roaster / green buyer who reorders specific lots season after season and could refer peer roasters; (3) the farm-visit / origin-tourism guest who became a fan on-site and wants to stay connected after leaving Volcán.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When my best Geisha customers reorder every season anyway, I want to formally reward their loyalty with tier perks (early access to the new harvest, reserve-lot allocations, free origin tour), so I can lock in repeat revenue without discounting my scarcest coffee into the ground.
- When a happy customer is about to share Janson with a friend, I want to hand them a one-tap referral link that gives the friend a real incentive (50% off first bag) and credits them back, so I can acquire vetted, higher-LTV customers at near-zero CAC instead of paying ad platforms.
- When a wholesale roaster reorders the same washed lot they bought last year, I want to recognize their cumulative volume and refer-a-roaster activity with allocation priority and freight credits, so I can deepen direct-trade relationships and keep my best lots flowing to people who showcase them well.
- When a guest finishes a farm tour or cupping at the reserve, I want to enroll them on the spot and tie their future purchases + tree adoption to that visit, so the origin experience converts into a lifelong member instead of a one-time memory.

**Features:**
- **Tiered membership program (Bean → Reserve → Founder)** — Points and lifetime-spend tiers unlock concrete, scarcity-backed perks (early harvest access, reserve-lot allocation rights, free shipping, an annual origin-tour credit) that reward loyalty without eroding price on a $30k/kg-ceiling Geisha.
- **Double-sided referral engine with unique links/codes** — Give-50%-off-first-bag / get-coffee-credit mechanics with attributed share links, fraud guards, and per-customer dashboards — turns fans into a low-CAC, high-LTV acquisition channel (referred customers convert ~1.5pts higher and churn less).
- **Adopt-a-Tree / Adopt-a-Lot patronage program** — Customers (and corporate sponsors) adopt a named tree or reserve a share of a specific lot/plot; each adoption funds a second tree planted in the 200ha reserve and returns an annual harvest box traced to that exact plot — a recurring, emotionally sticky revenue line unique to a real farm with a quetzal-habitat reserve.
- **Reserve-lot allocation & pre-sale waitlist** — Tier-gated first-right-of-refusal queue for scarce micro-lots and Best-of-Panama-style auction lots, with deposit/pre-order holds — converts scarcity into committed forward revenue and rewards the most loyal buyers with access money can't otherwise buy.
- **Wholesale loyalty (refer-a-roaster + volume allocation tiers)** — B2B mirror of the consumer program: cumulative-volume tiers grant allocation priority and freight/sample credits, and a refer-a-roaster flow rewards introductions to peer roasters — strengthens direct-trade stickiness on the green side, the farm's biggest revenue.
- **Member portal with traceable rewards ledger** — A single account view tying each customer's orders, points balance, tier, adopted trees, referral earnings, and the lot story (plot, picker, Brix, process) behind their last bag — makes loyalty tangible and reinforces the provenance that justifies the premium.
- **On-farm / QR enrollment for tour & cupping guests** — Scan-to-join at the reserve and on every bag's pack; auto-attributes the membership origin (tour, retail, wholesale, referral) — captures origin-tourism fans at the peak emotional moment and feeds the unified CRM.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Shopify + Shopify Customer Accounts (DTC store + identity backbone), LoyaltyLion or Smile.io (points/tiers engine) — or Rivo as the Shopify-native lower-cost option, ReferralCandy or Yotpo Referrals (double-sided referral link generation, attribution, fraud detection), Recharge or Skio (subscription billing that the loyalty tier perks hook into), Klaviyo (lifecycle email/SMS: tier-up, referral nudges, adoption renewal, harvest-box shipping) + Twilio for SMS/WhatsApp in-region, Stripe (deposits/pre-orders for reserve-lot holds and tree adoptions, plus wholesale invoicing), QR/NFC pack tags via Scantrust or Digital Link / GS1 2D barcode standard (links each bag to its lot story and an enroll/refer CTA), Optional blockchain provenance layer (e.g. Bext360 / a public-ledger anchor) so adopted-tree and reserve-lot claims are verifiable, Cropster (pull authoritative lot/cupping-score data into the lot-story shown in the member portal), Best of Panama / SCAP auction feed (surface auction-lot availability into the reserve allocation waitlist)

**Data model additions:** customer / member (unified CRM record: contact, channel of origin, lifetime spend, segment B2C/B2B), loyalty_account (points balance, tier, tier-anchor date, lifetime points), loyalty_tier (definition: name Bean/Reserve/Founder, thresholds, perk set), points_transaction (earn/redeem ledger: source order/referral/adoption, delta, balance), referral (referrer member, code/link, referred member, status, reward issued to each side, fraud flags), reward_redemption (perk claimed: free shipping, tour credit, allocation right — links to order), tree_adoption (member, plot/tree id → existing Plot, second-tree-planted ref, annual box fulfillment, renewal date), lot_reservation / allocation (member, target lotCode or plot, deposit via Stripe, status: waitlisted/held/fulfilled), wholesale_account (roaster, cumulative volume, allocation tier, refer-a-roaster links), enrollment_event (source: tour/pack-QR/checkout/referral; ties membership origin attribution)

**AI / automation:**
- Churn / next-best-action prediction — score each member's likelihood to lapse and auto-trigger the right Klaviyo flow (re-order nudge, surprise reserve-lot offer, tier-up reminder).
- Personalized allocation matching — recommend which scarce micro-lot to offer which member based on past cup-score preferences and purchase history, maximizing sell-through of small lots.
- Referral fraud detection — flag self-referral rings, disposable-email abuse, and unusual code velocity before rewards are issued.
- LTV-based dynamic perks — let AI tune the referral incentive and tier thresholds per cohort to hit target CAC/LTV instead of a static give-$10/get-$10.
- Auto-generated lot-story copy + tasting notes — turn the structured lot ledger (plot, Brix, process, cup score) into the narrative shown in the member portal and on referral landing pages.
- Conversational concierge for high-tier/Founder members — an AI assistant that answers 'when's the next Geisha drop,' 'how's my adopted tree,' and books origin tours.

**World-class:** World-class here is not a generic points punch-card — it is a closed loop where loyalty is paid in access, not discounts. A Janson member's portal shows the exact plot, picker, Brix and cup score behind their last bag (pulled from the same lot ledger that powers Farm Ops); their tier unlocks first-refusal on the next reserve micro-lot; their referral link converts friends at >5% with full fraud-safe attribution; their adopted tree shows a live photo and the second tree it funded in the reserve; and a wholesale roaster sees cumulative volume drive real allocation priority. Every touch (tour QR, pack QR, checkout) enrolls and attributes automatically into ONE customer record shared with marketing, sales, and provenance. The scarcity of Panama Geisha (a $30,204/kg world-record commodity) becomes the loyalty currency — the program monetizes desire for access rather than bleeding margin on the farm's rarest asset.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson is a real, vertically integrated Volcán farm with Geisha and a 200ha quetzal-habitat private reserve — assets that make generic e-commerce loyalty look thin. The coffee-specific angle: (1) reward in scarcity, not price — Panama Geisha sets world auction records (La Esmeralda's $30,204/kg Best of Panama 2025), so the most powerful perk is allocation/early access to reserve micro-lots, not 10% off; (2) Adopt-a-Tree plugs straight into the reserve conservation story — each adoption plants a second tree and ties a member to a named plot and quetzal habitat, turning ecology into recurring revenue; (3) provenance is the loyalty hook — Janson already has lot-level data (lotCode JC-564, plot, picker, Brix, process) flowing harvest→processing→green, so the member portal can show the literal story of the bag in someone's hand; (4) the wholesale/green channel is the real money, so a refer-a-roaster + volume-allocation tier is as important as consumer points; and (5) origin tourism at the reserve is the highest-intent enrollment moment — a tour guest who just cupped a 90+ Geisha at altitude is the easiest lifetime member the farm will ever sign.


#### Agro-tourism — farm tours, lodging & experience booking
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Emily Janson (and the family hospitality lead / front-desk + guide team) — the family members who personally run "Lagunas Adventures on The Janson Coffee Farm": guiding the coffee tours and Geisha tastings, managing the guest house, the 9-5 coffee shop, and the bike/kayak/horseback/lake experiences, while fielding the phone and WhatsApp reservation requests that come in 24/7. Secondary persona: the visiting guest (a coffee-curious tourist or specialty-coffee buyer extending a sourcing trip) who wants to book, prepay, and arrive prepared.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a traveler finds us on TripAdvisor/Viator at 11pm and we are asleep, I want them to see live availability and self-book + prepay a tour, room, or rental without a phone call, so I can stop losing the bookings that 'book up all the time' to the back-and-forth of phone tag.
- When I am leading a Geisha tasting and three WhatsApp messages, a Viator booking, and a walk-in all hit at once, I want every channel to feed one calendar with one source of truth for capacity, so I can stop double-booking the guest house or overfilling a tour group.
- When a guest checks in, I want their tour, lodging night, lake/horseback add-ons, coffee-shop tab, and bag-of-beans purchase to roll into one itinerary and one bill, so I can stop juggling a paper ledger and let them pay once in USD by card.
- When the season turns (harvest crowds, dry-season birding for quetzals, low shoulder months), I want to flex pricing, hours, and which experiences are bookable, so I can fill the reserve cabins and tour slots that would otherwise sit empty.

**Features:**
- **Experience catalog & live availability calendar** — One canonical list of every bookable thing (Economy Coffee Tour $10, premium Geisha tasting, guest-house nights, bike/kayak/horseback, private reserve birding walk) with per-experience capacity, duration, guide assignment, seasonality and required-notice rules (e.g. horseback = 24h notice + riding experience) — kills double-booking and replaces the 'call us, we book up' bottleneck with real-time slots.
- **Direct self-service booking engine + prepay checkout** — Mobile-first public booking widget embedded on jansoncoffee.com that takes deposits/full payment in USD; captures the high-intent late-night traveler with zero phone tag and reduces no-shows via prepay.
- **OTA channel manager (two-way sync)** — Pushes the same inventory to Viator, TripAdvisor Experiences, GetYourGuide, Airbnb Experiences and Booking.com (for the guest house) and pulls reservations back into the single calendar — Janson is ALREADY listed on TripAdvisor/Viator, so this stops the channel from being a manual island and ends overbooking across sources.
- **Guest profile, unified itinerary & single folio** — Each visitor gets one record: their tour + lodging nights + add-on rentals + coffee-shop tabs + retail bean purchases roll into one itinerary and one checkout — turns scattered cash/paper into a single bill and a complete guest history for repeat visits.
- **Guide & resource scheduling board** — Assigns family guides, horses, kayaks, bikes and rooms to bookings and shows conflicts before they happen — so a guide is never promised to two groups and a kayak is never rented twice in the same slot.
- **Automated guest comms (confirmation, pre-arrival, review request)** — Triggered email/WhatsApp: booking confirmation, what-to-bring + directions to Volcán, day-before reminder, and a post-visit review nudge to TripAdvisor — lifts ratings and cuts no-shows without manual messaging.
- **Farm-to-cup tour↔operations bridge** — Links each tour/tasting to the live farm data the rest of the app already holds (today's harvest plot, the actual lot fermenting in the wet mill, the variety mix) so guests taste the exact micro-lot they just walked past and can scan a QR to buy that traceable lot afterward — converts the tour into retail and turns operational data into a marketing asset no generic tour software can offer.
- **Dynamic seasonal pricing & yield management** — Rules for harvest-season demand, dry-season quetzal-birding peaks, weekday/weekend and group-size tiers, plus promo codes for the shoulder season — fills cabins and slots that would otherwise sit empty and protects the premium of the Geisha experience.
- **Tourism revenue & occupancy dashboard** — Agro-tourism KPIs (occupancy %, RevPAR for the guest house, tour fill rate, revenue per visitor, channel mix, retail attach rate) sit beside the existing harvest/processing dashboards — so the family sees the whole business (grow → process → sell → host) in one place.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Bókun (Viator/TripAdvisor-owned booking engine + 70+ OTA channel manager — tightest Viator fit; free starter tier suits the $0 constraint) or FareHarbor (no monthly fee under ~$1,500/mo revenue) as the booking/channel-manager backbone, Viator & TripAdvisor Experiences API (Janson is already listed there as 'Lagunas Adventures on The Janson Coffee Farm'), GetYourGuide, Klook and Airbnb Experiences for tour distribution, Booking.com / Expedia Lodging APIs (or Hostaway/Lodgify channel manager) for the guest-house nights, Stripe + Stripe Terminal (USD card payments, deposits/prepay, on-site coffee-shop & retail tap-to-pay; Panama uses USD so no FX), WhatsApp Business Platform API (Cloud API) — the de-facto booking + comms channel in Panama/Latin America, Twilio (SMS/email fallback) and Resend/Postmark for transactional email, Google Business Profile + Google Things to do + Google Calendar / iCal feeds for the guides, OpenWeather / Tomorrow.io weather API to surface conditions for outdoor activities (lake, horseback, birding) at booking time, eBird / Cornell Lab API to surface quetzal & resident-species sightings for the reserve birding experience, QR/traceability link to the existing lot codes so a tour ties to a real micro-lot for purchase, Currency/tax: Panama DGI electronic-invoice (factura electrónica) considerations for on-site retail receipts

**Data model additions:** experiences — bookable products: kind (tour | tasting | lodging | rental | activity | birding), name, description, base_price_usd, currency, duration_min, default_capacity, required_notice_hrs, requires_experience (bool, e.g. horseback), season_start/end, active, ord, experience_resources — physical/people inventory: kind (guide | horse | kayak | bike | room/cabin), name, capacity, links to workers(id) for family guides, bookings — a reservation: guest_id, channel (direct | viator | tripadvisor | getyourguide | airbnb | booking_com | walk_in | whatsapp | phone), external_ref (OTA booking id), status (pending | confirmed | checked_in | completed | cancelled | no_show), party_size, arrival_date, total_usd, deposit_usd, balance_usd, source_url, booking_items — line items within a booking: experience_id, scheduled_slot_id, qty, unit_price_usd, add-on flag — also covers coffee-shop tabs and retail bean purchases that tie a lot_code from the existing lots table, scheduled_slots — datetime instances of an experience with assigned resource_ids, capacity, booked_count, guide worker_id, status; the single-source-of-truth calendar the channel manager syncs against, guests — visitor CRM: name, email, phone, country, language, marketing_opt_in, lifetime_value_usd, visit_count, notes, guest_house_units — lodging-specific: cabin/room name, sleeps, nightly_rate_usd, amenities, view (lake | reserve | garden), payments — transactions: booking_id, processor (stripe), amount_usd, type (deposit | balance | onsite_retail | refund), status, external_charge_id, reviews — post-visit feedback synced from TripAdvisor/Google: rating, text, channel, responded (bool), tourism_kpis (or a view) — daily/weekly occupancy_pct, revpar_usd, tour_fill_pct, revenue_per_visitor, channel_mix, retail_attach_pct for the dashboard

**AI / automation:**
- AI booking concierge over WhatsApp Business API: answers availability/pricing/directions in English & Spanish, proposes itineraries, and creates a pending booking — turning the 24/7 inbound message flood into self-serve, with a strict guardrail that untrusted guest text can never trigger a payment or refund on its own
- Dynamic pricing engine: recommends slot prices from demand signals (harvest-season crowds, dry-season quetzal-birding peaks, lead time, weekday/weekend, group size, weather forecast) to maximize occupancy without eroding the Geisha-experience premium
- Demand forecasting tied to the farm calendar: predicts visitor volume around harvest and bird-migration windows so the family can pre-staff guides and stock the coffee shop
- Auto-drafted, on-brand multilingual review responses for TripAdvisor/Google, flagging only negative reviews for a human
- Smart itinerary builder: given a guest's stay length, interests and the weather, assembles a tour + lodging + add-on package and surfaces the matching micro-lot to taste and buy
- AI photo/caption generation for new OTA listings from farm imagery to keep Viator/GetYourGuide pages fresh
- No-show / cancellation risk scoring to set deposit requirements per booking

**World-class:** A traveler discovers Janson on Viator or jansoncoffee.com, sees live availability, and self-books a Geisha tasting + a guest-house night + a sunrise quetzal birding walk in one prepaid USD checkout at midnight. The booking lands on a single calendar that already knows a guide and the horses are free; an automated WhatsApp sends directions to Volcán and what to bring. On arrival the guest scans a QR that opens an itinerary tied to the live farm data — they taste the exact washed-Geisha micro-lot whose cherries were picked on Tizingal Alto that week and is fermenting in the mill, then one-tap buy that traceable lot shipped home, with the coffee-shop tab and rentals on one folio paid once. Every channel (Viator/GetYourGuide/Airbnb/Booking.com/walk-in/WhatsApp) is overbooking-proof because capacity is one source of truth; dynamic pricing keeps shoulder-season cabins full and the Geisha experience premium; and the family sees occupancy, RevPAR, tour fill, and retail-attach in the same dashboard as harvest and processing — the full grow→process→sell→host loop, closed.

**Coffee-specific angle:** This is uniquely Janson's to win because agro-tourism is already a real, operating revenue line — 'Lagunas Adventures on The Janson Coffee Farm' runs guided tours ($10 economy through premium Geisha tastings led by family members like Emily Janson), a charming guest house, a 9-5 coffee shop, bike/kayak/horseback rentals, a serene lake, and a 200-hectare private nature reserve that is genuine quetzal habitat. The killer differentiator no generic tour platform can replicate is the farm-to-cup bridge: the booking system lives inside the SAME app as plots, harvests, lots and processing batches, so a tour can be tied to the literal micro-lot a guest walks past, tastes, and then buys traceable (via the existing lot codes) — converting the experience into specialty green/roasted sales. The reserve enables a premium birding/eco product (Geisha + quetzals at 1,350-1,700 masl) that commands top-tier pricing. And the family's own constraint is the opportunity: they take reservations by phone today and 'book up all the time,' so capturing the bookings currently lost to phone tag is pure upside.


#### Reviews, reputation & community building
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm's brand/marketing lead (a Janson family member who owns the public story and the auction/wholesale relationships, running jansoncoffee.com). Secondary: the quality/cupping lead who needs third-party scores to back reputation claims; plus the two distinct audiences whose voices we capture — (a) green-coffee buyers/roasters who score and review lots, and (b) end consumers/home brewers who buy roasted bags direct.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a roaster cups a Janson lot or we place at Best of Panama, I want every score, review, award and certification pulled into one ledger tied to the exact lot, so I can prove quality with receipts instead of adjectives and lift our next auction and wholesale prices.
- When a customer scans the QR on a bag of roasted Geisha or finishes a great cup, I want to capture their review, photo and brew notes at the moment of delight, so I can turn one-off buyers into repeat subscribers and build a public wall of social proof that converts the next shopper.
- When a buyer or guest is deciding whether Janson is trustworthy, I want our certifications (Smithsonian Bird Friendly, Rainforest Alliance), auction results and verified reviews shown as tamper-evident verifiable badges, so I can charge a premium an honor-system claim never could.
- When I want a loyal following beyond a transaction, I want to run a Janson community — reserve-list early access to micro-lots, a quetzal-reserve conservation feed, harvest updates and a referral/loyalty loop — so I build a direct audience I don't rent from a marketplace or algorithm.

**Features:**
- **Reputation Ledger (unified score & review hub)** — One timeline aggregating third-party cupping scores (Coffee Review, SCA 100-pt), competition/auction results (Best of Panama, Cup of Excellence), press mentions and verified reviews — each row tied to a specific lotCode and harvest year. Turns scattered proof into one credibility asset that feeds pricing and pitch decks.
- **Scan-to-Review via per-lot QR** — Each bag/lot QR resolves to a public lot page (origin, plot, process, score) with a frictionless review + photo + brew-log capture. Reviews land at peak delight, raising review volume 5-10x vs email and feeding the social-proof wall.
- **Verified Trust Badges & cert wallet** — Tamper-evident badges for Smithsonian Bird Friendly, Rainforest Alliance, organic, plus auction-result and score badges with expiry tracking and renewal reminders. Lets the farm command a sustainability/quality premium that an unverifiable claim cannot.
- **Social-proof wall + auto-syndication** — Curated, moderated review/UGC wall embeddable on jansoncoffee.com product and lot pages, with rich-snippet schema for star ratings in Google results. Lifts D2C conversion and organic discovery.
- **Reserve List & community membership** — Opt-in audience that gets early/exclusive access to scarce micro-lots and Geisha allocations, harvest-season updates and a quetzal-reserve conservation feed. Builds an owned first-party audience and a waitlist that signals scarcity and drives auction/reserve demand.
- **Loyalty & referral engine** — Points and referral loop rewarding reviews, photos, referrals and repeat orders (not just purchases). Increases repeat-purchase rate and turns happy buyers into a growth channel.
- **Reputation inbox & response workflow** — Single queue ingesting reviews/mentions across Coffee Review, Trustpilot, Google, Instagram and the farm's own forms, with sentiment tagging and reply drafting. The owner answers every voice in one place, protecting reputation and surfacing buyer feedback to the quality team.
- **Buyer/roaster relationship CRM lite** — Profiles for roasters and buyers tying their past reviews, scores, lot purchases and auction bids together, so outreach for the next harvest is personalized and reputation is mapped to actual revenue relationships.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Coffee Review (Kenneth Davids — sample submission + published 100-pt scores/reviews), Alliance for Coffee Excellence — Cup of Excellence auction & scores, Best of Panama / SCAP auction platform (Panama Geisha lots; 2025 record $30,204/kg), SCA cupping protocol & 100-point scoring standard (Q-grader scores), Smithsonian Bird Friendly certification registry (directly relevant to the 200-ha quetzal reserve), Rainforest Alliance certification verification, USDA Organic / Fairtrade registries, Yotpo or Judge.me (reviews + loyalty + UGC) and Trustpilot (public company reputation), Google Business Profile + review rich-snippet (schema.org Review/AggregateRating), Instagram Graph API + TikTok for UGC ingestion and conservation/harvest feed, Roasters.app / Bean Conqueror / BrewLab (consumer brew-log + bean-scan apps that index the farm), Klaviyo or Resend (free/low tier) for reserve-list + community email, Discord or Circle community space for the reserve community, Shopify (D2C roasted storefront the QR and reviews attach to), Twilio Verify / hCaptcha for verified, non-spam review capture

**Data model additions:** Review (id, source [coffee_review|sca|buyer|consumer|google|trustpilot], lotCode, harvestYear, score, scoreScale, reviewerName, reviewerType, body, photos[], brewMethod, verified, sentiment, createdAt), CuppingScore (id, lotCode, scorer/Q-grader, sca100Breakdown {aroma,flavor,acidity,body,balance,...}, total, protocol, sessionDate), Award (id, competition [best_of_panama|cup_of_excellence], year, rank, lotCode, auctionPricePerKg, buyer, currency), Certification (id, body [smithsonian_bird_friendly|rainforest_alliance|organic|fairtrade], status, issuedDate, expiresDate, certNumber, verifyUrl, scope [farm|plot|lot]), ReputationBadge (id, type, label, sourceRefId, verifiable, embedToken), CommunityMember (id, name, email, type [reserve|buyer|consumer], joinedDate, loyaltyPoints, referredBy, tags[]), ReserveAllocation (id, memberId, lotCode, requestedKg, status, harvestYear), LoyaltyEvent (id, memberId, kind [review|photo|referral|purchase], points, refId, at), MentionInbox (id, channel, externalId, author, body, sentiment, status [new|replied|archived], replyDraft, at), BuyerProfile (id, company, contact, lotsPurchased[], reviewsGiven[], lastAuctionBid, relationshipValueUsd)

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-summarize incoming reviews/mentions into themes (e.g. 'jasmine/bergamot notes recurring on Geisha lots') and surface them to the quality team as actionable cupping feedback
- Draft on-brand, bilingual (EN/ES) replies to every review and mention for one-click approval in the reputation inbox
- Sentiment + spam/fraud scoring on review capture so the public wall stays authentic and detractors are caught early
- Generate lot-page marketing copy and social posts automatically from the reputation ledger (score + award + cert + top review) the moment a new high score lands
- Predictive pricing: model the next auction-reserve and wholesale price from historical score-to-price data so the marketing lead knows the dollar value of a new 90+ review
- Match consumer brew-log/flavor reviews to the SCA flavor wheel and recommend brew recipes back to drinkers, deepening community engagement
- Identify likely reserve-list converts and referral champions from loyalty/engagement signals for targeted micro-lot offers

**World-class:** A buyer or guest lands on a Janson lot page and sees a single, verifiable reputation story: the Q-grader/Coffee Review score, the Best of Panama placing and price, the Bird Friendly and Rainforest Alliance badges (each click-to-verify against the issuing registry), and a moderated wall of real buyer and consumer reviews with photos and brew notes — all anchored to that exact lotCode and harvest. Every roasted bag carries a QR that turns a sip into a review in under 20 seconds, feeding both the public wall (with Google rich snippets) and a private quality-feedback loop. The farm runs an owned reserve community that gets first dibs on scarce Geisha micro-lots and a living conservation feed from the quetzal reserve, with a loyalty/referral loop that compounds. The owner answers every review and mention from one inbox, and reputation is mapped directly to pricing power: each new high score or award measurably moves the next auction reserve and wholesale rate. Reputation is treated as a first-party, verifiable, lot-level asset — not rented from marketplaces or claimed on the honor system.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson uniquely straddles both reputation economies: it sells green to roasters (where Coffee Review scores, SCA cupping, Cup of Excellence and especially the Best of Panama Geisha auction — record $30,204/kg in 2025 — are the currency that sets price) AND roasted bags direct to consumers (where Yotpo/Trustpilot-style reviews and community drive conversion). A unified hub must serve both. The single biggest unfair advantage is the 200-hectare private quetzal nature reserve: Smithsonian Bird Friendly and Rainforest Alliance certification, verifiably badged and woven into a conservation story feed, converts the reserve into measurable reputation and premium — exactly the shade-canopy/habitat claim those bodies certify. Lot-level reputation (tying every score, review and award to a specific lotCode + harvest year, the same lotCode already in the Harvest/ProcessingBatch model) is the through-line that makes Janson's Geisha story provable rather than merely marketed.


### Finance, Compliance & Sustainability

#### Accounting & bookkeeping — QBO/Xero sync
*Priority:* **table-stakes**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Janette Janson — the owner-operator who wears the "farm controller / bookkeeper" hat. On a family specialty-coffee finca of Janson's size there is no full-time accountant: the owner closes the books at night and hands a shoebox of receipts to an external Panamanian contador (CPA) at month- and quarter-end for DGI filings. Secondary users: the external contador who lives in QBO/Xero and just wants clean, reconciled data; and Miguel Janson (managing owner) who wants a real-time P&L and per-lot cost-of-production without waiting on the accountant.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I pay pickers their per-kilo/daily wages, buy fertilizer and parchment bags, and pay diesel for the dryer, I want every operational event in Farm Operations to post itself to the right chart-of-accounts line in QuickBooks/Xero automatically, so I can stop double-keying receipts at midnight and hand my contador a reconciled ledger instead of a shoebox.
- When I sell a 30 kg box of green Geisha to a US roaster or a bag of roasted to a café in Panama City, I want the system to issue a DGI-compliant Factura Electrónica (SFEP/CUFE) and simultaneously create the AR invoice in QBO/Xero, so I get paid faster and stay legal with Panama's e-invoicing mandate without buying a separate tool.
- When a green-coffee lot finishes processing, I want the true cost of production per kilo (labor + inputs + processing + amortized establishment) rolled up against the price that lot sold for, so I can see which varieties and plots actually make money and price next season's Geisha auction lots with confidence.
- When my contador asks for the quarterly ITBMS (VAT) and income-tax numbers, I want one export that ties every ledger entry back to its farm event and receipt image, so the filing takes an hour instead of a week and survives a DGI audit.

**Features:**
- **QBO + Xero bi-directional sync engine (OAuth2)** — Connect once via QuickBooks Online OAuth2 (60-min access / 100-day rolling refresh tokens) or Xero OAuth2; a nightly + on-demand sync pushes farm-generated journal entries, bills, and invoices up and pulls back payment/reconciliation status. Eliminates manual re-keying and keeps the contador's QBO as the system of record while Farm Ops is the system of origin.
- **Account-mapping & chart-of-accounts wizard** — Maps each Farm Ops event type (payroll, agro-inputs, processing fuel, packaging, freight, green-sale, roasted-sale) to a specific QBO/Xero account + tax code, stored once. Turns domain events into correctly-coded ledger lines so the books reconcile without an accountant re-categorizing every line.
- **Picker payroll → payroll journal** — Rolls daily attendance and per-kilo picking (existing Worker.dailyRateUsd + Harvest.cherriesKg) into a weekly payroll run, computes net pay, Caja de Seguro Social (CSS) contributions and the décimo-tercer-mes accrual, and posts the labor expense + liability journal to QBO/Xero. Replaces the hand-tallied picking ledger that is the farm's single biggest and most error-prone cost.
- **Per-lot cost-of-production (lot costing) engine** — Attributes labor, inputs, processing fuel/water, packaging and amortized plot-establishment to each lotCode as it moves cherry→green, producing a true $/kg green and a gross-margin-by-lot/variety view. Lets Miguel price Geisha auction lots and cull unprofitable plots on data, not gut feel — the core differentiator.
- **DGI Factura Electrónica (SFEP) issuance** — On a confirmed sale, generates the signed XML e-invoice, obtains the CUFE from a PAC (or the DGI Facturador Gratuito for under B/36,000), and mirrors it as an AR invoice in QBO/Xero. Keeps Janson legal under Panama's 2026 e-invoicing mandate and removes a separate invoicing tool.
- **AP / receipt capture with OCR** — Snap a fertilizer or diesel receipt; OCR extracts vendor, amount, ITBMS, date; it posts as a Bill against the right expense account and attaches the image. Kills the shoebox and creates an audit-proof paper trail tied to the farm event.
- **Bank-feed reconciliation surface** — Pulls bank/credit transactions (via Plaid or QBO/Xero's native bank feeds) and auto-matches them to Farm Ops bills and payroll runs, surfacing only the unmatched. The owner reconciles in minutes; the contador inherits a clean feed.
- **Real-time P&L, cash-flow & seasonal-margin dashboard** — A liquid-glass finance dashboard showing season-to-date revenue, COGS, gross margin, cash runway and cost-per-kg trend — sourced from the same ledger that feeds QBO. Gives the owner a daily financial pulse instead of a quarter-late report.
- **Tax & filing pack (ITBMS / income tax)** — One export bundling the period's invoices, bills, e-invoice CUFEs and receipt images, formatted for the contador's DGI filing. Compresses quarter-end from a week to an hour and de-risks audits.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** QuickBooks Online Accounting API (REST, OAuth2, 2025 tiered pricing, reference-only webhooks), Xero Accounting API (OAuth2, 30-min tokens), Panama SFEP / Factura Electrónica via a certified PAC (e.g. EDICOM, P4 Software) or the DGI Facturador Gratuito for under B/36,000 — CUFE + Firma Electrónica Avanzada (qualified e-signature from a DNFE-registered provider), Plaid (or QBO/Xero native bank feeds) for bank-transaction reconciliation, Panamanian bank rails (Banco General, Banistmo) + ACH Yappy for local café receivables; Wise / Stripe for cross-border roaster payments in USD, Caja de Seguro Social (CSS) contribution tables + Panama décimo-tercer-mes payroll rules, OCR/document AI for receipt extraction (e.g. AWS Textract / Google Document AI / Anthropic Claude vision), Cropster / coffee-trade green-sale data as a costing/revenue source for lot reconciliation, ISO 4217 USD (Panama Balboa pegged 1:1, so effectively single-currency — simplifies the GL)

**Data model additions:** FinancialAccount (chart-of-accounts node: code, name, type, external QBO/Xero id), AccountMapping (Farm Ops event type → account + tax code), LedgerEntry / JournalLine (double-entry posting linked to source farm event), Bill / AP record (vendor, amount, ITBMS, account, receipt image ref), Vendor (suppliers: fertilizer, packaging, fuel), Customer (roasters, cafés, importers; tax id / RUC), SalesInvoice (AR; links to lotCode, customer, e-invoice CUFE), EInvoice (SFEP record: XML, CUFE, PAC status, signature), PayrollRun + PayrollLine (period, worker, gross, CSS, décimo accrual, net), LotCost (per-lotCode rollup: labor, inputs, processing, amortization → $/kg green), BankTransaction + ReconciliationMatch, TaxCode (ITBMS rates) and TaxPeriod (filing window), SyncJob / SyncLog (QBO/Xero push-pull audit, token state, conflict resolution)

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-categorization: an LLM maps a new vendor/receipt to the correct chart-of-accounts line and tax code, learning from past coding decisions.
- Receipt OCR + extraction (vendor, amount, ITBMS, date) turning a phone photo into a coded Bill.
- Anomaly/fraud detection: flags a payroll run or fuel bill that deviates from seasonal norms before it posts.
- Cash-flow forecasting: projects runway across the season using harvest curve + historical input spend + expected lot-sale dates.
- Cost-of-production copilot: explains why a lot's $/kg moved (e.g. 'labor up 14% — Tizingal pickers logged 22% more hours at lower brix').
- Natural-language finance Q&A: 'What was gross margin on Natural Geisha this season vs washed Caturra?' answered against the ledger.
- Reconciliation auto-match suggestions for bank-feed lines with confidence scoring, surfacing only true exceptions.

**World-class:** A farm owner pays a picking crew, buys a pallet of fertilizer, and ships a Geisha lot — and never opens QuickBooks. Each event is captured at its source (attendance, processing, sale), coded once, and flows as a correctly-classified, tax-aware ledger entry into QBO/Xero, with the bank feed auto-reconciling and the DGI e-invoice issued in the same gesture as the sale. The owner sees a live P&L and a per-kg cost-of-production for every lot and variety; the contador opens a fully reconciled book at quarter-end and files in an hour. Books are continuously closed, audit-ready, and every number traces back to a farm event with its receipt image attached. Accounting becomes a byproduct of running the farm, not a second night job.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Specialty coffee's economics are won or lost at the lot level: a single Geisha auction lot can sell for 50-100x a commodity-Caturra lot, and Janson processes washed/natural/honey/anaerobic variants of the same cherry — each with different labor, time-on-patio and yield loss. Generic bookkeeping can't see this; lot-level cost-of-production (tied to the existing lotCode that already threads Harvest → ProcessingBatch) is the coffee-specific superpower. Layer on Panama-specific realities the contador already deals with — ITBMS/DGI SFEP e-invoicing, CSS payroll and the décimo-tercer-mes, USD-pegged Balboa so cross-border roaster sales need no FX gymnastics — and Janson gets books that speak both 'specialty coffee' and 'Panamanian agribusiness' natively. Bonus: clean per-lot cost data is exactly what's demanded for transparency/direct-trade premiums and the EUDR/origin documentation buyers increasingly require.


#### Cost accounting & per-lot/per-plot profitability (COGS)
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm owner-operator / finance lead (in Janson's case, a Janson family member such as Janette or Miguel acting as managing partner) who must know the true landed cost and margin of every lot and plot. Secondary daily users: the farm administrator/bookkeeper who codes expenses and runs payroll, and the agronomy/mill managers whose input and labor decisions drive the cost lines. Tertiary consumer: the green-coffee sales lead who needs a defensible cost floor before quoting a buyer or setting a Best-of-Panama auction reserve.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I finish a harvest season, I want to see the fully-loaded cost per kilo of green coffee for each lot (labor + inputs + processing + drying + milling + overhead allocation), so I can know which lots actually made money and stop subsidizing the losers from the Geisha winners.
- When a buyer or the Best of Panama auction asks for a price on a specific Geisha lot, I want an instant view of that lot's true landed cost and break-even floor, so I can negotiate a margin instead of guessing and accidentally selling my best microlot below cost.
- When I'm deciding whether to renovate, graft, or abandon a plot next year, I want multi-season cost-per-hectare and cost-per-tree profitability for that plot, so I can put capital into the parcels that earn and retire the ones that drain cash.
- When I review the month, I want every peso of labor, fertilizer, fuel, drying gas, certification fee, and depreciation automatically attributed to the plots and lots that consumed it, so I close the books without a week of spreadsheet archaeology and can file accurate taxes in Panama (DGI / ISR).

**Features:**
- **Lot & plot cost ledger with activity-based costing (ABC) engine** — Every cost line (labor day, input application, machine-hour, drying-bed-day, milling pass) carries a cost-object tag (plot_id and/or lot_code). Direct costs attach to the lot/plot that consumed them; shared overhead (admin, depreciation, certifications, general farm maintenance) allocates by configurable drivers (harvested kg, tree count, area_ha, processing volume). Result: a defensible fully-loaded cost number rather than a gut feel.
- **Cost-flow tracer that follows the physical chain cherry to green** — Reuses the existing plot to harvest to lot_code to processing_batch spine: cherry-stage costs roll forward through the documented mass-loss curve (cherries shrink to ~18% green), so cost-per-kg is correctly restated on green weight at each stage. The owner sees why a Geisha washed lot costs 6x a commercial Caturra lot, line by line.
- **Per-lot P&L and break-even card** — For any lot: green kg produced, total cost, cost/kg green, the sale(s) recognized against it, gross margin %, and break-even price. Surfaces the cost floor sales needs before quoting auctions or buyers — turns the lone ytdRevenueUsd headline into true net contribution per lot.
- **Per-plot multi-season profitability (cost/ha, cost/tree, yield-adjusted)** — Renovate-vs-retire decisions: ranks all plots by margin/ha and margin/tree across seasons, normalizing for tree age and yield ramp, so capital flows to earning parcels and chronic drains are flagged.
- **Labor cost roll-up tied to piece-rate + day-rate payroll** — Converts the existing dailyRateUsd and per-picker cherriesKg into actual labor cost per lot/plot, supporting both day-rate and Panama-typical per-lata/per-kg piece rates, including the legally required 13th-month (decimo) accrual and CSS social-security employer load, so labor (the single biggest cost on a coffee farm) is attributed accurately, not averaged.
- **Input & consumables cost capture (fertilizer, agrochemicals, fuel, drying gas)** — Each task or application logs quantity x unit cost against a plot/lot, drawing from a maintained input price list and inventory; ties COGS to the agronomy work already modeled in tasks. Lets the owner see input intensity by plot and catch over-application.
- **Overhead & depreciation allocation module** — Wet/dry mill equipment, vehicles, drying patios, and the reserve's fixed costs depreciate on schedule and allocate to lots by driver, so cost/kg includes the real capital burden, not just cash outflows — the gap most farms miss when they think they're profitable.
- **Standard-cost vs actual variance & forecast** — Set a standard cost/kg at season start; the system shows running variance (labor overruns, input price spikes, drying re-work) so the owner intervenes mid-season instead of discovering the loss at year-end. Includes a what-if cost simulator for next season's budget.
- **Costing reports & accounting export** — One-click cost-per-lot statements for buyers/auctions, a season COGS summary, and a clean export (CSV + QuickBooks/Xero journal entries) so the external accountant and Panama DGI filing get correct numbers without re-keying.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** QuickBooks Online API and Xero API for chart-of-accounts sync, journal entries, and COGS reconciliation (free dev tiers; map cost objects to classes/tracking categories), Wave Accounting (genuinely free) as the $0-friendly default ledger/export target for a small farm, Cropster (Cropster Origin / C-sar) — the specialty-coffee standard for lot tracking and green inventory; sync lot_code, green kg, and cup score so cost data lines up with the quality and inventory system buyers already trust, Banco General / Banco Nacional de Panamá and Yappy (Panama's dominant local payment rail) for matching supplier payments and labor disbursements to cost lines, Panama CSS (Caja de Seguro Social) employer-contribution tables and the Codigo de Trabajo decimo (13th-month) rules to compute the true loaded labor cost, Panama DGI tax categories (ISR, the agricultural exemption thresholds) for tax-ready cost classification, Commodity & specialty price references: ICE Coffee 'C' futures and Best of Panama / Specialty Coffee Association auction clearing prices as the revenue/benchmark side of margin, Fuel & input supplier price feeds (local agro-supply invoices via OCR; optionally Melissa/Costa price lists) to keep the input cost list current, OANDA / exchangerate.host FX (USD is legal tender in Panama, but buyers and inputs may price in other currencies) — free FX API, OCR invoice capture (Google Document AI free tier / Tesseract) to turn supplier receipts and fuel tickets into coded cost lines

**Data model additions:** cost_categories — chart of cost accounts (labor, inputs, processing, drying, milling, overhead, depreciation, certification), each with a default allocation driver, cost_entries — the ledger line: amount_usd, date, category, cost_object (plot_id and/or lot_code, both nullable for farm-wide), source_event ref (harvest/task/batch/payroll), supplier, memo, input_items — fertilizers, agrochemicals, fuel, drying gas, packaging: unit, current unit_cost_usd, on-hand qty (inventory), input_applications — qty of an input_item applied to a plot/lot on a date (links to tasks), generating a cost_entry, labor_costing — derived from workers + harvests: day-rate and piece-rate runs, with css_employer_pct and decimo_accrual loading, attributed per lot/plot, equipment_assets — mill machinery, vehicles, patios/beds: acquisition cost, useful life, depreciation method, depreciation_schedule — periodic depreciation cost_entries allocated by driver, allocation_rules — driver definitions (by kg, tree count, area_ha, batch volume) and the periods they apply to, lot_cost_rollup (view) — per lot_code: green_kg, total cost split by category, cost_per_kg_green, break-even price, plot_cost_rollup (view) — per plot_id and season: cost/ha, cost/tree, margin, multi-season trend, sales / lot_sales — revenue recognized against a lot (buyer, kg, price/kg, channel: auction/direct/green/roasted) to compute true margin (replaces the single ytd_revenue_usd headline), cost_standards — season standard cost/kg by variety+method for variance analysis

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-coding of cost entries: an LLM classifies OCR'd supplier invoices and fuel tickets into the right cost_category and suggests the plot/lot to charge, learning from past corrections — turns receipts into a coded ledger with one tap.
- Anomaly and variance alerts: flag a lot whose labor or input cost/kg deviates sharply from its standard or from sibling lots (e.g. a plot suddenly burning 2x the fertilizer), surfacing over-application, theft, or a mis-coded entry mid-season.
- Predictive end-of-season cost/kg per lot from partial-season actuals + the historical mass-loss and drying-duration curves, so the owner knows the likely landing cost while there's still time to act.
- Natural-language costing Q&A: 'what was my cost per kilo on the JC-602 Geisha anaerobic, and what price do I need to clear 30% margin?' answered from the rollup, with the line-item breakdown.
- Renovate-vs-retire recommender: ranks plots by risk-adjusted multi-season margin/ha and proposes capital allocation, factoring tree-age yield ramp and varietal price outlook.
- Allocation-driver suggestion: AI proposes the most defensible overhead allocation basis per category and explains the margin impact of switching drivers, so the costing method is sound, not arbitrary.

**World-class:** World-class is activity-based, lot-level COGS that updates in near-real-time as the farm works — a picker logs kilos and the labor cost lands on the lot the same day; a fertilizer application debits that plot automatically; drying-bed occupancy accrues gas and depreciation by the day. The owner opens any lot and sees a fully-loaded cost/kg green broken into labor / inputs / processing / overhead, with a defensible break-even and live margin against the actual sale — accurate enough to put in front of a Best of Panama buyer and a tax auditor on the same screen. It reconciles to the penny with the external ledger (QuickBooks/Xero/Wave), carries multi-season trend so renovate-vs-retire is a data decision, and runs standard-cost variance so problems surface mid-season, not at close. Crucially it never asks the farm to do double data entry: costs ride on the operational events (harvests, tasks, processing batches) the team already records. The benchmark to beat is what Cropster + a competent farm accountant produce together — here it is one integrated system that a 14-person family farm can actually run.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Specialty coffee lives or dies on microlot economics, and Janson is exactly the operation where this matters most: a Geisha washed microlot can sell for 20-100x a commercial Caturra at the Best of Panama auction, but it is also far more labor- and process-intensive (selective ripe-only picking, anaerobic/honey fermentation, slow raised-bed drying). Without per-lot COGS, the farm literally cannot tell whether its trophy Geisha is funding the rest of the farm or quietly losing money on labor. The mass-loss curve already encoded in the processing data (cherries to ~18% green) is the exact cost-restatement backbone needed: cost/kg must be expressed on green weight, per process method, because Naturals and Anaerobics tie up drying beds far longer than Washed and that bed-time is real cost. Tying cost to the existing lot_code traceability spine also means the same number that proves provenance to a buyer proves profitability to the owner. And it is Panama-specific: USD legal tender (no FX headache on the books), CSS + decimo labor loading, DGI agricultural tax treatment, and Yappy/Banco General payment rails — plus the 200-hectare quetzal reserve as an allocable conservation/overhead and potential carbon/biodiversity cost-offset line.


#### Budgeting & financial forecasting
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm owner-operator / finance lead (in Janson's case the family principal, e.g. the Janson who runs the business, supported by a part-time/external accountant or bookkeeper). Secondary day-to-day input personas: the farm/agronomy manager (who owns the agronomy capex and input plan), the mill manager (processing throughput and conversion costs), and the harvest/labor supervisor (the single largest, most volatile seasonal cost line). The external accountant and any lender/bank relationship manager are consuming personas.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I'm planning the upcoming crop year before flowering, I want to build a season budget that ties projected cherry yield by plot and varietal to my expected costs (labor per lata, fertilizer, fungicide for roya, fuel, mill consumables) and my expected revenue by channel (green export, BoP Geisha auction, roasted retail), so I can decide how much to plant, hire, and borrow before I commit cash.
- When harvest labor is my biggest and most unpredictable cost and pickers are paid per lata/can of cherry, I want a rolling forecast that reprojects total picking cost and finish date from actual daily intake versus my plan, so I can catch a cost overrun or a thin year weeks early instead of at season close.
- When a Best-of-Panama or private buyer offers a price for a Geisha lot, I want to instantly see my fully-loaded cost-per-kg-of-green for that exact lot and the resulting margin, so I can negotiate or hold the lot with confidence instead of guessing.
- When fixed costs run all year but ~all my cash arrives in one harvest-and-sale window, I want a 12-month cash-flow projection with a low-balance early warning, so I can time input purchases, payroll, and any working-capital loan and never miss a CSS/DGI payment or a payroll.
- When the season closes, I want budget-versus-actual by plot, varietal, cost category, and sales channel rolled into a P&L, so I can see which lots actually made money and set next year's plan on evidence.

**Features:**
- **Season Budget Builder (yield-driven, bottom-up)** — One place to build the annual crop-year budget that auto-derives from the operational model already in the app: pulls expected_yield_kg and tree counts per plot/varietal, applies cherry-to-green conversion ratios per process method, and lets the owner layer cost assumptions (labor $/lata, inputs $/ha, mill $/qq) and revenue assumptions ($/lb green by channel). Output: a defensible season budget instead of a spreadsheet guess — and a number the family can align on before committing cash.
- **Rolling Harvest-Cost Forecast** — Reprojects total picking cost, completion date, and final yield daily from actual harvest intake (the harvests table) vs. the budgeted curve. Surfaces 'labor tracking 11% over plan, projected $14k overrun' weeks before close — turning the farm's single most volatile line into a managed, early-warning number.
- **Cost-per-kg-Green Engine (lot-level cost rollup)** — Allocates every cost — picking labor by lot, agronomy capex amortized per plot, milling/processing by batch, drying days, overhead — down to each traceability lot (lot_code), producing a fully-loaded cost-per-kg-green and break-even-per-quintal. The make-or-break specialty-coffee number; lets the owner price and negotiate Geisha lots from fact, not feel.
- **12-Month Cash-Flow Projection & Runway Alerts** — Models the brutal seasonality of coffee — year-round fixed costs against one concentrated harvest-and-sale cash inflow — into a month-by-month balance projection with a low-balance early warning. Tells the owner exactly when to buy inputs, when a working-capital line is needed, and guarantees CSS/DGI/payroll obligations are never missed.
- **Scenario & Sensitivity Planner** — Side-by-side what-ifs on the levers that actually move coffee economics: green price per channel, BoP auction premium for the Geisha lots, USD-side buyer FX, picking-labor rate, roya/weather-driven yield haircut, fertilizer price shock. Shows margin and cash impact of each, so the family plans against ranges (best/base/worst), not a single fragile point estimate.
- **Budget vs. Actual & Season P&L** — Live variance dashboard and end-of-season P&L sliced by plot, varietal, cost category, and sales channel — the close-the-loop view that proves which lots made money (often: a small Geisha block subsidizes volume Caturra) and feeds next year's Budget Builder with real coefficients.
- **Multi-Channel Revenue & AR Ledger** — Tracks each green/roasted sale and BoP auction settlement as a contract→shipment→invoice→payment chain, with expected vs. received and days-to-pay. Connects the operational lot to the money it earned, and feeds both the cash-flow projection and the cost-per-kg margin view with real prices.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** QuickBooks Online / Xero accounting API (sync chart of accounts, post journal entries, pull actuals so budgeting isn't a parallel silo), Cropster (specialty-coffee production/cost & lot management — the de-facto specialty standard; pull lot cost & cupping data, push/pull green inventory), Best of Panama / Specialty Coffee Association of Panama (SCAP) auction results & green-price benchmarks for the Geisha lots, ICE Coffee 'C' futures + Coffee Price Index (KC contract) and ICO/Comunicaffe indicator prices as the commodity revenue benchmark, ECX/CeCafé-style and a green-price reference such as the Coffee Quality Institute / Specialty Coffee price reports for differentials over 'C', Panama DGI/ANIP e-invoicing (Factura Electrónica) and CSS (Caja de Seguro Social) payroll/social-security obligation calendars for tax & contribution forecasting, Banco General / Banistmo / Global Bank (Panama) and an open-banking/CSV bank-feed for cash-position reconciliation; Wise/Mercury for USD buyer settlements, FX rate API (e.g. exchangerate.host / OpenExchangeRates / ECB) for EUR/JPY/KRW buyer-side conversion on green sales, OpenWeather / Visual Crossing / Davis weather-station feed and a satellite NDVI provider (Sentinel-2 via Sentinel Hub or Planet) to drive weather/roya yield-haircut scenarios, Stripe / WooCommerce / Shopify for the roasted-retail D2C channel revenue feed; Anthropic Claude API for the natural-language forecasting/explanation layer

**Data model additions:** budget (crop_year, status draft/approved/closed, scenario_name, base/best/worst flag), budget_line (links to budget, dimension: plot_id | varietal | cost_category | sales_channel, period_month, planned_amount_usd, planned_qty, unit, basis_note), cost_category (taxonomy: harvest_labor, agronomy_inputs, milling, drying, logistics, certification, overhead, reserve_stewardship, capex_amortization), cost_entry / actual_cost (date, cost_category_id, plot_id, lot_code, batch_id, worker_id, amount_usd, qty, unit — the actuals ledger that variance is computed against), lot_cost_rollup (lot_code, allocated_labor_usd, allocated_inputs_usd, milling_usd, overhead_usd, kg_green, cost_per_kg_green, breakeven_per_qq) — derived/materialized, sales_contract (buyer, channel green-export/auction/roasted-retail, lot_code(s), agreed_price_per_lb, currency, incoterm, expected_ship_date), sale / invoice (contract_id, lot_code, qty_lb, price, currency, fx_rate_to_usd, invoice_date, status), payment / ar_entry (invoice_id, amount_usd, received_date, expected_date) — drives cash-flow actuals, cash_flow_projection (month, opening_balance, projected_inflow, projected_outflow, closing_balance, min_balance_alert_threshold), price_benchmark (date, source ICE-C/BoP/CQI, varietal_or_grade, price_per_lb, differential_over_c) — external feed cache, yield_conversion_factor (process_method, cherry_to_green_ratio, default_overridable) — coffee-native coefficient table, fx_rate (date, currency_pair, rate) and obligation_calendar (DGI/ANIP, CSS, payroll due dates and amounts) for the tax/contribution cash timing

**AI / automation:**
- Yield-to-budget autoseed: from historical harvests + plot/varietal/tree data, propose the season yield and cost coefficients (labor $/lata, inputs $/ha) so the owner starts from a learned baseline, not a blank spreadsheet.
- Rolling reforecast that learns the season's intake curve and predicts final yield, finish date, and total picking cost daily — flagging overruns weeks early with a confidence band.
- Natural-language plain-Spanish/English variance narration via Claude: 'Geisha lot JC-564 yielded 8% under plan but auctioned 40% over budget — net +$2,100; Caturra labor is tracking 11% over, projected $14k overrun.'
- Cash-runway anomaly + low-balance early-warning agent that watches the projection and pings before a month dips below threshold, with the specific input-purchase or loan-draw to fix it.
- Price-intelligence assistant: ingests ICE-C, BoP auction history, and buyer offers to recommend an accept/hold decision per lot against the lot's true cost-per-kg-green, with a 'what auction price clears your break-even' answer.
- Scenario co-pilot: 'show me the season if roya cuts Geisha yield 15% and the C drops 20c' — auto-builds the worst-case and quantifies the cash and margin hit.
- Receipt/invoice OCR + auto-categorization of input and fuel purchases into cost_category, so actuals capture is near-zero-effort in the field.

**World-class:** World-class is a budget that is not a once-a-year spreadsheet but a living model: it is generated bottom-up from the same operational data the farm already records (plots, trees, harvests, batches, payroll), it reforecasts itself daily during harvest, and every dollar of cost traces to a lot so the owner sees true margin per Geisha block in real time. It is opinionated for coffee — it speaks in latas, quintales, cherry-to-green ratios, and $/lb-green-over-the-C — not generic 'expense categories.' It closes the loop: actuals from QuickBooks/Cropster/bank feeds flow back so next year's plan starts from this year's truth, and variance is explained in plain language ('Geisha lot JC-564 came in 8% under yield but auctioned 40% over plan, net +$2,100'). It is forecast-first and probabilistic (best/base/worst with weather and price ranges), it warns before cash runs short rather than reporting after, and it lets the family make the bet-the-farm decisions — how much Geisha to plant, whether to take the auction price or hold — on evidence, in minutes, from a phone in the field.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Coffee economics are uniquely punishing and uniquely high-variance, which is exactly why budgeting is a differentiator here. (1) Cash is brutally seasonal: fixed costs (agronomy, shade management, the 200ha reserve stewardship, salaried staff) run all year, but nearly all revenue lands in one harvest-and-sale window — a cash-flow projection isn't nice-to-have, it's solvency. (2) The economics live or die on cost-per-kg-of-green and break-even-per-quintal, computed from cherry-to-green conversion (~5–6:1 washed, varying by method) — generic accounting tools cannot express this; a coffee-native model must. (3) Janson's Geisha changes everything: a single small Geisha block, priced via Best of Panama auctions, can out-earn far larger volume blocks of Caturra/Catuaí — so lot-level margin (not farm-average) is the decision unit, and scenario planning on auction premium is the highest-leverage feature. (4) Labor is paid per lata/can of cherry to seasonal pickers (often Ngäbe-Buglé crews), making harvest cost both the largest line and the most volatile — the rolling harvest-cost forecast directly de-risks it. (5) Panama specifics simplify and sharpen the model: USD is legal tender (Balboa pegged 1:1) so FX risk sits on the buyer side (EUR/JPY/KRW specialty importers), while DGI/ANIP e-invoicing and CSS social-security contributions are the local obligations the cash-flow calendar must guarantee. The farm's vertical integration (grow→process→sell green + roasted) means the model must span agronomy capex through retail margin in one chain — something no off-the-shelf farm-accounting product does coffee-natively.


#### Invoicing, AR/AP & collections
*Priority:* **table-stakes**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm's finance lead / owner-operator (at Janson, a family member or comptroller who owns the books). Secondary actors the module must serve: the export sales manager who negotiates green-coffee contracts with importers/roasters, and the bookkeeper who reconciles bank/Wise/Stripe deposits and answers to the Panama DGI. On a 200-ha estate running grow-to-export, this is one or two people wearing every financial hat — they need leverage, not headcount.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I close a sale of a green-coffee lot to an overseas roaster or importer, I want to issue a compliant export commercial invoice that auto-pulls the lot's real weight, grade/cupping score, variety, process, HS code, Incoterm and contract price from our processing and contract records, so I can ship Cash-Against-Documents fast without re-typing data, FX errors, or a back-and-forth that delays the bill of lading.
- When a buyer is on Net 30/60/90 or a multi-tranche Letter of Credit, I want every open invoice tracked against its due date, partial payments and FX-settled deposits, so I can see exactly who owes us how much, chase late payers automatically, and never let a $40k+ Geisha receivable quietly age past due.
- When I'm paying pickers' daily wages, agronomy inputs (fertilizer, fungicide), mill supplies and freight forwarders, I want every payable captured, approved, scheduled and matched to the budget for that plot/batch, so I can manage cash through the lean pre-harvest months and know my true landed cost per pound before I price the next lot.
- When the DGI requires it (over B/.36,000 annual income or 100+ docs/month), I want each domestic sale and tax document issued as a digitally-signed SFEP/CUFE electronic invoice validated through a PAC, so we stay legal in Panama without learning the XML spec or risking a rejected factura.

**Features:**
- **Lot-linked export invoice builder** — Generates a compliant green-coffee commercial invoice straight from a sales contract + processing batch — weight, variety, grade/cupping score, process method, lot code, HS code (0901.11), Incoterm (FOB Balboa/EXW), country of origin and ICO marks auto-filled. Eliminates re-keying and the data errors that delay bills of lading and customs clearance.
- **Sales contract & pricing register** — Tracks each forward/spot contract: buyer, kg committed, price basis (fixed differential vs C-market, or auction-fixed), Incoterm, payment terms, delivery window. Surfaces over/under-committed inventory so the estate never double-sells a microlot or leaves a premium Geisha lot unsold.
- **AR aging & automated collections** — Dashboard of open receivables by buyer, currency and days-past-due with a dunning ladder (polite reminder → firm → escalation) auto-sent on schedule. Turns a $40k+ aging Geisha receivable from an invisible risk into a tracked, chased line — protects the cash that funds the next harvest.
- **Payment matching & multi-currency reconciliation** — Ingests deposits from Wise, bank wires and Stripe, matches them to invoices (including partial/tranche LC payments), books the realized FX gain/loss vs USD/Balboa, and closes the invoice. Bookkeeper stops manually tying deposits to invoices across currencies.
- **AP & purchase/expense capture with plot/batch costing** — Captures bills (inputs, freight forwarders, certification fees, mill supplies) and daily picker payroll, routes approvals, schedules payment runs, and tags each cost to a plot, lot or batch. Produces a true landed cost-per-pound that feeds pricing — so the farm never sells below cost.
- **Panama SFEP / CUFE e-invoicing connector** — Issues domestic sales as DGI-compliant XML facturas electrónicas, digitally signed (FEA) and pre-validated through a registered PAC, returning the CUFE and QR. Keeps Janson legal once it crosses the B/.36,000 / 100-docs-month threshold without anyone touching the XML spec.
- **Cash-flow forecast & harvest-cycle treasury view** — Projects inflows (contracted AR by expected settlement) against outflows (payroll, inputs, debt) across the agricultural year, flagging the lean pre-harvest crunch months. Lets the owner decide when to seek pre-harvest financing or an advance against a signed contract.
- **Document pack & financing-document generator** — One-click export document set — commercial invoice, packing list, certificate of origin, ICO certificate reference, phytosanitary placeholder — bundled for Cash-Against-Documents release and for lenders financing the lot against the contract.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Panama DGI SFEP e-invoicing via a registered PAC (e.g. EDICOM, Basware, FacturaHQ) — XML factura, FEA digital signature, CUFE + QR; SFEP free facturador below the B/.36,000 / 100-doc threshold, QuickBooks Online and Xero (GL sync, AR/AP push, chart-of-accounts mapping) for the books of record, Wise Business API (multi-currency receiving accounts, FX rates, incoming-payment webhooks), Stripe (card/ACH for domestic & roaster sales, Stripe Invoicing, payment-link checkout), Bank wire / SWIFT MT103 + ACH reconciliation; Mercado Pago / Yappy for local Panama payments, ICE / C-market coffee futures price feed (ICO indicator prices, NY 'C' contract) for differential-priced contracts, Cropster / Cropster Hub and Sucafina/importer portals for contract, lot and shipment data exchange, ICO export certificate workflow + Panama customs / Aduanas and a freight-forwarder/Incoterm document feed, FX rate provider (e.g. Open Exchange Rates / ECB) for realized & unrealized gain-loss bookkeeping, Letter-of-Credit / trade-finance document standards (UCP 600, Cash-Against-Documents) and optional ag-lending platforms for pre-harvest advances against signed contracts
