**Data model additions:** sales_contract (id, buyer_id, incoterm, payment_terms, price_basis, c_market_differential, kg_committed, delivery_window, status), buyer / customer (id, name, country, currency, default_terms, tax_id, importer/roaster type), invoice (id, contract_id, lot_code, type [export|domestic], hs_code, incoterm, currency, subtotal, fx_rate, issued_date, due_date, status [draft|sent|paid|overdue], cufe nullable), invoice_line_item (id, invoice_id, lot_code, variety, process, grade_score, kg, unit_price, amount), payment / receipt (id, invoice_id, amount, currency, rail [wise|stripe|wire|yappy], received_date, fx_gain_loss, lc_tranche nullable), vendor (id, name, category [labor|inputs|freight|certification|mill], tax_id, terms), bill / payable (id, vendor_id, amount, currency, due_date, status, plot_id nullable, lot_code nullable, batch_id nullable), payment_run (id, scheduled_date, status, total) + payout lines, ledger_account / chart_of_accounts mapping (account, gl_code, external_id for QBO/Xero), tax_document (id, invoice_id, cufe, pac_provider, signature_ref, qr, validation_status), fx_rate (date, base, quote, rate) and cash_flow_forecast (period, projected_in, projected_out)

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-match incoming Wise/bank/Stripe deposits to open invoices across currencies and partial/LC tranches, proposing the booking and FX gain/loss for one-click confirm
- Smart collections agent: predicts which receivables will go late from buyer payment history and seasonality, drafts and times dunning messages in the buyer's language, escalates only stuck ones
- OCR + LLM bill capture: photograph a vendor invoice or input receipt, extract amount/vendor/date, auto-categorize to a GL account and tag to the right plot/lot/batch
- Landed cost-per-pound and margin-per-lot computation that rolls labor, inputs, processing yield loss and freight into each lot, and recommends a floor price before a contract is signed
- Cash-flow forecasting that learns the harvest cycle and flags the pre-harvest crunch with a financing recommendation (amount, timing) against contracted AR
- Contract-vs-shipment reconciliation: flags when invoiced kg/grade/price drift from the signed contract or the actual processed lot before the document pack goes out
- Anomaly/fraud guard on payables — duplicate bills, out-of-band vendor bank-detail changes, prices above seasonal norms

**World-class:** World-class is a closed loop from cherry to cash with zero re-keying: the moment a processing batch becomes a sellable green lot, it is matched to a sales contract, and a compliant export invoice (HS code, Incoterm, ICO marks, cupping score) plus the full Cash-Against-Documents pack generate themselves; receivables age on a live board with auto-dunning; Wise/Stripe/wire deposits self-match across currencies and book FX gain/loss to QuickBooks; every picker wage and input bill is captured and costed to its plot/batch so landed cost-per-pound is always known before the next lot is priced; domestic sales clear DGI's SFEP/CUFE in one click via a PAC; and a treasury forecast shows the pre-harvest cash crunch months ahead so financing is arranged, not scrambled. The owner sees one number — net margin per lot and cash runway — and trusts it.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson sits at the high end of specialty: Best-of-Panama-calibre Geisha lots clear at extraordinary prices ($4,542/lb in 2024; a 98-point Julith washed Geisha in 2025), so a single microlot invoice can be a five-figure, FX-denominated, Cash-Against-Documents receivable where one aging error or one mistyped HS-code/Incoterm on the commercial invoice costs real money and stalls a container. Auction-fixed lots, forward differential contracts against the NY 'C', and direct-trade roaster relationships ($3–5/lb microlots) coexist — the module must price and invoice all three. It must carry the lot's cupping score, variety, process and traceability lot code (which already exist in Janson's processing model) straight onto the invoice as both a quality claim and a customs description, generate the ICO export certificate reference, and settle multi-currency deposits from importers like Sucafina or direct roasters via Wise/wire — all while the same engine drops domestic Panama sales into DGI's SFEP/CUFE e-invoicing through a PAC.


#### Banking, payments & multi-currency/FX
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Owner-operator / farm CFO (the Janson family principal who signs export contracts and pays the crew), supported by a part-time bookkeeper. On a specialty farm this is one person wearing the treasury, AP, and AR hats at once: they negotiate green-coffee sales in USD/EUR/GBP/JPY, receive international wires, then turn around to pay ~14 crew and local suppliers in cash/Yappy, all while financing the ~6-month gap between harvest spend and buyer payment.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When an international buyer commits to a lot at a per-pound price quoted in their currency (e.g. a Tokyo roaster buying Geisha in JPY), I want one place that locks the FX rate, issues the proforma/commercial invoice, and tracks the inbound wire end-to-end, so I can know my real USD landed price per lot before I ship and not lose 3-5% to surprise bank spreads and lifting fees.
- When the crew clocks out on Friday, I want piece-rate picker pay (kg x rate) and daily-rate staff pay computed straight from the harvest and attendance records and disbursed in one batch over Yappy/ACH, so I can pay 14 people accurately in minutes instead of counting cash and risking a mispaid or unpaid picker walking off mid-season.
- When I am 5 months from the first buyer payment but already spending on labor, fertilizer, and drying, I want to see my projected cash runway by week and tap pre-shipment finance (a green-clause LC advance or an exporter line) against signed contracts, so I can make payroll through the harvest without a personal loan or selling a lot early at a discount.
- When a wire lands short or late, I want the system to auto-reconcile the deposit against the lot invoice, flag the FX/fee variance, and chase the balance, so I can close each export sale to the cent and trust the YTD revenue figure on the dashboard.

**Features:**
- **Multi-currency export AR (invoice-to-cash per lot)** — Proforma + commercial invoice generated from a sales contract tied to a real lot code (e.g. JC-564), priced in the buyer's currency with a live landed-USD preview; turns ytd_revenue_usd from a single narrative figure into the sum of reconciled, traceable lot sales.
- **Multi-currency wallet + smart payout routing** — Hold USD/EUR/GBP/JPY balances; route each disbursement down the cheapest correct rail (inbound wire to receive, Yappy/ACH for crew + Panama suppliers, wire for foreign vendors), cutting per-transaction FX + lifting fees the farm currently eats blindly.
- **Crew payroll engine (piece-rate + daily-rate)** — Auto-computes pay from existing harvests (kg x picker rate) and attendance (daily_rate_usd x present days), produces a batch payout file, and pays the whole crew over Yappy in one approval, eliminating cash-counting errors and unpaid-picker risk.
- **FX rate-lock & exposure board** — Snapshot the rate at contract signing, mark every open invoice to market, and show total currency exposure by buyer so the owner sees gain/loss before settlement and can decide whether to lock a forward.
- **Cash-flow runway & pre-finance desk** — Week-by-week projection from committed contracts (AR), payroll/supplier obligations (AP), and harvest spend; flags the harvest cash gap and surfaces eligible green-clause LC / pre-shipment advance amounts against signed contracts so the farm finances the season instead of discounting lots.
- **Auto bank reconciliation & fee/variance ledger** — Matches inbound deposits to lot invoices via bank feed, attributes shortfalls to FX move vs bank fee vs partial payment, and books a clean double-entry trail so each export sale closes to the cent and the dashboard revenue is trustworthy.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Wise Business API (multi-currency receiving accounts + batch payouts at mid-market FX), Airwallex API (API-first multi-currency accounts, global collection, FX conversion), Payoneer (alternative inbound receiving for buyers in 190+ countries), Yappy / Banco General API (Panama instant local payments to crew + suppliers; ~25M txns/mo rail), ACH Panama / SINPE-equivalent local bank-to-bank transfers, SWIFT / Fedwire inbound wire reference + MT103 parsing for reconciliation, open.er-api / ECB / OANDA / Wise FX rate feeds for daily mark-to-market, Green-clause Letter of Credit + pre-shipment finance providers and exporter trade-finance lines (e.g. Ebury, specialty trade-finance desks), QuickBooks Online / Xero (sync the lot-AR and payroll ledger to the books), Stripe / direct e-commerce payment for roasted retail and farm-store sales (card + multi-currency checkout), Panama DGI electronic invoicing (factura electronica) for compliant commercial invoices

**Data model additions:** sales_contract (id, buyer_id, lot_code FK->lots, qty_lbs, price_per_lb, currency, incoterm, signed_date, fx_rate_at_signing), buyer (id, name, country, currency, bank_rail, contact), invoice (id, contract_id FK, type[proforma|commercial], currency, amount, landed_usd_estimate, issued_date, due_date, status), payment (id, invoice_id FK, rail[wire|wise|airwallex|yappy|ach], amount, currency, received_date, fx_rate_actual, bank_fee, variance_usd), fx_rate (date, base, quote, rate, source), fx_position / hedge (contract_id FK, locked_rate, instrument[forward|spot], expiry), wallet_balance (currency, available, held), payout_batch (id, period, total, rail, status, approved_by), payout_line (batch_id FK, worker_id FK->workers, basis[piece_rate|daily_rate], kg, days, amount), supplier (id, name, currency, rail) + supplier_bill (AP), finance_facility (id, type[green_clause_lc|pre_shipment_advance|line], contract_id FK, principal, advance_pct, drawn, rate, terms), ledger_entry (double-entry GL backing reconciliation)

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-match inbound wires to open lot invoices and explain each variance as FX move vs bank fee vs partial/short payment.
- FX timing assistant: given open exposure and rate-feed trends, recommend lock-now vs wait and size a forward against committed contracts.
- Cash-runway forecaster that blends committed AR, payroll obligations, and seasonal harvest spend to predict the week the farm goes cash-negative and the minimum pre-finance draw to cover it.
- Landed-margin advisor that prices a prospective contract in any currency, nets bank/FX/lifting fees, and shows real USD margin per lot before the owner accepts.
- Payroll anomaly detection (picker paid for kg with no matching harvest record, daily-rate paid on a rest-day, duplicate payout line) before the batch is approved.
- Pre-fill Panama DGI electronic commercial invoices and reconcile them against the GL to flag compliance gaps.

**World-class:** A world-class farm treasury makes money flow as traceable as the coffee. Every dollar is attributable to a lot the same way every cherry is: the owner sees real landed-USD margin per lot the moment a buyer commits, FX is locked or hedged on purpose rather than discovered at settlement, inbound wires auto-reconcile to invoices with fee/variance attribution, and the whole 14-person crew is paid accurately and instantly from harvest data in a single approval. Cash runway is projected weeks out so the harvest is financed by design (green-clause LC against contracts), not by selling lots cheap. Multi-currency balances let the farm receive in the buyer's currency and pay locally over Yappy without paying a spread twice. It feels like a private bank that already understands coffee.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Panama is a USD economy, so the trap is assuming there's no FX problem, when in reality the money risk is inbound: a single 100-lb Geisha lot can sell for thousands of dollars to a Japanese or Korean roaster who pays in JPY/KRW, and the farm loses 3-5% to bank spreads and lifting fees it never measures. Janson's auction-grade Geisha and reserve lots are high-value, low-volume, sold per-lot to named buyers, which makes per-lot invoice-to-cash and rate-lock far more valuable here than for a commodity producer. The same lot code (JC-564) that carries traceability from picker to drying bed should carry the sale through to a reconciled deposit, so a buyer paying a Geisha premium can see provenance and the farm can prove landed margin. Locally, the crew and Volcan suppliers live on Yappy and cash, so the payout side is Panama-domestic while the receivables side is global, which is the exact split this capability is built to bridge. The 6-month harvest-to-payment gap is acute for a family farm financing labor up front, making the pre-shipment / green-clause LC desk a real lifeline rather than a luxury.


#### Financing — ag-loans, grants & crop insurance
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Diana Janson — the farm owner/operator who is also the de-facto CFO. She is the one who signs loan paperwork at Banco Nacional / BDA in David, decides whether to renew ISA insurance each season, chases the MIDA/PROCAFÉ grant deadlines, and carries the cash-flow risk between the single annual harvest payout and 12 months of standing labor + fertilizer + diesel cost. Secondary user: an external agronomist/accountant or a lender's underwriter who needs a credible, evidence-backed view of the farm to extend or price credit.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When the harvest is still 6 months away but payroll, fertilizer and diesel are due now, I want to see exactly how much pre-harvest working capital I need and from which lender (Banco Nacional, BDA, or a specialty-coffee advance against my Geisha contract), so I can cover the gap without selling green coffee early at a bad price.
- When I am sitting in the ISA regional office in David renewing my agricultural insurance, I want to hand the underwriter my real per-plot yield history, altitude, variety mix and the lot-level harvest record straight from the system, so I get accurate coverage and a fair premium instead of guessing and being over- or under-insured.
- When a frost, drought, or excess-rain event hits during flowering, I want a parametric weather policy to detect the trigger automatically from the same weather station data I already track and pay out within days, so I am not waiting months for a manual loss adjuster while my cash runs out.
- When MIDA, PROCAFÉ, or an international body (USDA, IDB, Rainforest Alliance) opens a coffee-renovation grant or a low-interest credit line, I want to be alerted with the eligibility rules and a pre-filled application drawing on my farm records, so I never miss a deadline or leave subsidized money on the table.

**Features:**
- **Capital & Liabilities Ledger** — One canonical place for every loan, line of credit, grant, and advance — principal, rate, term, collateral (which plots/lots are pledged), disbursement and repayment schedule — so the owner sees total debt service against the season at a glance instead of in a drawer of paper.
- **Pre-Harvest Cash-Flow & Financing-Gap Planner** — Projects month-by-month cash position from standing labor + input + processing costs against the single annual harvest-sale inflow, surfaces the exact size and timing of the working-capital gap, and recommends the cheapest instrument to cover it — turning the seasonal cash crunch from a panic into a planned drawdown.
- **Underwriting Data Pack (lender/insurer export)** — A one-click, evidence-backed dossier — per-plot area/altitude/variety, multi-season yield history, lot traceability, processing throughput, worker roster — generated from the operational data the farm already captures, so Janson walks into ISA or Banco Nacional with the documentation lenders normally never get, unlocking better rates and faster approval.
- **Crop Insurance Manager (ISA + parametric)** — Tracks active ISA policies (coverage, premium, insured plots, claim status) and bundles an optional parametric weather-index policy keyed to the farm's own station data, so a covered drought/frost/excess-rain event triggers a fast payout automatically — replacing slow, disputable manual loss adjustment.
- **Grants & Subsidized-Credit Radar** — A curated, alert-driven catalog of relevant Panamanian and international programs (MIDA, PROCAFÉ, BDA preferential lines, IDB/USDA/Rainforest Alliance funds) with eligibility matching against the farm's profile and deadline reminders, so the owner captures non-dilutive money she would otherwise miss.
- **Collateral & Pledge Tracker** — Maps which plots, equipment, or forward green-coffee contracts back each facility, flags over-pledging and release conditions, and ties pledged lots to live harvest/processing status — so the owner never double-pledges a lot and lenders can see live collateral coverage.
- **Carbon & Sustainability Finance Module** — Quantifies the 200-ha reserve and shade-canopy carbon stock into a financeable asset and connects it to carbon-credit revenue (Rabobank ACORN model) and sustainability-linked loan rate discounts, turning conservation Janson already does into cash flow and cheaper capital — a future bet that compounds the brand.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** ISA — Instituto de Seguro Agropecuario (Panama state agricultural insurer; David/Boquete-Volcán regional office; actively expanding coffee coverage) — policy + claim records, Banco Nacional de Panamá (BNP) and Banco de Desarrollo Agropecuario (BDA) — domestic ag-credit lines, preferential/0%-rate producer programs, Parametric weather-index insurers: Etherisc (smart-contract automated payouts), OKO, and Pula — rainfall/temperature/frost-trigger crop cover bundled with credit, Specialty-coffee trade & pre-harvest finance: Sucafina (Farm-to-Roaster financing), Starbucks Global Farmer Fund, Grow Ahead (crowdfunded harvest loans), Specialty Coffee Finance / Alliance Funding Group, MIDA (Ministerio de Desarrollo Agropecuario) and PROCAFÉ / coffee-sector grant & renovation programs; IDB, USDA and Rainforest Alliance development funds, Weather data for parametric triggers: on-farm station + ECMWF / NOAA / Visual Crossing / Tomorrow.io APIs, cross-referenced with satellite NDVI (Sentinel-2 via Sentinel Hub) for yield-loss verification, Rabobank ACORN carbon platform + Verra/Gold Standard registries for shade-coffee/agroforestry carbon credits as collateral and sustainability-linked-loan rate discounts, Banking/FX rails for disbursement & repayment reconciliation (ACH/SWIFT, Wise, or local bank file import); USD is Panama's currency so no FX layer needed domestically

**Data model additions:** financing_facilities — loans, lines of credit, grants, advances (type, lender, principal_usd, interest_rate, term, disbursed_date, status), facility_schedule — per-period disbursement and repayment rows tied to a facility (due_date, principal_due, interest_due, paid), collateral_pledges — join of facility ↔ pledged asset (plot_id / lot_code / equipment), pledge_value, release_condition, insurance_policies — insurer (ISA/parametric), coverage_type, insured_plot_ids, sum_insured_usd, premium_usd, period, status, insurance_claims — policy_id, event_type (drought/frost/excess-rain/pest), trigger_value, filed_date, payout_usd, status, parametric_triggers — policy_id, metric (rainfall_mm/temp_c), threshold, window, weather_source — the rule that auto-fires a claim, grant_programs + grant_applications — program catalog (body, eligibility, deadline, max_award) and the farm's application/award records, cashflow_projection — monthly projected inflow/outflow rows feeding the financing-gap planner, carbon_assets — measured CO2 stock by plot/reserve zone, registry, credits_issued, price_per_tonne, revenue_usd

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-assembled, narrated Underwriting Data Pack — an LLM turns the raw per-plot/lot operational record into a lender-ready credit memo (yield stability, diversification, traceability strengths) plus a defensible repayment-capacity model.
- Financing-gap forecaster — ML projects the cash crunch from historical cost + harvest-timing patterns and recommends the optimal instrument and drawdown timing (cheapest cost of capital for the gap).
- Parametric trigger watchdog — continuously evaluates weather-station + NDVI data against each policy's thresholds and auto-drafts/files the claim the moment a covered event fires, with the owner only approving the send.
- Grant-eligibility matcher — scans incoming MIDA/PROCAFÉ/IDB program rules, matches against the farm profile, ranks by award-vs-effort, and pre-fills applications from existing farm records.
- Covenant & deadline sentinel — proactively warns before a loan covenant breach, an ISA renewal lapse, or a grant deadline, so nothing irreversible happens by oversight.

**World-class:** "A single financial cockpit where the farm's operational data is the credit and insurance underwriting evidence — not a separate spreadsheet. The owner sees total debt service, the precise pre-harvest financing gap, and every active policy in one view; subsidized grants and preferential credit lines surface automatically with pre-filled applications; insurance is bundled with credit and pays out parametrically within days of a weather event instead of months of manual adjustment; and conservation (the reserve, shade canopy) is monetized into carbon revenue and cheaper sustainability-linked capital. Capital becomes a planned, optimized input like fertilizer — sized, timed, and priced from real data — rather than an annual scramble. World-class means the farm never misses non-dilutive money, never over-pays for credit it can document away, and never carries an uninsured weather risk it could have transferred."

**Coffee-specific angle:** "Coffee's defining financial pain is the single annual harvest payout against 12 months of standing cost — the textbook working-capital gap that this pillar is built around, and the reason coffee is one of ISA's most-insured crops. Janson is uniquely positioned to win here: ISA's David office literally covers Boquete-Volcán and is actively designing expanded coffee coverage, so Janson can be an early, well-documented adopter. The farm's premium asset mix — Geisha and reserve/Panama-Geisha-auction-grade lots — makes forward-contract and pre-harvest advances against named lots far more bankable than commodity coffee, and the 200-ha quetzal-habitat reserve plus shade canopy is a ready-made carbon and sustainability-linked-loan asset (Rabobank ACORN model). The same per-lot traceability data that justifies a Best-of-Panama auction price doubles as the underwriting evidence that gets cheaper credit and accurate insurance — the operational story and the financial story are the same story."


#### Tax & regulatory filing — Panama + export
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm owner-operator / family principal at Janson who also wears the "finance & compliance" hat (in many Volcán farms this is the owner plus an external contador/gestor). Secondary stakeholders the module must serve: the external Panamanian accountant (contador público autorizado) who files DGI returns, and the export-logistics coordinator who assembles MIDA/VUCE/ICO/EUDR paperwork per shipment. This persona is non-technical, Spanish-first, deadline-driven, and personally liable for penalties — exactly the kind of user who today juggles a shoebox of PDFs, WhatsApp threads with the gestor, and the DGI/SFEP web portal by hand.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When the DGI monthly ITBMS / annual ISR deadline approaches, I want my green-coffee and roasted sales, payroll, and input costs already mapped to the right tax lines, so I can file (or hand my contador a clean package) without a last-minute scramble and without risking a penalty.
- When I issue an invoice for a green-coffee or roasted sale (domestic or export), I want it transmitted as a compliant factura electrónica through a DGI-authorized PAC under the SFEP, so the sale is legally valid the moment money changes hands and I never fall back to a manual factura.
- When I'm about to ship a lot abroad, I want every export document — MIDA/DECA phytosanitary certificate, ICO certificate of origin, VUCE export declaration, commercial invoice, packing list, and EUDR geolocation/due-diligence statement — auto-assembled from the lot's existing farm data, so the container clears customs on time and the buyer's compliance team accepts it on the first pass.
- When tax season comes, I want to know exactly how the agricultural ISR exemption (gross income up to B/.350,000) and reinvestment incentives apply to my numbers this year, so I claim every benefit I'm legally entitled to and have the evidence ready if DGI audits.

**Features:**
- **Fiscal calendar & obligations engine (Panama)** — A living calendar of every Janson obligation — monthly ITBMS, annual ISR, planilla/CSS social-security, tasa única, municipal aviso de operación renewal, MIDA/AUPSA registrations, ICO/export permit renewals — with countdown reminders, owner + contador assignment, and a green/amber/red 'are we filed?' status so nothing is ever missed.
- **Factura electrónica issuance via certified PAC (SFEP)** — Issue DGI-compliant electronic invoices and credit notes for green/roasted sales straight from a sale record; the system builds the XML, routes it to a registered PAC for validation, stores the CUFE and authorized PDF, and flags when Janson crosses the B/.36,000 / 100-docs-per-month threshold that forces a PAC instead of the free tool.
- **Tax-line ledger & ISR/ITBMS auto-classifier** — Every sale, payroll run, and farm input cost is tagged to the correct Panamanian tax line and ITBMS rate (incl. exempt agricultural categories), producing a one-click ISR estimate and ITBMS return draft, plus a clear view of the B/.350,000 agricultural-income exemption headroom and the up-to-30% agricultural-reinvestment deduction.
- **Export documentation assembler (per-lot dossier)** — For a shipment, pull the lot's plot/variety/process/weight data already in the system and generate the MIDA/DECA phytosanitary certificate request, ICO certificate of origin (with ICO mark and parcel serials), VUCE export declaration package, commercial invoice + packing list, and bill-of-lading data — turning a multi-day, multi-agency paper chase into a reviewed-and-submitted dossier.
- **EUDR & traceability compliance pack** — Auto-generates the EU Deforestation Regulation due-diligence statement for EU-bound lots: GPS polygon geolocation of each producing plot, the deforestation-free attestation (land cleared after 31 Dec 2020 is non-compliant), HS code, and a chain-of-custody trace back to harvest — the exact evidence EU buyers now demand under the Dec 2025/2026 EUDR rollout, which Janson's 200-ha reserve makes a genuine selling point.
- **Audit-ready document vault & filing archive** — Immutable, timestamped storage of every filed return, PAC-authorized invoice (CUFE), certificate, and receipt, organized by fiscal period and by export shipment, with retention timers — so a DGI, MIDA, or buyer audit is answered by exporting one folder instead of reconstructing years of paperwork.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** DGI / MEF SFEP (Sistema de Factura Electrónica de Panamá) under Ley 256 — the official e-invoicing standard, A DGI-registered PAC (Proveedor Autorizado Calificado), e.g. EDICOM, P4 Software, or another DGI-listed PAC, for invoice validation and CUFE issuance, DGI e-Tax 2.0 portal / declarations for ISR and ITBMS filing, CSS (Caja de Seguro Social) SIPE for planilla / social-security filings tied to the existing workers module, MIDA — Dirección Ejecutiva de Cuarentena Agropecuaria (DECA) for phytosanitary certificates; AUPSA for food-safety registration, MICI VUCE (Ventanilla Única de Comercio Exterior) — Chiriquí regional window — for export declarations, ICO (International Coffee Organization) Certificate of Origin rules and ICO identification mark/serial format, EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) Information System / TRACES for due-diligence statement submission, Autoridad Nacional de Aduanas (customs) declaration data and HS-code (Sistema Armonizado) reference, Municipio de Tierras Altas / Aviso de Operación (PanamaEmprende) for the operating-license renewal, Exchange-rate / FX reference (Panama uses USD/Balboa 1:1, but export invoices in EUR/other currencies need a rate source), Optional: QuickBooks Online or a local accounting package the contador already uses, for a clean handoff of the tax-line ledger

**Data model additions:** tax_obligations (obligation type: ITBMS/ISR/CSS/tasa_unica/aviso_operacion/MIDA/ICO, period, due_date, status, assignee, jurisdiction), tax_filings (links obligation, period, amounts, filed_date, confirmation/reference no., attached return doc, filed_by), electronic_invoices (sale_id, invoice/credit-note type, customer, line items, ITBMS amount, CUFE, PAC, xml_ref, authorized_pdf_ref, status), tax_line_mappings (maps a sale/cost/payroll category → DGI tax line + ITBMS rate / exempt flag), export_shipments (shipment id, buyer, destination country, incoterm, container/BL ref, departure date, status), shipment_lots (join: export_shipment ↔ lot_code ↔ green-coffee weight & grade), export_documents (shipment_id, doc_type: phytosanitary/ICO_origin/VUCE_decl/commercial_invoice/packing_list/EUDR_DDS, issuing_authority, reference no., issued_date, file_ref, status), plot_geolocation (plot_id → GPS polygon coordinates + deforestation-free attestation + land-establishment date — feeds EUDR), certifications (body, scope, certificate no., issued/expiry dates — e.g. organic, Rainforest Alliance, ICO exporter registration), compliance_documents_vault (entity_ref, period/shipment, doc_type, immutable hash, retention_until, uploaded_by)

**AI / automation:**
- Deadline-risk copilot: scans the obligations calendar plus current ledger state and surfaces 'you are 9 days from the ITBMS filing and 3 invoices are still un-issued' with the exact remediation steps.
- Auto-classification of sales/costs to Panamanian tax lines and ITBMS rates, with a confidence score and a 'review the 4 uncertain ones' queue for the contador — learns from corrections.
- Document extraction: OCR an incoming supplier receipt, fertilizer invoice, or freight bill and auto-create the cost record, tax-line tag, and vault entry.
- Export-dossier completeness checker: an LLM that reads the assembled MIDA/ICO/VUCE/EUDR pack against each authority's and buyer's checklist and flags the missing field before submission (e.g. 'ICO parcel serial not set', 'EUDR plot polygon missing').
- ISR optimization advisor: models the agricultural-income exemption (B/.350,000) and the up-to-30% reinvestment incentive against this year's projected numbers and recommends, with citations, the legally optimal claim.
- Spanish-first natural-language Q&A over the farm's own filings and Panamanian rules ('¿ya pagamos el ITBMS de mayo?', '¿qué necesito para exportar este lote a Alemania?').

**World-class:** "World-class means compliance is a by-product of running the farm, not a separate chore. The moment a green-coffee lot is sold, a valid factura electrónica is issued through a PAC and the sale is already mapped to its DGI tax line; the moment a shipment is booked, its full export dossier (MIDA phytosanitary, ICO origin, VUCE declaration, EUDR due-diligence statement with plot GPS polygons) is assembled from data the farm already captured at harvest and processing — reviewed, not retyped. The owner sees a single green/amber/red board of every Panama + export obligation with countdowns and one-click filing or contador handoff; deadlines are never missed and penalties never accrue. An audit by DGI, MIDA, or an EU buyer is answered by exporting one timestamped folder. It rivals what Cropster (traceability), Avalara/EDICOM (e-invoicing), and Descartes (trade compliance) each do in isolation — but unified on the same lot data, in Spanish, tuned to Panamanian law and specialty-coffee export reality."

**Coffee-specific angle:** "Janson's whole value chain — grow → harvest → wet/dry mill → green + roasted sales → export — is exactly the data an integrated tax-and-export module needs, and the existing schema already carries the keystone: the lot_code linking harvests → processing_batches. Tax/export filing is where that traceability finally pays off commercially. Two Janson-specific edges: (1) As a specialty origin selling Geisha and reserve lots to EU and other premium buyers, EUDR compliance is now mandatory for EU market access (Dec 2025/2026 rollout) and Janson's 200-ha private nature reserve and shade-grown, quetzal-habitat story make a deforestation-free, GPS-polygon-backed due-diligence statement a marketing asset, not just a hurdle — it lets Janson command premiums where less-traceable origins get shut out. (2) As an agricultural producer, Janson likely sits under or near the B/.350,000 gross-income ISR exemption and qualifies for agricultural reinvestment incentives — money left on the table today if no one models it. The ICO certificate of origin, MIDA/DECA phytosanitary cert, and MICI VUCE Chiriquí window are the concrete Panamanian export gates this module turns from a paper chase into one reviewed dossier per shipment."


#### Certifications — Organic, Rainforest Alliance, Fair Trade, Bird-Friendly, C.A.F.E.
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: Janette Janson, the farm's agronomist and de-facto compliance/certification lead (owns the audit binders and management plan). Secondary: Miguel Janson (supervisor/owner-operator who signs labor declarations and the social-premium reserve) and the external Internal Control System (ICS) coordinator / third-party auditor from the certifying body (CERES, Mayacert, NaturaCert, Rainforest Alliance/SCS, FLOCERT, Smithsonian) who needs lot-level evidence on demand.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a Rainforest Alliance / Organic / Bird-Friendly auditor schedules the annual inspection, I want every input application, shade record, labor declaration and lot trace already mapped to that specific standard's clauses, so I can pass the audit in days instead of digging through paper binders for three weeks.
- When a picker or agronomist is about to apply any input to a plot, I want the system to block prohibited substances and warn on buffer-zone and pre-harvest-interval rules for that plot's active certifications, so I never void our Organic status with one wrong spray.
- When I sell a Geisha micro-lot as 'Rainforest Alliance Certified' or 'Bird-Friendly', I want an unbroken chain-of-custody from the exact plots through fermentation, drying and the green-coffee bag marks, so the buyer (and an EUDR/customs check) can verify the claim and I can defend the premium.
- When the season closes, I want to calculate and allocate the Fair Trade sustainability premium owed to workers and the reserve fund, so I can prove to FLOCERT that the social premium reached the people who earned it and renew without a finding.

**Features:**
- **Certification Registry & Renewal Cockpit** — One status board per scheme (Organic NOP+EU, Rainforest Alliance 2020, Fair Trade, Smithsonian Bird-Friendly, C.A.F.E. Practices) with scope (which plots/lots are in-scope), certificate numbers, issuing body, valid-from/expiry, and a countdown to the next inspection plus auto-reminders, killing the 'we forgot to renew and lost the premium' failure mode.
- **Standard-to-Evidence Mapping Engine** — Each scheme's checklist clause is linked to the live farm record that satisfies it (shade_pct/altitude per plot, input log entries, worker attendance/wage rows, lot traces). The auditor sees a clause and one click reveals the evidence, turning audit prep from weeks of binder assembly into a generated, gap-flagged dossier.
- **Prohibited-Input Guard at point of application** — Extends the existing Pest Control / Fertilizing task into a structured input-application log (substance, active ingredient, rate, plot, buffer distance, re-entry & pre-harvest interval). It hard-blocks substances on the Organic National List / RA prohibited list for any plot under that certification and warns on PHI before harvest, making it impossible to silently decertify a plot.
- **Shade & Habitat Conformance Monitor** — Validates Bird-Friendly (>=40% shade, >=10m canopy, >=11 woody species, foliage strata) and RA shade/buffer rules against per-plot shade_pct and a new canopy/species inventory, producing the exact shade map and species count auditors demand and flagging plots that drift out of conformance before the inspector arrives.
- **Lot-Level Chain-of-Custody & Claim Ledger** — Tags each lot_code with the certifications its source plots carry, enforces non-mixing through wet/dry mill stages, and computes certified-volume reconciliation (certified-in vs certified-out) so every 'RA Certified'/'Bird-Friendly' claim on a green-coffee bag is defensible and the mass-balance proves no over-selling of the certified mark.
- **Fair Trade Premium & Reserve Ledger** — Allocates the Fair Trade premium per kg of certified coffee sold, splits it across workers and the mandated community/reserve fund, and records the democratic-decision and payout trail, producing the FLOCERT premium-use report and proving the social premium actually reached the people.
- **Audit Workspace & Non-Conformity Tracker** — A per-inspection workspace holding the auditor's findings, corrective-action plans with owners and due dates, and re-inspection evidence, so a minor non-conformity is closed on schedule instead of becoming a major finding that suspends the certificate.
- **EUDR Deforestation-Free Geolocation Pack** — Captures plot polygons/GPS and the established_year land-use cutoff, cross-checks against the 2020 deforestation baseline, and exports the EU Deforestation Regulation due-diligence statement and geolocation file required to keep exporting to Europe.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Rainforest Alliance Certification Platform (RACP) and the RA 2020 Sustainable Agriculture Standard data templates / traceability APIs, FLOCERT / Fairtrade International (FLOID, audit checklist, premium-use reporting), Smithsonian Migratory Bird Center Bird-Friendly coffee criteria and SMBC-approved certifier (e.g. NaturaCert/OIA) workflows, USDA NOP Organic INTEGRITY database + EU Organic TRACES NT, via accredited certifiers operating in Panama (CERES, Mayacert, Kiwa BCS), Starbucks C.A.F.E. Practices scorecard (SCS Global Services / SI verifier) and the Verifier/Inspector portal, EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) TRACES/Information System for geolocation due-diligence statements, Sedex SMETA / amfori BSCI for the labor and ethical-trade audit pillar that overlaps Fair Trade, Cropster / Cropster Origin for lot traceability and cupping-score linkage to certified lots, SCA cupping protocol and ICO (International Coffee Organization) marks, GrainPro/Ecotact bag and ICO lot identifiers on export, Panama MIDA (Ministerio de Desarrollo Agropecuario) and MiAmbiente/ANAM records for the 200ha private reserve / quetzal-habitat conservation evidence, Esri ArcGIS / QGIS or Google Earth Engine + Sentinel-2 NDVI for shade-canopy and deforestation-baseline verification, DocuSign / e-signature for management-plan and worker declaration sign-off; GS1 / QR for consumer-facing claim verification

**Data model additions:** certification_schemes (catalog: NOP/EU Organic, RA 2020, Fair Trade, Bird-Friendly, C.A.F.E. Practices; clauses, shade/wage thresholds), certifications (farm-held instance: scheme, certifier/issuing_body, certificate_no, status, valid_from, expiry, next_inspection), certification_plot_scope (join: which plots are in-scope for each certification, with in-scope date ranges), certification_lot_scope (join: which lot_codes carry which certifications + certified-volume balance), input_applications (substance, active_ingredient, rate, plot_id, applied_by worker, buffer_m, reentry_interval, pre_harvest_interval, allow/block decision) promotes Pest Control/Fertilizing tasks into the audited input log, prohibited_substances (per-scheme banned/restricted list the guard enforces), shade_canopy_records (plot_id, shade_pct snapshot, canopy_height_m, woody_species_count, strata) for Bird-Friendly/RA conformance, audits (inspection events: certification, auditor, body, scheduled/held date, outcome), non_conformities (audit finding, severity, corrective_action, owner worker_id, due, closed_evidence), premium_allocations (Fair Trade: kg_certified_sold, premium_per_kg, worker/reserve splits, payout date, decision_ref), geolocation_plots (plot polygon/GPS, land-use baseline 2020, EUDR due-diligence statement ref)

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-map a scheme's checklist clauses to existing farm records and surface a ranked gap list ('Bird-Friendly needs woody-species count on 3 plots; RA buffer evidence missing on Paso Ancho') before the auditor arrives.
- Pre-application substance screening: an agronomist types a product name and the model resolves its active ingredients, checks them against each in-scope plot's certification prohibited lists, and returns allow/block with the citing clause and PHI in seconds.
- Satellite/NDVI shade-canopy and deforestation estimation (Sentinel-2 via Earth Engine) to corroborate self-reported shade_pct and the EUDR 2020 baseline, flagging plots whose canopy drifted below Bird-Friendly thresholds.
- Generate the audit dossier and corrective-action narrative: assemble clause-linked evidence into the certifier's report format and draft corrective-action plans for each non-conformity.
- Renewal and inspection-window forecasting: predict the optimal inspection date around harvest/processing peaks and auto-draft renewal paperwork and worker declarations for e-signature.
- Premium-allocation explainer: compute the Fair Trade premium split and produce a plain-language, worker-facing and FLOCERT-facing summary of how each dollar was distributed.

**World-class:** World-class is 'audit-ready every day, not once a year': the certification scope, evidence map and conformance status are a live byproduct of normal farm operations, so any of the 5 schemes can produce a complete, clause-linked, gap-zero dossier on demand. Prohibited inputs are physically impossible to log against a certified plot; every certified bag carries a QR that resolves to its exact plots, shade profile, processing trail and the premium paid to named workers; deforestation-free geolocation and the Fair Trade premium ledger reconcile to the kilogram. The auditor's role shifts from discovery to confirmation, certificates never lapse, and the farm can credibly add a sixth certification with marginal effort because the underlying evidence graph is already complete.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson's whole value proposition is high-altitude (1,350-1,700 masl) shade-grown specialty Geisha plus a 200-hectare private quetzal-habitat reserve, which makes Bird-Friendly and Rainforest Alliance not paperwork but the literal embodiment of the brand, and the reserve is a ready-made conservation set-aside that most farms cannot claim. The certified shade-and-altitude story is exactly what lets a Volcan Geisha command auction/reserve pricing, so certification evidence is directly load-bearing on revenue. The existing model already holds the audit primitives: per-plot shade_pct and altitude (Bird-Friendly/RA shade thresholds), established_year (EUDR/land-use cutoff), worker daily_rate_usd and attendance (Fair Trade premium + SMETA labor), the lot_code chain across harvests and processing (chain-of-custody), and Pest Control/Fertilizing tasks like 'Apply organic compost' and 'foliar seaweed extract' that are already the organic input register. The certifications module is mostly connective tissue over data Janson is already capturing.


#### Audit readiness & compliance document management
*Priority:* **table-stakes**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the Farm Owner / Certification & Compliance Manager (at Janson, the family principal who signs export contracts and hosts auditors). Secondary daily users: the farm administrator/bookkeeper who files documents and chases expirations, and the agronomist/field supervisor who supplies the underlying evidence (inspection logs, agrochemical applications, plot maps). Tertiary: the visiting third-party auditor (Rainforest Alliance / organic CB) and the EU-side importer requesting a Due Diligence Statement pack.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When an importer or certifier asks me to prove a specific green-coffee lot is compliant, I want to generate one complete, current audit pack (geolocation polygons, certificates, agrochemical and labor records, full traceability chain) tied to that lot code in minutes, so I can close the sale and pass the audit without a week of digging through binders, WhatsApp photos and email threads.
- When my Rainforest Alliance, organic or Fair Trade certificate (or a worker contract / agrochemical permit) is approaching its expiry or renewal-audit date, I want to be alerted weeks ahead with the exact corrective tasks and missing documents, so I never let a certification lapse and lose my premium-market access.
- When I prepare an EUDR shipment to the EU, I want the system to assemble the per-plot GeoJSON polygons, the no-deforestation-since-2020 evidence and the chain-of-custody for that batch into a TRACES-ready Due Diligence Statement, so my European buyer can clear customs and I stay an eligible supplier past the 2026 deadline.
- When a surveillance auditor lands on the farm next week, I want a live readiness dashboard showing which non-conformities from last cycle are closed, which records are missing and who owns each gap, so the whole family and team walk into the audit knowing we will pass.

**Features:**
- **Lot-linked Document Vault & Audit Pack Builder** — Every certificate, contract, lab result, agrochemical record and inspection report is stored once and tagged to the existing lot_code / plot spine; one click compiles a stamped, indexed PDF audit pack for a chosen lot, buyer or certification scheme, turning a week of binder-digging into minutes and de-risking sales and audits.
- **Certification & Document Lifecycle Tracker** — A registry of every certificate, permit, license and worker contract with issue/expiry dates and renewal-audit windows; tiered reminders (90/60/30/7 days) auto-create renewal tasks in the existing tasks module, so a certification never silently lapses and the premium price tied to it is never lost.
- **EUDR Due Diligence Statement Generator** — Per-plot GeoJSON polygons (built from area_ha/altitude already modeled) plus deforestation-since-2020 evidence and the harvest-to-green chain-of-custody are assembled into a TRACES-NT-ready DDS export, keeping Janson an eligible EU supplier past the Dec 2026 large-operator deadline.
- **Audit Readiness Dashboard & Non-Conformity (CAPA) Tracker** — A live scorecard per scheme (Rainforest Alliance, organic, Fair Trade, SCA, EUDR) showing percent of required records present, open corrective actions with owners and due dates, and days-to-next-audit, so the team converges on a known-pass state before any auditor arrives.
- **Field Evidence Capture** — Agronomists log agrochemical applications, inspections and PPE/training events with geotagged photos against a plot; each becomes an immutable, timestamped audit record, eliminating the gap between what happened on the farm and what the binder can prove.
- **Requirement Library & Mapping Engine** — A maintained catalog of each scheme's clauses mapped to the data/document that satisfies it; the system shows exactly which requirements are met, partially met or missing, so compliance is a checklist that auto-fills from operations rather than a once-a-year scramble.
- **Auditor & Buyer Share Portal** — Time-boxed, read-only, watermarked links let an auditor or importer self-serve the exact evidence scope they need (no full-system access), with every view logged, cutting back-and-forth email and signaling professionalism to high-value buyers.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** EU TRACES-NT / EUDR Information System (mandatory Due Diligence Statement submission; GeoJSON, WGS-84 / EPSG-4326), Rainforest Alliance certification platform (RACP / RA-Cert) for certificate status and audit data, ECOCERT / Control Union / Kiwa BCS organic and EU/USDA-NOP certification bodies (inspection scheduling, certificate IDs), Fairtrade International / FLOCERT (Fair Trade certificate and FLO ID verification), Panama AUPSA (food-safety pre-arrival notification) and MIDA (Ministry of Agricultural Development) export records, IPPC ePhyto / GeNS system for phytosanitary certificates on green-coffee export, International Coffee Organization (ICO) certificate-of-origin / ICO marks, Satellite deforestation-verification providers: Space Intelligence, Satelligence, Global Forest Watch / Hansen tree-cover-loss datasets (no-deforestation-since-2020 proof), Cropster / Cropster Origin for cupping scores and processing/lot data feeding quality and traceability claims, DocuSign / e-signature rail for worker contracts and grower declarations, Cloud object storage (Supabase Storage free tier) for document blobs with checksum/hash for tamper-evidence, GS1 lot-coding standards and optional GACC (China customs) registration for non-EU export lanes

**Data model additions:** certifications (scheme, body, certificate_id, scope, issued_on, expires_on, status, renewal_audit_due), documents (id, title, doc_type, file_ref/hash, version, valid_from, valid_to, lot_code FK, plot_id FK, worker_id FK, uploaded_by, uploaded_at), compliance_requirements (scheme, clause_ref, description, evidence_type, mandatory) plus requirement_evidence_links (requirement to document/record, status met/partial/missing), audits (scheme, type certification|surveillance|buyer-due-diligence, auditor_body, scheduled_date, outcome, score), non_conformities / capa (audit_id, finding, severity, owner worker_id, corrective_task_id FK to tasks, due, status), plot_geometries (plot_id FK, geojson polygon WGS-84, source, captured_on) extending plots for EUDR, dds_statements (lot_code FK, plot_geometries[], deforestation_check_result, reference_number, submitted_to_traces_at, status), agrochemical_applications (plot_id FK, product, active_ingredient, dose, applied_by worker_id, applied_on, phi_days, organic_compliant bool), share_links (scope, expires_at, watermark, access_log[]) for the auditor/buyer portal

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-classify and tag uploaded documents (OCR + LLM) by scheme, doc_type, expiry date and the lot/plot/worker they belong to, so filing is hands-free and nothing lands untagged.
- Gap-detection assistant that reads the requirement library against live data and flags missing or stale evidence before an audit, with a plain-language brief of what is missing and who must fix it.
- Natural-language compliance Q&A over the vault (show me every agrochemical applied to Tizingal Alto in the last 90 days and whether it breaks organic rules) for instant auditor responses.
- Deforestation risk scoring: cross-check each plot polygon against satellite tree-cover-loss layers and surface any plot needing manual review for EUDR.
- Auto-draft corrective-action plans and renewal task lists from audit findings, pre-assigned to owners with realistic due dates.
- Traceability-integrity anomaly watch (e.g. a green-coffee lot whose summed mass exceeds the linked harvests' yield) to catch the chain-of-custody breaks auditors probe for.

**World-class:** World-class is zero-scramble, always-audit-ready compliance: the farm could host any auditor or answer any buyer's DDS request on 24 hours notice and pass, because evidence is captured the moment work happens and is permanently linked to the lot it belongs to. A single source of truth holds every certificate, expiry and requirement-to-evidence mapping; nothing relies on someone remembering a renewal date or finding a photo on a phone. EUDR DDS and certification packs generate themselves from operational data already in the system (harvests, processing, plots, workers), are tamper-evident (hashed, versioned, timestamped), and export in the exact machine formats each authority demands (GeoJSON for TRACES, ePhyto for phytosanitary). The owner sees one readiness score per scheme that is green not because someone said so, but because every mapped requirement has live evidence behind it.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson's Geisha and reserve lots earn world-record specialty prices precisely because of provenance and sustainability claims, and the 200-hectare private nature reserve / quetzal habitat is a powerful EUDR and biodiversity story that today lives only in marketing copy. This module turns that story into auditable, machine-verifiable evidence: each elite micro-lot (e.g. JC-602 Geisha Anaerobic) carries its own polygon, no-deforestation proof, shade-grown / bird-friendly records and chain-of-custody, so a Best of Panama auction buyer or an EU importer can verify the premium they pay. For a high-altitude single-origin Panama farm selling into the EU, EUDR is not optional - passing it is the price of keeping the most valuable buyers, and the reserve makes Janson the easy-pass case rather than the at-risk one.


#### Sustainability & carbon credits — sequestration via shade/reserve
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the Janson family farm owner-operator / sustainability lead (e.g. the Janson responsible for brand + buyer relationships), who owns the carbon-and-conservation story and signs off on certification spend. Secondary daily users: the head agronomist (logs shade trees, species, GPS canopy boundaries, regenerative practices that drive sequestration) and an external MRV/verification auditor (read-only access to evidence). Tertiary: green-coffee buyers and roasters who consume the carbon claim as a purchasing input.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a specialty roaster asks 'what's the carbon footprint and offset story of this Geisha lot?' before committing to a contract, I want to hand them a per-lot, verified CO2e balance (emissions vs. sequestration from shade + reserve), so I can win the contract at a premium instead of losing it to a farm with a glossier sustainability deck.
- When I'm deciding whether to invest in a 200-hectare nature reserve and dense shade canopy that costs me yield per hectare, I want to quantify and monetize the carbon those trees sequester (insets I can sell to my buyers, or credits I can issue under Verra/Gold Standard), so I can turn conservation from a pure cost into a revenue line that funds the quetzal habitat.
- When EUDR/CSRD reporting season arrives and a European importer demands geolocated, deforestation-free proof plus a Scope 3 carbon number for every shipment, I want all my plot polygons, land-use history, tree inventory and emissions already captured in one system, so I can produce the compliance pack in a day instead of scrambling for weeks and risking a blocked shipment.
- When my agronomist plants or prunes shade trees and logs fertilizer, fuel and mill-water use, I want each action to automatically update the farm's living carbon ledger, so the sequestration and emissions numbers are always audit-ready rather than reconstructed once a year from memory.

**Features:**
- **Carbon Ledger (farm + per-lot CO2e balance sheet)** — A living double-entry ledger: emissions (synthetic N fertilizer, mill fuel/electricity, transport, processing wastewater methane) on one side, removals (shade-tree + reserve biomass growth, soil organic carbon) on the other, rolled up farm-wide and allocated per lot/plot. Turns 'we're sustainable' into a defensible kgCO2e/kg-green number per lot.
- **Shade & Reserve Biomass Carbon Estimator** — Computes above- and below-ground carbon stock and annual sequestration from the existing Plot.shadePct, trees, areaHa, establishedYear plus a new shade-tree species inventory, using IPCC Tier 1/2 allometric equations for agroforestry — converting the 200ha quetzal reserve and dense Geisha shade into a quantified, year-over-year tCO2e asset.
- **Geolocation & Deforestation (EUDR) compliance pack** — Captures GPS polygon boundaries per plot + the reserve, runs a deforestation-free check against a satellite forest-cover baseline (since 2020 cutoff), and exports the geojson + due-diligence statement EU importers require — removing the single biggest market-access risk for a Panama-to-EU exporter.
- **Carbon Project & Credit/Inset Registry** — Models a carbon project (methodology, crediting period, baseline, additionality, buffer pool) and issues/tracks vintages of credits or supply-chain insets with serial numbers and retirement status, so Janson can either sell insets to its own roasters or list verified removal credits — converting conservation into a tracked revenue stream.
- **Practice-to-Carbon automation hooks** — Each agronomy task (shade planting, pruning, compost/biochar application, switching a plot to organic fertilizer) and each processing batch (water/energy per kg) auto-posts to the carbon ledger, so the MRV evidence trail builds itself from operations data already flowing through the app — no parallel spreadsheet.
- **Buyer-facing Sustainability Lot Passport** — A shareable, per-lot page/PDF showing the lot's net carbon balance, shade %, biodiversity (quetzal habitat), water and certification badges, tied to the existing traceability lotCode — a marketing-grade asset that lets Janson charge a sustainability premium and that roasters can show their own customers.
- **Certification & MRV audit room** — A document + evidence vault mapping each captured data point to the requirements of Verra VM0042 / Gold Standard / Rainforest Alliance / organic, with verifier read-only access and an immutable change log — collapsing audit prep from weeks to a guided checklist.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Verra Registry & VM0042 / AFOLU methodologies (afforestation, agroforestry, improved ag land management) for issuing/registering removal credits, Gold Standard for the Global Goals registry (alternative high-integrity standard, strong with smallholder/agroforestry), Cool Farm Tool API and/or the Coolfarm/Quantis methodology for farm-level GHG and water footprinting (widely used in coffee Scope 3 / insetting), ICVCM Core Carbon Principles + the SBTi / GHG Protocol Land Sector & Removals Guidance as the accounting frameworks the ledger conforms to, Satellite biomass & forest-cover MRV providers — e.g. Chloris Geospatial, Pachama, Sylvera, CTrees, or Planet/Sentinel-2 NDVI/canopy-height feeds — for remote biomass change and deforestation baselines, Global Forest Watch / JRC EU Deforestation Observatory map layers for the EUDR 2020 deforestation-free baseline, EU TRACES NT / EUDR Information System for submitting geolocation due-diligence statements, enveritas, Rainforest Alliance and Fairtrade certification bodies (Janson's likely existing sustainability programs) for badge + audit data exchange, Mapping/GIS: Mapbox or Google Earth Engine for drawing and storing plot/reserve polygons; optionally drone/LiDAR (DroneDeploy) for canopy-height ground-truthing, Carbon marketplaces / inset platforms — e.g. Patch, Cloverly, or commodity-trader inset programs (ECOM, olam) — for monetizing issued credits/insets to roasters

**Data model additions:** carbon_project (standard, methodology e.g. VM0042, crediting_period, baseline_scenario, additionality_notes, buffer_pool_pct, status), carbon_ledger_entry (date, scope 1/2/3, source vs sink, activity_ref to task/batch/plot, tCO2e signed, calc_method, evidence_url), shade_tree_inventory (plotId, species, count, dbh/height_estimate, planted_year, allometric_model) — extends the existing Plot, biomass_estimate (plotId or reserve, year, above_ground_tC, below_ground_tC, soil_organic_C, annual_sequestration_tCO2e, data_source: allometric|satellite), reserve_parcel (the 200ha quetzal reserve as a first-class conserved-land entity: areaHa, forest_type, polygon, protected_since, biodiversity_notes), plot_geometry (plotId, geojson_polygon, centroid_lat_lng, eudr_deforestation_status, baseline_check_date) — closes the geolocation gap on Plot, emission_factor (region, activity_type e.g. synthetic_N|diesel|mill_electricity, kgCO2e_per_unit, source) reference table, carbon_credit_vintage (projectId, vintage_year, tCO2e_issued, serial_range, status: issued|sold|inset-retired, buyer, price_usd), sustainability_certification (body: Verra|GoldStandard|RainforestAlliance|Organic, scheme, status, valid_until, audit_evidence_refs), lot_carbon_footprint (lotCode, gross_emissions_tCO2e, sequestration_allocated_tCO2e, net_kgCO2e_per_kg_green) — ties carbon to the existing traceability lotCode

**AI / automation:**
- Estimate shade-canopy carbon from imagery: a vision model on satellite/drone tiles infers canopy cover %, tree density and species mix, cross-checking the agronomist's manual tree counts and flagging plots whose logged shadePct diverges from observed canopy.
- Auto-draft the EUDR due-diligence statement and the annual MRV/verification report from ledger data — LLM assembles the narrative, methodology citations and evidence index so the owner reviews instead of writes.
- Emission-factor matching + anomaly detection: an LLM maps free-text task/purchase entries ('applied 12 bags urea on Talamanca') to the correct emission factor and unit, and flags outliers (a fuel or fertilizer entry implying an implausible footprint).
- Carbon-ROI advisor: scenario engine recommends where an extra hectare of shade or a biochar program yields the best tCO2e-per-dollar, and forecasts credit/inset revenue under current market prices — turning conservation choices into a modeled investment decision.
- Buyer-question copilot: answers roaster RFP sustainability questionnaires ('Scope 3 intensity? deforestation-free since when?') directly from the carbon ledger and certification room, with cited evidence.

**World-class:** "World-class is a continuous, audit-grade carbon and biodiversity ledger where the number is always live and always defensible: every agronomy and processing action posts to it automatically, satellite biomass MRV reconciles the model against observed reality each season, and the farm can at any moment produce (a) a per-lot net CO2e figure tied to its traceability code, (b) an EUDR-ready geolocated deforestation-free pack, and (c) a registered carbon project that issues high-integrity (ICVCM CCP-labelled) removal credits or supply-chain insets sold to its own roasters. The reserve and shade canopy stop being a cost center and become a quantified, monetized, brand-defining asset — conservation that pays for itself. Verification is a guided week, not a fire drill; buyers self-serve the sustainability passport; and the system models the carbon ROI of every future planting decision."

**Coffee-specific angle:** "This is Janson's single strongest, most authentic differentiator and almost no competitor can replicate it: a real 200-hectare private quetzal-habitat nature reserve plus dense shade-grown Geisha (the existing data already shows 33-60% shade across plots, 1,350-1,700 masl). That is a large, genuine carbon sink and biodiversity asset sitting adjacent to a premium product. Shade-grown specialty coffee at altitude is exactly the agroforestry system Verra VM0042 / Gold Standard agroforestry methods and coffee-sector insetting (Cool Farm Tool, ECOM-style programs) are built for. The Geisha already commands a price premium; pairing each Geisha lot passport with a verified net-negative or near-zero carbon balance and 'protects quetzal cloud-forest' biodiversity claim lets Janson command a sustainability premium AND open EU market access (EUDR), while monetizing the reserve as insets sold to the very roasters buying the coffee — a closed, story-rich loop from forest to cup that an industrial farm without a reserve simply cannot tell."


#### Water stewardship & mill wastewater/effluent treatment
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the Mill & Sustainability Manager (in Janson's case the owner-operator/head of post-harvest who runs the wet mill and answers to MiAmbiente and to buyers). Secondary actors: the Mill Operator (logs water + effluent at each batch), the Agronomist (reuses treated effluent/pulp compost on plots), and the External Auditor/Certifier (Rainforest Alliance, MiAmbiente inspector, importer's QA) who needs evidence, not anecdotes.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I depulp and ferment a Washed or Honey batch in peak harvest, I want every liter of water and the resulting aguas-mieles strength tied to the exact lot, so I can prove to MiAmbiente and my buyers that nothing high-COD effluent reached the Río Chiriquí Viejo and its quetzal-habitat tributaries.
- When MiAmbiente's discharge norm (DGNTI-COPANIT 35-2000) requires me to stay under BOD/COD/TSS/pH limits and renew my discharge permit, I want continuous treatment-pond monitoring and an auto-generated compliance report, so I can pass inspection and renew the permit without scrambling for paper records.
- When a roaster or importer asks for my water footprint per kg of green coffee for their ESG and origin story, I want a verifiable liters-per-kg figure and reuse rate per lot, so I can win the contract and command a reserve premium on a Geisha lot.
- When my anaerobic biodigester is producing biogas and my pulp pile is composting into fertilizer, I want to track captured methane, treated-water reuse, and compost returned to plots, so I can turn the mill's waste stream into measurable cost savings and a circular-economy claim instead of a liability.

**Features:**
- **Per-lot Water Ledger (intake + recirculation + effluent)** — Every Washed/Honey/Anaerobic batch logs fresh-water draw, recirculated water, and effluent volume against its lotCode, so the farm gets an auditable liters-per-kg-green figure per lot instead of an annual guess.
- **Effluent Treatment Train tracker (biodigester → wetland → reuse)** — Models the physical treatment chain (settling tank, anaerobic biodigester, constructed wetland, reuse pond) with inflow/outflow strength readings at each stage, so the manager sees COD/BOD knock-down in real time and catches an underperforming pond before it overflows into a stream.
- **Discharge Compliance & Permit module (COPANIT 35-2000)** — Stores the MiAmbiente discharge permit, its parameter limits and renewal date, flags any sample reading that breaches a limit, and one-click exports the regulator's report format, turning permit renewal from a fire drill into a button.
- **Water Footprint & Reuse dashboard** — Rolls per-lot ledger data into farm-level L/kg-green, recirculation %, and treated-water-reused %, benchmarked against eco-pulper targets (~0.2 L/kg), giving the farm a defensible number for buyers and certification.
- **Lab sample & IoT reading log** — Captures both manual lab grab-samples (pH, COD, BOD, TSS, nitrates, phosphates) and automated IoT sensor streams against a sampling point + timestamp, so a single source of truth backs every compliance and footprint claim.
- **Circular byproduct tracker (biogas + pulp compost)** — Tracks methane captured from the biodigester (kWh / cooking-gas equivalent) and pulp/mucilage diverted to compost then returned to specific plots, converting waste into a quantified cost saving and a circular-economy story for the brand.
- **Watershed & wet-season risk alerts** — Cross-references rainfall/weather with pond freeboard and harvest-load forecasts to warn when storm runoff plus peak depulping could push a pond over its limit, protecting the downstream quetzal-habitat reserve before a spill happens.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** MiAmbiente Panamá / DGNTI-COPANIT 35-2000 effluent-discharge norm (BOD/COD/TSS/pH/N/P limits) and COPANIT 24-99 treated-wastewater reuse norm — the legal targets the module enforces, Rainforest Alliance 2020 Sustainable Agriculture Standard + SAN water criteria, and Smithsonian Bird Friendly (quetzal-habitat angle) — certification evidence export, Penagos (ECOLINE / UCBE eco-pulper, ~0.2 L/kg) and Pinhalense ecological wet-mill equipment — water-use spec import + recirculation telemetry, Dragino WQS-LB LoRaWAN water-quality node (pH/EC/ORP/COD/turbidity probes) + LoRaWAN gateway / The Things Network for low-power, off-grid pond and intake monitoring, Electromagnetic / ultrasonic flow meters (e.g. Siemens MAG, Endress+Hauser) on intake and discharge lines via Modbus/4-20mA to a gateway for volume capture, Cropster Roast/Harvest + lot/QA platform — to attach the per-lot water footprint to the same lotCode buyers already see, Open-Meteo / Tomorrow.io weather + rainfall API and ANAM/ETESA hydromet data for watershed runoff risk, Cool Farm Tool / Water Footprint Network (ISO 14046 water-footprint accounting) and GHG Protocol factors for the biogas methane-capture and footprint math, Biogas/biodigester telemetry (flow + CH4 %) for captured-methane accounting

**Data model additions:** water_source — named intake (spring, river, well) with permitted volume and the watershed/sub-basin it draws from, water_usage — per-batch row keyed to ProcessingBatch.lotCode: freshLiters, recirculatedLiters, processStep (depulp/ferment/wash), litersPerKgCherry, effluent_batch — the aguas-mieles stream from a processing batch: volume, source lotCode, generated date, current treatment_stage, treatment_unit — physical asset (settling tank, anaerobic biodigester, constructed wetland, reuse pond) with capacity, freeboard, type, treatment_event — movement/strength reading as an effluent_batch passes through a treatment_unit (in/out COD, BOD, retention days), water_sample — lab or IoT reading at a sampling_point + timestamp: pH, COD, BOD, TSS, nitrate, phosphate, turbidity, source (lab|sensor), sampling_point — a monitored location (intake, pond outlet, discharge to stream) with geo-coords and which permit limit applies, discharge_permit — MiAmbiente permit: number, COPANIT-35-2000 parameter limits, issue/renewal dates, status, iot_sensor — registered LoRaWAN/flow device: type, unit, sampling_point, battery, last-seen, byproduct_record — captured biogas (m3 / CH4% / kWh-eq) and pulp-compost mass diverted and the plotId it was returned to, water_footprint_snapshot — periodic farm-level rollup: L/kg-green, recirculation %, treated-water-reuse %, compliance status

**AI / automation:**
- Anomaly detection on IoT sensor streams — flag a COD/pH spike or a flow reading inconsistent with logged batch volume (sensor drift, leak, or an untreated dump) before it becomes a discharge violation
- Predictive treatment-load forecasting — combine harvest forecast + processing-method mix + rainfall to predict effluent volume and COD load 7-14 days out, so ponds are pre-emptied and retention time stays adequate at peak
- Auto-drafted MiAmbiente compliance report and buyer/ESG water-footprint narrative from the underlying ledger and samples (Claude), turning raw rows into the regulator's and roaster's expected document
- Vision/OCR ingestion of paper lab certificates and handwritten mill logs into water_sample rows, so legacy and field records become queryable without re-keying
- Treated-effluent reuse advisor — recommend safe fertigation rates of nutrient-rich treated water to specific plots within COPANIT 24-99 reuse limits, closing the loop onto the farm's own trees

**World-class:** "World-class is a closed-loop, evidence-backed water system where every lot carries a verifiable liters-per-kg-green and zero-untreated-discharge claim. Fresh-water draw approaches eco-pulper benchmarks (~0.2 L/kg via Penagos/Pinhalense vs. 15-20 L/kg for traditional washing); 100% of aguas mieles passes a monitored treatment train (anaerobic biodigester capturing biogas → constructed wetland → reuse) that knocks >25,000 mg/L COD down below COPANIT 35-2000 limits; IoT sensors stream pond and discharge readings continuously with anomaly alerts; the MiAmbiente discharge report and the Rainforest Alliance/Bird Friendly water evidence pack generate from one click; and the byproduct loop (biogas as cooking-gas/heat, mucilage compost back to plots) turns the mill's biggest liability into a measurable cost saving and a marketing asset. The downstream Río Chiriquí Viejo and the reserve's quetzal-habitat streams stay clean — and the system can prove it."

**Coffee-specific angle:** "Janson sits in the Volcán highlands draining into the Río Chiriquí Viejo basin and owns a 200-hectare private nature reserve that is genuine resplendent-quetzal habitat — so mill effluent isn't an abstract compliance item, it's a direct threat to the very ecosystem that defines the brand and the reserve's tourism/conservation value. Critically, Janson runs exactly the high-water, high-strength methods: Washed and Honey (and Anaerobic) processing produce aguas mieles, while their Geisha and reserve lots are sold as premium specialty/competition coffee where buyers increasingly demand water-footprint and habitat evidence. The current app's ProcessingBatch already keys everything to a lotCode and tracks method and moisture but captures zero water-side data — this pillar closes that gap, letting Janson convert 'we protect the quetzal watershed' from a website tagline (jansoncoffee.com, est. 1990) into per-lot, auditable proof that lifts reserve/Geisha pricing and de-risks the MiAmbiente permit."


#### Biodiversity & conservation monitoring — quetzal/reserve, camera traps
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Reserve & Conservation Lead (the Janson family member or contracted naturalist/biologist who stewards the 200-hectare private reserve and quetzal habitat) — supported by the farm agronomist (canopy/shade decisions) and the owner/brand director (who turns conservation evidence into certification, marketing, and grant/credit revenue). Secondary contributors: birding-tour guides and visiting researchers/citizen scientists.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When the quetzals descend into our 1,350–1,700 masl band during the July–December non-breeding season, I want to know in near-real-time which sightings/recordings happened where and when, so I can guide birders to active feeding trees and prove our reserve is functioning quetzal habitat — not just claim it.
- When I deploy camera traps and acoustic recorders across the reserve and shade-coffee transects, I want the thousands of images/audio clips auto-triaged (blanks dropped, species pre-identified, quetzal/ocelot/puma flagged), so my tiny team reviews findings in hours instead of drowning in raw media for weeks.
- When it's time to renew Smithsonian Bird Friendly certification or apply for a biodiversity/nature credit, I want a defensible, time-stamped species inventory, canopy-cover/shade-diversity record, and trend over years exported in the auditor's format, so I pass the audit and unlock premium pricing and conservation finance without scrambling.
- When the agronomist plans to prune, thin shade, or convert a plot, I want to see the biodiversity and quetzal-food-tree (Lauraceae/wild-avocado) value of that exact parcel before the chainsaw, so a yield decision never silently destroys the habitat our brand is built on.

**Features:**
- **Camera-trap & sensor station registry (geospatial)** — Every camera trap, AudioMoth/SWIFT recorder, and station is a mapped asset with GPS, elevation band, battery/SD state, deploy/retrieve dates and the plot or reserve transect it covers — so gear is never lost, gaps in coverage are visible, and every detection is locatable to a parcel.
- **AI media triage pipeline (detect → classify → human-confirm)** — Ingested images run through MegaDetector (drop blank/empty frames, ~70%+ of captures) then SpeciesNet/local classifier; audio runs through BirdNET — leaving a small confirm-queue of probable species with confidence scores. Cuts review labor by an order of magnitude and surfaces the quetzal/ocelot/puma frames first.
- **Living species inventory + quetzal sighting log** — A cumulative, evidence-backed species list (each entry tied to a photo/audio/observation, observer, date, location) with a dedicated Resplendent Quetzal tracker showing seasonal presence against the altitudinal-migration calendar — the single source of truth for audits, the website, and tour planning.
- **Habitat & shade-canopy health layer** — Per-plot shade %, tree-species count and native-share, and Lauraceae/wild-avocado (quetzal food-tree) census, scored against Bird Friendly thresholds (≥40% shade, ≥10 tree species, ≥60% native) so the team sees compliance and habitat value at a glance and gets warned before a prune/thin breaches it.
- **Certification & biodiversity-credit dossier generator** — One-click export of the species inventory, canopy record, and year-over-year biodiversity trend in the format auditors and credit standards expect (Smithsonian Bird Friendly renewal packet, TNFD/Verra Nature Framework / Plan Vivo PV Nature evidence) — turning monitoring data into certification and conservation-finance revenue.
- **Reserve story & live biodiversity feed for brand/tours** — A public-facing 'Reserve' panel (latest verified quetzal sighting, species-count ticker, camera-trap highlight reel, eBird hotspot embed) that lets the brand sell the conservation premium and tour guides plan routes to currently-active feeding trees.
- **Conservation alerts & seasonal playbook** — Push alerts on high-value or first-of-season detections (first quetzal of the descent, a predator like puma, an IUCN-threatened species, or a poacher/human-on-camera frame), plus a seasonal task-link so 'thin shade in Block A' auto-checks the habitat layer first.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** MegaDetector v6 (Microsoft AI for Good) — open-source animal/person/vehicle detector to drop blank frames, Wildlife Insights / SpeciesNet (Google) — camera-trap species classifier; or self-hosted AddaxAI / TrapTagger as the $0 review UI, BirdNET (Cornell K. Lisa Yang Center for Conservation Bioacoustics) — bird-call species ID from acoustic recordings, AudioMoth (Open Acoustic Devices) + Cornell SWIFT — low-cost autonomous recording units (hardware) for passive acoustic monitoring, eBird API 2.0 + Merlin (Cornell Lab of Ornithology) — pull/contribute sightings, embed the reserve as an eBird hotspot, iNaturalist API — citizen-science observations from guests/researchers, with photo-vision suggestions, GBIF API — publish the reserve's verified occurrence records to the global biodiversity repository (credibility for audits/credits), Smithsonian Bird Friendly program / STRI (Latin-American office is in Panama) + Quality Certification Services (QCS) — certification body and audit packet, TNFD, Verra Nature Framework / SD VISta, and Plan Vivo PV Nature Biodiversity Standard — biodiversity/nature-credit issuance standards, Camera-trap hardware (Reconyx HyperFire, Browning/Bushnell) — EXIF/timestamp ingestion; optional cellular/LoRaWAN-connected traps, GBIF Backbone / IUCN Red List API — canonical taxonomy and conservation-status enrichment for every logged species
