**Data model additions:** camera_stations (id, label, kind: camera-trap|acoustic-recorder|weather|manual, hardware_model, lat, lng, elevation_masl, plot_id FK→plots nullable, reserve_zone, deployed_date, retrieved_date, battery_pct, storage_state, active), media_captures (id, station_id FK, captured_at timestamp, media_kind: image|audio, file_ref, ai_status: pending|detected|classified|confirmed|blank, megadetector_conf, top_species_id FK, top_species_conf, reviewed_by, reviewed_at), species (id, common_name, scientific_name, taxon_group: bird|mammal|amphibian|plant|insect, gbif_taxon_key, iucn_status, is_quetzal_food_tree bool, native bool, flagship bool), species_detections (id, species_id FK, media_capture_id FK nullable, station_id FK nullable, plot_id FK nullable, observed_at, source: camera|acoustic|ebird|inaturalist|manual, observer, count, confidence, lat, lng, verified bool), habitat_assessments (id, plot_id FK, assessed_on, shade_pct, tree_species_count, native_share_pct, lauraceae_count, canopy_layers, bird_friendly_compliant bool, notes), reserve_zones (id, name, area_ha, elevation_min, elevation_max, habitat_type, protected bool, quetzal_corridor bool), certifications (id, scheme: bird-friendly|organic|rfa|biodiversity-credit, body, status, issued_on, expires_on, dossier_ref, credits_issued numeric)

**AI / automation:**
- MegaDetector + SpeciesNet/BirdNET pipeline auto-triages camera and acoustic media: drops blank frames, pre-classifies species with confidence, and routes only the uncertain or high-value captures to a human confirm-queue (10x+ labor reduction).
- Flagship/anomaly detection: auto-flag first-of-season quetzal, IUCN-threatened species, large predators (puma/ocelot), and human-on-camera frames (anti-poaching) for instant push alerts.
- Habitat-impact guardrail: when a Pruning/Weeding/Planting task touches a plot, an AI check cross-references the habitat layer and warns if the action would drop the plot below Bird Friendly shade/native-species thresholds or remove Lauraceae food trees.
- Quetzal-presence forecasting: correlate historical detections with elevation, Lauraceae fruiting and weather to predict peak descent windows and recommend tour timing.
- Auto-drafted audit/credit dossier: LLM assembles the species inventory, canopy record, and year-over-year biodiversity trend narrative into the Smithsonian Bird Friendly / Verra Nature Framework submission format, with every claim linked to its evidence record.
- Acoustic biodiversity index: compute a soundscape/species-richness trend from BirdNET output as an early-warning signal of habitat health changes.

**World-class:** A reserve that runs as a continuously-instrumented living lab: a mapped mesh of camera traps and acoustic recorders streams media that AI triages within hours, so the team confirms a curated handful of detections instead of sifting raw cards. The Resplendent Quetzal's July–December descent into Janson's exact elevation band is tracked sighting-by-sighting against Lauraceae fruiting, and every shade-management decision is checked against a live habitat layer before it happens. Years of evidence compound into an unbroken, auditor-ready biodiversity time-series that renews Smithsonian Bird Friendly automatically, qualifies the 200 ha for verified biodiversity/nature credits, and feeds GBIF and a public reserve story — so conservation is not a cost center but a measured, revenue-generating asset the brand is provably built on. World-class = the farm can answer 'is biodiversity here improving, and can you prove it?' with a chart, not a claim.

**Coffee-specific angle:** This is Janson's signature edge, not a generic add-on. Their 200-hectare private reserve is quetzal habitat, and Janson sits at 1,350–1,700 masl — precisely the 1,000–1,500 m band the Resplendent Quetzal descends into during the July–December non-breeding season to feed on Lauraceae (wild-avocado) fruit. That's a real, datable biological event the system can track, not marketing fluff. The Smithsonian Bird Friendly program — the gold standard for shade-grown coffee — was literally born in Panama and runs its Latin-American office at STRI in Panama, and its quetzal-habitat criteria (≥40% shade, ≥10 tree species, ≥60% native) map directly onto the farm's existing per-plot shadePct field. A specialty Geisha producer already commands a premium; pairing verified quetzal/biodiversity evidence with the green/roasted lots turns the reserve into a story that justifies reserve-tier pricing, supports birding eco-tourism on-site, and opens biodiversity-credit revenue on land that isn't planted to coffee.


#### ESG & impact reporting
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm owner / sustainability lead at Janson Coffee (the Janson family principal who signs off on certifications, carbon claims, and the buyer-facing impact narrative). Secondary contributors: the head agronomist (supplies shade %, tree counts, input logs, reserve monitoring), the wet-mill/processing manager (water + energy + waste data), and the export/sales lead (who attaches the impact story and EUDR due-diligence statement to each green-coffee lot sold to roasters).

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a European roaster places an order for a Geisha lot, I want to instantly generate a plot-level, deforestation-free Due Diligence Statement with GPS polygons and a 2020-baseline forest-cover check, so I can keep selling into the EU after the Dec-2026 EUDR deadline without scrambling for paperwork per shipment.
- When I close a harvest season, I want to compute a per-kilo green-bean carbon footprint (and net sequestration from my shade canopy and 200-ha reserve) using a recognized methodology, so I can make a credible carbon-negative claim and price the premium it earns instead of greenwashing.
- When a buyer or certifier (Rainforest Alliance, organic, Bird Friendly) audits me, I want every claim — shade cover, agrochemical inputs, living-wage pay, water use — backed by timestamped operational records I already capture, so I pass the audit in days instead of reconstructing a year of evidence from memory.
- When I publish my annual impact report and the QR code on each bag of roasted coffee, I want the biodiversity, water, labor-equity, and climate numbers to come straight from the live farm system, so customers and the public see verifiable proof — quetzal sightings, liters-of-water-per-kilo, % living income — not a marketing brochure.

**Features:**
- **EUDR Due Diligence & Geolocation Engine** — Per-plot GPS polygons + 2020 forest-cover baseline check produce a one-click, lot-linked Due Diligence Statement ready for the EU TRACES/Information System — keeps Janson sellable into Europe past the Dec-2026 deadline and turns compliance into a sales asset.
- **Carbon & GHG Ledger (Cool Farm Tool methodology)** — Converts existing input/energy/shade/tree data into a per-kilo CO2e footprint with sequestration from shade canopy and the reserve, surfacing a defensible carbon-negative number that commands a price premium and feeds buyers' Scope-3 reports.
- **Certification Evidence Vault & Audit Mode** — Maps every operational record (shade %, input logs, wages, water) to Rainforest Alliance / Organic / Bird Friendly / Fairtrade clauses, so an annual audit is a generated dossier, not a fire drill — cuts audit prep from weeks to days and protects the certification premiums.
- **Biodiversity & Reserve Monitor** — Tracks quetzal and indicator-species sightings, canopy/shade trends, and the 200-ha reserve's protected status, producing the habitat-conservation evidence that underpins Bird Friendly certification and the brand's nature-reserve story.
- **Living-Income & Social-Equity Tracker** — Benchmarks worker daily rates against the regional living-income reference price and logs safety/training, giving a hard social-impact metric for Fairtrade/B-Corp-style reporting and the farmgate-transparency claim buyers now demand.
- **Water & Mill Resource Footprint** — Logs wet-mill water-per-kilo, wastewater treatment, and energy at the processing stage — the highest-impact part of coffee's footprint — so reduction targets are measurable and reportable rather than anecdotal.
- **Public Impact Report & Bag QR Generator** — Auto-builds the annual ESG/impact report and a scannable per-lot QR landing page from live data, letting consumers trace a bag to its plot, picker, processing method, and verified climate/biodiversity numbers — converting transparency into brand equity and direct-trade trust.
- **Carbon-Credit / Payment-for-Ecosystem-Services Project Workspace** — Stages agroforestry/reserve data against Plan Vivo / Verra / Gold Standard requirements to pursue verified carbon or biodiversity credits, opening a new revenue line from land Janson already protects.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** EU EUDR Information System / TRACES NT (Due Diligence Statement submission + reference numbers), Global Forest Watch / JRC Global Forest Cover 2020 + Sentinel-2 & ESA WorldCover for deforestation-free baseline verification, Cool Farm Tool API (GHG, water, biodiversity quantification), Sentinel-2 / Planet / satellite NDVI providers for canopy + shade-cover MRV, Rainforest Alliance certification platform & Bird Friendly (Smithsonian Migratory Bird Center) criteria, Fairtrade International / Living Income Reference Price datasets, USDA / EU organic certifier data feeds, Verra VCS Registry, Gold Standard, and Plan Vivo (carbon/biodiversity credit registries), Enveritas Gold verification (satellite + on-site sustainability assessment), Global Coffee Platform (GCP) sustainability reporting framework, iNaturalist / eBird APIs for quetzal & indicator-species biodiversity logging, GS1 Digital Link / QR standard for consumer-facing per-lot traceability

**Data model additions:** esg_metrics (period, metric type, value, unit, methodology, source — the time-series spine of all reporting), plot_geometries (extends plots with GPS polygon/WKT, area_ha cross-check, forest_cover_2020_status for EUDR), due_diligence_statements (lot_code FK, plot polygons, risk assessment, EU reference number, buyer, shipment), carbon_ledger_entries (emission/sequestration source, scope 1/2/3, CO2e kg, factor, methodology=Cool Farm), certifications (scheme, status, issued/expiry, certifier, scope of plots) + certification_evidence (clause → linked operational record), biodiversity_observations (species, count, observer, plot/reserve, date, photo — quetzal & indicators), reserve_parcels (the 200-ha reserve, protected status, canopy/carbon stock), input_applications (agrochemical/organic input, rate, plot, date — feeds carbon + organic audit), water_energy_logs (mill water L, wastewater treatment, kWh/fuel per batch), living_income_benchmarks (region, reference price, year) joined against worker daily_rate_usd, impact_reports (period, generated artifact, public QR/lot landing pages)

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-draft the narrative annual impact report from the metric time-series, with citations back to source records (LLM over esg_metrics) so a human edits rather than writes from scratch.
- Satellite-change detection that flags any plot polygon showing canopy loss vs the 2020 baseline before it becomes an EUDR or Bird Friendly compliance problem.
- Carbon-hotspot advisor: analyze the GHG ledger and recommend the highest-leverage reductions (e.g. compost pruned material, swap synthetic N, add shade trees) ranked by CO2e impact and cost.
- Certification gap-scanner that reads incoming audit criteria and Janson's evidence vault, then produces a prioritized 'missing evidence' checklist before the auditor arrives.
- Computer-vision species ID on trail-cam / phone photos to log quetzal and indicator-species sightings into the biodiversity monitor with confidence scores.
- Conversational query over all ESG data ('what was our water-per-kilo on washed Geisha last season vs this one?') for instant buyer-call answers.

**World-class:** "A buyer scans a bag, sees the exact plot polygon on a map, the picker, the processing method, a verified carbon-negative figure, liters-of-water-per-kilo, and this season's quetzal count — every number traceable to a timestamped farm record and a recognized methodology (Cool Farm, EUDR baseline, Bird Friendly). The owner submits an EUDR Due Diligence Statement per lot in one click, renews three certifications from a single evidence vault with zero reconstruction, publishes an audited annual impact report generated from live data, and monetizes the 200-ha reserve through verified carbon/biodiversity credits. Compliance is not overhead — it is a differentiated, premium-commanding product feature, and the farm's real environmental stewardship is finally legible and bankable."


**Coffee-specific angle:** "Janson is almost uniquely positioned to win here: high-altitude (1,350-1,700 masl) shade-grown Geisha already skews carbon-negative, and a 200-hectare private quetzal reserve is exactly the biodiversity asset Bird Friendly (Smithsonian) and Verra/Plan Vivo credits reward — most farms have to manufacture this story; Janson lives it. The same shade canopy that produces the complex Geisha cup that fetches Best-of-Panama / reserve-auction prices is the carbon sink and habitat the ESG module quantifies. EUDR is existential for any Panamanian estate selling green coffee to European roasters, and Janson's plot-level records + established-year + altitude data are already the raw inputs a Due Diligence Statement needs. The bag-QR impact page turns Janson's genuine stewardship into the premium-justifying narrative that direct-trade specialty buyers are actively paying for."



#### Food safety & GAP — HACCP, GlobalG.A.P.
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Food Safety & Compliance Lead. At Janson this is the Mill Manager / Quality Lead wearing a compliance hat, supported by the head Agronomist for field practices and the farm owner who signs the certification declaration. Audited annually by a GlobalG.A.P.-approved Certification Body (CB) inspector. Secondary user: the seasonal QA/mill operator who logs the daily CCP checks on the wet mill, drying patios, and warehouse.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When the GlobalG.A.P. (IFA crops) recertification audit is 6 weeks out, I want every control point — water tests, agrochemical records, worker hygiene training, harvest-interval (PHI) compliance — already assembled and gap-checked against the current checklist version, so I can walk the inspector through a self-audit-clean farm instead of scrambling to reconstruct a year of paper.
- When a mill operator is fermenting and drying a Geisha lot, I want the system to flag in real time when a HACCP critical limit is breached (fermentation tank temp/time, drying moisture above the mold/ochratoxin-A risk threshold, parchment held too long), so I can take corrective action and log it before a defect or food-safety hazard reaches a buyer.
- When I apply a fungicide or herbicide to a plot, I want to record the product, dose, applicator, re-entry interval (REI) and pre-harvest interval (PHI), and have the system block harvest scheduling on that plot until the PHI clears, so I never ship coffee with an MRL (maximum residue limit) violation that fails an EU/US import test.
- When an importer or a roaster's auditor asks 'prove this Panama Geisha lot is safe and traceable,' I want one-click export of a lot food-safety dossier — origin plot, every agrochemical touch, water and hygiene records, drying logs, active certificates — so I can close the sale and command the reserve/auction premium without a week of email archaeology.

**Features:**
- **GlobalG.A.P. IFA self-audit & checklist engine** — Maps every GlobalG.A.P. Integrated Farm Assurance v6 (Crops/Smart) control point — Major Must / Minor Must / Recommendation — to evidence in the system. A live compliance score and a red/amber/green gap list mean recertification is a continuous state, not a 2-week fire drill; the farm walks into the CB audit already self-audit-clean.
- **HACCP plan + CCP monitoring on the mill/drying line** — Encodes the hazard analysis (ochratoxin-A/mold from over-moist or slow drying, foreign-matter, microbial) and the critical control points with critical limits (fermentation temp/time, drying moisture <11–12%, parchment rest time). Operators log readings against limits; an out-of-limit reading forces a corrective-action entry. Turns food safety from tribal knowledge into an enforced, auditable workflow.
- **Agrochemical / input register with PHI & REI enforcement** — Logs every fertilizer, fungicide (e.g. for roya/leaf-rust), and herbicide application — product, active ingredient, dose, applicator, plot, weather. The system computes PHI/REI windows and HARD-BLOCKS harvest scheduling on a plot until PHI clears, and warns on re-entry during REI. Directly prevents MRL-violation rejections at EU/US/Japan import (the single most expensive specialty-coffee compliance failure).
- **Lot traceability dossier (one-click export)** — Stitches plot → harvest lotCode → processing batch → warehouse → shipment into an exportable, auditor-ready PDF/JSON dossier with all food-safety evidence attached. Closes B2B sales faster and substantiates the reserve/auction premium; satisfies one-step-back/one-step-forward traceability (EU GFL Art.18 / FSMA 204).
- **Water & hygiene compliance module** — Tracks the annual potable/irrigation water microbiology + heavy-metal test (a GlobalG.A.P. mandatory control point), wet-mill water reuse, worker hygiene/health declarations, and PPE/training records with expiry alerts. Closes the highest-frequency audit non-conformity categories (water testing and worker welfare) before the inspector finds them.
- **Non-conformity & corrective-action (CAPA) register** — Every breach (CCP excursion, expired cert, missed PHI) opens a CAPA ticket with root cause, corrective + preventive action, owner, and due date — closed-loop and timestamped. Auditors specifically score whether non-conformities are managed; an evidenced CAPA log converts findings into 'managed' rather than 'open' and protects the certificate.
- **Document & certificate vault with expiry radar** — Single home for the GlobalG.A.P. certificate, GGN number, organic/Rainforest Alliance/Fair Trade certs, MSDS/labels, water reports, and training certificates, each with an expiry date and an alert cadence. No certificate ever lapses silently and revenue-blocks a shipment at the worst moment.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** GlobalG.A.P. Database / GGN (Global GAP Number) registration + checklist data (IFA v6 control points) and Add-on standards (GRASP for worker welfare), Cropster (the specialty-coffee industry standard for green/roast lot management) and Cropster Origin for lot, cupping-score, and traceability sync, SCA (Specialty Coffee Association) cupping protocol + Q-grader score records to tie quality to the food-safety lot, Accredited lab APIs / portals for water microbiology, heavy metals, and ochratoxin-A / pesticide-residue (MRL) testing — e.g. Eurofins, SGS, Intertek, Certification body audit platforms — NSF, Control Union, Kiwa BCS, Rainforest Alliance / RA Connect, Fairtrade FLOCERT, USDA NOP organic, EU TRACES / FSMA 204 (FDA food traceability) export formats; GS1 standards (GTIN/GLN) and EPCIS events for one-up/one-down traceability, Pesticide reference data — EU MRL database, Codex Alimentarius MRLs, CABI Crop Protection Compendium for PHI/REI lookup, Weather/agro APIs (already in-app: weather strip) extended with spray-window suitability — e.g. Meteomatics / OpenWeather + a roya (leaf rust) early-warning model, Hardware: Bluetooth/IoT moisture meters and thermohygrometers for drying beds (e.g. Sinar / KPM grain moisture, SensorPush / Govee temp-humidity loggers), QR/RFID lot tags for parchment bags

**Data model additions:** agrochemical_applications (plotId, productId, activeIngredient, doseRate, applicatorWorkerId, date, weather, phiDays, reiHours, phiClearsOn), input_products (name, type [fertilizer/fungicide/herbicide], activeIngredient, registrationNo, msdsUrl, defaultPhiDays, defaultReiHours), haccp_plan + ccp_definitions (processStage, hazardType, criticalLimitMin/Max, monitoringFrequency, correctiveAction), ccp_readings (batchId, ccpId, value, takenBy, takenAt, inLimit, correctiveActionId?), non_conformities / capa (source, severity [major-must/minor-must], rootCause, correctiveAction, preventiveAction, ownerId, dueDate, status, evidenceUrls), compliance_controls (standard [GLOBALGAP_IFA_V6/HACCP], controlPointId, level, status [green/amber/red], evidenceRefs, lastReviewed), certificates (type, scope, ggn, issuer, issuedOn, expiresOn, status, fileUrl), lab_tests (subject [water/soil/green-coffee], parameter [OTA/MRL/E.coli/heavy-metal], lab, sampleDate, result, threshold, pass), hygiene_training_records (workerId, topic [hygiene/PPE/agrochemical-handling], completedOn, expiresOn, certUrl), lot_dossier (lotCode → linked plotId, harvestIds, batchId, applications, labTests, certificates — a materialized traceability view)

**AI / automation:**
- MRL/PHI risk co-pilot: at spray time, cross-checks the chosen product's active ingredient against the destination market's MRL + Codex/EU databases and the lot's expected harvest date, then warns 'this application risks an EU MRL violation; PHI clears after the planned pick' before the chemical is even applied.
- Ochratoxin-A / mold risk prediction: learns from drying-bed moisture, temperature, humidity, and rain-day patterns to forecast which batches are drifting toward the OTA-risk zone and recommends turning/cover actions before a CCP limit is breached.
- Audit-readiness scoring + auto-narrative: continuously scores GlobalG.A.P. compliance, surfaces the top gaps, and drafts the corrective-action and self-assessment narrative text the inspector expects — turning a 2-week prep into a one-click brief.
- Document OCR + auto-filing: photograph a paper water-lab report, MSDS, or training certificate and the model extracts dates/parameters, files it to the right control point, and sets the expiry alert — kills the manual-paperwork failure mode that causes most non-conformities.
- Conversational audit assistant: 'show me proof lot JC-602 has no open food-safety issues' assembles the dossier and answers the inspector or buyer in plain language with citations to the underlying records.

**World-class:** World-class here means food safety and certification are a continuous, always-green state rather than an annual scramble: the GlobalG.A.P. compliance score is live on a dashboard, every agrochemical application self-enforces its PHI before a single cherry can be scheduled for harvest, drying-bed moisture and fermentation temps stream from IoT sensors into HACCP CCP logs with automatic corrective-action prompts, and any lot can produce an auditor- and importer-ready food-safety dossier in one click. The inspector's annual visit becomes a confirmation, not a discovery; an importer's MRL or OTA test never surprises the farm because the risk was flagged at spray time and at the drying bed. It is Cropster-grade lot traceability fused with a HACCP/GlobalG.A.P. compliance brain — the kind of system that lets a small Panamanian farm clear EU/US/Japan import scrutiny and command a Best of Panama / reserve-auction premium with zero compliance friction.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson sells specialty green and roasted coffee into export channels (EU, US, Asia, plus the Best of Panama / reserve-auction circuit) where Geisha lots fetch extraordinary premiums — and those exact buyers and importers run MRL pesticide-residue and ochratoxin-A tests and increasingly demand GlobalG.A.P./Rainforest Alliance certification and full one-up/one-down traceability. The farm's own LOT_CODES (JC-541…JC-611) and processing batches (washed/natural/honey/anaerobic, with moisture % already tracked) are the perfect spine to hang HACCP CCPs and a per-lot food-safety dossier on. The 200-hectare quetzal-habitat nature reserve and high-altitude shade-grown model are a natural fit for the sustainability/biodiversity control points of GlobalG.A.P. and Rainforest Alliance — turning compliance into part of the brand story rather than overhead. A roya (leaf-rust) fungicide program and broca (berry-borer) control — both already in the task board — are the highest-risk agrochemical touches that the PHI/MRL guardrail directly protects against, which is precisely what keeps a high-value Geisha micro-lot sellable at the top of the market.


#### Government / ICO / national coffee authority reporting
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Owner / export-compliance lead (the Janson family principal who signs export paperwork), supported by the farm administrator who compiles the data. On a Panama specialty estate this is one or two people wearing both the "exporter of record" hat and the "agronomic record-keeper" hat — they personally answer to MIDA, the APA (ex-AUPSA), the ICO certificate-of-origin regime, and increasingly to EU buyers' EUDR due-diligence demands. Secondary persona: the EU/US green-coffee buyer's compliance officer who needs Janson to hand them a clean, machine-readable due-diligence packet per lot.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a green-coffee container is ready to ship to a buyer, I want every required document (commercial invoice, ICO Certificate of Origin, MIDA/APA phytosanitary + food-safety certificate, certificate of origin under the trade agreement, EUDR Due Diligence Statement) to be auto-assembled from the lot data I already captured, so I can clear customs and satisfy the buyer without re-keying figures into five government portals at midnight before the booking.
- When an EU buyer (or their importer) asks 'prove this lot is deforestation-free and legally produced,' I want to hand them a per-lot packet with GPS plot polygons, the post-2020 land-use evidence, and the chain of custody from cherry to bag, so I can keep access to the EU market past the Dec 30 2026 / Jun 30 2027 EUDR deadlines instead of being dropped as an un-traceable supplier.
- When the ICO / Panama coffee authority needs my monthly and annual export-statistics return, I want the system to total exports by destination, varietal, process and 60kg-bag equivalents and pre-fill the statutory return, so I stay a compliant registered exporter and the national figures reported to the ICO are accurate without me hand-tallying spreadsheets.
- When auditors for an organic, Rainforest Alliance, Fair Trade or Best-of-Panama/SCAP certification arrive, I want one click to produce the traceability, input-use and labor records for the lots in scope, so I pass the audit and protect the price premium my Geisha and reserve lots command.

**Features:**
- **Export Document Generator (per-lot dossier)** — Turns the existing lot_code chain (harvest plot -> processing batch -> green bag) into a one-click bundle: commercial invoice, ICO Certificate of Origin (ICC-102-9 Rev.5 format, 60kg-bag equivalents), packing list, phytosanitary + food-safety certificate request to MIDA/APA, and certificate of origin under the US-Panama TPA. Eliminates midnight re-keying and customs-hold errors.
- **EUDR Due-Diligence Workbench** — Captures GPS plot geometry (point for plots <4 ha, polygon for larger), pins production date after the 2020 cutoff, runs a deforestation risk check against satellite baselines, and emits a TRACES NT-ready Due Diligence Statement (DDS) per lot with a verification reference the buyer can quote. Keeps Janson sellable into the EU and lets it charge the 'EUDR-ready' premium.
- **Statutory Reporting Calendar & Returns** — A compliance calendar of every recurring obligation (MIDA exporter renewal, APA food-safety registration, ICO monthly/annual statistics return, certification audit windows) with auto-drafted, pre-filled returns and reminders, so nothing lapses and the exporter registration is never suspended.
- **Certification & Audit Evidence Room** — A read-model that, scoped to a date range or lot set, exports the traceability, agro-input (matched to the existing Tasks fertilizing/pest-control records), and labor records auditors demand for Organic (ISO/IEC 17065), Rainforest Alliance, Fair Trade and SCAP/Best-of-Panama. Cuts audit prep from weeks to minutes and defends the price premium.
- **Origin & Provenance QR / public lot passport** — A buyer- and consumer-facing lot passport (farm, plot, altitude, varietal, process, harvest dates, deforestation-free attestation) served from the same lot data and printable as a QR on the bag. Turns compliance data into a marketing asset for Janson's reserve and Geisha lots.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** EU TRACES NT (Information System for Official Controls) — mandatory submission interface for every EUDR Due Diligence Statement, International Coffee Organization (ICO) — Certificate of Origin regime (ICC-102-9 Rev. 5) and World Coffee Statistics return, Panama Ministry of Agricultural Development (MIDA) — exporter registration / agricultural export authorization, Panama Food Agency (APA, the post-2021 successor to AUPSA) — food-safety registration & export sanitary certificate, National phytosanitary authority for the phytosanitary certificate accompanying green-coffee export, US Customs (CBP) under the US-Panama Trade Promotion Agreement (PATPA) — certificate of origin / preferential tariff, Satellite deforestation / land-use baselines for EUDR risk screening: Global Forest Watch / WRI, JRC Global Forest Cover, plus NDVI providers (Sentinel-2 via Sentinel Hub, Planet), Fairtrade's free EUDR geolocation tool and traceability platforms (Meridia, TraceX) as interop targets for buyers who standardize on them, Certification bodies & standards: ISO/IEC 17065 organic certifiers, Rainforest Alliance, Fairtrade International, and the Specialty Coffee Association of Panama (SCAP) / Best of Panama competition registry, GS1 standards (GTIN/GLN + Digital Link) for the bag-level QR lot passport, Cropster / Cropster Origin as the green-coffee inventory & lot-traceability system specialty exporters already use — sync lots and cupping scores

**Data model additions:** regulatory_bodies (ICO, MIDA, APA, EU/TRACES, SCAP, certifier) reference table, compliance_obligations (body, type, cadence, next_due, status, owner) — drives the reporting calendar, export_shipments (buyer, destination_country, incoterm, booking, container, total_green_kg, bags_60kg_equiv) linking to lot_codes, shipment_lots (join: export_shipment -> lot_code -> green_kg) reusing the existing lots table as the traceability spine, export_documents (shipment_id, doc_type[ICO_COO|phyto|food_safety|TPA_COO|invoice|DDS], status, issued_at, reference_no, file_ref), eudr_due_diligence_statements (lot_code, traces_reference, risk_level, assessed_at, evidence_ref), plot_geometries (plot_id -> GeoJSON point/polygon, area_source, land_use_pre_2020_evidence) extending plots, certifications (scheme[Organic|RFA|Fairtrade|SCAP], cert_no, valid_from, valid_to, scope_lots[]) + certification_audits, lot_passport (lot_code -> public provenance payload + QR/GS1 Digital Link), statutory_returns (body, period, payload_json, submitted_at) — the ICO/MIDA statistics filings

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-classify and pre-fill statutory returns and certificates from existing lot/harvest/processing data, with an LLM that maps farm records to each authority's required field schema and flags missing fields before submission.
- EUDR deforestation risk scoring: pull Sentinel-2 / Global Forest Watch tiles for each plot polygon, run a change-detection model against the 2020 baseline, and auto-set the DDS risk level with human-in-the-loop review only on flagged plots.
- A compliance copilot that reads incoming buyer emails ('send me your EUDR docs for lot JC-564') and assembles the correct per-lot packet, drafting the reply with the documents attached.
- Deadline-aware agent that watches the obligations calendar and the Panama/ICO/EU regulatory feeds, drafts the next return, and escalates only true judgment calls (e.g., a new APA rule change) to the owner.
- Anomaly detection across the chain of custody (e.g., green_kg exceeding plausible yield from the source plot's cherry weight) to catch data errors before they reach a regulator and trigger an audit.

**World-class:** A Volcán family estate ships a Geisha container to Berlin and, in under five minutes, the system has: assembled the ICO Certificate of Origin and Panama export certificates from data captured once at harvest; submitted a valid EUDR Due Diligence Statement to TRACES NT with plot polygons proving deforestation-free since 2020; handed the buyer a signed, machine-readable provenance packet; updated the running ICO statistics return; and refreshed the QR lot passport on the bag. Nothing is re-keyed, nothing lapses, every claim is backed by the immutable cherry-to-bag chain of custody, and the same data that satisfies a regulator also sells the coffee. Compliance stops being a cost center and becomes a moat: small estates that can't produce this packet lose EU access, while Janson's is automatic.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson sits in Volcán, Chiriquí — the exact heartland (with Boquete) of Panama's specialty industry and the SCAP / Best of Panama scene, and it exports green and roasted to discerning EU/US/Asian buyers who command the Geisha premium. That makes EUDR existential rather than theoretical: the Dec 30 2026 (large/medium operators) and Jun 30 2027 (small/micro) deadlines mean any EU buyer will demand farm-level geolocation and a Due Diligence Statement, and a 200-hectare private nature reserve adjacent to the coffee plots is a powerful, literally true 'deforestation-free / quetzal-habitat-conserving' story to encode into the DDS and the lot passport. Janson's existing lot_code spine (harvest plot -> processing batch -> green) is already the chain of custody these regimes require, and tying each plot's GPS polygon + established_year to the post-2020 cutoff is a near-trivial extension that converts existing records into regulatory gold — and into a marketing differentiator for reserve and competition lots.


### Platform & Intelligence (cross-cutting)

#### Unified data model & single source of truth
*Priority:* **table-stakes**  ·  *Opportunity:* **10/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm's "operations owner / data steward" — at Janson Coffee this is Janette Janson (agronomist-owner) and the farm administrator who together own the numbers. Secondary daily users: the mill operator and supervisor who key in batch and labor data, and Miguel Janson (field ops) who needs every screen to agree. The unified model is cross-cutting infrastructure, so its real "user" is every other persona who currently re-enters or reconciles the same fact (kg, lot, tree, masl) in multiple places.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I look at the dashboard headline (season target, harvested-to-date, YTD revenue) and then at the harvest log, I want both to be computed from the same underlying records, so I can trust a single number in front of a buyer or my family instead of reconciling hand-authored aggregates against raw rows.
- When a Geisha buyer in Tokyo asks 'which trees, which block, which altitude, which fermentation, who picked it' for the bag they're holding, I want to follow one unbroken chain of identifiers from finished green-coffee lot back through milling, drying, fermentation, the day's pick, the plot, and the worker, so I can answer in seconds with provenance I can prove.
- When a picker logs cherries in kg, the mill weighs parchment in quintales, and a buyer orders in 30 kg / 69 kg / 70 kg bags, I want every weight to live in one canonical unit with a recorded conversion (and yield factor at each stage), so cherry-to-green ratios, costs, and inventory never silently disagree.
- When someone edits a plot's tree count, reassigns a lot, or corrects a moisture reading, I want the system to keep an immutable record of who changed what and when, so I can audit a mistake, satisfy an organic/Rainforest Alliance auditor, and roll back bad data without losing history.

**Features:**
- **Canonical Entity Registry + global ID scheme** — One stable identity per real-world thing (farm, plot, tree-block, lot, batch, worker, party, document). Replaces today's bare text PKs and the orphan `lots(code)` table with a typed registry so every module references the SAME plot/lot/worker — kills duplicate and drifting copies of the same fact.
- **Lot genealogy / traceability graph (lot lineage edges)** — Models the real many-to-many chain harvest-pick → cherry lot → fermentation/processing batch → drying → parchment → milled → green lot → roast → bag, with split/merge edges (one lot blends two picks; one batch splits to two patios). Turns lot_code from a string into a queryable lineage so any finished bag resolves to its trees, day, picker, and method in one traversal.
- **Canonical unit & yield-factor ledger (UCUM-based)** — Every quantity stored once in a base unit with explicit conversions (kg ↔ quintal ↔ lb ↔ 30/69/70 kg bag) and a recorded yield factor at each processing stage (typical ~5.4:1 cherry-to-green). Cherry-to-green ratios, cost-per-green-kg, and inventory all derive from one place and can never disagree across screens.
- **Append-only event log + temporal/audit history (event sourcing-lite + system-versioned tables)** — Every state change (harvest logged, batch advanced, plot edited, lot shipped) becomes an immutable timestamped event with actor + before/after. Powers the activity feed for free, gives full who-changed-what audit for organic/RA certification, and enables point-in-time 'what did we believe on harvest day' replay.
- **Derived-metrics / semantic layer (materialized views, not hand-typed aggregates)** — Replaces the hand-authored `season_summary`, `daily_cherries`, `weekly_harvest`, `variety_shares` tables with definitions computed from base records (with caching). The dashboard headline and the raw log become mathematically the same number — the #1 trust problem in the current schema.
- **Multi-farm / org & parcel hierarchy with PostGIS geometry** — A farm → block → plot → tree-row hierarchy with real polygon geometry and the 200 ha reserve as a conservation parcel. Lets Janson add a second farm or a buying-station for outgrowers later, and lets every plot-level fact carry true coordinates for NDVI, GPS-stamped picks, and map views — the schema today has only altitude/area scalars, no geometry.
- **Reference-data & controlled vocabularies (varieties, methods, certifications, parties)** — Promotes today's hardcoded enums (variety, method, role) into versioned reference tables plus a `parties` table (buyers, importers, certifiers, outgrowers, the family). Adding the next anaerobic experiment or a new buyer is data, not a migration + redeploy, and external codes (HS tariff, ICO, certification IDs) attach cleanly.
- **Measurements & documents store (typed observations + attachments)** — A generic typed-measurement table (brix, moisture, pH, temp, NDVI, soil N-P-K) and a document/attachment registry (cupping scorecards, certificates, contracts, photos) keyed to any entity. Gives every other pillar a single place to write sensor readings, lab results, and files instead of inventing one-off columns.
- **Data-quality & reconciliation guardrails** — Constraints + checks that mass-balance lot weights (sum of stage outputs ≤ inputs × max yield), block-level harvested-kg against expected-yield, and flag impossible edits. Turns the SSOT from 'a place data lives' into 'a place bad data can't quietly enter.'

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Cropster (Origin/Cup) — the de-facto specialty-coffee traceability + cupping system; bidirectional lot/cupping sync so Janson's lineage interoperates with what importers/roasters already use, Sucafina / Algrano / green-coffee marketplaces — export the canonical green lot + provenance for listings, PostGIS (Supabase Postgres extension) for parcel geometry; GeoJSON / Shapefile / KML import-export for plot boundaries, UCUM (Unified Code for Units of Measure) as the units standard backing the conversion ledger, GS1 standards (GTIN for retail bags, GLN for the farm location, and 2D Data Matrix / QR Link) so finished-bag traceability is industry-standard, not a homegrown code, EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) geolocation data model + the EU TRACES / Due Diligence Statement format — green-coffee exporters to the EU must now supply plot polygons + harvest data; the SSOT is where that originates, ICO (International Coffee Organization) country/lot codes and HS tariff code 0901 for export documentation, Certification bodies' schemas — Rainforest Alliance (RA) traceability, USDA/EU Organic, Smithsonian Bird Friendly (apt for the quetzal reserve), Fair Trade — as reference-data + audit-export targets, OpenTelemetry / structured audit export and Supabase Realtime for change-stream propagation to other modules, ISO 8601 (dates/times) and ISO 4217 (currency, USD/PAB) as canonical formats in the registry

**Data model additions:** organizations / farms (the org boundary; supports Janson + future second farm or outgrower buying-station), parcels & geo_boundaries (PostGIS polygons for block/plot/tree-row + the 200 ha reserve, with altitude bands), entity_registry (canonical typed IDs + soft references that every module joins to), parties (buyers, importers, certifiers, outgrowers, family members — unifies the implicit 'picker/worker/buyer' identities), lots (promoted from bare code → typed lot with stage, owner, current_qty, certification flags), lot_lineage_edges (split/merge genealogy linking pick → batch → drying → parchment → green → roast → bag), units & unit_conversions & stage_yield_factors (canonical-unit + conversion + yield ledger), measurements (typed observations: brix, moisture, pH, temp, NDVI, soil nutrients) keyed to any entity, documents / attachments (cupping scorecards, certificates, contracts, photos) keyed to any entity, events / change_log (append-only, actor + before/after — supersedes the standalone `activity` table), reference_data tables (varieties, process_methods, certifications, roles, currencies — versioned, replacing frozen enums), metric_definitions + derived_metrics (materialized) — replaces hand-authored season_summary / daily_cherries / weekly_harvest / variety_shares

**AI / automation:**
- Entity resolution / dedup — fuzzy-match and merge duplicate plots, workers, lots, or buyer names entering from spreadsheets, SMS picker logs, or Cropster, so the registry stays one-record-per-thing without manual cleanup
- Natural-language provenance answers — 'show me everything about lot JC-564' returns the full lineage, cupping score, picker, weather on harvest day, and certifications, by traversing the graph and narrating it (Claude over the SSOT)
- Anomaly & data-quality detection — flag a mass-balance violation (green-kg implies impossible yield), a moisture reading that jumped 8% overnight, or a plot whose harvested-kg exceeds expected, before bad data propagates to the dashboard
- Auto-classification & enrichment — infer process method from the fermentation/drying timeline, suggest the HS/ICO/certification codes for an export lot, and draft EUDR due-diligence statements from the stored geolocation + harvest records
- Schema-aware import copilot — map a messy supplier or lab CSV onto the canonical entities/units, proposing conversions and flagging unmatched columns, so onboarding new data sources is minutes not migrations

**World-class:** "World-class is an immutable, time-travel-able single source of truth where every number on every screen is derived (never hand-typed), every real-world thing has exactly one canonical ID, and any finished bag of Geisha resolves in one query to its trees, GPS polygon, picker, fermentation curve, cupping score, and certifications — with a full who-changed-what audit trail. Units are canonical with explicit conversions and per-stage yield factors so cherry-to-green and cost-per-kg can never disagree. The model is multi-farm and EUDR/GS1/Cropster-interoperable from day one, change events stream to every other module in real time, and data-quality constraints make impossible data physically unenterable. It is the spine the agronomy, processing, sales, sustainability, labor, and finance pillars all bolt onto — add a capability and it reuses the same entities, units, events, and audit rather than inventing its own."

**Coffee-specific angle:** "For a single-estate specialty producer selling lot-separated Geisha at auction-level prices, provenance IS the product — buyers pay a premium precisely because the chain from a specific 1,690 masl Tizingal Alto block through a named anaerobic fermentation to a cupping score is verifiable. Janson already runs the whole chain (grow → wet/dry mill → green + roasted), so the lot_code that today is a bare string must become a true genealogy graph spanning farm-to-bag. The 200 ha quetzal reserve is a real Bird Friendly / sustainability asset that needs to live as a first-class conservation parcel in the model. And as an EU-exporting Panamanian producer, Janson now legally needs plot-polygon geolocation tied to harvest records for EUDR due diligence — the unified model is where that data is born, not bolted on. Janson's tiny-but-premium scale means the SSOT can be rigorous and auditable without enterprise weight."


#### IoT/sensor ingestion & telemetry platform
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the Farm Operations Manager / lead Agronomist at Janson (the person who today walks the 200ha across 1,350-1,700 masl bands deciding where to irrigate, when to spray, and which drying beds to turn). Secondary daily users: the Mill Operator (fermentation tank + drying-bed telemetry), the Owner (single-pane farm health + alert escalation), and the picking-crew Supervisor (microclimate frost/rain alerts that move a crew). The platform is cross-cutting infrastructure, so its real "user" is every other pillar that consumes the live signal — agronomy, processing, and traceability all read from it.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a cold front rolls off Barú at night during the dry season, I want an automatic frost/low-temperature alert per altitude band on my phone, so I can mobilize the crew to run sprinklers or fans on the exposed high blocks before the buds and cherries are damaged.
- When I am deciding whether a drying batch on Bed 7 is ready to bag, I want continuous moisture, ambient temp/RH, and bed-turn telemetry instead of a single hand-meter reading, so I can pull each lot at the exact 10.5-11% moisture window without over- or under-drying a $40/lb Geisha lot.
- When a fermentation tank is running an anaerobic Geisha, I want live pH, temperature, and dissolved-CO2/Brix telemetry with a target curve, so I can stop fermentation at the precise flavor inflection instead of guessing by clock and smell.
- When I plan irrigation and fertigation across plots with very different soil and shade, I want per-plot soil-moisture, soil-temperature and EC at root depth, so I only water the blocks that actually need it and prove the reserve's watershed isn't being over-drawn.
- When a sensor or gateway goes silent, I want to know within minutes that the data stopped (not discover a dead frost sensor after I lost a block), so a failed guard is treated as an incident rather than silent green.

**Features:**
- **Device & gateway registry (digital twin of the sensor estate)** — Every physical sensor, gateway, and tank/bed probe is registered with location (plot/block/bed/tank), install date, calibration record, battery/firmware, and last-seen — so the manager sees the whole estate's health on one map and knows exactly which device fed which reading for traceability.
- **Universal ingestion pipeline (MQTT + LoRaWAN uplink + HTTP webhook)** — A single fault-tolerant intake that decodes LoRaWAN payloads, MQTT topics, and vendor webhooks into one normalized time-series store, so any new sensor brand can be onboarded by writing one decoder rather than rebuilding the backend — the platform every other pillar reads from.
- **Time-series telemetry store + downsampling/rollups** — Raw readings retained at full resolution short-term, automatically rolled into hourly/daily aggregates per device and per plot, so charts and the dashboard's (currently hand-authored) weather/moisture tiles render instantly without scanning millions of rows — and stays inside free-tier storage limits.
- **Microclimate map by altitude band** — Live temp/RH/rainfall/leaf-wetness overlaid on the plot map and grouped by the 1,350-1,700 masl bands, so frost risk, GDD accumulation, and disease pressure are read per real microclimate instead of one farm-wide forecast number.
- **Alert & escalation engine (threshold + rate-of-change + silence detection)** — Rule-based and trend-based alerts (frost approaching, drying moisture target hit, fermentation pH break, tank temp runaway, soil too dry) routed to the right person by WhatsApp/SMS/push with acknowledge + escalate, so a $/lb-critical event reaches a human in minutes — and a sensor going dark is itself an alert (dead-guard-is-an-incident).
- **Processing telemetry binding (tank + drying-bed live curves)** — Fermentation tanks and drying beds stream pH/temp/moisture/RH directly onto the existing processing_batch record, replacing the static moisture_pct field with a live curve and target window per lot — turning the processing page from a snapshot into a real-time control board.
- **Offline-first edge buffering** — Gateways buffer and backfill readings when farm connectivity drops (common in Volcán highland), so no data is lost during outages and the timeline is gap-flagged rather than silently missing — critical when the data feeds traceability and certification claims.
- **Sensor-data quality & calibration tracking** — Drift detection, out-of-range/stuck-value flagging, and calibration-due reminders per device, so decisions and certification reports are never made on a quietly miscalibrated probe.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** LoRaWAN via The Things Network / The Things Stack (free community LoRaWAN network server) for long-range low-power farm sensors across 200ha, MQTT broker (Eclipse Mosquitto self-hosted, or HiveMQ Cloud free tier) as the device messaging backbone, Sensor hardware: Sensoterra / Teralytic soil-moisture+EC probes, METER Group ECH2O / ATMOS 41 all-in-one weather stations, Davis Vantage Pro2 station, Dragino LoRaWAN soil + leaf-wetness + temp/RH nodes, Sensaphone/Monnit cold-chain probes for tanks, Drying & fermentation: Cropster Roast/Cup + Cropster Origin (specialty-coffee industry standard for processing data; tank pH/temp logging), DRYGAIR/grain-style moisture probes, Anaerobic-tank pH/CO2 sensors (Atlas Scientific EZO pH/RTD probes on an ESP32 over MQTT), Weather/forecast APIs to fuse with on-farm sensors: Open-Meteo (free, no key), Tomorrow.io, OpenWeatherMap, NASA POWER and Meteoblue for highland modeling, Satellite/remote sensing fusion: Sentinel-2 NDVI via Sentinel Hub / Copernicus, Planet Labs, EOS Crop Monitoring for canopy stress to cross-check ground sensors, Connectivity/edge: Helium LongFi, Starlink for the reserve's remote gateways, ESP32/Raspberry Pi edge gateway with Balena for fleet OTA updates, Alert delivery: Twilio / WhatsApp Business API, Telegram Bot API, web push (free) — meeting crews where they already are, Standards: MQTT 5, LoRaWAN 1.0.4, SenML (RFC 8428) for sensor payload normalization, Sparkplug B for industrial topic structure, ISOBUS/AgGateway ADAPT for future machinery, OGC SensorThings API for interoperable querying, Time-series + free-tier hosting: Supabase Postgres + TimescaleDB-style hypertable/partitioning, or InfluxDB Cloud free tier; Supabase Realtime for live dashboard push

**Data model additions:** devices — id, type (soil/weather/leaf-wetness/tank-pH/bed-moisture/gateway), vendor/model, plot_id FK, block, bed/tank ref, install_date, firmware, battery_pct, last_seen_at, status, calibration fields, device_locations / sensor_placements — links a device to a plot/block/bed/tank with depth, altitude_band, and effective date range (sensors move between drying beds and tanks across the season), sensor_readings (hypertable, time-series) — device_id FK, metric (temp_c, rh_pct, soil_moisture_pct, ec, ph, leaf_wetness, rainfall_mm, bed_moisture_pct, brix), value, unit, recorded_at, quality_flag, reading_rollups — device_id/plot_id, metric, bucket (hourly/daily), min/max/avg/sum, for fast charting and free-tier-friendly retention, alert_rules — scope (device/plot/bed/tank/altitude_band), metric, operator/threshold or rate-of-change, target window, severity, channel, recipients, alerts — rule_id FK, fired_at, value, status (open/ack/escalated/resolved), acknowledged_by, acknowledged_at, device_health_events — battery-low, silence-detected, firmware, calibration-due, drift-detected (powers the dead-guard-is-an-incident rule), telemetry_bindings — joins live readings to a processing_batch (tank/bed) or harvest microclimate window for traceability, weather_observations — replaces the hand-authored weather table with real station + forecast-API fused observations per altitude band

**AI / automation:**
- Frost/low-temp prediction: fuse on-farm temp/RH/dew-point trend with Open-Meteo forecast to predict a frost event hours ahead per altitude band, not just alert when it's already happening.
- Drying-curve completion forecast: model each drying bed's moisture trajectory from live RH/temp/sun and predict the hour each lot will hit the 10.5-11% target, so the manager schedules bagging instead of constantly re-checking.
- Fermentation end-point detection: learn the pH/temp/Brix inflection that correlates with cup-quality outcomes per method (Washed/Natural/Honey/Anaerobic) and recommend the stop moment for each tank.
- Disease/pest pressure index: leaf-wetness + temp + RH duration models for coffee leaf rust (roya) and broca, triggering targeted scouting tasks into the existing tasks table before an outbreak.
- Anomaly & sensor-drift detection: ML flag for stuck/drifting/implausible readings so bad data never silently drives a decision or a certification claim.
- Irrigation/fertigation optimizer: per-plot soil-moisture + ET model recommends exactly which blocks to water and how much, minimizing draw on the reserve's watershed.
- Natural-language telemetry copilot: 'why did Bed 7 stall last night?' answered from the time-series + weather context, with the supporting chart.

**World-class:** ["A new sensor is unboxed and live on the map in under 10 minutes with zero backend code — auto-decode via a SenML/LoRaWAN profile, auto-bind to its plot/bed/tank.", "Every number on the dashboard, processing curve, and certification report is traceable to a specific device reading with a calibration and confidence stamp — no hand-authored figures.", "Alerts are precise enough to be trusted and acted on (low false-positive rate via rate-of-change + multi-sensor fusion), reach the right person on their channel in <2 minutes, and escalate if unacknowledged.", "The system degrades gracefully: connectivity loss buffers at the edge and backfills; a dead sensor raises an incident instead of showing stale-green; data gaps are explicitly flagged in the timeline.", "It is the single source of environmental truth the whole platform reads from — agronomy, processing, traceability, and certification all consume the same telemetry, so there is one reality, not five."]

**Coffee-specific angle:** "Janson's value is specialty Geisha and reserve lots where a few degrees or a few hours of over-drying destroys a premium that can be $40+/lb green — so per-altitude-band microclimate (1,350-1,700 masl), live fermentation-tank curves for the Anaerobic/Honey processes, and exact drying-window control are directly margin-defining, not nice-to-have telemetry. The 200ha quetzal-habitat private reserve adds a conservation-grade angle: stream-flow, watershed soil-moisture, canopy/shade and microclimate sensors let Janson prove sustainable water use and habitat conditions, which feeds Rainforest Alliance / organic / Bird-Friendly certification and the farm-to-cup story (since 1990) with hard, device-traceable data. It also fuses naturally with Cropster, the specialty-coffee industry's de-facto processing standard, so Janson's mill telemetry speaks the language buyers and roasters already trust."


#### Remote-sensing / satellite data pipeline
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** The farm agronomist / production manager (Janson's jefe de campo) who walks 10 dispersed blocks across 1,350-1,700 masl and today decides where to scout, irrigate, fertilize and worry by foot and memory. Secondary personas: the owner-exporter who must sign EUDR due-diligence statements for EU green-coffee buyers, and the reserve steward guarding the 200-ha quetzal habitat.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a 6.5-ha block like Talamanca is too big to walk every week, I want each plot's canopy vigor (NDVI/NDRE) trended automatically from satellite between my foot inspections, so I can send scouts only to the lots that are actually declining instead of guessing from the last place I stood.
- When an EU buyer asks me to prove our Geisha micro-lot is deforestation-free under EUDR before they will take the container, I want a mapped polygon for every plot with a satellite-backed 'no forest loss since 2020-12-31' check and an exportable due-diligence dossier, so I can sign the DDS and keep the lot legal to ship into Europe.
- When roya (coffee leaf rust) or a cold snap is starting on the high Geisha at 1,690 masl, I want a satellite red-edge stress flag plus a frost/temperature alert pinned to the exact plot before damage is visible from the road, so I can spray or act in the 48-hour window instead of after defoliation.
- When I am planning next season's renovation and shade management, I want each plot's multi-year NDVI baseline, canopy-shade percentage, and elevation/slope/aspect from the terrain model, so I can decide which aging blocks (e.g. Palmira, est. 2008) to stump and where to add shade trees.

**Features:**
- **Plot boundary mapper (GeoJSON polygons)** — Draw or import a precise polygon for each of the 10 plots - the keystone geometry every satellite metric, the EUDR dossier, and per-hectare yield math hangs off. Upgrades today's name-only Plot record into a real geo-referenced lot with computed area to reconcile against the stated areaHa.
- **Sentinel-2 NDVI/NDRE/EVI time-series engine** — A scheduled pipeline that pulls cloud-screened 10m vegetation indices per plot polygon every ~5-day revisit, stores the per-pixel mean/min and a sparkline history, and flags plots trending below their own seasonal baseline. Tells the agronomist where vigor is dropping before it is visible on foot.
- **Per-plot satellite health card + map overlay** — A liquid-glass plot detail view with an NDVI heatmap tile, a vigor trend line, current vs baseline delta, and cloud/data-quality badge - so the existing healthy/watch/at-risk Plot status becomes data-driven instead of a hand-set label.
- **EUDR deforestation-free verification & DDS dossier** — Run each plot polygon against forest-loss layers (GFW GLAD/RADD alerts, Hansen tree-cover-loss, JRC Tropical Moist Forest), assert 'no loss since 2020-12-31', and generate an exportable due-diligence statement bundle (polygon GeoJSON + screenshots + risk verdict) that the owner attaches to EU shipments.
- **Roya (leaf-rust) & crop-stress early-warning** — Combine the red-edge stress signal (NDRE drop) with weather (humidity/temperature) into a per-plot rust-risk score and push an alert when a plot enters the danger window - protecting the high-value Geisha from defoliation in the actionable 48h window.
- **Frost & cold-snap watch for high-elevation Geisha** — Fuse land-surface-temperature and DEM elevation/cold-air-drainage with forecast lows to flag frost risk on the 1,690 masl plots (Tizingal Alto, Las Lagunas) overnight, so crews can act before bud/leaf damage.
- **Terrain & shade intelligence (DEM + canopy)** — Derive elevation, slope, aspect and a canopy/shade estimate per plot from the Copernicus DEM and ESA WorldCover, feeding renovation, shade-tree and erosion-risk planning that the current shadePct field only guesses at.
- **Reserve & biodiversity monitor** — Track forest integrity / tree-cover change across the 200-ha private nature reserve from the same alert layers, turning Janson's quetzal-habitat conservation story into auditable, time-stamped evidence for marketing and carbon/biodiversity claims.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem (free Sentinel-2 L2A imagery; STAC + openEO + Sentinel Hub Process/Statistical APIs within the free user quota), Sentinel Hub (NDVI/NDRE/EVI evalscripts, Statistical API for per-polygon aggregates, WMS tiles for map overlays), Sentinel-1 SAR (radar, cloud-penetrating - critical given Volcan's heavy afternoon cloud/fog) for gap-filling optical NDVI, Global Forest Watch Data API (GLAD weekly 30m + RADD near-real-time 10m deforestation alerts, GeoJSON/JSON) for EUDR + reserve monitoring, Hansen Global Forest Change tree-cover-loss layers and JRC EU Forest Observatory / Tropical Moist Forest dataset for the 2020-12-31 EUDR baseline, Open Foris Whisp (FAO) - free EUDR plot-level deforestation-risk convergence-of-evidence API, Copernicus DEM (GLO-30) and ESA WorldCover for elevation/slope/aspect and canopy/land-cover, OpenET / MODIS-Landsat evapotranspiration and NASA POWER for ET and agro-climate context, Open-Meteo / Tomorrow.io weather API for forecast lows feeding the frost and roya-risk models (extends the existing weather module), Standards: OGC GeoJSON, STAC, Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF (COG), WGS84/EPSG:4326, and the EUDR Due Diligence Statement schema for the dossier export

**Data model additions:** plot_geometry (plot_id, polygon GeoJSON/PostGIS geometry, computed_area_ha, centroid, source: drawn|imported|gps), satellite_observation (id, plot_id, captured_at, source: sentinel-2|sentinel-1, index_type: ndvi|ndre|evi|lst, mean/min/max/stddev, cloud_pct, valid), ndvi_baseline (plot_id, doy/week, baseline_mean, baseline_stddev) - per-plot seasonal reference for anomaly detection, vegetation_alert (id, plot_id, kind: vigor-drop|rust-risk|frost-risk, severity, detected_at, evidence_json, resolved_at) - links to existing FarmTask, deforestation_check (id, plot_id|reserve, source: glad|radd|hansen|jrc, baseline_date 2020-12-31, loss_detected bool, checked_at, alert_geojson), eudr_dossier (id, lot_code, plot_ids[], polygon_bundle, risk_verdict, dds_status, exported_at) - ties to existing LOT_CODES / ProcessingBatch, terrain_profile (plot_id, elevation_min/max/mean, slope_deg, aspect, canopy_cover_pct from WorldCover), reserve_parcel (id, name, polygon, baseline tree_cover, monitored bool) for the 200-ha nature reserve

**AI / automation:**
- Per-plot anomaly detection on the NDVI/NDRE time series (compare each revisit against the plot's own seasonal baseline) to auto-set healthy/watch/at-risk and rank which plots need a human this week.
- Roya (leaf-rust) risk model fusing red-edge stress with humidity/temperature - a learned classifier (literature reports F1 ~0.78 from Sentinel-2 + ground data) that fires before visible defoliation.
- Optical-to-radar gap-filling: ML fusion of Sentinel-1 SAR with Sentinel-2 so cloud-obscured days (the norm in Volcan) still yield a usable vigor estimate.
- Satellite-informed yield forecasting - regress integrated growing-season NDVI per plot against historical harvestedKg to predict the season target and reconcile it against expectedYieldKg.
- An LLM agronomist-copilot that turns the raw index deltas + weather + deforestation checks into a plain-Spanish daily briefing and a ranked scout list ('go to Paso Ancho first'), and drafts the EUDR DDS narrative automatically.
- Automated EUDR convergence-of-evidence scoring (Whisp-style) that combines multiple forest layers into one defensible deforestation-free verdict per polygon.
- Frost-risk nowcasting that combines DEM cold-air-drainage modeling with forecast lows to predict which low-lying high-elevation pockets freeze tonight.

**World-class:** A grower opens any plot and sees a living satellite picture: a 5-day-fresh NDVI/NDRE heatmap clipped exactly to the polygon, the current value plotted against the plot's own multi-year seasonal baseline, and the healthy/watch/at-risk status set automatically by the data with a plain-language 'why' (e.g. 'Paso Ancho NDRE is 18% below its June baseline - red-edge stress consistent with roya; humidity high 3 days; scout block C-row 4'). Cloud-blocked optical days silently fall back to Sentinel-1 radar so the trend never has holes. EUDR compliance is one button: every lot already has a polygon, the deforestation-free check is green and dated, and the DDS dossier exports in seconds. The whole thing runs on free public Earth-observation data, refreshes on a schedule, and surfaces only the 2-3 plots that need a human this week - turning a part-time agronomist into the equivalent of someone watching all 44 hectares every single day.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson sells specialty green coffee into export markets that include the EU, so EUDR is not theoretical - by the 2026/2027 deadlines every Geisha and Pacamara lot literally cannot enter Europe without a geolocated polygon and a deforestation-free due-diligence statement; building the satellite pipeline doubles as the compliance engine that keeps the highest-margin coffee sellable. Janson's value lives at the top of the hill: the prized Geisha sits at 1,640-1,700 masl (Tizingal Alto, Las Lagunas, Baru Vista) where it is most exposed to frost and where red-edge rust detection protects the most $/kg - so the remote-sensing layer is aimed squarely at the reserve-tier, competition-lot coffee that defines the brand. The same forest-change layers that prove EUDR compliance also quantify and timestamp the health of the 200-ha private quetzal reserve, converting Janson's shade-grown, bird-friendly conservation narrative into auditable evidence for buyers and origin storytelling. And because the farm is in Volcan under Baru's near-daily afternoon cloud, optical-plus-radar fusion is not a nicety but a requirement for a trustworthy signal.


#### AI/ML layer — yield prediction, disease image-detection, demand forecast, anomaly detection
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: Don Janson / the head agronomist-owner ("the agronomist") who carries the yield-and-quality risk for the whole farm and decides where crews, money, and inputs go each week. Secondary users who consume the same intelligence layer: the mill master (processing anomaly + quality prediction), the field supervisor (in-pocket disease scan + scout alerts on a phone), and the commercial/sales lead (demand + price forecast for when-to-sell green lots).

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I am planning the season weeks before the cherries ripen, I want a trustworthy per-plot yield forecast (kg cherries and projected green-coffee out-turn) with a confidence band, so I can pre-commit crew sizing, financing, and forward-sell volumes to buyers without over- or under-promising.
- When a picker or supervisor sees suspicious leaves or cherries in the field, I want to photograph them on a phone (even offline) and get an instant call on whether it is leaf rust (Hemileia vastatrix), Cercospora, Phoma, miner, or berry-borer damage plus a severity and a treatment recommendation, so I can prune/spray the right plots early before a 30-55% yield loss spreads.
- When I am deciding which green-coffee lots to sell now versus hold, I want a demand-and-price forecast tied to the ICE Coffee C market, my Geisha/reserve premium history, and buyer demand signals, so I can time sales and lock the best margin on a perishable, price-volatile asset.
- When a processing batch or a plot is drifting off its normal pattern (fermentation running hot, drying moisture stalling, a plot's NDVI/harvest pace falling behind its block), I want to be alerted with the likely cause and the comparable historical baseline, so I can intervene before I lose cup quality or yield instead of finding out at the cupping table.

**Features:**
- **Per-plot yield forecaster** — A model that fuses each Plot's history (variety, altitude, trees, shade%, established year), in-season harvest pace, ripeness%/Brix curves, and satellite NDVI + weather into a forecast of cherry kg and green out-turn with a confidence band and a phenology-stage flowering-to-harvest timeline. Outcome: defensible season targets, crew/finance pre-planning, and credible forward-sale volumes — replaces gut-feel expectations on Plot.expectedYieldKg.
- **Phone disease & pest image-detector (offline-capable)** — A camera flow in the field app that runs a coffee-specific CNN/YOLO classifier (leaf rust, Cercospora, Phoma, leaf miner, CBB-bored cherries) on-device, returns disease + severity + a geotagged scouting record auto-attached to the right plot, and recommends prune/spray timing. Outcome: early intervention that protects the 30-55% of yield rust can destroy, and a spatial heatmap of infection pressure across blocks.
- **Green-coffee demand & price forecaster (sell-timing copilot)** — Ties live ICE Coffee C (KC) futures + the farm's own historical premium realized over C (Geisha reserve vs. commercial washed) to a sell-now-vs-hold recommendation per green lot, with margin and inventory-aging context. Outcome: better timing on a price-volatile, perishable asset and a clear margin story for the commercial lead.
- **Anomaly & deviation watch (plots + processing)** — Continuous baseline models that flag a plot or batch deviating from its own/peer history — fermentation temperature/time running hot, drying moisture stalling above target, a plot's harvest pace or NDVI falling behind its block — surfaced as ranked alerts with the comparable baseline and likely cause. Outcome: defects caught mid-process instead of at the cupping table; quiet yield drains caught early.
- **Pest-pressure & frost/weather risk forecaster** — A coffee-berry-borer degree-day model (thermal accumulation from local temps predicts generation cycles and spray windows) plus rust-conducive-weather and cold/frost-risk alerts driven by the weather feed, scoped to Volcán's 1,350-1,700 masl microclimate. Outcome: spray and protective actions timed to the actual pest/weather window rather than the calendar.
- **Cup-quality predictor from green-bean NIR/sensory** — A model that predicts likely cup score / quality grade from green-bean near-infrared spectra and the batch's processing fingerprint, flagging lots before they reach the cupping table and routing premium-potential lots to reserve handling. Outcome: protects the specialty premium and prioritizes the lots worth the extra processing care.
- **Natural-language farm copilot over the unified model** — An LLM assistant grounded on the farm's own structured data (plots, harvests, batches, forecasts, alerts) that answers 'which plots are behind target and why' or 'draft this week's spray plan' and explains every forecast/anomaly in plain Spanish/English. Outcome: the intelligence layer becomes usable by non-data-savvy field staff and turns predictions into decisions.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Sentinel-2 via Sentinel Hub / Copernicus Data Space (free tier) for NDVI/EVI time series per plot polygon; Farmonaut satellite+weather API as a managed alternative, Open-Meteo / OpenWeather / NASA POWER weather + forecast APIs for temperature, rainfall, humidity feeding yield, degree-day pest, and frost-risk models, ICE Coffee 'C' (KC) Arabica futures price feed (Barchart / Investing.com / a market-data API) for the demand/price forecaster, Coffee-specific vision model stack: TensorFlow Lite / ONNX / YOLOv8-v11 CNN for on-device leaf-rust/Cercospora/Phoma/miner/CBB detection, trained on PlantVillage + BRACOL/coffee-leaf datasets, Demetria-style handheld near-infrared (NIR) green-bean scanner + cloud API, and Cropster Lab/Origin API for cupping scores and roast/quality data sync, Anthropic Claude API for the natural-language copilot and explanation layer over the structured data (grounded, not free-text answers), Cloud ML hosting on a free/low-cost tier (Vercel functions + Supabase pgvector for feature/embedding store; model inference via a serverless endpoint or on-device for the camera path), SCA (Specialty Coffee Association) cupping protocol and Q-grade scales as the quality-label standard; degree-day phenology models from published CBB thermal-tolerance research as the pest-model basis

**Data model additions:** model_run — a versioned forecast/inference execution (model name+version, inputs hash, scope plot/batch/lot, run_at, status), yield_forecast — plotId, season, predicted cherry kg + green out-turn, confidence_low/high, phenology stage, model_run ref, scouting_observation — geotagged field record: plotId, photo ref, detected_disease, severity, confidence, recommended_action, observer, captured_at, synced flag (offline queue), disease_detection — per-image inference result tied to a scouting_observation (class probabilities, bbox, model_version), anomaly_alert — entityType (plot|batch|lot), entityId, metric, observed vs baseline, severity, likely_cause, status (open/ack/resolved), raised_at, price_forecast — green lot/grade, C-market reference, predicted premium, sell-vs-hold recommendation, horizon, generated_at, pest_risk_window — plotId/block, model (CBB degree-day | rust-weather | frost), risk_level, recommended_window_start/end, cup_quality_prediction — lotCode, predicted_score/grade, NIR/sensory feature ref, processing fingerprint, confidence, ndvi_reading — plotId, date, ndvi/evi value, source (Sentinel-2), cloud_pct — the satellite feature feeding yield + anomaly models, model_feedback — human label/correction on a prediction (actual yield, confirmed disease, realized sale price) closing the training loop

**AI / automation:**
- Yield prediction: gradient-boosted / LSTM models fusing NDVI time series, weather, and per-plot harvest history — auto-revised as the season's ripeness/Brix data lands
- On-device disease/pest image classification (leaf rust, Cercospora, Phoma, miner, CBB) with severity scoring and a block-level infection heatmap
- Demand/price forecasting against ICE Coffee C plus the farm's realized-premium history, producing per-lot sell-now-vs-hold recommendations
- Unsupervised/streaming anomaly detection on processing batches (fermentation temp/time, drying moisture curve) and plots (harvest pace, NDVI drift) with baseline comparison and likely-cause attribution
- Degree-day pest-cycle and weather-conducive-disease/frost forecasting that auto-schedules spray/scout/protect FarmTasks in the right window
- Green-bean NIR + processing-fingerprint cup-quality prediction to triage lots before cupping and protect the specialty premium
- Active-learning feedback loop: every confirmed disease, actual yield, and realized sale price is captured as a label that retrains and improves the models over time
- LLM copilot that turns forecasts/alerts into plain-language decisions and drafts spray/harvest/sell plans grounded on the farm's own data

**World-class:** World-class means the farm runs on prediction, not reaction: every plot has a living yield forecast with a confidence band that tightens as the season progresses and is back-tested against actuals each year; a supervisor can diagnose leaf rust from a phone photo in seconds, offline, with the result auto-mapped to the right plot and folded into an infection-pressure heatmap; processing deviations and quiet yield drains are caught mid-process with the historical baseline shown, not discovered at the cupping table; sell-timing is informed by the live C-market and the farm's own premium history rather than guesswork; every model is explainable, versioned, and improves from a closed human-feedback loop; and the whole layer is conversational so non-technical field staff actually use it. The bar is set by Demetria (NIR green-bean AI), Cropster (quality data backbone), and research-grade YOLO leaf-rust detection and CBB degree-day phenology models — assembled into one coherent intelligence layer over a unified farm data model.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson's edge is specialty Geisha and reserve lots at 1,350-1,700 masl in Volcán's distinct microclimate, where a single cupping-score point or one rust outbreak swings a lot's value enormously — so the AI layer must be tuned to high-value, low-volume, quality-first economics, not commodity bulk. Concretely: the price/demand forecaster models the realized premium of Geisha/reserve lots over the ICE Coffee C base (Geisha sells multiples above commodity), not just the futures price; cup-quality prediction protects that premium by triaging which micro-lots deserve anaerobic/honey reserve handling; the disease detector is trained on the species pressures at this altitude (leaf rust pressure rising with warming, CBB whose degree-day generation count is altitude-dependent — fewer generations at Janson's elevation than lowland farms, a real model input); and because Janson manages a 200-hectare quetzal-habitat nature reserve, the same NDVI/satellite and shade%/canopy features double as a conservation-monitoring and shade-coffee/sustainability-certification evidence layer — an angle a commodity farm could never use.


#### AI agronomist / conversational copilot for the team
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: Janson's lead agronomist / farm manager (the person walking the plots in Volcán who decides today's spray, prune, fertigation, and pick-order calls). Secondary, served by the same copilot with role-scoped views: (a) the owner/GM asking margin, yield-forecast and "should we sell this lot green or roasted" questions; (b) the wet-mill operator at 2am needing a fermentation/drying decision; (c) crew supervisors on a $40 Android phone needing voice answers in Spanish in the field with poor signal. The copilot is one brain over the whole unified model, with answers tuned per role.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I'm standing in Tizingal Alto and the lower leaves show yellow-orange spots, I want to photograph the leaf, ask 'what is this and what do I do', and get an identification (e.g. coffee leaf rust / Hemileia vastatrix) with a Janson-specific action plan that already knows this block's variety (Geisha = rust-susceptible), altitude, shade %, last spray, and the 10-day forecast — so I can act today instead of waiting for the agronomist's weekly walk.
- When I open the app on Monday morning, I want a proactive briefing — 'rust pressure HIGH on Blocks A/C after 4 wet days; Bed 7 natural is 2 days from over-drying; Crew Norte is 1 picker short of clearing the ripe Geisha before rain Thursday' — so the system surfaces what needs my attention instead of me hunting through six dashboards.
- When the owner asks me 'are we going to hit our 18,000 kg green target and what's it worth', I want to ask the copilot in plain Spanish and get a grounded forecast with the assumptions, the at-risk lots, and the revenue range cited from our own harvest, processing-loss, and price data — so I can answer with numbers I trust, not a gut feel.
