- When a mill operator messages at night 'fermentation tank 3 is at 14 hours, ambient dropped to 16°C, pH 4.2 — pull it or wait', I want the copilot to answer from this lot's variety/method/Brix history and our SOPs, log the decision against the lot, and escalate to the agronomist only if it's outside safe bounds — so a good call gets made fast and is traceable later.

**Features:**
- **Grounded farm chat (RAG over the unified model)** — A single conversational surface (web + WhatsApp + voice) that answers any question by retrieving from Janson's OWN data — plots, harvests, processing batches, workers, tasks, weather, costs, lab cuppings — and citing the rows it used. Turns six read-only dashboards into one 'ask anything' interface; eliminates the 'where do I look for that' tax.
- **Photo-based plant doctor (vision diagnosis)** — Snap a leaf/cherry/berry photo in-field; get pest/disease/deficiency ID with confidence, look-alikes ruled out, and a block-aware treatment plan (re-entry interval, organic vs chemical given certification status, product on-hand). Catches rust, broca (coffee berry borer), ojo de gallo (American leaf spot — endemic in wet Volcán highlands), and N/Mg/B deficiencies days earlier.
- **Proactive daily briefing + alerting** — A scheduled agent that runs each dawn, fuses weather + phenology + open tasks + drying moisture + labor roster into a ranked 'today's decisions' card and pushes the top 1-2 risks to WhatsApp. Shifts the farm from reactive firefighting to anticipatory management — the highest-leverage outcome of the whole system.
- **Tool-using action agent (read now, guarded write later)** — The copilot can DO things, not just talk: create a spray task for Block C, reassign a picker, mark a drying bed for turn, draft a green-coffee offer. Every write goes through typed, permission-scoped, audited tools with a confirm step for anything operational — so 'tell the system' replaces 'open the form'.
- **Voice + WhatsApp + offline-tolerant field access** — Spanish-first voice Q&A and WhatsApp thread so a supervisor with a cheap phone and patchy 3G can ask 'cuántos kilos lleva la Cuadrilla Norte hoy' hands-free. Meets the actual Volcán field reality instead of assuming a desk and good wifi.
- **Decision memory & explainability ledger** — Every copilot answer that drove an action (pull this tank, spray this block) is logged with the data it cited and the human who confirmed it, queryable later ('why did we pull JC-564 early?'). Builds institutional knowledge that survives staff turnover and underwrites traceability/certification claims.
- **SOP & varietal knowledge base ingestion** — Ingest Janson's own SOPs, agronomist notes, prior-season post-mortems, and authoritative refs (World Coffee Research variety catalog, SCA/CQI protocols) so answers reflect HOW JANSON FARMS, not generic web advice — the difference between a toy and a trusted advisor.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Anthropic Claude API (Opus for reasoning/forecast synthesis, Sonnet for chat, Haiku for cheap classification + the dawn-briefing batch) — primary LLM, with native vision for the plant-doctor and tool-use for the action agent, Vercel AI SDK + Vercel AI Gateway (streaming chat, tool calling, provider failover, per-feature cost tracking) — fits the existing Next.js 15 / Vercel $0-tier stack, Supabase pgvector (embeddings store for SOPs/notes/varietal docs) + Supabase Edge Functions for the scheduled dawn-briefing agent — stays on the existing free Supabase backend, no new vendor bill, Twilio WhatsApp Business API (or Meta Cloud API directly) for field chat + push alerts in Spanish, OpenWeather / Open-Meteo + a hyperlocal source (Tomorrow.io) for the forecast inputs the briefing fuses; pinned to Volcán/Chiriquí coordinates, Plantix / Kindwise crop.health API as a vision fallback / second opinion for pest-disease ID, cross-checked against Claude vision, World Coffee Research Variety Catalog + SCA/CQI cupping protocols as authoritative ingested knowledge (rust susceptibility, optimal altitude, processing notes per Geisha/Caturra/Catuaí/Pacamara), Cropster Cup / Hub export (industry-standard cupping + roast/processing data) so lab scores and roast curves feed the copilot's quality reasoning, OpenAI Whisper (or Claude-native audio path) for Spanish speech-to-text on the voice channel, Sentry + the AI Gateway usage logs for observability and the per-feature monthly cost cap

**Data model additions:** ai_conversations (id, persona/role, channel: web|whatsapp|voice, started_at, user_ref), ai_messages (id, conversation_id, role: user|assistant|tool, content, model, token_in/out, cost_usd, latency_ms), ai_citations (message_id, source_table, source_row_id, snippet) — grounds every answer to real rows for explainability, knowledge_documents (id, kind: sop|note|varietal|protocol|postmortem, title, body, source_url), knowledge_chunks (id, document_id, chunk_text, embedding vector) — pgvector store for RAG, ai_tool_invocations (id, message_id, tool_name, args_json, status: proposed|confirmed|executed|rejected, confirmed_by, executed_at) — the guarded write/audit ledger, ai_briefings (id, date, ranked_items_json, top_alert_text, delivered_channels) — the dawn-briefing output, diagnosis_events (id, plot_id, photo_url, predicted_label, confidence, lookalikes_json, action_plan, agronomist_override, created_by) — plant-doctor history, joinable to plots, ai_feature_config (feature, enabled, monthly_cost_cap_usd, model) — per-feature kill switch + cost cap (mirrors a pattern Andres already trusts)

**AI / automation:**
- RAG-grounded Q&A with mandatory citations to Janson's own rows — every claim traceable, hallucination-resistant
- Vision-based pest/disease/deficiency diagnosis from a field photo, block-context-aware (variety, altitude, shade, last spray, certification status)
- Proactive scheduled reasoning: a dawn agent that fuses weather + phenology + drying moisture + labor + open tasks into a ranked decision list and pushes the top risk
- Yield + revenue forecasting in natural language, grounded in the farm's own harvest/processing-loss/price history with stated assumptions and ranges
- Tool-using action agent with typed, permission-scoped, audited writes and a human-confirm gate for operational/financial actions (never let untrusted channel text drive an unconfirmed write)
- Anomaly detection narrated in plain language ('Bed 7 is drying 30% faster than its cohort — likely over-exposed')
- Cost governance baked in: model tiering (Haiku for classify/batch, Sonnet for chat, Opus for forecasts), prompt caching of the SOP/varietal context, per-feature monthly caps and a kill switch

**World-class:** World-class is a copilot that is (1) GROUNDED — every answer cites Janson's own rows, never generic web advice, and says 'I don't know / I'd ask the agronomist' at the edge of safe bounds; (2) PROACTIVE — it opens the conversation each morning with the 1-2 decisions that matter, not waiting to be asked; (3) MULTIMODAL & FIELD-NATIVE — photo in, Spanish voice in/out, works over WhatsApp on a cheap phone with bad signal; (4) AGENTIC BUT SAFE — it can take real actions through typed, audited, permission-scoped tools with a confirm gate, and no untrusted inbound message can ever silently drive a write; (5) EXPLAINABLE & ACCOUNTABLE — every decision it influenced is logged with its evidence and the human who confirmed it, so the farm builds institutional memory and can defend traceability/certification claims; (6) ECONOMICAL — model-tiered, prompt-cached, hard monthly cost caps so it stays viable on a $0/free-tier philosophy. Benchmark: a new hire could run the wet mill correctly at 2am by asking the copilot, and a returning owner could understand the entire season by chatting for five minutes.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson grows rust-SUSCEPTIBLE Geisha at 1,350-1,700 masl in the wet Volcán highlands, so the copilot's disease brain must be tuned to the two diseases that actually punish this microclimate — coffee leaf rust (Hemileia vastatrix) and ojo de gallo (American leaf spot, which thrives in exactly this cool-wet-high-shade niche) — plus the coffee berry borer (broca). It must reason about VARIETAL economics: a single bad fermentation/drying call on a Geisha lot torches the highest-value coffee on the farm (Geisha sells at multiples of Caturra/Catuaí), so the mill-decision and quality-reasoning paths should weight Geisha and competition/reserve lots far more heavily. It should know Janson's full chain (grow→pick→wet/dry mill→green→roast→sell) and answer the owner's signature question — 'sell this lot as green or roast it' — using real processing-loss and price data. The 200-hectare quetzal reserve is a sustainability/biodiversity asset: the copilot should help frame shade-grown, bird-friendly, traceable-origin claims for buyers and certification (e.g. Smithsonian Bird Friendly, Rainforest Alliance), turning Janson's conservation story into a marketable, defensible narrative grounded in the same data.


#### Mobile & offline-first field app — low/no connectivity
*Priority:* **table-stakes**  ·  *Opportunity:* **10/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the picking-crew supervisor / field foreman (e.g. a "Crew Norte" lead) who walks the plots all day on a low-end Android phone and must record what happens at the tree — kilos picked per worker, lot, plot — with zero signal. Secondary personas served by the same offline shell: the roving agronomist doing plot scouting/pest counts, the wet-mill operator logging fermentation/drying readings on the patios, and the farm owner/manager who needs the field data to land reliably back in the dashboard.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I'm weighing a picker's cherries at the collection point on a ridge with no cell signal, I want to record the kilos, the picker, the plot and the lot on my phone in two taps and have it stick, so I can pay people accurately at day's end without re-keying a paper notebook.
- When I'm scouting a far block deep in the reserve and spot leaf rust (roya) or borer (broca), I want to drop a GPS-pinned, photo-tagged observation against that exact tree-row even though I'm fully offline, so the agronomist sees precisely where the outbreak is the moment my phone re-connects.
- When the mill operator logs hourly fermentation pH/temperature and drying-bed moisture on the patio where Wi-Fi doesn't reach, I want those readings timestamped and queued locally, so the processing batch record stays complete and the lot's traceability isn't broken by a dead zone.
- When two supervisors edited the same picker's tally or the same batch on different phones while both were offline, I want the system to merge our changes deterministically and flag only the true conflicts for me, so a sync never silently overwrites someone's morning of work.

**Features:**
- **Installable offline-first PWA (service worker + app shell precache)** — The same Next.js app installs to the home screen and fully boots with no network — no separate native app to maintain, keeping the $0/one-codebase constraint while giving field crews a real app icon and full-screen UX on cheap Android.
- **Local-first write store with background sync queue (IndexedDB + outbox)** — Every harvest weigh-in, scouting note, attendance mark and processing reading is written to the device immediately and queued in a durable outbox that drains automatically when signal returns; the picker gets paid and the lot stays complete regardless of coverage.
- **CRDT / last-writer-wins-with-vector-clock conflict reconciliation + human conflict tray** — Multi-device edits made offline merge deterministically; only genuine semantic conflicts (two different kg for the same tally) surface in a review tray, so concurrent supervisors never silently clobber each other's data.
- **On-device GPS + offline basemap of the 200ha (plot/block geofences, cached tiles)** — Pins every observation and weigh-in to a plot/row using cached map tiles and the device GPS with no data plan, turning vague 'somewhere in Block A' notes into exact coordinates the agronomist can navigate back to.
- **Bluetooth hardware capture: weigh-scale, moisture meter, refractometer** — Reads kilos, parchment moisture % and Brix straight off the instrument into the record over BLE, eliminating transcription error on the numbers that drive payroll, drying decisions and quality scoring.
- **Offline barcode/QR + NFC lot-bag scanning** — Scan a printed QR or tap an NFC tag on a cherry bag/parchment sack to attach weights and move it through stages with no connection — the field origin of the traceability chain that later proves provenance to a green buyer.
- **Voice-to-field-note capture (on-device transcription)** — A supervisor with wet, gloved hands dictates 'Tizingal Alto, row 12, heavy borer' instead of typing; on-device speech-to-text drops it into a structured observation, so capture never stalls in the rain.
- **Conflict-free offline attendance & daily-tally roll-up with end-of-day signed summary** — Marks present/absent and accumulates per-picker kg through the day offline, producing a tamper-evident end-of-day crew summary the worker can thumbprint/sign on-screen — the input to fair, same-day pay.
- **Sync-health & data-freshness indicator + manual 'sync now' on Wi-Fi** — A persistent badge shows 'N records pending / last synced 2h ago' so a supervisor knows whether to walk to the office Wi-Fi before going home — no silent data loss, full trust in the queue.
- **Peer-to-peer device handoff (local Wi-Fi Direct / hotspot relay)** — When one phone reaches signal at the farmhouse and others don't, devices gossip queued records peer-to-peer so the whole crew's day reaches the server through a single connected handset.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Workbox / Next.js PWA service worker (Serwist) for app-shell precache and offline routing, IndexedDB via Dexie.js (or RxDB/PouchDB) as the durable on-device store, Background Sync API + Periodic Background Sync for automatic outbox drain, ElectricSQL or PowerSync or Supabase + Yjs/Automerge CRDT layer for local-first sync against the existing Supabase Postgres, ODK / XLSForm + XForm standard for portable, versioned field-form definitions (interoperability with NGO/co-op tooling), MapLibre GL + offline MBTiles/PMTiles basemaps (OpenStreetMap / Mapbox tiles cached for the 200ha reserve), Web Bluetooth API to read BLE weigh-scales, Felicita/Acaia-class scales, parchment moisture meters and digital refractometers (Brix), Web NFC API + html5-qrcode / ZXing for offline lot-bag QR and NFC tag scanning, Cropster (export the synced processing/cupping data into Cropster Origin for the green-coffee side), Twilio / WhatsApp Business API as a fallback SMS/USSD capture and sync-confirmation channel for the deepest dead zones, LoRaWAN gateway (e.g. The Things Network) bridge so fixed patio/drying-bed IoT sensors backhaul to the same sync endpoint, Sentry offline-queue + structured logging to catch sync failures without phoning home in real time

**Data model additions:** device_registry (device_id, assigned_user, last_seen_at, app_version, push token), sync_outbox / mutation_log (mutation_id, entity_type, entity_id, op, payload, client_clock/vector_clock, captured_at, captured_by_device, synced_at, status), sync_conflicts (conflict_id, entity_type, entity_id, base_version, candidate_versions[], resolution, resolved_by, resolved_at), field_observations (id, plot_id, row/sub-block, lat, lng, accuracy_m, kind: pest|disease|phenology|damage, severity, photo_refs[], note, observed_by, observed_at, offline_captured bool), media_attachments (id, parent_entity, local_uri, remote_url, sha256, mime, bytes, upload_status) — photos/voice captured offline, uploaded later, geo_features (plot_id, geojson polygon/rows, centroid, area_ha) — the offline plot/block geofences and map layers, device_capture_events extending harvests/attendance with capture_lat/lng, capture_source: manual|bluetooth_scale|qr, instrument_id, instrument_readings (id, batch_id|plot_id, instrument_type: scale|moisture|brix|ph, value, unit, ble_device_id, read_at), form_definitions (form_id, version, xlsform/xform spec, published_at) for versioned offline forms, sync_audit (record-level provenance: which device/user/clock produced each authoritative value) for traceability defensibility

**AI / automation:**
- On-device (TensorFlow Lite / ONNX Runtime Web) leaf-rust and coffee-borer image classifier so a scouting photo is auto-tagged with disease + confidence while fully offline, pre-triaging outbreaks before sync.
- Smart sync prioritization: an on-device model orders the outbox so payroll-critical weigh-ins and high-severity pest alerts drain first when a thin connection appears, rather than blindly FIFO.
- Anomaly detection on capture (e.g. a 9,000 kg single-picker tally or a moisture reading 30% off the batch trend) flags likely fat-finger errors at the point of entry, offline.
- Auto plot/lot inference: from GPS + cached geofences, pre-fill the plot, block and lot code so the supervisor confirms rather than types — fewer taps, fewer mis-assignments.
- Conflict auto-resolution suggestions: an LLM/ruleset proposes the most-likely-correct merge for flagged conflicts (e.g. sum vs overwrite for additive tallies) with one-tap accept.
- On-device voice transcription + intent parsing (Spanish) to turn dictated field notes into structured observations without a server round-trip.

**World-class:** "World-class is a field worker never thinking about the network. Capture is instant and local; the app boots in <1s with no signal, survives the phone dying mid-form, and the only network-aware UI is a quiet, honest freshness badge. Sync is conflict-free-by-construction (CRDT), so two supervisors editing the same crew offline merge cleanly and only true semantic conflicts ever reach a human — in a calm review tray, never a silent overwrite. Every record carries device/user/timestamp/GPS provenance so the data is defensible all the way to a certification audit. Hardware (scale, moisture meter, refractometer, QR/NFC) feeds numbers directly, so payroll and quality data are transcription-error-free. It runs on a $120 Android phone, in the rain, with gloved hands, in Spanish, on one battery charge for a full picking day — and reaches the server even from a dead zone via peer-to-peer relay or an SMS fallback. The benchmark to beat is Farmbrite 'Scout Mode' and ODK/CommCare-grade offline robustness, but local-first and conflict-aware in a way those tools are not."

**Coffee-specific angle:** "Janson's growing plots climb from 1,350 to 1,700 masl across a 200-hectare private cloud-forest reserve (quetzal habitat) — steep, dense-canopy, mountainous terrain in Volcán where cellular coverage is patchy at the farmhouse and effectively dead under the trees and in the far blocks. That is the textbook condition that makes offline-first the foundation, not a feature: the highest-value moments — weighing a picker's Geisha cherries at a remote collection point, pinning a roya/broca outbreak in a far block, logging an anaerobic-fermentation pH on the patio — all happen exactly where there is no signal. Because Janson runs the full chain (grow → harvest → wet/dry mill → green + roasted sale) and competes on micro-lot Geisha that commands top prices at origin auctions, the field-captured kilos, GPS lot origin, ripeness/Brix and process readings are the first link in the traceability and quality story sold to specialty green buyers. If that link is captured on paper or lost in a dead zone, the premium provenance can't be proven. This offline layer is the data foundation the entire integrated system stands on."


#### Real-time dashboards, KPIs & alerting
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm owner-operator / GM (a Janson family principal — e.g. Janette or Miguel Janson) who needs a single "state of the farm" glass-pane across grow, harvest, mill, labor, and sales. Secondary daily users: the head agronomist (plot-health & pest alerts), the wet/dry-mill master (process-window alerts: fermentation time, drying moisture), the harvest/crew supervisor (live picking pace & payroll-day totals), and the commercial/export lead (lot readiness & sales KPIs). Alert recipients extend to field crews via WhatsApp/SMS even though they never open the app.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I wake up during peak harvest, I want one glance at today's cherry intake, pickers present, batches in-window, and cash position, so I can decide where to put my attention before I leave the house.
- When a critical condition crosses a threshold (a drying bed exceeds target moisture, a fermentation tank passes its time window, a frost/heavy-rain front is incoming, broca pressure spikes on a plot), I want to be pushed an alert on my phone with the exact lot/plot and recommended action, so I act before the quality or crop is lost — not discover it the next morning.
- When I'm reviewing the season mid-cycle, I want to see actual vs target yield, cost-per-kg, labor productivity, and projected finish against last year, so I can re-forecast volume to my green buyers and adjust crew size and credit needs.
- When the mill master is off-site, I want drying/fermentation KPIs and their alerts routed to whoever is on shift with acknowledge/escalate, so a missed reading never silently ruins a $40/lb Geisha micro-lot.
- When my agronomist closes out a week, I want a plot-health scorecard (NDVI trend, soil moisture, scouting counts, days-since-task) ranked worst-first, so I can triage interventions to the blocks that move the season.

**Features:**
- **Live operations dashboard (role-aware, multi-tenant home)** — Replaces the current static 4-KPI page with a real-time, persona-tuned home (Owner / Agronomist / Mill / Supervisor / Commercial views) that streams updates via Supabase Realtime as harvests, weighings, and readings land — owner sees the whole farm at a glance instead of stitching it together by phone.
- **KPI definition & metric registry (single source of truth)** — One place that defines each KPI's formula, unit, target, owner, and refresh cadence (today-kg, season-to-date vs target, yield kg/ha, cost-per-kg-green, labor $/kg, drying days, cup-score avg, on-time-task %, AR days). Every card, alert, and report reads the same definition, so numbers never disagree across screens.
- **Threshold & alert-rule engine** — User-configurable rules (e.g. 'drying batch moisture > 12.5%', 'fermentation hours > method window', 'plot days-since-inspection > 14', 'daily intake < 70% of 7-day avg', 'AR > 60 days') that evaluate on every new data point and fire typed alerts with severity, dedupe, and snooze — turns latent problems into push notifications before loss occurs.
- **Multi-channel notification delivery + acknowledge/escalate** — Routes alerts to in-app inbox, web push, email, and WhatsApp/SMS for field staff who never log in, with on-call rotation, acknowledge, and auto-escalate if unacknowledged — guarantees a critical drying/frost alert actually reaches a human and is owned, not lost.
- **Weather & frost/rain early-warning band** — Live Volcán microclimate feed with rain-probability, frost risk, and accumulated rainfall driving harvest-pace and drying-decision alerts ('heavy rain in 2h — pull beds', 'frost risk tonight on high blocks') — directly protects cherries on raised beds and informs daily picking calls.
- **Season pacing & re-forecast widget** — Actual cumulative intake vs target and vs prior-season curve, with a projected finish date and confidence band — lets the commercial lead re-commit volumes to green buyers and the owner right-size labor/credit mid-season instead of guessing at season end.
- **Plot-health scorecard & map heatmap** — Worst-first ranked plots scored on NDVI trend, soil-moisture, scouting/broca counts, shade %, and days-since-task, plotted on a satellite/farm map — focuses scarce agronomy hours on the blocks that move yield and quality.
- **Alert timeline / audit & MTTR analytics** — Every alert's fire→ack→resolve lifecycle is logged, so the farm can see recurring failure modes (which beds always run hot, which crew lags) and report mean-time-to-resolve — turns firefighting into a managed, improvable process.
- **TV/kiosk 'mill wall' mode + daily owner digest** — A full-screen rotating board for the mill/packhouse wall and a scheduled morning WhatsApp/email digest ('yesterday: 642kg, 3 lots to drying, 1 open frost alert') — keeps the whole team aligned without anyone opening a laptop.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Supabase Realtime (Postgres logical-replication channels) for live KPI streaming — already the app's stack, free tier, Web Push via the W3C Push API + VAPID keys (free, no vendor) for browser/PWA alerts, WhatsApp Business Cloud API (Meta) and/or Twilio Programmable Messaging for SMS/WhatsApp field alerts in Spanish — the channel crews actually read, Resend or Postmark free tier for transactional email alerts and the daily digest, OpenWeatherMap One Call 3.0 / Tomorrow.io / Visual Crossing for Volcán microclimate, rain probability, and frost-risk forecasting, Sentinel-2 NDVI/NDRE via Sentinel Hub or Microsoft Planetary Computer (free) for satellite plot-vigor trends feeding the plot-health scorecard, Cropster (Cropster Origin / C-sar) and/or Sucafina-style trade APIs for cup-score and green-lot KPIs and traceability sync, Bluetooth/USB moisture meters (e.g. Sinar / Aqua-Boy / DICKEY-john) and bench-scale capture for drying-moisture and weighing data points that drive alerts, ICE Coffee 'C' futures + a specialty differential feed (Coffee Quality Institute / SCA price references) for cost-vs-market KPIs, ECMWF/IMHPA (Panama's national met service) frost and severe-weather advisories as an authoritative regional overlay, Grafana/OpenTelemetry-style dashboards optional for the kiosk wall; PWA install for phone-home-screen alerts, Slack/Telegram bot as an optional internal alert channel for the office team

**Data model additions:** metric_definitions (id, key, label, formula/source, unit, target, direction, owner_role, refresh_cadence) — the KPI registry SSOT, metric_snapshots (metric_key, scope_type[farm|plot|batch|crew|lot], scope_id, value, captured_at) — time-series for sparklines, trends, and re-forecast, alert_rules (id, metric_key/source, scope filter, operator, threshold, severity, window, dedupe_key, channels[], on_call_group, enabled), alerts (id, rule_id, scope_ref, severity, status[open|acked|resolved|snoozed], fired_at, acked_by, acked_at, resolved_at, recommended_action), notification_deliveries (id, alert_id, channel, recipient, status, sent_at, error) — proof an alert actually reached someone, sensor_readings (id, source_device, scope_type, scope_id, kind[moisture|temp|rainfall|soil], value, unit, read_at) — generalizes mill/field readings, weather_observations & weather_forecasts (location, ts, temp, rain_prob, rainfall_mm, frost_risk) — beyond today's 5-day literal strip, plot_health_scores (plot_id, period, ndvi, soil_moisture, scouting_count, days_since_task, composite_score, rank), dashboard_layouts / user_dashboard_prefs (user_id, role, pinned_metrics, view config) for role-aware homes, on_call_rotations & escalation_policies (group, member order, timeout, escalate_to), alert_acknowledgements / alert_events audit log (alert_id, actor, action, at) for MTTR analytics

**AI / automation:**
- Anomaly detection on metric_snapshots / sensor_readings (e.g. seasonal-baseline + EWMA) so the system flags 'drying running 1.4°C hot vs this bed's history' or 'intake unexpectedly low for this rainfall' instead of only firing on static hand-set thresholds
- Natural-language alerts and a daily narrative digest generated by Claude ('Yesterday Talamanca led intake; bed 7 crossed moisture target at 14:20 and is acked; frost risk tonight on the 1,650m blocks — consider pulling beds') — turns raw KPIs into a Spanish/English brief the family actually reads
- Predictive harvest-finish and yield re-forecast from intake curve + NDVI + weather, with a confidence band, replacing manual end-of-season guesses
- Alert-fatigue tuning: Claude/ML clusters and ranks alerts, suggests threshold adjustments, and auto-snoozes known-benign patterns so the inbox stays trustworthy
- Pest/disease pressure forecasting (broca/roya) from scouting counts + temperature/humidity to pre-emptively raise plot alerts and recommend the intervention window
- Conversational 'ask the farm' KPI query ('what was cost-per-kg on Geisha washed lots last month?') over the metric registry via tool-calling, so non-technical family members get answers without building reports

**World-class:** "World-class is an operations control tower where every number across grow→harvest→mill→sell shares one definition and updates live, where a threshold breach becomes an actionable, acknowledged, audited alert on the right person's phone in seconds (in their language), and where the system anticipates — predicting the drying overshoot, the frost night, the yield shortfall — before it costs money. It is role-aware (owner, agronomist, mill, crew lead each see their job), reachable off-platform (WhatsApp/SMS/push for people who never open a laptop), measurable (MTTR on alerts, alert precision, KPI-vs-target trended), and trustworthy (no metric disagrees between two screens, no critical alert silently dropped). The bar is Cropster-grade roast/process telemetry fused with utility-grade SCADA alerting and a Stripe-clean dashboard — but tuned to a single highland specialty farm, not a generic BI tool."

**Coffee-specific angle:** "For Janson, the alerting payoff is concentrated in two places where coffee money is made or lost: the mill and the reserve. A single anaerobic or honey-processed Geisha micro-lot can fetch $40-300+/lb, but it is destroyed by a few hours of over-fermentation or a drying bed that overshoots ~10-12% moisture — exactly the windowed, time-sensitive conditions a real-time threshold+ack alert is built for, and exactly what a once-a-day manual logbook misses. Volcán's 1,350-1,700 masl green-season pattern (clear mornings, heavy afternoon rain, occasional frost on the high blocks) makes weather-driven harvest/drying alerts genuinely protective of cherries on raised beds. Tying KPIs to the existing lot-code traceability (JC-xxx) means a cup-score or moisture alert is pinned to the exact lot a buyer is waiting on. And the 200-hectare quetzal reserve is a brand and biodiversity asset — shade-canopy %, NDVI, and reserve-buffer health become KPIs the farm can dashboard and even surface to certification bodies (Rainforest Alliance / Bird-Friendly), turning conservation into a measured, marketable story rather than a claim."


#### BI / analytics / self-serve custom reporting
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the Janson family owner-operator / farm general manager (think the second-generation Janson running the finca day-to-day) — the person who today asks "how is the season actually going?" and gets answers from spreadsheets, the wet-mill notebook, and the agronomist's memory. Secondary self-serve consumers: the head agronomist (plot-level yield/health), the wet/dry mill manager (process & outturn analytics), the finance/admin person (cost-per-kg, payroll, revenue, certification reporting), and the export/sales lead (lot profitability, green inventory). World-class means each of these can answer their own questions without waiting on a developer or a "report request."

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When it's mid-harvest and cherries are flooding the receiving station, I want to see real-time intake vs. target by plot/variety/picker on one screen, so I can decide where to send crews tomorrow and whether the mill can keep up.
- When I'm pricing a Geisha micro-lot for a buyer, I want to trace true landed cost per kg of green for that exact lot — picking labor + processing days + drying loss + overhead — so I can quote a margin I can actually defend instead of guessing.
- When the season ends (and at any board/family review), I want to build my own comparison report across years, plots, varieties, and process methods without writing SQL or emailing the developer, so I can see what's improving and what's declining and back next year's plan with numbers.
- When a certification auditor (Rainforest Alliance / organic / a buyer's traceability request) shows up, I want to export a defensible, lot-level report tying every green bag back to plots, pick dates, workers, and process records in minutes, so I keep the certification and close the sale.
- When something drifts — outturn ratio dropping, a plot's yield below trend, labor cost-per-kg spiking — I want to be alerted automatically with the 'why' attached, so I catch it during the season instead of discovering it in the year-end spreadsheet.

**Features:**
- **Semantic metrics layer (single source of truth for every number)** — Defines canonical metrics once — cherry intake, outturn ratio (cherry:green), yield kg/ha, ripeness %, brix, cost-per-kg-green, drying days, moisture-at-milling, labor productivity kg/picker-day, revenue-per-lot — so every chart, export, and alert agrees. Kills the 'three spreadsheets, three different totals' problem and replaces today's hand-authored daily_cherries/weekly_harvest/variety_shares/season_summary literals with computed, always-correct aggregates.
- **Self-serve report builder (drag-and-drop, no SQL)** — A guided builder where the owner/agronomist picks a metric, dimensions (plot, variety, block, altitude band, process method, crew, picker, lot, day/week/season), filters, and a viz, then saves and shares it. Eliminates the developer-as-bottleneck — the whole reason today's dashboard is static literals — and turns 'I wonder if…' into an answer in 60 seconds.
- **Lot-cost & lot-profitability ledger** — Activity-based costing that rolls picking labor, processing/drying labor, inputs, and allocated overhead onto each lot_code, then nets against the lot's green-coffee sale price. Surfaces true margin per micro-lot — the single most decision-relevant number for a specialty farm selling $40+/lb Geisha alongside commodity Caturra.
- **Cross-season cohort & benchmark comparison** — Year-over-year and plot-vs-plot comparisons normalized by area, tree age, and altitude band, with variance highlighting. Lets the family see 'Tizingal Alto Geisha is up 14% kg/ha but outturn fell 3 pts' and plan replanting/pruning/shade with evidence rather than gut feel.
- **Scheduled exports & boardroom-grade PDF/CSV report packs** — One-click, brand-styled (Janson forest #00291D) season report, certification traceability pack, and buyer lot sheets — exportable to PDF/CSV/Google Sheets and emailable on a schedule. Turns a week of manual spreadsheet assembly for audits and buyer requests into a button.
- **Embedded analytics inside every operational screen** — Contextual mini-charts and 'compare to plan / to last year' deltas right on the plot, harvest, processing, and worker pages (extending the existing chart primitives), so insight lives where decisions are made instead of in a separate reporting silo.
- **Anomaly & threshold alerting engine** — Watches the metrics layer for drift (outturn below X, moisture out of the 10-12% milling window, a plot tracking under its yield curve, labor cost-per-kg spiking) and pushes an alert with the contributing breakdown. Converts BI from look-back reporting into in-season intervention.
- **Natural-language 'ask your farm' query** — Type or speak 'cost per kg of green for the washed Geisha lots this season vs last' and get a chart + number + the query it ran. Makes the analytics genuinely self-serve for non-technical family members and Spanish-speaking staff, not just the spreadsheet-fluent.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Cropster (Cropster Origin / Cup-of-Excellence-grade green & roast analytics — the specialty-coffee BI standard; import/export lot, process, and cupping data via their API), Metabase or Apache Superset (embeddable open-source BI engine — self-hostable on a free tier, powers the self-serve report builder without per-seat SaaS cost; aligns with the $0 constraint), Cube.dev or dbt + the Postgres semantic layer (defines/serves the canonical metrics; dbt models the marts on top of the existing Supabase Postgres), Supabase Postgres + materialized views / pg_cron (computed aggregates replacing the hand-authored literal tables; pg_cron refreshes marts nightly — already in the stack, no new bill), QuickBooks Online / Xero (pull cost, payroll, and revenue actuals so lot-cost and profitability use real financials, not estimates), SCA/SCAA cupping score & Q-grade standards + Specialty Coffee Transaction Guide pricing benchmarks (so quality and price metrics are comparable to the market), Certification body data formats — Rainforest Alliance, USDA/EU Organic, Fair Trade (traceability export templates that satisfy auditor requirements), Open-Meteo / Visual Crossing / NOAA weather APIs + satellite NDVI (Sentinel-2 via Sentinel Hub or Planet) to correlate yield/quality against degree-days, rainfall, and canopy vigor, Google Sheets API + a PDF engine (Puppeteer/React-pdf) for scheduled exports and buyer/audit report packs, Resend or Postmark (free-tier email) to deliver scheduled report packs and anomaly alerts, GS1 / verifiable lot-code & blockchain-traceability standards (e.g. Farmer Connect/Thank My Farmer) for buyer-facing bean-to-cup provenance reporting

**Data model additions:** metric_definitions (canonical metric registry: id, name, formula/sql, unit, grain, owner — the semantic layer SSOT), report_definitions (saved self-serve reports: owner, metric refs, dimensions, filters, viz type, layout), dashboards / dashboard_widgets (composable boards of saved reports with positions), report_schedules (cron expression, recipients, export format, delivery channel), fact_harvest / fact_processing / fact_labor (star-schema fact marts derived from the operational tables, replacing the hand-authored daily_cherries/weekly_harvest/variety_shares literals), dim_plot / dim_variety / dim_worker / dim_lot / dim_date / dim_process_method (conformed dimensions, including derived attributes like altitude_band and tree_age), lot_costs (activity-based cost allocations per lot_code: labor, inputs, overhead) and lot_sales (green sale price/qty) → enables lot profitability, cost_categories / cost_allocation_rules (how overhead and shared labor split onto lots), metric_snapshots (period-close frozen values for year-over-year comparison that survive late edits to source rows), alert_rules / alert_events (threshold + anomaly definitions and the fired-alert log with contributing breakdown), saved_queries / nl_query_log (natural-language question → generated query + result, for the 'ask your farm' feature and audit trail), report_audit_log (who exported what lot/certification pack when — required for traceability defensibility)

**AI / automation:**
- Natural-language-to-query: translate a Spanish or English question into a safe, parameterized query against the semantic layer and render the result — self-serve BI for non-technical family/staff (Claude with the metric_definitions catalog as tool context; never raw free-text SQL against the DB).
- Auto-generated season narrative: an LLM writes the plain-language 'state of the season' summary on top of the metrics ('intake is 64% of target and 6% ahead of last year, driven by Caturra; Geisha outturn slipped — likely the wet-week drying') so the family reads insight, not just charts.
- Anomaly detection & root-cause hinting: statistical/ML drift detection on yield, outturn, moisture, and cost-per-kg, with the LLM attaching the most-correlated contributing dimension to each alert.
- Yield & outturn forecasting: time-series models (Prophet/lightweight regression) project end-of-season intake and green outturn by plot/variety, with NDVI + weather as features — turns the static target into a living forecast.
- Smart report suggestions: surface 'reports like this farm usually wants now' (mid-harvest crew-allocation report, pre-audit traceability pack) based on season phase.
- Insight-to-action: when an alert fires, auto-draft the corresponding task (e.g. 're-pick plot X' or 'pull Bed 7 off the patio') into the existing tasks module, closing the loop from analytics to operations.

**World-class:** "World-class is a governed semantic layer where every number has one definition, feeding a true self-serve experience: a non-technical family member or the head agronomist builds, saves, and shares a report — or just asks a question in Spanish — in under a minute, with sub-second response on a season's data and zero developer involvement. It's Cropster-grade specialty depth (lot-level outturn, cupping, and true landed cost-per-kg-green) fused with Looker/Metabase-grade self-serve flexibility, embedded contextually in every operational screen rather than siloed in a 'reports' tab. Analytics are proactive — anomalies find the user and arrive with a drafted action — and exports are audit- and buyer-grade with full lot traceability and an access log. The bar is: the family never again assembles a spreadsheet by hand, never waits on a developer for a number, and can defend every figure (margin, yield, certification claim) to a buyer or auditor on demand."

**Coffee-specific angle:** "For a specialty estate like Janson, BI is the difference between selling commodity cherries and selling provenance. The metrics that matter are coffee-specific and almost nonexistent in generic farm software: cherry-to-green outturn ratio (typically ~5-6:1, and a half-point swing is real money), brix/ripeness at pick, moisture-at-milling (the 10-12% window), drying-day curves by patio/raised bed, and — above all — true cost-per-kg-green by lot so a $40+/lb washed Geisha micro-lot can be priced against the Specialty Coffee Transaction Guide instead of guessed. Janson's altitude spread (1,350-1,700 masl) and varietal mix (Geisha, Caturra, Catuaí, Pacamara, Typica) make altitude-band × variety × process-method comparison a first-class analytical lens. The 200-hectare quetzal reserve and shade canopy turn sustainability metrics (shade %, NDVI vigor, water use) into certification-grade reporting that is itself a sales asset — and the existing lot_code traceability spine (harvest → processing batch → green) is exactly the join that makes bean-to-cup, audit-ready provenance reporting possible. Replacing today's hand-authored aggregate tables with a computed semantic layer is the concrete first step from 'pretty static dashboard' to 'world-class agribusiness intelligence.'"


#### Integration hub — API, webhooks, connectors (QBO, Shopify, weather, carriers)
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm's owner-operator / general manager (the Janson family principal who runs grow→process→sell and personally owns the QuickBooks books, the Shopify storefront on jansoncoffee.com, and the buyer relationships). Secondary: a part-time bookkeeper/admin and a technically-light "ops lead" who would configure connectors. The Integration Hub is the one place a non-developer toggles connections on, sees green/red health, and trusts that numbers reconcile across systems — they should never have to hand-key the same harvest weight, lot, or sale into three tools.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When my static 7-day forecast in the dashboard is just hand-typed numbers, I want the weather panel to pull a real live forecast and frost/heavy-rain alerts for my exact plot coordinates (1,350–1,700 masl), so I can decide tonight whether to push tomorrow's picking crew or protect drying patios from rain.
- When I sell a roasted bag on jansoncoffee.com or invoice a green-coffee importer, I want that order and its lot code to flow automatically into my books and decrement the right green/roasted inventory, so I can close the month without re-keying sales and so I always know how many kg of each lot I have left to sell.
- When a specialty buyer or certification auditor asks for the full story of a lot, I want one tap to publish that lot's harvest, processing, and cupping data out to the platforms they already use (Cropster, a QR traceability page, the certifier's portal), so I win the contract and the price premium without a week of spreadsheet archaeology.
- When a connector silently stops syncing (token expired, API down, a sale didn't post), I want to be told immediately with the exact failed record and a one-click retry, so a dead integration never quietly drifts my inventory or my books out of truth.

**Features:**
- **Connector catalog + OAuth/credential vault** — A non-developer toggles each integration on from one screen (Connect QuickBooks, Connect Shopify, Connect Weather, Connect Cropster) with guided OAuth/token entry; encrypted secret storage means no API keys live in spreadsheets or code, and a connection is provably owned by the farm.
- **Live weather connector (replaces the static `weather` table)** — Turns today's hand-typed 7-day forecast into a real feed keyed to each plot's lat/long+altitude, with frost / heavy-rain / wind alerts that drive picking and patio-drying decisions — the single highest-leverage swap because the panel already exists and is fake.
- **Commerce + accounting sync engine (Shopify <-> QuickBooks <-> inventory)** — Orders and invoices post once and reconcile everywhere: a roasted-bag sale or a green-coffee export invoice auto-creates the QBO transaction and decrements lot inventory, so revenue (today's static `season_summary.ytd_revenue_usd`) becomes a live, reconciled number and month-end stops being manual.
- **Outbound lot-publish / traceability push (Cropster, QR page, certifier portals)** — One action publishes a lot's full harvest→processing→cupping record to the platforms buyers and auditors use, plus a public QR provenance page per bag — converts Janson's existing traceability data into a sales and certification asset.
- **Webhook bus + event log + dead-letter retry** — Inbound webhooks (new Shopify order, QBO change, weather alert) and outbound deliveries run through one durable, signed, idempotent event log with automatic retry and a dead-letter queue — so no event is processed twice and no failure is invisible.
- **Integration health dashboard + alerting** — A live green/amber/red board per connector (last sync, records synced, last error, token-expiry countdown) with email/WhatsApp alerts on failure — a dead guard becomes a loud incident, not silent inventory/books drift.
- **Field-mapping & reconciliation studio** — A visual mapper binds external fields to the internal model (Shopify SKU↔lot/roast, QBO account↔revenue category) with a reconciliation report flagging anything that didn't match — prevents the silent mis-categorization that quietly corrupts financials.
- **Public read API + outbound webhooks for the farm** — A documented, key-scoped REST/GraphQL API and outbound webhooks let buyers, a future mobile picker app, or a partner roaster pull live lot/inventory/availability data — the farm becomes a system others integrate with, not just a consumer.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** QuickBooks Online API (Intuit OAuth2 + webhooks) for accounting; Xero API as an alternative, Shopify Admin API + Storefront API + Shopify webhooks (orders/create, refunds) for the jansoncoffee.com roasted-coffee storefront; Stripe / WooCommerce as alternates, OpenWeather One Call API and US NWS/NOAA api.weather.gov (free) for live forecast; Tomorrow.io or Meteomatics for agronomic frost/rain alerts; Open-Meteo as a $0 fallback, Cropster (Cropster Origin / C-sar) and Sucafina/Algrano specialty-coffee marketplaces for outbound lot + cupping publishing, Certification body portals/standards: Rainforest Alliance, Fair Trade USA, USDA Organic / NOP, plus SCA cupping-score schema and ICO/GS1 lot identifiers for traceability, Carrier / logistics APIs: EasyPost or Shippo (multi-carrier), DHL Express / FedEx / UPS for green-coffee export and roasted DTC fulfillment, plus customs/phytosanitary docs, Payment & FX rails: Stripe, Wise/PayPal for cross-border importer payments and USD/PAB settlement, Messaging/alert delivery: Twilio / WhatsApp Business API and Resend/Postmark email for connector-health and weather alerts, Vercel Cron + Supabase Edge Functions / Database Webhooks as the $0 scheduler and serverless runtime for the sync engine (no paid infra), Standards layer: REST/OpenAPI 3 spec + signed (HMAC) webhooks; OAuth2/PKCE for inbound auth; iCal/CSV/Cropster-export formats for portability

**Data model additions:** connectors — one row per available integration (kind: quickbooks|shopify|weather|cropster|carrier|payment; status; config jsonb), connector_credentials — encrypted OAuth tokens / API keys, scopes, expires_at, refresh_token (vault, not in code), integration_events — durable event log: direction (inbound|outbound), connector_id, payload jsonb, idempotency_key (unique), status (pending|delivered|failed|dead_letter), attempts, last_error, signature, field_mappings — external_field ↔ internal_field bindings per connector (e.g. shopify_sku → lot_code/roast, qbo_account → revenue_category), webhook_subscriptions — outbound endpoints the farm exposes: url, secret, event_types[], active, api_keys — scoped keys for the farm's public read API (scopes, rate_limit, last_used_at), external_orders — normalized Shopify/importer orders linked to lot_code + worker/customer, feeding inventory + revenue, inventory_balances — green & roasted kg per lot_code (the reconciled stock the sync engine decrements; ties into the existing `lots` anchor), sync_runs — per-connector run history: started_at, records_synced, errors, duration (powers the health dashboard), weather_observations — live forecast/alert rows keyed to plot lat/long+altitude (replaces the static `weather` table with real feed data)

**AI / automation:**
- Auto field-mapping suggester — an LLM proposes Shopify-SKU→lot/roast and QBO-account→category mappings from sample payloads, so a non-developer accepts mappings instead of authoring them.
- Anomaly detection on the event stream — flag a sale that didn't decrement inventory, a duplicate-looking order, or revenue posting to the wrong account before it corrupts the books.
- Natural-language connector status — 'What broke and what do I do?' summarizes the health board + dead-letter queue into a plain-language action ('Shopify token expired 2h ago, reconnect here; 3 orders queued, retry now').
- Weather-to-action agent — converts a frost/heavy-rain alert into a recommended task ('cover Patio 2 tonight, delay Tizingal-Alto pick') and can auto-draft a task in the existing tasks table.
- Demand/price intelligence — blends Shopify sell-through + ICE 'C' / specialty differential feeds to suggest which lots to list, hold, or reprice.
- Auto-mapped traceability narrative — generate the buyer-facing lot story (origin, altitude, varietal, process, cupping notes) for the QR page and Cropster push from structured lot data.

**World-class:** "World-class is an integration layer the owner forgets exists because it never drifts: every connector is one-click OAuth, every event is idempotent, signed, logged, retried, and dead-lettered, and a failure surfaces as a loud, actionable alert with one-click retry — never silent inventory or books drift (the 'dead guard is an incident' principle). It's bidirectional and bus-based, not point-to-point spaghetti: a single durable event log fans in (Shopify orders, QBO changes, weather alerts) and fans out (Cropster lot publish, certifier portals, the farm's own public API + outbound webhooks). A non-developer manages it from one health board with token-expiry countdowns and a visual field mapper; numbers reconcile across grow→process→sell so 'YTD revenue' and 'kg of lot X remaining' are always one live truth. The bar is set by Cropster's farm-to-roaster connectivity, Shopify's webhook/idempotency discipline, and Stripe's API/observability ergonomics — and it must hit all of this on $0 infra (Vercel Cron + Supabase Edge Functions + free weather APIs)."

**Coffee-specific angle:** "Janson runs the entire chain itself — grow at 1,350–1,700 masl, wet/dry mill, and sell both specialty green (to importers) AND roasted (DTC on jansoncoffee.com) — which is exactly why an integration hub matters more here than for a farm that only sells cherries: the same lot must reconcile across a green-export invoice in QuickBooks, a roasted-bag SKU in Shopify, and a traceability record a specialty buyer or Rainforest Alliance auditor demands. The existing `lots` table is already the shared traceability anchor across harvests and processing — the hub extends it outward to commerce, books, and buyer platforms. The coffee killer feature is the live weather connector tied to plot coordinates (frost/rain at altitude directly drives picking and patio-drying), and the differentiator is outbound lot-publishing to Cropster + a QR provenance page that turns Janson's Geisha/reserve story (and its 200-ha quetzal nature reserve) into a measurable price premium and a certification fast-path."


#### Identity, roles, permissions & multi-tenant (single-farm vs SaaS)
*Priority:* **table-stakes**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm owner/principal (e.g. Janson family principal such as Miguel or Janette Janson) acting as account administrator — the person who decides who gets access to what. Secondary personas served by the capability: the field supervisor (delegated admin over their crew), the agronomist, the mill operator, the bookkeeper/accountant, the seasonal picker (PIN/phone-only, lowest trust), and — once Janson sells the platform or onboards partner farms / co-op members / green-coffee buyers — the external buyer and the platform operator (Janson-as-SaaS-vendor) who governs every tenant farm.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a new picker shows up at the start of the Geisha harvest, I want to grant them a phone/PIN login that only lets them log their own picked weight and see their own pay — and revoke it the day the season ends — so I can put a real person behind every kilo logged without exposing payroll, plot maps, or buyer pricing.
- When my agronomist needs full plot, soil, and pest data but must never see worker daily rates or buyer contract prices, I want to assign a role that grants exactly that slice, so I can delegate the agronomy without leaking labor cost or commercial margin.
- When I (the owner) am traveling and want my farm supervisor to approve attendance and reassign tasks for the Tizingal crew but not touch finances or other crews, I want to delegate scoped admin over just that crew, so the farm keeps running without handing over the keys to everything.
- When a specialty green-coffee buyer in the US asks to see live traceability and lot data for the three reserve Geisha lots they pre-bought, I want to issue them a read-only external account scoped to only their lots, so I can prove provenance and build trust without exposing the rest of the farm or other buyers' allocations.
- When we decide to sell this system to other Volcán/Boquete farms (or run our co-op on it), I want every farm's data to be hard-isolated by default with its own roles and branding, so I can operate as a multi-tenant SaaS with zero risk of Farm A ever seeing Farm B's harvest, payroll, or buyer book.

**Features:**
- **Identity & auth foundation (link Worker → real login)** — Turns today's anonymous read-only app into an accountable system: every action is attributable to a real person. Bridges the existing worker_role data label to actual auth identities so 'who logged this kilo / approved this batch' is answerable.
- **Phone-OTP + low-friction PIN auth for field staff** — Pickers and crew with no email and intermittent connectivity can sign in by phone number + 6-digit OTP or a kiosk PIN at the mill — no passwords to forget, works on a shared device, matches the reality of a Panamanian farm workforce.
- **Role & permission model (RBAC + ABAC overlay)** — Named roles (Owner, Admin, Supervisor, Agronomist, Mill Operator, Bookkeeper, Picker, Buyer, Viewer) each mapped to fine-grained permissions (e.g. payroll:read, plots:write, lots:export, buyer-pricing:read). Attribute scoping (this crew, this plot block, these lots) layers on top, so access matches the org chart exactly instead of all-or-nothing.
- **Crew/block/lot data scoping (the ABAC dimension)** — A supervisor sees only their crew's workers, attendance, and tasks; an agronomist sees only assigned plot blocks; a buyer sees only their contracted lots. Reuses the farm's real structure (CREWS, plot blocks, lot codes) as the access boundary — the same dimensions the operational app already organizes data by.
- **Multi-tenant farm isolation (Row-Level Security by tenant_id)** — Adds a farms/tenant boundary so the platform can serve one farm today and many farms tomorrow with hard data isolation enforced at the database layer — Janson can run its own farm now and later sell to neighbors or run a producer co-op without re-architecting.
- **Delegated admin & invitations with scoped grants** — The owner invites people by phone/email, assigns a role + scope, and delegates limited admin to supervisors — onboarding a seasonal crew or a new buyer takes seconds, and the owner never has to be the single bottleneck.
- **External / buyer & partner portal accounts** — Read-only, lot-scoped accounts for green-coffee buyers, importers, certifiers, and co-op members — provenance and traceability sharing becomes a trust-building sales asset instead of a PDF emailed around.
- **Audit log & session governance** — Immutable record of every privileged action (grant, revoke, payroll edit, lot export, price change) plus active-session and device management — essential for certification audits, dispute resolution, and SaaS-grade trust.
- **Seasonal access lifecycle & auto-expiry** — Grants for seasonal pickers and short-term buyer accounts carry an expiry tied to the harvest season or contract end and auto-revoke — no dangling access after the season, which is the #1 real-world security gap on farms with churning seasonal labor.
- **Tenant branding, plan tiers & feature flags** — Per-tenant logo/colors and a plan/entitlement layer (seats, modules, lot count) so the same codebase serves a one-family farm free-tier and a paying multi-estate operation — the commercial backbone if Janson productizes.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Supabase Auth (GoTrue) — already in the stack; phone OTP, email magic-link, JWT with custom claims, and Postgres Row-Level Security as the tenant/scope enforcement layer (free tier, fits the $0 constraint), Twilio Verify or MessageBird — SMS/WhatsApp OTP delivery for phone-only field workers (WhatsApp is the dominant channel in Panama); falls back to email magic-link, WhatsApp Business Cloud API (Meta) — OTP + notifications over the channel farm workers actually use, Auth0 / Clerk / WorkOS — drop-in enterprise SSO, SAML/OIDC, and SCIM provisioning for the future SaaS tier when partner farms or corporate buyers demand single sign-on, OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect + SAML 2.0 standards — so buyer/importer organizations can federate their own identity provider, Cloudflare Turnstile / hCaptcha — free bot/abuse protection on the public sign-in and buyer-portal endpoints, TOTP authenticator apps (RFC 6238) + WebAuthn/passkeys — step-up MFA for owner/admin and finance roles, RBAC/ABAC standards reference (NIST RBAC, XACML/OPA policy model) and Open Policy Agent (OPA) as the policy engine if authorization outgrows pure RLS, Stripe Billing / Lemon Squeezy — seat- and plan-based entitlement metering when the multi-tenant SaaS tier monetizes (gates the feature-flag layer), Resend / Postmark — transactional invite, password-reset, and grant-notification email (generous free tiers)

**Data model additions:** tenants (farms) — id, name, slug, branding (logo/colors), plan/tier, locale, created_at; the root isolation key, user_accounts — id (links to Supabase auth.users), tenant_id, worker_id (nullable FK bridging the existing workers table to a real login), email, phone, status, last_seen, mfa_enabled, roles — id, tenant_id, key (owner/admin/supervisor/agronomist/mill_operator/bookkeeper/picker/buyer/viewer), name, is_system, description, permissions — id, resource (plots/harvests/workers/payroll/processing/tasks/lots/buyer_pricing/audit), action (read/write/export/approve/delete) — the granular capability catalog, role_permissions — role_id → permission_id mapping (the RBAC join), user_roles — user_account_id → role_id, plus scope JSON (crew_ids, plot_block_ids, lot_codes) for the ABAC overlay, granted_by, expires_at, invitations — id, tenant_id, phone/email, role_id, scope, token, status, expires_at, accepted_at, audit_log — id, tenant_id, actor_user_id, action, resource, resource_id, before/after JSON, ip, device, created_at (append-only), sessions / devices — id, user_account_id, device fingerprint, channel (phone/email/kiosk), issued_at, revoked_at, plan_entitlements / feature_flags — tenant_id, plan, seats, enabled_modules, lot_quota — gates the SaaS tier, tenant_id column added to EVERY existing operational table (plots, workers, harvests, processing_batches, tasks, lots, activity, and the dashboard aggregate tables) as the RLS anchor

**AI / automation:**
- Anomaly detection on the audit log — flag unusual access (a picker account exporting lot data, a login from an unexpected country, payroll edited at 3am) and surface it to the owner; an LLM (Claude) summarizes the day's privileged actions in plain Spanish.
- Natural-language access requests — owner types 'give the new mill guy access to drying batches and his own pay until end of season' and an LLM proposes the exact role + scope + expiry for one-tap approval (the request still requires explicit owner confirmation; an AI suggestion never auto-grants).
- Least-privilege drift detection — periodically analyze who actually uses which permissions and recommend trimming unused grants (e.g. 'the agronomist hasn't touched payroll in 90 days — revoke?').
- Auto-classified onboarding — infer a sensible default role from the worker's existing operational worker_role (Picker→picker login scoped to self; Mill Operator→processing scope) so linking the workers table to logins is one click, owner-confirmed.
- Smart session risk scoring + adaptive step-up — only prompt for MFA when the risk model (new device, sensitive action, off-pattern time) warrants it, keeping the field experience frictionless while protecting money/admin actions.
- GUARDRAIL: all AI here is advisory — it suggests grants, flags risk, and drafts summaries, but every actual permission change is human-approved. Untrusted external input (a buyer-portal message, an SMS) can never drive a grant.

**World-class:** A picker scans a QR at the mill kiosk, taps their phone number, enters a WhatsApp OTP, and is in — seeing only their own picked kilos and pay, nothing else, with their access auto-expiring the day the season closes. The owner manages the whole org from one screen: a visual matrix of people × roles × scopes (crews, plot blocks, lots), invites by phone in two taps, and watches an immutable audit feed of every privileged action. Authorization is enforced at the database layer (Postgres RLS keyed on tenant_id + JWT claims), so a permission bug can't leak data even if the UI is wrong — security is not an honor system in the front-end. Every farm on the platform is hard-isolated by default; a buyer logs into a branded portal and sees live, cryptographically-traceable provenance for exactly their lots. Step-up MFA (passkeys/TOTP) guards money and admin actions, SSO/SCIM is available for enterprise buyers, and the same model scales seamlessly from a single family farm to a 200-tenant co-op without a re-architecture. World-class here means: least-privilege by default, attributable by design, isolation proven at the data layer, and onboarding so frictionless that a non-technical farm owner can run it from their phone in Spanish.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Coffee farming is intensely seasonal and trust-driven, which makes identity the unsexy-but-foundational pillar. (1) The workforce is a churning seasonal crew of pickers — mostly phone-only, often shared devices at the mill, frequently indigenous Ngäbe-Buglé workers during the Panama harvest — so auth must be phone-OTP/PIN over WhatsApp in Spanish, and grants must auto-expire at season's end. (2) The existing worker_role enum (Picker, Agronomist, Mill Operator, Supervisor, Driver) is the natural seed for the system role model — it already encodes the farm's org chart; this capability simply gives those labels real teeth as access boundaries. (3) Janson's value lives in scarce, high-value reserve Geisha lots that pre-sell to specialty buyers and importers — buyer/importer read-only accounts scoped to exactly their lots turn provenance and the 200-hectare quetzal-habitat reserve story into a trust and sales asset, and feed cleanly into traceability/certification (SCA, Rainforest Alliance, organic) audits. (4) The realistic growth path — Janson licensing this to neighboring Volcán/Boquete estates or running a producer co-op — is precisely a multi-tenant SaaS, so the tenant-isolation backbone is what lets the family's own farm tool become a regional product without re-architecture.


#### Notifications — WhatsApp/SMS/push, Latam-first
*Priority:* **table-stakes**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Miguel Janson (Farm Supervisor / Field Ops) is the primary persona — the human dispatcher who today phones, WhatsApps, or physically walks to find each crew lead, picker, mill operator and driver. Secondary recipients: Janette Janson (Agronomist) for agronomy/pest alerts; Néstor Gómez and Yarisel Pitti (Mill Operators) for processing thresholds; Raúl Santamaría (Driver) for logistics/pickup; the 8 pickers (Spanish-speaking, WhatsApp-native on +507 mobiles, near-zero app-install appetite); and the farm owner for exception/escalation digests. The persona reality: rural Volcán has patchy LTE under the Barú canopy, everyone lives in WhatsApp, almost nobody will install or log into a SaaS dashboard — so the notification IS the product surface for most of the workforce.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a frost or heavy-rain window is forecast for tonight (the weather feed flips a plot to at-risk), I want every crew lead and the agronomist to get a Spanish WhatsApp alert naming the exact plots and the protective action, so I can mobilize frost protection before dawn without phoning eleven people one by one.
- When a processing batch crosses a critical threshold (fermentation timer expires, drying-bed moisture drops to 11%, a lot is ready to turn or bag), I want the on-shift mill operator to get a WhatsApp/push alert tagging the lot code, bed and required action, so I can stop over-fermentation or mold ruining a 40-dollar-a-pound Geisha micro-lot.
- When I assign or reschedule an agronomy task, I want the assignee to receive it on WhatsApp with one-tap 'done / blocked / need help' quick-reply buttons, so the task board stays live from the field without anyone opening the web app.
- When a picker hasn't clocked in by 6:30am or a daily lot is flagged for low ripeness, I want a targeted exception alert (not a firehose of every event), so I can reassign the route and protect cup quality that same morning instead of discovering it at payroll.
- When a green-coffee buyer's shipment, sample, or payment milestone changes, I want the owner and Raúl to get an English notification with the lot, contract and next action, so a 22,000-dollar export container never stalls on the dock for a missed document.

**Features:**
- **WhatsApp Business Cloud API channel (primary)** — Reaches 100% of the workforce on the app they already use; verified Business sender + green check builds trust. Pre-approved template messages (Utility category, ~free or fractions of a cent in PA) carry alerts even outside the 24h session window; inbound replies open a free 24h service window for two-way task updates.
- **SMS fallback + voice-call escalation (Twilio)** — Guarantees delivery when a picker is off WhatsApp or under-canopy with no data — SMS rides cell voice-band; for life/crop-critical frost alerts unacknowledged in N minutes, auto-place a Spanish text-to-speech voice call so a freeze warning is never missed.
- **Web Push + installable PWA for office/admin** — Free, no per-message cost, instant desktop/mobile push for Miguel and Janette at the farm office; pairs with the existing Next.js app so the dashboard becomes a real-time console without a native app build.
- **Rules engine ('Alert Recipes')** — Turns farm thresholds into self-serve triggers — frost < N°C, rainPct > 75% on an at-risk plot, drying moisture <= target, fermentation hours elapsed, task overdue, picker no-show, lot ready-to-ship — each mapping conditions to a template, audience and channel. The farm tunes its own alerts without code.
- **Audience routing by role / crew / plot / shift** — A frost alert hits only crew leads + agronomist for the affected blocks; a mill threshold hits only the on-shift operator; a logistics event hits owner + driver. No firehose — every person gets exactly their slice, which is what keeps notifications trusted instead of muted.
- **Two-way quick-reply task loop** — Outbound task templates carry WhatsApp interactive buttons (Hecho / Bloqueado / Necesito ayuda); the inbound reply webhook writes status straight onto FarmTask. The field updates the board by tapping a button — zero app, zero typing.
- **Acknowledgement, escalation & on-call rotation** — Critical alerts require an ack; unacknowledged ones escalate up a chain (operator -> supervisor -> owner) and across channels (push -> WhatsApp -> SMS -> voice). A spoiled-lot or lost-frost-night event always finds a human.
- **Spanish/English i18n + localized templates** — Field crew get Spanish (es-PA), buyers/owner get English, with per-recipient locale and Panama-timezone/quiet-hours so a non-urgent task never pings a picker at 11pm. Localization is table-stakes for a bilingual export farm.
- **Daily/weekly digest & morning brief** — A 5:30am Spanish WhatsApp brief to Miguel — today's tasks, weather risk, batches needing attention, who's clocked in — and a weekly English owner digest. Batches low-urgency signals so urgent alerts stay rare and credible.
- **Per-recipient preferences, opt-in/opt-out & consent ledger** — Each worker sets channel + quiet hours + categories; explicit WhatsApp opt-in is captured and timestamped (Meta policy + data-protection requirement). Protects deliverability quality rating and respects the workforce.
- **Delivery log, retries & dead-letter inbox** — Every send is tracked sent->delivered->read->failed with provider receipts; failed sends retry then surface in an admin inbox so a missed harvest-window alert is visible, not silently dropped.
- **Broadcast / announcement composer** — One-to-many Spanish broadcasts to a crew or the whole farm (payday, weather closure, road grading, meeting) from the dashboard — replaces the supervisor's manual WhatsApp group blast with audited, templated, opt-out-respecting sends.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** WhatsApp Business Platform (Meta Cloud API) — primary channel, template/HSM messages, interactive quick-reply buttons, inbound webhook; verified Business sender, Twilio — SMS fallback, Programmable Voice for TTS escalation calls, and Twilio's WhatsApp Business API as an alternative BSP, Gupshup or 360dialog (Meta Business Solution Providers) — Latam-focused WhatsApp BSPs with strong PA/LatAm pricing and onboarding, Web Push via VAPID (web-push standard) + Service Worker — free push to the PWA, no vendor, OpenWeatherMap / Open-Meteo / Visual Crossing — frost & rain forecast feeds that fire weather-triggered alerts (Open-Meteo is free), Expo Push or Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) — if a thin native wrapper is ever added for office staff, Resend or Amazon SES — transactional email for buyer/owner digests and export-document notifications (generous/cheap free tiers), Supabase Realtime + Postgres triggers + pg_cron / Edge Functions — the free-tier backbone that detects threshold crossings and enqueues sends, Meta WhatsApp Business Management API — programmatic template submission/approval and quality-rating monitoring, ICS / Google Calendar feed — task-due reminders syndicated to calendars for office staff

**Data model additions:** notification_channels — per-recipient enrolled channels (whatsapp | sms | push | voice | email) with verified identifier (E.164 phone, push subscription, email) and verification/consent state, notification_preferences — per worker/role: enabled categories, locale (es-PA | en), quiet-hours window, timezone, channel priority order, alert_rules ('Alert Recipes') — trigger definition (event type + threshold expression), target audience query, template_id, channel, severity, ack-required flag, escalation policy ref, notification_templates — versioned localized message bodies (es/en) with variable slots and the approved Meta template name/category, notifications — a fired alert instance: rule_id, subject entity (plotId | batchId | taskId | workerId | lotCode), severity, created_at, dedupe key, notification_deliveries — one row per recipient×channel attempt: status (queued|sent|delivered|read|failed), provider, provider_message_id, error, retry_count, cost, acknowledgements — recipient, notification_id, ack_at, channel, response value (e.g. task quick-reply Hecho/Bloqueado), escalation_policies — ordered steps (delay, next-channel, next-recipient) referenced by alert_rules, consent_log — append-only opt-in/opt-out events per recipient×channel with timestamp + source (WhatsApp opt-in compliance), inbound_messages — raw inbound WhatsApp/SMS payloads mapped to a worker + intent (task status update, free-text), linking the two-way loop back to FarmTask

**AI / automation:**
- Spanish/English auto-translation and tone-adjusted phrasing of alert bodies (field crew Spanish, buyers English) from a single source event, via Claude
- Smart digesting / noise suppression — cluster and summarize low-priority events into one morning brief, learning each recipient's mute patterns so only genuine exceptions break through
- Natural-language Alert Recipe authoring — Miguel types 'avísame en español si helada bajo 4 grados en lotes en riesgo' and the LLM compiles it into a structured alert_rule
- Inbound free-text intent parsing — interpret a picker's WhatsApp reply ('terminé el lote de Talamanca, faltó malla') into a structured task status + note + flag
- Predictive frost/harvest-window alerts — fuse forecast + altitude + plot status to warn 'Geisha at Las Lagunas likely peak-ripe in 3 days, schedule the pick' before a human notices
- Severity & escalation tuning — model learns which alert types actually get acted on vs ignored to recommend de-escalating chronically-muted categories
- Send-time optimization — predict the best moment to reach each worker (post-clock-in, lunch) to maximize read/ack rates without spamming

**World-class:** World-class is an event-driven, channel-agnostic alerting fabric where every farm signal — weather, processing thresholds, task changes, attendance, logistics — flows through one rules engine and reaches exactly the right person on the channel they actually read, in their language, with guaranteed delivery and acknowledgement. It is Latam-first: WhatsApp is the default surface (not an afterthought), with SMS and TTS-voice escalation so a frost warning physically cannot be missed, and a free Web Push/PWA console for office staff. It is two-way: the field updates the system by tapping a WhatsApp button, never opening an app. It is quiet by design — exception-only, digested, quiet-hours-aware — so alerts stay trusted instead of muted. Every send is logged, retried, and auditable with provider read-receipts and a consent ledger; templates are versioned and localized; and an LLM layer authors rules from plain Spanish, translates per-recipient, and suppresses noise. The benchmark is PagerDuty/Opsgenie-grade reliability and escalation, delivered over consumer messaging rails the rural workforce already lives in.

**Coffee-specific angle:** For Janson Coffee the highest-value, most coffee-specific triggers are the ones that protect a tiny, irreplaceable, ultra-high-value crop in a single highland micro-climate. A frost or hard-rain night at 1,350–1,700 masl can damage a season's Geisha; the moment a plot flips to at-risk, the alert fabric must mobilize crews before dawn. In the wet/dry mill, specialty processing is unforgiving — an anaerobic Geisha ferment over-running by hours, or a drying bed slipping past target moisture, is the difference between a 40-dollar-a-pound competition lot and a defect, so lot-code-tagged threshold alerts to the on-shift operator directly defend margin. Because the workforce is Spanish-speaking, WhatsApp-native, and spread across remote blocks under canopy with patchy data, WhatsApp-first with SMS/voice fallback isn't a preference — it's the only design that reaches Crew Norte, Tizingal and the mill. The same fabric speaks English to the export buyers (sample shipped, contract signed, payment due, certification/quetzal-reserve traceability events), bridging the bilingual grower-to-buyer chain on one rail.


#### Document & media management — photos, certs, contracts
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the Farm Owner / Operations Manager (the Janson family principal who answers buyers, auditors and the bank). Secondary daily users: the Agronomist (field photos, soil/pest evidence, agrochemical receipts), the Mill/QC lead (cupping sheets, moisture certs, lab reports), and an Admin/Export coordinator who assembles certification + export dossiers. Auditors (Rainforest Alliance / organic / EUDR) and green-coffee buyers are external read-only consumers of curated document packages.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a Rainforest Alliance or organic auditor schedules an annual visit, I want every required record (agrochemical applications, worker contracts, training logs, traceability volumes) already linked to the right plot/lot and tagged to the checklist, so I can hand over a complete dossier in an afternoon instead of digging through WhatsApp, paper binders and three laptops the week before.
- When a green-coffee buyer in Tokyo or Oslo asks 'show me this Geisha lot's story', I want to generate a single shareable, branded lot dossier — origin photos, the cupping score sheet, processing method, moisture cert, altitude/plot map, harvest dates — from data already attached to lot JC-602, so I can close the sale at a premium without re-photographing or re-typing anything.
- When the agronomist finishes a plot inspection or a pest scouting walk, I want to snap geotagged, timestamped photos from my phone offline in the field and have them auto-attach to that plot and that task once I'm back on wifi, so the visual record of canopy, CBB damage or leaf rust is permanent evidence — not lost in a camera roll.
- When a contract, certificate, lease, or agrochemical permit is about to expire, I want to be reminded 60/30/7 days out with the document already on file, so I never ship against a lapsed organic certificate or get caught at customs with an expired phytosanitary permit.

**Features:**
