- **Polymorphic media & document store with attach-to-anything** — Any file (photo, PDF, lab report, contract, cert, voice memo) attaches to any entity — plot, harvest, lot, processing batch, worker, task, or the farm itself — via a single attachments join, so every record carries its own evidence trail and nothing lives in a disconnected folder.
- **Mobile-first field capture (offline-first PWA + geotag/EXIF)** — Pickers and the agronomist capture geotagged, timestamped photos and short clips in dead-zone fincas; uploads queue and sync on reconnect, auto-attaching to the plot/task by GPS-to-plot-boundary match, turning every field walk into permanent visual evidence.
- **Certification & compliance vault with expiry tracking** — Organic, Rainforest Alliance, Fair Trade, phytosanitary, business and land-lease documents are stored as typed records with issuer, scope, valid-from/until and renewal reminders (60/30/7 day), so a lapsed cert can never silently gate a shipment.
- **Lot dossier / digital passport generator** — One click assembles a branded, shareable PDF or web link for a traceability lot — origin photos, plot + altitude + varietal, cupping score, processing method, moisture cert, harvest window — letting Janson sell the Geisha story and command a specialty premium.
- **Contract & buyer-agreement repository with e-signature** — Sales contracts, picker labor agreements, and supplier POs are stored, versioned, and signable in-app, so terms, volumes and prices are auditable and disputes resolve against a signed source of truth.
- **Audit-package / dossier builder mapped to standard checklists** — Select a standard (RA 2026 Regenerative, USDA/EU organic, EUDR) and the system bundles every linked document against that standard's checklist, flags gaps before the auditor arrives, and exports the package — collapsing audit prep from weeks to a day.
- **OCR + AI auto-classification and full-text search** — Dropped invoices, receipts and certs are OCR'd, classified (cert vs receipt vs lab report), date- and entity-matched, and made full-text searchable, so 'find the 2025 organic certificate' or 'all glyphosate-free attestations' returns in seconds.
- **Access control, watermarking & expiring share links** — Sensitive contracts stay internal while buyer-facing lot dossiers go out as watermarked, time-limited links with view analytics, so Janson controls who sees what and proves provenance without emailing loose PDFs.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Supabase Storage (free-tier object store + signed URLs) as the file backend; Postgres for the attachments metadata model, Cropster Origin / Cropster Cup — pull cupping score sheets and lot/sample sheets (SCA & CoE protocols) as attachable documents, Rainforest Alliance traceability platform / Certificate Holder portal (Traceability Annex v1.4) and EUDR geolocation/due-diligence record export, USDA Organic / EU organic (NOP / EU 2018/848) and Fair Trade USA certificate records; Panama MIDA phytosanitary + AUPSA export permits, SCA CVA cupping form / score sheet standard and Cup of Excellence sheet templates for structured QC docs, DocuSign or Dropbox Sign (free tier) for e-signature on contracts and labor agreements, Google Cloud Vision / Tesseract OCR (or AWS Textract free tier) for receipt/cert text extraction and classification, EXIF/GPS metadata parsing (exifr) + the existing plot polygons for auto-attach-by-location, WhatsApp Business Cloud API — ingest photos/docs farm staff already send via WhatsApp into the vault, GS1 / verifiable-credential digital product passport standards for the buyer-facing lot passport

**Data model additions:** media_assets — every uploaded file: storage path/signed-URL, mime, bytes, checksum/hash, capturer (worker_id), captured_at, EXIF lat/lng, width/height, OCR text, AI-classified kind, documents — typed business documents (certificate | contract | lab_report | invoice/receipt | permit | cupping_sheet | photo_set | manual): title, doc_type, issuer, scope, valid_from, valid_until, status, current_version_id, document_versions — immutable version history per document (version_no, media_asset_id, uploaded_by, note) for audit-grade trails, attachments — polymorphic join: entity_type (plot|harvest|lot|processing_batch|worker|task|farm) + entity_id ↔ media_asset_id/document_id, with role/caption (one file can attach to many records), certifications — certificate scheme (organic|rainforest_alliance|fair_trade|eudr|phytosanitary), certifying_body, certificate_number, covered plots/lots, expiry, reminder_offsets, lot_dossiers / lot_passports — generated buyer-facing packages per lot_code: included asset ids, public_share_token, watermark flag, expires_at, view count, audit_packages — a standard + checklist snapshot: standard_ref, generated_by, generated_at, included document ids, gap/missing list, share_links — token, target (document or dossier), expires_at, watermark, view/download log for access analytics

**AI / automation:**
- OCR + LLM auto-classification of dropped files into doc_type (cert vs receipt vs lab report vs cupping sheet) with extracted issuer, dates and amounts pre-filled for one-tap confirmation
- Auto-attach photos to the correct plot by matching EXIF GPS against plot boundary polygons; flag low-confidence matches for review
- Expiry/gap intelligence: scan the vault against the active certification checklists and surface 'this shipment's dossier relies on a cert expiring in 21 days' and 'RA checklist item 3.2 has no linked evidence'
- Vision models on field photos to pre-tag agronomic signals (leaf rust, coffee berry borer, ripeness, canopy/shade) and auto-link the photo to the relevant plot task and inspection record
- Natural-language search and Q&A over the whole vault ('show every organic attestation touching the Geisha plots this season') with cited source documents
- Auto-draft the buyer-facing lot story / dossier copy from structured lot data (varietal, altitude, method, cupping notes) for the agronomist to edit, not write from scratch

**World-class:** World-class is a single source of truth where every photo, certificate, contract and lab report is born attached to the plot, lot or worker it describes — captured on a phone in an offline finca, auto-geotagged to the right plot polygon, OCR-classified and instantly searchable. Audit prep is a button, not a fire drill: pick a standard, see green/red gaps against its checklist, export the dossier. A buyer scans a QR on a bag of green and sees a tamper-evident digital passport — origin photos, GPS coordinates, cupping score, harvest dates, processing — proving the Geisha is what Janson says it is. Certificates never lapse silently because the system nags 60/30/7 days out and can fail-close a shipment dossier built on an expired cert. Nothing is a loose file in a folder; everything is a typed, versioned, access-controlled, provenance-stamped record that makes Janson money (premium-justifying transparency) and saves time (audits, customs, disputes).

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson's edge is provenance: estate-grown Geisha from 1,350-1,700 masl beside a 200-hectare quetzal reserve, fully vertically integrated (grow to green to roast). That premium only converts to price if it can be proven. The lot dossier / digital passport turns Janson's real story — plot altitude, shade %, varietal, cupping score, washed/natural/anaerobic method, harvest window, even quetzal-habitat shade-grown / biodiversity evidence — into a buyer-facing artifact that justifies specialty pricing. The compliance vault directly serves the certifications Panama specialty estates actually carry (organic, Rainforest Alliance, Fair Trade) plus the new EUDR deforestation due-diligence that EU buyers now demand — and ties each document to the exact plot polygon and traceability lot code (JC-xxx) that already anchor the existing harvests/processing model. Cupping sheets flow in from Cropster, the de-facto specialty-coffee QC system, rather than being re-keyed.


#### Workflow & automation engine — rules, triggers, scheduled jobs
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm manager / operations lead (e.g. Janette the agronomist + Miguel the field supervisor at Janson) who today carries the whole operation in their head and a WhatsApp group — deciding every day who picks where, which drying beds get turned, when to spray for broca, and chasing overdue tasks by walking the rows. Secondary beneficiaries: the owner (wants the operation to keep running without them), mill operators (Nestor/Yarisel), and the admin/bookkeeper who reacts to events after the fact. The automation engine serves the manager first, but its triggers fire on behalf of everyone.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a processing batch's moisture drops to the 10–12% target on the patio, I want the system to automatically flag the batch ready-to-bag, alert the mill operator, and open a 'rest in parchment' task — so I can lock in cup quality without a person hovering over a moisture meter every hour.
- When the 5-day forecast shows rain in the next 48h during peak harvest, I want the engine to auto-reprioritize the day plan (pull pickers to the ripest at-risk plots, halt natural/honey drying, cover the beds) and push the new plan to crew leads — so a weather event doesn't rot a week of cherry.
- When broca (berry borer) trap counts or a scouting task crosses a threshold on any plot, I want a spray/sanitation task auto-created, assigned to the agronomist, with a re-entry-interval lockout on picking that plot — so pest pressure is caught on a rule, not on whoever happened to notice.
- When a task goes overdue, a worker is marked absent on a harvest day, or a plot flips to 'at-risk', I want the right person notified on the right channel within minutes and an escalation if it's still untouched by tomorrow — so things stop slipping through the cracks while I'm out of signal in the field.
- When the season ends, I want recurring agronomic calendars (post-bloom foliar feed, shade pruning, soil sampling, frost watch) to generate themselves each cycle from templates tied to each plot's variety and altitude — so institutional knowledge runs on autopilot instead of living in my memory.

**Features:**
- **Rule builder (no-code WHEN/IF/THEN)** — A visual trigger→condition→action editor over the unified event bus (harvest logged, batch stage/moisture change, plot status flip, task overdue, attendance, weather threshold, sensor reading). Lets the manager encode farm logic once and have it fire forever — the core differentiator vs a static task list.
- **Scheduled & recurring jobs (agronomic calendar engine)** — Cron-style and relative/seasonal recurrences (e.g. 'foliar feed 14 days after first bloom on each Geisha plot', 'turn drying beds every 2h during daylight', 'soil pH sample every March'). Materializes tasks automatically from per-plot, per-variety templates so the annual coffee calendar self-populates.
- **Event bus + trigger log (the nervous system)** — Every domain mutation emits a typed event; the engine subscribes. A durable, replayable event log + per-rule execution history (fired / skipped / why) gives an audit trail and makes automations debuggable instead of magic — table stakes for trust.
- **Multi-channel notifications & escalation ladder** — Actions route to WhatsApp (Cloud API), SMS, email, and in-app — with quiet hours, acknowledgement tracking, and time-based escalation (unacked → supervisor → owner). Turns 'someone should have told me' into a guaranteed delivery, which is what actually changes behavior on a rural farm.
- **Action library (typed effects)** — Reusable actions the THEN clause can call: create/assign task, reprioritize day plan, advance batch stage, set plot status, generate purchase/reorder, notify, write activity-log entry, hit a webhook. A growing catalog means non-technical staff compose new automations from safe building blocks.
- **Approval gates & safe-mode (human-in-the-loop)** — Any rule touching money, compliance lockouts (pesticide re-entry interval), or irreversible state can require one-tap approval before firing; a global dry-run/simulation mode previews 'what would have fired over the last 30 days'. Lets the farm automate aggressively without fear of a runaway rule emptying a plot or skipping a safety interval.
- **Weather- & sensor-reactive automations** — First-class triggers on forecast deltas (frost, rain probability, GDD accumulation) and on IoT readings (soil moisture, patio humidity), so the highest-value, most time-sensitive coffee decisions (frost watch at 1,350–1,700 masl, drying-curve protection) run in real time.
- **Automation marketplace / template gallery** — Shareable, installable recipe packs ('Volcán frost-watch', 'broca IPM ladder', 'washed-process drying SOP') pre-built for specialty coffee — a new estate gets world-class agronomic SOPs as automations on day one instead of authoring from scratch.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** WhatsApp Business Cloud API (Meta) — the dominant farm-crew channel in Panama, for notifications/acknowledgements, Twilio (SMS + Programmable Voice) and SendGrid/Resend (email) for fallback notification rails, Supabase: pg_cron (scheduled jobs), Database Webhooks + Realtime (event bus), Edge Functions (action runners), pg_net for outbound webhooks — all on the free tier per the $0 constraint, Inngest or Trigger.dev — durable workflow/step orchestration with retries, delays and human-in-the-loop steps (free tiers) for anything beyond pg_cron, Weather/climate APIs as trigger sources: Open-Meteo (free), Tomorrow.io, OpenWeather — frost, rain-probability, GDD, Satellite/NDVI providers as trigger sources: Sentinel Hub / Sentinel-2, Planet — plot-health deltas that fire scouting rules, Cropster & Cropster Origin — push processing/cupping events in, pull QC thresholds out so 'cup score < X' or 'moisture target hit' can drive automations, IoT/LoRaWAN ingestion: The Things Network + soil-moisture/temperature sensors (e.g. SenseCAP, Davis weather stations) via MQTT webhook as real-time triggers, Zapier / Make / n8n and a generic outbound + inbound webhook (HMAC-signed) so the engine interoperates with any external tool, Slack/Telegram for office-side notifications; Google Calendar (CalDAV) sync for scheduled agronomic tasks, Standards: RFC 5545 (iCalendar RRULE) for recurrences and CRON expressions for schedules; CloudEvents 1.0 for the event envelope

**Data model additions:** automation_rules — id, name, owner, trigger_type, condition (JSON expression), is_active, requires_approval, safe_mode, created_by, rule_actions — id, rule_id, action_type (enum: create_task, notify, advance_stage, set_plot_status, reprioritize_plan, webhook, ...), action_config (JSON), order, scheduled_jobs — id, name, schedule (cron / iCal RRULE), relative_anchor (e.g. plot.bloom_date + N days), target_scope (plot/variety/farm), template_ref, next_run_at, last_run_at, automation_events (event log) — id, event_type, entity_type, entity_id, payload (CloudEvents JSON), occurred_at, source (user/sensor/weather/system), rule_executions (run history) — id, rule_id, triggering_event_id, status (fired/skipped/failed), reason, started_at, finished_at, retries, output, notifications — id, execution_id, channel, recipient, body, status (queued/sent/delivered/ack/escalated), acknowledged_at, escalation_policies — id, name, ladder (ordered steps: role + delay), quiet_hours, approval_requests — id, execution_id, action_id, requested_at, decided_by, decision, decided_at, task_templates — id, title, category, default_assignee_role, default_priority, recurrence_rule, variety_filter, altitude_band (powers the agronomic-calendar engine), webhooks — id, direction (in/out), url, secret_hmac, event_filter, last_status, sensor_readings — id, sensor_id, plot_id/patio, metric, value, read_at (trigger source for moisture/soil/weather automations), automation_recipes — id, slug, name, description, manifest (rules+jobs+templates bundle), installed_at (the marketplace/template gallery)

**AI / automation:**
- Natural-language rule authoring: 'tell me what should happen' → the engine drafts a WHEN/IF/THEN rule from plain Spanish/English (Claude tool-calling against the typed action library), with a preview before activation
- Anomaly-detection triggers: ML on the event stream flags abnormal drying curves, yield drops, or attendance/yield mismatches and auto-opens an investigation task instead of waiting for a hand-coded threshold
- Predictive scheduling: forecast harvest peaks from bloom dates + GDD + historical yield and auto-staff/auto-sequence picking across plots to smooth labor and beat the rain
- AI escalation summarizer: when an automation escalates, generate a one-line WhatsApp summary ('Batch JC-564 stalled at 18% moisture for 9h on patio 3 — likely overcast; turn + tarp') so the human gets context, not just an alert
- Rule-conflict & dead-rule detection: AI reviews the rule set for contradictions, never-firing conditions, and infinite-loop risk before activation, and suggests consolidations
- Recipe recommendation: based on this estate's varieties/altitude/methods, suggest SOP automation packs to install ('your washed Geisha lots have no drying-turn schedule — add one?')

**World-class:** A farm where the operation runs itself between decisions: the manager writes intent once ('protect drying quality', 'catch broca early', 'never miss the post-bloom feed') and the engine executes it across thousands of events without supervision — reliably, observably, and reversibly. World-class means (1) a no-code rule builder a non-technical agronomist actually uses, (2) a durable, exactly-once event/job runtime with full execution history, retries, dead-letter handling and replay, (3) dry-run simulation before any rule goes live, (4) guaranteed multi-channel delivery with acknowledgement + escalation, (5) human-in-the-loop gates on irreversible/financial/compliance actions, and (6) an installable library of specialty-coffee SOP recipes. The bar is Inngest/Temporal-grade durability married to a Zapier-grade authoring UX, tuned for agriculture. The killer property: the system surfaces 'here's what I did and why' so trust compounds and the owner can step away.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Specialty coffee at Janson is won or lost on time-critical, knowledge-heavy moments that today live in one person's head: the drying curve (turning beds, hitting 10–12% moisture without over- or under-drying a Geisha lot worth 10x a commodity bean), frost watch at 1,350–1,700 masl, broca IPM timing, and the post-bloom→harvest agronomic calendar that differs by variety (Geisha vs Caturra/Catuaí/Pacamara) and microclimate. The automation engine is exactly the layer that encodes this estate-specific tacit knowledge as rules and schedules — protecting cup score and reserve/Geisha lot value, enforcing pesticide re-entry intervals for organic/certification compliance, and keeping the 200-ha quetzal reserve's shade-management cadence on track. It is the connective tissue that lets 'grow → harvest → process → sell' react to itself: a harvest event flows into a processing rule flows into a 'ready to cup/bag' alert flows into a green-coffee availability update for buyers.


#### Audit log & data governance
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm owner/manager (Janson family principal, e.g. Miguel/Janette Janson) acting as data steward and the person who signs certification and export attestations. Secondary daily users: agronomists, mill operators, and crew supervisors who generate the records; plus an external auditor (certification body, EU importer, regulator) who must trust the trail. A system administrator owns retention, access, and integrity policy.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a record I depend on changes (a harvest weight, a lot's process method, a moisture reading, a payroll rate, a green-lot price), I want to see exactly who changed it, from what to what, when, and from where, so I can trust the number, catch a fat-finger or fraud, and reconstruct the truth months later for an export or a buyer dispute.
- When an EU importer or a certification auditor (Rainforest Alliance, USDA/organic, Fair Trade, the new EUDR deforestation file) asks me to prove a green lot's chain of custody and that records were not backdated, I want a tamper-evident, time-anchored, exportable audit trail per lot, so I can pass the audit in a day instead of rebuilding spreadsheets for a week.
- When I onboard a seasonal supervisor or grant a buyer read-only portal access, I want least-privilege, time-boxed roles plus a log of every login, export, and data view they made, so a temporary worker cannot quietly alter pickers' kilos or leak pricing, and I can revoke and review cleanly at offboarding.
- When I correct a wrong entry (a misweighed cherry delivery, a duplicated lot), I want the correction to keep the original visible as a superseded version with a reason code, so labor and pay disputes over kilos-picked are settled by an evidence trail rather than memory.

**Features:**
- **Append-only audit ledger (every write path)** — A Postgres AFTER INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE trigger on every operational table writes an immutable row to a single audit_log table: actor, action, table, row id, before/after JSONB diff, timestamp, and request context (IP, role, app version). Gives a complete, queryable who-changed-what history with zero developer effort per new feature.
- **Hash-chained tamper-evidence + external anchoring** — Each audit row stores a SHA-256 of (its payload + prior row hash), forming a chain; a daily root hash is anchored to a free external timestamp (OpenTimestamps / RFC-3161 TSA). Any silent edit or deletion breaks the chain and is detectable, turning the log into auditor- and court-grade evidence.
- **Per-record version history + reason-coded corrections** — Every record surfaces a History drawer showing each version (before/after, who, when, reason). Corrections never overwrite: the prior value is kept as superseded. Settles labor/pay disputes and weight discrepancies with an evidence trail instead of memory.
- **Role-based access control with least-privilege presets** — Replaces the current single anon-read model with named roles (owner, agronomist, mill operator, supervisor, buyer-portal, auditor-readonly) backed by Postgres RLS + Supabase Auth, with time-boxed/seasonal grants and one-click revoke. Lets the farm safely open write access and a buyer portal without exposing pricing or payroll.
- **Access & export log** — Logs not just writes but reads that matter: logins, lot-traceability exports, payroll views, pricing views, and bulk downloads. Detects leaks and proves to buyers/regulators exactly who saw a confidential lot or price sheet.
- **Live data lineage on the activity feed** — Upgrades today's hand-authored activity feed into a derived view of the audit ledger, so 'Lot JC-552 moved to drying' is a real event linked to the underlying mutation, actor, and source record — zero drift between the narrative and the data.
- **Retention, legal-hold & right-to-be-forgotten governance** — Per-entity retention policies, legal holds that freeze deletion, and a worker-PII redaction path that tombstones personal data while preserving the financial/traceability skeleton. Keeps the farm compliant with Panama's Law 81 data-protection regime and EU partners' GDPR expectations.
- **Signed, tamper-evident lot traceability dossier (export)** — One click compiles a signed PDF/JSON dossier for any green lot: full chain from plot+harvest to ferment to dry to mill to green to shipment, every actor and edit, with the hash-chain proof attached. The single artifact an importer or certifier asks for, generated on demand.
- **Integrity monitor & anomaly alerts** — A scheduled check re-verifies the hash chain and flags off-hours edits, impossible weight changes (output > input), backdated rows, and disabled triggers, alerting the owner. A dead guard is itself an incident — the system proves it is still watching.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Supabase Auth + Postgres Row Level Security (named roles, sessions, RLS policies), Supabase Realtime / Postgres logical decoding (CDC) to stream changes into the ledger and the live activity feed, OpenTimestamps (Bitcoin-anchored, free) and/or an RFC-3161 Time-Stamping Authority (e.g. FreeTSA) for tamper-proof time anchoring, Rainforest Alliance certification platform and Fair Trade / FLO-CERT record requirements, USDA Organic / NOP and EU Organic record-keeping standards, EUDR (EU Deforestation Regulation) due-diligence statement format + geolocated plot polygons (GeoJSON, TRACES NT), GS1 standards (GTIN/GLN) and EPCIS 2.0 event vocabulary for interoperable chain-of-custody events, Cropster / Cropster Origin (specialty lot and cupping data) as an upstream/downstream source to reconcile against, Panama Law 81 of 2019 (personal-data protection) and GDPR alignment for the buyer/worker PII redaction path, GPG / Ed25519 signing (libsodium or a free KMS) to cryptographically sign exported traceability dossiers, QR/lot-code linking on lots.code so a scanned bag resolves to its verified audit dossier

**Data model additions:** audit_log (id, occurred_at, actor_id, actor_role, action[INSERT|UPDATE|DELETE], target_table, target_id, before_jsonb, after_jsonb, request_ip, app_version, row_hash, prev_hash) — append-only, INSERT-only RLS, audit_anchor (id, anchored_at, period_root_hash, ots_proof/tsa_token, verified_at) — daily external time-anchor receipts, record_versions (entity_table, entity_id, version_no, snapshot_jsonb, reason_code, superseded_by, created_by, created_at) — reason-coded version history, roles & user_roles (role, user_id, granted_by, valid_from, valid_to) — time-boxed least-privilege grants, access_log (id, occurred_at, actor_id, kind[login|export|view_payroll|view_pricing|download], target, ip) — read/export auditing, retention_policy & legal_hold (entity_table, retention_days, hold_active, hold_reason, applied_by) — retention + legal-hold governance, data_subject_redaction (worker_id, redacted_at, redacted_by, tombstone_jsonb) — PII right-to-be-forgotten preserving the financial/traceability skeleton, lot_dossier (lot_code, generated_at, generated_by, signature, payload_uri) — signed chain-of-custody exports keyed to lots.code

**AI / automation:**
- Anomaly detection over the audit stream: flag off-hours/out-of-pattern edits, impossible weight transitions (processing output > input, harvest kg vs picker capacity), backdated timestamps, and disabled-trigger gaps — surfaced as owner alerts.
- Natural-language audit query ('show every change to lot JC-552 moisture readings and who made them last month') translated to a safe, read-only ledger query via a Claude tool-use agent scoped to the audit schema.
- Auto-drafted certification and EUDR due-diligence narratives generated from the verified chain-of-custody dossier, so the owner reviews and signs rather than writes from scratch.
- Plain-language change summaries: turn raw before/after JSONB diffs into human sentences for the History drawer and activity feed ('Rosa's kilos for lot JC-552 corrected 84 -> 81 by supervisor, reason: scale recalibration').
- Risk scoring on access patterns: score each session/export for leak or fraud risk (new device, bulk pricing download, dormant seasonal account reactivating) and prompt step-up review.
- Reconciliation agent that cross-checks the internal ledger against external sources (Cropster lot data, weighbridge tickets) and flags divergences for human resolution.

**World-class:** World-class is an invisible, zero-effort governance fabric: every write across the whole platform is automatically and immutably logged by a database trigger (no per-feature wiring), the log is hash-chained and externally time-anchored so tampering is provable, and any record's full version history is one click away in the UI. Access is least-privilege by default with time-boxed seasonal roles and a complete access/export log. For any green lot the owner produces a cryptographically signed chain-of-custody dossier on demand that satisfies EUDR, organic, Rainforest Alliance, and a skeptical importer in a single artifact. An integrity monitor continuously re-verifies the chain and alerts on anomalies, so the system proves not just what happened but that the guard itself is still alive. Crucially the audit layer is a true cross-cutting platform service: one canonical ledger serves traceability, certification, payroll-dispute resolution, and security alike.

**Coffee-specific angle:** For a Geisha-and-reserve specialty house like Janson, the audit trail IS the product's provenance. A washed Geisha micro-lot from a named plot in a 200-hectare quetzal reserve sells on a story of altitude, varietal, process, and origin integrity — buyers pay 10-50x commodity for verifiable single-lot traceability. The audit ledger turns that story into proof: an unbroken, tamper-evident chain from a specific Tizingal Alto plot polygon and picker, through anaerobic ferment on Patio 1, drying on Bed 7 at logged moisture, milling, and export shipment, every edit attributable. The same ledger makes Janson EUDR-ready (geolocated, deforestation-free plot evidence is now mandatory to sell green coffee into the EU) and certification-audit-ready in a day. It also resolves the most common on-farm friction: pickers paid by the kilo trust the weights when corrections are reason-coded and the original is never silently overwritten.


#### Localization & i18n — Spanish/English, metric units, Panama specifics
*Priority:* **table-stakes**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: Janette Janson (bilingual owner/agronomist) who configures the system, plus the Spanish-monolingual field workforce who must operate it daily — Néstor Gómez (mill operator reading processing screens), the cuadrilla supervisors logging picker weights, and Raúl the driver. Secondary: international green-coffee buyers and certification auditors who consume Janson-facing exports in English. The current app is 100% English, hardcoded en-US, USD-only, lang="en" — a structural mismatch: the people doing the work read the system in their second (or no) language.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I (mill operator Néstor) open the processing pipeline mid-shift, I want every stage label, moisture alert and batch instruction in Panamanian Spanish, so I can act on it without guessing or asking the owner to translate.
- When I (Janette, owner) onboard a new picking crew at the start of the Geisha harvest, I want to flip the whole interface to español and have weights in kg, temps in °C, and dates as dd/mm/aaaa by default, so the cuadrilla is productive on day one instead of fighting an English UI.
- When I (export coordinator) generate a lot sheet, GrainPro packing list, or certification report for a US/EU buyer, I want it rendered in English with the buyer's units and their currency, so the same lot data serves both my Spanish field team and my English-speaking customers from one source of truth.
- When I (supervisor) get a low-moisture or rain-risk push alert on my phone, I want it in Spanish with Panama-local time and metric units, so I read it correctly under field pressure without mental conversion.

**Features:**
- **Locale framework (next-intl) with es-PA default, en-US secondary** — Replaces every hardcoded en-US/lang=en string with a message catalog; Spanish becomes the default for the farm workforce, English a one-tap toggle for buyers/owner — the workforce can actually read the system they operate.
- **Externalized message catalogs (es-PA / en-US JSON) covering 100% of UI copy + domain enums** — Every label, nav item, status (healthy/watch/at-risk -> sano/vigilar/en riesgo), process method (Washed->Lavado, Honey->Miel/Honey), role and task category gets a translation key, so no English leaks into the field-facing screens and copy changes don't require code edits.
- **Unit & format engine (metric-first: kg, ha, masl, mm rain, °C, Brix; dd/mm/aaaa dates; es-PA number grouping)** — Centralizes the current kg()/usd()/shortDate() helpers into locale-aware Intl formatters so a buyer can see lb/USD while the field team sees kg/B/. — one number, two correct renderings — eliminating manual conversion errors on weights and prices.
- **Panama currency & money handling (Balboa B/. at 1:1 USD parity, dual-symbol display)** — Models that Panama's legal tender is the Balboa pegged 1:1 to the USD and uses USD banknotes; payroll/sales show B/. for local labor and USD for export contracts correctly, matching how money actually works in Volcán.
- **Per-user language preference + instant in-app toggle (persisted, no reload jank)** — Owner sees English dashboards, the same screen on the supervisor's phone is Spanish; preference persists per device so nobody re-toggles each login — respects the buttery-smooth, no-reflow quality bar.
- **Locale-aware document/export renderer (lot sheets, payroll slips, cert reports, ICO/SCA forms)** — Generates the outbound PDFs/CSVs in the *recipient's* locale (Spanish payroll slip for the worker, English lot card for the US importer) from one dataset, so traceability and labor docs are correct for whoever reads them.
- **Spanish-first push/SMS/WhatsApp notification templates with Panama timezone (America/Panama, no DST)** — Field alerts (rain risk, drying moisture threshold, attendance) reach supervisors in correct Spanish and correct local time, so an alert is acted on, not misread.
- **Pseudo-locale + missing-key CI guard (translation coverage gate)** — A test that fails when any UI string lacks an es-PA/en-US key catches English leaking into the Spanish build before it ships — making 'fully bilingual' an enforced promise, not an honor system (mirrors the global promise->enforcement rule).

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** next-intl (App Router-native i18n routing, server-component message loading) — preferred over react-i18next for the RSC architecture here, ICU MessageFormat (pluralization/gender/select; Panamanian Spanish plural + number rules), Intl.NumberFormat / Intl.DateTimeFormat / Intl.RelativeTimeFormat (browser-native, $0 — no library needed for number/date/currency once locale wired), CLDR locale data (es-PA, en-US: decimal separators, currency symbol placement, first-day-of-week), IANA time zone database — America/Panama (UTC-5, no daylight saving), critical for harvest/attendance timestamps, ISO 4217 currency codes (PAB Balboa, USD) and the de-facto B/. 1:1 USD peg, ISO/IEC metric units (SI) as the canonical store unit, with imperial as a presentation-only conversion for export docs, Crowdin or Tolgee (free OSS tier) for managing the es-PA/en-US catalogs collaboratively — Janette edits Spanish copy without touching code, WhatsApp Business / Twilio template messaging with per-locale message templates (Spanish is the dominant channel for Panama field staff), Unicode BCP-47 language tags (es-PA, en-US, with future es-419 Latin-American Spanish fallback)

**Data model additions:** locale / language enum (es-PA default, en-US; future es-419, gun = Ngäbere as a stretch for indigenous Ngäbe-Buglé pickers), user_preference (preferred_locale, unit_system metric|imperial, currency_display PAB|USD, timezone, date_format) — keyed per user/device, translation_message (namespace, key, locale, value, status: machine|human|reviewed) — the catalog, if managed in-DB rather than JSON files, domain_enum_label (entity, enum_value, locale, label) — externalizes PlotStatus/ProcessMethod/WorkerRole/TaskCategory translations so enums render localized everywhere, tenant_locale_config (default_locale, supported_locales, default_unit_system, base_currency, timezone) — farm-level defaults, money_amount value object (amount, currency: PAB|USD) and measure value object (value, unit) — typed units so a kg can never be silently treated as a lb

**AI / automation:**
- LLM-assisted draft translation (Claude) of new es-PA strings with a human-review status flag — Janette approves rather than authors from scratch; never auto-ship machine copy unreviewed
- Coffee-domain glossary enforcement so terms of art stay correct in Spanish (e.g. 'parchment'->pergamino, 'cherry'->cereza, 'washed'->lavado, Geisha stays Geisha) — the AI respects a locked glossary, not literal translation
- Voice/STT field capture in Panamanian Spanish (and basic Ngäbere) so a picker or mill operator logs weights/observations by speaking — lowers the literacy/typing barrier in the field
- Locale-detection + smart default (geo + Accept-Language) that lands Panama field users in es-PA and overseas buyers in en-US automatically, with manual override
- Auto-localized outbound buyer comms: AI drafts the English lot narrative/cupping note from the Spanish field log so the export desk doesn't hand-translate every lot story

**World-class:** World-class means the system is genuinely bilingual end-to-end with zero English leakage on field screens: es-PA is the unmarked default, en-US a frictionless toggle, and a CI coverage gate guarantees no untranslated key ever ships. Units are SI-canonical in storage (kg, ha, masl, °C, mm, Brix) and rendered per-user, so the same lot reads kg/B/. for the cuadrilla and lb/USD for a US importer with no manual conversion and no rounding drift. Time is anchored to America/Panama (no DST traps), currency models the Balboa/USD 1:1 reality, and every outbound document/notification renders in the recipient's locale from one dataset. Copy lives in catalogs editable by the owner via a free TMS, not buried in JSX. It is invisible when right and impossible to get subtly wrong — the bar the rest of an agribusiness platform's correctness depends on, since a mislabeled weight or currency anywhere downstream corrupts traceability, payroll, and buyer trust.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson sits in Volcán, Chiriquí — a Panama specialty origin whose own brand is bilingual ('from our farm to your cup since 1990'). The field workforce is Spanish-monolingual (and a slice of the regional picking labor is indigenous Ngäbe-Buglé, Ngäbere-speaking), while ownership and the Panama-Geisha export market (Best of Panama auctions, US/EU/Asia importers) operate in English. Localization is therefore the seam between Janson's two worlds: the operational system must be Spanish-first for the people who grow, pick, ferment and dry, yet produce English, buyer-locale lot cards, cupping notes and certification paperwork for a green-coffee market where a single Geisha lot can sell for hundreds of dollars per pound. Panama specifics matter concretely: the Balboa pegged 1:1 to the USD (so USD bills circulate but local accounting reads B/.), America/Panama with no daylight saving (harvest/attendance timestamps never shift), and metric units throughout — masl altitude bands (1,350–1,700) are a core quality-and-marketing fact that must never be mangled into feet.


#### Security, privacy & backup/DR
*Priority:* **table-stakes**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm owner/general manager (Janson family principal) who is legally and financially accountable for the business — its bank rails, buyer contracts, worker PII, and certification records. Secondary daily users: the office/finance administrator who manages logins, payments and exports; and the IT-literate family member or part-time contractor who acts as de-facto sysadmin. The system must assume NO full-time security staff and intermittent rural connectivity (Volcán), so security has to be largely automatic and self-defending rather than expert-operated.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When the farm moves from a public read-only dashboard to a system that records payroll, buyer contracts, green-coffee invoices and bank details, I want every sensitive record locked behind authenticated, role-scoped access with a tamper-evident audit trail, so I can prove only authorized people touched money and worker data and defend that to a buyer, an auditor, or Panamanian labor authorities.
- When my laptop is stolen, ransomware hits, or someone fat-fingers a DELETE during peak harvest, I want point-in-time restore to any minute in the last 30 days plus an off-site immutable copy, so I never lose a season of traceability, payroll, and lot data that can't be re-created.
- When a specialty buyer, a SCA/Rainforest Alliance auditor, or an EUDR due-diligence check asks how I protect worker personal data and farm geolocation, I want a ready data-protection posture (encryption, access logs, retention, consent, breach plan, data-residency) I can hand over, so I close high-value reserve-lot and EU export deals instead of being disqualified on a security questionnaire.
- When a picker, agronomist, or seasonal crew lead logs in from a shared phone on patchy mountain connectivity, I want passwordless/MFA login, least-privilege roles, and an instant remote revoke when someone leaves, so a lost device or an ex-worker can never reach payroll, financials, or the export buyer list.

**Features:**
- **Identity, SSO & MFA (Supabase Auth + WebAuthn passkeys)** — Replaces the current no-auth public app with authenticated logins; passkeys/magic-link suit low-literacy field workers and survive shared devices, MFA protects money/PII accounts — the precondition for any write path existing at all.
- **Role-based + row-level access control (RBAC over Postgres RLS)** — Least-privilege roles (owner, finance-admin, agronomist, crew-lead, picker, read-only buyer/auditor guest) enforced in the database, not the UI, so a picker physically cannot read payroll or the buyer list even via the raw API — closes the biggest risk of adding write paths.
- **Tamper-evident audit log of every sensitive read/write** — Append-only, hash-chained ledger of who-did-what-when on payroll, payments, contracts, lot edits and exports; this is the evidence an auditor, buyer, or labor inspector wants and the forensic trail after any incident.
- **Automated backups + 30-day point-in-time restore + off-site immutable copy** — Daily encrypted logical dumps to a separate cloud bucket with object-lock/immutability plus PITR, so ransomware, an accidental TRUNCATE, or a provider outage never costs a season of irreplaceable traceability and payroll data.
- **Field-data conflict-safe sync & device security** — Offline-first encrypted local cache for harvest/processing entry on the mountain with deterministic conflict resolution on reconnect, plus remote wipe/session-revoke for lost or ex-worker devices — keeps field capture trustworthy and revocable.
- **Secrets, key & env management with rotation** — Central vault for DB service-role keys, payment-rail credentials, weather/satellite API keys and webhook signing secrets, with scheduled rotation and no secret ever in the client bundle — prevents the classic leaked-key breach as integrations multiply.
- **Privacy & data-governance console (consent, retention, DSAR, residency)** — Tracks worker-PII consent, data-retention/auto-purge schedules, subject-access/export-or-delete handling, and tagging of EU-export data — turns Panamanian Law 81 / GDPR-for-EU-buyers compliance into a switch-on posture rather than a scramble.
- **DR runbook + restore drills + status/uptime monitoring** — A documented, tested recovery plan with defined RPO/RTO, scheduled quarterly restore drills that actually verify backups, and uptime/security monitoring — so 'we have backups' is proven, not assumed, and the free-tier auto-pause/outage never silently loses data.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Supabase Auth (email magic-link, OAuth) + WebAuthn/passkeys for MFA, Postgres Row-Level Security as the enforcement layer (Supabase free tier today; PITR/daily-backup tier when money/PII land), Cloudflare (Turnstile bot/abuse protection, WAF, Zero Trust Access for admin routes) — free tier, Off-site immutable backups: Cloudflare R2 or Backblaze B2 with Object Lock / S3 versioning + lifecycle (free/near-free egress), 1Password / Doppler / Infisical (or Supabase Vault) for secrets storage + scheduled rotation, pg_dump + cron (Supabase scheduled function or a free GitHub-free scheduler like a Vercel cron) for logical backups, gzip + age/GPG client-side encryption, Sentry (free tier) for error monitoring + audit-relevant alerting; BetterStack/UptimeRobot free tier for uptime + auto-pause keep-alive, Resend / email for breach-notification and security alerts, Standards/frameworks to map to: Panama Law 81 of 2019 (personal data protection), GDPR (for EU buyers + EUDR geolocation data), SOC 2 / ISO 27001 control vocabulary for buyer security questionnaires, OWASP ASVS for the app, NIST 800-34 for the DR/contingency plan, Stripe / Wise / local Panamanian bank rails — referenced only as the protected data sources whose credentials and PCI scope this pillar must isolate (handled by the payments pillar, secured here)

**Data model additions:** app_user — identity, linked to a worker/person, auth provider, MFA/passkey status, last_login, status (active/suspended), role + user_role — named least-privilege roles (owner, finance_admin, agronomist, crew_lead, picker, buyer_guest, auditor_guest) and their assignments, driving RLS, permission_grant — fine-grained capability grants (read/write/export per resource: payroll, payments, contracts, lots, plots-geo), audit_log — append-only, hash-chained: actor, action, resource type+id, before/after diff hash, ip/device, timestamp, data_export_log — every CSV/PDF/API bulk export of sensitive data, for DSAR + anomaly detection, backup_run — each backup: timestamp, scope, size, target bucket, checksum, encryption key id, success/fail, verified_at, restore_drill — recorded restore tests: date, RPO/RTO achieved, verifier, outcome (proves backups actually work), consent_record — worker-PII consent: subject, purpose, granted/withdrawn timestamps (Law 81 / GDPR), retention_policy — per-data-class retention period + auto-purge schedule + legal-hold flag, data_subject_request — DSAR/erasure requests: subject, type, status, fulfilled_at, exported_artifact ref, secret_inventory — non-secret metadata for managed credentials: name, owner, last_rotated, next_rotation_due, scope (no values stored), device_session — active sessions/devices: user, device fingerprint, last_seen, trusted flag, revoked_at (for remote revoke/wipe), security_event — anomaly/alert stream: type (new-country login, bulk export, failed-backup, auto-pause-warning), severity, acknowledged_by

**AI / automation:**
- Anomaly detection on the audit + export logs — flag a picker account suddenly reading payroll, an off-hours bulk export of the buyer list, or impossible-travel logins, and alert the owner in plain language.
- Backup-and-restore health agent — verifies each backup's checksum and restorability, auto-runs a sandbox restore drill, and pages the owner only when a backup is actually unrecoverable (not just 'job ran').
- Natural-language audit/compliance Q&A — 'who edited the JC-564 lot record and when?' or 'list everyone with access to payroll' answered over the RLS-scoped audit log, so the non-technical owner can self-serve forensics and auditor responses.
- Auto-generated security posture & questionnaire responder — drafts the buyer/auditor security-questionnaire answers and a one-page data-protection statement from the live control state (RLS roles, backup config, retention policies), keeping it always current.
- PII auto-classification — scans new free-text/columns for personal data (names, phone, ID numbers) and suggests the right role-scope, retention, and consent tagging before it leaks into an unprotected field.
- Phishing/social-engineering coaching — context-aware nudges for finance-admin actions (e.g., a payment-detail change request) since rural small-business owners are prime BEC targets as money rails come online.

**World-class:** "World-class is security that is invisible until needed and provable on demand. Identity is passwordless (passkeys) with adaptive MFA only when risk warrants; authorization is enforced in the database via RLS so the UI is never the only gate; every sensitive action writes to an append-only, hash-chained audit log the owner can search in plain language ('show me everyone who saw payroll last week'). Backups are continuous (PITR to the minute) with an off-site, object-locked, immutable copy that ransomware cannot touch, and restores are drilled quarterly with a one-command, evidence-producing runbook — RPO measured in minutes, RTO in under an hour. Secrets live in a vault, rotate automatically, and never reach the browser. Privacy is built-in: worker-PII consent and retention are tracked, data is auto-purged on schedule, EU-export and farm-geolocation data are tagged for residency, and a subject-access or delete request is one click. And critically for a small farm with no security team, the whole posture self-monitors and alerts: a failed backup, an anomalous bulk export, a login from a new country, or a free-tier project about to auto-pause all page the owner before they become an incident. The end state is that Janson can hand a buyer or auditor a one-page security posture and pass the questionnaire that wins reserve-lot and EU-export deals."

**Coffee-specific angle:** "For Janson specifically, the data being protected is uniquely high-stakes and irreplaceable: a Geisha/reserve lot's full traceability chain (plot → picker → fermentation → drying → green) is the provenance that justifies premium auction and direct-trade pricing — if that chain is lost or tampered with, the premium evaporates, so its integrity and immutable off-site backup is a revenue asset, not IT overhead. Two coffee-specific compliance pressures make this a deal-gate: (1) the EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) requires exporters to submit precise plot geolocation polygons with due diligence — that geodata, plus the 200-hectare quetzal reserve boundaries, is sensitive and must be handled to GDPR standards for EU buyers; (2) certification audits (Rainforest Alliance, organic, SCA) and high-end direct buyers increasingly send data-protection questionnaires, where a ready posture closes deals and its absence disqualifies. Operationally it's a rural, seasonal, shared-device, intermittent-connectivity farm in Volcán with no security staff and a hard $0/free-tier constraint today — so the design must be offline-tolerant, self-defending, and stage the spend: free-tier RLS + off-site free-bucket immutable backups now, upgrading to PITR/paid backups only at the moment payroll, payments, and buyer contracts actually enter the system."


#### Maps/GIS engine & field visualization
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **9/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm agronomist / production manager (Janson's "jefe de campo") who walks the blocks daily and needs to see the whole estate spatially. Secondary: the owner/GM (estate-wide planning, reserve stewardship, certification audits) and the field-crew supervisor (assigning pickers to mapped sub-blocks). Tertiary: the GIS/data steward who digitizes boundaries and maintains layer accuracy.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I take over field planning each morning, I want to open one estate map that shows every plot boundary colored by live status (healthy / watch / at-risk), overdue tasks, and active crews, so I can decide where to send people before I leave the office instead of driving block to block to find out.
- When a plot starts underperforming or a leaf-rust outbreak is suspected, I want to drop a geotagged scouting pin from my phone (with a photo and severity) and see it land on the exact spot within the plot polygon, so I can track the spread spatially and trigger a targeted treatment task on just the affected sub-block — not spray the whole farm.
- When I review an elevation-sensitive variety like Geisha, I want to overlay plot boundaries on a terrain/slope and elevation-band layer, so I can confirm which sub-blocks actually sit in the 1,650–1,700 masl sweet spot and plan replanting where altitude, slope and shade truly favor that varietal.
- When an SCA / Rainforest Alliance / organic auditor visits, I want to export a precise map of every plot's area, the 200-hectare quetzal reserve boundary, and the buffer zones between production and conservation land, so I can prove acreage, traceability geometry, and that we are not encroaching on protected habitat.

**Features:**
- **Estate map canvas (MapLibre GL + satellite/terrain basemap)** — One interactive, buttery-smooth WebGL map of the whole Volcán estate with every plot rendered as a real polygon — replaces the abstract plot cards with a true spatial home screen that loads instantly and feels native.
- **Plot boundary editor & digitizer (draw / edit / split / merge polygons; import GeoJSON/KML/Shapefile)** — Turns each abstract plot record into a surveyed boundary with computed area_ha, perimeter, centroid and elevation band — so acreage is measured, not guessed, and sub-blocks can be split out as the farm subdivides.
- **Status & data choropleth layers (color plots by status, harvest %, yield/ha, variety, last-inspected age)** — Lets the agronomist read the health and progress of the entire farm in one glance; the same geometry powers every pillar's spatial view without re-drawing maps.
- **Drop-a-pin field scouting (mobile geolocation + photo + severity, snapped into the containing plot)** — Captures pests, disease, frost damage, broken irrigation and tree gaps at exact coordinates, building a spatial incident history and auto-spawning a task on the right sub-block.
- **Layer manager + terrain/slope/elevation-band overlays + microclimate zones** — Stacks NDVI, elevation, slope, aspect, soil and weather-station layers over the boundaries so altitude-/shade-sensitive variety decisions (especially Geisha) are made on real terrain, not a table column.
- **GPS crew & picker tracks + day-of-harvest heatmap** — Shows where crews actually worked and where cherries were picked, turning the workers/harvests data into a spatial yield map that exposes under-picked rows and verifies coverage.
- **Measure & area tools + map export (PDF/GeoJSON/print) for audits and planning** — Instant distance/area measurement and a one-click audit-grade map export of plots, reserve and buffers for certifiers, banks (collateral), and replanting plans.
- **Reserve & conservation layer (200-ha quetzal reserve, buffers, riparian zones, shade-tree canopy)** — Makes the private nature reserve a first-class spatial entity so the team can enforce no-encroachment buffers and tell the conservation story certifiers and buyers love.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** MapLibre GL JS (open-source WebGL map renderer — $0, no Mapbox token lock-in), Esri World Imagery / Sentinel-2 cloudless satellite basemap tiles (free tiers); OpenStreetMap & OpenTopoMap for street/terrain, Sentinel Hub / Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem and Planet (Education/NICFI) for satellite imagery + NDVI tiles over the estate, Mapbox/MapTiler Terrain-RGB or AWS Terrain Tiles + Copernicus DEM / NASA SRTM for elevation, slope and aspect (3D hillshade), PostGIS on Supabase (free tier) — geometry(Polygon/Point,4326) columns, ST_Area/ST_Contains/ST_Intersects spatial joins, Browser Geolocation API + Capacitor Geolocation for high-accuracy mobile field pin-dropping; optional NTRIP/RTK GNSS for survey-grade boundary capture, Turf.js for client-side geospatial math (area, centroid, buffer, point-in-polygon) with no server round-trip, @mapbox/mapbox-gl-draw / Terra Draw for polygon digitizing; tokml / @tmcw/togeojson + shapefile.js for KML/Shapefile import-export, OGC standards: GeoJSON (RFC 7946), KML, WMTS/XYZ tiles, GeoPackage — the interchange formats certifiers and ag-tech tools (Cropster, FarmOS) expect, Open-Meteo / weather-station geolocation to bind microclimate data to plot centroids (free, no key)

**Data model additions:** geo_boundaries (id, plot_id FK, geometry geometry(Polygon,4326), computed area_ha, perimeter_m, centroid, elevation_band, source survey|drawn|imported, captured_at) — adds the missing spatial geometry plots currently lack, map_layers (id, name, type raster|vector|tile, source_url, opacity, z_index, category ndvi|terrain|soil|weather|conservation, visible_by_default, season_date for time-slider), scouting_pins (id, plot_id FK, geometry geometry(Point,4326), category pest|disease|frost|irrigation|tree-gap, severity, photo_url, note, reported_by FK workers, reported_at, linked_task_id), gps_tracks (id, worker_id FK, crew, geometry geometry(LineString,4326), recorded_date, distance_m) — picker/crew movement for coverage + harvest heatmap, sub_blocks (id, plot_id FK, geometry geometry(Polygon,4326), label, variety, tree_count, planting_year) — split a plot polygon into managed rows/sections, conservation_zones (id, name, kind reserve|buffer|riparian|shade-canopy, geometry geometry(Polygon,4326), protected, hectares) — the 200-ha quetzal reserve + buffers as first-class geometry, map_exports (id, title, bbox, layer_set jsonb, format pdf|geojson|kml, generated_at, generated_by) — audit/replanting map artifacts, elevation_samples / terrain_cache (plot_id FK, min/max/mean masl, mean_slope_deg, dominant_aspect) — derived from DEM for varietal-placement queries

**AI / automation:**
- Auto-digitize plot boundaries from satellite imagery with a segmentation model (SAM-style) so the steward draws a rough outline and AI snaps it to the real canopy edge — cuts boundary capture from hours to minutes.
- NDVI anomaly detection per plot/sub-block over time: AI flags zones where vegetation vigor drops faster than the plot's own seasonal baseline and drops a suggested scouting pin before the eye catches it.
- Spatial yield forecasting: regress harvested_kg against each polygon's elevation band, slope, aspect, shade and historical NDVI to predict yield/ha per sub-block and recommend where to replant which variety (Geisha vs Caturra).
- Vision triage on scouting-pin photos — classify leaf rust / broca / nutrient deficiency and pre-fill severity, so a field photo becomes a typed, mapped, treatable incident automatically.
- Natural-language map querying ('show me the at-risk Geisha lots above 1,650 m that haven't been inspected in 10 days and highlight them') that compiles to a PostGIS spatial filter and zooms the map there.
- Optimal-route generation for crews/inspections across the drawn boundaries (slope-aware walking routes), and auto-clustering of nearby scouting pins into a single treatment task.

**World-class:** A world-class farm GIS feels like Google Earth fused with an ops dashboard: sub-second WebGL pan/zoom over a high-res satellite estate, every plot a crisp glass-edged polygon you can tap to fly into. Layers toggle instantly (NDVI, elevation, slope, status, crews, scouting) with smooth opacity blends; a time-slider scrubs NDVI and harvest progress week-by-week across the season. Boundaries are survey-accurate (drawn or RTK-captured), areas auto-compute, and every other module (a harvest, a task, a pest report, a processing lot) is one click from its geometry — spatial is the primary index of the whole system, not a decorative tab. It works offline-first in the field on a phone, snaps pins into the right polygon, and exports audit-grade maps. The map is the home screen, and it is honest: what you see is the live state of the farm.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson sits on the steep volcanic slopes of Volcán (1,350–1,700 masl) where 60-meter elevation differences and slope/aspect decide whether a Geisha lot earns a Panama-auction price or just a commodity score — a GIS that overlays elevation bands and microclimate on each boundary turns terrain into a varietal-placement and quality-prediction tool, not just a pretty map. The 200-hectare private quetzal reserve is a genuine spatial asset: mapping its boundary, the production/conservation buffers and the shade-tree canopy is exactly the bird-friendly, Rainforest-Alliance-shade story that commands premiums and that auditors want to verify cartographically. Because Janson runs the full chain (grow → wet/dry mill → green/roasted sale), plot geometry is the first link of geographic traceability: a buyer can trace a specific Geisha micro-lot back to a drawn sub-block at a named elevation on Barú's flank — provenance you can point to on a map, which is the premium-specialty selling proposition itself.


#### Hardware & device ecosystem — scales, moisture meters, drones, gateways
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Don Ardiles, the wet-mill / dry-mill operator and processing lead at Janson — plus the agronomist who walks the drying patios and the maintenance hand who keeps the gateways powered. He is the one who today writes moisture %, batch weights and bed positions on a clipboard and re-types them into the Farm Operations app at night. Secondary persona: the farm owner/QC lead who needs those readings to be trustworthy and traceable per lot.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a fresh lot comes off the depulper and onto the platform scale, I want the weight to land in the lot's record by itself, so I can keep both hands on the coffee and never transcribe a number wrong into the JC-### lot.
- When I walk a drying bed with the moisture meter, I want to tap the bean and have the % attach to that exact batch and bed automatically, so I pull the coffee at 10.5–11% target moisture instead of guessing or over-drying away the Geisha premium.
- When a sensor on a remote patio or the cherry-reception scale goes quiet, drops battery, or drifts out of calibration, I want the system to flag it before I trust a bad reading, so a dead gauge doesn't silently corrupt a season of traceability data.
- When the owner asks 'what is the real loss ratio cherry-to-green on this Pacamara lot,' I want every weigh-point (cherry in, parchment, milled, green out) captured from the scales automatically, so the mass-balance per lot is audited, not reconstructed from memory.

**Features:**
- **Device Registry & fleet console** — One screen listing every scale, moisture meter, drone, weather station and gateway with model, firmware, battery, last-seen, signal and assigned location — so the operator knows at a glance which instruments are alive and which need attention before harvest peak.
- **Scale auto-capture for weigh-points** — Platform/bench scales (cherry reception, parchment, milled, green) stream weights tagged to the active lot and pipeline stage, eliminating clipboard transcription and giving an audited cherry-to-green mass balance per lot.
- **Moisture-meter pairing & target alerts** — BLE moisture/density/temperature readings bind to a specific batch + drying bed; the app warns at over-dry (<10%) or wet-store risk (>12%) so coffee is pulled at the 10.5–11% specialty target and the Geisha/Pacamara premium isn't lost to over-drying or mold.
- **LoRaWAN patio & micro-climate mesh** — Long-range battery sensors on drying beds, fermentation tanks and reserve plots report temperature/humidity/soil-moisture every few minutes over a single farm gateway, turning manual rounds into continuous coverage across 1,350–1,700 masl terrain.
- **Drone flight & imagery intake** — NDVI / multispectral drone passes are catalogued per plot, linking flight date, sensor and orthomosaic to the plot record so canopy stress, gaps and quetzal-reserve boundaries are tracked over time rather than living on a pilot's SD card.
- **Calibration & health watchdog** — Tracks each device's calibration due-date, drift checks and offline thresholds, raising a task when a gauge is overdue or silent — so no batch is graded on an uncalibrated or dead instrument.
- **Offline-first edge buffer & sync** — Readings queue locally on the mill/field device and reconcile when connectivity returns (rural Volcán has patchy signal), guaranteeing no weigh-point or moisture reading is ever dropped.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** Sinar moisture meters — CP7070 (BLE + USB, Sinar MLog app data export) and AgriPro 6095 / BeanPro 6070 for green-coffee moisture/density/temperature, Generic BLE/SPP and RS-232/USB platform & bench scales (e.g. Ohaus, Mettler Toledo, A&D) via a serial-to-MQTT bridge for cherry-reception and parchment/green weigh-points, LoRaWAN ecosystem — Milesight UG65 gateway + EM500-SMTC soil/EC sensors, RAKwireless and Seeed SenseCAP field sensors, registered on The Things Network / The Things Stack device repository, MQTT / Sparkplug-B as the device telemetry backbone; Webhooks + REST for cloud-connected gauges, DJI Agras / Mavic 3M (multispectral) and Pix4D / DroneDeploy for orthomosaic + NDVI processing of drone passes, Davis Instruments / Ambient Weather on-farm stations and the Ecowitt / WeatherLink APIs for hyperlocal forecast and frost-risk, Cropster Origin / Cup as the downstream destination for verified moisture, density and lot weights so QC and roasting share one source of truth, GS1 / QR + RFID (Zebra printers, NFC tags) for lot-and-bag identity at every weigh-point

**Data model additions:** devices — id, type (scale | moisture_meter | drone | gateway | weather_station | soil_sensor), make, model, serial, firmware, connectivity (ble | lorawan | usb | wifi | cellular), assigned_location (plot_id | patio | mill_station), battery_pct, signal, status (online | offline | maintenance), last_seen, device_readings — id, device_id, taken_at, metric (weight_kg | moisture_pct | bean_density | temp_c | humidity_pct | soil_moisture | ndvi), value, unit, batch_id (nullable), plot_id (nullable), captured_by (worker_id), source (auto | manual_override), calibrations — id, device_id, performed_at, performed_by, reference_value, drift, next_due, passed (bool), gateways — id, name, location, protocol (lorawan | wifi), connected_device_count, uplink_status, last_uplink_at, drone_flights — id, plot_id, flown_at, device_id, sensor (rgb | multispectral), orthomosaic_url, ndvi_avg, notes, device_alerts — id, device_id, kind (offline | low_battery | calibration_due | reading_out_of_range), severity, raised_at, resolved_at, linked_task_id

**AI / automation:**
- Anomaly detection on sensor streams (Claude or a lightweight statistical baseline) that flags a moisture or weight reading inconsistent with the batch's stage/history — catching a fat-fingered manual entry or a drifting gauge before it pollutes the lot record.
- Predictive drying-curve estimation: from LoRaWAN bed temp/humidity plus periodic moisture spot-checks, forecast hours-to-target per bed so the operator schedules turning and pull-off optimally and protects delicate Geisha lots.
- Auto-reconciliation: AI ties an unlabeled scale weight to the most likely active lot/stage by time, location and expected mass-balance, with a confidence score the operator confirms.
- Predictive maintenance / calibration scheduling that learns each instrument's drift rate and battery decay to pre-empt failures before harvest peak.
- Natural-language device console — 'which beds are above 12% moisture and which gateways are offline' answered over live telemetry, with proactive push alerts.

**World-class:** World-class is an instrument layer the operator never has to think about: plug in a Sinar meter or step a crate onto the scale and the number is already in the right lot, stage and bed — captured, calibrated, timestamped and traceable, with zero transcription. Every weigh-point from cherry-reception to green is auto-logged, giving an audited mass balance per lot; every drying bed has a continuous moisture+climate curve, not a once-a-day guess. Devices self-report battery, signal, firmware and calibration status on one fleet console; a dead or drifting gauge raises a task before anyone trusts its reading (a dead guard is itself an incident). It is offline-first for rural Volcán, vendor-agnostic over MQTT/BLE/LoRaWAN so the farm is never locked to one brand, and it feeds verified data straight into Cropster and the traceability record. The hardware layer is what turns the rest of the system from typed-in claims into measured, defensible ground truth.

**Coffee-specific angle:** For specialty coffee the money is in the data the hardware produces. Janson's Geisha and Pacamara command premiums only if the lot story is verifiable — and the two numbers buyers and graders care most about, green-bean moisture (10.5–11% target) and the cherry-to-green yield ratio, are exactly the scale and moisture-meter readings this layer captures. Over-drying a Geisha by two points or storing parchment wet erases the premium; a Sinar BeanPro reading bound to the right micro-lot prevents that. The 200-hectare quetzal reserve and the 1,350–1,700 masl spread make LoRaWAN and drone NDVI genuinely valuable: long-range, low-power sensing covers terrain you cannot walk daily, and multispectral passes monitor canopy and reserve boundaries without disturbing habitat. Per-lot device provenance is also the raw material for certification and for the 'from our farm to your cup since 1990' traceability story.


#### Edge computing / on-farm gateway with offline sync
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the farm operations lead / IT-leaning agronomist at Janson who owns the question "does the data actually arrive and is it trustworthy." Secondary daily beneficiaries whose work the gateway silently enables: the mill operator running fermentation/drying sensor logging at the wet+dry mill; pickers and supervisors recording harvests on phones up in the high Geisha blocks (1,690 masl); and the field agronomist walking the 200-ha quetzal reserve edge where there is zero cell signal.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When a picking crew logs cherries by weight up in the 1,690 masl Geisha block with no cell or wifi signal, I want every scan, weight and lot tag captured locally and synced the instant a device comes back in range, so I can trust that no harvest record is ever lost and the daily kg total on the dashboard is actually complete.
- When fermentation tanks and drying patios at the wet/dry mill need temperature, pH and moisture readings every few minutes around the clock, I want an on-farm gateway to ingest sensor data even while the internet is down and reconcile it to the right lot/batch when it reconnects, so I can prove process control and protect a $40+/lb Geisha lot from a spoiled ferment.
- When a sudden cold front, frost risk, or heavy rain hits the upper plots, I want the gateway to evaluate sensor + weather rules locally and fire an alert (SMS / siren / push) within seconds without waiting on a cloud round-trip, so I can act before the cloud even knows there is a problem.
- When two supervisors edit the same task or harvest on different offline phones during harvest week, I want deterministic conflict resolution and a clear audit trail when they re-sync, so I can be confident the unified record is correct and never silently overwritten.

**Features:**
- **Finca Edge gateway appliance** — A ruggedized low-power box (Raspberry Pi 5 / industrial fanless mini-PC, PoE + solar/UPS) physically at the mill that runs a local Postgres + PostgREST mirror, an MQTT broker and a LoRaWAN concentrator. Collects every sensor reading and field entry on-prem so the farm keeps operating fully when the internet is down (which on a Volcán farm is often). Turns the current cloud-only read app into an offline-first system of record.
- **Offline-first sync engine with CRDT/last-writer-wins reconciliation** — Bidirectional, conflict-aware sync between field devices to the edge gateway to Supabase cloud. Each row carries a vector/lamport clock + device_id + dirty flag; reconnect replays a durable outbox. Deterministic merge (CRDT for counters like daily_kg, explicit conflict queue for human edits) means no harvest or task edit is ever silently lost during harvest-week chaos.
- **Installable offline PWA + local capture cache** — The Next.js app becomes a PWA (service worker, IndexedDB outbox, background sync) so pickers and supervisors record harvests, attendance and tasks fully offline on cheap Android phones, then auto-flush to the gateway over LAN/LoRa. Field work never stops because a plot has no bars.
- **LoRaWAN sensor mesh ingestion** — Long-range (km-scale, low-power) ingestion of soil-moisture, leaf-wetness, temperature, frost and tank/patio probes scattered across dispersed plots and the reserve edge — distances and tree cover that wifi/BLE cannot reach. One gateway covers the whole estate cheaply.
- **Local rules + edge alerting engine** — Evaluates frost/rain/ferment-overheat/over-fermentation rules on the gateway itself and fires SMS, a physical mill siren/relay, or push within seconds — protecting high-value lots even during a total cloud outage.
- **Fleet management, OTA updates & remote health** — Remote provisioning, signed over-the-air firmware/app updates, watchdog auto-restart, disk/queue/battery telemetry and a 'last seen / sync lag' panel surfaced in the existing dashboard, so the ops lead knows instantly if a node went dark rather than discovering a silent two-day data hole.
- **Edge data buffering & store-and-forward with backpressure** — Ring-buffer + durable WAL on the gateway holds days of readings during outages and throttles upload on metered links, guaranteeing zero data loss and predictable bandwidth cost on a $0-recurring posture.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** LoRaWAN: ChirpStack (self-hosted, open-source network server) + RAK Wireless / Dragino concentrator gateways and end-node sensors, The Things Stack / The Things Network (community LoRaWAN backhaul, free tier), MQTT broker: Eclipse Mosquitto / EMQX (self-hosted on the gateway), Supabase Realtime + PostgREST + Logical Replication as the cloud sync target (already the app's backend), PowerSync or ElectricSQL for Postgres to client offline replication and conflict handling, Service-worker / PWA: Workbox + IndexedDB; background-sync API, Sensors/hardware: Sensirion SHT (temp/humidity), Atlas Scientific pH probes (fermentation), Decagon/METER TEROS soil-moisture, Davis/Tempest weather station, frost sensors, Edge OS/fleet: balenaCloud or Mender for OTA + fleet management on Raspberry Pi 5 / Seeed reTerminal, Weather APIs for local rule context: Open-Meteo (free), Tomorrow.io, ETESA Panama hydromet feeds, Alerting rails: Twilio / Africastalk-equivalent local Panama SMS gateway, GPIO relay for a physical mill siren, Web Push (VAPID), Time-series storage on edge: TimescaleDB / InfluxDB; MQTT Sparkplug B for industrial payload standard, Standards: LoRaWAN 1.0.4, MQTT 5, OGC SensorThings API for interoperable sensor data, ISOBUS where machinery applies

**Data model additions:** edge_gateways (id, location, hardware_model, firmware_version, last_seen_at, sync_lag_seconds, battery_pct, disk_free_pct, status), sensor_nodes (id, gateway_id FK, node_type [soil-moisture|leaf-wetness|temp|ph|frost|weather|tank|patio], plot_id FK nullable, processing_batch_id FK nullable, lorawan_dev_eui, install_date, last_reading_at, status), sensor_readings (id, node_id FK, observed_at, metric, value, unit, quality_flag) — time-series, links to plots and lots/batches, sync_log (id, device_id, table_name, row_pk, op [insert|update|delete], lamport_clock, vector_clock jsonb, applied_at, origin [field|edge|cloud]), sync_conflicts (id, table_name, row_pk, field_device_value jsonb, edge_value jsonb, resolution [auto-lww|auto-crdt|manual], resolved_by, resolved_at), edge_outbox (id, device_id, payload jsonb, created_at, flushed_at, attempts) — durable store-and-forward queue, edge_alert_rules (id, gateway_id FK, name, condition_expr, channel [sms|siren|push], target, active, evaluated_locally bool), edge_alerts (id, rule_id FK, node_id FK, fired_at, value, acknowledged_by, acknowledged_at), device_registry (id, kind [phone|tablet|gateway|node], owner_worker_id FK, last_seen_at, app_version, push_token), ota_deployments (id, gateway_id FK, artifact_version, channel, rolled_out_at, status)

**AI / automation:**
- On-device (gateway) anomaly detection: flag sensor drift, stuck/dead probes, or out-of-band fermentation curves before a lot is ruined — runs locally so it works during cloud outages.
- Edge-resident fermentation-curve modeling: predict optimal pH/temp end-point per lot and method (Washed/Natural/Honey/Anaerobic) and alert the mill operator at the right moment, even offline.
- Smart sync prioritization: an ML policy that decides what to upload first on a thin/metered link (alerts and high-value Geisha lot data before bulk historical readings) to minimize bandwidth cost.
- Frost/microclimate nowcasting at the edge: fuse local sensor + Open-Meteo data into a short-horizon frost probability that fires faster than any cloud forecast for the specific 1,690 masl blocks.
- Gateway-side data-quality scoring that auto-tags readings with a quality_flag so downstream dashboards and certification reports never trust a faulty probe.
- Conflict-resolution copilot: when a human-edit conflict can't auto-merge, an LLM summarizes the divergence in plain Spanish and proposes the likely-correct record for one-tap approval.

**World-class:** World-class is an estate that runs identically whether the internet is up or down: every harvest scan, attendance mark, sensor tick and task edit is captured locally with sub-second local alerting, and reconciles to a single cloud source of truth with zero data loss and deterministic, auditable conflict resolution. Operators never think about connectivity — the system degrades gracefully and self-heals. Fleet health (last-seen, sync-lag, battery, disk) is observable at a glance, nodes update over-the-air, and a probe dying or a gateway going dark raises an alert in minutes, not when someone notices a two-day hole in the data. It is the John Deere Operations Center / Cropster-grade reliability bar, but built offline-first for a connectivity-poor cloud-forest estate on a near-zero recurring budget using open-source (ChirpStack, Mosquitto, Supabase, PowerSync).

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson's value is concentrated in a few high-altitude, high-price Geisha and reserve lots where a single spoiled fermentation or a frost event at 1,690 masl can wipe out tens of thousands of dollars — yet those exact plots and the 200-ha quetzal reserve are where connectivity is worst. The edge gateway is precisely what lets process-control telemetry (tank pH/temp, patio moisture) and frost alerting reach the high-value, low-signal parts of the estate and survive the frequent Volcán internet/power outages. It also underpins traceability credibility: an unbroken, locally-captured sensor + harvest record per lot code (the existing JC-xxx lots) is what backs a premium provenance and certification story 'from our farm to your cup since 1990' — the cloud app today has no idea where its numbers physically come from, and this layer is the missing physical-world data spine for every other pillar.


#### Voice / hands-free field data capture
*Priority:* **differentiator**  ·  *Opportunity:* **8/10**

**Persona:** Primary: the field picker and crew supervisor (cuadrilla lead) on the harvest line — hands full of cherry, standing at the receiving station or mid-row, often a Ngäbe-Buglé seasonal worker whose first language may be Ngäbere, not Spanish, and who is functionally low-literacy with a phone screen. Secondary: the roving agronomo/scout walking plots logging tree-health and pest observations, and the wet-mill operator timing fermentation tanks and turning drying beds with wet/dirty gloves.
