They share one trait: at the exact moment a fact is true (weigh-in, sighting, tank pH, bed turn), their hands and eyes are occupied, so today the fact is scrawled on a paper notebook and key-entered hours later — or lost.

**Jobs-to-be-done:**
- When I'm at the receiving station weighing a picker's basket at dusk with cherry-stained hands and a line of 30 pickers waiting, I want to just say 'Maria, lote Tizingal, dieciocho kilos' and have it logged against the right worker, plot and lot, so I can keep the line moving and not lose the day's tally to a smudged paper book.
- When I'm walking a row at 1,600 m with no cell signal and spot broca (coffee borer beetle) on three trees, I want to speak the observation, snap a photo, and have it geo-pinned hands-free, so I can flag a pest hotspot the moment I see it instead of trying to remember six sightings until I'm back at the office.
- When I'm a Ngäbere-first seasonal picker who can't comfortably type a Spanish form, I want to report my own basket weight by voice in my own language and hear it read back to confirm, so I trust that my pay-by-the-kilo count is right and I don't need a supervisor to enter it for me.
- When I'm the mill operator turning drying beds and checking a fermentation tank, I want to call out 'Bed siete, voltear, humedad once por ciento' over a Bluetooth headset, so the batch's moisture and turn-log updates without me peeling off wet gloves to touch a screen.

**Features:**
- **Hands-free harvest weigh-in (voice + Bluetooth scale)** — Picker basket weights logged by spoken command at the receiving station, auto-resolving worker + plot + lot from a slot-filling grammar; pairs with a Bluetooth crane/bench scale (Dini Argeo / Adam Equipment) so the kilo number is captured automatically and only the name/lot is spoken — kills the paper tally book and the nightly re-keying, and makes pay-by-the-kilo instantly auditable.
- **Offline voice scouting notes with geo-pin + photo** — Agronomo speaks a pest/disease/phenology observation while walking; on-device ASR transcribes it offline, attaches GPS + a photo, and classifies it into the Pest Control / Soil / phenology taxonomy — turns a 1,600 m no-signal walk into structured, mappable scouting data that drives the pest-hotspot heatmap and spray decisions.
- **Multilingual voice intents (Spanish + Ngäbere + English) with read-back confirmation** — A grammar/intent layer that understands farm-domain Spanish, basic Ngäbere harvest phrases, and English, then reads the parsed result back aloud ('Confirmas: Maria, Tizingal, dieciocho kilos?') before commit — gives a low-literacy, non-Spanish-first workforce a trustworthy self-service path and cuts supervisor data-entry bottlenecks.
- **Voice-first mill & drying log over headset** — Mill operator dictates fermentation pH/temperature, tank start/stop, and bed-turn + moisture events over an IP-rated Bluetooth headset — wet/dirty hands never touch a screen — keeping the processing_batches timeline live and defensible for SCA cupping traceability.
- **Voice command bar inside the existing app (push-to-talk)** — A single mic affordance in the topbar that turns any spoken sentence into a draft record across harvests/tasks/processing/inspections, shown as a confirmable card before it writes — one consistent capture surface layered onto the current read-only console so voice is a write-path overlay, not a separate app.
- **Offline-first capture queue with conflict-safe sync** — Every voice event is written to a local IndexedDB/SQLite queue with a client-generated id, timestamp and recorded-audio blob, then syncs to Supabase when signal returns with idempotent upserts — guarantees zero data loss on the mountain and makes the audio itself the audit trail when a parse is disputed.

**Integrations / vendors / hardware:** On-device ASR: whisper.cpp / faster-whisper (Spanish), Vosk (Kaldi, ~50MB-1.8GB offline edge models, Raspberry Pi-capable), and Moonshine (27MB, resource-constrained) for fully offline transcription at altitude with no cell signal, Apple Speech framework (SFSpeechRecognizer, on-device mode) and Android on-device SpeechRecognizer / Google Eloquent (Gemini Nano local dictation) as the OS-native fallback path, Cloud ASR for high-accuracy reconciliation when online: Deepgram Nova, AssemblyAI, Google Cloud Speech-to-Text, OpenAI Whisper API (Spanish + es-PA locale), Claude (Anthropic) for intent parsing / slot-filling and free-form note structuring — turning 'vi broca en tres matas cerca del beneficio' into a typed Pest Control observation with entity resolution, AgVoice — the established ag voice-to-data + workflow vendor and its industrial Bluetooth headset pattern, as a build-vs-partner reference and headset spec, Bluetooth bench/crane scales for auto weigh-in: Dini Argeo, Adam Equipment, Avery Weigh-Tronix (Bluetooth/serial weight broadcast), IP67 rugged Bluetooth headsets / bone-conduction earpieces (e.g. BlueParrott, Shokz OpenComm) for noisy, wet, gloved mill and harvest environments, Text-to-speech for read-back confirmation: Apple AVSpeechSynthesizer / Android TTS on-device, ElevenLabs or Google TTS for richer Spanish playback when online, Web Speech API (SpeechRecognition + SpeechSynthesis) for the in-browser push-to-talk path on the existing Next.js PWA, Capacitor / PWA + Service Worker + IndexedDB (or SQLite via Capacitor) for the offline capture queue, syncing to Supabase Postgres on reconnect

**Data model additions:** voice_capture_event — id, captured_at, captured_by (worker_id), source ('headset'|'phone'|'station'), language ('es'|'gn'|'en'), raw_transcript, audio_blob_ref, asr_engine, asr_confidence, parsed_intent, parse_confidence, status ('draft'|'confirmed'|'rejected'|'synced'), geo_lat, geo_lng, device_id, client_event_id (idempotency key), linked_record_type, linked_record_id, voice_intent — id, name (e.g. 'log_harvest','log_scouting','log_bed_turn'), grammar/slots schema, target_table, example_utterances (for few-shot + training), enabled, scouting_observation — id, observed_at, observer_worker_id, plot_id, geo_lat, geo_lng, category (extends task_category: pest/disease/phenology/soil), severity, free_text, photo_ref, source_voice_event_id, status — the new entity voice scouting writes into (the schema has no field-observation table today), processing_event — id, batch_id (FK processing_batches), event_type ('bed_turn'|'ferment_ph'|'moisture_reading'|'stage_change'), value_numeric, unit, recorded_at, recorded_by, source_voice_event_id — an event log under the existing batch snapshot so the mill timeline is captured, not just the current stage, device_registration — id, device_id, assigned_worker_id, last_sync_at, app_version, default_language — to manage offline edge devices and per-device sync state, harvest gains: source_voice_event_id + scale_device_id columns so a weigh-in's provenance (spoken vs typed, which scale) is traceable

**AI / automation:**
- Slot-filling intent parser (Claude) that resolves fuzzy spoken names to the right worker/plot/lot via the existing roster — 'Tizingal' to plot p-tizingal-alto, 'Maria' to the one Maria on Crew Norte working that plot today, with disambiguation prompts when ambiguous
- Confidence-gated auto-commit: high-confidence parses with a clean read-back auto-write; low-confidence ones route to a human-confirm card — so trust scales without a supervisor checking every line
- Accent / domain adaptation: fine-tune or prompt-bias the ASR on Panamanian Spanish, coffee jargon (broca, ojo de gallo, beneficio, lote, despulpado) and crew member names so recognition holds up on real farm vocabulary
- Anomaly flagging on spoken numbers: a 180 kg basket weight or 200% ripeness is flagged for re-confirmation before commit (fat-finger / mis-hear guard on the pay-critical path)
- Auto-classification of free-form scouting speech into the pest/disease/phenology taxonomy + severity, feeding the pest-hotspot map and predictive spray timing
- Daily voice digest: spoken end-of-day summary read back to the supervisor ('Hoy: 412 kilos, tres cuadrillas, dos focos de broca en Tizingal') for eyes-free review while driving down the mountain
- Speaker ID to auto-attribute a basket to the picker who spoke, so self-service weigh-in needs no name at all on enrolled crews

**World-class:** World-class is a picker walking up to the station, the Bluetooth scale auto-reads the weight, the picker says one phrase in their own language, the system reads it back, and the kilo is on their pay ledger before the next basket lands — zero paper, zero re-keying, zero literacy barrier, and it works with no signal at 1,700 m. It's AgVoice-grade hands-free scouting (industrial headset, transcribe-while-walking) fused with FIELD-scale automatic weigh capture, but coffee-specific and multilingual. Every voice event keeps its audio blob as a tamper-evident audit trail, parses with confidence-gated auto-commit, and syncs conflict-free when connectivity returns. The bar: 95%+ intent accuracy on domain Spanish, sub-2-second round trip, fully offline-capable, and a non-Spanish-first seasonal worker can self-report their own pay-critical weight and trust it.

**Coffee-specific angle:** Janson's geography makes this a differentiator, not a gimmick: plots sit at 1,350-1,700 m on volcano slopes where cell coverage is patchy-to-absent, so offline-first voice capture is the only capture that survives in the row. The harvest workforce is heavily Ngäbe-Buglé seasonal labor — many Ngäbere-first and low-literacy — and pay is by the kilo, so a trustworthy, multilingual, read-back voice weigh-in directly removes the supervisor data-entry bottleneck and the paper-tally disputes that plague Panamanian coffee fincas. On the value side, Janson sells award-tier Geisha into reserve auctions where buyers pay for traceability and process transparency; voice-captured fermentation pH, bed-turn and moisture events give a defensible, SCA/cupping-grade batch story per micro-lot. And the 200-hectare quetzal reserve makes hands-free, geo-pinned voice scouting a natural fit for biodiversity/shade-canopy and pest observations logged on foot without disturbing habitat.
